```rst
\* Fixed several typos in the init-script documentation.



\* A regression caused `Task.exchange` and `Task.routing\_key` to no longer

&#x20; have any effect. This is now fixed.



\* Routing user guide: Fixes typo, routers in :setting:`CELERY\_ROUTES` must be

&#x20; instances, not classes.



\* :program:`celeryev` didn't create pidfile even though the

&#x20; :option:`--pidfile <celery events --pidfile>` argument was set.



\* Task logger format was no longer used (Issue #317).



&#x20;  The id and name of the task is now part of the log message again.



\* A safe version of ``repr()`` is now used in strategic places to ensure

&#x20; objects with a broken ``\_\_repr\_\_`` doesn't crash the worker, or otherwise

&#x20; make errors hard to understand (Issue #298).



\* Remote control command :control:`active\_queues`: didn't account for queues added

&#x20; at runtime.



&#x20;   In addition the dictionary replied by this command now has a different

&#x20;   structure: the exchange key is now a dictionary containing the

&#x20;   exchange declaration in full.



\* The :option:`celery worker -Q` option removed unused queue

&#x20; declarations, so routing of tasks could fail.



&#x20;   Queues are no longer removed, but rather `app.amqp.queues.consume\_from()`

&#x20;   is used as the list of queues to consume from.



&#x20;   This ensures all queues are available for routing purposes.



\* ``celeryctl``: Now supports the `inspect active\_queues` command.



.. \_version-2.2.2:



2.2.2

=====

:release-date: 2011-02-03 04:00 p.m. CET

:release-by: Ask Solem



.. \_v222-fixes:



Fixes

\-----



\* ``celerybeat`` couldn't read the schedule properly, so entries in

&#x20; :setting:`CELERYBEAT\_SCHEDULE` wouldn't be scheduled.



\* Task error log message now includes `exc\_info` again.



\* The `eta` argument can now be used with `task.retry`.



&#x20;   Previously it was overwritten by the countdown argument.



\* ``celery multi``/``celeryd\_detach``: Now logs errors occurring when executing

&#x20; the `celery worker` command.



\* daemonizing tutorial: Fixed typo ``--time-limit 300`` ->

&#x20; ``--time-limit=300``



\* Colors in logging broke non-string objects in log messages.



\* ``setup\_task\_logger`` no longer makes assumptions about magic task kwargs.



.. \_version-2.2.1:



2.2.1

=====

:release-date: 2011-02-02 04:00 p.m. CET

:release-by: Ask Solem



.. \_v221-fixes:



Fixes

\-----



\* Eventlet pool was leaking memory (Issue #308).



\* Deprecated function ``celery.execute.delay\_task`` was accidentally removed,

&#x20; now available again.



\* ``BasePool.on\_terminate`` stub didn't exist



\* ``celeryd\_detach``: Adds readable error messages if user/group name

&#x20; doesn't exist.



\* Smarter handling of unicode decode errors when logging errors.



.. \_version-2.2.0:



2.2.0

=====

:release-date: 2011-02-01 10:00 AM CET

:release-by: Ask Solem



.. \_v220-important:



Important Notes

\---------------



\* Carrot has been replaced with :pypi:`Kombu`



&#x20;   Kombu is the next generation messaging library for Python,

&#x20;   fixing several flaws present in Carrot that was hard to fix

&#x20;   without breaking backwards compatibility.



&#x20;   Also it adds:



&#x20;   \* First-class support for virtual transports; Redis, Django ORM,

&#x20;     SQLAlchemy, Beanstalk, MongoDB, CouchDB and in-memory.

&#x20;   \* Consistent error handling with introspection,

&#x20;   \* The ability to ensure that an operation is performed by gracefully

&#x20;     handling connection and channel errors,

&#x20;   \* Message compression (:mod:`zlib`, :mod:`bz2`, or custom compression schemes).



&#x20;   This means that `ghettoq` is no longer needed as the

&#x20;   functionality it provided is already available in Celery by default.

&#x20;   The virtual transports are also more feature complete with support

&#x20;   for exchanges (direct and topic). The Redis transport even supports

&#x20;   fanout exchanges so it's able to perform worker remote control

&#x20;   commands.



\* Magic keyword arguments pending deprecation.



&#x20;   The magic keyword arguments were responsible for many problems

&#x20;   and quirks: notably issues with tasks and decorators, and name

&#x20;   collisions in keyword arguments for the unaware.



&#x20;   It wasn't easy to find a way to deprecate the magic keyword arguments,

&#x20;   but we think this is a solution that makes sense and it won't

&#x20;   have any adverse effects for existing code.



&#x20;   The path to a magic keyword argument free world is:



&#x20;       \* the `celery.decorators` module is deprecated and the decorators

&#x20;         can now be found in `celery.task`.

&#x20;       \* The decorators in `celery.task` disables keyword arguments by

&#x20;         default

&#x20;       \* All examples in the documentation have been changed to use

&#x20;         `celery.task`.



&#x20;       This means that the following will have magic keyword arguments

&#x20;       enabled (old style):



&#x20;           .. code-block:: python



&#x20;               from celery.decorators import task



&#x20;               @task()

&#x20;               def add(x, y, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;                   print('In task %s' % kwargs\['task\_id'])

&#x20;                   return x + y



&#x20;       And this won't use magic keyword arguments (new style):



&#x20;           .. code-block:: python



&#x20;               from celery.task import task



&#x20;               @task()

&#x20;               def add(x, y):

&#x20;                   print('In task %s' % add.request.id)

&#x20;                   return x + y



&#x20;   In addition, tasks can choose not to accept magic keyword arguments by

&#x20;   setting the `task.accept\_magic\_kwargs` attribute.



&#x20;   .. admonition:: Deprecation



&#x20;       Using the decorators in :mod:`celery.decorators` emits a

&#x20;       :class:`PendingDeprecationWarning` with a helpful message urging

&#x20;       you to change your code, in version 2.4 this will be replaced with

&#x20;       a :class:`DeprecationWarning`, and in version 4.0 the

&#x20;       :mod:`celery.decorators` module will be removed and no longer exist.



&#x20;       Similarly, the `task.accept\_magic\_kwargs` attribute will no

&#x20;       longer have any effect starting from version 4.0.



\* The magic keyword arguments are now available as `task.request`



&#x20;   This is called \*the context\*. Using thread-local storage the

&#x20;   context contains state that's related to the current request.



&#x20;   It's mutable and you can add custom attributes that'll only be seen

&#x20;   by the current task request.



&#x20;   The following context attributes are always available:



&#x20;   =====================================  ===================================

&#x20;   \*\*Magic Keyword Argument\*\*             \*\*Replace with\*\*

&#x20;   =====================================  ===================================

&#x20;   `kwargs\['task\_id']`                    `self.request.id`

&#x20;   `kwargs\['delivery\_info']`              `self.request.delivery\_info`

&#x20;   `kwargs\['task\_retries']`               `self.request.retries`

&#x20;   `kwargs\['logfile']`                    `self.request.logfile`

&#x20;   `kwargs\['loglevel']`                   `self.request.loglevel`

&#x20;   `kwargs\['task\_is\_eager']`              `self.request.is\_eager`

&#x20;   \*\*NEW\*\*                                `self.request.args`

&#x20;   \*\*NEW\*\*                                `self.request.kwargs`

&#x20;   =====================================  ===================================



&#x20;   In addition, the following methods now automatically uses the current

&#x20;   context, so you don't have to pass `kwargs` manually anymore:



&#x20;       \* `task.retry`

&#x20;       \* `task.get\_logger`

&#x20;       \* `task.update\_state`



\* `Eventlet`\_ support.



&#x20;   This is great news for I/O-bound tasks!



&#x20;   To change pool implementations you use the :option:`celery worker --pool`

&#x20;   argument, or globally using the

&#x20;   :setting:`CELERYD\_POOL` setting. This can be the full name of a class,

&#x20;   or one of the following aliases: `processes`, `eventlet`, `gevent`.



&#x20;   For more information please see the :ref:`concurrency-eventlet` section

&#x20;   in the User Guide.



&#x20;   .. admonition:: Why not gevent?



&#x20;       For our first alternative concurrency implementation we've focused

&#x20;       on `Eventlet`\_, but there's also an experimental `gevent`\_ pool

&#x20;       available. This is missing some features, notably the ability to

&#x20;       schedule ETA tasks.



&#x20;       Hopefully the `gevent`\_ support will be feature complete by

&#x20;       version 2.3, but this depends on user demand (and contributions).



.. \_`Eventlet`: http://eventlet.net

.. \_`gevent`: http://gevent.org



\* Python 2.4 support deprecated!



&#x20;   We're happy^H^H^H^H^Hsad to announce that this is the last version

&#x20;   to support Python 2.4.



&#x20;   You're urged to make some noise if you're currently stuck with

&#x20;   Python 2.4. Complain to your package maintainers, sysadmins and bosses:

&#x20;   tell them it's time to move on!



&#x20;   Apart from wanting to take advantage of :keyword:`with` statements,

&#x20;   coroutines, conditional expressions and enhanced :keyword:`try` blocks,

&#x20;   the code base now contains so many 2.4 related hacks and workarounds

&#x20;   it's no longer just a compromise, but a sacrifice.



&#x20;   If it really isn't your choice, and you don't have the option to upgrade

&#x20;   to a newer version of Python, you can just continue to use Celery 2.2.

&#x20;   Important fixes can be back ported for as long as there's interest.



\* worker: Now supports Autoscaling of child worker processes.



&#x20;   The :option:`--autoscale <celery worker --autoscale>` option can be used

&#x20;   to configure the minimum and maximum number of child worker processes:



&#x20;   .. code-block:: text



&#x20;       --autoscale=AUTOSCALE

&#x20;            Enable autoscaling by providing

&#x20;            max\_concurrency,min\_concurrency. Example:

&#x20;              --autoscale=10,3 (always keep 3 processes, but grow to

&#x20;             10 if necessary).



\* Remote Debugging of Tasks



&#x20;  ``celery.contrib.rdb`` is an extended version of :mod:`pdb` that

&#x20;  enables remote debugging of processes that doesn't have terminal

&#x20;  access.



&#x20;  Example usage:



&#x20;  .. code-block:: text



&#x20;       from celery.contrib import rdb

&#x20;       from celery.task import task



&#x20;       @task()

&#x20;       def add(x, y):

&#x20;           result = x + y

&#x20;           # set breakpoint

&#x20;           rdb.set\_trace()

&#x20;           return result



&#x20;   :func:`\~celery.contrib.rdb.set\_trace` sets a breakpoint at the current

&#x20;   location and creates a socket you can telnet into to remotely debug

&#x20;   your task.



&#x20;   The debugger may be started by multiple processes at the same time,

&#x20;   so rather than using a fixed port the debugger will search for an

&#x20;   available port, starting from the base port (6900 by default).

&#x20;   The base port can be changed using the environment variable

&#x20;   :envvar:`CELERY\_RDB\_PORT`.



&#x20;   By default the debugger will only be available from the local host,

&#x20;   to enable access from the outside you have to set the environment

&#x20;   variable :envvar:`CELERY\_RDB\_HOST`.



&#x20;   When the worker encounters your breakpoint it will log the following

&#x20;   information::



&#x20;       \[INFO/MainProcess] Received task:

&#x20;           tasks.add\[d7261c71-4962-47e5-b342-2448bedd20e8]

&#x20;       \[WARNING/PoolWorker-1] Remote Debugger:6900:

&#x20;           Please telnet 127.0.0.1 6900.  Type `exit` in session to continue.

&#x20;       \[2011-01-18 14:25:44,119: WARNING/PoolWorker-1] Remote Debugger:6900:

&#x20;           Waiting for client...



&#x20;   If you telnet the port specified you'll be presented

&#x20;   with a ``pdb`` shell:



&#x20;   .. code-block:: console



&#x20;       $ telnet localhost 6900

&#x20;       Connected to localhost.

&#x20;       Escape character is '^]'.

&#x20;       > /opt/devel/demoapp/tasks.py(128)add()

&#x20;       -> return result

&#x20;       (Pdb)



&#x20;   Enter ``help`` to get a list of available commands,

&#x20;   It may be a good idea to read the `Python Debugger Manual`\_ if

&#x20;   you have never used `pdb` before.



.. \_`Python Debugger Manual`: http://docs.python.org/library/pdb.html





\* Events are now transient and is using a topic exchange (instead of direct).



&#x20;   The `CELERYD\_EVENT\_EXCHANGE`, `CELERYD\_EVENT\_ROUTING\_KEY`,

&#x20;   `CELERYD\_EVENT\_EXCHANGE\_TYPE` settings are no longer in use.



&#x20;   This means events won't be stored until there's a consumer, and the

&#x20;   events will be gone as soon as the consumer stops. Also it means there

&#x20;   can be multiple monitors running at the same time.



&#x20;   The routing key of an event is the type of event (e.g., `worker.started`,

&#x20;   `worker.heartbeat`, `task.succeeded`, etc. This means a consumer can

&#x20;   filter on specific types, to only be alerted of the events it cares about.



&#x20;   Each consumer will create a unique queue, meaning it's in effect a

&#x20;   broadcast exchange.



&#x20;   This opens up a lot of possibilities, for example the workers could listen

&#x20;   for worker events to know what workers are in the neighborhood, and even

&#x20;   restart workers when they go down (or use this information to optimize

&#x20;   tasks/autoscaling).



&#x20;   .. note::



&#x20;       The event exchange has been renamed from ``"celeryevent"``

&#x20;       to ``"celeryev"`` so it doesn't collide with older versions.



&#x20;       If you'd like to remove the old exchange you can do so

&#x20;       by executing the following command:



&#x20;       .. code-block:: console



&#x20;           $ camqadm exchange.delete celeryevent



\* The worker now starts without configuration, and configuration can be

&#x20; specified directly on the command-line.



&#x20; Configuration options must appear after the last argument, separated

&#x20; by two dashes:



&#x20; .. code-block:: console



&#x20;     $ celery worker -l info -I tasks -- broker.host=localhost broker.vhost=/app



\* Configuration is now an alias to the original configuration, so changes

&#x20; to the original will reflect Celery at runtime.



\* `celery.conf` has been deprecated, and modifying `celery.conf.ALWAYS\_EAGER`

&#x20; will no longer have any effect.



&#x20;   The default configuration is now available in the

&#x20;   :mod:`celery.app.defaults` module. The available configuration options

&#x20;   and their types can now be introspected.



\* Remote control commands are now provided by `kombu.pidbox`, the generic

&#x20; process mailbox.



\* Internal module `celery.worker.listener` has been renamed to

&#x20; `celery.worker.consumer`, and `.CarrotListener` is now `.Consumer`.



\* Previously deprecated modules `celery.models` and

&#x20; `celery.management.commands` have now been removed as per the deprecation

&#x20; time-line.



\* \[Security: Low severity] Removed `celery.task.RemoteExecuteTask` and

&#x20;   accompanying functions: `dmap`, `dmap\_async`, and `execute\_remote`.



&#x20;   Executing arbitrary code using pickle is a potential security issue if

&#x20;   someone gains unrestricted access to the message broker.



&#x20;   If you really need this functionality, then you'd've to add

&#x20;   this to your own project.



\* \[Security: Low severity] The `stats` command no longer transmits the

&#x20; broker password.



&#x20;   One would've needed an authenticated broker connection to receive

&#x20;   this password in the first place, but sniffing the password at the

&#x20;   wire level would've been possible if using unencrypted communication.



.. \_v220-news:



News

\----



\* The internal module `celery.task.builtins` has been removed.



\* The module `celery.task.schedules` is deprecated, and

&#x20; `celery.schedules` should be used instead.



&#x20;   For example if you have::



&#x20;       from celery.task.schedules import crontab



&#x20;   You should replace that with::



&#x20;       from celery.schedules import crontab



&#x20;   The module needs to be renamed because it must be possible

&#x20;   to import schedules without importing the `celery.task` module.



\* The following functions have been deprecated and is scheduled for

&#x20; removal in version 2.3:



&#x20;   \* `celery.execute.apply\_async`



&#x20;       Use `task.apply\_async()` instead.



&#x20;   \* `celery.execute.apply`



&#x20;       Use `task.apply()` instead.



&#x20;   \* `celery.execute.delay\_task`



&#x20;       Use `registry.tasks\[name].delay()` instead.



\* Importing `TaskSet` from `celery.task.base` is now deprecated.



&#x20;   You should use::



&#x20;       >>> from celery.task import TaskSet



&#x20;   instead.



\* New remote control commands:



&#x20;   \* `active\_queues`



&#x20;       Returns the queue declarations a worker is currently consuming from.



\* Added the ability to retry publishing the task message in

&#x20; the event of connection loss or failure.



&#x20;   This is disabled by default but can be enabled using the

&#x20;   :setting:`CELERY\_TASK\_PUBLISH\_RETRY` setting, and tweaked by

&#x20;   the :setting:`CELERY\_TASK\_PUBLISH\_RETRY\_POLICY` setting.



&#x20;   In addition `retry`, and `retry\_policy` keyword arguments have

&#x20;   been added to `Task.apply\_async`.



&#x20;   .. note::



&#x20;       Using the `retry` argument to `apply\_async` requires you to

&#x20;       handle the publisher/connection manually.



\* Periodic Task classes (`@periodic\_task`/`PeriodicTask`) will \*not\* be

&#x20; deprecated as previously indicated in the source code.



&#x20;   But you're encouraged to use the more flexible

&#x20;   :setting:`CELERYBEAT\_SCHEDULE` setting.



\* Built-in daemonization support of the worker using `celery multi`

&#x20; is no longer experimental and is considered production quality.



&#x20;    See :ref:`daemon-generic` if you want to use the new generic init

&#x20;    scripts.



\* Added support for message compression using the

&#x20; :setting:`CELERY\_MESSAGE\_COMPRESSION` setting, or the `compression` argument

&#x20; to `apply\_async`. This can also be set using routers.



\* worker: Now logs stack-trace of all threads when receiving the

&#x20;  `SIGUSR1` signal (doesn't work on CPython 2.4, Windows or Jython).



&#x20;   Inspired by https://gist.github.com/737056



\* Can now remotely terminate/kill the worker process currently processing

&#x20; a task.



&#x20;   The `revoke` remote control command now supports a `terminate` argument

&#x20;   Default signal is `TERM`, but can be specified using the `signal`

&#x20;   argument. Signal can be the uppercase name of any signal defined

&#x20;   in the :mod:`signal` module in the Python Standard Library.



&#x20;   Terminating a task also revokes it.



&#x20;   Example::



&#x20;       >>> from celery.task.control import revoke



&#x20;       >>> revoke(task\_id, terminate=True)

&#x20;       >>> revoke(task\_id, terminate=True, signal='KILL')

&#x20;       >>> revoke(task\_id, terminate=True, signal='SIGKILL')



\* `TaskSetResult.join\_native`: Backend-optimized version of `join()`.



&#x20;   If available, this version uses the backends ability to retrieve

&#x20;   multiple results at once, unlike `join()` which fetches the results

&#x20;   one by one.



&#x20;   So far only supported by the AMQP result backend. Support for Memcached

&#x20;   and Redis may be added later.



\* Improved implementations of `TaskSetResult.join` and `AsyncResult.wait`.



&#x20;  An `interval` keyword argument have been added to both so the

&#x20;  polling interval can be specified (default interval is 0.5 seconds).



&#x20;   A `propagate` keyword argument have been added to `result.wait()`,

&#x20;   errors will be returned instead of raised if this is set to False.



&#x20;   .. warning::



&#x20;       You should decrease the polling interval when using the database

&#x20;       result backend, as frequent polling can result in high database load.





\* The PID of the child worker process accepting a task is now sent as a field

&#x20; with the :event:`task-started` event.



\* The following fields have been added to all events in the worker class:



&#x20;   \* `sw\_ident`: Name of worker software (e.g., ``"py-celery"``).

&#x20;   \* `sw\_ver`: Software version (e.g., 2.2.0).

&#x20;   \* `sw\_sys`: Operating System (e.g., Linux, Windows, Darwin).



\* For better accuracy the start time reported by the multiprocessing worker

&#x20; process is used when calculating task duration.



&#x20;   Previously the time reported by the accept callback was used.



\* `celerybeat`: New built-in daemonization support using the `--detach`

&#x20;  option.



\* `celeryev`: New built-in daemonization support using the `--detach`

&#x20;  option.



\* `TaskSet.apply\_async`: Now supports custom publishers by using the

&#x20; `publisher` argument.



\* Added :setting:`CELERY\_SEND\_TASK\_SENT\_EVENT` setting.



&#x20;   If enabled an event will be sent with every task, so monitors can

&#x20;   track tasks before the workers receive them.



\* `celerybeat`: Now reuses the broker connection when calling

&#x20;  scheduled tasks.



\* The configuration module and loader to use can now be specified on

&#x20; the command-line.



&#x20;   For example:



&#x20;   .. code-block:: console



&#x20;       $ celery worker --config=celeryconfig.py --loader=myloader.Loader



\* Added signals: `beat\_init` and `beat\_embedded\_init`



&#x20;   \* :signal:`celery.signals.beat\_init`



&#x20;       Dispatched when :program:`celerybeat` starts (either standalone or

&#x20;       embedded). Sender is the :class:`celery.beat.Service` instance.



&#x20;   \* :signal:`celery.signals.beat\_embedded\_init`



&#x20;       Dispatched in addition to the :signal:`beat\_init` signal when

&#x20;       :program:`celerybeat` is started as an embedded process. Sender

&#x20;       is the :class:`celery.beat.Service` instance.



\* Redis result backend: Removed deprecated settings `REDIS\_TIMEOUT` and

&#x20; `REDIS\_CONNECT\_RETRY`.



\* CentOS init-script for :program:`celery worker` now available in `extra/centos`.



\* Now depends on :pypi:`pyparsing` version 1.5.0 or higher.



&#x20;   There have been reported issues using Celery with :pypi:`pyparsing` 1.4.x,

&#x20;   so please upgrade to the latest version.



\* Lots of new unit tests written, now with a total coverage of 95%.



.. \_v220-fixes:



Fixes

\-----



\* `celeryev` Curses Monitor: Improved resize handling and UI layout

&#x20; (Issue #274 + Issue #276)



\* AMQP Backend: Exceptions occurring while sending task results are now

&#x20; propagated instead of silenced.



&#x20;   the worker will then show the full traceback of these errors in the log.



\* AMQP Backend: No longer deletes the result queue after successful

&#x20; poll, as this should be handled by the

&#x20; :setting:`CELERY\_AMQP\_TASK\_RESULT\_EXPIRES` setting instead.



\* AMQP Backend: Now ensures queues are declared before polling results.



\* Windows: worker: Show error if running with `-B` option.



&#x20;   Running ``celerybeat`` embedded is known not to work on Windows, so

&#x20;   users are encouraged to run ``celerybeat`` as a separate service instead.



\* Windows: Utilities no longer output ANSI color codes on Windows



\* ``camqadm``: Now properly handles :kbd:`Control-c` by simply exiting instead

&#x20; of showing confusing traceback.



\* Windows: All tests are now passing on Windows.



\* Remove bin/ directory, and `scripts` section from :file:`setup.py`.



&#x20;   This means we now rely completely on setuptools entry-points.



.. \_v220-experimental:



Experimental

\------------



\* Jython: worker now runs on Jython using the threaded pool.



&#x20;   All tests pass, but there may still be bugs lurking around the corners.



\* PyPy: worker now runs on PyPy.



&#x20;   It runs without any pool, so to get parallel execution you must start

&#x20;   multiple instances (e.g., using :program:`multi`).



&#x20;   Sadly an initial benchmark seems to show a 30% performance decrease on

&#x20;   ``pypy-1.4.1`` + JIT. We would like to find out why this is, so stay tuned.



\* :class:`PublisherPool`: Experimental pool of task publishers and

&#x20; connections to be used with the `retry` argument to `apply\_async`.



&#x20; The example code below will re-use connections and channels, and

&#x20; retry sending of the task message if the connection is lost.



&#x20; .. code-block:: python



&#x20;   from celery import current\_app



&#x20;   # Global pool

&#x20;   pool = current\_app().amqp.PublisherPool(limit=10)



&#x20;   def my\_view(request):

&#x20;       with pool.acquire() as publisher:

&#x20;           add.apply\_async((2, 2), publisher=publisher, retry=True)



```



\### 代码文件: docs\\history\\changelog-2.3.rst

```rst

.. \_changelog-2.3:



===============================

&#x20;Change history for Celery 2.3

===============================



.. contents::

&#x20;   :local:



.. \_version-2.3.4:



2.3.4

=====

:release-date: 2011-11-25 04:00 p.m. GMT

:release-by: Ask Solem



.. \_v234-security-fixes:



Security Fixes

\--------------



\* \[Security: `CELERYSA-0001`\_] Daemons would set effective id's rather than

&#x20; real id's when the :option:`!--uid`/

&#x20; :option:`!--gid` arguments to :program:`celery multi`,

&#x20; :program:`celeryd\_detach`, :program:`celery beat` and

&#x20; :program:`celery events` were used.



&#x20; This means privileges weren't properly dropped, and that it would

&#x20; be possible to regain supervisor privileges later.





.. \_`CELERYSA-0001`:

&#x20;   https://github.com/celery/celery/tree/master/docs/sec/CELERYSA-0001.txt



Fixes

\-----



\* Backported fix for #455 from 2.4 to 2.3.



\* StateDB wasn't saved at shutdown.



\* Fixes worker sometimes hanging when hard time limit exceeded.





.. \_version-2.3.3:



2.3.3

=====

:release-date: 2011-16-09 05:00 p.m. BST

:release-by: Mher Movsisyan



\* Monkey patching :attr:`sys.stdout` could result in the worker

&#x20; crashing if the replacing object didn't define :meth:`isatty`

&#x20; (Issue #477).



\* ``CELERYD`` option in :file:`/etc/default/celeryd` shouldn't

&#x20; be used with generic init-scripts.





.. \_version-2.3.2:



2.3.2

=====

:release-date: 2011-10-07 05:00 p.m. BST

:release-by: Ask Solem



.. \_v232-news:



News

\----



\* Improved Contributing guide.



&#x20;   If you'd like to contribute to Celery you should read the

&#x20;   :ref:`Contributing Gudie <contributing>`.



&#x20;   We're looking for contributors at all skill levels, so don't

&#x20;   hesitate!



\* Now depends on Kombu 1.3.1



\* ``Task.request`` now contains the current worker host name (Issue #460).



&#x20;   Available as ``task.request.hostname``.



\* It's now easier for app subclasses to extend how they're pickled.

&#x20;   (see :class:`celery.app.AppPickler`).



.. \_v232-fixes:



Fixes

\-----



\* `purge/discard\_all` wasn't working correctly (Issue #455).



\* The coloring of log messages didn't handle non-ASCII data well

&#x20; (Issue #427).



\* \[Windows] the multiprocessing pool tried to import ``os.kill``

&#x20; even though this isn't available there (Issue #450).



\* Fixes case where the worker could become unresponsive because of tasks

&#x20; exceeding the hard time limit.



\* The :event:`task-sent` event was missing from the event reference.



\* ``ResultSet.iterate`` now returns results as they finish (Issue #459).



&#x20;   This wasn't the case previously, even though the documentation

&#x20;   states this was the expected behavior.



\* Retries will no longer be performed when tasks are called directly

&#x20; (using ``\_\_call\_\_``).



&#x20;  Instead the exception passed to ``retry`` will be re-raised.



\* Eventlet no longer crashes if autoscale is enabled.



&#x20;   growing and shrinking eventlet pools is still not supported.



\* ``py24`` target removed from :file:`tox.ini`.





.. \_version-2.3.1:



2.3.1

=====

:release-date: 2011-08-07 08:00 p.m. BST

:release-by: Ask Solem



Fixes

\-----



\* The :setting:`CELERY\_AMQP\_TASK\_RESULT\_EXPIRES` setting didn't work,

&#x20; resulting in an AMQP related error about not being able to serialize

&#x20; floats while trying to publish task states (Issue #446).



.. \_version-2.3.0:



2.3.0

=====

:release-date: 2011-08-05 12:00 p.m. BST

:tested: CPython: 2.5, 2.6, 2.7; PyPy: 1.5; Jython: 2.5.2

:release-by: Ask Solem



.. \_v230-important:



Important Notes

\---------------



\* Now requires Kombu 1.2.1



\* Results are now disabled by default.



&#x20;   The AMQP backend wasn't a good default because often the users were

&#x20;   not consuming the results, resulting in thousands of queues.



&#x20;   While the queues can be configured to expire if left unused, it wasn't

&#x20;   possible to enable this by default because this was only available in

&#x20;   recent RabbitMQ versions (2.1.1+)



&#x20;   With this change enabling a result backend will be a conscious choice,

&#x20;   which will hopefully lead the user to read the documentation and be aware

&#x20;   of any common pitfalls with the particular backend.



&#x20;   The default backend is now a dummy backend

&#x20;   (:class:`celery.backends.base.DisabledBackend`). Saving state is simply an

&#x20;   no-op, and AsyncResult.wait(), .result, .state, etc. will raise

&#x20;   a :exc:`NotImplementedError` telling the user to configure the result backend.



&#x20;   For help choosing a backend please see :ref:`task-result-backends`.



&#x20;   If you depend on the previous default which was the AMQP backend, then

&#x20;   you have to set this explicitly before upgrading::



&#x20;       CELERY\_RESULT\_BACKEND = 'amqp'



&#x20;   .. note::



&#x20;       For :pypi:`django-celery` users the default backend is

&#x20;       still ``database``, and results are not disabled by default.



\* The Debian init-scripts have been deprecated in favor of the generic-init.d

&#x20; init-scripts.



&#x20;   In addition generic init-scripts for ``celerybeat`` and ``celeryev`` has

&#x20;   been added.



.. \_v230-news:



News

\----



\* Automatic connection pool support.



&#x20;   The pool is used by everything that requires a broker connection, for

&#x20;   example calling tasks, sending broadcast commands, retrieving results

&#x20;   with the AMQP result backend, and so on.



&#x20;   The pool is disabled by default, but you can enable it by configuring the

&#x20;   :setting:`BROKER\_POOL\_LIMIT` setting::



&#x20;       BROKER\_POOL\_LIMIT = 10



&#x20;   A limit of 10 means a maximum of 10 simultaneous connections can co-exist.

&#x20;   Only a single connection will ever be used in a single-thread

&#x20;   environment, but in a concurrent environment (threads, greenlets, etc., but

&#x20;   not processes) when the limit has been exceeded, any try to acquire a

&#x20;   connection will block the thread and wait for a connection to be released.

&#x20;   This is something to take into consideration when choosing a limit.



&#x20;   A limit of :const:`None` or 0 means no limit, and connections will be

&#x20;   established and closed every time.



\* Introducing Chords (taskset callbacks).



&#x20;   A chord is a task that only executes after all of the tasks in a taskset

&#x20;   has finished executing. It's a fancy term for "taskset callbacks"

&#x20;   adopted from

&#x20;   `Cω  <http://research.microsoft.com/en-us/um/cambridge/projects/comega/>`\_).



&#x20;   It works with all result backends, but the best implementation is

&#x20;   currently provided by the Redis result backend.



&#x20;   Here's an example chord::



&#x20;       >>> chord(add.subtask((i, i))

&#x20;       ...         for i in xrange(100))(tsum.subtask()).get()

&#x20;       9900



&#x20;   Please read the :ref:`Chords section in the user guide <canvas-chord>`, if you

&#x20;   want to know more.



\* Time limits can now be set for individual tasks.



&#x20;   To set the soft and hard time limits for a task use the ``time\_limit``

&#x20;   and ``soft\_time\_limit`` attributes:



&#x20;   .. code-block:: python



&#x20;       import time



&#x20;       @task(time\_limit=60, soft\_time\_limit=30)

&#x20;       def sleeptask(seconds):

&#x20;           time.sleep(seconds)



&#x20;   If the attributes are not set, then the workers default time limits

&#x20;   will be used.



&#x20;   New in this version you can also change the time limits for a task

&#x20;   at runtime using the :func:`time\_limit` remote control command::



&#x20;       >>> from celery.task import control

&#x20;       >>> control.time\_limit('tasks.sleeptask',

&#x20;       ...                    soft=60, hard=120, reply=True)

&#x20;       \[{'worker1.example.com': {'ok': 'time limits set successfully'}}]



&#x20;   Only tasks that starts executing after the time limit change will be affected.



&#x20;   .. note::



&#x20;       Soft time limits will still not work on Windows or other platforms

&#x20;       that don't have the ``SIGUSR1`` signal.



\* Redis backend configuration directive names changed to include the

&#x20;  ``CELERY\_`` prefix.





&#x20;   =====================================  ===================================

&#x20;   \*\*Old setting name\*\*                   \*\*Replace with\*\*

&#x20;   =====================================  ===================================

&#x20;   `REDIS\_HOST`                           `CELERY\_REDIS\_HOST`

&#x20;   `REDIS\_PORT`                           `CELERY\_REDIS\_PORT`

&#x20;   `REDIS\_DB`                             `CELERY\_REDIS\_DB`

&#x20;   `REDIS\_PASSWORD`                       `CELERY\_REDIS\_PASSWORD`

&#x20;   =====================================  ===================================



&#x20;   The old names are still supported but pending deprecation.



\* PyPy: The default pool implementation used is now multiprocessing

&#x20; if running on PyPy 1.5.



\* multi: now supports "pass through" options.



&#x20;   Pass through options makes it easier to use Celery without a

&#x20;   configuration file, or just add last-minute options on the command

&#x20;   line.



&#x20;   Example use:



&#x20;   .. code-block:: console



&#x20;       $ celery multi start 4  -c 2  -- broker.host=amqp.example.com \\

&#x20;                                        broker.vhost=/               \\

&#x20;                                        celery.disable\_rate\_limits=yes



\* ``celerybeat``: Now retries establishing the connection (Issue #419).



\* ``celeryctl``: New ``list bindings`` command.



&#x20;   Lists the current or all available bindings, depending on the

&#x20;   broker transport used.



\* Heartbeat is now sent every 30 seconds (previously every 2 minutes).



\* ``ResultSet.join\_native()`` and ``iter\_native()`` is now supported by

&#x20; the Redis and Cache result backends.



&#x20;   This is an optimized version of ``join()`` using the underlying

&#x20;   backends ability to fetch multiple results at once.



\* Can now use SSL when sending error e-mails by enabling the

&#x20; :setting:`EMAIL\_USE\_SSL` setting.



\* ``events.default\_dispatcher()``: Context manager to easily obtain

&#x20; an event dispatcher instance using the connection pool.



\* Import errors in the configuration module won't be silenced anymore.



\* ResultSet.iterate:  Now supports the ``timeout``, ``propagate`` and

&#x20; ``interval`` arguments.



\* ``with\_default\_connection`` ->  ``with default\_connection``



\* TaskPool.apply\_async:  Keyword arguments ``callbacks`` and ``errbacks``

&#x20; has been renamed to ``callback`` and ``errback`` and take a single scalar

&#x20; value instead of a list.



\* No longer propagates errors occurring during process cleanup (Issue #365)



\* Added ``TaskSetResult.delete()``, which will delete a previously

&#x20; saved taskset result.



\* ``celerybeat`` now syncs every 3 minutes instead of only at

&#x20; shutdown (Issue #382).



\* Monitors now properly handles unknown events, so user-defined events

&#x20; are displayed.



\* Terminating a task on Windows now also terminates all of the tasks child

&#x20; processes (Issue #384).



\* worker: ``-I|--include`` option now always searches the current directory

&#x20; to import the specified modules.



\* Cassandra backend: Now expires results by using TTLs.



\* Functional test suite in ``funtests`` is now actually working properly, and

&#x20; passing tests.



.. \_v230-fixes:



Fixes

\-----



\* ``celeryev`` was trying to create the pidfile twice.



\* celery.contrib.batches: Fixed problem where tasks failed

&#x20; silently (Issue #393).



\* Fixed an issue where logging objects would give "<Unrepresentable",

&#x20; even though the objects were.



\* ``CELERY\_TASK\_ERROR\_WHITE\_LIST`` is now properly initialized

&#x20; in all loaders.



\* ``celeryd\_detach`` now passes through command line configuration.



\* Remote control command ``add\_consumer`` now does nothing if the

&#x20; queue is already being consumed from.



```



\### 代码文件: docs\\history\\changelog-2.4.rst

```rst

.. \_changelog-2.4:



===============================

&#x20;Change history for Celery 2.4

===============================



.. contents::

&#x20;   :local:



.. \_version-2.4.5:



2.4.5

=====

:release-date: 2011-12-02 05:00 p.m. GMT

:release-by: Ask Solem



\* Periodic task interval schedules were accidentally rounded down,

&#x20; resulting in some periodic tasks being executed early.



\* Logging of humanized times in the beat log is now more detailed.



\* New :ref:`brokers` section in the Getting Started part of the Documentation



&#x20;   This replaces the old "Other queues" tutorial, and adds

&#x20;   documentation for MongoDB, Beanstalk and CouchDB.



.. \_version-2.4.4:



2.4.4

=====

:release-date: 2011-11-25 04:00 p.m. GMT

:release-by: Ask Solem



.. \_v244-security-fixes:



Security Fixes

\--------------



\* \[Security: `CELERYSA-0001`\_] Daemons would set effective id's rather than

&#x20; real id's when the :option:`!--uid`/

&#x20; :option:`!--gid` arguments to

&#x20; :program:`celery multi`, :program:`celeryd\_detach`,

&#x20; :program:`celery beat` and :program:`celery events` were used.



&#x20; This means privileges weren't properly dropped, and that it would

&#x20; be possible to regain supervisor privileges later.





.. \_`CELERYSA-0001`:

&#x20;   https://github.com/celery/celery/tree/master/docs/sec/CELERYSA-0001.txt



.. \_v244-fixes:



Fixes

\-----



\* Processes pool: Fixed rare deadlock at shutdown (Issue #523).



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Ionel Maries Christian.



\* Webhook tasks issued the wrong HTTP POST headers (Issue #515).



&#x20;   The \*Content-Type\* header has been changed from

&#x20;   ``application/json`` ⇒  ``application/x-www-form-urlencoded``,

&#x20;   and adds a proper \*Content-Length\* header.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Mitar.



\* Daemonization tutorial: Adds a configuration example using Django and

&#x20; virtualenv together (Issue #505).



&#x20;   Contributed by Juan Ignacio Catalano.



\* generic init-scripts now automatically creates log and pid file

&#x20; directories (Issue #545).



&#x20;   Contributed by Chris Streeter.



.. \_version-2.4.3:



2.4.3

=====

:release-date: 2011-11-22 06:00 p.m. GMT

:release-by: Ask Solem



\* Fixes module import typo in `celeryctl` (Issue #538).



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Chris Streeter.



.. \_version-2.4.2:



2.4.2

=====

:release-date: 2011-11-14 12:00 p.m. GMT

:release-by: Ask Solem



\* Program module no longer uses relative imports so that it's

&#x20; possible to do ``python -m celery.bin.name``.



.. \_version-2.4.1:



2.4.1

=====

:release-date: 2011-11-07 06:00 p.m. GMT

:release-by: Ask Solem



\* ``celeryctl inspect`` commands was missing output.



\* processes pool: Decrease polling interval for less idle CPU usage.



\* processes pool: MaybeEncodingError wasn't wrapped in ExceptionInfo

&#x20; (Issue #524).



\* worker: would silence errors occurring after task consumer started.



\* logging: Fixed a bug where unicode in stdout redirected log messages

&#x20; couldn't be written (Issue #522).



.. \_version-2.4.0:



2.4.0

=====

:release-date: 2011-11-04 04:00 p.m. GMT

:release-by: Ask Solem



.. \_v240-important:



Important Notes

\---------------



\* Now supports Python 3.



\* Fixed deadlock in worker process handling (Issue #496).



&#x20;   A deadlock could occur after spawning new child processes because

&#x20;   the logging library's mutex wasn't properly reset after fork.



&#x20;   The symptoms of this bug affecting would be that the worker simply

&#x20;   stops processing tasks, as none of the workers child processes

&#x20;   are functioning. There was a greater chance of this bug occurring

&#x20;   with ``maxtasksperchild`` or a time-limit enabled.



&#x20;   This is a workaround for http://bugs.python.org/issue6721#msg140215.



&#x20;   Be aware that while this fixes the logging library lock,

&#x20;   there could still be other locks initialized in the parent

&#x20;   process, introduced by custom code.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Harm Verhagen.



\* AMQP Result backend: Now expires results by default.



&#x20;   The default expiration value is now taken from the

&#x20;   :setting:`CELERY\_TASK\_RESULT\_EXPIRES` setting.



&#x20;   The old :setting:`CELERY\_AMQP\_TASK\_RESULT\_EXPIRES` setting has been

&#x20;   deprecated and will be removed in version 4.0.



&#x20;   Note that this means that the result backend requires RabbitMQ 2.1.0 or

&#x20;   higher, and that you have to disable expiration if you're running

&#x20;   with an older version. You can do so by disabling the

&#x20;   :setting:`CELERY\_TASK\_RESULT\_EXPIRES` setting::



&#x20;       CELERY\_TASK\_RESULT\_EXPIRES = None



\* Eventlet: Fixed problem with shutdown (Issue #457).



\* Broker transports can be now be specified using URLs



&#x20;   The broker can now be specified as a URL instead.

&#x20;   This URL must have the format:



&#x20;   .. code-block:: text



&#x20;       transport://user:password@hostname:port/virtual\_host



&#x20;   for example the default broker is written as:



&#x20;   .. code-block:: text



&#x20;       amqp://guest:guest@localhost:5672//



&#x20;   The scheme is required, so that the host is identified

&#x20;   as a URL and not just a host name.

&#x20;   User, password, port and virtual\_host are optional and

&#x20;   defaults to the particular transports default value.



&#x20;   .. note::



&#x20;       Note that the path component (virtual\_host) always starts with a

&#x20;       forward-slash. This is necessary to distinguish between the virtual

&#x20;       host ``''`` (empty) and ``'/'``, which are both acceptable virtual

&#x20;       host names.



&#x20;       A virtual host of ``'/'`` becomes:



&#x20;       .. code-block:: text



&#x20;           amqp://guest:guest@localhost:5672//



&#x20;       and a virtual host of ``''`` (empty) becomes:



&#x20;       .. code-block:: text



&#x20;           amqp://guest:guest@localhost:5672/



&#x20;       So the leading slash in the path component is \*\*always required\*\*.



&#x20;   In addition the :setting:`BROKER\_URL` setting has been added as an alias

&#x20;   to ``BROKER\_HOST``. Any broker setting specified in both the URL and in

&#x20;   the configuration will be ignored, if a setting isn't provided in the URL

&#x20;   then the value from the configuration will be used as default.



&#x20;   Also, programs now support the :option:`--broker <celery --broker>`

&#x20;   option to specify a broker URL on the command-line:



&#x20;   .. code-block:: console



&#x20;       $ celery worker -b redis://localhost



&#x20;       $ celery inspect -b amqp://guest:guest@localhost//e



&#x20;   The environment variable :envvar:`CELERY\_BROKER\_URL` can also be used to

&#x20;   easily override the default broker used.



\* The deprecated :func:`celery.loaders.setup\_loader` function has been removed.



\* The :setting:`CELERY\_TASK\_ERROR\_WHITELIST` setting has been replaced

&#x20; by a more flexible approach (Issue #447).



&#x20;   The error mail sending logic is now available as ``Task.ErrorMail``,

&#x20;   with the implementation (for reference) in :mod:`celery.utils.mail`.



&#x20;   The error mail class can be sub-classed to gain complete control

&#x20;   of when error messages are sent, thus removing the need for a separate

&#x20;   white-list setting.



&#x20;   The :setting:`CELERY\_TASK\_ERROR\_WHITELIST` setting has been deprecated,

&#x20;   and will be removed completely in version 4.0.



\* Additional Deprecations



&#x20;   The following functions has been deprecated and is scheduled for removal in

&#x20;   version 4.0:



&#x20;   =====================================  ===================================

&#x20;   \*\*Old function\*\*                       \*\*Alternative\*\*

&#x20;   =====================================  ===================================

&#x20;   `celery.loaders.current\_loader`        `celery.current\_app.loader`

&#x20;   `celery.loaders.load\_settings`         `celery.current\_app.conf`

&#x20;   `celery.execute.apply`                 `Task.apply`

&#x20;   `celery.execute.apply\_async`           `Task.apply\_async`

&#x20;   `celery.execute.delay\_task`            `celery.execute.send\_task`

&#x20;   =====================================  ===================================



&#x20;   The following settings has been deprecated and is scheduled for removal

&#x20;   in version 4.0:



&#x20;   =====================================  ===================================

&#x20;   \*\*Old setting\*\*                        \*\*Alternative\*\*

&#x20;   =====================================  ===================================

&#x20;   `CELERYD\_LOG\_LEVEL`                    ``celery worker --loglevel=``

&#x20;   `CELERYD\_LOG\_FILE`                     ``celery worker --logfile=``

&#x20;   `CELERYBEAT\_LOG\_LEVEL`                 ``celery beat --loglevel=``

&#x20;   `CELERYBEAT\_LOG\_FILE`                  ``celery beat --logfile=``

&#x20;   `CELERYMON\_LOG\_LEVEL`                  ``celerymon --loglevel=``

&#x20;   `CELERYMON\_LOG\_FILE`                   ``celerymon --logfile=``

&#x20;   =====================================  ===================================



.. \_v240-news:



News

\----



\* No longer depends on :pypi:`pyparsing`.



\* Now depends on Kombu 1.4.3.



\* CELERY\_IMPORTS can now be a scalar value (Issue #485).



&#x20;   It's too easy to forget to add the comma after the sole element of a

&#x20;   tuple, and this is something that often affects newcomers.



&#x20;   The docs should probably use a list in examples, as using a tuple

&#x20;   for this doesn't even make sense. Nonetheless, there are many

&#x20;   tutorials out there using a tuple, and this change should be a help

&#x20;   to new users.



&#x20;   Suggested by :github\_user:`jsaxon-cars`.



\* Fixed a memory leak when using the thread pool (Issue #486).



&#x20;   Contributed by Kornelijus Survila.



\* The ``statedb`` wasn't saved at exit.



&#x20;   This has now been fixed and it should again remember previously

&#x20;   revoked tasks when a ``--statedb`` is enabled.



\* Adds :setting:`EMAIL\_USE\_TLS` to enable secure SMTP connections

&#x20; (Issue #418).



&#x20;   Contributed by Stefan Kjartansson.



\* Now handles missing fields in task messages as documented in the message

&#x20; format documentation.



&#x20;   \* Missing required field throws :exc:`\~@InvalidTaskError`

&#x20;   \* Missing args/kwargs is assumed empty.



&#x20;   Contributed by Chris Chamberlin.



\* Fixed race condition in :mod:`celery.events.state` (``celerymon``/``celeryev``)

&#x20; where task info would be removed while iterating over it (Issue #501).



\* The Cache, Cassandra, MongoDB, Redis and Tyrant backends now respects

&#x20; the :setting:`CELERY\_RESULT\_SERIALIZER` setting (Issue #435).



&#x20;   This means that only the database (Django/SQLAlchemy) backends

&#x20;   currently doesn't support using custom serializers.



&#x20;   Contributed by Steeve Morin



\* Logging calls no longer manually formats messages, but delegates

&#x20; that to the logging system, so tools like Sentry can easier

&#x20; work with the messages (Issue #445).



&#x20;   Contributed by Chris Adams.



\* ``multi`` now supports a ``stop\_verify`` command to wait for

&#x20; processes to shutdown.



\* Cache backend didn't work if the cache key was unicode (Issue #504).



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Neil Chintomby.



\* New setting :setting:`CELERY\_RESULT\_DB\_SHORT\_LIVED\_SESSIONS` added,

&#x20; which if enabled will disable the caching of SQLAlchemy sessions

&#x20; (Issue #449).



&#x20;   Contributed by Leo Dirac.



\* All result backends now implements ``\_\_reduce\_\_`` so that they can

&#x20; be pickled (Issue #441).



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Remy Noel



\* multi didn't work on Windows (Issue #472).



\* New-style ``CELERY\_REDIS\_\*`` settings now takes precedence over

&#x20; the old ``REDIS\_\*`` configuration keys (Issue #508).



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Joshua Ginsberg



\* Generic beat init-script no longer sets `bash -e` (Issue #510).



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Roger Hu.



\* Documented that Chords don't work well with :command:`redis-server` versions

&#x20; before 2.2.



&#x20;   Contributed by Dan McGee.



\* The :setting:`CELERYBEAT\_MAX\_LOOP\_INTERVAL` setting wasn't respected.



\* ``inspect.registered\_tasks`` renamed to ``inspect.registered`` for naming

&#x20; consistency.



&#x20;   The previous name is still available as an alias.



&#x20;   Contributed by Mher Movsisyan



\* Worker logged the string representation of args and kwargs

&#x20; without safe guards (Issue #480).



\* RHEL init-script: Changed worker start-up priority.



&#x20;   The default start / stop priorities for MySQL on RHEL are:



&#x20;   .. code-block:: console



&#x20;       # chkconfig: - 64 36



&#x20;   Therefore, if Celery is using a database as a broker / message store, it

&#x20;   should be started after the database is up and running, otherwise errors

&#x20;   will ensue. This commit changes the priority in the init-script to:



&#x20;   .. code-block:: console



&#x20;       # chkconfig: - 85 15



&#x20;   which are the default recommended settings for 3-rd party applications

&#x20;   and assure that Celery will be started after the database service \& shut

&#x20;   down before it terminates.



&#x20;   Contributed by Yury V. Zaytsev.



\* KeyValueStoreBackend.get\_many didn't respect the ``timeout`` argument

&#x20; (Issue #512).



\* beat/events's ``--workdir`` option didn't :manpage:`chdir(2)` before after

&#x20; configuration was attempted (Issue #506).



\* After deprecating 2.4 support we can now name modules correctly, since we

&#x20; can take use of absolute imports.



&#x20;   Therefore the following internal modules have been renamed:



&#x20;       ``celery.concurrency.evlet``    -> ``celery.concurrency.eventlet``

&#x20;       ``celery.concurrency.evg``      -> ``celery.concurrency.gevent``



\* :file:`AUTHORS` file is now sorted alphabetically.



&#x20;   Also, as you may have noticed the contributors of new features/fixes are

&#x20;   now mentioned in the Changelog.



```



\### 代码文件: docs\\history\\changelog-2.5.rst

```rst

.. \_changelog-2.5:



===============================

&#x20;Change history for Celery 2.5

===============================



This document contains change notes for bugfix releases in the 2.5.x series,

please see :ref:`whatsnew-2.5` for an overview of what's

new in Celery 2.5.



If you're looking for versions prior to 2.5 you should visit our

:ref:`history` of releases.



.. contents::

&#x20;   :local:



.. \_version-2.5.5:



2.5.5

=====

:release-date: 2012-06-06 04:00 p.m. BST

:release-by: Ask Solem



This is a dummy release performed for the following goals:



\- Protect against force upgrading to Kombu 2.2.0

\- Version parity with :pypi:`django-celery`



.. \_version-2.5.3:



2.5.3

=====

:release-date: 2012-04-16 07:00 p.m. BST

:release-by: Ask Solem



\* A bug causes messages to be sent with UTC time-stamps even though

&#x20; :setting:`CELERY\_ENABLE\_UTC` wasn't enabled (Issue #636).



\* ``celerybeat``: No longer crashes if an entry's args is set to None

&#x20; (Issue #657).



\* Auto-reload didn't work if a module's ``\_\_file\_\_`` attribute

&#x20; was set to the modules ``.pyc`` file.  (Issue #647).



\* Fixes early 2.5 compatibility where ``\_\_package\_\_`` doesn't exist

&#x20; (Issue #638).



.. \_version-2.5.2:



2.5.2

=====

:release-date: 2012-04-13 04:30 p.m. GMT

:release-by: Ask Solem



.. \_v252-news:



News

\----



\- Now depends on Kombu 2.1.5.



\- Django documentation has been moved to the main Celery docs.



&#x20;   See :ref:`django`.



\- New :signal:`celeryd\_init` signal can be used to configure workers

&#x20; by hostname.



\- Signal.connect can now be used as a decorator.



&#x20;   Example:



&#x20;   .. code-block:: python



&#x20;       from celery.signals import task\_sent



&#x20;       @task\_sent.connect

&#x20;       def on\_task\_sent(\*\*kwargs):

&#x20;           print('sent task: %r' % (kwargs,))



\- Invalid task messages are now rejected instead of acked.



&#x20;   This means that they will be moved to the dead-letter queue

&#x20;   introduced in the latest RabbitMQ version (but must be enabled

&#x20;   manually, consult the RabbitMQ documentation).



\- Internal logging calls has been cleaned up to work

&#x20; better with tools like Sentry.



&#x20;   Contributed by David Cramer.



\- New method ``subtask.clone()`` can be used to clone an existing

&#x20; subtask with augmented arguments/options.



&#x20;   Example:



&#x20;   .. code-block:: pycon



&#x20;       >>> s = add.subtask((5,))

&#x20;       >>> new = s.clone(args=(10,), countdown=5})

&#x20;       >>> new.args

&#x20;       (10, 5)



&#x20;       >>> new.options

&#x20;       {'countdown': 5}



\- Chord callbacks are now triggered in eager mode.



.. \_v252-fixes:



Fixes

\-----



\- Programs now verifies that the pidfile is actually written correctly

&#x20; (Issue #641).



&#x20;   Hopefully this will crash the worker immediately if the system

&#x20;   is out of space to store the complete pidfile.



&#x20;   In addition, we now verify that existing pidfiles contain

&#x20;   a new line so that a partially written pidfile is detected as broken,

&#x20;   as before doing:



&#x20;   .. code-block:: console



&#x20;       $ echo -n "1" > celeryd.pid



&#x20;   would cause the worker to think that an existing instance was already

&#x20;   running (init has pid 1 after all).



\- Fixed 2.5 compatibility issue with use of print\_exception.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Martin Melin.



\- Fixed 2.5 compatibility issue with imports.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Iurii Kriachko.



\- All programs now fix up ``\_\_package\_\_`` when called as main.



&#x20;   This fixes compatibility with Python 2.5.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Martin Melin.



\- \[celery control|inspect] can now be configured on the command-line.



&#x20;   Like with the worker it is now possible to configure Celery settings

&#x20;   on the command-line for celery control|inspect



&#x20;   .. code-block:: console



&#x20;       $ celery inspect -- broker.pool\_limit=30



\- Version dependency for :pypi:`python-dateutil` fixed to be strict.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Thomas Meson.



\- ``Task.\_\_call\_\_`` is now optimized away in the task tracer

&#x20; rather than when the task class is created.



&#x20;   This fixes a bug where a custom \_\_call\_\_  may mysteriously disappear.



\- Auto-reload's ``inotify`` support has been improved.



&#x20;   Contributed by Mher Movsisyan.



\- The Django broker documentation has been improved.



\- Removed confusing warning at top of routing user guide.



.. \_version-2.5.1:



2.5.1

=====

:release-date: 2012-03-01 01:00 p.m. GMT

:release-by: Ask Solem



.. \_v251-fixes:



Fixes

\-----



\* Eventlet/Gevent: A small typo caused the worker to hang when eventlet/gevent

&#x20; was used, this was because the environment wasn't monkey patched

&#x20; early enough.



\* Eventlet/Gevent: Another small typo caused the mediator to be started

&#x20; with eventlet/gevent, which would make the worker sometimes hang at shutdown.



\* :mod:`multiprocessing`: Fixed an error occurring if the pool was stopped

&#x20; before it was properly started.



\* Proxy objects now redirects ``\_\_doc\_\_`` and ``\_\_name\_\_`` so ``help(obj)``

&#x20; works.



\* Internal timer (timer2) now logs exceptions instead of swallowing them

&#x20; (Issue #626).



\* celery shell: can now be started with

&#x20; :option:`--eventlet <celery shell --eventlet>` or

&#x20; :option:`--gevent <celery shell --gevent>` options to apply their

&#x20; monkey patches.



.. \_version-2.5.0:



2.5.0

=====

:release-date: 2012-02-24 04:00 p.m. GMT

:release-by: Ask Solem



See :ref:`whatsnew-2.5`.



Since the changelog has gained considerable size, we decided to

do things differently this time: by having separate "what's new"

documents for major version changes.



Bugfix releases will still be found in the changelog.





```



\### 代码文件: docs\\history\\changelog-3.0.rst

```rst

.. \_changelog-3.0:



===============================

&#x20;Change history for Celery 3.0

===============================



.. contents::

&#x20;   :local:



If you're looking for versions prior to 3.0.x you should go to :ref:`history`.



.. \_version-3.0.24:



3.0.24

======

:release-date: 2013-10-11 04:40 p.m. BST

:release-by: Ask Solem



\- Now depends on :ref:`Kombu 2.5.15 <kombu:version-2.5.15>`.



\- Now depends on :pypi:`billiard` version 2.7.3.34.



\- AMQP Result backend:  No longer caches queue declarations.



&#x20;   The queues created by the AMQP result backend are always unique,

&#x20;   so caching the declarations caused a slow memory leak.



\- Worker: Fixed crash when hostname contained Unicode characters.



&#x20;   Contributed by Daodao.



\- The worker would no longer start if the `-P solo` pool was selected

&#x20; (Issue #1548).



\- Redis/Cache result backends wouldn't complete chords

&#x20; if any of the tasks were retried (Issue #1401).



\- Task decorator is no longer lazy if app is finalized.



\- AsyncResult: Fixed bug with ``copy(AsyncResult)`` when no

&#x20; ``current\_app`` available.



\- ResultSet: Now properly propagates app when passed string id's.



\- Loader now ignores :envvar:`CELERY\_CONFIG\_MODULE` if value is empty string.



\- Fixed race condition in Proxy object where it tried to

&#x20; delete an attribute twice, resulting in :exc:`AttributeError`.



\- Task methods now works with the :setting:`CELERY\_ALWAYS\_EAGER` setting

&#x20; (Issue #1478).



\- :class:`\~kombu.common.Broadcast` queues were accidentally declared

&#x20; when publishing tasks (Issue #1540).



\- New :envvar:`C\_FAKEFORK` environment variable can be used to

&#x20; debug the init-scripts.



&#x20;   Setting this will skip the daemonization step so that errors

&#x20;   printed to stderr after standard outs are closed can be seen:



&#x20;   .. code-block:: console



&#x20;       $ C\_FAKEFORK /etc/init.d/celeryd start



&#x20;   This works with the `celery multi` command in general.



\- ``get\_pickleable\_etype`` didn't always return a value (Issue #1556).

\- Fixed bug where ``app.GroupResult.restore`` would fall back to the default

&#x20; app.



\- Fixed rare bug where built-in tasks would use the current\_app.



\- :func:`\~celery.platforms.maybe\_fileno` now handles :exc:`ValueError`.



.. \_version-3.0.23:



3.0.23

======

:release-date: 2013-09-02 01:00 p.m. BST

:release-by: Ask Solem



\- Now depends on :ref:`Kombu 2.5.14 <kombu:version-2.5.14>`.



\- ``send\_task`` didn't honor ``link`` and ``link\_error`` arguments.



&#x20;   This had the side effect of chains not calling unregistered tasks,

&#x20;   silently discarding them.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Taylor Nelson.



\- :mod:`celery.state`: Optimized precedence lookup.



&#x20;   Contributed by Matt Robenolt.



\- POSIX: Daemonization didn't redirect ``sys.stdin`` to ``/dev/null``.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Alexander Smirnov.



\- Canvas: group bug caused fallback to default app when ``.apply\_async`` used

&#x20; (Issue #1516)



\- Canvas: generator arguments wasn't always pickleable.



.. \_version-3.0.22:



3.0.22

======

:release-date: 2013-08-16 04:30 p.m. BST

:release-by: Ask Solem



\- Now depends on :ref:`Kombu 2.5.13 <kombu:version-2.5.13>`.



\- Now depends on :pypi:`billiard` 2.7.3.32



\- Fixed bug with monthly and yearly Crontabs (Issue #1465).



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Guillaume Gauvrit.



\- Fixed memory leak caused by time limits (Issue #1129, Issue #1427)



\- Worker will now sleep if being restarted more than 5 times

&#x20; in one second to avoid spamming with ``worker-online`` events.



\- Includes documentation fixes



&#x20;   Contributed by: Ken Fromm, Andreas Savvides, Alex Kiriukha,

&#x20;   Michael Fladischer.



.. \_version-3.0.21:



3.0.21

======

:release-date: 2013-07-05 04:30 p.m. BST

:release-by: Ask Solem



\- Now depends on :pypi:`billiard` 2.7.3.31.



&#x20;   This version fixed a bug when running without the billiard C extension.



\- 3.0.20 broke eventlet/gevent support (worker not starting).



\- Fixed memory leak problem when MongoDB result backend was used with the

&#x20; gevent pool.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Ross Lawley.



.. \_version-3.0.20:



3.0.20

======

:release-date: 2013-06-28 04:00 p.m. BST

:release-by: Ask Solem



\- Contains workaround for deadlock problems.



&#x20;   A better solution will be part of Celery 3.1.



\- Now depends on :ref:`Kombu 2.5.12 <kombu:version-2.5.12>`.



\- Now depends on :pypi:`billiard` 2.7.3.30.



\- :option:`--loader <celery --loader>` argument no longer supported

&#x20; importing loaders from the current directory.



\- \[Worker] Fixed memory leak when restarting after connection lost

&#x20; (Issue #1325).



\- \[Worker] Fixed UnicodeDecodeError at start-up (Issue #1373).



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Jessica Tallon.



\- \[Worker] Now properly rewrites unpickleable exceptions again.



\- Fixed possible race condition when evicting items from the revoked task set.



\- \[generic-init.d] Fixed compatibility with Ubuntu's minimal Dash

&#x20; shell (Issue #1387).



&#x20;   Fix contributed by :github\_user:`monkut`.



\- ``Task.apply``/``ALWAYS\_EAGER`` now also executes callbacks and errbacks

&#x20; (Issue #1336).



\- \[Worker] The :signal:`worker-shutdown` signal was no longer being dispatched

&#x20; (Issue #1339)j



\- \[Python 3] Fixed problem with threading.Event.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Xavier Ordoquy.



\- \[Python 3] Now handles ``io.UnsupportedOperation`` that may be raised

&#x20; by ``file.fileno()`` in Python 3.



\- \[Python 3] Fixed problem with ``qualname``.



\- \[events.State] Now ignores unknown event-groups.



\- \[MongoDB backend] No longer uses deprecated ``safe`` parameter.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by :github\_user:`rfkrocktk`.



\- The eventlet pool now imports on Windows.



\- \[Canvas] Fixed regression where immutable chord members may receive

&#x20; arguments (Issue #1340).



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Peter Brook.



\- \[Canvas] chain now accepts generator argument again (Issue #1319).



\- ``celery.migrate`` command now consumes from all queues if no queues

&#x20; specified.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by John Watson.



.. \_version-3.0.19:



3.0.19

======

:release-date: 2013-04-17 04:30:00 p.m. BST

:release-by: Ask Solem



\- Now depends on :pypi:`billiard` 2.7.3.28



\- A Python 3 related fix managed to disable the deadlock fix

&#x20; announced in 3.0.18.



&#x20;   Tests have been added to make sure this doesn't happen again.



\- Task retry policy:  Default max\_retries is now 3.



&#x20;   This ensures clients won't be hanging while the broker is down.



&#x20;   .. note::



&#x20;       You can set a longer retry for the worker by

&#x20;       using the :signal:`celeryd\_after\_setup` signal:



&#x20;       .. code-block:: python



&#x20;               from celery.signals import celeryd\_after\_setup



&#x20;               @celeryd\_after\_setup.connect

&#x20;               def configure\_worker(instance, conf, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;                   conf.CELERY\_TASK\_PUBLISH\_RETRY\_POLICY = {

&#x20;                       'max\_retries': 100,

&#x20;                       'interval\_start': 0,

&#x20;                       'interval\_max': 1,

&#x20;                       'interval\_step': 0.2,

&#x20;                   }



\- Worker: Will now properly display message body in error messages

&#x20; even if the body is a buffer instance.



\- 3.0.18 broke the MongoDB result backend (Issue #1303).



.. \_version-3.0.18:



3.0.18

======

:release-date: 2013-04-12 05:00:00 p.m. BST

:release-by: Ask Solem



\- Now depends on :pypi:`kombu` 2.5.10.



&#x20;   See the :ref:`kombu changelog <kombu:version-2.5.10>`.



\- Now depends on :pypi:`billiard` 2.7.3.27.



\- Can now specify a white-list of accepted serializers using

&#x20; the new :setting:`CELERY\_ACCEPT\_CONTENT` setting.



&#x20;   This means that you can force the worker to discard messages

&#x20;   serialized with pickle and other untrusted serializers.

&#x20;   For example to only allow JSON serialized messages use::



&#x20;       CELERY\_ACCEPT\_CONTENT = \['json']



&#x20;   you can also specify MIME types in the white-list::



&#x20;       CELERY\_ACCEPT\_CONTENT = \['application/json']



\- Fixed deadlock in multiprocessing's pool caused by the

&#x20; semaphore not being released when terminated by signal.



\- Processes Pool: It's now possible to debug pool processes using GDB.



\- ``celery report`` now censors possibly secret settings, like passwords

&#x20; and secret tokens.



&#x20;   You should still check the output before pasting anything

&#x20;   on the internet.



\- Connection URLs now ignore multiple '+' tokens.



\- Worker/``statedb``: Now uses pickle protocol 2 (Python 2.5+)



\- Fixed Python 3 compatibility issues.



\- Worker:  A warning is now given if a worker is started with the

&#x20; same node name as an existing worker.



\- Worker: Fixed a deadlock that could occur while revoking tasks (Issue #1297).



\- Worker: The :sig:`HUP` handler now closes all open file descriptors

&#x20; before restarting to ensure file descriptors doesn't leak (Issue #1270).



\- Worker: Optimized storing/loading the revoked tasks list (Issue #1289).



&#x20;   After this change the :option:`celery worker --statedb` file will

&#x20;   take up more disk space, but loading from and storing the revoked

&#x20;   tasks will be considerably faster (what before took 5 minutes will

&#x20;   now take less than a second).



\- Celery will now suggest alternatives if there's a typo in the

&#x20; broker transport name (e.g., ``ampq`` -> ``amqp``).



\- Worker: The auto-reloader would cause a crash if a monitored file

&#x20; was unlinked.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Agris Ameriks.



\- Fixed AsyncResult pickling error.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Thomas Minor.



\- Fixed handling of Unicode in logging output when using log colors

&#x20; (Issue #427).



\- :class:`\~celery.app.utils.ConfigurationView` is now a ``MutableMapping``.



&#x20;   Contributed by Aaron Harnly.



\- Fixed memory leak in LRU cache implementation.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Romuald Brunet.



\- ``celery.contrib.rdb``: Now works when sockets are in non-blocking mode.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Theo Spears.



\- The `inspect reserved` remote control command included active (started) tasks

&#x20; with the reserved tasks (Issue #1030).



\- The :signal:`task\_failure` signal received a modified traceback object

&#x20; meant for pickling purposes, this has been fixed so that it now

&#x20; receives the real traceback instead.



\- The ``@task`` decorator silently ignored positional arguments,

&#x20; it now raises the expected :exc:`TypeError` instead (Issue #1125).



\- The worker will now properly handle messages with invalid

&#x20; ETA/expires fields (Issue #1232).



\- The ``pool\_restart`` remote control command now reports

&#x20; an error if the :setting:`CELERYD\_POOL\_RESTARTS` setting isn't set.



\- :meth:`@add\_defaults`` can now be used with non-dict objects.



\- Fixed compatibility problems in the Proxy class (Issue #1087).



&#x20;   The class attributes ``\_\_module\_\_``, ``\_\_name\_\_`` and ``\_\_doc\_\_``

&#x20;   are now meaningful string objects.



&#x20;   Thanks to Marius Gedminas.



\- MongoDB Backend: The :setting:`MONGODB\_BACKEND\_SETTINGS` setting

&#x20; now accepts a ``option`` key that lets you forward arbitrary kwargs

&#x20; to the underlying ``pymongo.Connection`` object (Issue #1015).



\- Beat: The daily backend cleanup task is no longer enabled

&#x20; for result backends that support automatic result expiration (Issue #1031).



\- Canvas list operations now takes application instance from the first

&#x20; task in the list, instead of depending on the ``current\_app`` (Issue #1249).



\- Worker: Message decoding error log message now includes traceback

&#x20; information.



\- Worker: The start-up banner now includes system platform.



\- ``celery inspect|status|control`` now gives an error if used

&#x20; with a SQL based broker transport.



.. \_version-3.0.17:



3.0.17

======

:release-date: 2013-03-22 04:00:00 p.m. UTC

:release-by: Ask Solem



\- Now depends on kombu 2.5.8



\- Now depends on billiard 2.7.3.23



\- RabbitMQ/Redis: thread-less and lock-free rate-limit implementation.



&#x20;   This means that rate limits pose minimal overhead when used with

&#x20;   RabbitMQ/Redis or future transports using the event-loop,

&#x20;   and that the rate-limit implementation is now thread-less and lock-free.



&#x20;   The thread-based transports will still use the old implementation for

&#x20;   now, but the plan is to use the timer also for other

&#x20;   broker transports in Celery 3.1.



\- Rate limits now works with eventlet/gevent if using RabbitMQ/Redis as the

&#x20; broker.



\- A regression caused ``task.retry`` to ignore additional keyword arguments.



&#x20;   Extra keyword arguments are now used as execution options again.

&#x20;   Fix contributed by Simon Engledew.



\- Windows: Fixed problem with the worker trying to pickle the Django settings

&#x20; module at worker start-up.



\- generic-init.d:  No longer double quotes ``$CELERYD\_CHDIR`` (Issue #1235).



\- generic-init.d: Removes bash-specific syntax.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Pär Wieslander.



\- Cassandra Result Backend: Now handles the

&#x20; :exc:`\~pycassa.AllServersUnavailable` error (Issue #1010).



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Jared Biel.



\- Result: Now properly forwards apps to GroupResults when deserializing

&#x20; (Issue #1249).



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Charles-Axel Dein.



\- ``GroupResult.revoke`` now supports the ``terminate`` and ``signal``

&#x20; keyword arguments.



\- Worker: Multiprocessing pool workers now import task modules/configuration

&#x20; before setting up the logging system so that logging signals can be

&#x20; connected before they're dispatched.



\- chord:  The ``AsyncResult`` instance returned now has its ``parent``

&#x20; attribute set to the header ``GroupResult``.



&#x20;   This is consistent with how ``chain`` works.



.. \_version-3.0.16:



3.0.16

======

:release-date: 2013-03-07 04:00:00 p.m. UTC

:release-by: Ask Solem



\- Happy International Women's Day!



&#x20;   We have a long way to go, so this is a chance for you to get involved in one

&#x20;   of the organizations working for making our communities more

&#x20;   diverse.



&#x20;    - PyLadies — http://pyladies.com

&#x20;    - Girls Who Code — http://www.girlswhocode.com

&#x20;    - Women Who Code — http://www.meetup.com/Women-Who-Code-SF/



\- Now depends on :pypi:`kombu` version 2.5.7



\- Now depends on :pypi:`billiard` version 2.7.3.22



\- AMQP heartbeats are now disabled by default.



&#x20;   Some users experiences issues with heartbeats enabled,

&#x20;   and it's not strictly necessary to use them.



&#x20;   If you're experiencing problems detecting connection failures,

&#x20;   you can re-enable heartbeats by configuring the :setting:`BROKER\_HEARTBEAT`

&#x20;   setting.



\- Worker: Now propagates connection errors occurring in multiprocessing

&#x20; callbacks, so that the connection can be reset (Issue #1226).



\- Worker: Now propagates connection errors occurring in timer callbacks,

&#x20; so that the connection can be reset.



\- The modules in :setting:`CELERY\_IMPORTS` and :setting:`CELERY\_INCLUDE`

&#x20; are now imported in the original order (Issue #1161).



&#x20;   The modules in :setting:`CELERY\_IMPORTS` will be imported first,

&#x20;   then continued by :setting:`CELERY\_INCLUDE`.



&#x20;   Thanks to Joey Wilhelm.



\- New bash completion for ``celery`` available in the git repository:



&#x20;   https://github.com/celery/celery/tree/3.0/extra/bash-completion



&#x20;   You can source this file or put it in ``bash\_completion.d`` to

&#x20;   get auto-completion for the ``celery`` command-line utility.



\- The node name of a worker can now include unicode characters (Issue #1186).



\- The repr of a ``crontab`` object now displays correctly (Issue #972).



\- ``events.State`` no longer modifies the original event dictionary.



\- No longer uses ``Logger.warn`` deprecated in Python 3.



\- Cache Backend: Now works with chords again (Issue #1094).



\- Chord unlock now handles errors occurring while calling the callback.



\- Generic worker init.d script: Status check is now performed by

&#x20; querying the pid of the instance instead of sending messages.



&#x20;   Contributed by Milen Pavlov.



\- Improved init-scripts for CentOS.



&#x20;   - Updated to support Celery 3.x conventions.

&#x20;   - Now uses CentOS built-in ``status`` and ``killproc``

&#x20;   - Support for multi-node / multi-pid worker services.

&#x20;   - Standard color-coded CentOS service-init output.

&#x20;   - A test suite.



&#x20;   Contributed by Milen Pavlov.



\- ``ResultSet.join`` now always works with empty result set (Issue #1219).



\- A ``group`` consisting of a single task is now supported (Issue #1219).



\- Now supports the ``pycallgraph`` program (Issue #1051).



\- Fixed Jython compatibility problems.



\- Django tutorial: Now mentions that the example app must be added to

&#x20; ``INSTALLED\_APPS`` (Issue #1192).



.. \_version-3.0.15:



3.0.15

======

:release-date: 2013-02-11 04:30:00 p.m. UTC

:release-by: Ask Solem



\- Now depends on billiard 2.7.3.21 which fixed a syntax error crash.



\- Fixed bug with :setting:`CELERY\_SEND\_TASK\_SENT\_EVENT`.



.. \_version-3.0.14:



3.0.14

======

:release-date: 2013-02-08 05:00:00 p.m. UTC

:release-by: Ask Solem



\- Now depends on Kombu 2.5.6



\- Now depends on billiard 2.7.3.20



\- ``execv`` is now disabled by default.



&#x20;   It was causing too many problems for users, you can still enable

&#x20;   it using the `CELERYD\_FORCE\_EXECV` setting.



&#x20;   execv was only enabled when transports other than AMQP/Redis was used,

&#x20;   and it's there to prevent deadlocks caused by mutexes not being released

&#x20;   before the process forks. Unfortunately it also changes the environment

&#x20;   introducing many corner case bugs that're hard to fix without adding

&#x20;   horrible hacks. Deadlock issues are reported far less often than the

&#x20;   bugs that execv are causing, so we now disable it by default.



&#x20;   Work is in motion to create non-blocking versions of these transports

&#x20;   so that execv isn't necessary (which is the situation with the amqp

&#x20;   and redis broker transports)



\- Chord exception behavior defined (Issue #1172).



&#x20;   From Celery 3.1 the chord callback will change state to FAILURE

&#x20;   when a task part of a chord raises an exception.



&#x20;   It was never documented what happens in this case,

&#x20;   and the actual behavior was very unsatisfactory, indeed

&#x20;   it will just forward the exception value to the chord callback.



&#x20;   For backward compatibility reasons we don't change to the new

&#x20;   behavior in a bugfix release, even if the current behavior was

&#x20;   never documented. Instead you can enable the

&#x20;   :setting:`CELERY\_CHORD\_PROPAGATES` setting to get the new behavior

&#x20;   that'll be default from Celery 3.1.



&#x20;   See more at :ref:`chord-errors`.



\- worker: Fixes bug with ignored and retried tasks.



&#x20;   The ``on\_chord\_part\_return`` and ``Task.after\_return`` callbacks,

&#x20;   nor the ``task\_postrun`` signal should be called when the task was

&#x20;   retried/ignored.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Vlad.



\- ``GroupResult.join\_native`` now respects the ``propagate`` argument.



\- ``subtask.id`` added as an alias to ``subtask\['options'].id``



&#x20;   .. code-block:: pycon



&#x20;       >>> s = add.s(2, 2)

&#x20;       >>> s.id = 'my-id'

&#x20;       >>> s\['options']

&#x20;       {'task\_id': 'my-id'}



&#x20;       >>> s.id

&#x20;       'my-id'



\- worker: Fixed error `Could not start worker processes` occurring

&#x20; when restarting after connection failure (Issue #1118).



\- Adds new signal :signal:`task-retried` (Issue #1169).



\- `celery events --dumper` now handles connection loss.



\- Will now retry sending the task-sent event in case of connection failure.



\- amqp backend:  Now uses ``Message.requeue`` instead of republishing

&#x20; the message after poll.



\- New :setting:`BROKER\_HEARTBEAT\_CHECKRATE` setting introduced to modify the

&#x20; rate at which broker connection heartbeats are monitored.



&#x20;   The default value was also changed from 3.0 to 2.0.



\- :class:`celery.events.state.State` is now pickleable.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Mher Movsisyan.



\- :class:`celery.utils.functional.LRUCache` is now pickleable.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Mher Movsisyan.



\- The stats broadcast command now includes the workers pid.



&#x20;   Contributed by Mher Movsisyan.
```
