```rst
\- New ``conf`` remote control command to get a workers current configuration.



&#x20;   Contributed by Mher Movsisyan.



\- Adds the ability to modify the chord unlock task's countdown

&#x20; argument (Issue #1146).



&#x20;   Contributed by Jun Sakai



\- beat: The scheduler now uses the `now()`` method of the schedule,

&#x20; so that schedules can provide a custom way to get the current date and time.



&#x20;   Contributed by Raphaël Slinckx



\- Fixed pickling of configuration modules on Windows or when execv is used

&#x20; (Issue #1126).



\- Multiprocessing logger is now configured with loglevel ``ERROR``

&#x20; by default.



&#x20;   Since 3.0 the multiprocessing loggers were disabled by default

&#x20;   (only configured when the :envvar:`MP\_LOG` environment variable was set).



.. \_version-3.0.13:



3.0.13

======

:release-date: 2013-01-07 04:00:00 p.m. UTC

:release-by: Ask Solem



\- Now depends on Kombu 2.5



&#x20;   - :pypi:`amqp` has replaced :pypi:`amqplib` as the default transport,

&#x20;     gaining support for AMQP 0.9, and the RabbitMQ extensions,

&#x20;     including Consumer Cancel Notifications and heartbeats.



&#x20;   - support for multiple connection URLs for failover.



&#x20;   - Read more in the :ref:`Kombu 2.5 changelog <kombu:version-2.5.0>`.



\- Now depends on billiard 2.7.3.19



\- Fixed a deadlock issue that could occur when the producer pool

&#x20; inherited the connection pool instance of the parent process.



\- The :option:`--loader <celery --loader>` option now works again (Issue #1066).



\- :program:`celery` umbrella command: All sub-commands now supports

&#x20; the :option:`--workdir <celery --workdir>` option (Issue #1063).



\- Groups included in chains now give GroupResults (Issue #1057)



&#x20;   Previously it would incorrectly add a regular result instead of a group

&#x20;   result, but now this works:



&#x20;   .. code-block:: pycon



&#x20;       >>> # \[4 + 4, 4 + 8, 16 + 8]

&#x20;       >>> res = (add.s(2, 2) | group(add.s(4), add.s(8), add.s(16)))()

&#x20;       >>> res

&#x20;       <GroupResult: a0acf905-c704-499e-b03a-8d445e6398f7 \[

&#x20;           4346501c-cb99-4ad8-8577-12256c7a22b1,

&#x20;           b12ead10-a622-4d44-86e9-3193a778f345,

&#x20;           26c7a420-11f3-4b33-8fac-66cd3b62abfd]>



\- Chains can now chain other chains and use partial arguments (Issue #1057).



&#x20;   Example:



&#x20;   .. code-block:: pycon



&#x20;       >>> c1 = (add.s(2) | add.s(4))

&#x20;       >>> c2 = (add.s(8) | add.s(16))



&#x20;       >>> c3 = (c1 | c2)



&#x20;       >>> # 8 + 2 + 4 + 8 + 16

&#x20;       >>> assert c3(8).get() == 38



\- Subtasks can now be used with unregistered tasks.



&#x20;   You can specify subtasks even if you just have the name::



&#x20;       >>> s = subtask(task\_name, args=(), kwargs=())

&#x20;       >>> s.delay()



\- The :program:`celery shell` command now always adds the current

&#x20; directory to the module path.



\- The worker will now properly handle the :exc:`pytz.AmbiguousTimeError`

&#x20; exception raised when an ETA/countdown is prepared while being in DST

&#x20; transition (Issue #1061).



\- force\_execv: Now makes sure that task symbols in the original

&#x20; task modules will always use the correct app instance (Issue #1072).



\- AMQP Backend: Now republishes result messages that have been polled

&#x20; (using ``result.ready()`` and friends, ``result.get()`` won't do this

&#x20; in this version).



\- Crontab schedule values can now "wrap around"



&#x20;   This means that values like ``11-1`` translates to ``\[11, 12, 1]``.



&#x20;   Contributed by Loren Abrams.



\- ``multi stopwait`` command now shows the pid of processes.



&#x20;   Contributed by Loren Abrams.



\- Handling of ETA/countdown fixed when the :setting:`CELERY\_ENABLE\_UTC`

&#x20;  setting is disabled (Issue #1065).



\- A number of unneeded properties were included in messages,

&#x20; caused by accidentally passing ``Queue.as\_dict`` as message properties.



\- Rate limit values can now be float



&#x20;   This also extends the string format so that values like ``"0.5/s"`` works.



&#x20;   Contributed by Christoph Krybus



\- Fixed a typo in the broadcast routing documentation (Issue #1026).



\- Rewrote confusing section about idempotence in the task user guide.



\- Fixed typo in the daemonization tutorial (Issue #1055).



\- Fixed several typos in the documentation.



&#x20;   Contributed by Marius Gedminas.



\- Batches: Now works when using the eventlet pool.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Thomas Grainger.



\- Batches: Added example sending results to ``celery.contrib.batches``.



&#x20;   Contributed by Thomas Grainger.



\- MongoDB backend: Connection ``max\_pool\_size`` can now be set in

&#x20; :setting:`CELERY\_MONGODB\_BACKEND\_SETTINGS`.



&#x20;   Contributed by Craig Younkins.



\- Fixed problem when using earlier versions of :pypi:`pytz`.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Vlad.



\- Docs updated to include the default value for the

&#x20; :setting:`CELERY\_TASK\_RESULT\_EXPIRES` setting.



\- Improvements to the :pypi:`django-celery` tutorial.



&#x20;   Contributed by Locker537.



\- The ``add\_consumer`` control command didn't properly persist

&#x20; the addition of new queues so that they survived connection failure

&#x20; (Issue #1079).





3.0.12

======

:release-date: 2012-11-06 02:00 p.m. UTC

:release-by: Ask Solem



\- Now depends on kombu 2.4.8



&#x20;   - \[Redis] New and improved fair queue cycle algorithm (Kevin McCarthy).

&#x20;   - \[Redis] Now uses a Redis-based mutex when restoring messages.

&#x20;   - \[Redis] Number of messages that can be restored in one interval is no

&#x20;             longer limited (but can be set using the

&#x20;             ``unacked\_restore\_limit``

&#x20;             :setting:`transport option <BROKER\_TRANSPORT\_OPTIONS>`).

&#x20;   - Heartbeat value can be specified in broker URLs (Mher Movsisyan).

&#x20;   - Fixed problem with msgpack on Python 3 (Jasper Bryant-Greene).



\- Now depends on billiard 2.7.3.18



\- Celery can now be used with static analysis tools like PyDev/PyCharm/pylint

&#x20; etc.



\- Development documentation has moved to Read The Docs.



&#x20;   The new URL is: https://docs.celeryq.dev/en/master



\- New :setting:`CELERY\_QUEUE\_HA\_POLICY` setting used to set the default

&#x20; HA policy for queues when using RabbitMQ.



\- New method ``Task.subtask\_from\_request`` returns a subtask using the current

&#x20; request.



\- Results get\_many method didn't respect timeout argument.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Remigiusz Modrzejewski



\- generic\_init.d scripts now support setting :envvar:`CELERY\_CREATE\_DIRS` to

&#x20; always create log and pid directories (Issue #1045).



&#x20;   This can be set in your :file:`/etc/default/celeryd`.



\- Fixed strange kombu import problem on Python 3.2 (Issue #1034).



\- Worker: ETA scheduler now uses millisecond precision (Issue #1040).



\- The :option:`--config <celery --config>` argument to programs is

&#x20; now supported by all loaders.



\- The :setting:`CASSANDRA\_OPTIONS` setting has now been documented.



&#x20;   Contributed by Jared Biel.



\- Task methods (:mod:`celery.contrib.methods`) cannot be used with the old

&#x20; task base class, the task decorator in that module now inherits from the new.



\- An optimization was too eager and caused some logging messages to never emit.



\- ``celery.contrib.batches`` now works again.



\- Fixed missing white-space in ``bdist\_rpm`` requirements (Issue #1046).



\- Event state's ``tasks\_by\_name`` applied limit before filtering by name.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Alexander A. Sosnovskiy.



.. \_version-3.0.11:



3.0.11

======

:release-date: 2012-09-26 04:00 p.m. UTC

:release-by: Ask Solem



\- \[security:low] generic-init.d scripts changed permissions of /var/log \& /var/run



&#x20;   In the daemonization tutorial the recommended directories were as follows:



&#x20;   .. code-block:: bash



&#x20;       CELERYD\_LOG\_FILE="/var/log/celery/%n.log"

&#x20;       CELERYD\_PID\_FILE="/var/run/celery/%n.pid"



&#x20;   But in the scripts themselves the default files were ``/var/log/celery%n.log``

&#x20;   and ``/var/run/celery%n.pid``, so if the user didn't change the location

&#x20;   by configuration, the directories ``/var/log`` and ``/var/run`` would be

&#x20;   created - and worse have their permissions and owners changed.



&#x20;   This change means that:



&#x20;       - Default pid file is ``/var/run/celery/%n.pid``

&#x20;       - Default log file is ``/var/log/celery/%n.log``



&#x20;       - The directories are only created and have their permissions

&#x20;         changed if \*no custom locations are set\*.



&#x20;   Users can force paths to be created by calling the ``create-paths``

&#x20;   sub-command:



&#x20;   .. code-block:: console



&#x20;       $ sudo /etc/init.d/celeryd create-paths



&#x20;   .. admonition:: Upgrading Celery won't update init-scripts



&#x20;       To update the init-scripts you have to re-download

&#x20;       the files from source control and update them manually.

&#x20;       You can find the init-scripts for version 3.0.x at:



&#x20;           https://github.com/celery/celery/tree/3.0/extra/generic-init.d



\- Now depends on billiard 2.7.3.17



\- Fixes request stack protection when app is initialized more than

&#x20; once (Issue #1003).



\- ETA tasks now properly works when system timezone isn't same

&#x20; as the configured timezone (Issue #1004).



\- Terminating a task now works if the task has been sent to the

&#x20; pool but not yet acknowledged by a pool process (Issue #1007).



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Alexey Zatelepin



\- Terminating a task now properly updates the state of the task to revoked,

&#x20; and sends a ``task-revoked`` event.



\- Generic worker init-script now waits for workers to shutdown by default.



\- Multi: No longer parses --app option (Issue #1008).



\- Multi: ``stop\_verify`` command renamed to ``stopwait``.



\- Daemonization: Now delays trying to create pidfile/logfile until after

&#x20; the working directory has been changed into.



\- :program:`celery worker` and :program:`celery beat` commands now respects

&#x20; the :option:`--no-color <celery --no-color>` option (Issue #999).



\- Fixed typos in eventlet examples (Issue #1000)



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Bryan Bishop.

&#x20;   Congratulations on opening bug #1000!



\- Tasks that raise :exc:`\~celery.exceptions.Ignore` are now acknowledged.



\- Beat: Now shows the name of the entry in ``sending due task`` logs.



.. \_version-3.0.10:



3.0.10

======

:release-date: 2012-09-20 05:30 p.m. BST

:release-by: Ask Solem



\- Now depends on kombu 2.4.7



\- Now depends on billiard 2.7.3.14



&#x20;   - Fixes crash at start-up when using Django and pre-1.4 projects

&#x20;     (``setup\_environ``).



&#x20;   - Hard time limits now sends the KILL signal shortly after TERM,

&#x20;     to terminate processes that have signal handlers blocked by C extensions.



&#x20;   - Billiard now installs even if the C extension cannot be built.



&#x20;       It's still recommended to build the C extension if you're using

&#x20;       a transport other than RabbitMQ/Redis (or use forced execv for some

&#x20;       other reason).



&#x20;   - Pool now sets a ``current\_process().index`` attribute that can be used to create

&#x20;     as many log files as there are processes in the pool.



\- Canvas: chord/group/chain no longer modifies the state when called



&#x20;   Previously calling a chord/group/chain would modify the ids of subtasks

&#x20;   so that:



&#x20;   .. code-block:: pycon



&#x20;       >>> c = chord(\[add.s(2, 2), add.s(4, 4)], xsum.s())

&#x20;       >>> c()

&#x20;       >>> c() <-- call again



&#x20;   at the second time the ids for the tasks would be the same as in the

&#x20;   previous invocation. This is now fixed, so that calling a subtask

&#x20;   won't mutate any options.



\- Canvas: Chaining a chord to another task now works (Issue #965).



\- Worker: Fixed a bug where the request stack could be corrupted if

&#x20; relative imports are used.



&#x20;   Problem usually manifested itself as an exception while trying to

&#x20;   send a failed task result (``NoneType does not have id attribute``).



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Sam Cooke.



\- Tasks can now raise :exc:`\~celery.exceptions.Ignore` to skip updating states

&#x20; or events after return.



&#x20;   Example:



&#x20;   .. code-block:: python



&#x20;       from celery.exceptions import Ignore



&#x20;       @task

&#x20;       def custom\_revokes():

&#x20;           if redis.sismember('tasks.revoked', custom\_revokes.request.id):

&#x20;               raise Ignore()



\- The worker now makes sure the request/task stacks aren't modified

&#x20; by the initial ``Task.\_\_call\_\_``.



&#x20;   This would previously be a problem if a custom task class defined

&#x20;   ``\_\_call\_\_`` and also called ``super()``.



\- Because of problems the fast local optimization has been disabled,

&#x20; and can only be enabled by setting the :envvar:`USE\_FAST\_LOCALS` attribute.



\- Worker: Now sets a default socket timeout of 5 seconds at shutdown

&#x20; so that broken socket reads don't hinder proper shutdown (Issue #975).



\- More fixes related to late eventlet/gevent patching.



\- Documentation for settings out of sync with reality:



&#x20;   - :setting:`CELERY\_TASK\_PUBLISH\_RETRY`



&#x20;       Documented as disabled by default, but it was enabled by default

&#x20;       since 2.5 as stated by the 2.5 changelog.



&#x20;   - :setting:`CELERY\_TASK\_PUBLISH\_RETRY\_POLICY`



&#x20;       The default max\_retries had been set to 100, but documented as being

&#x20;       3, and the interval\_max was set to 1 but documented as 0.2.

&#x20;       The default setting are now set to 3 and 0.2 as it was originally

&#x20;       documented.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Matt Long.



\- Worker: Log messages when connection established and lost have been improved.



\- The repr of a Crontab schedule value of '0' should be '\*'  (Issue #972).



\- Revoked tasks are now removed from reserved/active state in the worker

&#x20; (Issue #969)



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Alexey Zatelepin.



\- gevent: Now supports hard time limits using ``gevent.Timeout``.



\- Documentation: Links to init-scripts now point to the 3.0 branch instead

&#x20; of the development branch (master).



\- Documentation: Fixed typo in signals user guide (Issue #986).



&#x20;   ``instance.app.queues`` -> ``instance.app.amqp.queues``.



\- Eventlet/gevent: The worker didn't properly set the custom app

&#x20; for new greenlets.



\- Eventlet/gevent: Fixed a bug where the worker could not recover

&#x20; from connection loss (Issue #959).



&#x20;   Also, because of a suspected bug in gevent the

&#x20;   :setting:`BROKER\_CONNECTION\_TIMEOUT` setting has been disabled

&#x20;   when using gevent



3.0.9

=====

:release-date: 2012-08-31 06:00 p.m. BST

:release-by: Ask Solem



\- Important note for users of Django and the database scheduler!



&#x20;   Recently a timezone issue has been fixed for periodic tasks,

&#x20;   but erroneous timezones could have already been stored in the

&#x20;   database, so for the fix to work you need to reset

&#x20;   the ``last\_run\_at`` fields.



&#x20;   You can do this by executing the following command:



&#x20;   .. code-block:: console



&#x20;       $ python manage.py shell

&#x20;       >>> from djcelery.models import PeriodicTask

&#x20;       >>> PeriodicTask.objects.update(last\_run\_at=None)



&#x20;   You also have to do this if you change the timezone or

&#x20;   :setting:`CELERY\_ENABLE\_UTC` setting.



\- Note about the :setting:`CELERY\_ENABLE\_UTC` setting.



&#x20;   If you previously disabled this just to force periodic tasks to work with

&#x20;   your timezone, then you're now \*encouraged to re-enable it\*.



\- Now depends on Kombu 2.4.5 which fixes PyPy + Jython installation.



\- Fixed bug with timezones when :setting:`CELERY\_ENABLE\_UTC` is disabled

&#x20; (Issue #952).



\- Fixed a typo in the ``celerybeat`` upgrade mechanism (Issue #951).



\- Make sure the `exc\_info` argument to logging is resolved (Issue #899).



\- Fixed problem with Python 3.2 and thread join timeout overflow (Issue #796).



\- A test case was occasionally broken for Python 2.5.



\- Unit test suite now passes for PyPy 1.9.



\- App instances now supports the :keyword:`with` statement.



&#x20;   This calls the new :meth:`@close` method at exit, which

&#x20;   cleans up after the app like closing pool connections.



&#x20;   Note that this is only necessary when dynamically creating apps,

&#x20;   for example "temporary" apps.



\- Support for piping a subtask to a chain.



&#x20;   For example:



&#x20;   .. code-block:: python



&#x20;       pipe = sometask.s() | othertask.s()

&#x20;       new\_pipe = mytask.s() | pipe



&#x20;   Contributed by Steve Morin.



\- Fixed problem with group results on non-pickle serializers.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Steeve Morin.



.. \_version-3.0.8:



3.0.8

=====

:release-date: 2012-08-29 05:00 p.m. BST

:release-by: Ask Solem



\- Now depends on Kombu 2.4.4



\- Fixed problem with :pypi:`amqplib` and receiving larger message payloads

&#x20; (Issue #922).



&#x20;   The problem would manifest itself as either the worker hanging,

&#x20;   or occasionally a ``Framing error`` exception appearing.



&#x20;   Users of the new ``pyamqp://`` transport must upgrade to

&#x20;   :pypi:`amqp` 0.9.3.



\- Beat: Fixed another timezone bug with interval and Crontab schedules

&#x20; (Issue #943).



\- Beat: The schedule file is now automatically cleared if the timezone

&#x20; is changed.



&#x20;   The schedule is also cleared when you upgrade to 3.0.8 from an earlier

&#x20;   version, this to register the initial timezone info.



\- Events: The :event:`worker-heartbeat` event now include processed and active

&#x20; count fields.



&#x20;   Contributed by Mher Movsisyan.



\- Fixed error with error email and new task classes (Issue #931).



\- ``BaseTask.\_\_call\_\_`` is no longer optimized away if it has been monkey

&#x20; patched.



\- Fixed shutdown issue when using gevent (Issue #911 \& Issue #936).



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Thomas Meson.



.. \_version-3.0.7:



3.0.7

=====

:release-date: 2012-08-24 05:00 p.m. BST

:release-by: Ask Solem



\- Fixes several problems with periodic tasks and timezones (Issue #937).



\- Now depends on kombu 2.4.2



&#x20;   - Redis: Fixes a race condition crash



&#x20;   - Fixes an infinite loop that could happen when retrying establishing

&#x20;     the broker connection.



\- Daemons now redirect standard file descriptors to :file:`/dev/null`



&#x20;   Though by default the standard outs are also redirected

&#x20;   to the logger instead, but you can disable this by changing

&#x20;   the :setting:`CELERY\_REDIRECT\_STDOUTS` setting.



\- Fixes possible problems when eventlet/gevent is patched too late.



\- ``LoggingProxy`` no longer defines ``fileno()`` (Issue #928).



\- Results are now ignored for the chord unlock task.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Steeve Morin.



\- Cassandra backend now works if result expiry is disabled.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Steeve Morin.



\- The traceback object is now passed to signal handlers instead

&#x20; of the string representation.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Adam DePue.



\- Celery command: Extensions are now sorted by name.



\- A regression caused the :event:`task-failed` event to be sent

&#x20; with the exception object instead of its string representation.



\- The worker daemon would try to create the pid file before daemonizing

&#x20; to catch errors, but this file wasn't immediately released (Issue #923).



\- Fixes Jython compatibility.



\- ``billiard.forking\_enable`` was called by all pools not just the

&#x20; processes pool, which would result in a useless warning if the billiard

&#x20; C extensions weren't installed.



.. \_version-3.0.6:



3.0.6

=====

:release-date: 2012-08-17 11:00 p.mp.m. Ask Solem



\- Now depends on kombu 2.4.0



\- Now depends on billiard 2.7.3.12



\- Redis: Celery now tries to restore messages whenever there are no messages

&#x20; in the queue.



\- Crontab schedules now properly respects :setting:`CELERY\_TIMEZONE` setting.



&#x20;   It's important to note that Crontab schedules uses UTC time by default

&#x20;   unless this setting is set.



&#x20;   Issue #904 and :pypi:`django-celery` #150.



\- ``billiard.enable\_forking`` is now only set by the processes pool.



\- The transport is now properly shown by :program:`celery report`

&#x20; (Issue #913).



\- The `--app` argument now works if the last part is a module name

&#x20; (Issue #921).



\- Fixed problem with unpickleable exceptions (billiard #12).



\- Adds ``task\_name`` attribute to ``EagerResult`` which is always

&#x20; :const:`None` (Issue #907).



\- Old Task class in :mod:`celery.task` no longer accepts magic kwargs by

&#x20; default (Issue #918).



&#x20;   A regression long ago disabled magic kwargs for these, and since

&#x20;   no one has complained about it we don't have any incentive to fix it now.



\- The ``inspect reserved`` control command didn't work properly.



\- Should now play better with tools for static analysis by explicitly

&#x20; specifying dynamically created attributes in the :mod:`celery` and

&#x20; :mod:`celery.task` modules.



\- Terminating a task now results in

&#x20; :exc:`\~celery.exceptions.RevokedTaskError` instead of a ``WorkerLostError``.



\- ``AsyncResult.revoke`` now accepts ``terminate`` and ``signal`` arguments.



\- The :event:`task-revoked` event now includes new fields: ``terminated``,

&#x20; ``signum``, and ``expired``.



\- The argument to :class:`\~celery.exceptions.TaskRevokedError` is now one

&#x20; of the reasons ``revoked``, ``expired`` or ``terminated``.



\- Old Task class does no longer use :class:`classmethod` for ``push\_request``

&#x20; and ``pop\_request``  (Issue #912).



\- ``GroupResult`` now supports the ``children`` attribute (Issue #916).



\- ``AsyncResult.collect`` now respects the ``intermediate`` argument

&#x20; (Issue #917).



\- Fixes example task in documentation (Issue #902).



\- Eventlet fixed so that the environment is patched as soon as possible.



\- eventlet: Now warns if Celery related modules that depends on threads

&#x20; are imported before eventlet is patched.



\- Improved event and camera examples in the monitoring guide.



\- Disables celery command setuptools entry-points if the command can't be

&#x20; loaded.



\- Fixed broken ``dump\_request`` example in the tasks guide.







.. \_version-3.0.5:



3.0.5

=====

:release-date: 2012-08-01 04:00 p.m. BST

:release-by: Ask Solem



\- Now depends on kombu 2.3.1 + billiard 2.7.3.11



\- Fixed a bug with the -B option (``cannot pickle thread.lock objects``)

&#x20; (Issue #894 + Issue #892, + :pypi:`django-celery` #154).



\- The :control:`restart\_pool` control command now requires the

&#x20; :setting:`CELERYD\_POOL\_RESTARTS` setting to be enabled



&#x20;   This change was necessary as the multiprocessing event that the restart

&#x20;   command depends on is responsible for creating many semaphores/file

&#x20;   descriptors, resulting in problems in some environments.



\- ``chain.apply`` now passes args to the first task (Issue #889).



\- Documented previously secret options to the :pypi:`django-celery` monitor

&#x20; in the monitoring user guide (Issue #396).



\- Old changelog are now organized in separate documents for each series,

&#x20; see :ref:`history`.



.. \_version-3.0.4:



3.0.4

=====

:release-date: 2012-07-26 07:00 p.m. BST

:release-by: Ask Solem



\- Now depends on Kombu 2.3



\- New experimental standalone Celery monitor: Flower



&#x20;   See :ref:`monitoring-flower` to read more about it!



&#x20;   Contributed by Mher Movsisyan.



\- Now supports AMQP heartbeats if using the new ``pyamqp://`` transport.



&#x20;   - The :pypi:`amqp` transport requires the :pypi:`amqp` library to be installed:



&#x20;       .. code-block:: console



&#x20;           $ pip install amqp



&#x20;   - Then you need to set the transport URL prefix to ``pyamqp://``.



&#x20;   - The default heartbeat value is 10 seconds, but this can be changed using

&#x20;     the :setting:`BROKER\_HEARTBEAT` setting::



&#x20;       BROKER\_HEARTBEAT = 5.0



&#x20;   - If the broker heartbeat is set to 10 seconds, the heartbeats will be

&#x20;     monitored every 5 seconds (double the heartbeat rate).



&#x20;   See the :ref:`Kombu 2.3 changelog <kombu:version-2.3.0>` for more information.



\- Now supports RabbitMQ Consumer Cancel Notifications, using the ``pyamqp://``

&#x20; transport.



&#x20;   This is essential when running RabbitMQ in a cluster.



&#x20;   See the :ref:`Kombu 2.3 changelog <kombu:version-2.3.0>` for more information.



\- Delivery info is no longer passed directly through.



&#x20;   It was discovered that the SQS transport adds objects that can't

&#x20;   be pickled to the delivery info mapping, so we had to go back

&#x20;   to using the white-list again.



&#x20;   Fixing this bug also means that the SQS transport is now working again.



\- The semaphore wasn't properly released when a task was revoked (Issue #877).



&#x20;   This could lead to tasks being swallowed and not released until a worker

&#x20;   restart.



&#x20;   Thanks to Hynek Schlawack for debugging the issue.



\- Retrying a task now also forwards any linked tasks.



&#x20;   This means that if a task is part of a chain (or linked in some other

&#x20;   way) and that even if the task is retried, then the next task in the chain

&#x20;   will be executed when the retry succeeds.



\- Chords: Now supports setting the interval and other keyword arguments

&#x20; to the chord unlock task.



&#x20;   - The interval can now be set as part of the chord subtasks kwargs::



&#x20;       chord(header)(body, interval=10.0)



&#x20;   - In addition the chord unlock task now honors the Task.default\_retry\_delay

&#x20;     option, used when none is specified, which also means that the default

&#x20;     interval can also be changed using annotations:



&#x20;       .. code-block:: python



&#x20;           CELERY\_ANNOTATIONS = {

&#x20;               'celery.chord\_unlock': {

&#x20;                   'default\_retry\_delay': 10.0,

&#x20;               }

&#x20;           }



\- New :meth:`@add\_defaults` method can add new default configuration

&#x20; dictionaries to the applications configuration.



&#x20;   For example::



&#x20;       config = {'FOO': 10}



&#x20;       app.add\_defaults(config)



&#x20;   is the same as ``app.conf.update(config)`` except that data won't be

&#x20;   copied, and that it won't be pickled when the worker spawns child

&#x20;   processes.



&#x20;   In addition the method accepts a callable::



&#x20;       def initialize\_config():

&#x20;           # insert heavy stuff that can't be done at import time here.



&#x20;       app.add\_defaults(initialize\_config)



&#x20;   which means the same as the above except that it won't happen

&#x20;   until the Celery configuration is actually used.



&#x20;   As an example, Celery can lazily use the configuration of a Flask app::



&#x20;       flask\_app = Flask()

&#x20;       app = Celery()

&#x20;       app.add\_defaults(lambda: flask\_app.config)



\- Revoked tasks weren't marked as revoked in the result backend (Issue #871).



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Hynek Schlawack.



\- Event-loop now properly handles the case when the :manpage:`epoll` poller

&#x20; object has been closed (Issue #882).



\- Fixed syntax error in ``funtests/test\_leak.py``



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Catalin Iacob.



\- group/chunks: Now accepts empty task list (Issue #873).



\- New method names:



&#x20;   - ``Celery.default\_connection()`` ➠  :meth:`\~@connection\_or\_acquire`.

&#x20;   - ``Celery.default\_producer()``   ➠  :meth:`\~@producer\_or\_acquire`.



&#x20;   The old names still work for backward compatibility.





.. \_version-3.0.3:



3.0.3

=====

:release-date: 2012-07-20 09:17 p.m. BST

:release-by: Ask Solem



\- :pypi:`amqplib` passes the channel object as part of the delivery\_info

&#x20; and it's not pickleable, so we now remove it.



.. \_version-3.0.2:



3.0.2

=====

:release-date: 2012-07-20 04:00 p.m. BST

:release-by: Ask Solem



\- A bug caused the following task options to not take defaults from the

&#x20;  configuration (Issue #867 + Issue #858)



&#x20;   The following settings were affected:



&#x20;   - :setting:`CELERY\_IGNORE\_RESULT`

&#x20;   - :setting:`CELERYD\_SEND\_TASK\_ERROR\_EMAILS`

&#x20;   - :setting:`CELERY\_TRACK\_STARTED`

&#x20;   - :setting:`CElERY\_STORE\_ERRORS\_EVEN\_IF\_IGNORED`



&#x20;   Fix contributed by John Watson.



\- Task Request: ``delivery\_info`` is now passed through as-is (Issue #807).



\- The ETA argument now supports datetime's with a timezone set (Issue #855).



\- The worker's banner displayed the autoscale settings in the wrong order

&#x20; (Issue #859).



\- Extension commands are now loaded after concurrency is set up

&#x20; so that they don't interfere with things like eventlet patching.



\- Fixed bug in the threaded pool (Issue #863)



\- The task failure handler mixed up the fields in :func:`sys.exc\_info`.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Rinat Shigapov.



\- Fixed typos and wording in the docs.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Paul McMillan



\- New setting: :setting:`CELERY\_WORKER\_DIRECT`



&#x20;   If enabled each worker will consume from their own dedicated queue

&#x20;   which can be used to route tasks to specific workers.



\- Fixed several edge case bugs in the add consumer remote control command.



\- :mod:`\~celery.contrib.migrate`: Can now filter and move tasks to specific

&#x20; workers if :setting:`CELERY\_WORKER\_DIRECT` is enabled.



&#x20;   Among other improvements, the following functions have been added:



&#x20;       \* ``move\_direct(filterfun, \*\*opts)``

&#x20;       \* ``move\_direct\_by\_id(task\_id, worker\_hostname, \*\*opts)``

&#x20;       \* ``move\_direct\_by\_idmap({task\_id: worker\_hostname, ...}, \*\*opts)``

&#x20;       \* ``move\_direct\_by\_taskmap({task\_name: worker\_hostname, ...}, \*\*opts)``



\- :meth:`\~celery.Celery.default\_connection` now accepts a pool argument that

&#x20; if set to false causes a new connection to be created instead of acquiring

&#x20; one from the pool.



\- New signal: :signal:`celeryd\_after\_setup`.



\- Default loader now keeps lowercase attributes from the configuration module.



.. \_version-3.0.1:



3.0.1

=====

:release-date: 2012-07-10 06:00 p.m. BST

:release-by: Ask Solem



\- Now depends on kombu 2.2.5



\- inspect now supports limit argument::



&#x20;   myapp.control.inspect(limit=1).ping()



\- Beat: now works with timezone aware datetime's.



\- Task classes inheriting ``from celery import Task``

&#x20; mistakenly enabled ``accept\_magic\_kwargs``.



\- Fixed bug in ``inspect scheduled`` (Issue #829).



\- Beat: Now resets the schedule to upgrade to UTC.



\- The :program:`celery worker` command now works with eventlet/gevent.



&#x20;   Previously it wouldn't patch the environment early enough.



\- The :program:`celery` command now supports extension commands

&#x20; using setuptools entry-points.



&#x20;   Libraries can add additional commands to the :program:`celery`

&#x20;   command by adding an entry-point like::



&#x20;       setup(

&#x20;           entry\_points=\[

&#x20;               'celery.commands': \[

&#x20;                   'foo = my.module:Command',

&#x20;           ],

&#x20;       ],

&#x20;       ...)



&#x20;   The command must then support the interface of

&#x20;   :class:`celery.bin.base.Command`.



\- contrib.migrate: New utilities to move tasks from one queue to another.



&#x20;   - :func:`\~celery.contrib.migrate.move\_tasks`

&#x20;   - :func:`\~celery.contrib.migrate.move\_task\_by\_id`



\- The :event:`task-sent` event now contains ``exchange`` and ``routing\_key``

&#x20; fields.



\- Fixes bug with installing on Python 3.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Jed Smith.



.. \_version-3.0.0:



3.0.0 (Chiastic Slide)

======================

:release-date: 2012-07-07 01:30 p.m. BST

:release-by: Ask Solem



See :ref:`whatsnew-3.0`.



```



\### 代码文件: docs\\history\\changelog-3.1.rst

```rst

.. \_changelog-3.1:



================

&#x20;Change history

================



This document contains change notes for bugfix releases in the 3.1.x series

(Cipater), please see :ref:`whatsnew-3.1` for an overview of what's

new in Celery 3.1.



.. \_version-3.1.26:



3.1.26

======

:release-date: 2018-23-03 16:00 PM IST

:release-by: Omer Katz



\- Fixed a crash caused by tasks cycling between Celery 3 and Celery 4 workers.



.. \_version-3.1.25:



3.1.25

======

:release-date: 2016-10-10 12:00 PM PDT

:release-by: Ask Solem



\- \*\*Requirements\*\*



&#x20;   - Now depends on :ref:`Kombu 3.0.37 <kombu:version-3.0.37>`



\- Fixed problem with chords in group introduced in 3.1.24 (Issue #3504).



.. \_version-3.1.24:



3.1.24

======

:release-date: 2016-09-30 04:21 PM PDT

:release-by: Ask Solem



\- \*\*Requirements\*\*



&#x20;   - Now depends on :ref:`Kombu 3.0.36 <kombu:version-3.0.36>`.



\- Now supports Task protocol 2 from the future 4.0 release.



&#x20;   Workers running 3.1.24 are now able to process messages

&#x20;   sent using the `new task message protocol`\_ to be introduced

&#x20;   in Celery 4.0.



&#x20;   Users upgrading to Celery 4.0 when this is released are encouraged

&#x20;   to upgrade to this version as an intermediate step, as this

&#x20;   means workers not yet upgraded will be able to process

&#x20;   messages from clients/workers running 4.0.



.. \_`new task message protocol`:

&#x20;   https://docs.celeryq.dev/en/master/internals/protocol.html#version-2



\- ``Task.send\_events`` can now be set to disable sending of events

&#x20; for that task only.



&#x20;   Example when defining the task:



&#x20;   .. code-block:: python



&#x20;       @app.task(send\_events=False)

&#x20;       def add(x, y):

&#x20;           return x + y



\- \*\*Utils\*\*: Fixed compatibility with recent :pypi:`psutil` versions

&#x20; (Issue #3262).



\- \*\*Canvas\*\*: Chord now forwards partial arguments to its subtasks.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Tayfun Sen.



\- \*\*App\*\*: Arguments to app such as ``backend``, ``broker``, etc

&#x20; are now pickled and sent to the child processes on Windows.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Jeremy Zafran.



\- \*\*Deployment\*\*: Generic init scripts now supports being symlinked

&#x20; in runlevel directories (Issue #3208).



\- \*\*Deployment\*\*: Updated CentOS scripts to work with CentOS 7.



&#x20;   Contributed by Joe Sanford.



\- \*\*Events\*\*: The curses monitor no longer crashes when the

&#x20; result of a task is empty.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Dongweiming.



\- \*\*Worker\*\*: ``repr(worker)`` would crash when called early

&#x20; in the startup process (Issue #2514).



\- \*\*Tasks\*\*: GroupResult now defines \_\_bool\_\_ and \_\_nonzero\_\_.



&#x20;   This is to fix an issue where a ResultSet or GroupResult with an empty

&#x20;   result list are not properly tupled with the as\_tuple() method when it is

&#x20;   a parent result. This is due to the as\_tuple() method performing a logical

&#x20;   and operation on the ResultSet.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Colin McIntosh.



\- \*\*Worker\*\*: Fixed wrong values in autoscale related logging message.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by ``@raducc``.



\- Documentation improvements by



&#x20;   \* Alexandru Chirila

&#x20;   \* Michael Aquilina

&#x20;   \* Mikko Ekström

&#x20;   \* Mitchel Humpherys

&#x20;   \* Thomas A. Neil

&#x20;   \* Tiago Moreira Vieira

&#x20;   \* Yuriy Syrovetskiy

&#x20;   \* ``@dessant``



.. \_version-3.1.23:



3.1.23

======

:release-date: 2016-03-09 06:00 P.M PST

:release-by: Ask Solem



\- \*\*Programs\*\*: Last release broke support for the ``--hostnmame`` argument

&#x20; to :program:`celery multi` and :program:`celery worker --detach`

&#x20; (Issue #3103).



\- \*\*Results\*\*: MongoDB result backend could crash the worker at startup

&#x20; if not configured using an URL.



.. \_version-3.1.22:



3.1.22

======

:release-date: 2016-03-07 01:30 P.M PST

:release-by: Ask Solem



\- \*\*Programs\*\*: The worker would crash immediately on startup on

&#x20; ``backend.as\_uri()`` when using some result backends (Issue #3094).



\- \*\*Programs\*\*: :program:`celery multi`/:program:`celery worker --detach`

&#x20; would create an extraneous logfile including literal formats (e.g. ``%I``)

&#x20; in the filename (Issue #3096).



.. \_version-3.1.21:



3.1.21

======

:release-date: 2016-03-04 11:16 a.m. PST

:release-by: Ask Solem



\- \*\*Requirements\*\*



&#x20;   - Now depends on :ref:`Kombu 3.0.34 <kombu:version-3.0.34>`.



&#x20;   - Now depends on :mod:`billiard` 3.3.0.23.



\- \*\*Prefork pool\*\*: Fixes 100% CPU loop on Linux :manpage:`epoll`

&#x20; (Issue #1845).



&#x20;   Also potential fix for: Issue #2142, Issue #2606



\- \*\*Prefork pool\*\*: Fixes memory leak related to processes exiting

&#x20; (Issue #2927).



\- \*\*Worker\*\*: Fixes crash at start-up when trying to censor passwords

&#x20; in MongoDB and Cache result backend URLs (Issue #3079, Issue #3045,

&#x20; Issue #3049, Issue #3068, Issue #3073).



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Maxime Verger.



\- \*\*Task\*\*: An exception is now raised if countdown/expires is less

&#x20; than -2147483648 (Issue #3078).



\- \*\*Programs\*\*: :program:`celery shell --ipython` now compatible with newer

&#x20; :pypi:`IPython` versions.



\- \*\*Programs\*\*: The DuplicateNodeName warning emitted by inspect/control

&#x20; now includes a list of the node names returned.



&#x20;   Contributed by Sebastian Kalinowski.



\- \*\*Utils\*\*: The ``.discard(item)`` method of

&#x20; :class:`\~celery.utils.collections.LimitedSet` didn't actually remove the item

&#x20; (Issue #3087).



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Dave Smith.



\- \*\*Worker\*\*: Node name formatting now emits less confusing error message

&#x20; for unmatched format keys (Issue #3016).



\- \*\*Results\*\*: RPC/AMQP backends: Fixed deserialization of JSON exceptions

&#x20; (Issue #2518).



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Allard Hoeve.



\- \*\*Prefork pool\*\*: The `process inqueue damaged` error message now includes

&#x20; the original exception raised.



\- \*\*Documentation\*\*: Includes improvements by:



&#x20;   - Jeff Widman.



.. \_version-3.1.20:



3.1.20

======

:release-date: 2016-01-22 06:50 p.m. UTC

:release-by: Ask Solem



\- \*\*Requirements\*\*



&#x20;   - Now depends on :ref:`Kombu 3.0.33 <kombu:version-3.0.33>`.



&#x20;   - Now depends on :mod:`billiard` 3.3.0.22.



&#x20;       Includes binary wheels for Microsoft Windows x86 and x86\_64!



\- \*\*Task\*\*: Error emails now uses ``utf-8`` character set by default

&#x20; (Issue #2737).



\- \*\*Task\*\*: Retry now forwards original message headers (Issue #3017).



\- \*\*Worker\*\*: Bootsteps can now hook into ``on\_node\_join``/``leave``/``lost``.



&#x20;   See :ref:`extending-consumer-attributes` for an example.



\- \*\*Events\*\*: Fixed handling of DST timezones (Issue #2983).



\- \*\*Results\*\*: Redis backend stopped respecting certain settings.



&#x20;   Contributed by Jeremy Llewellyn.



\- \*\*Results\*\*: Database backend now properly supports JSON exceptions

&#x20; (Issue #2441).



\- \*\*Results\*\*: Redis ``new\_join`` didn't properly call task errbacks on chord

&#x20; error (Issue #2796).



\- \*\*Results\*\*: Restores Redis compatibility with Python :pypi:`redis` < 2.10.0

&#x20; (Issue #2903).



\- \*\*Results\*\*: Fixed rare issue with chord error handling (Issue #2409).



\- \*\*Tasks\*\*: Using queue-name values in :setting:`CELERY\_ROUTES` now works

&#x20; again (Issue #2987).



\- \*\*General\*\*: Result backend password now sanitized in report output

&#x20; (Issue #2812, Issue #2004).



\- \*\*Configuration\*\*: Now gives helpful error message when the result backend

&#x20; configuration points to a module, and not a class (Issue #2945).



\- \*\*Results\*\*: Exceptions sent by JSON serialized workers are now properly

&#x20; handled by pickle configured workers.



\- \*\*Programs\*\*: ``celery control autoscale`` now works (Issue #2950).



\- \*\*Programs\*\*: ``celery beat --detached`` now runs after fork callbacks.



\- \*\*General\*\*: Fix for LRU cache implementation on Python 3.5 (Issue #2897).



&#x20;   Contributed by Dennis Brakhane.



&#x20;   Python 3.5's ``OrderedDict`` doesn't allow mutation while it is being

&#x20;   iterated over. This breaks "update" if it is called with a dict

&#x20;   larger than the maximum size.



&#x20;   This commit changes the code to a version that doesn't iterate over

&#x20;   the dict, and should also be a little bit faster.



\- \*\*Init-scripts\*\*: The beat init-script now properly reports service as down

&#x20; when no pid file can be found.



&#x20;   Eric Zarowny



\- \*\*Beat\*\*: Added cleaning of corrupted scheduler files for some storage

&#x20; backend errors (Issue #2985).



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Aleksandr Kuznetsov.



\- \*\*Beat\*\*: Now syncs the schedule even if the schedule is empty.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Colin McIntosh.



\- \*\*Supervisord\*\*: Set higher process priority in the :pypi:`supervisord`

&#x20;   example.



&#x20;   Contributed by George Tantiras.



\- \*\*Documentation\*\*: Includes improvements by:



&#x20;   :github\_user:`Bryson`

&#x20;   Caleb Mingle

&#x20;   Christopher Martin

&#x20;   Dieter Adriaenssens

&#x20;   Jason Veatch

&#x20;   Jeremy Cline

&#x20;   Juan Rossi

&#x20;   Kevin Harvey

&#x20;   Kevin McCarthy

&#x20;   Kirill Pavlov

&#x20;   Marco Buttu

&#x20;   :github\_user:`Mayflower`

&#x20;   Mher Movsisyan

&#x20;   Michael Floering

&#x20;   :github\_user:`michael-k`

&#x20;   Nathaniel Varona

&#x20;   Rudy Attias

&#x20;   Ryan Luckie

&#x20;   Steven Parker

&#x20;   :github\_user:`squfrans`

&#x20;   Tadej Janež

&#x20;   TakesxiSximada

&#x20;   Tom S



.. \_version-3.1.19:



3.1.19

======

:release-date: 2015-10-26 01:00 p.m. UTC

:release-by: Ask Solem



\- \*\*Requirements\*\*



&#x20;   - Now depends on :ref:`Kombu 3.0.29 <kombu:version-3.0.29>`.



&#x20;   - Now depends on :mod:`billiard` 3.3.0.21.



\-  \*\*Results\*\*: Fixed MongoDB result backend URL parsing problem

&#x20;  (Issue celery/kombu#375).



\- \*\*Worker\*\*: Task request now properly sets ``priority`` in delivery\_info.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Gerald Manipon.



\- \*\*Beat\*\*: PyPy shelve may raise ``KeyError`` when setting keys

&#x20; (Issue #2862).



\- \*\*Programs\*\*: :program:`celery beat --deatched` now working on PyPy.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Krzysztof Bujniewicz.



\- \*\*Results\*\*: Redis result backend now ensures all pipelines are cleaned up.



&#x20;   Contributed by Justin Patrin.



\- \*\*Results\*\*: Redis result backend now allows for timeout to be set in the

&#x20; query portion of the result backend URL.



&#x20;   For example ``CELERY\_RESULT\_BACKEND = 'redis://?timeout=10'``



&#x20;   Contributed by Justin Patrin.



\- \*\*Results\*\*: ``result.get`` now properly handles failures where the

&#x20; exception value is set to :const:`None` (Issue #2560).



\- \*\*Prefork pool\*\*: Fixed attribute error ``proc.dead``.



\- \*\*Worker\*\*: Fixed worker hanging when gossip/heartbeat disabled

&#x20; (Issue #1847).



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Aaron Webber and Bryan Helmig.



\- \*\*Results\*\*: MongoDB result backend now supports pymongo 3.x

&#x20; (Issue #2744).



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Sukrit Khera.



\- \*\*Results\*\*: RPC/AMQP backends didn't deserialize exceptions properly

&#x20; (Issue #2691).



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Sukrit Khera.



\- \*\*Programs\*\*: Fixed problem with :program:`celery amqp`'s

&#x20; ``basic\_publish`` (Issue #2013).



\- \*\*Worker\*\*: Embedded beat now properly sets app for thread/process

&#x20; (Issue #2594).



\- \*\*Documentation\*\*: Many improvements and typos fixed.



&#x20;   Contributions by:



&#x20;       Carlos Garcia-Dubus

&#x20;       D. Yu

&#x20;       :github\_user:`jerry`

&#x20;       Jocelyn Delalande

&#x20;       Josh Kupershmidt

&#x20;       Juan Rossi

&#x20;       :github\_user:`kanemra`

&#x20;       Paul Pearce

&#x20;       Pavel Savchenko

&#x20;       Sean Wang

&#x20;       Seungha Kim

&#x20;       Zhaorong Ma



.. \_version-3.1.18:



3.1.18

======

:release-date: 2015-04-22 05:30 p.m. UTC

:release-by: Ask Solem



\- \*\*Requirements\*\*



&#x20;   - Now depends on :ref:`Kombu 3.0.25 <kombu:version-3.0.25>`.



&#x20;   - Now depends on :mod:`billiard` 3.3.0.20.



\- \*\*Django\*\*: Now supports Django 1.8 (Issue #2536).



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Bence Tamas and Mickaël Penhard.



\- \*\*Results\*\*: MongoDB result backend now compatible with pymongo 3.0.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Fatih Sucu.



\- \*\*Tasks\*\*: Fixed bug only happening when a task has multiple callbacks

&#x20; (Issue #2515).



&#x20;   Fix contributed by NotSqrt.



\- \*\*Commands\*\*: Preload options now support ``--arg value`` syntax.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by John Anderson.



\- \*\*Compat\*\*: A typo caused ``celery.log.setup\_logging\_subsystem`` to be

&#x20; undefined.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Gunnlaugur Thor Briem.



\- \*\*init-scripts\*\*: The beat generic init-script now uses

&#x20; :file:`/bin/sh` instead of :command:`bash` (Issue #2496).



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Jelle Verstraaten.



\- \*\*Django\*\*: Fixed a :exc:`TypeError` sometimes occurring in logging

&#x20; when validating models.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Alexander.



\- \*\*Commands\*\*: Worker now supports new

&#x20; :option:`--executable <celery worker --executable>` argument that can

&#x20; be used with :option:`celery worker --detach`.



&#x20;   Contributed by Bert Vanderbauwhede.



\- \*\*Canvas\*\*: Fixed crash in chord unlock fallback task (Issue #2404).



\- \*\*Worker\*\*: Fixed rare crash occurring with

&#x20; :option:`--autoscale <celery worker --autoscale>` enabled (Issue #2411).



\- \*\*Django\*\*: Properly recycle worker Django database connections when the

&#x20; Django ``CONN\_MAX\_AGE`` setting is enabled (Issue #2453).



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Luke Burden.



.. \_version-3.1.17:



3.1.17

======

:release-date: 2014-11-19 03:30 p.m. UTC

:release-by: Ask Solem



.. admonition:: Don't enable the `CELERYD\_FORCE\_EXECV` setting!



&#x20;   Please review your configuration and disable this option if you're using the

&#x20;   RabbitMQ or Redis transport.



&#x20;   Keeping this option enabled after 3.1 means the async based prefork pool will

&#x20;   be disabled, which can easily cause instability.



\- \*\*Requirements\*\*



&#x20;   - Now depends on :ref:`Kombu 3.0.24 <kombu:version-3.0.24>`.



&#x20;       Includes the new Qpid transport coming in Celery 3.2, backported to

&#x20;       support those who may still require Python 2.6 compatibility.



&#x20;   - Now depends on :mod:`billiard` 3.3.0.19.



&#x20;   - ``celery\[librabbitmq]`` now depends on librabbitmq 1.6.1.



\- \*\*Task\*\*: The timing of ETA/countdown tasks were off after the example ``LocalTimezone``

&#x20; implementation in the Python documentation no longer works in Python 3.4.

&#x20; (Issue #2306).



\- \*\*Task\*\*: Raising :exc:`\~celery.exceptions.Ignore` no longer sends

&#x20; ``task-failed`` event (Issue #2365).



\- \*\*Redis result backend\*\*: Fixed unbound local errors.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Thomas French.



\- \*\*Task\*\*: Callbacks wasn't called properly if ``link`` was a list of

&#x20; signatures (Issue #2350).



\- \*\*Canvas\*\*: chain and group now handles json serialized signatures

&#x20; (Issue #2076).



\- \*\*Results\*\*: ``.join\_native()`` would accidentally treat the ``STARTED``

&#x20; state as being ready (Issue #2326).



&#x20;   This could lead to the chord callback being called with invalid arguments

&#x20;   when using chords with the :setting:`CELERY\_TRACK\_STARTED` setting

&#x20;   enabled.



\- \*\*Canvas\*\*: The ``chord\_size`` attribute is now set for all canvas primitives,

&#x20; making sure more combinations will work with the ``new\_join`` optimization

&#x20; for Redis (Issue #2339).



\- \*\*Task\*\*: Fixed problem with app not being properly propagated to

&#x20; ``trace\_task`` in all cases.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by :github\_user:`kristaps`.



\- \*\*Worker\*\*: Expires from task message now associated with a timezone.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Albert Wang.



\- \*\*Cassandra result backend\*\*: Fixed problems when using detailed mode.



&#x20;   When using the Cassandra backend in detailed mode, a regression

&#x20;   caused errors when attempting to retrieve results.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Gino Ledesma.



\- \*\*Mongodb Result backend\*\*: Pickling the backend instance will now include

&#x20; the original URL (Issue #2347).



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Sukrit Khera.



\- \*\*Task\*\*: Exception info wasn't properly set for tasks raising

&#x20; :exc:`\~celery.exceptions.Reject` (Issue #2043).



\- \*\*Worker\*\*: Duplicates are now removed when loading the set of revoked tasks

&#x20; from the worker state database (Issue #2336).



\- \*\*celery.contrib.rdb\*\*: Fixed problems with ``rdb.set\_trace`` calling stop

&#x20; from the wrong frame.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by :github\_user:`llllllllll`.



\- \*\*Canvas\*\*: ``chain`` and ``chord`` can now be immutable.



\- \*\*Canvas\*\*: ``chord.apply\_async`` will now keep partial args set in

&#x20; ``self.args`` (Issue #2299).



\- \*\*Results\*\*: Small refactoring so that results are decoded the same way in

&#x20; all result backends.



\- \*\*Logging\*\*: The ``processName`` format was introduced in Python 2.6.2 so for

&#x20; compatibility this format is now excluded when using earlier versions

&#x20; (Issue #1644).



.. \_version-3.1.16:



3.1.16

======

:release-date: 2014-10-03 06:00 p.m. UTC

:release-by: Ask Solem



\- \*\*Worker\*\*: 3.1.15 broke :option:`-Ofair <celery worker -O>`

&#x20; behavior (Issue #2286).



&#x20;   This regression could result in all tasks executing

&#x20;   in a single child process if ``-Ofair`` was enabled.



\- \*\*Canvas\*\*: ``celery.signature`` now properly forwards app argument

&#x20; in all cases.



\- \*\*Task\*\*: ``.retry()`` didn't raise the exception correctly

&#x20; when called without a current exception.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Andrea Rabbaglietti.



\- \*\*Worker\*\*: The ``enable\_events`` remote control command

&#x20; disabled worker-related events by mistake (Issue #2272).



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Konstantinos Koukopoulos.



\- \*\*Django\*\*: Adds support for Django 1.7 class names in INSTALLED\_APPS

&#x20; when using ``app.autodiscover\_tasks()``  (Issue #2248).



\- \*\*Sphinx\*\*: ``celery.contrib.sphinx`` now uses ``getfullargspec``

&#x20; on Python 3 (Issue #2302).



\- \*\*Redis/Cache Backends\*\*: Chords will now run at most once if one or more tasks

&#x20; in the chord are executed multiple times for some reason.



.. \_version-3.1.15:



3.1.15

======

:release-date: 2014-09-14 11:00 p.m. UTC

:release-by: Ask Solem



\- \*\*Django\*\*: Now makes sure ``django.setup()`` is called

&#x20; before importing any task modules (Django 1.7 compatibility, Issue #2227)



\- \*\*Results\*\*: ``result.get()`` was misbehaving by calling

&#x20; ``backend.get\_task\_meta`` in a :keyword:`finally` call leading to

&#x20; AMQP result backend queues not being properly cleaned up (Issue #2245).



.. \_version-3.1.14:



3.1.14

======

:release-date: 2014-09-08 03:00 p.m. UTC

:release-by: Ask Solem



\- \*\*Requirements\*\*



&#x20;   - Now depends on :ref:`Kombu 3.0.22 <kombu:version-3.0.22>`.



\- \*\*Init-scripts\*\*: The generic worker init-scripts ``status`` command

&#x20; now gets an accurate pidfile list (Issue #1942).



\- \*\*Init-scripts\*\*: The generic beat script now implements the ``status``

&#x20;  command.



&#x20;   Contributed by John Whitlock.



\- \*\*Commands\*\*: Multi now writes informational output to stdout instead of stderr.



\- \*\*Worker\*\*: Now ignores not implemented error for ``pool.restart``

&#x20; (Issue #2153).



\- \*\*Task\*\*: Retry no longer raises retry exception when executed in eager

&#x20; mode (Issue #2164).



\- \*\*AMQP Result backend\*\*: Now ensured ``on\_interval`` is called at least

&#x20; every second for blocking calls to properly propagate parent errors.



\- \*\*Django\*\*: Compatibility with Django 1.7 on Windows (Issue #2126).



\- \*\*Programs\*\*: :option:`!--umask` argument can now be

&#x20; specified in both octal (if starting with 0) or decimal.





.. \_version-3.1.13:



3.1.13

======



Security Fixes

\--------------



\* \[Security: `CELERYSA-0002`\_] Insecure default umask.



&#x20;   The built-in utility used to daemonize the Celery worker service sets

&#x20;   an insecure umask by default (umask 0).



&#x20;   This means that any files or directories created by the worker will

&#x20;   end up having world-writable permissions.



&#x20;   Special thanks to Red Hat for originally discovering and reporting the

&#x20;   issue!



&#x20;   This version will no longer set a default umask by default, so if unset

&#x20;   the umask of the parent process will be used.



.. \_`CELERYSA-0002`:

&#x20;   https://github.com/celery/celery/tree/master/docs/sec/CELERYSA-0002.txt



News

\----



\- \*\*Requirements\*\*



&#x20;   - Now depends on :ref:`Kombu 3.0.21 <kombu:version-3.0.21>`.



&#x20;   - Now depends on :mod:`billiard` 3.3.0.18.





\- \*\*App\*\*: ``backend`` argument now also sets the :setting:`CELERY\_RESULT\_BACKEND`

&#x20; setting.



\- \*\*Task\*\*: ``signature\_from\_request`` now propagates ``reply\_to`` so that

&#x20; the RPC backend works with retried tasks (Issue #2113).



\- \*\*Task\*\*: ``retry`` will no longer attempt to re-queue the task if sending

&#x20; the retry message fails.



&#x20;   Unrelated exceptions being raised could cause a message loop, so it was

&#x20;   better to remove this behavior.



\- \*\*Beat\*\*: Accounts for standard 1ms drift by always waking up 0.010s

&#x20; earlier.



&#x20;   This will adjust the latency so that the periodic tasks won't move

&#x20;   1ms after every invocation.



\- Documentation fixes



&#x20;   Contributed by Yuval Greenfield, Lucas Wiman, :github\_user:`nicholsonjf`.



\- \*\*Worker\*\*: Removed an outdated assert statement that could lead to errors

&#x20; being masked (Issue #2086).







.. \_version-3.1.12:



3.1.12

======

:release-date: 2014-06-09 10:12 p.m. UTC

:release-by: Ask Solem



\- \*\*Requirements\*\*



&#x20;   Now depends on :ref:`Kombu 3.0.19 <kombu:version-3.0.19>`.



\- \*\*App\*\*: Connections weren't being closed after fork due to an error in the

&#x20; after fork handler (Issue #2055).



&#x20;   This could manifest itself by causing framing errors when using RabbitMQ.

&#x20;   (``Unexpected frame``).



\- \*\*Django\*\*: ``django.setup()`` was being called too late when

&#x20; using Django 1.7 (Issue #1802).



\- \*\*Django\*\*: Fixed problems with event timezones when using Django

&#x20; (``Substantial drift``).



&#x20;   Celery didn't take into account that Django modifies the

&#x20;   ``time.timeone`` attributes and friends.



\- \*\*Canvas\*\*: ``Signature.link`` now works when the link option is a scalar

&#x20; value (Issue #2019).



\- \*\*Prefork pool\*\*: Fixed race conditions for when file descriptors are

&#x20; removed from the event loop.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Roger Hu.



\- \*\*Prefork pool\*\*: Improved solution for dividing tasks between child

&#x20; processes.



&#x20;   This change should improve performance when there are many child

&#x20;   processes, and also decrease the chance that two subsequent tasks are

&#x20;   written to the same child process.



\- \*\*Worker\*\*: Now ignores unknown event types, instead of crashing.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Illes Solt.



\- \*\*Programs\*\*: :program:`celery worker --detach` no longer closes open file

&#x20; descriptors when :envvar:`C\_FAKEFORK` is used so that the workers output

&#x20; can be seen.



\- \*\*Programs\*\*: The default working directory for :program:`celery worker

&#x20; --detach` is now the current working directory, not ``/``.



\- \*\*Canvas\*\*: ``signature(s, app=app)`` didn't upgrade serialized signatures

&#x20; to their original class (``subtask\_type``) when the ``app`` keyword argument

&#x20; was used.



\- \*\*Control\*\*: The ``duplicate nodename`` warning emitted by control commands

&#x20; now shows the duplicate node name.



\- \*\*Tasks\*\*: Can now call ``ResultSet.get()`` on a result set without members.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Alexey Kotlyarov.



\- \*\*App\*\*: Fixed strange traceback mangling issue for

&#x20; ``app.connection\_or\_acquire``.



\- \*\*Programs\*\*: The :program:`celery multi stopwait` command is now documented

&#x20; in usage.



\- \*\*Other\*\*: Fixed cleanup problem with ``PromiseProxy`` when an error is

&#x20; raised while trying to evaluate the promise.



\- \*\*Other\*\*: The utility used to censor configuration values now handles

&#x20; non-string keys.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Luke Pomfrey.



\- \*\*Other\*\*: The ``inspect conf`` command didn't handle non-string keys well.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Jay Farrimond.



\- \*\*Programs\*\*: Fixed argument handling problem in

&#x20; :program:`celery worker --detach`.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Dmitry Malinovsky.



\- \*\*Programs\*\*: :program:`celery worker --detach` didn't forward working

&#x20; directory option (Issue #2003).



\- \*\*Programs\*\*: :program:`celery inspect registered` no longer includes

&#x20; the list of built-in tasks.



\- \*\*Worker\*\*: The ``requires`` attribute for boot steps weren't being handled

&#x20; correctly (Issue #2002).



\- \*\*Eventlet\*\*: The eventlet pool now supports the ``pool\_grow`` and

&#x20; ``pool\_shrink`` remote control commands.



&#x20;   Contributed by Mher Movsisyan.



\- \*\*Eventlet\*\*: The eventlet pool now implements statistics for

&#x20; :program:``celery inspect stats``.



&#x20;   Contributed by Mher Movsisyan.



\- \*\*Documentation\*\*: Clarified ``Task.rate\_limit`` behavior.



&#x20;   Contributed by Jonas Haag.



\- \*\*Documentation\*\*: ``AbortableTask`` examples now updated to use the new

&#x20; API (Issue #1993).



\- \*\*Documentation\*\*: The security documentation examples used an out of date

&#x20; import.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Ian Dees.



\- \*\*Init-scripts\*\*: The CentOS init-scripts didn't quote

&#x20; :envvar:`CELERY\_CHDIR`.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by :github\_user:`ffeast`.



.. \_version-3.1.11:



3.1.11

======

:release-date: 2014-04-16 11:00 p.m. UTC

:release-by: Ask Solem



\- \*\*Now compatible with RabbitMQ 3.3.0\*\*



&#x20;   You need to run Celery 3.1.11 or later when using RabbitMQ 3.3,

&#x20;   and if you use the ``librabbitmq`` module you also have to upgrade

&#x20;   to librabbitmq 1.5.0:



&#x20;   .. code-block:: bash



&#x20;       $ pip install -U librabbitmq



\- \*\*Requirements\*\*:



&#x20;   - Now depends on :ref:`Kombu 3.0.15 <kombu:version-3.0.15>`.



&#x20;   - Now depends on `billiard 3.3.0.17`\_.



&#x20;   - Bundle ``celery\[librabbitmq]`` now depends on :mod:`librabbitmq` 1.5.0.



.. \_`billiard 3.3.0.17`:

&#x20;   https://github.com/celery/billiard/blob/master/CHANGES.txt



\- \*\*Tasks\*\*: The :setting:`CELERY\_DEFAULT\_DELIVERY\_MODE` setting was being

&#x20; ignored (Issue #1953).



\- \*\*Worker\*\*: New :option:`celery worker --heartbeat-interval` can be used

&#x20; to change the time (in seconds) between sending event heartbeats.



&#x20;   Contributed by Matthew Duggan and Craig Northway.



\- \*\*App\*\*: Fixed memory leaks occurring when creating lots of temporary

&#x20; app instances (Issue #1949).



\- \*\*MongoDB\*\*: SSL configuration with non-MongoDB transport breaks MongoDB

&#x20; results backend (Issue #1973).



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Brian Bouterse.



\- \*\*Logging\*\*: The color formatter accidentally modified ``record.msg``

&#x20; (Issue #1939).



\- \*\*Results\*\*: Fixed problem with task trails being stored multiple times,

&#x20; causing ``result.collect()`` to hang (Issue #1936, Issue #1943).



\- \*\*Results\*\*: ``ResultSet`` now implements a ``.backend`` attribute for

&#x20; compatibility with ``AsyncResult``.



\- \*\*Results\*\*: ``.forget()`` now also clears the local cache.



\- \*\*Results\*\*: Fixed problem with multiple calls to ``result.\_set\_cache``

&#x20; (Issue #1940).



\- \*\*Results\*\*: ``join\_native`` populated result cache even if disabled.



\- \*\*Results\*\*: The YAML result serializer should now be able to handle storing

&#x20; exceptions.



\- \*\*Worker\*\*: No longer sends task error emails for expected errors (in

&#x20; ``@task(throws=(..., )))``.



\- \*\*Canvas\*\*: Fixed problem with exception deserialization when using

&#x20; the JSON serializer (Issue #1987).



\- \*\*Eventlet\*\*: Fixes crash when ``celery.contrib.batches`` attempted to

&#x20; cancel a non-existing timer (Issue #1984).



\- Can now import ``celery.version\_info\_t``, and ``celery.five`` (Issue #1968).





.. \_version-3.1.10:



3.1.10

======

:release-date: 2014-03-22 09:40 p.m. UTC

:release-by: Ask Solem



\- \*\*Requirements\*\*:



&#x20;   - Now depends on :ref:`Kombu 3.0.14 <kombu:version-3.0.14>`.



\- \*\*Results\*\*:



&#x20;   Reliability improvements to the SQLAlchemy database backend. Previously the

&#x20;   connection from the MainProcess was improperly shared with the workers.

&#x20;   (Issue #1786)



\- \*\*Redis:\*\* Important note about events (Issue #1882).



&#x20;   There's a new transport option for Redis that enables monitors

&#x20;   to filter out unwanted events. Enabling this option in the workers

&#x20;   will increase performance considerably:



&#x20;   .. code-block:: python



&#x20;       BROKER\_TRANSPORT\_OPTIONS = {'fanout\_patterns': True}



&#x20;   Enabling this option means that your workers won't be able to see

&#x20;   workers with the option disabled (or is running an older version of

&#x20;   Celery), so if you do enable it then make sure you do so on all

&#x20;   nodes.



&#x20;   See :ref:`redis-caveats`.



&#x20;   This will be the default in Celery 3.2.



\- \*\*Results\*\*: The :class:`@AsyncResult` object now keeps a local cache

&#x20; of the final state of the task.



&#x20;   This means that the global result cache can finally be disabled,

&#x20;   and you can do so by setting :setting:`CELERY\_MAX\_CACHED\_RESULTS` to

&#x20;   :const:`-1`. The lifetime of the cache will then be bound to the

&#x20;   lifetime of the result object, which will be the default behavior

&#x20;   in Celery 3.2.



\- \*\*Events\*\*: The "Substantial drift" warning message is now logged once

&#x20; per node name only (Issue #1802).



\- \*\*Worker\*\*: Ability to use one log file per child process when using the

&#x20; prefork pool.



&#x20;   This can be enabled by using the new ``%i`` and ``%I`` format specifiers

&#x20;   for the log file name. See :ref:`worker-files-process-index`.



\- \*\*Redis\*\*: New experimental chord join implementation.



&#x20;   This is an optimization for chords when using the Redis result backend,

&#x20;   where the join operation is now considerably faster and using less

&#x20;   resources than the previous strategy.



&#x20;   The new option can be set in the result backend URL:



&#x20;   .. code-block:: python



&#x20;       CELERY\_RESULT\_BACKEND = 'redis://localhost?new\_join=1'



&#x20;   This must be enabled manually as it's incompatible

&#x20;   with workers and clients not using it, so be sure to enable

&#x20;   the option in all clients and workers if you decide to use it.



\- \*\*Multi\*\*: With ``-opt:index`` (e.g., ``-c:1``) the index now always refers

&#x20; to the position of a node in the argument list.



&#x20;   This means that referring to a number will work when specifying a list

&#x20;   of node names and not just for a number range:



&#x20;   .. code-block:: bash



&#x20;       celery multi start A B C D -c:1 4 -c:2-4 8



&#x20;   In this example ``1`` refers to node A (as it's the first node in the

&#x20;   list).



\- \*\*Signals\*\*: The sender argument to ``Signal.connect`` can now be a proxy

&#x20; object, which means that it can be used with the task decorator

&#x20; (Issue #1873).



\- \*\*Task\*\*: A regression caused the ``queue`` argument to ``Task.retry`` to be

&#x20; ignored (Issue #1892).



\- \*\*App\*\*: Fixed error message for :meth:`\~@Celery.config\_from\_envvar`.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Dmitry Malinovsky.



\- \*\*Canvas\*\*: Chords can now contain a group of other chords (Issue #1921).



\- \*\*Canvas\*\*: Chords can now be combined when using the amqp result backend

&#x20; (a chord where the callback is also a chord).



\- \*\*Canvas\*\*: Calling ``result.get()`` for a chain task will now complete

&#x20; even if one of the tasks in the chain is ``ignore\_result=True``

&#x20; (Issue #1905).



\- \*\*Canvas\*\*: Worker now also logs chord errors.



\- \*\*Canvas\*\*: A chord task raising an exception will now result in

&#x20; any errbacks (``link\_error``) to the chord callback to also be called.



\- \*\*Results\*\*: Reliability improvements to the SQLAlchemy database backend

&#x20; (Issue #1786).



&#x20;   Previously the connection from the ``MainProcess`` was improperly

&#x20;   inherited by child processes.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Ionel Cristian Mărieș.



\- \*\*Task\*\*: Task callbacks and errbacks are now called using the group

&#x20; primitive.



\- \*\*Task\*\*: ``Task.apply`` now properly sets ``request.headers``

&#x20; (Issue #1874).



\- \*\*Worker\*\*: Fixed :exc:`UnicodeEncodeError` occurring when worker is started

&#x20; by :pypi:`supervisor`.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Codeb Fan.



\- \*\*Beat\*\*: No longer attempts to upgrade a newly created database file

&#x20; (Issue #1923).



\- \*\*Beat\*\*: New setting :setting:``CELERYBEAT\_SYNC\_EVERY`` can be be used

&#x20; to control file sync by specifying the number of tasks to send between

&#x20; each sync.



&#x20;   Contributed by Chris Clark.



\- \*\*Commands\*\*: :program:`celery inspect memdump` no longer crashes

&#x20; if the :mod:`psutil` module isn't installed (Issue #1914).



\- \*\*Worker\*\*: Remote control commands now always accepts json serialized

&#x20; messages (Issue #1870).



\- \*\*Worker\*\*: Gossip will now drop any task related events it receives

&#x20; by mistake (Issue #1882).





.. \_version-3.1.9:



3.1.9

=====

:release-date: 2014-02-10 06:43 p.m. UTC

:release-by: Ask Solem



\- \*\*Requirements\*\*:



&#x20;   - Now depends on :ref:`Kombu 3.0.12 <kombu:version-3.0.12>`.



\- \*\*Prefork pool\*\*: Better handling of exiting child processes.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Ionel Cristian Mărieș.



\- \*\*Prefork pool\*\*: Now makes sure all file descriptors are removed

&#x20; from the hub when a process is cleaned up.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Ionel Cristian Mărieș.



\- \*\*New Sphinx extension\*\*: for autodoc documentation of tasks:

&#x20; :mod:`celery.contrib.spinx` (Issue #1833).



\- \*\*Django\*\*: Now works with Django 1.7a1.



\- \*\*Task\*\*: Task.backend is now a property that forwards to ``app.backend``

&#x20; if no custom backend has been specified for the task (Issue #1821).



\- \*\*Generic init-scripts\*\*: Fixed bug in stop command.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Rinat Shigapov.



\- \*\*Generic init-scripts\*\*: Fixed compatibility with GNU :manpage:`stat`.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Paul Kilgo.



\- \*\*Generic init-scripts\*\*: Fixed compatibility with the minimal

&#x20; :program:`dash` shell (Issue #1815).



\- \*\*Commands\*\*: The :program:`celery amqp basic.publish` command wasn't

&#x20; working properly.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Andrey Voronov.



\- \*\*Commands\*\*: Did no longer emit an error message if the pidfile exists

&#x20; and the process is still alive (Issue #1855).



\- \*\*Commands\*\*: Better error message for missing arguments to preload

&#x20; options (Issue #1860).



\- \*\*Commands\*\*: :program:`celery -h` didn't work because of a bug in the

&#x20; argument parser (Issue #1849).



\- \*\*Worker\*\*: Improved error message for message decoding errors.



\- \*\*Time\*\*: Now properly parses the `Z` timezone specifier in ISO 8601 date

&#x20; strings.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Martin Davidsson.



\- \*\*Worker\*\*: Now uses the \*negotiated\* heartbeat value to calculate

&#x20; how often to run the heartbeat checks.



\- \*\*Beat\*\*: Fixed problem with beat hanging after the first schedule

&#x20; iteration (Issue #1822).



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Roger Hu.



\- \*\*Signals\*\*: The header argument to :signal:`before\_task\_publish` is now

&#x20; always a dictionary instance so that signal handlers can add headers.



\- \*\*Worker\*\*: A list of message headers is now included in message related

&#x20; errors.



.. \_version-3.1.8:



3.1.8

=====

:release-date: 2014-01-17 10:45 p.m. UTC

:release-by: Ask Solem



\- \*\*Requirements\*\*:



&#x20;   - Now depends on :ref:`Kombu 3.0.10 <kombu:version-3.0.10>`.



&#x20;   - Now depends on `billiard 3.3.0.14`\_.



.. \_`billiard 3.3.0.14`:

&#x20;   https://github.com/celery/billiard/blob/master/CHANGES.txt



\- \*\*Worker\*\*: The event loop wasn't properly reinitialized at consumer restart

&#x20; which would force the worker to continue with a closed ``epoll`` instance on

&#x20; Linux, resulting in a crash.



\- \*\*Events:\*\* Fixed issue with both heartbeats and task events that could

&#x20; result in the data not being kept in sorted order.



&#x20;   As a result this would force the worker to log "heartbeat missed"

&#x20;   events even though the remote node was sending heartbeats in a timely manner.



\- \*\*Results:\*\* The pickle serializer no longer converts group results to tuples,

&#x20; and will keep the original type (\*Issue #1750\*).



\- \*\*Results:\*\* ``ResultSet.iterate`` is now pending deprecation.



&#x20;   The method will be deprecated in version 3.2 and removed in version 3.3.



&#x20;   Use ``result.get(callback=)`` (or ``result.iter\_native()`` where available)

&#x20;   instead.



\- \*\*Worker\*\*\\|eventlet/gevent: A regression caused :kbd:`Control-c` to be

&#x20; ineffective for shutdown.



\- \*\*Redis result backend:\*\* Now using a pipeline to store state changes

&#x20; for improved performance.



&#x20;   Contributed by Pepijn de Vos.



\- \*\*Redis result backend:\*\* Will now retry storing the result if disconnected.



\- \*\*Worker\*\*\\|gossip: Fixed attribute error occurring when another node leaves.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Brodie Rao.



\- \*\*Generic init-scripts:\*\* Now runs a check at start-up to verify

&#x20; that any configuration scripts are owned by root and that they

&#x20; aren't world/group writable.



&#x20;   The init-script configuration is a shell script executed by root,

&#x20;   so this is a preventive measure to ensure that users don't

&#x20;   leave this file vulnerable to changes by unprivileged users.



&#x20;   .. note::



&#x20;       Note that upgrading Celery won't update the init-scripts,

&#x20;       instead you need to manually copy the improved versions from the

&#x20;       source distribution:

&#x20;       https://github.com/celery/celery/tree/3.1/extra/generic-init.d



\- \*\*Commands\*\*: The :program:`celery purge` command now warns that the operation

&#x20; will delete all tasks and prompts the user for confirmation.



&#x20;   A new :option:`-f <celery purge -f>` was added that can be used to disable

&#x20;   interactive mode.



\- \*\*Task\*\*: ``.retry()`` didn't raise the value provided in the ``exc`` argument

&#x20; when called outside of an error context (\*Issue #1755\*).



\- \*\*Commands:\*\* The :program:`celery multi` command didn't forward command

&#x20; line configuration to the target workers.



&#x20;   The change means that multi will forward the special ``--`` argument and

&#x20;   configuration content at the end of the arguments line to the specified

&#x20;   workers.



&#x20;   Example using command-line configuration to set a broker heartbeat

&#x20;   from :program:`celery multi`:



&#x20;   .. code-block:: bash



&#x20;       $ celery multi start 1 -c3 -- broker.heartbeat=30



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Antoine Legrand.



\- \*\*Canvas:\*\* ``chain.apply\_async()`` now properly forwards execution options.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Konstantin Podshumok.



\- \*\*Redis result backend:\*\* Now takes ``connection\_pool`` argument that can be

&#x20; used to change the connection pool class/constructor.



\- \*\*Worker:\*\* Now truncates very long arguments and keyword arguments logged by

&#x20; the pool at debug severity.



\- \*\*Worker:\*\* The worker now closes all open files on :sig:`SIGHUP` (regression)

&#x20; (\*Issue #1768\*).



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Brodie Rao



\- \*\*Worker:\*\* Will no longer accept remote control commands while the

&#x20; worker start-up phase is incomplete (\*Issue #1741\*).



\- \*\*Commands:\*\* The output of the event dump utility

&#x20; (:program:`celery events -d`) can now be piped into other commands.



\- \*\*Documentation:\*\* The RabbitMQ installation instructions for macOS was

&#x20; updated to use modern Homebrew practices.



&#x20;   Contributed by Jon Chen.



\- \*\*Commands:\*\* The :program:`celery inspect conf` utility now works.



\- \*\*Commands:\*\* The :option:`--no-color <celery --no-color>` argument was

&#x20; not respected by all commands (\*Issue #1799\*).



\- \*\*App:\*\* Fixed rare bug with ``autodiscover\_tasks()`` (\*Issue #1797\*).



\- \*\*Distribution:\*\* The sphinx docs will now always add the parent directory

&#x20; to path so that the current Celery source code is used as a basis for

&#x20; API documentation (\*Issue #1782\*).



\- \*\*Documentation:\*\* :pypi:`supervisor` examples contained an

&#x20; extraneous '-' in a :option:`--logfile <celery worker --logfile>` argument

&#x20; example.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Mohammad Almeer.



.. \_version-3.1.7:



3.1.7

=====

:release-date: 2013-12-17 06:00 p.m. UTC

:release-by: Ask Solem



.. \_v317-important:



Important Notes

\---------------



Init-script security improvements

\---------------------------------



Where the generic init-scripts (for ``celeryd``, and ``celerybeat``) before

delegated the responsibility of dropping privileges to the target application,

it will now use ``su`` instead, so that the Python program isn't trusted

with superuser privileges.



This isn't in reaction to any known exploit, but it will

limit the possibility of a privilege escalation bug being abused in the

future.



You have to upgrade the init-scripts manually from this directory:

https://github.com/celery/celery/tree/3.1/extra/generic-init.d



AMQP result backend

\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~



The 3.1 release accidentally left the amqp backend configured to be

non-persistent by default.



Upgrading from 3.0 would give a "not equivalent" error when attempting to

set or retrieve results for a task. That's unless you manually set the

persistence setting::



&#x20;   CELERY\_RESULT\_PERSISTENT = True



This version restores the previous value so if you already forced

the upgrade by removing the existing exchange you must either

keep the configuration by setting ``CELERY\_RESULT\_PERSISTENT = False``

or delete the ``celeryresults`` exchange again.



Synchronous subtasks

\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~



Tasks waiting for the result of a subtask will now emit

a :exc:`RuntimeWarning` warning when using the prefork pool,

and in 3.2 this will result in an exception being raised.



It's not legal for tasks to block by waiting for subtasks

as this is likely to lead to resource starvation and eventually

deadlock when using the prefork pool (see also :ref:`task-synchronous-subtasks`).



If you really know what you're doing you can avoid the warning (and

the future exception being raised) by moving the operation in a

white-list block:



.. code-block:: python



&#x20;   from celery.result import allow\_join\_result



&#x20;   @app.task

&#x20;   def misbehaving():

&#x20;       result = other\_task.delay()

&#x20;       with allow\_join\_result():

&#x20;           result.get()



Note also that if you wait for the result of a subtask in any form

when using the prefork pool you must also disable the pool prefetching

behavior with the worker :ref:`-Ofair option <optimizing-prefetch-limit>`.



.. \_v317-fixes:



Fixes

\-----



\- Now depends on :ref:`Kombu 3.0.8 <kombu:version-3.0.8>`.



\- Now depends on :mod:`billiard` 3.3.0.13



\- Events: Fixed compatibility with non-standard json libraries

&#x20; that sends float as :class:`decimal.Decimal` (Issue #1731)



\- Events: State worker objects now always defines attributes:

&#x20; ``active``, ``processed``, ``loadavg``, ``sw\_ident``, ``sw\_ver``

&#x20; and ``sw\_sys``.



\- Worker: Now keeps count of the total number of tasks processed,

&#x20; not just by type (``all\_active\_count``).



\- Init-scripts:  Fixed problem with reading configuration file

&#x20; when the init-script is symlinked to a runlevel (e.g., ``S02celeryd``).

&#x20; (Issue #1740).



&#x20;   This also removed a rarely used feature where you can symlink the script

&#x20;   to provide alternative configurations. You instead copy the script

&#x20;   and give it a new name, but perhaps a better solution is to provide

&#x20;   arguments to ``CELERYD\_OPTS`` to separate them:



&#x20;   .. code-block:: bash



&#x20;       CELERYD\_NODES="X1 X2 Y1 Y2"

&#x20;       CELERYD\_OPTS="-A:X1 x -A:X2 x -A:Y1 y -A:Y2 y"



\- Fallback chord unlock task is now always called after the chord header

&#x20; (Issue #1700).



&#x20;   This means that the unlock task won't be started if there's

&#x20;   an error sending the header.



\- Celery command: Fixed problem with arguments for some control commands.



&#x20;   Fix contributed by Konstantin Podshumok.



\- Fixed bug in ``utcoffset`` where the offset when in DST would be

&#x20; completely wrong (Issue #1743).



\- Worker: Errors occurring while attempting to serialize the result of a

&#x20; task will now cause the task to be marked with failure and a
```
