```rst
Celery supports Eventlet as an alternative execution pool implementation and

in some cases superior to prefork. However, you need to ensure one task doesn't

block the event loop too long. Generally, CPU-bound operations don't go well

with Eventlet. Also note that some libraries, usually with C extensions,

cannot be monkeypatched and therefore cannot benefit from using Eventlet.

Please refer to their documentation if you are not sure. For example, pylibmc

does not allow cooperation with Eventlet but psycopg2 does when both of them

are libraries with C extensions.





The prefork pool can take use of multiple processes, but how many is

often limited to a few processes per CPU. With Eventlet you can efficiently

spawn hundreds, or thousands of green threads. In an informal test with a

feed hub system the Eventlet pool could fetch and process hundreds of feeds

every second, while the prefork pool spent 14 seconds processing 100

feeds. Note that this is one of the applications async I/O is especially good

at (asynchronous HTTP requests). You may want a mix of both Eventlet and

prefork workers, and route tasks according to compatibility or

what works best.



Enabling Eventlet

=================



You can enable the Eventlet pool by using the :option:`celery worker -P`

worker option.



.. code-block:: console



&#x20;   $ celery -A proj worker -P eventlet -c 1000



.. \_eventlet-examples:



Examples

========



See the `Eventlet examples`\_ directory in the Celery distribution for

some examples taking use of Eventlet support.



.. \_`Eventlet`: http://eventlet.net

.. \_`epoll(4)`: http://linux.die.net/man/4/epoll

.. \_`libevent`: http://monkey.org/\~provos/libevent/

.. \_`highly scalable non-blocking I/O`:

&#x20;   https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Asynchronous\_I/O#Select.28.2Fpoll.29\_loops

.. \_`Coroutines`: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Coroutine

.. \_`Eventlet examples`:

&#x20;   https://github.com/celery/celery/tree/main/examples/eventlet





```



\### 代码文件: docs\\userguide\\concurrency\\gevent.rst

```rst

.. \_concurrency-eventlet:



===========================

&#x20;Concurrency with gevent

===========================



.. \_gevent-introduction:



Introduction

============



The `gevent`\_ homepage describes it a coroutine\_ -based Python\_ networking library that uses

`greenlet <https://greenlet.readthedocs.io>`\_ to provide a high-level synchronous API on top of the `libev`\_

or `libuv`\_ event loop.



Features include:



\* Fast event loop based on `libev`\_ or `libuv`\_.

\* Lightweight execution units based on greenlets.

\* API that reuses concepts from the Python standard library (for

&#x20; examples there are `events`\_ and

&#x20; `queues`\_).

\* `Cooperative sockets with SSL support <http://www.gevent.org/api/index.html#networking>`\_

\* `Cooperative DNS queries <http://www.gevent.org/dns.html>`\_ performed through a threadpool,

&#x20; dnspython, or c-ares.

\* `Monkey patching utility <http://www.gevent.org/intro.html#monkey-patching>`\_ to get 3rd party modules to become cooperative

\* TCP/UDP/HTTP servers

\* Subprocess support (through `gevent.subprocess`\_)

\* Thread pools



gevent is `inspired by eventlet`\_ but features a more consistent API,

simpler implementation and better performance. Read why others `use

gevent`\_ and check out the list of the `open source projects based on

gevent`\_.





Enabling gevent

=================



You can enable the gevent pool by using the

:option:`celery worker -P gevent` or  :option:`celery worker --pool=gevent`

worker option.



.. code-block:: console



&#x20;   $ celery -A proj worker -P gevent -c 1000



.. \_eventlet-examples:



Examples

========



See the `gevent examples`\_ directory in the Celery distribution for

some examples taking use of Eventlet support.



Known issues

============

There is a known issue using python 3.11 and gevent.

The issue is documented `here`\_ and addressed in a `gevent issue`\_.

Upgrading to greenlet 3.0 solves it.



.. \_events: http://www.gevent.org/api/gevent.event.html#gevent.event.Event

.. \_queues: http://www.gevent.org/api/gevent.queue.html#gevent.queue.Queue

.. \_`gevent`: http://www.gevent.org/

.. \_`gevent examples`:

&#x20;   https://github.com/celery/celery/tree/main/examples/gevent

.. \_gevent.subprocess: http://www.gevent.org/api/gevent.subprocess.html#module-gevent.subprocess



.. \_coroutine: https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Coroutine

.. \_Python: http://python.org

.. \_libev: http://software.schmorp.de/pkg/libev.html

.. \_libuv: http://libuv.org

.. \_inspired by eventlet: http://blog.gevent.org/2010/02/27/why-gevent/

.. \_use gevent: http://groups.google.com/group/gevent/browse\_thread/thread/4de9703e5dca8271

.. \_open source projects based on gevent: https://github.com/gevent/gevent/wiki/Projects

.. \_what's new: http://www.gevent.org/whatsnew\_1\_5.html

.. \_changelog: http://www.gevent.org/changelog.html

.. \_here: https://github.com/celery/celery/issues/8425

.. \_gevent issue: https://github.com/gevent/gevent/issues/1985



```



\### 代码文件: docs\\userguide\\concurrency\\index.rst

```rst

.. \_concurrency:



=============

&#x20;Concurrency

=============



:Release: |version|

:Date: |today|



Concurrency in Celery enables the parallel execution of tasks. The default

model, `prefork`, is well-suited for many scenarios and generally recommended

for most users.  In fact, switching to another mode will silently disable

certain features like `soft\_timeout` and `max\_tasks\_per\_child`.



This page gives a quick overview of the available options which you can pick

between using the `--pool` option when starting the worker.



Overview of Concurrency Options

\-------------------------------



\- `prefork`: The default option, ideal for CPU-bound tasks and most use cases.

&#x20; It is robust and recommended unless there's a specific need for another model.

\- `eventlet` and `gevent`: Designed for IO-bound tasks, these models use

&#x20; greenlets for high concurrency. Note that certain features, like `soft\_timeout`,

&#x20; are not available in these modes.  These have detailed documentation pages

&#x20; linked below.

\- `solo`: Executes tasks sequentially in the main thread.

\- `threads`: Utilizes threading for concurrency, available if the

&#x20; `concurrent.futures` module is present.

\- `custom`: Enables specifying a custom worker pool implementation through

&#x20; environment variables.



.. toctree::

&#x20;   :maxdepth: 2



&#x20;   eventlet

&#x20;   gevent



.. note::

&#x20;   While alternative models like `eventlet` and `gevent` are available, they

&#x20;   may lack certain features compared to `prefork`. We recommend `prefork` as

&#x20;   the starting point unless specific requirements dictate otherwise.



```



\### 代码文件: docs\\\_ext\\celerydocs.py

```python

import typing



from docutils import nodes

from sphinx.errors import NoUri



APPATTRS = {

&#x20;   'amqp': 'celery.app.amqp.AMQP',

&#x20;   'backend': 'celery.backends.base.BaseBackend',

&#x20;   'conf': 'celery.app.utils.Settings',

&#x20;   'control': 'celery.app.control.Control',

&#x20;   'events': 'celery.events.Events',

&#x20;   'loader': 'celery.app.loaders.base.BaseLoader',

&#x20;   'log': 'celery.app.log.Logging',

&#x20;   'pool': 'kombu.connection.ConnectionPool',

&#x20;   'tasks': 'celery.app.registry.Registry',



&#x20;   'AsyncResult': 'celery.result.AsyncResult',

&#x20;   'ResultSet': 'celery.result.ResultSet',

&#x20;   'GroupResult': 'celery.result.GroupResult',

&#x20;   'Worker': 'celery.apps.worker.Worker',

&#x20;   'WorkController': 'celery.worker.WorkController',

&#x20;   'Beat': 'celery.apps.beat.Beat',

&#x20;   'Task': 'celery.app.task.Task',

&#x20;   'signature': 'celery.canvas.Signature',

}



APPDIRECT = {

&#x20;   'on\_configure', 'on\_after\_configure', 'on\_after\_finalize',

&#x20;   'set\_current', 'set\_default', 'close', 'on\_init', 'start',

&#x20;   'worker\_main', 'task', 'gen\_task\_name', 'finalize',

&#x20;   'add\_defaults', 'config\_from\_object', 'config\_from\_envvar',

&#x20;   'config\_from\_cmdline', 'setup\_security', 'autodiscover\_tasks',

&#x20;   'send\_task', 'connection', 'connection\_or\_acquire',

&#x20;   'producer\_or\_acquire', 'prepare\_config', 'now',

&#x20;   'select\_queues', 'either', 'bugreport', 'create\_task\_cls',

&#x20;   'subclass\_with\_self', 'annotations', 'current\_task', 'oid',

&#x20;   'timezone', '\_\_reduce\_keys\_\_', 'fixups', 'finalized', 'configured',

&#x20;   'add\_periodic\_task',

&#x20;   'autofinalize', 'steps', 'user\_options', 'main', 'clock',

}



APPATTRS.update({x: f'celery.Celery.{x}' for x in APPDIRECT})



ABBRS = {

&#x20;   'Celery': 'celery.Celery',

}



ABBR\_EMPTY = {

&#x20;   'exc': 'celery.exceptions',

}

DEFAULT\_EMPTY = 'celery.Celery'





def typeify(S, type):

&#x20;   if type in ('meth', 'func'):

&#x20;       return S + '()'

&#x20;   return S





def shorten(S, newtarget, src\_dict):

&#x20;   if S.startswith('@-'):

&#x20;       return S\[2:]

&#x20;   elif S.startswith('@'):

&#x20;       if src\_dict is APPATTRS:

&#x20;           return '.'.join(\['app', S\[1:]])

&#x20;       return S\[1:]

&#x20;   return S





def get\_abbr(pre, rest, type, orig=None):

&#x20;   if pre:

&#x20;       for d in APPATTRS, ABBRS:

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               return d\[pre], rest, d

&#x20;           except KeyError:

&#x20;               pass

&#x20;       raise KeyError('Unknown abbreviation: {} ({})'.format(

&#x20;           '.'.join(\[pre, rest]) if orig is None else orig, type,

&#x20;       ))

&#x20;   else:

&#x20;       for d in APPATTRS, ABBRS:

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               return d\[rest], '', d

&#x20;           except KeyError:

&#x20;               pass

&#x20;   return ABBR\_EMPTY.get(type, DEFAULT\_EMPTY), rest, ABBR\_EMPTY





def resolve(S, type):

&#x20;   if '.' not in S:

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           getattr(typing, S)

&#x20;       except AttributeError:

&#x20;           pass

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           return f'typing.{S}', None

&#x20;   orig = S

&#x20;   if S.startswith('@'):

&#x20;       S = S.lstrip('@-')

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           pre, rest = S.split('.', 1)

&#x20;       except ValueError:

&#x20;           pre, rest = '', S



&#x20;       target, rest, src = get\_abbr(pre, rest, type, orig)

&#x20;       return '.'.join(\[target, rest]) if rest else target, src

&#x20;   return S, None





def pkg\_of(module\_fqdn):

&#x20;   return module\_fqdn.split('.', 1)\[0]





def basename(module\_fqdn):

&#x20;   return module\_fqdn.lstrip('@').rsplit('.', -1)\[-1]





def modify\_textnode(T, newtarget, node, src\_dict, type):

&#x20;   src = node.children\[0].rawsource

&#x20;   return nodes.Text(

&#x20;       (typeify(basename(T), type) if '\~' in src

&#x20;        else typeify(shorten(T, newtarget, src\_dict), type)),

&#x20;       src,

&#x20;   )





def maybe\_resolve\_abbreviations(app, env, node, contnode):

&#x20;   domainname = node.get('refdomain')

&#x20;   target = node\['reftarget']

&#x20;   type = node\['reftype']

&#x20;   if target.startswith('@'):

&#x20;       newtarget, src\_dict = resolve(target, type)

&#x20;       node\['reftarget'] = newtarget

&#x20;       # shorten text if '\~' is not enabled.

&#x20;       if len(contnode) and isinstance(contnode\[0], nodes.Text):

&#x20;           contnode\[0] = modify\_textnode(target, newtarget, node,

&#x20;                                         src\_dict, type)

&#x20;       if domainname:

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               domain = env.domains\[node.get('refdomain')]

&#x20;           except KeyError:

&#x20;               raise NoUri

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               return domain.resolve\_xref(env, node\['refdoc'], app.builder,

&#x20;                                          type, newtarget,

&#x20;                                          node, contnode)

&#x20;           except KeyError:

&#x20;               raise NoUri





def setup(app):

&#x20;   app.connect(

&#x20;       'missing-reference',

&#x20;       maybe\_resolve\_abbreviations,

&#x20;   )



&#x20;   app.add\_crossref\_type(

&#x20;       directivename='sig',

&#x20;       rolename='sig',

&#x20;       indextemplate='pair: %s; sig',

&#x20;   )

&#x20;   app.add\_crossref\_type(

&#x20;       directivename='state',

&#x20;       rolename='state',

&#x20;       indextemplate='pair: %s; state',

&#x20;   )

&#x20;   app.add\_crossref\_type(

&#x20;       directivename='control',

&#x20;       rolename='control',

&#x20;       indextemplate='pair: %s; control',

&#x20;   )

&#x20;   app.add\_crossref\_type(

&#x20;       directivename='event',

&#x20;       rolename='event',

&#x20;       indextemplate='pair: %s; event',

&#x20;   )



&#x20;   return {

&#x20;       'parallel\_read\_safe': True

&#x20;   }



```

# celery 项目代码文件大全 





\### 代码文件: celery\\beat.py

```python

"""The periodic task scheduler."""



import copy

import dbm

import errno

import heapq

import os

import shelve

import sys

import time

import traceback

from calendar import timegm

from collections import namedtuple

from functools import total\_ordering

from threading import Event, Thread



from billiard import ensure\_multiprocessing

from billiard.common import reset\_signals

from billiard.context import Process

from kombu.utils.functional import maybe\_evaluate, reprcall

from kombu.utils.objects import cached\_property



from . import \_\_version\_\_, platforms, signals

from .exceptions import reraise

from .schedules import crontab, maybe\_schedule

from .utils.functional import is\_numeric\_value

from .utils.imports import load\_extension\_class\_names, symbol\_by\_name

from .utils.log import get\_logger, iter\_open\_logger\_fds

from .utils.time import humanize\_seconds, maybe\_make\_aware



\_\_all\_\_ = (

&#x20;   'SchedulingError', 'ScheduleEntry', 'Scheduler',

&#x20;   'PersistentScheduler', 'Service', 'EmbeddedService',

)



event\_t = namedtuple('event\_t', ('time', 'priority', 'entry'))



logger = get\_logger(\_\_name\_\_)

debug, info, error, warning = (logger.debug, logger.info,

&#x20;                              logger.error, logger.warning)



DEFAULT\_MAX\_INTERVAL = 300  # 5 minutes





class SchedulingError(Exception):

&#x20;   """An error occurred while scheduling a task."""





class BeatLazyFunc:

&#x20;   """A lazy function declared in 'beat\_schedule' and called before sending to worker.



&#x20;   Example:



&#x20;       beat\_schedule = {

&#x20;           'test-every-5-minutes': {

&#x20;               'task': 'test',

&#x20;               'schedule': 300,

&#x20;               'kwargs': {

&#x20;                   "current": BeatCallBack(datetime.datetime.now)

&#x20;               }

&#x20;           }

&#x20;       }



&#x20;   """



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, func, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       self.\_func = func

&#x20;       self.\_func\_params = {

&#x20;           "args": args,

&#x20;           "kwargs": kwargs

&#x20;       }



&#x20;   def \_\_call\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return self.delay()



&#x20;   def delay(self):

&#x20;       return self.\_func(\*self.\_func\_params\["args"], \*\*self.\_func\_params\["kwargs"])





@total\_ordering

class ScheduleEntry:

&#x20;   """An entry in the scheduler.



&#x20;   Arguments:

&#x20;       name (str): see :attr:`name`.

&#x20;       schedule (\~celery.schedules.schedule): see :attr:`schedule`.

&#x20;       args (Tuple): see :attr:`args`.

&#x20;       kwargs (Dict): see :attr:`kwargs`.

&#x20;       options (Dict): see :attr:`options`.

&#x20;       last\_run\_at (\~datetime.datetime): see :attr:`last\_run\_at`.

&#x20;       total\_run\_count (int): see :attr:`total\_run\_count`.

&#x20;       relative (bool): Is the time relative to when the server starts?

&#x20;   """



&#x20;   #: The task name

&#x20;   name = None



&#x20;   #: The schedule (:class:`\~celery.schedules.schedule`)

&#x20;   schedule = None



&#x20;   #: Positional arguments to apply.

&#x20;   args = None



&#x20;   #: Keyword arguments to apply.

&#x20;   kwargs = None



&#x20;   #: Task execution options.

&#x20;   options = None



&#x20;   #: The time and date of when this task was last scheduled.

&#x20;   last\_run\_at = None



&#x20;   #: Total number of times this task has been scheduled.

&#x20;   total\_run\_count = 0



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, name=None, task=None, last\_run\_at=None,

&#x20;                total\_run\_count=None, schedule=None, args=(), kwargs=None,

&#x20;                options=None, relative=False, app=None):

&#x20;       self.app = app

&#x20;       self.name = name

&#x20;       self.task = task

&#x20;       self.args = args

&#x20;       self.kwargs = kwargs if kwargs else {}

&#x20;       self.options = options if options else {}

&#x20;       self.schedule = maybe\_schedule(schedule, relative, app=self.app)

&#x20;       self.last\_run\_at = last\_run\_at or self.default\_now()

&#x20;       self.total\_run\_count = total\_run\_count or 0



&#x20;   def default\_now(self):

&#x20;       return self.schedule.now() if self.schedule else self.app.now()

&#x20;   \_default\_now = default\_now  # compat



&#x20;   def \_next\_instance(self, last\_run\_at=None):

&#x20;       """Return new instance, with date and count fields updated."""

&#x20;       return self.\_\_class\_\_(\*\*dict(

&#x20;           self,

&#x20;           last\_run\_at=last\_run\_at or self.default\_now(),

&#x20;           total\_run\_count=self.total\_run\_count + 1,

&#x20;       ))

&#x20;   \_\_next\_\_ = next = \_next\_instance  # for 2to3



&#x20;   def \_\_reduce\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return self.\_\_class\_\_, (

&#x20;           self.name, self.task, self.last\_run\_at, self.total\_run\_count,

&#x20;           self.schedule, self.args, self.kwargs, self.options,

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def update(self, other):

&#x20;       """Update values from another entry.



&#x20;       Will only update "editable" fields:

&#x20;           ``task``, ``schedule``, ``args``, ``kwargs``, ``options``.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       self.\_\_dict\_\_.update({

&#x20;           'task': other.task, 'schedule': other.schedule,

&#x20;           'args': other.args, 'kwargs': other.kwargs,

&#x20;           'options': other.options,

&#x20;       })



&#x20;   def is\_due(self):

&#x20;       """See :meth:`\~celery.schedules.schedule.is\_due`."""

&#x20;       return self.schedule.is\_due(self.last\_run\_at)



&#x20;   def \_\_iter\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return iter(vars(self).items())



&#x20;   def \_\_repr\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return '<{name}: {0.name} {call} {0.schedule}'.format(

&#x20;           self,

&#x20;           call=reprcall(self.task, self.args or (), self.kwargs or {}),

&#x20;           name=type(self).\_\_name\_\_,

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def \_\_lt\_\_(self, other):

&#x20;       if isinstance(other, ScheduleEntry):

&#x20;           # How the object is ordered doesn't really matter, as

&#x20;           # in the scheduler heap, the order is decided by the

&#x20;           # preceding members of the tuple ``(time, priority, entry)``.

&#x20;           #

&#x20;           # If all that's left to order on is the entry then it can

&#x20;           # just as well be random.

&#x20;           return id(self) < id(other)

&#x20;       return NotImplemented



&#x20;   def editable\_fields\_equal(self, other):

&#x20;       for attr in ('task', 'args', 'kwargs', 'options', 'schedule'):

&#x20;           if getattr(self, attr) != getattr(other, attr):

&#x20;               return False

&#x20;       return True



&#x20;   def \_\_eq\_\_(self, other):

&#x20;       """Test schedule entries equality.



&#x20;       Will only compare "editable" fields:

&#x20;       ``task``, ``schedule``, ``args``, ``kwargs``, ``options``.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.editable\_fields\_equal(other)





def \_evaluate\_entry\_args(entry\_args):

&#x20;   if not entry\_args:

&#x20;       return \[]

&#x20;   return \[

&#x20;       v() if isinstance(v, BeatLazyFunc) else v

&#x20;       for v in entry\_args

&#x20;   ]





def \_evaluate\_entry\_kwargs(entry\_kwargs):

&#x20;   if not entry\_kwargs:

&#x20;       return {}

&#x20;   return {

&#x20;       k: v() if isinstance(v, BeatLazyFunc) else v

&#x20;       for k, v in entry\_kwargs.items()

&#x20;   }





class Scheduler:

&#x20;   """Scheduler for periodic tasks.



&#x20;   The :program:`celery beat` program may instantiate this class

&#x20;   multiple times for introspection purposes, but then with the

&#x20;   ``lazy`` argument set.  It's important for subclasses to

&#x20;   be idempotent when this argument is set.



&#x20;   Arguments:

&#x20;       schedule (\~celery.schedules.schedule): see :attr:`schedule`.

&#x20;       max\_interval (int): see :attr:`max\_interval`.

&#x20;       lazy (bool): Don't set up the schedule.

&#x20;   """



&#x20;   Entry = ScheduleEntry



&#x20;   #: The schedule dict/shelve.

&#x20;   schedule = None



&#x20;   #: Maximum time to sleep between re-checking the schedule.

&#x20;   max\_interval = DEFAULT\_MAX\_INTERVAL



&#x20;   #: How often to sync the schedule (3 minutes by default)

&#x20;   sync\_every = 3 \* 60



&#x20;   #: How many tasks can be called before a sync is forced.

&#x20;   sync\_every\_tasks = None



&#x20;   \_last\_sync = None

&#x20;   \_tasks\_since\_sync = 0



&#x20;   logger = logger  # compat



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, app, schedule=None, max\_interval=None,

&#x20;                Producer=None, lazy=False, sync\_every\_tasks=None, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       self.app = app

&#x20;       self.data = maybe\_evaluate({} if schedule is None else schedule)

&#x20;       self.max\_interval = (max\_interval or

&#x20;                            app.conf.beat\_max\_loop\_interval or

&#x20;                            self.max\_interval)

&#x20;       self.Producer = Producer or app.amqp.Producer

&#x20;       self.\_heap = None

&#x20;       self.old\_schedulers = None

&#x20;       self.sync\_every\_tasks = (

&#x20;           app.conf.beat\_sync\_every if sync\_every\_tasks is None

&#x20;           else sync\_every\_tasks)

&#x20;       if not lazy:

&#x20;           self.setup\_schedule()



&#x20;   def install\_default\_entries(self, data):

&#x20;       entries = {}

&#x20;       if self.app.conf.result\_expires and \\

&#x20;               not self.app.backend.supports\_autoexpire:

&#x20;           if 'celery.backend\_cleanup' not in data:

&#x20;               entries\['celery.backend\_cleanup'] = {

&#x20;                   'task': 'celery.backend\_cleanup',

&#x20;                   'schedule': crontab('0', '4', '\*'),

&#x20;                   'options': {'expires': 12 \* 3600}}

&#x20;       self.update\_from\_dict(entries)



&#x20;   def apply\_entry(self, entry, producer=None):

&#x20;       info('Scheduler: Sending due task %s (%s)', entry.name, entry.task)

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           result = self.apply\_async(entry, producer=producer, advance=False)

&#x20;       except Exception as exc:  # pylint: disable=broad-except

&#x20;           error('Message Error: %s\\n%s',

&#x20;                 exc, traceback.format\_stack(), exc\_info=True)

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           if result and hasattr(result, 'id'):

&#x20;               debug('%s sent. id->%s', entry.task, result.id)

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               debug('%s sent.', entry.task)



&#x20;   def adjust(self, n, drift=-0.010):

&#x20;       if n and n > 0:

&#x20;           return n + drift

&#x20;       return n



&#x20;   def is\_due(self, entry):

&#x20;       return entry.is\_due()



&#x20;   def \_when(self, entry, next\_time\_to\_run, mktime=timegm):

&#x20;       """Return a utc timestamp, make sure heapq in correct order."""

&#x20;       adjust = self.adjust



&#x20;       as\_now = maybe\_make\_aware(entry.default\_now())



&#x20;       return (mktime(as\_now.utctimetuple()) +

&#x20;               as\_now.microsecond / 1e6 +

&#x20;               (adjust(next\_time\_to\_run) or 0))



&#x20;   def populate\_heap(self, event\_t=event\_t, heapify=heapq.heapify):

&#x20;       """Populate the heap with the data contained in the schedule."""

&#x20;       priority = 5

&#x20;       self.\_heap = \[]

&#x20;       for entry in self.schedule.values():

&#x20;           is\_due, next\_call\_delay = entry.is\_due()

&#x20;           self.\_heap.append(event\_t(

&#x20;               self.\_when(

&#x20;                   entry,

&#x20;                   0 if is\_due else next\_call\_delay

&#x20;               ) or 0,

&#x20;               priority, entry

&#x20;           ))

&#x20;       heapify(self.\_heap)



&#x20;   # pylint disable=redefined-outer-name

&#x20;   def tick(self, event\_t=event\_t, min=min, heappop=heapq.heappop,

&#x20;            heappush=heapq.heappush):

&#x20;       """Run a tick - one iteration of the scheduler.



&#x20;       Executes one due task per call.



&#x20;       Returns:

&#x20;           float: preferred delay in seconds for next call.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       adjust = self.adjust

&#x20;       max\_interval = self.max\_interval



&#x20;       if (self.\_heap is None or

&#x20;               not self.schedules\_equal(self.old\_schedulers, self.schedule)):

&#x20;           self.old\_schedulers = copy.copy(self.schedule)

&#x20;           self.populate\_heap()



&#x20;       H = self.\_heap



&#x20;       if not H:

&#x20;           return max\_interval



&#x20;       event = H\[0]

&#x20;       entry = event\[2]

&#x20;       is\_due, next\_time\_to\_run = self.is\_due(entry)

&#x20;       if is\_due:

&#x20;           verify = heappop(H)

&#x20;           if verify is event:

&#x20;               next\_entry = self.reserve(entry)

&#x20;               self.apply\_entry(entry, producer=self.producer)

&#x20;               heappush(H, event\_t(self.\_when(next\_entry, next\_time\_to\_run),

&#x20;                                   event\[1], next\_entry))

&#x20;               return 0

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               heappush(H, verify)

&#x20;               return min(verify\[0], max\_interval)

&#x20;       adjusted\_next\_time\_to\_run = adjust(next\_time\_to\_run)

&#x20;       return min(adjusted\_next\_time\_to\_run if is\_numeric\_value(adjusted\_next\_time\_to\_run) else max\_interval,

&#x20;                  max\_interval)



&#x20;   def schedules\_equal(self, old\_schedules, new\_schedules):

&#x20;       if old\_schedules is new\_schedules is None:

&#x20;           return True

&#x20;       if old\_schedules is None or new\_schedules is None:

&#x20;           return False

&#x20;       if set(old\_schedules.keys()) != set(new\_schedules.keys()):

&#x20;           return False

&#x20;       for name, old\_entry in old\_schedules.items():

&#x20;           new\_entry = new\_schedules.get(name)

&#x20;           if not new\_entry:

&#x20;               return False

&#x20;           if new\_entry != old\_entry:

&#x20;               return False

&#x20;       return True



&#x20;   def should\_sync(self):

&#x20;       return (

&#x20;           (not self.\_last\_sync or

&#x20;            (time.monotonic() - self.\_last\_sync) > self.sync\_every) or

&#x20;           (self.sync\_every\_tasks and

&#x20;            self.\_tasks\_since\_sync >= self.sync\_every\_tasks)

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def reserve(self, entry):

&#x20;       new\_entry = self.schedule\[entry.name] = next(entry)

&#x20;       return new\_entry



&#x20;   def apply\_async(self, entry, producer=None, advance=True, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       # Update time-stamps and run counts before we actually execute,

&#x20;       # so we have that done if an exception is raised (doesn't schedule

&#x20;       # forever.)

&#x20;       entry = self.reserve(entry) if advance else entry

&#x20;       task = self.app.tasks.get(entry.task)



&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           entry\_args = \_evaluate\_entry\_args(entry.args)

&#x20;           entry\_kwargs = \_evaluate\_entry\_kwargs(entry.kwargs)

&#x20;           if task:

&#x20;               return task.apply\_async(entry\_args, entry\_kwargs,

&#x20;                                       producer=producer,

&#x20;                                       \*\*entry.options)

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               return self.send\_task(entry.task, entry\_args, entry\_kwargs,

&#x20;                                     producer=producer,

&#x20;                                     \*\*entry.options)

&#x20;       except Exception as exc:  # pylint: disable=broad-except

&#x20;           reraise(SchedulingError, SchedulingError(

&#x20;               "Couldn't apply scheduled task {0.name}: {exc}".format(

&#x20;                   entry, exc=exc)), sys.exc\_info()\[2])

&#x20;       finally:

&#x20;           self.\_tasks\_since\_sync += 1

&#x20;           if self.should\_sync():

&#x20;               self.\_do\_sync()



&#x20;   def send\_task(self, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       return self.app.send\_task(\*args, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def setup\_schedule(self):

&#x20;       self.install\_default\_entries(self.data)

&#x20;       self.merge\_inplace(self.app.conf.beat\_schedule)



&#x20;   def \_do\_sync(self):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           debug('beat: Synchronizing schedule...')

&#x20;           self.sync()

&#x20;       finally:

&#x20;           self.\_last\_sync = time.monotonic()

&#x20;           self.\_tasks\_since\_sync = 0



&#x20;   def sync(self):

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   def close(self):

&#x20;       self.sync()



&#x20;   def add(self, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       entry = self.Entry(app=self.app, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;       self.schedule\[entry.name] = entry

&#x20;       return entry



&#x20;   def \_maybe\_entry(self, name, entry):

&#x20;       if isinstance(entry, self.Entry):

&#x20;           entry.app = self.app

&#x20;           return entry

&#x20;       return self.Entry(\*\*dict(entry, name=name, app=self.app))



&#x20;   def update\_from\_dict(self, dict\_):

&#x20;       self.schedule.update({

&#x20;           name: self.\_maybe\_entry(name, entry)

&#x20;           for name, entry in dict\_.items()

&#x20;       })



&#x20;   def merge\_inplace(self, b):

&#x20;       schedule = self.schedule

&#x20;       A, B = set(schedule), set(b)



&#x20;       # Remove items from disk not in the schedule anymore.

&#x20;       for key in A ^ B:

&#x20;           schedule.pop(key, None)



&#x20;       # Update and add new items in the schedule

&#x20;       for key in B:

&#x20;           entry = self.Entry(\*\*dict(b\[key], name=key, app=self.app))

&#x20;           if schedule.get(key):

&#x20;               schedule\[key].update(entry)

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               schedule\[key] = entry



&#x20;   def \_ensure\_connected(self):

&#x20;       # callback called for each retry while the connection

&#x20;       # can't be established.

&#x20;       def \_error\_handler(exc, interval):

&#x20;           error('beat: Connection error: %s. '

&#x20;                 'Trying again in %s seconds...', exc, interval)



&#x20;       return self.connection.ensure\_connection(

&#x20;           \_error\_handler, self.app.conf.broker\_connection\_max\_retries

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def get\_schedule(self):

&#x20;       return self.data



&#x20;   def set\_schedule(self, schedule):

&#x20;       self.data = schedule

&#x20;   schedule = property(get\_schedule, set\_schedule)



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def connection(self):

&#x20;       return self.app.connection\_for\_write()



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def producer(self):

&#x20;       return self.Producer(self.\_ensure\_connected(), auto\_declare=False)



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def info(self):

&#x20;       return ''





class PersistentScheduler(Scheduler):

&#x20;   """Scheduler backed by :mod:`shelve` database."""



&#x20;   persistence = shelve

&#x20;   known\_suffixes = ('', '.db', '.dat', '.bak', '.dir')



&#x20;   \_store = None



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       self.schedule\_filename = kwargs.get('schedule\_filename')

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(\*args, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def \_remove\_db(self):

&#x20;       for suffix in self.known\_suffixes:

&#x20;           with platforms.ignore\_errno(errno.ENOENT):

&#x20;               os.remove(self.schedule\_filename + suffix)



&#x20;   def \_open\_schedule(self):

&#x20;       return self.persistence.open(self.schedule\_filename, writeback=True)



&#x20;   def \_destroy\_open\_corrupted\_schedule(self, exc):

&#x20;       error('Removing corrupted schedule file %r: %r',

&#x20;             self.schedule\_filename, exc, exc\_info=True)

&#x20;       self.\_remove\_db()

&#x20;       return self.\_open\_schedule()



&#x20;   def setup\_schedule(self):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           self.\_store = self.\_open\_schedule()

&#x20;           # In some cases there may be different errors from a storage

&#x20;           # backend for corrupted files.  Example - DBPageNotFoundError

&#x20;           # exception from bsddb.  In such case the file will be

&#x20;           # successfully opened but the error will be raised on first key

&#x20;           # retrieving.

&#x20;           self.\_store.keys()

&#x20;       except Exception as exc:  # pylint: disable=broad-except

&#x20;           self.\_store = self.\_destroy\_open\_corrupted\_schedule(exc)



&#x20;       self.\_create\_schedule()



&#x20;       tz = self.app.conf.timezone

&#x20;       stored\_tz = self.\_store.get('tz')

&#x20;       if stored\_tz is not None and stored\_tz != tz:

&#x20;           warning('Reset: Timezone changed from %r to %r', stored\_tz, tz)

&#x20;           self.\_store.clear()   # Timezone changed, reset db!

&#x20;       utc = self.app.conf.enable\_utc

&#x20;       stored\_utc = self.\_store.get('utc\_enabled')

&#x20;       if stored\_utc is not None and stored\_utc != utc:

&#x20;           choices = {True: 'enabled', False: 'disabled'}

&#x20;           warning('Reset: UTC changed from %s to %s',

&#x20;                   choices\[stored\_utc], choices\[utc])

&#x20;           self.\_store.clear()   # UTC setting changed, reset db!

&#x20;       entries = self.\_store.setdefault('entries', {})

&#x20;       self.merge\_inplace(self.app.conf.beat\_schedule)

&#x20;       self.install\_default\_entries(self.schedule)

&#x20;       self.\_store.update({

&#x20;           '\_\_version\_\_': \_\_version\_\_,

&#x20;           'tz': tz,

&#x20;           'utc\_enabled': utc,

&#x20;       })

&#x20;       self.sync()

&#x20;       debug('Current schedule:\\n' + '\\n'.join(

&#x20;           repr(entry) for entry in entries.values()))



&#x20;   def \_create\_schedule(self):

&#x20;       for \_ in (1, 2):

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               self.\_store\['entries']

&#x20;           except (KeyError, UnicodeDecodeError, TypeError):

&#x20;               # new schedule db

&#x20;               try:

&#x20;                   self.\_store\['entries'] = {}

&#x20;               except (KeyError, UnicodeDecodeError, TypeError) + dbm.error as exc:

&#x20;                   self.\_store = self.\_destroy\_open\_corrupted\_schedule(exc)

&#x20;                   continue

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               if '\_\_version\_\_' not in self.\_store:

&#x20;                   warning('DB Reset: Account for new \_\_version\_\_ field')

&#x20;                   self.\_store.clear()   # remove schedule at 2.2.2 upgrade.

&#x20;               elif 'tz' not in self.\_store:

&#x20;                   warning('DB Reset: Account for new tz field')

&#x20;                   self.\_store.clear()   # remove schedule at 3.0.8 upgrade

&#x20;               elif 'utc\_enabled' not in self.\_store:

&#x20;                   warning('DB Reset: Account for new utc\_enabled field')

&#x20;                   self.\_store.clear()   # remove schedule at 3.0.9 upgrade

&#x20;           break



&#x20;   def get\_schedule(self):

&#x20;       return self.\_store\['entries']



&#x20;   def set\_schedule(self, schedule):

&#x20;       self.\_store\['entries'] = schedule

&#x20;   schedule = property(get\_schedule, set\_schedule)



&#x20;   def sync(self):

&#x20;       if self.\_store is not None:

&#x20;           self.\_store.sync()



&#x20;   def close(self):

&#x20;       self.sync()

&#x20;       self.\_store.close()



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def info(self):

&#x20;       return f'    . db -> {self.schedule\_filename}'





class Service:

&#x20;   """Celery periodic task service."""



&#x20;   scheduler\_cls = PersistentScheduler



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, app, max\_interval=None, schedule\_filename=None,

&#x20;                scheduler\_cls=None):

&#x20;       self.app = app

&#x20;       self.max\_interval = (max\_interval or

&#x20;                            app.conf.beat\_max\_loop\_interval)

&#x20;       self.scheduler\_cls = scheduler\_cls or self.scheduler\_cls

&#x20;       self.schedule\_filename = (

&#x20;           schedule\_filename or app.conf.beat\_schedule\_filename)



&#x20;       self.\_is\_shutdown = Event()

&#x20;       self.\_is\_stopped = Event()



&#x20;   def \_\_reduce\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return self.\_\_class\_\_, (self.max\_interval, self.schedule\_filename,

&#x20;                               self.scheduler\_cls, self.app)



&#x20;   def start(self, embedded\_process=False):

&#x20;       info('beat: Starting...')

&#x20;       debug('beat: Ticking with max interval->%s',

&#x20;             humanize\_seconds(self.scheduler.max\_interval))



&#x20;       signals.beat\_init.send(sender=self)

&#x20;       if embedded\_process:

&#x20;           signals.beat\_embedded\_init.send(sender=self)

&#x20;           platforms.set\_process\_title('celery beat')



&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           while not self.\_is\_shutdown.is\_set():

&#x20;               interval = self.scheduler.tick()

&#x20;               if interval and interval > 0.0:

&#x20;                   debug('beat: Waking up %s.',

&#x20;                         humanize\_seconds(interval, prefix='in '))

&#x20;                   time.sleep(interval)

&#x20;                   if self.scheduler.should\_sync():

&#x20;                       self.scheduler.\_do\_sync()

&#x20;       except (KeyboardInterrupt, SystemExit):

&#x20;           self.\_is\_shutdown.set()

&#x20;       finally:

&#x20;           self.sync()



&#x20;   def sync(self):

&#x20;       self.scheduler.close()

&#x20;       self.\_is\_stopped.set()



&#x20;   def stop(self, wait=False):

&#x20;       info('beat: Shutting down...')

&#x20;       self.\_is\_shutdown.set()

&#x20;       wait and self.\_is\_stopped.wait()  # block until shutdown done.



&#x20;   def get\_scheduler(self, lazy=False,

&#x20;                     extension\_namespace='celery.beat\_schedulers'):

&#x20;       filename = self.schedule\_filename

&#x20;       aliases = dict(load\_extension\_class\_names(extension\_namespace))

&#x20;       return symbol\_by\_name(self.scheduler\_cls, aliases=aliases)(

&#x20;           app=self.app,

&#x20;           schedule\_filename=filename,

&#x20;           max\_interval=self.max\_interval,

&#x20;           lazy=lazy,

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def scheduler(self):

&#x20;       return self.get\_scheduler()





class \_Threaded(Thread):

&#x20;   """Embedded task scheduler using threading."""



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, app, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_()

&#x20;       self.app = app

&#x20;       self.service = Service(app, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;       self.daemon = True

&#x20;       self.name = 'Beat'



&#x20;   def run(self):

&#x20;       self.app.set\_current()

&#x20;       self.service.start()



&#x20;   def stop(self):

&#x20;       self.service.stop(wait=True)





try:

&#x20;   ensure\_multiprocessing()

except NotImplementedError:     # pragma: no cover

&#x20;   \_Process = None

else:

&#x20;   class \_Process(Process):



&#x20;       def \_\_init\_\_(self, app, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;           super().\_\_init\_\_()

&#x20;           self.app = app

&#x20;           self.service = Service(app, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;           self.name = 'Beat'



&#x20;       def run(self):

&#x20;           reset\_signals(full=False)

&#x20;           platforms.close\_open\_fds(\[

&#x20;               sys.\_\_stdin\_\_, sys.\_\_stdout\_\_, sys.\_\_stderr\_\_,

&#x20;           ] + list(iter\_open\_logger\_fds()))

&#x20;           self.app.set\_default()

&#x20;           self.app.set\_current()

&#x20;           self.service.start(embedded\_process=True)



&#x20;       def stop(self):

&#x20;           self.service.stop()

&#x20;           self.terminate()





def EmbeddedService(app, max\_interval=None, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;   """Return embedded clock service.



&#x20;   Arguments:

&#x20;       thread (bool): Run threaded instead of as a separate process.

&#x20;           Uses :mod:`multiprocessing` by default, if available.

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   if kwargs.pop('thread', False) or \_Process is None:

&#x20;       # Need short max interval to be able to stop thread

&#x20;       # in reasonable time.

&#x20;       return \_Threaded(app, max\_interval=1, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;   return \_Process(app, max\_interval=max\_interval, \*\*kwargs)



```

```python

"""A directed acyclic graph of reusable components."""



from collections import deque

from threading import Event



from kombu.common import ignore\_errors

from kombu.utils.encoding import bytes\_to\_str

from kombu.utils.imports import symbol\_by\_name



from .utils.graph import DependencyGraph, GraphFormatter

from .utils.imports import instantiate, qualname

from .utils.log import get\_logger



try:

&#x20;   from greenlet import GreenletExit

except ImportError:

&#x20;   IGNORE\_ERRORS = ()

else:

&#x20;   IGNORE\_ERRORS = (GreenletExit,)



\_\_all\_\_ = ('Blueprint', 'Step', 'StartStopStep', 'ConsumerStep')



\#: States

RUN = 0x1

CLOSE = 0x2

TERMINATE = 0x3



logger = get\_logger(\_\_name\_\_)





def \_pre(ns, fmt):

&#x20;   return f'| {ns.alias}: {fmt}'





def \_label(s):

&#x20;   return s.name.rsplit('.', 1)\[-1]





class StepFormatter(GraphFormatter):

&#x20;   """Graph formatter for :class:`Blueprint`."""



&#x20;   blueprint\_prefix = '⧉'

&#x20;   conditional\_prefix = '∘'

&#x20;   blueprint\_scheme = {

&#x20;       'shape': 'parallelogram',

&#x20;       'color': 'slategray4',

&#x20;       'fillcolor': 'slategray3',

&#x20;   }



&#x20;   def label(self, step):

&#x20;       return step and '{}{}'.format(

&#x20;           self.\_get\_prefix(step),

&#x20;           bytes\_to\_str(

&#x20;               (step.label or \_label(step)).encode('utf-8', 'ignore')),

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def \_get\_prefix(self, step):

&#x20;       if step.last:

&#x20;           return self.blueprint\_prefix

&#x20;       if step.conditional:

&#x20;           return self.conditional\_prefix

&#x20;       return ''



&#x20;   def node(self, obj, \*\*attrs):

&#x20;       scheme = self.blueprint\_scheme if obj.last else self.node\_scheme

&#x20;       return self.draw\_node(obj, scheme, attrs)



&#x20;   def edge(self, a, b, \*\*attrs):

&#x20;       if a.last:

&#x20;           attrs.update(arrowhead='none', color='darkseagreen3')

&#x20;       return self.draw\_edge(a, b, self.edge\_scheme, attrs)





class Blueprint:

&#x20;   """Blueprint containing bootsteps that can be applied to objects.



&#x20;   Arguments:

&#x20;       steps Sequence\[Union\[str, Step]]: List of steps.

&#x20;       name (str): Set explicit name for this blueprint.

&#x20;       on\_start (Callable): Optional callback applied after blueprint start.

&#x20;       on\_close (Callable): Optional callback applied before blueprint close.

&#x20;       on\_stopped (Callable): Optional callback applied after

&#x20;           blueprint stopped.

&#x20;   """



&#x20;   GraphFormatter = StepFormatter



&#x20;   name = None

&#x20;   state = None

&#x20;   started = 0

&#x20;   default\_steps = set()

&#x20;   state\_to\_name = {

&#x20;       0: 'initializing',

&#x20;       RUN: 'running',

&#x20;       CLOSE: 'closing',

&#x20;       TERMINATE: 'terminating',

&#x20;   }



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, steps=None, name=None,

&#x20;                on\_start=None, on\_close=None, on\_stopped=None):

&#x20;       self.name = name or self.name or qualname(type(self))

&#x20;       self.types = set(steps or \[]) | set(self.default\_steps)

&#x20;       self.on\_start = on\_start

&#x20;       self.on\_close = on\_close

&#x20;       self.on\_stopped = on\_stopped

&#x20;       self.shutdown\_complete = Event()

&#x20;       self.steps = {}



&#x20;   def start(self, parent):

&#x20;       self.state = RUN

&#x20;       if self.on\_start:

&#x20;           self.on\_start()

&#x20;       for i, step in enumerate(s for s in parent.steps if s is not None):

&#x20;           self.\_debug('Starting %s', step.alias)

&#x20;           self.started = i + 1

&#x20;           step.start(parent)

&#x20;           logger.debug('^-- substep ok')



&#x20;   def human\_state(self):

&#x20;       return self.state\_to\_name\[self.state or 0]



&#x20;   def info(self, parent):

&#x20;       info = {}

&#x20;       for step in parent.steps:

&#x20;           info.update(step.info(parent) or {})

&#x20;       return info



&#x20;   def close(self, parent):

&#x20;       if self.on\_close:

&#x20;           self.on\_close()

&#x20;       self.send\_all(parent, 'close', 'closing', reverse=False)



&#x20;   def restart(self, parent, method='stop',

&#x20;               description='restarting', propagate=False):

&#x20;       self.send\_all(parent, method, description, propagate=propagate)



&#x20;   def send\_all(self, parent, method,

&#x20;                description=None, reverse=True, propagate=True, args=()):

&#x20;       description = description or method.replace('\_', ' ')

&#x20;       steps = reversed(parent.steps) if reverse else parent.steps

&#x20;       for step in steps:

&#x20;           if step:

&#x20;               fun = getattr(step, method, None)

&#x20;               if fun is not None:

&#x20;                   self.\_debug('%s %s...',

&#x20;                               description.capitalize(), step.alias)

&#x20;                   try:

&#x20;                       fun(parent, \*args)

&#x20;                   except Exception as exc:  # pylint: disable=broad-except

&#x20;                       if propagate:

&#x20;                           raise

&#x20;                       logger.exception(

&#x20;                           'Error on %s %s: %r', description, step.alias, exc)



&#x20;   def stop(self, parent, close=True, terminate=False):

&#x20;       what = 'terminating' if terminate else 'stopping'

&#x20;       if self.state in (CLOSE, TERMINATE):

&#x20;           return



&#x20;       if self.state != RUN or self.started != len(parent.steps):

&#x20;           # Not fully started, can safely exit.

&#x20;           self.state = TERMINATE

&#x20;           self.shutdown\_complete.set()

&#x20;           return

&#x20;       self.close(parent)

&#x20;       self.state = CLOSE



&#x20;       self.restart(

&#x20;           parent, 'terminate' if terminate else 'stop',

&#x20;           description=what, propagate=False,

&#x20;       )



&#x20;       if self.on\_stopped:

&#x20;           self.on\_stopped()

&#x20;       self.state = TERMINATE

&#x20;       self.shutdown\_complete.set()



&#x20;   def join(self, timeout=None):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           # Will only get here if running green,

&#x20;           # makes sure all greenthreads have exited.

&#x20;           self.shutdown\_complete.wait(timeout=timeout)

&#x20;       except IGNORE\_ERRORS:

&#x20;           pass



&#x20;   def apply(self, parent, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       """Apply the steps in this blueprint to an object.



&#x20;       This will apply the ``\_\_init\_\_`` and ``include`` methods

&#x20;       of each step, with the object as argument::



&#x20;           step = Step(obj)

&#x20;           ...

&#x20;           step.include(obj)



&#x20;       For :class:`StartStopStep` the services created

&#x20;       will also be added to the objects ``steps`` attribute.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       self.\_debug('Preparing bootsteps.')

&#x20;       order = self.order = \[]

&#x20;       steps = self.steps = self.claim\_steps()



&#x20;       self.\_debug('Building graph...')

&#x20;       for S in self.\_finalize\_steps(steps):

&#x20;           step = S(parent, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;           steps\[step.name] = step

&#x20;           order.append(step)

&#x20;       self.\_debug('New boot order: {%s}',

&#x20;                   ', '.join(s.alias for s in self.order))

&#x20;       for step in order:

&#x20;           step.include(parent)

&#x20;       return self



&#x20;   def connect\_with(self, other):

&#x20;       self.graph.adjacent.update(other.graph.adjacent)

&#x20;       self.graph.add\_edge(type(other.order\[0]), type(self.order\[-1]))



&#x20;   def \_\_getitem\_\_(self, name):

&#x20;       return self.steps\[name]



&#x20;   def \_find\_last(self):

&#x20;       return next((C for C in self.steps.values() if C.last), None)



&#x20;   def \_firstpass(self, steps):

&#x20;       for step in steps.values():

&#x20;           step.requires = \[symbol\_by\_name(dep) for dep in step.requires]

&#x20;       stream = deque(step.requires for step in steps.values())

&#x20;       while stream:

&#x20;           for node in stream.popleft():

&#x20;               node = symbol\_by\_name(node)

&#x20;               if node.name not in self.steps:

&#x20;                   steps\[node.name] = node

&#x20;               stream.append(node.requires)



&#x20;   def \_finalize\_steps(self, steps):

&#x20;       last = self.\_find\_last()

&#x20;       self.\_firstpass(steps)

&#x20;       it = ((C, C.requires) for C in steps.values())

&#x20;       G = self.graph = DependencyGraph(

&#x20;           it, formatter=self.GraphFormatter(root=last),

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       if last:

&#x20;           for obj in G:

&#x20;               if obj != last:

&#x20;                   G.add\_edge(last, obj)

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           return G.topsort()

&#x20;       except KeyError as exc:

&#x20;           raise KeyError('unknown bootstep: %s' % exc)



&#x20;   def claim\_steps(self):

&#x20;       return dict(self.load\_step(step) for step in self.types)



&#x20;   def load\_step(self, step):

&#x20;       step = symbol\_by\_name(step)

&#x20;       return step.name, step



&#x20;   def \_debug(self, msg, \*args):

&#x20;       return logger.debug(\_pre(self, msg), \*args)



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def alias(self):

&#x20;       return \_label(self)





class StepType(type):

&#x20;   """Meta-class for steps."""



&#x20;   name = None

&#x20;   requires = None



&#x20;   def \_\_new\_\_(cls, name, bases, attrs):

&#x20;       module = attrs.get('\_\_module\_\_')

&#x20;       qname = f'{module}.{name}' if module else name

&#x20;       attrs.update(

&#x20;           \_\_qualname\_\_=qname,

&#x20;           name=attrs.get('name') or qname,

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       return super().\_\_new\_\_(cls, name, bases, attrs)



&#x20;   def \_\_str\_\_(cls):

&#x20;       return cls.name



&#x20;   def \_\_repr\_\_(cls):

&#x20;       return 'step:{0.name}{{{0.requires!r}}}'.format(cls)





class Step(metaclass=StepType):

&#x20;   """A Bootstep.



&#x20;   The :meth:`\_\_init\_\_` method is called when the step

&#x20;   is bound to a parent object, and can as such be used

&#x20;   to initialize attributes in the parent object at

&#x20;   parent instantiation-time.

&#x20;   """



&#x20;   #: Optional step name, will use ``qualname`` if not specified.

&#x20;   name = None



&#x20;   #: Optional short name used for graph outputs and in logs.

&#x20;   label = None



&#x20;   #: Set this to true if the step is enabled based on some condition.

&#x20;   conditional = False



&#x20;   #: List of other steps that that must be started before this step.

&#x20;   #: Note that all dependencies must be in the same blueprint.

&#x20;   requires = ()



&#x20;   #: This flag is reserved for the workers Consumer,

&#x20;   #: since it is required to always be started last.

&#x20;   #: There can only be one object marked last

&#x20;   #: in every blueprint.

&#x20;   last = False



&#x20;   #: This provides the default for :meth:`include\_if`.

&#x20;   enabled = True



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, parent, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   def include\_if(self, parent):

&#x20;       """Return true if bootstep should be included.



&#x20;       You can define this as an optional predicate that decides whether

&#x20;       this step should be created.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.enabled



&#x20;   def instantiate(self, name, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       return instantiate(name, \*args, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def \_should\_include(self, parent):

&#x20;       if self.include\_if(parent):

&#x20;           return True, self.create(parent)

&#x20;       return False, None



&#x20;   def include(self, parent):

&#x20;       return self.\_should\_include(parent)\[0]



&#x20;   def create(self, parent):

&#x20;       """Create the step."""



&#x20;   def \_\_repr\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return f'<step: {self.alias}>'



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def alias(self):

&#x20;       return self.label or \_label(self)



&#x20;   def info(self, obj):

&#x20;       pass





class StartStopStep(Step):

&#x20;   """Bootstep that must be started and stopped in order."""



&#x20;   #: Optional obj created by the :meth:`create` method.

&#x20;   #: This is used by :class:`StartStopStep` to keep the

&#x20;   #: original service object.

&#x20;   obj = None



&#x20;   def start(self, parent):

&#x20;       if self.obj:

&#x20;           return self.obj.start()



&#x20;   def stop(self, parent):

&#x20;       if self.obj:

&#x20;           return self.obj.stop()



&#x20;   def close(self, parent):

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   def terminate(self, parent):

&#x20;       if self.obj:

&#x20;           return getattr(self.obj, 'terminate', self.obj.stop)()



&#x20;   def include(self, parent):

&#x20;       inc, ret = self.\_should\_include(parent)

&#x20;       if inc:

&#x20;           self.obj = ret

&#x20;           parent.steps.append(self)

&#x20;       return inc





class ConsumerStep(StartStopStep):

&#x20;   """Bootstep that starts a message consumer."""



&#x20;   requires = ('celery.worker.consumer:Connection',)

&#x20;   consumers = None



&#x20;   def get\_consumers(self, channel):

&#x20;       raise NotImplementedError('missing get\_consumers')



&#x20;   def start(self, c):

&#x20;       channel = c.connection.channel()

&#x20;       self.consumers = self.get\_consumers(channel)

&#x20;       for consumer in self.consumers or \[]:

&#x20;           consumer.consume()



&#x20;   def stop(self, c):

&#x20;       self.\_close(c, True)



&#x20;   def shutdown(self, c):

&#x20;       self.\_close(c, False)



&#x20;   def \_close(self, c, cancel\_consumers=True):

&#x20;       channels = set()

&#x20;       for consumer in self.consumers or \[]:

&#x20;           if cancel\_consumers:

&#x20;               ignore\_errors(c.connection, consumer.cancel)

&#x20;           if consumer.channel:

&#x20;               channels.add(consumer.channel)

&#x20;       for channel in channels:

&#x20;           ignore\_errors(c.connection, channel.close)



```

```python
"""Composing task work-flows.



.. seealso:



&#x20;   You should import these from :mod:`celery` and not this module.

"""



import itertools

import operator

import warnings

from abc import ABCMeta, abstractmethod

from collections import deque

from collections.abc import MutableSequence

from copy import deepcopy

from functools import partial as \_partial

from functools import reduce

from operator import itemgetter

from types import GeneratorType

from kombu.utils.objects import cached\_property

from kombu.utils.uuid import uuid

from celery.\_state import current\_app

from celery.exceptions import CPendingDeprecationWarning

from celery.result import GroupResult, allow\_join\_result

from celery.utils import abstract

from celery.utils.collections import ChainMap

from celery.utils.functional import \_regen

from celery.utils.functional import chunks as \_chunks

from celery.utils.functional import is\_list, maybe\_list, regen, seq\_concat\_item, seq\_concat\_seq

from celery.utils.objects import getitem\_property

from celery.utils.text import remove\_repeating\_from\_task, truncate

&#x20;   'Signature', 'chain', 'xmap', 'xstarmap', 'chunks',

&#x20;   'group', 'chord', 'signature', 'maybe\_signature',

)





def maybe\_unroll\_group(group):

&#x20;   """Unroll group with only one member.

&#x20;   This allows treating a group of a single task as if it

&#x20;   was a single task without pre-knowledge."""

&#x20;   # Issue #1656

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       size = len(group.tasks)

&#x20;   except TypeError:

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           size = group.tasks.\_\_length\_hint\_\_()

&#x20;       except (AttributeError, TypeError):

&#x20;           return group

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           return list(group.tasks)\[0] if size == 1 else group

&#x20;   else:

&#x20;       return group.tasks\[0] if size == 1 else group





def task\_name\_from(task):

&#x20;   return getattr(task, 'name', task)





def \_stamp\_regen\_task(task, visitor, append\_stamps, \*\*headers):

&#x20;   """When stamping a sequence of tasks created by a generator,

&#x20;   we use this function to stamp each task in the generator

&#x20;   without exhausting it."""



&#x20;   task.stamp(visitor, append\_stamps, \*\*headers)

&#x20;   return task





def \_merge\_dictionaries(d1, d2, aggregate\_duplicates=True):

&#x20;   """Merge two dictionaries recursively into the first one.



&#x20;   Example:

&#x20;   >>> d1 = {'dict': {'a': 1}, 'list': \[1, 2], 'tuple': (1, 2)}

&#x20;   >>> d2 = {'dict': {'b': 2}, 'list': \[3, 4], 'set': {'a', 'b'}}

&#x20;   >>> \_merge\_dictionaries(d1, d2)



&#x20;   d1 will be modified to: {

&#x20;       'dict': {'a': 1, 'b': 2},

&#x20;       'list': \[1, 2, 3, 4],

&#x20;       'tuple': (1, 2),

&#x20;       'set': {'a', 'b'}

&#x20;   }



&#x20;   Arguments:

&#x20;       d1 (dict): Dictionary to merge into.

&#x20;       d2 (dict): Dictionary to merge from.

&#x20;       aggregate\_duplicates (bool):

&#x20;           If True, aggregate duplicated items (by key) into a list of all values in d1 in the same key.

&#x20;           If False, duplicate keys will be taken from d2 and override the value in d1.

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   if not d2:

&#x20;       return



&#x20;   for key, value in d1.items():

&#x20;       if key in d2:

&#x20;           if isinstance(value, dict):

&#x20;               \_merge\_dictionaries(d1\[key], d2\[key])

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               if isinstance(value, (int, float, str)):

&#x20;                   d1\[key] = \[value] if aggregate\_duplicates else value

&#x20;               if isinstance(d2\[key], list) and isinstance(d1\[key], list):

&#x20;                   d1\[key].extend(d2\[key])

&#x20;               elif aggregate\_duplicates:

&#x20;                   if d1\[key] is None:

&#x20;                       d1\[key] = \[]

&#x20;                   else:

&#x20;                       d1\[key] = list(d1\[key])

&#x20;                   d1\[key].append(d2\[key])

&#x20;   for key, value in d2.items():

&#x20;       if key not in d1:

&#x20;           d1\[key] = value





class StampingVisitor(metaclass=ABCMeta):

&#x20;   """Stamping API.  A class that provides a stamping API possibility for

&#x20;   canvas primitives. If you want to implement stamping behavior for

&#x20;   a canvas primitive override method that represents it.

&#x20;   """



&#x20;   def on\_group\_start(self, group, \*\*headers) -> dict:

&#x20;       """Method that is called on group stamping start.



&#x20;        Arguments:

&#x20;            group (group): Group that is stamped.

&#x20;            headers (Dict): Partial headers that could be merged with existing headers.

&#x20;        Returns:

&#x20;            Dict: headers to update.

&#x20;        """

&#x20;       return {}



&#x20;   def on\_group\_end(self, group, \*\*headers) -> None:

&#x20;       """Method that is called on group stamping end.



&#x20;        Arguments:

&#x20;            group (group): Group that is stamped.

&#x20;            headers (Dict): Partial headers that could be merged with existing headers.

&#x20;        """

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   def on\_chain\_start(self, chain, \*\*headers) -> dict:

&#x20;       """Method that is called on chain stamping start.



&#x20;        Arguments:

&#x20;            chain (chain): Chain that is stamped.

&#x20;            headers (Dict): Partial headers that could be merged with existing headers.

&#x20;        Returns:

&#x20;            Dict: headers to update.

&#x20;        """

&#x20;       return {}



&#x20;   def on\_chain\_end(self, chain, \*\*headers) -> None:

&#x20;       """Method that is called on chain stamping end.



&#x20;        Arguments:

&#x20;            chain (chain): Chain that is stamped.

&#x20;            headers (Dict): Partial headers that could be merged with existing headers.

&#x20;        """

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   @abstractmethod

&#x20;   def on\_signature(self, sig, \*\*headers) -> dict:

&#x20;       """Method that is called on signature stamping.



&#x20;        Arguments:

&#x20;            sig (Signature): Signature that is stamped.

&#x20;            headers (Dict): Partial headers that could be merged with existing headers.

&#x20;        Returns:

&#x20;            Dict: headers to update.

&#x20;        """



&#x20;   def on\_chord\_header\_start(self, sig, \*\*header) -> dict:

&#x20;       """Method that is called on сhord header stamping start.



&#x20;        Arguments:

&#x20;            sig (chord): chord that is stamped.

&#x20;            headers (Dict): Partial headers that could be merged with existing headers.

&#x20;        Returns:

&#x20;            Dict: headers to update.

&#x20;        """

&#x20;       if not isinstance(sig.tasks, group):

&#x20;           sig.tasks = group(sig.tasks)

&#x20;       return self.on\_group\_start(sig.tasks, \*\*header)



&#x20;   def on\_chord\_header\_end(self, sig, \*\*header) -> None:

&#x20;       """Method that is called on сhord header stamping end.



&#x20;          Arguments:

&#x20;              sig (chord): chord that is stamped.

&#x20;              headers (Dict): Partial headers that could be merged with existing headers.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       self.on\_group\_end(sig.tasks, \*\*header)



&#x20;   def on\_chord\_body(self, sig, \*\*header) -> dict:

&#x20;       """Method that is called on chord body stamping.



&#x20;        Arguments:

&#x20;            sig (chord): chord that is stamped.

&#x20;            headers (Dict): Partial headers that could be merged with existing headers.

&#x20;        Returns:

&#x20;            Dict: headers to update.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return {}



&#x20;   def on\_callback(self, callback, \*\*header) -> dict:

&#x20;       """Method that is called on callback stamping.



&#x20;        Arguments:

&#x20;            callback (Signature): callback that is stamped.

&#x20;            headers (Dict): Partial headers that could be merged with existing headers.

&#x20;        Returns:

&#x20;            Dict: headers to update.

&#x20;        """

&#x20;       return {}



&#x20;   def on\_errback(self, errback, \*\*header) -> dict:

&#x20;       """Method that is called on errback stamping.



&#x20;        Arguments:

&#x20;            errback (Signature): errback that is stamped.

&#x20;            headers (Dict): Partial headers that could be merged with existing headers.

&#x20;        Returns:

&#x20;            Dict: headers to update.

&#x20;        """

&#x20;       return {}





@abstract.CallableSignature.register

class Signature(dict):

&#x20;   """Task Signature.



&#x20;   Class that wraps the arguments and execution options

&#x20;   for a single task invocation.



&#x20;   Used as the parts in a :class:`group` and other constructs,

&#x20;   or to pass tasks around as callbacks while being compatible

&#x20;   with serializers with a strict type subset.



&#x20;   Signatures can also be created from tasks:



&#x20;   - Using the ``.signature()`` method that has the same signature

&#x20;     as ``Task.apply\_async``:



&#x20;       .. code-block:: pycon



&#x20;           >>> add.signature(args=(1,), kwargs={'kw': 2}, options={})



&#x20;   - or the ``.s()`` shortcut that works for star arguments:



&#x20;       .. code-block:: pycon



&#x20;           >>> add.s(1, kw=2)



&#x20;   - the ``.s()`` shortcut does not allow you to specify execution options

&#x20;     but there's a chaining `.set` method that returns the signature:



&#x20;       .. code-block:: pycon



&#x20;           >>> add.s(2, 2).set(countdown=10).set(expires=30).delay()



&#x20;   Note:

&#x20;       You should use :func:`\~celery.signature` to create new signatures.

&#x20;       The ``Signature`` class is the type returned by that function and

&#x20;       should be used for ``isinstance`` checks for signatures.



&#x20;   See Also:

&#x20;       :ref:`guide-canvas` for the complete guide.



&#x20;   Arguments:

&#x20;       task (Union\[Type\[celery.app.task.Task], str]): Either a task

&#x20;           class/instance, or the name of a task.

&#x20;       args (Tuple): Positional arguments to apply.

&#x20;       kwargs (Dict): Keyword arguments to apply.

&#x20;       options (Dict): Additional options to :meth:`Task.apply\_async`.



&#x20;   Note:

&#x20;       If the first argument is a :class:`dict`, the other

&#x20;       arguments will be ignored and the values in the dict will be used

&#x20;       instead::



&#x20;           >>> s = signature('tasks.add', args=(2, 2))

&#x20;           >>> signature(s)

&#x20;           {'task': 'tasks.add', args=(2, 2), kwargs={}, options={}}

&#x20;   """



&#x20;   TYPES = {}

&#x20;   \_app = \_type = None

&#x20;   # The following fields must not be changed during freezing/merging because

&#x20;   # to do so would disrupt completion of parent tasks

&#x20;   \_IMMUTABLE\_OPTIONS = {"group\_id", "stamped\_headers"}



&#x20;   @classmethod

&#x20;   def register\_type(cls, name=None):

&#x20;       """Register a new type of signature.

&#x20;       Used as a class decorator, for example:

&#x20;       >>> @Signature.register\_type()

&#x20;       >>> class mysig(Signature):

&#x20;       >>>     pass

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       def \_inner(subclass):

&#x20;           cls.TYPES\[name or subclass.\_\_name\_\_] = subclass

&#x20;           return subclass



&#x20;       return \_inner



&#x20;   @classmethod

&#x20;   def from\_dict(cls, d, app=None):

&#x20;       """Create a new signature from a dict.

&#x20;       Subclasses can override this method to customize how are

&#x20;       they created from a dict.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       typ = d.get('subtask\_type')

&#x20;       if typ:

&#x20;           target\_cls = cls.TYPES\[typ]

&#x20;           if target\_cls is not cls:

&#x20;               return target\_cls.from\_dict(d, app=app)

&#x20;       return Signature(d, app=app)



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, task=None, args=None, kwargs=None, options=None,

&#x20;                type=None, subtask\_type=None, immutable=False,

&#x20;                app=None, \*\*ex):

&#x20;       self.\_app = app



&#x20;       if isinstance(task, dict):

&#x20;           super().\_\_init\_\_(task)  # works like dict(d)

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           # Also supports using task class/instance instead of string name.

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               task\_name = task.name

&#x20;           except AttributeError:

&#x20;               task\_name = task

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               self.\_type = task



&#x20;           super().\_\_init\_\_(

&#x20;               task=task\_name, args=tuple(args or ()),

&#x20;               kwargs=kwargs or {},

&#x20;               options=dict(options or {}, \*\*ex),

&#x20;               subtask\_type=subtask\_type,

&#x20;               immutable=immutable,

&#x20;           )



&#x20;   def \_\_call\_\_(self, \*partial\_args, \*\*partial\_kwargs):

&#x20;       """Call the task directly (in the current process)."""

&#x20;       args, kwargs, \_ = self.\_merge(partial\_args, partial\_kwargs, None)

&#x20;       return self.type(\*args, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def delay(self, \*partial\_args, \*\*partial\_kwargs):

&#x20;       """Shortcut to :meth:`apply\_async` using star arguments."""

&#x20;       return self.apply\_async(partial\_args, partial\_kwargs)



&#x20;   def apply(self, args=None, kwargs=None, \*\*options):

&#x20;       """Call task locally.



&#x20;       Same as :meth:`apply\_async` but executed the task inline instead

&#x20;       of sending a task message.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       args = args if args else ()

&#x20;       kwargs = kwargs if kwargs else {}

&#x20;       # Extra options set to None are dismissed

&#x20;       options = {k: v for k, v in options.items() if v is not None}

&#x20;       # For callbacks: extra args are prepended to the stored args.

&#x20;       args, kwargs, options = self.\_merge(args, kwargs, options)

&#x20;       return self.type.apply(args, kwargs, \*\*options)



&#x20;   def apply\_async(self, args=None, kwargs=None, route\_name=None, \*\*options):

&#x20;       """Apply this task asynchronously.



&#x20;       Arguments:

&#x20;           args (Tuple): Partial args to be prepended to the existing args.

&#x20;           kwargs (Dict): Partial kwargs to be merged with existing kwargs.

&#x20;           options (Dict): Partial options to be merged

&#x20;               with existing options.



&#x20;       Returns:

&#x20;           \~@AsyncResult: promise of future evaluation.



&#x20;       See also:

&#x20;           :meth:`\~@Task.apply\_async` and the :ref:`guide-calling` guide.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       args = args if args else ()

&#x20;       kwargs = kwargs if kwargs else {}

&#x20;       # Extra options set to None are dismissed

&#x20;       options = {k: v for k, v in options.items() if v is not None}

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           \_apply = self.\_apply\_async

&#x20;       except IndexError:  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;           # no tasks for chain, etc to find type

&#x20;           return

&#x20;       # For callbacks: extra args are prepended to the stored args.

&#x20;       if args or kwargs or options:

&#x20;           args, kwargs, options = self.\_merge(args, kwargs, options)

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           args, kwargs, options = self.args, self.kwargs, self.options

&#x20;       # pylint: disable=too-many-function-args

&#x20;       #   Works on this, as it's a property

&#x20;       return \_apply(args, kwargs, \*\*options)



&#x20;   def \_merge(self, args=None, kwargs=None, options=None, force=False):

&#x20;       """Merge partial args/kwargs/options with existing ones.



&#x20;       If the signature is immutable and ``force`` is False, the existing

&#x20;       args/kwargs will be returned as-is and only the options will be merged.



&#x20;       Stamped headers are considered immutable and will not be merged regardless.



&#x20;       Arguments:

&#x20;           args (Tuple): Partial args to be prepended to the existing args.

&#x20;           kwargs (Dict): Partial kwargs to be merged with existing kwargs.

&#x20;           options (Dict): Partial options to be merged with existing options.

&#x20;           force (bool): If True, the args/kwargs will be merged even if the signature is

&#x20;               immutable. The stamped headers are not affected by this option and will not

&#x20;               be merged regardless.



&#x20;       Returns:

&#x20;           Tuple: (args, kwargs, options)

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       args = args if args else ()

&#x20;       kwargs = kwargs if kwargs else {}

&#x20;       if options is not None:

&#x20;           # We build a new options dictionary where values in `options`

&#x20;           # override values in `self.options` except for keys which are

&#x20;           # noted as being immutable (unrelated to signature immutability)

&#x20;           # implying that allowing their value to change would stall tasks

&#x20;           immutable\_options = self.\_IMMUTABLE\_OPTIONS

&#x20;           if "stamped\_headers" in self.options:

&#x20;               immutable\_options = self.\_IMMUTABLE\_OPTIONS.union(set(self.options.get("stamped\_headers", \[])))

&#x20;           # merge self.options with options without overriding stamped headers from self.options

&#x20;           new\_options = {\*\*self.options, \*\*{

&#x20;               k: v for k, v in options.items()

&#x20;               if k not in immutable\_options or k not in self.options

&#x20;           }}

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           new\_options = self.options

&#x20;       if self.immutable and not force:

&#x20;           return (self.args, self.kwargs, new\_options)

&#x20;       return (tuple(args) + tuple(self.args) if args else self.args,

&#x20;               dict(self.kwargs, \*\*kwargs) if kwargs else self.kwargs,

&#x20;               new\_options)



&#x20;   def clone(self, args=None, kwargs=None, \*\*opts):

&#x20;       """Create a copy of this signature.



&#x20;       Arguments:

&#x20;           args (Tuple): Partial args to be prepended to the existing args.

&#x20;           kwargs (Dict): Partial kwargs to be merged with existing kwargs.

&#x20;           options (Dict): Partial options to be merged with

&#x20;               existing options.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       args = args if args else ()

&#x20;       kwargs = kwargs if kwargs else {}

&#x20;       # need to deepcopy options so origins links etc. is not modified.

&#x20;       if args or kwargs or opts:

&#x20;           args, kwargs, opts = self.\_merge(args, kwargs, opts)

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           args, kwargs, opts = self.args, self.kwargs, self.options

&#x20;       signature = Signature.from\_dict({'task': self.task,

&#x20;                                        'args': tuple(args),

&#x20;                                        'kwargs': kwargs,

&#x20;                                        'options': deepcopy(opts),

&#x20;                                        'subtask\_type': self.subtask\_type,

&#x20;                                        'immutable': self.immutable},

&#x20;                                       app=self.\_app)

&#x20;       signature.\_type = self.\_type

&#x20;       return signature



&#x20;   partial = clone



&#x20;   def freeze(self, \_id=None, group\_id=None, chord=None,

&#x20;              root\_id=None, parent\_id=None, group\_index=None):

&#x20;       """Finalize the signature by adding a concrete task id.



&#x20;       The task won't be called and you shouldn't call the signature

&#x20;       twice after freezing it as that'll result in two task messages

&#x20;       using the same task id.



&#x20;       The arguments are used to override the signature's headers during

&#x20;       freezing.



&#x20;       Arguments:

&#x20;           \_id (str): Task id to use if it didn't already have one.

&#x20;               New UUID is generated if not provided.

&#x20;           group\_id (str): Group id to use if it didn't already have one.

&#x20;           chord (Signature): Chord body when freezing a chord header.

&#x20;           root\_id (str): Root id to use.

&#x20;           parent\_id (str): Parent id to use.

&#x20;           group\_index (int): Group index to use.



&#x20;       Returns:

&#x20;           \~@AsyncResult: promise of future evaluation.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       # pylint: disable=redefined-outer-name

&#x20;       #   XXX chord is also a class in outer scope.

&#x20;       opts = self.options

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           # if there is already an id for this task, return it

&#x20;           tid = opts\['task\_id']

&#x20;       except KeyError:

&#x20;           # otherwise, use the \_id sent to this function, falling back on a generated UUID

&#x20;           tid = opts\['task\_id'] = \_id or uuid()

&#x20;       if root\_id:

&#x20;           opts\['root\_id'] = root\_id

&#x20;       if parent\_id:

&#x20;           opts\['parent\_id'] = parent\_id

&#x20;       if 'reply\_to' not in opts:

&#x20;           # fall back on unique ID for this thread in the app

&#x20;           opts\['reply\_to'] = self.app.thread\_oid

&#x20;       if group\_id and "group\_id" not in opts:

&#x20;           opts\['group\_id'] = group\_id

&#x20;       if chord:

&#x20;           opts\['chord'] = chord

&#x20;       if group\_index is not None:

&#x20;           opts\['group\_index'] = group\_index

&#x20;       # pylint: disable=too-many-function-args

&#x20;       #   Works on this, as it's a property.

&#x20;       return self.AsyncResult(tid)



&#x20;   \_freeze = freeze



&#x20;   def replace(self, args=None, kwargs=None, options=None):

&#x20;       """Replace the args, kwargs or options set for this signature.



&#x20;       These are only replaced if the argument for the section is

&#x20;       not :const:`None`.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       signature = self.clone()

&#x20;       if args is not None:

&#x20;           signature.args = args

&#x20;       if kwargs is not None:

&#x20;           signature.kwargs = kwargs

&#x20;       if options is not None:

&#x20;           signature.options = options

&#x20;       return signature



&#x20;   def set(self, immutable=None, \*\*options):

&#x20;       """Set arbitrary execution options (same as ``.options.update(…)``).



&#x20;       Returns:

&#x20;               (i.e., it will return ``self``).

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       if immutable is not None:

&#x20;           self.set\_immutable(immutable)

&#x20;       self.options.update(options)

&#x20;       return self



&#x20;   def set\_immutable(self, immutable):
```
