```python
&#x20;           The ``task\_allow\_error\_cb\_on\_chord\_header`` setting controls whether

&#x20;           error callbacks are allowed on the header. If this setting is

&#x20;           ``False`` (the current default), then the error callback will only be

&#x20;           applied to the body.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       errback = maybe\_signature(errback)



&#x20;       if self.app.conf.task\_allow\_error\_cb\_on\_chord\_header:

&#x20;           for task in maybe\_list(self.tasks) or \[]:

&#x20;               task.link\_error(errback.clone(immutable=True))

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           # Once this warning is removed, the whole method needs to be refactored to:

&#x20;           # 1. link the error callback to each task in the header

&#x20;           # 2. link the error callback to the body

&#x20;           # 3. return the error callback

&#x20;           # In summary, up to 4 lines of code + updating the method docstring.

&#x20;           warnings.warn(

&#x20;               "task\_allow\_error\_cb\_on\_chord\_header=False is pending deprecation in "

&#x20;               "a future release of Celery.\\n"

&#x20;               "Please test the new behavior by setting task\_allow\_error\_cb\_on\_chord\_header to True "

&#x20;               "and report any concerns you might have in our issue tracker before we make a final decision "

&#x20;               "regarding how errbacks should behave when used with chords.",

&#x20;               CPendingDeprecationWarning

&#x20;           )



&#x20;       # Edge case for nested chords in the header

&#x20;       for task in maybe\_list(self.tasks) or \[]:

&#x20;           if isinstance(task, chord):

&#x20;               # Let the nested chord do the error linking itself on its

&#x20;               # header and body where needed, based on the current configuration

&#x20;               task.link\_error(errback)



&#x20;       self.body.link\_error(errback)

&#x20;       return errback



&#x20;   def set\_immutable(self, immutable):

&#x20;       """Sets the immutable flag on the chord header only.



&#x20;       Note:

&#x20;           Does not affect the chord body.



&#x20;       Arguments:

&#x20;           immutable (bool): The new mutability value for chord header.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       for task in self.tasks:

&#x20;           task.set\_immutable(immutable)



&#x20;   def \_\_repr\_\_(self):

&#x20;       if self.body:

&#x20;           if isinstance(self.body, \_chain):

&#x20;               return remove\_repeating\_from\_task(

&#x20;                   self.body.tasks\[0]\['task'],

&#x20;                   '%({} | {!r})'.format(

&#x20;                       self.body.tasks\[0].reprcall(self.tasks),

&#x20;                       chain(self.body.tasks\[1:], app=self.\_app),

&#x20;                   ),

&#x20;               )

&#x20;           return '%' + remove\_repeating\_from\_task(

&#x20;               self.body\['task'], self.body.reprcall(self.tasks))

&#x20;       return f'<chord without body: {self.tasks!r}>'



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def app(self):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_app(self.body)



&#x20;   def \_get\_app(self, body=None):

&#x20;       app = self.\_app

&#x20;       if app is None:

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               tasks = self.tasks.tasks  # is a group

&#x20;           except AttributeError:

&#x20;               tasks = self.tasks

&#x20;           if tasks:

&#x20;               app = tasks\[0].\_app

&#x20;           if app is None and body is not None:

&#x20;               app = body.\_app

&#x20;       return app if app is not None else current\_app



&#x20;   tasks = getitem\_property('kwargs.header', 'Tasks in chord header.')

&#x20;   body = getitem\_property('kwargs.body', 'Body task of chord.')





\# Add a back-compat alias for the previous `chord` class name which conflicts

\# with keyword arguments elsewhere in this file

chord = \_chord





def signature(varies, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;   """Create new signature.



&#x20;   - if the first argument is a signature already then it's cloned.

&#x20;   - if the first argument is a dict, then a Signature version is returned.



&#x20;   Returns:

&#x20;       Signature: The resulting signature.

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   app = kwargs.get('app')

&#x20;   if isinstance(varies, dict):

&#x20;       if isinstance(varies, abstract.CallableSignature):

&#x20;           return varies.clone()

&#x20;       return Signature.from\_dict(varies, app=app)

&#x20;   return Signature(varies, \*args, \*\*kwargs)





subtask = signature  # XXX compat





def maybe\_signature(d, app=None, clone=False):

&#x20;   """Ensure obj is a signature, or None.



&#x20;   Arguments:

&#x20;       d (Optional\[Union\[abstract.CallableSignature, Mapping]]):

&#x20;           Signature or dict-serialized signature.

&#x20;       app (celery.Celery):

&#x20;           App to bind signature to.

&#x20;       clone (bool):

&#x20;           If d' is already a signature, the signature

&#x20;          will be cloned when this flag is enabled.



&#x20;   Returns:

&#x20;       Optional\[abstract.CallableSignature]

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   if d is not None:

&#x20;       if isinstance(d, abstract.CallableSignature):

&#x20;           if clone:

&#x20;               d = d.clone()

&#x20;       elif isinstance(d, dict):

&#x20;           d = signature(d)



&#x20;       if app is not None:

&#x20;           d.\_app = app

&#x20;   return d





maybe\_subtask = maybe\_signature  # XXX compat



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\exceptions.py

```python

"""Celery error types.



Error Hierarchy

===============



\- :exc:`Exception`

&#x20;   - :exc:`celery.exceptions.CeleryError`

&#x20;       - :exc:`\~celery.exceptions.ImproperlyConfigured`

&#x20;       - :exc:`\~celery.exceptions.SecurityError`

&#x20;       - :exc:`\~celery.exceptions.TaskPredicate`

&#x20;           - :exc:`\~celery.exceptions.Ignore`

&#x20;           - :exc:`\~celery.exceptions.Reject`

&#x20;           - :exc:`\~celery.exceptions.Retry`

&#x20;       - :exc:`\~celery.exceptions.TaskError`

&#x20;           - :exc:`\~celery.exceptions.QueueNotFound`

&#x20;           - :exc:`\~celery.exceptions.IncompleteStream`

&#x20;           - :exc:`\~celery.exceptions.NotRegistered`

&#x20;           - :exc:`\~celery.exceptions.AlreadyRegistered`

&#x20;           - :exc:`\~celery.exceptions.TimeoutError`

&#x20;           - :exc:`\~celery.exceptions.MaxRetriesExceededError`

&#x20;           - :exc:`\~celery.exceptions.TaskRevokedError`

&#x20;           - :exc:`\~celery.exceptions.InvalidTaskError`

&#x20;           - :exc:`\~celery.exceptions.ChordError`

&#x20;       - :exc:`\~celery.exceptions.BackendError`

&#x20;           - :exc:`\~celery.exceptions.BackendGetMetaError`

&#x20;           - :exc:`\~celery.exceptions.BackendStoreError`

&#x20;   - :class:`kombu.exceptions.KombuError`

&#x20;       - :exc:`\~celery.exceptions.OperationalError`



&#x20;           Raised when a transport connection error occurs while

&#x20;           sending a message (be it a task, remote control command error).



&#x20;           .. note::

&#x20;               This exception does not inherit from

&#x20;               :exc:`\~celery.exceptions.CeleryError`.

&#x20;   - \*\*billiard errors\*\* (prefork pool)

&#x20;       - :exc:`\~celery.exceptions.SoftTimeLimitExceeded`

&#x20;       - :exc:`\~celery.exceptions.TimeLimitExceeded`

&#x20;       - :exc:`\~celery.exceptions.WorkerLostError`

&#x20;       - :exc:`\~celery.exceptions.Terminated`

\- :class:`UserWarning`

&#x20;   - :class:`\~celery.exceptions.CeleryWarning`

&#x20;       - :class:`\~celery.exceptions.AlwaysEagerIgnored`

&#x20;       - :class:`\~celery.exceptions.DuplicateNodenameWarning`

&#x20;       - :class:`\~celery.exceptions.FixupWarning`

&#x20;       - :class:`\~celery.exceptions.NotConfigured`

&#x20;       - :class:`\~celery.exceptions.SecurityWarning`

\- :exc:`BaseException`

&#x20;   - :exc:`SystemExit`

&#x20;       - :exc:`\~celery.exceptions.WorkerTerminate`

&#x20;       - :exc:`\~celery.exceptions.WorkerShutdown`

"""



import numbers



from billiard.exceptions import SoftTimeLimitExceeded, Terminated, TimeLimitExceeded, WorkerLostError

from click import ClickException

from kombu.exceptions import OperationalError



\_\_all\_\_ = (

&#x20;   'reraise',

&#x20;   # Warnings

&#x20;   'CeleryWarning',

&#x20;   'AlwaysEagerIgnored', 'DuplicateNodenameWarning',

&#x20;   'FixupWarning', 'NotConfigured', 'SecurityWarning',



&#x20;   # Core errors

&#x20;   'CeleryError',

&#x20;   'ImproperlyConfigured', 'SecurityError',



&#x20;   # Kombu (messaging) errors.

&#x20;   'OperationalError',



&#x20;   # Task semi-predicates

&#x20;   'TaskPredicate', 'Ignore', 'Reject', 'Retry',



&#x20;   # Task related errors.

&#x20;   'TaskError', 'QueueNotFound', 'IncompleteStream',

&#x20;   'NotRegistered', 'AlreadyRegistered', 'TimeoutError',

&#x20;   'MaxRetriesExceededError', 'TaskRevokedError',

&#x20;   'InvalidTaskError', 'ChordError',



&#x20;   # Backend related errors.

&#x20;   'BackendError', 'BackendGetMetaError', 'BackendStoreError',



&#x20;   # Billiard task errors.

&#x20;   'SoftTimeLimitExceeded', 'TimeLimitExceeded',

&#x20;   'WorkerLostError', 'Terminated',



&#x20;   # Deprecation warnings (forcing Python to emit them).

&#x20;   'CPendingDeprecationWarning', 'CDeprecationWarning',



&#x20;   # Worker shutdown semi-predicates (inherits from SystemExit).

&#x20;   'WorkerShutdown', 'WorkerTerminate',



&#x20;   'CeleryCommandException',

)



from celery.utils.serialization import get\_pickleable\_exception



UNREGISTERED\_FMT = """\\

Task of kind {0} never registered, please make sure it's imported.\\

"""





def reraise(tp, value, tb=None):

&#x20;   """Reraise exception."""

&#x20;   if value.\_\_traceback\_\_ is not tb:

&#x20;       raise value.with\_traceback(tb)

&#x20;   raise value





class CeleryWarning(UserWarning):

&#x20;   """Base class for all Celery warnings."""





class AlwaysEagerIgnored(CeleryWarning):

&#x20;   """send\_task ignores :setting:`task\_always\_eager` option."""





class DuplicateNodenameWarning(CeleryWarning):

&#x20;   """Multiple workers are using the same nodename."""





class FixupWarning(CeleryWarning):

&#x20;   """Fixup related warning."""





class NotConfigured(CeleryWarning):

&#x20;   """Celery hasn't been configured, as no config module has been found."""





class SecurityWarning(CeleryWarning):

&#x20;   """Potential security issue found."""





class CeleryError(Exception):

&#x20;   """Base class for all Celery errors."""





class TaskPredicate(CeleryError):

&#x20;   """Base class for task-related semi-predicates."""





class Retry(TaskPredicate):

&#x20;   """The task is to be retried later."""



&#x20;   #: Optional message describing context of retry.

&#x20;   message = None



&#x20;   #: Exception (if any) that caused the retry to happen.

&#x20;   exc = None



&#x20;   #: Time of retry (ETA), either :class:`numbers.Real` or

&#x20;   #: :class:`\~datetime.datetime`.

&#x20;   when = None



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, message=None, exc=None, when=None, is\_eager=False,

&#x20;                sig=None, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       from kombu.utils.encoding import safe\_repr

&#x20;       self.message = message

&#x20;       if isinstance(exc, str):

&#x20;           self.exc, self.excs = None, exc

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           self.exc, self.excs = get\_pickleable\_exception(exc), safe\_repr(exc) if exc else None

&#x20;       self.when = when

&#x20;       self.is\_eager = is\_eager

&#x20;       self.sig = sig

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(self, exc, when, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def humanize(self):

&#x20;       if isinstance(self.when, numbers.Number):

&#x20;           return f'in {self.when}s'

&#x20;       return f'at {self.when}'



&#x20;   def \_\_str\_\_(self):

&#x20;       if self.message:

&#x20;           return self.message

&#x20;       if self.excs:

&#x20;           return f'Retry {self.humanize()}: {self.excs}'

&#x20;       return f'Retry {self.humanize()}'



&#x20;   def \_\_reduce\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return self.\_\_class\_\_, (self.message, self.exc, self.when)





RetryTaskError = Retry  # XXX compat





class Ignore(TaskPredicate):

&#x20;   """A task can raise this to ignore doing state updates."""





class Reject(TaskPredicate):

&#x20;   """A task can raise this if it wants to reject/re-queue the message."""



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, reason=None, requeue=False):

&#x20;       self.reason = reason

&#x20;       self.requeue = requeue

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(reason, requeue)



&#x20;   def \_\_repr\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return f'reject requeue={self.requeue}: {self.reason}'





class ImproperlyConfigured(CeleryError):

&#x20;   """Celery is somehow improperly configured."""





class SecurityError(CeleryError):

&#x20;   """Security related exception."""





class TaskError(CeleryError):

&#x20;   """Task related errors."""





class QueueNotFound(KeyError, TaskError):

&#x20;   """Task routed to a queue not in ``conf.queues``."""





class IncompleteStream(TaskError):

&#x20;   """Found the end of a stream of data, but the data isn't complete."""





class NotRegistered(KeyError, TaskError):

&#x20;   """The task is not registered."""



&#x20;   def \_\_repr\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return UNREGISTERED\_FMT.format(self)





class AlreadyRegistered(TaskError):

&#x20;   """The task is already registered."""

&#x20;   # XXX Unused





class TimeoutError(TaskError):

&#x20;   """The operation timed out."""





class MaxRetriesExceededError(TaskError):

&#x20;   """The tasks max restart limit has been exceeded."""



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       self.task\_args = kwargs.pop("task\_args", \[])

&#x20;       self.task\_kwargs = kwargs.pop("task\_kwargs", dict())

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(\*args, \*\*kwargs)





class TaskRevokedError(TaskError):

&#x20;   """The task has been revoked, so no result available."""





class InvalidTaskError(TaskError):

&#x20;   """The task has invalid data or ain't properly constructed."""





class ChordError(TaskError):

&#x20;   """A task part of the chord raised an exception."""





class CPendingDeprecationWarning(PendingDeprecationWarning):

&#x20;   """Warning of pending deprecation."""





class CDeprecationWarning(DeprecationWarning):

&#x20;   """Warning of deprecation."""





class WorkerTerminate(SystemExit):

&#x20;   """Signals that the worker should terminate immediately."""





SystemTerminate = WorkerTerminate  # XXX compat





class WorkerShutdown(SystemExit):

&#x20;   """Signals that the worker should perform a warm shutdown."""





class BackendError(Exception):

&#x20;   """An issue writing or reading to/from the backend."""





class BackendGetMetaError(BackendError):

&#x20;   """An issue reading from the backend."""



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       self.task\_id = kwargs.get('task\_id', "")



&#x20;   def \_\_repr\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return super().\_\_repr\_\_() + " task\_id:" + self.task\_id





class BackendStoreError(BackendError):

&#x20;   """An issue writing to the backend."""



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       self.state = kwargs.get('state', "")

&#x20;       self.task\_id = kwargs.get('task\_id', "")



&#x20;   def \_\_repr\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return super().\_\_repr\_\_() + " state:" + self.state + " task\_id:" + self.task\_id





class CeleryCommandException(ClickException):

&#x20;   """A general command exception which stores an exit code."""



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, message, exit\_code):

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(message=message)

&#x20;       self.exit\_code = exit\_code
```

```python
"""Proxy/PromiseProxy implementation.



This module contains critical utilities that needs to be loaded as

soon as possible, and that shall not load any third party modules.



Parts of this module is Copyright by Werkzeug Team.

"""



import operator

import sys

from functools import reduce

from importlib import import\_module

from types import ModuleType



\_\_all\_\_ = ('Proxy', 'PromiseProxy', 'try\_import', 'maybe\_evaluate')



\_\_module\_\_ = \_\_name\_\_  # used by Proxy class body





def \_default\_cls\_attr(name, type\_, cls\_value):

&#x20;   # Proxy uses properties to forward the standard

&#x20;   # class attributes \_\_module\_\_, \_\_name\_\_ and \_\_doc\_\_ to the real

&#x20;   # object, but these needs to be a string when accessed from

&#x20;   # the Proxy class directly.  This is a hack to make that work.

&#x20;   # -- See Issue #1087.



&#x20;   def \_\_new\_\_(cls, getter):

&#x20;       instance = type\_.\_\_new\_\_(cls, cls\_value)

&#x20;       instance.\_\_getter = getter

&#x20;       return instance



&#x20;   def \_\_get\_\_(self, obj, cls=None):

&#x20;       return self.\_\_getter(obj) if obj is not None else self



&#x20;   return type(name, (type\_,), {

&#x20;       '\_\_new\_\_': \_\_new\_\_, '\_\_get\_\_': \_\_get\_\_,

&#x20;   })





def try\_import(module, default=None):

&#x20;   """Try to import and return module.



&#x20;   Returns None if the module does not exist.

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       return import\_module(module)

&#x20;   except ImportError:

&#x20;       return default





class Proxy:

&#x20;   """Proxy to another object."""



&#x20;   # Code stolen from werkzeug.local.Proxy.

&#x20;   \_\_slots\_\_ = ('\_\_local', '\_\_args', '\_\_kwargs', '\_\_dict\_\_')



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, local,

&#x20;                args=None, kwargs=None, name=None, \_\_doc\_\_=None):

&#x20;       object.\_\_setattr\_\_(self, '\_Proxy\_\_local', local)

&#x20;       object.\_\_setattr\_\_(self, '\_Proxy\_\_args', args or ())

&#x20;       object.\_\_setattr\_\_(self, '\_Proxy\_\_kwargs', kwargs or {})

&#x20;       if name is not None:

&#x20;           object.\_\_setattr\_\_(self, '\_\_custom\_name\_\_', name)

&#x20;       if \_\_doc\_\_ is not None:

&#x20;           object.\_\_setattr\_\_(self, '\_\_doc\_\_', \_\_doc\_\_)



&#x20;   @\_default\_cls\_attr('name', str, \_\_name\_\_)

&#x20;   def \_\_name\_\_(self):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           return self.\_\_custom\_name\_\_

&#x20;       except AttributeError:

&#x20;           return self.\_get\_current\_object().\_\_name\_\_



&#x20;   @\_default\_cls\_attr('qualname', str, \_\_name\_\_)

&#x20;   def \_\_qualname\_\_(self):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           return self.\_\_custom\_name\_\_

&#x20;       except AttributeError:

&#x20;           return self.\_get\_current\_object().\_\_qualname\_\_



&#x20;   @\_default\_cls\_attr('module', str, \_\_module\_\_)

&#x20;   def \_\_module\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_current\_object().\_\_module\_\_



&#x20;   @\_default\_cls\_attr('doc', str, \_\_doc\_\_)

&#x20;   def \_\_doc\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_current\_object().\_\_doc\_\_



&#x20;   def \_get\_class(self):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_current\_object().\_\_class\_\_



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def \_\_class\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_class()



&#x20;   def \_get\_current\_object(self):

&#x20;       """Get current object.



&#x20;       This is useful if you want the real

&#x20;       object behind the proxy at a time for performance reasons or because

&#x20;       you want to pass the object into a different context.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       loc = object.\_\_getattribute\_\_(self, '\_Proxy\_\_local')

&#x20;       if not hasattr(loc, '\_\_release\_local\_\_'):

&#x20;           return loc(\*self.\_\_args, \*\*self.\_\_kwargs)

&#x20;       try:  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;           # not sure what this is about

&#x20;           return getattr(loc, self.\_\_name\_\_)

&#x20;       except AttributeError:  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;           raise RuntimeError(f'no object bound to {self.\_\_name\_\_}')



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def \_\_dict\_\_(self):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           return self.\_get\_current\_object().\_\_dict\_\_

&#x20;       except RuntimeError:  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;           raise AttributeError('\_\_dict\_\_')



&#x20;   def \_\_repr\_\_(self):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           obj = self.\_get\_current\_object()

&#x20;       except RuntimeError:  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;           return f'<{self.\_\_class\_\_.\_\_name\_\_} unbound>'

&#x20;       return repr(obj)



&#x20;   def \_\_bool\_\_(self):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           return bool(self.\_get\_current\_object())

&#x20;       except RuntimeError:  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;           return False



&#x20;   \_\_nonzero\_\_ = \_\_bool\_\_  # Py2



&#x20;   def \_\_dir\_\_(self):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           return dir(self.\_get\_current\_object())

&#x20;       except RuntimeError:  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;           return \[]



&#x20;   def \_\_getattr\_\_(self, name):

&#x20;       if name == '\_\_members\_\_':

&#x20;           return dir(self.\_get\_current\_object())

&#x20;       return getattr(self.\_get\_current\_object(), name)



&#x20;   def \_\_setitem\_\_(self, key, value):

&#x20;       self.\_get\_current\_object()\[key] = value



&#x20;   def \_\_delitem\_\_(self, key):

&#x20;       del self.\_get\_current\_object()\[key]



&#x20;   def \_\_setattr\_\_(self, name, value):

&#x20;       setattr(self.\_get\_current\_object(), name, value)



&#x20;   def \_\_delattr\_\_(self, name):

&#x20;       delattr(self.\_get\_current\_object(), name)



&#x20;   def \_\_str\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return str(self.\_get\_current\_object())



&#x20;   def \_\_lt\_\_(self, other):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_current\_object() < other



&#x20;   def \_\_le\_\_(self, other):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_current\_object() <= other



&#x20;   def \_\_eq\_\_(self, other):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_current\_object() == other



&#x20;   def \_\_ne\_\_(self, other):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_current\_object() != other



&#x20;   def \_\_gt\_\_(self, other):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_current\_object() > other



&#x20;   def \_\_ge\_\_(self, other):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_current\_object() >= other



&#x20;   def \_\_hash\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return hash(self.\_get\_current\_object())



&#x20;   def \_\_call\_\_(self, \*a, \*\*kw):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_current\_object()(\*a, \*\*kw)



&#x20;   def \_\_len\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return len(self.\_get\_current\_object())



&#x20;   def \_\_getitem\_\_(self, i):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_current\_object()\[i]



&#x20;   def \_\_iter\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return iter(self.\_get\_current\_object())



&#x20;   def \_\_contains\_\_(self, i):

&#x20;       return i in self.\_get\_current\_object()



&#x20;   def \_\_add\_\_(self, other):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_current\_object() + other



&#x20;   def \_\_sub\_\_(self, other):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_current\_object() - other



&#x20;   def \_\_mul\_\_(self, other):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_current\_object() \* other



&#x20;   def \_\_floordiv\_\_(self, other):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_current\_object() // other



&#x20;   def \_\_mod\_\_(self, other):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_current\_object() % other



&#x20;   def \_\_divmod\_\_(self, other):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_current\_object().\_\_divmod\_\_(other)



&#x20;   def \_\_pow\_\_(self, other):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_current\_object() \*\* other



&#x20;   def \_\_lshift\_\_(self, other):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_current\_object() << other



&#x20;   def \_\_rshift\_\_(self, other):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_current\_object() >> other



&#x20;   def \_\_and\_\_(self, other):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_current\_object() \& other



&#x20;   def \_\_xor\_\_(self, other):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_current\_object() ^ other



&#x20;   def \_\_or\_\_(self, other):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_current\_object() | other



&#x20;   def \_\_div\_\_(self, other):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_current\_object().\_\_div\_\_(other)



&#x20;   def \_\_truediv\_\_(self, other):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_current\_object().\_\_truediv\_\_(other)



&#x20;   def \_\_neg\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return -(self.\_get\_current\_object())



&#x20;   def \_\_pos\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return +(self.\_get\_current\_object())



&#x20;   def \_\_abs\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return abs(self.\_get\_current\_object())



&#x20;   def \_\_invert\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return \~(self.\_get\_current\_object())



&#x20;   def \_\_complex\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return complex(self.\_get\_current\_object())



&#x20;   def \_\_int\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return int(self.\_get\_current\_object())



&#x20;   def \_\_float\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return float(self.\_get\_current\_object())



&#x20;   def \_\_oct\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return oct(self.\_get\_current\_object())



&#x20;   def \_\_hex\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return hex(self.\_get\_current\_object())



&#x20;   def \_\_index\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_current\_object().\_\_index\_\_()



&#x20;   def \_\_coerce\_\_(self, other):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_current\_object().\_\_coerce\_\_(other)



&#x20;   def \_\_enter\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_current\_object().\_\_enter\_\_()



&#x20;   def \_\_exit\_\_(self, \*a, \*\*kw):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_current\_object().\_\_exit\_\_(\*a, \*\*kw)



&#x20;   def \_\_reduce\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_current\_object().\_\_reduce\_\_()





class PromiseProxy(Proxy):

&#x20;   """Proxy that evaluates object once.



&#x20;   :class:`Proxy` will evaluate the object each time, while the

&#x20;   promise will only evaluate it once.

&#x20;   """



&#x20;   \_\_slots\_\_ = ('\_\_pending\_\_', '\_\_weakref\_\_')



&#x20;   def \_get\_current\_object(self):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           return object.\_\_getattribute\_\_(self, '\_\_thing')

&#x20;       except AttributeError:

&#x20;           return self.\_\_evaluate\_\_()



&#x20;   def \_\_then\_\_(self, fun, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       if self.\_\_evaluated\_\_():

&#x20;           return fun(\*args, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;       from collections import deque

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           pending = object.\_\_getattribute\_\_(self, '\_\_pending\_\_')

&#x20;       except AttributeError:

&#x20;           pending = None

&#x20;       if pending is None:

&#x20;           pending = deque()

&#x20;           object.\_\_setattr\_\_(self, '\_\_pending\_\_', pending)

&#x20;       pending.append((fun, args, kwargs))



&#x20;   def \_\_evaluated\_\_(self):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           object.\_\_getattribute\_\_(self, '\_\_thing')

&#x20;       except AttributeError:

&#x20;           return False

&#x20;       return True



&#x20;   def \_\_maybe\_evaluate\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_current\_object()



&#x20;   def \_\_evaluate\_\_(self,

&#x20;                    \_clean=('\_Proxy\_\_local',

&#x20;                            '\_Proxy\_\_args',

&#x20;                            '\_Proxy\_\_kwargs')):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           thing = Proxy.\_get\_current\_object(self)

&#x20;       except Exception:

&#x20;           raise

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           object.\_\_setattr\_\_(self, '\_\_thing', thing)

&#x20;           for attr in \_clean:

&#x20;               try:

&#x20;                   object.\_\_delattr\_\_(self, attr)

&#x20;               except AttributeError:  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;                   # May mask errors so ignore

&#x20;                   pass

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               pending = object.\_\_getattribute\_\_(self, '\_\_pending\_\_')

&#x20;           except AttributeError:

&#x20;               pass

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               try:

&#x20;                   while pending:

&#x20;                       fun, args, kwargs = pending.popleft()

&#x20;                       fun(\*args, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;               finally:

&#x20;                   try:

&#x20;                       object.\_\_delattr\_\_(self, '\_\_pending\_\_')

&#x20;                   except AttributeError:  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;                       pass

&#x20;           return thing





def maybe\_evaluate(obj):

&#x20;   """Attempt to evaluate promise, even if obj is not a promise."""

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       return obj.\_\_maybe\_evaluate\_\_()

&#x20;   except AttributeError:

&#x20;       return obj





\#  ############# Module Generation ##########################



\# Utilities to dynamically

\# recreate modules, either for lazy loading or

\# to create old modules at runtime instead of

\# having them litter the source tree.



\# import fails in python 2.5. fallback to reduce in stdlib





MODULE\_DEPRECATED = """

The module %s is deprecated and will be removed in a future version.

"""



DEFAULT\_ATTRS = {'\_\_file\_\_', '\_\_path\_\_', '\_\_doc\_\_', '\_\_all\_\_'}





\# im\_func is no longer available in Py3.

\# instead the unbound method itself can be used.

def fun\_of\_method(method):

&#x20;   return method





def getappattr(path):

&#x20;   """Get attribute from current\_app recursively.



&#x20;   Example: ``getappattr('amqp.get\_task\_consumer')``.



&#x20;   """

&#x20;   from celery import current\_app

&#x20;   return current\_app.\_rgetattr(path)





COMPAT\_MODULES = {

&#x20;   'celery': {

&#x20;       'execute': {

&#x20;           'send\_task': 'send\_task',

&#x20;       },

&#x20;       'log': {

&#x20;           'get\_default\_logger': 'log.get\_default\_logger',

&#x20;           'setup\_logging\_subsystem': 'log.setup\_logging\_subsystem',

&#x20;           'redirect\_stdouts\_to\_logger': 'log.redirect\_stdouts\_to\_logger',

&#x20;       },

&#x20;       'messaging': {

&#x20;           'TaskConsumer': 'amqp.TaskConsumer',

&#x20;           'establish\_connection': 'connection',

&#x20;           'get\_consumer\_set': 'amqp.TaskConsumer',

&#x20;       },

&#x20;       'registry': {

&#x20;           'tasks': 'tasks',

&#x20;       },

&#x20;   },

}



\#: We exclude these from dir(celery)

DEPRECATED\_ATTRS = set(COMPAT\_MODULES\['celery'].keys()) | {'subtask'}





class class\_property:



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, getter=None, setter=None):

&#x20;       if getter is not None and not isinstance(getter, classmethod):

&#x20;           getter = classmethod(getter)

&#x20;       if setter is not None and not isinstance(setter, classmethod):

&#x20;           setter = classmethod(setter)

&#x20;       self.\_\_get = getter

&#x20;       self.\_\_set = setter



&#x20;       info = getter.\_\_get\_\_(object)  # just need the info attrs.

&#x20;       self.\_\_doc\_\_ = info.\_\_doc\_\_

&#x20;       self.\_\_name\_\_ = info.\_\_name\_\_

&#x20;       self.\_\_module\_\_ = info.\_\_module\_\_



&#x20;   def \_\_get\_\_(self, obj, type=None):

&#x20;       if obj and type is None:

&#x20;           type = obj.\_\_class\_\_

&#x20;       return self.\_\_get.\_\_get\_\_(obj, type)()



&#x20;   def \_\_set\_\_(self, obj, value):

&#x20;       if obj is None:

&#x20;           return self

&#x20;       return self.\_\_set.\_\_get\_\_(obj)(value)



&#x20;   def setter(self, setter):

&#x20;       return self.\_\_class\_\_(self.\_\_get, setter)





def reclassmethod(method):

&#x20;   return classmethod(fun\_of\_method(method))





class LazyModule(ModuleType):

&#x20;   \_compat\_modules = ()

&#x20;   \_all\_by\_module = {}

&#x20;   \_direct = {}

&#x20;   \_object\_origins = {}



&#x20;   def \_\_getattr\_\_(self, name):

&#x20;       if name in self.\_object\_origins:

&#x20;           module = \_\_import\_\_(self.\_object\_origins\[name], None, None,

&#x20;                               \[name])

&#x20;           for item in self.\_all\_by\_module\[module.\_\_name\_\_]:

&#x20;               setattr(self, item, getattr(module, item))

&#x20;           return getattr(module, name)

&#x20;       elif name in self.\_direct:  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;           module = \_\_import\_\_(self.\_direct\[name], None, None, \[name])

&#x20;           setattr(self, name, module)

&#x20;           return module

&#x20;       return ModuleType.\_\_getattribute\_\_(self, name)



&#x20;   def \_\_dir\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return \[

&#x20;           attr for attr in set(self.\_\_all\_\_) | DEFAULT\_ATTRS

&#x20;           if attr not in DEPRECATED\_ATTRS

&#x20;       ]



&#x20;   def \_\_reduce\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return import\_module, (self.\_\_name\_\_,)





def create\_module(name, attrs, cls\_attrs=None, pkg=None,

&#x20;                 base=LazyModule, prepare\_attr=None):

&#x20;   fqdn = '.'.join(\[pkg.\_\_name\_\_, name]) if pkg else name

&#x20;   cls\_attrs = {} if cls\_attrs is None else cls\_attrs

&#x20;   pkg, \_, modname = name.rpartition('.')

&#x20;   cls\_attrs\['\_\_module\_\_'] = pkg



&#x20;   attrs = {

&#x20;       attr\_name: (prepare\_attr(attr) if prepare\_attr else attr)

&#x20;       for attr\_name, attr in attrs.items()

&#x20;   }

&#x20;   module = sys.modules\[fqdn] = type(

&#x20;       modname, (base,), cls\_attrs)(name)

&#x20;   module.\_\_dict\_\_.update(attrs)

&#x20;   return module





def recreate\_module(name, compat\_modules=None, by\_module=None, direct=None,

&#x20;                   base=LazyModule, \*\*attrs):

&#x20;   compat\_modules = compat\_modules or COMPAT\_MODULES.get(name, ())

&#x20;   by\_module = by\_module or {}

&#x20;   direct = direct or {}

&#x20;   old\_module = sys.modules\[name]

&#x20;   origins = get\_origins(by\_module)



&#x20;   \_all = tuple(set(reduce(

&#x20;       operator.add,

&#x20;       \[tuple(v) for v in \[compat\_modules, origins, direct, attrs]],

&#x20;   )))

&#x20;   cattrs = {

&#x20;       '\_compat\_modules': compat\_modules,

&#x20;       '\_all\_by\_module': by\_module, '\_direct': direct,

&#x20;       '\_object\_origins': origins,

&#x20;       '\_\_all\_\_': \_all,

&#x20;   }

&#x20;   new\_module = create\_module(name, attrs, cls\_attrs=cattrs, base=base)

&#x20;   new\_module.\_\_dict\_\_.update({

&#x20;       mod: get\_compat\_module(new\_module, mod) for mod in compat\_modules

&#x20;   })

&#x20;   new\_module.\_\_spec\_\_ = old\_module.\_\_spec\_\_

&#x20;   return old\_module, new\_module





def get\_compat\_module(pkg, name):

&#x20;   def prepare(attr):

&#x20;       if isinstance(attr, str):

&#x20;           return Proxy(getappattr, (attr,))

&#x20;       return attr



&#x20;   attrs = COMPAT\_MODULES\[pkg.\_\_name\_\_]\[name]

&#x20;   if isinstance(attrs, str):

&#x20;       fqdn = '.'.join(\[pkg.\_\_name\_\_, name])

&#x20;       module = sys.modules\[fqdn] = import\_module(attrs)

&#x20;       return module

&#x20;   attrs\['\_\_all\_\_'] = list(attrs)

&#x20;   return create\_module(name, dict(attrs), pkg=pkg, prepare\_attr=prepare)





def get\_origins(defs):

&#x20;   origins = {}

&#x20;   for module, attrs in defs.items():

&#x20;       origins.update({attr: module for attr in attrs})

&#x20;   return origins



```

```python
"""Platforms.



Utilities dealing with platform specifics: signals, daemonization,

users, groups, and so on.

"""



import atexit

import errno

import math

import numbers

import os

import platform as \_platform

import signal as \_signal

import sys

import warnings

from contextlib import contextmanager



from billiard.compat import close\_open\_fds, get\_fdmax

from billiard.util import set\_pdeathsig as \_set\_pdeathsig

\# fileno used to be in this module

from kombu.utils.compat import maybe\_fileno

from kombu.utils.encoding import safe\_str



from .exceptions import SecurityError, SecurityWarning, reraise

from .local import try\_import



try:

&#x20;   from billiard.process import current\_process

except ImportError:

&#x20;   current\_process = None



\_setproctitle = try\_import('setproctitle')

resource = try\_import('resource')

pwd = try\_import('pwd')

grp = try\_import('grp')

mputil = try\_import('multiprocessing.util')



\_\_all\_\_ = (

&#x20;   'EX\_OK', 'EX\_FAILURE', 'EX\_UNAVAILABLE', 'EX\_USAGE', 'SYSTEM',

&#x20;   'IS\_macOS', 'IS\_WINDOWS', 'SIGMAP', 'pyimplementation', 'LockFailed',

&#x20;   'get\_fdmax', 'Pidfile', 'create\_pidlock', 'close\_open\_fds',

&#x20;   'DaemonContext', 'detached', 'parse\_uid', 'parse\_gid', 'setgroups',

&#x20;   'initgroups', 'setgid', 'setuid', 'maybe\_drop\_privileges', 'signals',

&#x20;   'signal\_name', 'set\_process\_title', 'set\_mp\_process\_title',

&#x20;   'get\_errno\_name', 'ignore\_errno', 'fd\_by\_path', 'isatty',

)



\# exitcodes

EX\_OK = getattr(os, 'EX\_OK', 0)

EX\_FAILURE = 1

EX\_UNAVAILABLE = getattr(os, 'EX\_UNAVAILABLE', 69)

EX\_USAGE = getattr(os, 'EX\_USAGE', 64)

EX\_CANTCREAT = getattr(os, 'EX\_CANTCREAT', 73)



SYSTEM = \_platform.system()

IS\_macOS = SYSTEM == 'Darwin'

IS\_WINDOWS = SYSTEM == 'Windows'



DAEMON\_WORKDIR = '/'



PIDFILE\_FLAGS = os.O\_CREAT | os.O\_EXCL | os.O\_WRONLY

PIDFILE\_MODE = ((os.R\_OK | os.W\_OK) << 6) | ((os.R\_OK) << 3) | (os.R\_OK)



PIDLOCKED = """ERROR: Pidfile ({0}) already exists.

Seems we're already running? (pid: {1})"""



ROOT\_DISALLOWED = """\\

Running a worker with superuser privileges when the

worker accepts messages serialized with pickle is a very bad idea!



If you really want to continue then you have to set the C\_FORCE\_ROOT

environment variable (but please think about this before you do).



User information: uid={uid} euid={euid} gid={gid} egid={egid}

"""



ROOT\_DISCOURAGED = """\\

You're running the worker with superuser privileges: this is

absolutely not recommended!



Please specify a different user using the --uid option.



User information: uid={uid} euid={euid} gid={gid} egid={egid}

"""



ASSUMING\_ROOT = """\\

An entry for the specified gid or egid was not found.

We're assuming this is a potential security issue.

"""



SIGNAMES = {

&#x20;   sig for sig in dir(\_signal)

&#x20;   if sig.startswith('SIG') and '\_' not in sig

}

SIGMAP = {getattr(\_signal, name): name for name in SIGNAMES}





def isatty(fh):

&#x20;   """Return true if the process has a controlling terminal."""

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       return fh.isatty()

&#x20;   except AttributeError:

&#x20;       pass





def pyimplementation():

&#x20;   """Return string identifying the current Python implementation."""

&#x20;   if hasattr(\_platform, 'python\_implementation'):

&#x20;       return \_platform.python\_implementation()

&#x20;   elif sys.platform.startswith('java'):

&#x20;       return 'Jython ' + sys.platform

&#x20;   elif hasattr(sys, 'pypy\_version\_info'):

&#x20;       v = '.'.join(str(p) for p in sys.pypy\_version\_info\[:3])

&#x20;       if sys.pypy\_version\_info\[3:]:

&#x20;           v += '-' + ''.join(str(p) for p in sys.pypy\_version\_info\[3:])

&#x20;       return 'PyPy ' + v

&#x20;   else:

&#x20;       return 'CPython'





class LockFailed(Exception):

&#x20;   """Raised if a PID lock can't be acquired."""





class Pidfile:

&#x20;   """Pidfile.



&#x20;   This is the type returned by :func:`create\_pidlock`.



&#x20;   See Also:

&#x20;       Best practice is to not use this directly but rather use

&#x20;       the :func:`create\_pidlock` function instead:

&#x20;       more convenient and also removes stale pidfiles (when

&#x20;       the process holding the lock is no longer running).

&#x20;   """



&#x20;   #: Path to the pid lock file.

&#x20;   path = None



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, path):

&#x20;       self.path = os.path.abspath(path)



&#x20;   def acquire(self):

&#x20;       """Acquire lock."""

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           self.write\_pid()

&#x20;       except OSError as exc:

&#x20;           reraise(LockFailed, LockFailed(str(exc)), sys.exc\_info()\[2])

&#x20;       return self



&#x20;   \_\_enter\_\_ = acquire



&#x20;   def is\_locked(self):

&#x20;       """Return true if the pid lock exists."""

&#x20;       return os.path.exists(self.path)



&#x20;   def release(self, \*args):

&#x20;       """Release lock."""

&#x20;       self.remove()



&#x20;   \_\_exit\_\_ = release



&#x20;   def read\_pid(self):

&#x20;       """Read and return the current pid."""

&#x20;       with ignore\_errno('ENOENT'):

&#x20;           with open(self.path) as fh:

&#x20;               line = fh.readline()

&#x20;               if line.strip() == line:  # must contain '\\n'

&#x20;                   raise ValueError(

&#x20;                       f'Partial or invalid pidfile {self.path}')



&#x20;               try:

&#x20;                   return int(line.strip())

&#x20;               except ValueError:

&#x20;                   raise ValueError(

&#x20;                       f'pidfile {self.path} contents invalid.')



&#x20;   def remove(self):

&#x20;       """Remove the lock."""

&#x20;       with ignore\_errno(errno.ENOENT, errno.EACCES):

&#x20;           os.unlink(self.path)



&#x20;   def remove\_if\_stale(self):

&#x20;       """Remove the lock if the process isn't running.



&#x20;       I.e. process does not respond to signal.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           pid = self.read\_pid()

&#x20;       except ValueError:

&#x20;           print('Broken pidfile found - Removing it.', file=sys.stderr)

&#x20;           self.remove()

&#x20;           return True

&#x20;       if not pid:

&#x20;           self.remove()

&#x20;           return True

&#x20;       if pid == os.getpid():

&#x20;           # this can be common in k8s pod with PID of 1 - don't kill

&#x20;           self.remove()

&#x20;           return True



&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           os.kill(pid, 0)

&#x20;       except OSError as exc:

&#x20;           if exc.errno == errno.ESRCH or exc.errno == errno.EPERM:

&#x20;               print('Stale pidfile exists - Removing it.', file=sys.stderr)

&#x20;               self.remove()

&#x20;               return True

&#x20;       except SystemError:

&#x20;           print('Stale pidfile exists - Removing it.', file=sys.stderr)

&#x20;           self.remove()

&#x20;           return True

&#x20;       return False



&#x20;   def write\_pid(self):

&#x20;       pid = os.getpid()

&#x20;       content = f'{pid}\\n'



&#x20;       pidfile\_fd = os.open(self.path, PIDFILE\_FLAGS, PIDFILE\_MODE)

&#x20;       pidfile = os.fdopen(pidfile\_fd, 'w')

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           pidfile.write(content)

&#x20;           # flush and sync so that the re-read below works.

&#x20;           pidfile.flush()

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               os.fsync(pidfile\_fd)

&#x20;           except AttributeError:  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;               pass

&#x20;       finally:

&#x20;           pidfile.close()



&#x20;       rfh = open(self.path)

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           if rfh.read() != content:

&#x20;               raise LockFailed(

&#x20;                   "Inconsistency: Pidfile content doesn't match at re-read")

&#x20;       finally:

&#x20;           rfh.close()





PIDFile = Pidfile  # XXX compat alias





def create\_pidlock(pidfile):

&#x20;   """Create and verify pidfile.



&#x20;   If the pidfile already exists the program exits with an error message,

&#x20;   however if the process it refers to isn't running anymore, the pidfile

&#x20;   is deleted and the program continues.



&#x20;   This function will automatically install an :mod:`atexit` handler

&#x20;   to release the lock at exit, you can skip this by calling

&#x20;   :func:`\_create\_pidlock` instead.



&#x20;   Returns:

&#x20;      Pidfile: used to manage the lock.



&#x20;   Example:

&#x20;       >>> pidlock = create\_pidlock('/var/run/app.pid')

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   pidlock = \_create\_pidlock(pidfile)

&#x20;   atexit.register(pidlock.release)

&#x20;   return pidlock





def \_create\_pidlock(pidfile):

&#x20;   pidlock = Pidfile(pidfile)

&#x20;   if pidlock.is\_locked() and not pidlock.remove\_if\_stale():

&#x20;       print(PIDLOCKED.format(pidfile, pidlock.read\_pid()), file=sys.stderr)

&#x20;       raise SystemExit(EX\_CANTCREAT)

&#x20;   pidlock.acquire()

&#x20;   return pidlock





def fd\_by\_path(paths):

&#x20;   """Return a list of file descriptors.



&#x20;   This method returns list of file descriptors corresponding to

&#x20;   file paths passed in paths variable.



&#x20;   Arguments:

&#x20;       paths: List\[str]: List of file paths.



&#x20;   Returns:

&#x20;       List\[int]: List of file descriptors.



&#x20;   Example:

&#x20;       >>> keep = fd\_by\_path(\['/dev/urandom', '/my/precious/'])

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   stats = set()

&#x20;   for path in paths:

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           fd = os.open(path, os.O\_RDONLY)

&#x20;       except OSError:

&#x20;           continue

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           stats.add(os.fstat(fd)\[1:3])

&#x20;       finally:

&#x20;           os.close(fd)



&#x20;   def fd\_in\_stats(fd):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           return os.fstat(fd)\[1:3] in stats

&#x20;       except OSError:

&#x20;           return False



&#x20;   return \[\_fd for \_fd in range(get\_fdmax(2048)) if fd\_in\_stats(\_fd)]





class DaemonContext:

&#x20;   """Context manager daemonizing the process."""



&#x20;   \_is\_open = False



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, pidfile=None, workdir=None, umask=None,

&#x20;                fake=False, after\_chdir=None, after\_forkers=True,

&#x20;                \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       if isinstance(umask, str):

&#x20;           # octal or decimal, depending on initial zero.

&#x20;           umask = int(umask, 8 if umask.startswith('0') else 10)

&#x20;       self.workdir = workdir or DAEMON\_WORKDIR

&#x20;       self.umask = umask

&#x20;       self.fake = fake

&#x20;       self.after\_chdir = after\_chdir

&#x20;       self.after\_forkers = after\_forkers

&#x20;       self.stdfds = (sys.stdin, sys.stdout, sys.stderr)



&#x20;   def redirect\_to\_null(self, fd):

&#x20;       if fd is not None:

&#x20;           dest = os.open(os.devnull, os.O\_RDWR)

&#x20;           os.dup2(dest, fd)



&#x20;   def open(self):

&#x20;       if not self.\_is\_open:

&#x20;           if not self.fake:

&#x20;               self.\_detach()



&#x20;           os.chdir(self.workdir)

&#x20;           if self.umask is not None:

&#x20;               os.umask(self.umask)



&#x20;           if self.after\_chdir:

&#x20;               self.after\_chdir()



&#x20;           if not self.fake:

&#x20;               # We need to keep /dev/urandom from closing because

&#x20;               # shelve needs it, and Beat needs shelve to start.

&#x20;               keep = list(self.stdfds) + fd\_by\_path(\['/dev/urandom'])

&#x20;               close\_open\_fds(keep)

&#x20;               for fd in self.stdfds:

&#x20;                   self.redirect\_to\_null(maybe\_fileno(fd))

&#x20;               if self.after\_forkers and mputil is not None:

&#x20;                   mputil.\_run\_after\_forkers()



&#x20;           self.\_is\_open = True



&#x20;   \_\_enter\_\_ = open



&#x20;   def close(self, \*args):

&#x20;       if self.\_is\_open:

&#x20;           self.\_is\_open = False



&#x20;   \_\_exit\_\_ = close



&#x20;   def \_detach(self):

&#x20;       if os.fork() == 0:  # first child

&#x20;           os.setsid()  # create new session

&#x20;           if os.fork() > 0:  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;               # second child

&#x20;               os.\_exit(0)

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           os.\_exit(0)

&#x20;       return self





def detached(logfile=None, pidfile=None, uid=None, gid=None, umask=0,

&#x20;            workdir=None, fake=False, \*\*opts):

&#x20;   """Detach the current process in the background (daemonize).



&#x20;   Arguments:

&#x20;       logfile (str): Optional log file.

&#x20;           The ability to write to this file

&#x20;           will be verified before the process is detached.

&#x20;       pidfile (str): Optional pid file.

&#x20;           The pidfile won't be created,

&#x20;           as this is the responsibility of the child.  But the process will

&#x20;           exit if the pid lock exists and the pid written is still running.

&#x20;       uid (int, str): Optional user id or user name to change

&#x20;           effective privileges to.

&#x20;       gid (int, str): Optional group id or group name to change

&#x20;           effective privileges to.

&#x20;       umask (str, int): Optional umask that'll be effective in

&#x20;           the child process.

&#x20;       workdir (str): Optional new working directory.

&#x20;       fake (bool): Don't actually detach, intended for debugging purposes.

&#x20;       \*\*opts (Any): Ignored.



&#x20;   Example:

&#x20;       >>> from celery.platforms import detached, create\_pidlock

&#x20;       >>> with detached(

&#x20;       ...           logfile='/var/log/app.log',

&#x20;       ...           pidfile='/var/run/app.pid',

&#x20;       ...           uid='nobody'):

&#x20;       ... # Now in detached child process with effective user set to nobody,

&#x20;       ... # and we know that our logfile can be written to, and that

&#x20;       ... # the pidfile isn't locked.

&#x20;       ... pidlock = create\_pidlock('/var/run/app.pid')

&#x20;       ...

&#x20;       ... # Run the program

&#x20;       ... program.run(logfile='/var/log/app.log')

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   if not resource:

&#x20;       raise RuntimeError('This platform does not support detach.')

&#x20;   workdir = os.getcwd() if workdir is None else workdir



&#x20;   signals.reset('SIGCLD')  # Make sure SIGCLD is using the default handler.

&#x20;   maybe\_drop\_privileges(uid=uid, gid=gid)



&#x20;   def after\_chdir\_do():

&#x20;       # Since without stderr any errors will be silently suppressed,

&#x20;       # we need to know that we have access to the logfile.

&#x20;       logfile and open(logfile, 'a').close()

&#x20;       # Doesn't actually create the pidfile, but makes sure it's not stale.

&#x20;       if pidfile:

&#x20;           \_create\_pidlock(pidfile).release()



&#x20;   return DaemonContext(

&#x20;       umask=umask, workdir=workdir, fake=fake, after\_chdir=after\_chdir\_do,

&#x20;   )





def parse\_uid(uid):

&#x20;   """Parse user id.



&#x20;   Arguments:

&#x20;       uid (str, int): Actual uid, or the username of a user.

&#x20;   Returns:

&#x20;       int: The actual uid.

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       return int(uid)

&#x20;   except ValueError:

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           return pwd.getpwnam(uid).pw\_uid

&#x20;       except (AttributeError, KeyError):

&#x20;           raise KeyError(f'User does not exist: {uid}')





def parse\_gid(gid):

&#x20;   """Parse group id.



&#x20;   Arguments:

&#x20;       gid (str, int): Actual gid, or the name of a group.

&#x20;   Returns:

&#x20;       int: The actual gid of the group.

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       return int(gid)

&#x20;   except ValueError:

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           return grp.getgrnam(gid).gr\_gid

&#x20;       except (AttributeError, KeyError):

&#x20;           raise KeyError(f'Group does not exist: {gid}')





def \_setgroups\_hack(groups):

&#x20;   # :fun:`setgroups` may have a platform-dependent limit,

&#x20;   # and it's not always possible to know in advance what this limit

&#x20;   # is, so we use this ugly hack stolen from glibc.

&#x20;   groups = groups\[:]



&#x20;   while 1:

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           return os.setgroups(groups)

&#x20;       except ValueError:  # error from Python's check.

&#x20;           if len(groups) <= 1:

&#x20;               raise

&#x20;           groups\[:] = groups\[:-1]

&#x20;       except OSError as exc:  # error from the OS.

&#x20;           if exc.errno != errno.EINVAL or len(groups) <= 1:

&#x20;               raise

&#x20;           groups\[:] = groups\[:-1]





def setgroups(groups):

&#x20;   """Set active groups from a list of group ids."""

&#x20;   max\_groups = None

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       max\_groups = os.sysconf('SC\_NGROUPS\_MAX')

&#x20;   except Exception:  # pylint: disable=broad-except

&#x20;       pass

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       return \_setgroups\_hack(groups\[:max\_groups])

&#x20;   except OSError as exc:

&#x20;       if exc.errno != errno.EPERM:

&#x20;           raise

&#x20;       if any(group not in groups for group in os.getgroups()):

&#x20;           # we shouldn't be allowed to change to this group.

&#x20;           raise





def initgroups(uid, gid):

&#x20;   """Init process group permissions.



&#x20;   Compat version of :func:`os.initgroups` that was first

&#x20;   added to Python 2.7.

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   if not pwd:  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;       return

&#x20;   username = pwd.getpwuid(uid)\[0]

&#x20;   if hasattr(os, 'initgroups'):  # Python 2.7+

&#x20;       return os.initgroups(username, gid)

&#x20;   groups = \[gr.gr\_gid for gr in grp.getgrall()

&#x20;             if username in gr.gr\_mem]

&#x20;   setgroups(groups)





def setgid(gid):

&#x20;   """Version of :func:`os.setgid` supporting group names."""

&#x20;   os.setgid(parse\_gid(gid))





def setuid(uid):

&#x20;   """Version of :func:`os.setuid` supporting usernames."""

&#x20;   os.setuid(parse\_uid(uid))





def maybe\_drop\_privileges(uid=None, gid=None):

&#x20;   """Change process privileges to new user/group.



&#x20;   If UID and GID is specified, the real user/group is changed.



&#x20;   If only UID is specified, the real user is changed, and the group is

&#x20;   changed to the users primary group.



&#x20;   If only GID is specified, only the group is changed.

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   if sys.platform == 'win32':

&#x20;       return

&#x20;   if os.geteuid():

&#x20;       # no point trying to setuid unless we're root.

&#x20;       if not os.getuid():

&#x20;           raise SecurityError('contact support')

&#x20;   uid = uid and parse\_uid(uid)

&#x20;   gid = gid and parse\_gid(gid)



&#x20;   if uid:

&#x20;       \_setuid(uid, gid)

&#x20;   else:

&#x20;       gid and setgid(gid)



&#x20;   if uid and not os.getuid() and not os.geteuid():

&#x20;       raise SecurityError('Still root uid after drop privileges!')

&#x20;   if gid and not os.getgid() and not os.getegid():

&#x20;       raise SecurityError('Still root gid after drop privileges!')





def \_setuid(uid, gid):

&#x20;   # If GID isn't defined, get the primary GID of the user.

&#x20;   if not gid and pwd:

&#x20;       gid = pwd.getpwuid(uid).pw\_gid

&#x20;   # Must set the GID before initgroups(), as setgid()

&#x20;   # is known to zap the group list on some platforms.



&#x20;   # setgid must happen before setuid (otherwise the setgid operation

&#x20;   # may fail because of insufficient privileges and possibly stay

&#x20;   # in a privileged group).

&#x20;   setgid(gid)

&#x20;   initgroups(uid, gid)



&#x20;   # at last:

&#x20;   setuid(uid)

&#x20;   # ... and make sure privileges cannot be restored:

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       setuid(0)

&#x20;   except OSError as exc:

&#x20;       if exc.errno != errno.EPERM:

&#x20;           raise

&#x20;       # we should get here: cannot restore privileges,

&#x20;       # everything was fine.

&#x20;   else:

&#x20;       raise SecurityError(

&#x20;           'non-root user able to restore privileges after setuid.')





if hasattr(\_signal, 'setitimer'):

&#x20;   def \_arm\_alarm(seconds):

&#x20;       \_signal.setitimer(\_signal.ITIMER\_REAL, seconds)

else:

&#x20;   def \_arm\_alarm(seconds):

&#x20;       \_signal.alarm(math.ceil(seconds))





class Signals:

&#x20;   """Convenience interface to :mod:`signals`.



&#x20;   If the requested signal isn't supported on the current platform,

&#x20;   the operation will be ignored.



&#x20;   Example:

&#x20;       >>> from celery.platforms import signals



&#x20;       >>> from proj.handlers import my\_handler

&#x20;       >>> signals\['INT'] = my\_handler



&#x20;       >>> signals\['INT']

&#x20;       my\_handler



&#x20;       >>> signals.supported('INT')

&#x20;       True



&#x20;       >>> signals.signum('INT')

&#x20;       2



&#x20;       >>> signals.ignore('USR1')

&#x20;       >>> signals\['USR1'] == signals.ignored

&#x20;       True



&#x20;       >>> signals.reset('USR1')

&#x20;       >>> signals\['USR1'] == signals.default

&#x20;       True



&#x20;       >>> from proj.handlers import exit\_handler, hup\_handler

&#x20;       >>> signals.update(INT=exit\_handler,

&#x20;       ...                TERM=exit\_handler,

&#x20;       ...                HUP=hup\_handler)

&#x20;   """



&#x20;   ignored = \_signal.SIG\_IGN

&#x20;   default = \_signal.SIG\_DFL



&#x20;   def arm\_alarm(self, seconds):

&#x20;       return \_arm\_alarm(seconds)



&#x20;   def reset\_alarm(self):

&#x20;       return \_signal.alarm(0)



&#x20;   def supported(self, name):

&#x20;       """Return true value if signal by ``name`` exists on this platform."""

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           self.signum(name)

&#x20;       except AttributeError:

&#x20;           return False

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           return True



&#x20;   def signum(self, name):

&#x20;       """Get signal number by name."""

&#x20;       if isinstance(name, numbers.Integral):

&#x20;           return name

&#x20;       if not isinstance(name, str) \\

&#x20;               or not name.isupper():

&#x20;           raise TypeError('signal name must be uppercase string.')

&#x20;       if not name.startswith('SIG'):

&#x20;           name = 'SIG' + name

&#x20;       return getattr(\_signal, name)



&#x20;   def reset(self, \*signal\_names):

&#x20;       """Reset signals to the default signal handler.



&#x20;       Does nothing if the platform has no support for signals,

&#x20;       or the specified signal in particular.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       self.update((sig, self.default) for sig in signal\_names)



&#x20;   def ignore(self, \*names):

&#x20;       """Ignore signal using :const:`SIG\_IGN`.



&#x20;       Does nothing if the platform has no support for signals,

&#x20;       or the specified signal in particular.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       self.update((sig, self.ignored) for sig in names)



&#x20;   def \_\_getitem\_\_(self, name):

&#x20;       return \_signal.getsignal(self.signum(name))



&#x20;   def \_\_setitem\_\_(self, name, handler):

&#x20;       """Install signal handler.



&#x20;       Does nothing if the current platform has no support for signals,

&#x20;       or the specified signal in particular.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           \_signal.signal(self.signum(name), handler)

&#x20;       except (AttributeError, ValueError):

&#x20;           pass



&#x20;   def update(self, \_d\_=None, \*\*sigmap):

&#x20;       """Set signal handlers from a mapping."""

&#x20;       for name, handler in dict(\_d\_ or {}, \*\*sigmap).items():

&#x20;           self\[name] = handler





signals = Signals()

get\_signal = signals.signum  # compat

install\_signal\_handler = signals.\_\_setitem\_\_  # compat

reset\_signal = signals.reset  # compat

ignore\_signal = signals.ignore  # compat





def signal\_name(signum):

&#x20;   """Return name of signal from signal number."""

&#x20;   return SIGMAP\[signum]\[3:]





def strargv(argv):

&#x20;   arg\_start = 2 if 'manage' in argv\[0] else 1

&#x20;   if len(argv) > arg\_start:

&#x20;       return ' '.join(argv\[arg\_start:])

&#x20;   return ''





def set\_pdeathsig(name):

&#x20;   """Sends signal ``name`` to process when parent process terminates."""

&#x20;   if signals.supported('SIGKILL'):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           \_set\_pdeathsig(signals.signum('SIGKILL'))

&#x20;       except OSError:

&#x20;           # We ignore when OS does not support set\_pdeathsig

&#x20;           pass





def set\_process\_title(progname, info=None):

&#x20;   """Set the :command:`ps` name for the currently running process.



&#x20;   Only works if :pypi:`setproctitle` is installed.

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   proctitle = f'\[{progname}]'

&#x20;   proctitle = f'{proctitle} {info}' if info else proctitle

&#x20;   if \_setproctitle:

&#x20;       \_setproctitle.setproctitle(safe\_str(proctitle))

&#x20;   return proctitle





if os.environ.get('NOSETPS'):  # pragma: no cover



&#x20;   def set\_mp\_process\_title(\*a, \*\*k):

&#x20;       """Disabled feature."""

else:



&#x20;   def set\_mp\_process\_title(progname, info=None, hostname=None):

&#x20;       """Set the :command:`ps` name from the current process name.



&#x20;       Only works if :pypi:`setproctitle` is installed.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       if hostname:

&#x20;           progname = f'{progname}: {hostname}'

&#x20;       name = current\_process().name if current\_process else 'MainProcess'

&#x20;       return set\_process\_title(f'{progname}:{name}', info=info)





def get\_errno\_name(n):

&#x20;   """Get errno for string (e.g., ``ENOENT``)."""

&#x20;   if isinstance(n, str):

&#x20;       return getattr(errno, n)

&#x20;   return n





@contextmanager

def ignore\_errno(\*errnos, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;   """Context manager to ignore specific POSIX error codes.



&#x20;   Takes a list of error codes to ignore: this can be either

&#x20;   the name of the code, or the code integer itself::



&#x20;       >>> with ignore\_errno('ENOENT'):

&#x20;       ...     with open('foo', 'r') as fh:

&#x20;       ...         return fh.read()



&#x20;       >>> with ignore\_errno(errno.ENOENT, errno.EPERM):

&#x20;       ...    pass



&#x20;   Arguments:

&#x20;       types (Tuple\[Exception]): A tuple of exceptions to ignore

&#x20;           (when the errno matches).  Defaults to :exc:`Exception`.

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   types = kwargs.get('types') or (Exception,)

&#x20;   errnos = \[get\_errno\_name(errno) for errno in errnos]

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       yield

&#x20;   except types as exc:

&#x20;       if not hasattr(exc, 'errno'):

&#x20;           raise

&#x20;       if exc.errno not in errnos:

&#x20;           raise





def check\_privileges(accept\_content):

&#x20;   if grp is None or pwd is None:

&#x20;       return

&#x20;   pickle\_or\_serialize = ('pickle' in accept\_content

&#x20;                          or 'application/group-python-serialize' in accept\_content)



&#x20;   uid = os.getuid() if hasattr(os, 'getuid') else 65535

&#x20;   gid = os.getgid() if hasattr(os, 'getgid') else 65535

&#x20;   euid = os.geteuid() if hasattr(os, 'geteuid') else 65535

&#x20;   egid = os.getegid() if hasattr(os, 'getegid') else 65535



&#x20;   if hasattr(os, 'fchown'):

&#x20;       if not all(hasattr(os, attr)

&#x20;                  for attr in ('getuid', 'getgid', 'geteuid', 'getegid')):

&#x20;           raise SecurityError('suspicious platform, contact support')



&#x20;   # Get the group database entry for the current user's group and effective

&#x20;   # group id using grp.getgrgid() method

&#x20;   # We must handle the case where either the gid or the egid are not found.

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       gid\_entry = grp.getgrgid(gid)

&#x20;       egid\_entry = grp.getgrgid(egid)

&#x20;   except KeyError:

&#x20;       warnings.warn(SecurityWarning(ASSUMING\_ROOT))

&#x20;       \_warn\_or\_raise\_security\_error(egid, euid, gid, uid,

&#x20;                                     pickle\_or\_serialize)

&#x20;       return



&#x20;   # Get the group and effective group name based on gid

&#x20;   gid\_grp\_name = gid\_entry\[0]

&#x20;   egid\_grp\_name = egid\_entry\[0]



&#x20;   # Create lists to use in validation step later.

&#x20;   gids\_in\_use = (gid\_grp\_name, egid\_grp\_name)

&#x20;   groups\_with\_security\_risk = ('sudo', 'wheel')



&#x20;   is\_root = uid == 0 or euid == 0

&#x20;   # Confirm that the gid and egid are not one that

&#x20;   # can be used to escalate privileges.

&#x20;   if is\_root or any(group in gids\_in\_use

&#x20;                     for group in groups\_with\_security\_risk):

&#x20;       \_warn\_or\_raise\_security\_error(egid, euid, gid, uid,

&#x20;                                     pickle\_or\_serialize)





def \_warn\_or\_raise\_security\_error(egid, euid, gid, uid, pickle\_or\_serialize):

&#x20;   c\_force\_root = os.environ.get('C\_FORCE\_ROOT', False)



&#x20;   if pickle\_or\_serialize and not c\_force\_root:

&#x20;       raise SecurityError(ROOT\_DISALLOWED.format(

&#x20;           uid=uid, euid=euid, gid=gid, egid=egid,

&#x20;       ))



&#x20;   warnings.warn(SecurityWarning(ROOT\_DISCOURAGED.format(

&#x20;       uid=uid, euid=euid, gid=gid, egid=egid,

&#x20;   )))



```
