```python

"""Task results/state and results for groups of tasks."""



import datetime

import time

from collections import deque

from contextlib import contextmanager

from weakref import proxy



from dateutil.parser import isoparse

from kombu.utils.objects import cached\_property

from vine import Thenable, barrier, promise



from . import current\_app, states

from .\_state import \_set\_task\_join\_will\_block, task\_join\_will\_block

from .app import app\_or\_default

from .exceptions import ImproperlyConfigured, IncompleteStream, TimeoutError

from .utils.graph import DependencyGraph, GraphFormatter



try:

&#x20;   import tblib

except ImportError:

&#x20;   tblib = None



\_\_all\_\_ = (

&#x20;   'ResultBase', 'AsyncResult', 'ResultSet',

&#x20;   'GroupResult', 'EagerResult', 'result\_from\_tuple',

)



E\_WOULDBLOCK = """\\

Never call result.get() within a task!

See https://docs.celeryq.dev/en/latest/userguide/tasks.html\\

\#avoid-launching-synchronous-subtasks

"""





def assert\_will\_not\_block():

&#x20;   if task\_join\_will\_block():

&#x20;       raise RuntimeError(E\_WOULDBLOCK)





@contextmanager

def allow\_join\_result():

&#x20;   reset\_value = task\_join\_will\_block()

&#x20;   \_set\_task\_join\_will\_block(False)

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       yield

&#x20;   finally:

&#x20;       \_set\_task\_join\_will\_block(reset\_value)





@contextmanager

def denied\_join\_result():

&#x20;   reset\_value = task\_join\_will\_block()

&#x20;   \_set\_task\_join\_will\_block(True)

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       yield

&#x20;   finally:

&#x20;       \_set\_task\_join\_will\_block(reset\_value)





class ResultBase:

&#x20;   """Base class for results."""



&#x20;   #: Parent result (if part of a chain)

&#x20;   parent = None





@Thenable.register

class AsyncResult(ResultBase):

&#x20;   """Query task state.



&#x20;   Arguments:

&#x20;       id (str): See :attr:`id`.

&#x20;       backend (Backend): See :attr:`backend`.

&#x20;   """



&#x20;   app = None



&#x20;   #: Error raised for timeouts.

&#x20;   TimeoutError = TimeoutError



&#x20;   #: The task's UUID.

&#x20;   id = None



&#x20;   #: The task result backend to use.

&#x20;   backend = None



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, id, backend=None,

&#x20;                task\_name=None,            # deprecated

&#x20;                app=None, parent=None):

&#x20;       if id is None:

&#x20;           raise ValueError(

&#x20;               f'AsyncResult requires valid id, not {type(id)}')

&#x20;       self.app = app\_or\_default(app or self.app)

&#x20;       self.id = id

&#x20;       self.backend = backend or self.app.backend

&#x20;       self.parent = parent

&#x20;       self.on\_ready = promise(self.\_on\_fulfilled, weak=True)

&#x20;       self.\_cache = None

&#x20;       self.\_ignored = False



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def ignored(self):

&#x20;       """If True, task result retrieval is disabled."""

&#x20;       if hasattr(self, '\_ignored'):

&#x20;           return self.\_ignored

&#x20;       return False



&#x20;   @ignored.setter

&#x20;   def ignored(self, value):

&#x20;       """Enable/disable task result retrieval."""

&#x20;       self.\_ignored = value



&#x20;   def then(self, callback, on\_error=None, weak=False):

&#x20;       self.backend.add\_pending\_result(self, weak=weak)

&#x20;       return self.on\_ready.then(callback, on\_error)



&#x20;   def \_on\_fulfilled(self, result):

&#x20;       self.backend.remove\_pending\_result(self)

&#x20;       return result



&#x20;   def as\_tuple(self):

&#x20;       parent = self.parent

&#x20;       return (self.id, parent and parent.as\_tuple()), None



&#x20;   def as\_list(self):

&#x20;       """Return as a list of task IDs."""

&#x20;       results = \[]

&#x20;       parent = self.parent

&#x20;       results.append(self.id)

&#x20;       if parent is not None:

&#x20;           results.extend(parent.as\_list())

&#x20;       return results



&#x20;   def forget(self):

&#x20;       """Forget the result of this task and its parents."""

&#x20;       self.\_cache = None

&#x20;       if self.parent:

&#x20;           self.parent.forget()



&#x20;       self.backend.remove\_pending\_result(self)

&#x20;       self.backend.forget(self.id)



&#x20;   def revoke(self, connection=None, terminate=False, signal=None,

&#x20;              wait=False, timeout=None):

&#x20;       """Send revoke signal to all workers.



&#x20;       Any worker receiving the task, or having reserved the

&#x20;       task, \*must\* ignore it.



&#x20;       Arguments:

&#x20;           terminate (bool): Also terminate the process currently working

&#x20;               on the task (if any).

&#x20;           signal (str): Name of signal to send to process if terminate.

&#x20;               Default is TERM.

&#x20;           wait (bool): Wait for replies from workers.

&#x20;               The ``timeout`` argument specifies the seconds to wait.

&#x20;               Disabled by default.

&#x20;           timeout (float): Time in seconds to wait for replies when

&#x20;               ``wait`` is enabled.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       self.app.control.revoke(self.id, connection=connection,

&#x20;                               terminate=terminate, signal=signal,

&#x20;                               reply=wait, timeout=timeout)



&#x20;   def revoke\_by\_stamped\_headers(self, headers, connection=None, terminate=False, signal=None,

&#x20;                                 wait=False, timeout=None):

&#x20;       """Send revoke signal to all workers only for tasks with matching headers values.



&#x20;       Any worker receiving the task, or having reserved the

&#x20;       task, \*must\* ignore it.

&#x20;       All header fields \*must\* match.



&#x20;       Arguments:

&#x20;           headers (dict\[str, Union(str, list)]): Headers to match when revoking tasks.

&#x20;           terminate (bool): Also terminate the process currently working

&#x20;               on the task (if any).

&#x20;           signal (str): Name of signal to send to process if terminate.

&#x20;               Default is TERM.

&#x20;           wait (bool): Wait for replies from workers.

&#x20;               The ``timeout`` argument specifies the seconds to wait.

&#x20;               Disabled by default.

&#x20;           timeout (float): Time in seconds to wait for replies when

&#x20;               ``wait`` is enabled.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       self.app.control.revoke\_by\_stamped\_headers(headers, connection=connection,

&#x20;                                                  terminate=terminate, signal=signal,

&#x20;                                                  reply=wait, timeout=timeout)



&#x20;   def get(self, timeout=None, propagate=True, interval=0.5,

&#x20;           no\_ack=True, follow\_parents=True, callback=None, on\_message=None,

&#x20;           on\_interval=None, disable\_sync\_subtasks=True,

&#x20;           EXCEPTION\_STATES=states.EXCEPTION\_STATES,

&#x20;           PROPAGATE\_STATES=states.PROPAGATE\_STATES):

&#x20;       """Wait until task is ready, and return its result.



&#x20;       Warning:

&#x20;          Waiting for tasks within a task may lead to deadlocks.

&#x20;          Please read :ref:`task-synchronous-subtasks`.



&#x20;       Warning:

&#x20;          Backends use resources to store and transmit results. To ensure

&#x20;          that resources are released, you must eventually call

&#x20;          :meth:`\~@AsyncResult.get` or :meth:`\~@AsyncResult.forget` on

&#x20;          EVERY :class:`\~@AsyncResult` instance returned after calling

&#x20;          a task.



&#x20;       Arguments:

&#x20;           timeout (float): How long to wait, in seconds, before the

&#x20;               operation times out. This is the setting for the publisher

&#x20;               (celery client) and is different from `timeout` parameter of

&#x20;               `@app.task`, which is the setting for the worker. The task

&#x20;               isn't terminated even if timeout occurs.

&#x20;           propagate (bool): Re-raise exception if the task failed.

&#x20;           interval (float): Time to wait (in seconds) before retrying to

&#x20;               retrieve the result.  Note that this does not have any effect

&#x20;               when using the RPC/redis result store backends, as they don't

&#x20;               use polling.

&#x20;           no\_ack (bool): Enable amqp no ack (automatically acknowledge

&#x20;               message).  If this is :const:`False` then the message will

&#x20;               \*\*not be acked\*\*.

&#x20;           follow\_parents (bool): Re-raise any exception raised by

&#x20;               parent tasks.

&#x20;           disable\_sync\_subtasks (bool): Disable tasks to wait for sub tasks

&#x20;               this is the default configuration. CAUTION do not enable this

&#x20;               unless you must.



&#x20;       Raises:

&#x20;           celery.exceptions.TimeoutError: if `timeout` isn't

&#x20;               :const:`None` and the result does not arrive within

&#x20;               `timeout` seconds.

&#x20;           Exception: If the remote call raised an exception then that

&#x20;               exception will be re-raised in the caller process.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       if self.ignored:

&#x20;           return



&#x20;       if disable\_sync\_subtasks:

&#x20;           assert\_will\_not\_block()

&#x20;       \_on\_interval = promise()

&#x20;       if follow\_parents and propagate and self.parent:

&#x20;           \_on\_interval = promise(self.\_maybe\_reraise\_parent\_error, weak=True)

&#x20;           self.\_maybe\_reraise\_parent\_error()

&#x20;       if on\_interval:

&#x20;           \_on\_interval.then(on\_interval)



&#x20;       if self.\_cache:

&#x20;           if propagate:

&#x20;               self.maybe\_throw(callback=callback)

&#x20;           return self.result



&#x20;       self.backend.add\_pending\_result(self)

&#x20;       return self.backend.wait\_for\_pending(

&#x20;           self, timeout=timeout,

&#x20;           interval=interval,

&#x20;           on\_interval=\_on\_interval,

&#x20;           no\_ack=no\_ack,

&#x20;           propagate=propagate,

&#x20;           callback=callback,

&#x20;           on\_message=on\_message,

&#x20;       )

&#x20;   wait = get  # deprecated alias to :meth:`get`.



&#x20;   def \_maybe\_reraise\_parent\_error(self):

&#x20;       for node in reversed(list(self.\_parents())):

&#x20;           node.maybe\_throw()



&#x20;   def \_parents(self):

&#x20;       node = self.parent

&#x20;       while node:

&#x20;           yield node

&#x20;           node = node.parent



&#x20;   def collect(self, intermediate=False, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       """Collect results as they return.



&#x20;       Iterator, like :meth:`get` will wait for the task to complete,

&#x20;       but will also follow :class:`AsyncResult` and :class:`ResultSet`

&#x20;       returned by the task, yielding ``(result, value)`` tuples for each

&#x20;       result in the tree.



&#x20;       An example would be having the following tasks:



&#x20;       .. code-block:: python



&#x20;           from celery import group

&#x20;           from proj.celery import app



&#x20;           @app.task(trail=True)

&#x20;           def A(how\_many):

&#x20;               return group(B.s(i) for i in range(how\_many))()



&#x20;           @app.task(trail=True)

&#x20;           def B(i):

&#x20;               return pow2.delay(i)



&#x20;           @app.task(trail=True)

&#x20;           def pow2(i):

&#x20;               return i \*\* 2



&#x20;       .. code-block:: pycon



&#x20;           >>> from celery.result import ResultBase

&#x20;           >>> from proj.tasks import A



&#x20;           >>> result = A.delay(10)

&#x20;           >>> \[v for v in result.collect()

&#x20;           ...  if not isinstance(v, (ResultBase, tuple))]

&#x20;           \[0, 1, 4, 9, 16, 25, 36, 49, 64, 81]



&#x20;       Note:

&#x20;           The ``Task.trail`` option must be enabled

&#x20;           so that the list of children is stored in ``result.children``.

&#x20;           This is the default but enabled explicitly for illustration.



&#x20;       Yields:

&#x20;           Tuple\[AsyncResult, Any]: tuples containing the result instance

&#x20;           of the child task, and the return value of that task.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       for \_, R in self.iterdeps(intermediate=intermediate):

&#x20;           yield R, R.get(\*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def get\_leaf(self):

&#x20;       value = None

&#x20;       for \_, R in self.iterdeps():

&#x20;           value = R.get()

&#x20;       return value



&#x20;   def iterdeps(self, intermediate=False):

&#x20;       stack = deque(\[(None, self)])



&#x20;       is\_incomplete\_stream = not intermediate



&#x20;       while stack:

&#x20;           parent, node = stack.popleft()

&#x20;           yield parent, node

&#x20;           if node.ready():

&#x20;               stack.extend((node, child) for child in node.children or \[])

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               if is\_incomplete\_stream:

&#x20;                   raise IncompleteStream()



&#x20;   def ready(self):

&#x20;       """Return :const:`True` if the task has executed.



&#x20;       If the task is still running, pending, or is waiting

&#x20;       for retry then :const:`False` is returned.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.state in self.backend.READY\_STATES



&#x20;   def successful(self):

&#x20;       """Return :const:`True` if the task executed successfully."""

&#x20;       return self.state == states.SUCCESS



&#x20;   def failed(self):

&#x20;       """Return :const:`True` if the task failed."""

&#x20;       return self.state == states.FAILURE



&#x20;   def throw(self, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       self.on\_ready.throw(\*args, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def maybe\_throw(self, propagate=True, callback=None):

&#x20;       cache = self.\_get\_task\_meta() if self.\_cache is None else self.\_cache

&#x20;       state, value, tb = (

&#x20;           cache\['status'], cache\['result'], cache.get('traceback'))

&#x20;       if state in states.PROPAGATE\_STATES and propagate:

&#x20;           self.throw(value, self.\_to\_remote\_traceback(tb))

&#x20;       if callback is not None:

&#x20;           callback(self.id, value)

&#x20;       return value

&#x20;   maybe\_reraise = maybe\_throw   # XXX compat alias



&#x20;   def \_to\_remote\_traceback(self, tb):

&#x20;       if tb and tblib is not None and self.app.conf.task\_remote\_tracebacks:

&#x20;           return tblib.Traceback.from\_string(tb).as\_traceback()



&#x20;   def build\_graph(self, intermediate=False, formatter=None):

&#x20;       graph = DependencyGraph(

&#x20;           formatter=formatter or GraphFormatter(root=self.id, shape='oval'),

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       for parent, node in self.iterdeps(intermediate=intermediate):

&#x20;           graph.add\_arc(node)

&#x20;           if parent:

&#x20;               graph.add\_edge(parent, node)

&#x20;       return graph



&#x20;   def \_\_str\_\_(self):

&#x20;       """`str(self) -> self.id`."""

&#x20;       return str(self.id)



&#x20;   def \_\_hash\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return hash(self.id)



&#x20;   def \_\_repr\_\_(self):

&#x20;   def \_\_eq\_\_(self, other):

&#x20;       elif isinstance(other, str):

&#x20;       return NotImplemented



&#x20;   def \_\_copy\_\_(self):

&#x20;       )

&#x20;   def \_\_reduce\_args\_\_(self):

&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def \_maybe\_set\_cache(self, meta):

&#x20;       if meta:

&#x20;           state = meta\['status']

&#x20;               d = self.\_set\_cache(self.backend.meta\_from\_decoded(meta))

&#x20;               self.on\_ready(self)

&#x20;               return d

&#x20;       return meta



&#x20;   def \_get\_task\_meta(self):

&#x20;           return self.\_maybe\_set\_cache(self.backend.get\_task\_meta(self.id))

&#x20;       return self.\_cache

&#x20;   def \_set\_cache(self, d):

&#x20;       children = d.get('children')

&#x20;       if children:

&#x20;           d\['children'] = \[

&#x20;               result\_from\_tuple(child, self.app) for child in children

&#x20;           ]

&#x20;       self.\_cache = d

&#x20;       return d



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;       """Task return value.



&#x20;       Note:

&#x20;           When the task has been executed, this contains the return value.

&#x20;           If the task raised an exception, this will be the exception

&#x20;           instance.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_task\_meta()\['result']

&#x20;   info = result



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def traceback(self):

&#x20;       """Get the traceback of a failed task."""

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_task\_meta().get('traceback')



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def state(self):

&#x20;       """The tasks current state.



&#x20;       Possible values includes:



&#x20;           \*PENDING\*



&#x20;               The task is waiting for execution.



&#x20;           \*STARTED\*



&#x20;               The task has been started.



&#x20;           \*RETRY\*



&#x20;               The task is to be retried, possibly because of failure.



&#x20;           \*FAILURE\*



&#x20;               The task raised an exception, or has exceeded the retry limit.

&#x20;               The :attr:`result` attribute then contains the

&#x20;               exception raised by the task.



&#x20;           \*SUCCESS\*



&#x20;               The task executed successfully.  The :attr:`result` attribute

&#x20;               then contains the tasks return value.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_task\_meta()\['status']

&#x20;   @property

&#x20;       return self.id



&#x20;   @task\_id.setter

&#x20;   def task\_id(self, id):

&#x20;       self.id = id



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def name(self):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_task\_meta().get('name')



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def args(self):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_task\_meta().get('args')



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def kwargs(self):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_task\_meta().get('kwargs')



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def worker(self):

&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def date\_done(self):

&#x20;       """UTC date and time."""

&#x20;       date\_done = self.\_get\_task\_meta().get('date\_done')

&#x20;       if date\_done and not isinstance(date\_done, datetime.datetime):

&#x20;           return isoparse(date\_done)

&#x20;       return date\_done



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def retries(self):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_task\_meta().get('retries')



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def queue(self):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_task\_meta().get('queue')





@Thenable.register

class ResultSet(ResultBase):

&#x20;   """A collection of results.



&#x20;   Arguments:

&#x20;       results (Sequence\[AsyncResult]): List of result instances.

&#x20;   """



&#x20;   \_app = None



&#x20;   #: List of results in in the set.

&#x20;   results = None



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, results, app=None, ready\_barrier=None, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       self.\_app = app

&#x20;       self.results = results

&#x20;       self.on\_ready = promise(args=(proxy(self),))

&#x20;       self.\_on\_full = ready\_barrier or barrier(results)

&#x20;       if self.\_on\_full:

&#x20;           self.\_on\_full.then(promise(self.\_on\_ready, weak=True))



&#x20;   def add(self, result):

&#x20;       """Add :class:`AsyncResult` as a new member of the set.



&#x20;       Does nothing if the result is already a member.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       if result not in self.results:

&#x20;           self.results.append(result)

&#x20;           if self.\_on\_full:

&#x20;               self.\_on\_full.add(result)



&#x20;   def \_on\_ready(self):

&#x20;       if self.backend.is\_async:

&#x20;           self.on\_ready()



&#x20;   def remove(self, result):

&#x20;       """Remove result from the set; it must be a member.



&#x20;       Raises:

&#x20;           KeyError: if the result isn't a member.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       if isinstance(result, str):

&#x20;           result = self.app.AsyncResult(result)

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           self.results.remove(result)

&#x20;       except ValueError:

&#x20;           raise KeyError(result)



&#x20;   def discard(self, result):

&#x20;       """Remove result from the set if it is a member.



&#x20;       Does nothing if it's not a member.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           self.remove(result)

&#x20;       except KeyError:

&#x20;           pass



&#x20;   def update(self, results):

&#x20;       """Extend from iterable of results."""

&#x20;       self.results.extend(r for r in results if r not in self.results)



&#x20;   def clear(self):

&#x20;       """Remove all results from this set."""

&#x20;       self.results\[:] = \[]  # don't create new list.



&#x20;   def successful(self):

&#x20;       """Return true if all tasks successful.



&#x20;       Returns:

&#x20;           bool: true if all of the tasks finished

&#x20;               successfully (i.e. didn't raise an exception).

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return all(result.successful() for result in self.results)



&#x20;   def failed(self):

&#x20;       """Return true if any of the tasks failed.



&#x20;       Returns:

&#x20;           bool: true if one of the tasks failed.

&#x20;               (i.e., raised an exception)

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return any(result.failed() for result in self.results)



&#x20;   def maybe\_throw(self, callback=None, propagate=True):

&#x20;       for result in self.results:

&#x20;           result.maybe\_throw(callback=callback, propagate=propagate)

&#x20;   maybe\_reraise = maybe\_throw  # XXX compat alias.



&#x20;   def waiting(self):

&#x20;       """Return true if any of the tasks are incomplete.



&#x20;       Returns:

&#x20;           bool: true if one of the tasks are still

&#x20;               waiting for execution.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return any(not result.ready() for result in self.results)



&#x20;   def ready(self):

&#x20;       """Did all of the tasks complete? (either by success of failure).



&#x20;       Returns:

&#x20;           bool: true if all of the tasks have been executed.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return all(result.ready() for result in self.results)



&#x20;   def completed\_count(self):

&#x20;       """Task completion count.



&#x20;       Note that `complete` means `successful` in this context. In other words, the

&#x20;       return value of this method is the number of ``successful`` tasks.



&#x20;       Returns:

&#x20;           int: the number of complete (i.e. successful) tasks.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return sum(int(result.successful()) for result in self.results)



&#x20;   def forget(self):

&#x20;       """Forget about (and possible remove the result of) all the tasks."""

&#x20;       for result in self.results:

&#x20;           result.forget()



&#x20;   def revoke(self, connection=None, terminate=False, signal=None,

&#x20;              wait=False, timeout=None):

&#x20;       """Send revoke signal to all workers for all tasks in the set.



&#x20;       Arguments:

&#x20;           terminate (bool): Also terminate the process currently working

&#x20;               on the task (if any).

&#x20;           signal (str): Name of signal to send to process if terminate.

&#x20;               Default is TERM.

&#x20;           wait (bool): Wait for replies from worker.

&#x20;               The ``timeout`` argument specifies the number of seconds

&#x20;               to wait.  Disabled by default.

&#x20;           timeout (float): Time in seconds to wait for replies when

&#x20;               the ``wait`` argument is enabled.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       self.app.control.revoke(\[r.id for r in self.results],

&#x20;                               connection=connection, timeout=timeout,

&#x20;                               terminate=terminate, signal=signal, reply=wait)



&#x20;   def \_\_iter\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return iter(self.results)



&#x20;   def \_\_getitem\_\_(self, index):

&#x20;       """`res\[i] -> res.results\[i]`."""

&#x20;       return self.results\[index]



&#x20;   def get(self, timeout=None, propagate=True, interval=0.5,

&#x20;           callback=None, no\_ack=True, on\_message=None,

&#x20;           disable\_sync\_subtasks=True, on\_interval=None):

&#x20;       """See :meth:`join`.



&#x20;       This is here for API compatibility with :class:`AsyncResult`,

&#x20;       in addition it uses :meth:`join\_native` if available for the

&#x20;       current result backend.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return (self.join\_native if self.supports\_native\_join else self.join)(

&#x20;           timeout=timeout, propagate=propagate,

&#x20;           interval=interval, callback=callback, no\_ack=no\_ack,

&#x20;           on\_message=on\_message, disable\_sync\_subtasks=disable\_sync\_subtasks,

&#x20;           on\_interval=on\_interval,

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def join(self, timeout=None, propagate=True, interval=0.5,

&#x20;            callback=None, no\_ack=True, on\_message=None,

&#x20;            disable\_sync\_subtasks=True, on\_interval=None):

&#x20;       """Gather the results of all tasks as a list in order.



&#x20;       Note:

&#x20;           This can be an expensive operation for result store

&#x20;           backends that must resort to polling (e.g., database).



&#x20;           You should consider using :meth:`join\_native` if your backend

&#x20;           supports it.



&#x20;       Warning:

&#x20;           Waiting for tasks within a task may lead to deadlocks.

&#x20;           Please see :ref:`task-synchronous-subtasks`.



&#x20;       Arguments:

&#x20;           timeout (float): The number of seconds to wait for results

&#x20;               before the operation times out.

&#x20;           propagate (bool): If any of the tasks raises an exception,

&#x20;               the exception will be re-raised when this flag is set.

&#x20;           interval (float): Time to wait (in seconds) before retrying to

&#x20;               retrieve a result from the set.  Note that this does not have

&#x20;               any effect when using the amqp result store backend,

&#x20;               as it does not use polling.

&#x20;           callback (Callable): Optional callback to be called for every

&#x20;               result received.  Must have signature ``(task\_id, value)``

&#x20;               No results will be returned by this function if a callback

&#x20;               is specified.  The order of results is also arbitrary when a

&#x20;               callback is used.  To get access to the result object for

&#x20;               a particular id you'll have to generate an index first:

&#x20;               ``index = {r.id: r for r in gres.results.values()}``

&#x20;               Or you can create new result objects on the fly:

&#x20;               ``result = app.AsyncResult(task\_id)`` (both will

&#x20;               take advantage of the backend cache anyway).

&#x20;           no\_ack (bool): Automatic message acknowledgment (Note that if this

&#x20;               is set to :const:`False` then the messages

&#x20;               \*will not be acknowledged\*).

&#x20;           disable\_sync\_subtasks (bool): Disable tasks to wait for sub tasks

&#x20;               this is the default configuration. CAUTION do not enable this

&#x20;               unless you must.



&#x20;       Raises:

&#x20;           celery.exceptions.TimeoutError: if ``timeout`` isn't

&#x20;               :const:`None` and the operation takes longer than ``timeout``

&#x20;               seconds.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       if disable\_sync\_subtasks:

&#x20;           assert\_will\_not\_block()

&#x20;       time\_start = time.monotonic()

&#x20;       remaining = None



&#x20;       if on\_message is not None:

&#x20;           raise ImproperlyConfigured(

&#x20;               'Backend does not support on\_message callback')



&#x20;       results = \[]

&#x20;       for result in self.results:

&#x20;           remaining = None

&#x20;           if timeout:

&#x20;               remaining = timeout - (time.monotonic() - time\_start)

&#x20;               if remaining <= 0.0:

&#x20;                   raise TimeoutError('join operation timed out')

&#x20;           value = result.get(

&#x20;               timeout=remaining, propagate=propagate,

&#x20;               interval=interval, no\_ack=no\_ack, on\_interval=on\_interval,

&#x20;               disable\_sync\_subtasks=disable\_sync\_subtasks,

&#x20;           )

&#x20;           if callback:

&#x20;               callback(result.id, value)

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               results.append(value)

&#x20;       return results



&#x20;   def then(self, callback, on\_error=None, weak=False):

&#x20;       return self.on\_ready.then(callback, on\_error)



&#x20;   def iter\_native(self, timeout=None, interval=0.5, no\_ack=True,

&#x20;                   on\_message=None, on\_interval=None):

&#x20;       """Backend optimized version of :meth:`iterate`.



&#x20;       .. versionadded:: 2.2



&#x20;       Note that this does not support collecting the results

&#x20;       for different task types using different backends.



&#x20;       This is currently only supported by the amqp, Redis and cache

&#x20;       result backends.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.backend.iter\_native(

&#x20;           self,

&#x20;           timeout=timeout, interval=interval, no\_ack=no\_ack,

&#x20;           on\_message=on\_message, on\_interval=on\_interval,

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def join\_native(self, timeout=None, propagate=True,

&#x20;                   interval=0.5, callback=None, no\_ack=True,

&#x20;                   on\_message=None, on\_interval=None,

&#x20;                   disable\_sync\_subtasks=True):

&#x20;       """Backend optimized version of :meth:`join`.



&#x20;       .. versionadded:: 2.2



&#x20;       Note that this does not support collecting the results

&#x20;       for different task types using different backends.



&#x20;       This is currently only supported by the amqp, Redis and cache

&#x20;       result backends.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       if disable\_sync\_subtasks:

&#x20;           assert\_will\_not\_block()

&#x20;       order\_index = None if callback else {

&#x20;           result.id: i for i, result in enumerate(self.results)

&#x20;       }

&#x20;       acc = None if callback else \[None for \_ in range(len(self))]

&#x20;       for task\_id, meta in self.iter\_native(timeout, interval, no\_ack,

&#x20;                                             on\_message, on\_interval):

&#x20;           if isinstance(meta, list):

&#x20;               value = \[]

&#x20;               for children\_result in meta:

&#x20;                   value.append(children\_result.get())

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               value = meta\['result']

&#x20;               if propagate and meta\['status'] in states.PROPAGATE\_STATES:

&#x20;                   raise value

&#x20;           if callback:

&#x20;               callback(task\_id, value)

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               acc\[order\_index\[task\_id]] = value

&#x20;       return acc



&#x20;   def \_iter\_meta(self, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       return (meta for \_, meta in self.backend.get\_many(

&#x20;           {r.id for r in self.results}, max\_iterations=1, \*\*kwargs

&#x20;       ))



&#x20;   def \_failed\_join\_report(self):

&#x20;       return (res for res in self.results

&#x20;               if res.backend.is\_cached(res.id) and

&#x20;               res.state in states.PROPAGATE\_STATES)



&#x20;   def \_\_len\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return len(self.results)



&#x20;   def \_\_eq\_\_(self, other):

&#x20;       if isinstance(other, ResultSet):

&#x20;           return other.results == self.results

&#x20;       return NotImplemented



&#x20;   def \_\_repr\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return f'<{type(self).\_\_name\_\_}: \[{", ".join(r.id for r in self.results)}]>'



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def supports\_native\_join(self):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           return self.results\[0].supports\_native\_join

&#x20;       except IndexError:

&#x20;           pass



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def app(self):

&#x20;       if self.\_app is None:

&#x20;           self.\_app = (self.results\[0].app if self.results else

&#x20;                        current\_app.\_get\_current\_object())

&#x20;       return self.\_app



&#x20;   @app.setter

&#x20;   def app(self, app):

&#x20;       self.\_app = app



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def backend(self):

&#x20;       return self.app.backend if self.app else self.results\[0].backend





@Thenable.register

class GroupResult(ResultSet):

&#x20;   """Like :class:`ResultSet`, but with an associated id.



&#x20;   This type is returned by :class:`\~celery.group`.



&#x20;   It enables inspection of the tasks state and return values as

&#x20;   a single entity.



&#x20;   Arguments:

&#x20;       id (str): The id of the group.

&#x20;       results (Sequence\[AsyncResult]): List of result instances.

&#x20;       parent (ResultBase): Parent result of this group.

&#x20;   """



&#x20;   #: The UUID of the group.

&#x20;   id = None



&#x20;   #: List/iterator of results in the group

&#x20;   results = None



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, id=None, results=None, parent=None, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       self.id = id

&#x20;       self.parent = parent

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(results, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def \_on\_ready(self):

&#x20;       self.backend.remove\_pending\_result(self)

&#x20;       super().\_on\_ready()



&#x20;   def save(self, backend=None):

&#x20;       """Save group-result for later retrieval using :meth:`restore`.



&#x20;       Example:

&#x20;           >>> def save\_and\_restore(result):

&#x20;           ...     result.save()

&#x20;           ...     result = GroupResult.restore(result.id)

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return (backend or self.app.backend).save\_group(self.id, self)



&#x20;   def delete(self, backend=None):

&#x20;       """Remove this result if it was previously saved."""

&#x20;       (backend or self.app.backend).delete\_group(self.id)



&#x20;   def \_\_reduce\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return self.\_\_class\_\_, self.\_\_reduce\_args\_\_()



&#x20;   def \_\_reduce\_args\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return self.id, self.results



&#x20;   def \_\_bool\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return bool(self.id or self.results)

&#x20;   \_\_nonzero\_\_ = \_\_bool\_\_  # Included for Py2 backwards compatibility



&#x20;   def \_\_eq\_\_(self, other):

&#x20;       if isinstance(other, GroupResult):

&#x20;           return (

&#x20;               other.id == self.id and

&#x20;               other.results == self.results and

&#x20;               other.parent == self.parent

&#x20;           )

&#x20;       elif isinstance(other, str):

&#x20;           return other == self.id

&#x20;       return NotImplemented



&#x20;   def \_\_repr\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return f'<{type(self).\_\_name\_\_}: {self.id} \[{", ".join(r.id for r in self.results)}]>'



&#x20;   def \_\_str\_\_(self):

&#x20;       """`str(self) -> self.id`."""

&#x20;       return str(self.id)



&#x20;   def \_\_hash\_\_(self):

&#x20;       """`hash(self) -> hash(self.id)`."""

&#x20;       return hash(self.id)



&#x20;   def as\_tuple(self):

&#x20;       return (

&#x20;           (self.id, self.parent and self.parent.as\_tuple()),

&#x20;           \[r.as\_tuple() for r in self.results]

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def children(self):

&#x20;       return self.results



&#x20;   @classmethod

&#x20;   def restore(cls, id, backend=None, app=None):

&#x20;       """Restore previously saved group result."""

&#x20;       app = app or (

&#x20;           cls.app if not isinstance(cls.app, property) else current\_app

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       backend = backend or app.backend

&#x20;       return backend.restore\_group(id)





@Thenable.register

class EagerResult(AsyncResult):

&#x20;   """Result that we know has already been executed."""



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, id, ret\_value, state, traceback=None, name=None):

&#x20;       # pylint: disable=super-init-not-called

&#x20;       # XXX should really not be inheriting from AsyncResult

&#x20;       self.id = id

&#x20;       self.\_result = ret\_value

&#x20;       self.\_state = state

&#x20;       self.\_traceback = traceback

&#x20;       self.\_name = name

&#x20;       self.on\_ready = promise()

&#x20;       self.on\_ready(self)



&#x20;   def then(self, callback, on\_error=None, weak=False):

&#x20;       return self.on\_ready.then(callback, on\_error)



&#x20;   def \_get\_task\_meta(self):

&#x20;       return self.\_cache



&#x20;   def \_\_reduce\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return self.\_\_class\_\_, self.\_\_reduce\_args\_\_()



&#x20;   def \_\_reduce\_args\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return (self.id, self.\_result, self.\_state, self.\_traceback)



&#x20;   def \_\_copy\_\_(self):

&#x20;       cls, args = self.\_\_reduce\_\_()

&#x20;       return cls(\*args)



&#x20;   def ready(self):

&#x20;       return True



&#x20;   def get(self, timeout=None, propagate=True,

&#x20;           disable\_sync\_subtasks=True, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       if disable\_sync\_subtasks:

&#x20;           assert\_will\_not\_block()



&#x20;       if self.successful():

&#x20;           return self.result

&#x20;       elif self.state in states.PROPAGATE\_STATES:

&#x20;           if propagate:

&#x20;               raise self.result if isinstance(

&#x20;                   self.result, Exception) else Exception(self.result)

&#x20;           return self.result

&#x20;   wait = get  # XXX Compat (remove 5.0)



&#x20;   def forget(self):

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   def revoke(self, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       self.\_state = states.REVOKED



&#x20;   def \_\_repr\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return f'<EagerResult: {self.id}>'



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def \_cache(self):

&#x20;       return {

&#x20;           'task\_id': self.id,

&#x20;           'result': self.\_result,

&#x20;           'status': self.\_state,

&#x20;           'traceback': self.\_traceback,

&#x20;           'name': self.\_name,

&#x20;       }



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def result(self):

&#x20;       """The tasks return value."""

&#x20;       return self.\_result



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def state(self):

&#x20;       """The tasks state."""

&#x20;       return self.\_state

&#x20;   status = state



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def traceback(self):

&#x20;       """The traceback if the task failed."""

&#x20;       return self.\_traceback



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def supports\_native\_join(self):

&#x20;       return False





def result\_from\_tuple(r, app=None):

&#x20;   """Deserialize result from tuple."""

&#x20;   # earlier backends may just pickle, so check if

&#x20;   # result is already prepared.

&#x20;   app = app\_or\_default(app)

&#x20;   Result = app.AsyncResult

&#x20;   if not isinstance(r, ResultBase):

&#x20;       res, nodes = r

&#x20;       id, parent = res if isinstance(res, (list, tuple)) else (res, None)

&#x20;       if parent:

&#x20;           parent = result\_from\_tuple(parent, app)



&#x20;       if nodes is not None:

&#x20;           return app.GroupResult(

&#x20;               id, \[result\_from\_tuple(child, app) for child in nodes],

&#x20;               parent=parent,

&#x20;           )



&#x20;       return Result(id, parent=parent)

&#x20;   return r



```

```python
"""Schedules define the intervals at which periodic tasks run."""

from \_\_future\_\_ import annotations



import re

from bisect import bisect, bisect\_left

from collections import namedtuple

from datetime import datetime, timedelta, tzinfo

from typing import Any, Callable, Iterable, Mapping, Sequence, Union



from kombu.utils.objects import cached\_property



from celery import Celery



from . import current\_app

from .utils.collections import AttributeDict

from .utils.time import (ffwd, humanize\_seconds, localize, maybe\_make\_aware, maybe\_timedelta, remaining, timezone,

&#x20;                        weekday, yearmonth)



\_\_all\_\_ = (

&#x20;   'ParseException', 'schedule', 'crontab', 'crontab\_parser',

&#x20;   'maybe\_schedule', 'solar',

)



schedstate = namedtuple('schedstate', ('is\_due', 'next'))



CRON\_PATTERN\_INVALID = """\\

Invalid crontab pattern.  Valid range is {min}-{max}. \\

'{value}' was found.\\

"""



CRON\_INVALID\_TYPE = """\\

Argument cronspec needs to be of any of the following types: \\

int, str, or an iterable type. {type!r} was given.\\

"""



CRON\_REPR = """\\

<crontab: {0.\_orig\_minute} {0.\_orig\_hour} {0.\_orig\_day\_of\_month} {0.\_orig\_month\_of\_year} \\

{0.\_orig\_day\_of\_week} (m/h/dM/MY/d)>\\

"""



SOLAR\_INVALID\_LATITUDE = """\\

Argument latitude {lat} is invalid, must be between -90 and 90.\\

"""



SOLAR\_INVALID\_LONGITUDE = """\\

Argument longitude {lon} is invalid, must be between -180 and 180.\\

"""



SOLAR\_INVALID\_EVENT = """\\

Argument event "{event}" is invalid, must be one of {all\_events}.\\

"""





Cronspec = Union\[int, str, Iterable\[int]]





def cronfield(s: Cronspec | None) -> Cronspec:

&#x20;   return '\*' if s is None else s





class ParseException(Exception):

&#x20;   """Raised by :class:`crontab\_parser` when the input can't be parsed."""





class BaseSchedule:



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, nowfun: Callable | None = None, app: Celery | None = None):

&#x20;       self.nowfun = nowfun

&#x20;       self.\_app = app



&#x20;   def now(self) -> datetime:

&#x20;       return (self.nowfun or self.app.now)()



&#x20;   def remaining\_estimate(self, last\_run\_at: datetime) -> timedelta:

&#x20;       raise NotImplementedError()



&#x20;   def is\_due(self, last\_run\_at: datetime) -> tuple\[bool, datetime]:

&#x20;       raise NotImplementedError()



&#x20;   def maybe\_make\_aware(

&#x20;           self, dt: datetime, naive\_as\_utc: bool = True) -> datetime:

&#x20;       return maybe\_make\_aware(dt, self.tz, naive\_as\_utc=naive\_as\_utc)



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def app(self) -> Celery:

&#x20;       return self.\_app or current\_app.\_get\_current\_object()



&#x20;   @app.setter

&#x20;   def app(self, app: Celery) -> None:

&#x20;       self.\_app = app



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def tz(self) -> tzinfo:

&#x20;       return self.app.timezone



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def utc\_enabled(self) -> bool:

&#x20;       return self.app.conf.enable\_utc



&#x20;   def to\_local(self, dt: datetime) -> datetime:

&#x20;       if not self.utc\_enabled:

&#x20;           return timezone.to\_local\_fallback(dt)

&#x20;       return dt



&#x20;   def \_\_eq\_\_(self, other: Any) -> bool:

&#x20;       if isinstance(other, BaseSchedule):

&#x20;           return other.nowfun == self.nowfun

&#x20;       return NotImplemented





class schedule(BaseSchedule):

&#x20;   """Schedule for periodic task.



&#x20;   Arguments:

&#x20;       run\_every (float, \~datetime.timedelta): Time interval.

&#x20;       relative (bool):  If set to True the run time will be rounded to the

&#x20;           resolution of the interval.

&#x20;       nowfun (Callable): Function returning the current date and time

&#x20;           (:class:`\~datetime.datetime`).

&#x20;       app (Celery): Celery app instance.

&#x20;   """



&#x20;   relative: bool = False



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, run\_every: float | timedelta | None = None,

&#x20;                relative: bool = False, nowfun: Callable | None = None, app: Celery

&#x20;                | None = None) -> None:

&#x20;       self.run\_every = maybe\_timedelta(run\_every)

&#x20;       self.relative = relative

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(nowfun=nowfun, app=app)



&#x20;   def remaining\_estimate(self, last\_run\_at: datetime) -> timedelta:

&#x20;       return remaining(

&#x20;           self.maybe\_make\_aware(last\_run\_at), self.run\_every,

&#x20;           self.maybe\_make\_aware(self.now()), self.relative,

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def is\_due(self, last\_run\_at: datetime) -> tuple\[bool, datetime]:

&#x20;       """Return tuple of ``(is\_due, next\_time\_to\_check)``.



&#x20;       Notes:

&#x20;           - next time to check is in seconds.



&#x20;           - ``(True, 20)``, means the task should be run now, and the next

&#x20;               time to check is in 20 seconds.



&#x20;           - ``(False, 12.3)``, means the task is not due, but that the

&#x20;             scheduler should check again in 12.3 seconds.



&#x20;       The next time to check is used to save energy/CPU cycles,

&#x20;       it does not need to be accurate but will influence the precision

&#x20;       of your schedule.  You must also keep in mind

&#x20;       the value of :setting:`beat\_max\_loop\_interval`,

&#x20;       that decides the maximum number of seconds the scheduler can

&#x20;       sleep between re-checking the periodic task intervals.  So if you

&#x20;       have a task that changes schedule at run-time then your next\_run\_at

&#x20;       check will decide how long it will take before a change to the

&#x20;       schedule takes effect.  The max loop interval takes precedence

&#x20;       over the next check at value returned.



&#x20;       .. admonition:: Scheduler max interval variance



&#x20;           The default max loop interval may vary for different schedulers.

&#x20;           For the default scheduler the value is 5 minutes, but for example

&#x20;           the :pypi:`django-celery-beat` database scheduler the value

&#x20;           is 5 seconds.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       last\_run\_at = self.maybe\_make\_aware(last\_run\_at)

&#x20;       rem\_delta = self.remaining\_estimate(last\_run\_at)

&#x20;       remaining\_s = max(rem\_delta.total\_seconds(), 0)

&#x20;       if remaining\_s == 0:

&#x20;           return schedstate(is\_due=True, next=self.seconds)

&#x20;       return schedstate(is\_due=False, next=remaining\_s)



&#x20;   def \_\_repr\_\_(self) -> str:

&#x20;       return f'<freq: {self.human\_seconds}>'



&#x20;   def \_\_eq\_\_(self, other: Any) -> bool:

&#x20;       if isinstance(other, schedule):

&#x20;           return self.run\_every == other.run\_every

&#x20;       return self.run\_every == other



&#x20;   def \_\_reduce\_\_(self) -> tuple\[type,

&#x20;                                 tuple\[timedelta, bool, Callable | None]]:

&#x20;       return self.\_\_class\_\_, (self.run\_every, self.relative, self.nowfun)



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def seconds(self) -> int | float:

&#x20;       return max(self.run\_every.total\_seconds(), 0)



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def human\_seconds(self) -> str:

&#x20;       return humanize\_seconds(self.seconds)





class crontab\_parser:

&#x20;   """Parser for Crontab expressions.



&#x20;   Any expression of the form 'groups'

&#x20;   (see BNF grammar below) is accepted and expanded to a set of numbers.

&#x20;   These numbers represent the units of time that the Crontab needs to

&#x20;   run on:



&#x20;   .. code-block:: bnf



&#x20;       digit   :: '0'..'9'

&#x20;       dow     :: 'a'..'z'

&#x20;       number  :: digit+ | dow+

&#x20;       steps   :: number

&#x20;       range   :: number ( '-' number ) ?

&#x20;       numspec :: '\*' | range

&#x20;       expr    :: numspec ( '/' steps ) ?

&#x20;       groups  :: expr ( ',' expr ) \*



&#x20;   The parser is a general purpose one, useful for parsing hours, minutes and

&#x20;   day of week expressions.  Example usage:



&#x20;   .. code-block:: pycon



&#x20;       >>> minutes = crontab\_parser(60).parse('\*/15')

&#x20;       \[0, 15, 30, 45]

&#x20;       >>> hours = crontab\_parser(24).parse('\*/4')

&#x20;       \[0, 4, 8, 12, 16, 20]

&#x20;       >>> day\_of\_week = crontab\_parser(7).parse('\*')

&#x20;       \[0, 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6]



&#x20;   It can also parse day of month and month of year expressions if initialized

&#x20;   with a minimum of 1.  Example usage:



&#x20;   .. code-block:: pycon



&#x20;       >>> days\_of\_month = crontab\_parser(31, 1).parse('\*/3')

&#x20;       \[1, 4, 7, 10, 13, 16, 19, 22, 25, 28, 31]

&#x20;       >>> months\_of\_year = crontab\_parser(12, 1).parse('\*/2')

&#x20;       \[1, 3, 5, 7, 9, 11]

&#x20;       >>> months\_of\_year = crontab\_parser(12, 1).parse('2-12/2')

&#x20;       \[2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12]



&#x20;   The maximum possible expanded value returned is found by the formula:



&#x20;       :math:`max\_ + min\_ - 1`

&#x20;   """



&#x20;   ParseException = ParseException



&#x20;   \_range = r'(\\w+?)-(\\w+)'

&#x20;   \_steps = r'/(\\w+)?'

&#x20;   \_star = r'\\\*'



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, max\_: int = 60, min\_: int = 0):

&#x20;       self.max\_ = max\_

&#x20;       self.min\_ = min\_

&#x20;       self.pats: tuple\[tuple\[re.Pattern, Callable], ...] = (

&#x20;           (re.compile(self.\_range + self.\_steps), self.\_range\_steps),

&#x20;           (re.compile(self.\_range), self.\_expand\_range),

&#x20;           (re.compile(self.\_star + self.\_steps), self.\_star\_steps),

&#x20;           (re.compile('^' + self.\_star + '$'), self.\_expand\_star),

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def parse(self, spec: str) -> set\[int]:

&#x20;       acc = set()

&#x20;       for part in spec.split(','):

&#x20;           if not part:

&#x20;               raise self.ParseException('empty part')

&#x20;           acc |= set(self.\_parse\_part(part))

&#x20;       return acc



&#x20;   def \_parse\_part(self, part: str) -> list\[int]:

&#x20;       for regex, handler in self.pats:

&#x20;           m = regex.match(part)

&#x20;           if m:

&#x20;               return handler(m.groups())

&#x20;       return self.\_expand\_range((part,))



&#x20;   def \_expand\_range(self, toks: Sequence\[str]) -> list\[int]:

&#x20;       fr = self.\_expand\_number(toks\[0])

&#x20;       if len(toks) > 1:

&#x20;           to = self.\_expand\_number(toks\[1])

&#x20;           if to < fr:  # Wrap around max\_ if necessary

&#x20;               return (list(range(fr, self.min\_ + self.max\_)) +

&#x20;                       list(range(self.min\_, to + 1)))

&#x20;           return list(range(fr, to + 1))

&#x20;       return \[fr]



&#x20;   def \_range\_steps(self, toks: Sequence\[str]) -> list\[int]:

&#x20;       if len(toks) != 3 or not toks\[2]:

&#x20;           raise self.ParseException('empty filter')

&#x20;       return self.\_expand\_range(toks\[:2])\[::int(toks\[2])]



&#x20;   def \_star\_steps(self, toks: Sequence\[str]) -> list\[int]:

&#x20;       if not toks or not toks\[0]:

&#x20;           raise self.ParseException('empty filter')

&#x20;       return self.\_expand\_star()\[::int(toks\[0])]



&#x20;   def \_expand\_star(self, \*args: Any) -> list\[int]:

&#x20;       return list(range(self.min\_, self.max\_ + self.min\_))



&#x20;   def \_expand\_number(self, s: str) -> int:

&#x20;       if isinstance(s, str) and s\[0] == '-':

&#x20;           raise self.ParseException('negative numbers not supported')

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           i = int(s)

&#x20;       except ValueError:

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               i = yearmonth(s)

&#x20;           except KeyError:

&#x20;               try:

&#x20;                   i = weekday(s)

&#x20;               except KeyError:

&#x20;                   raise ValueError(f'Invalid weekday literal {s!r}.')



&#x20;       max\_val = self.min\_ + self.max\_ - 1

&#x20;       if i > max\_val:

&#x20;           raise ValueError(

&#x20;               f'Invalid end range: {i} > {max\_val}.')

&#x20;       if i < self.min\_:

&#x20;           raise ValueError(

&#x20;               f'Invalid beginning range: {i} < {self.min\_}.')



&#x20;       return i





class crontab(BaseSchedule):

&#x20;   """Crontab schedule.



&#x20;   A Crontab can be used as the ``run\_every`` value of a

&#x20;   periodic task entry to add :manpage:`crontab(5)`-like scheduling.



&#x20;   Like a :manpage:`cron(5)`-job, you can specify units of time of when

&#x20;   you'd like the task to execute.  It's a reasonably complete

&#x20;   implementation of :command:`cron`'s features, so it should provide a fair

&#x20;   degree of scheduling needs.



&#x20;   You can specify a minute, an hour, a day of the week, a day of the

&#x20;   month, and/or a month in the year in any of the following formats:



&#x20;   .. attribute:: minute



&#x20;       - A (list of) integers from 0-59 that represent the minutes of

&#x20;         an hour of when execution should occur; or

&#x20;       - A string representing a Crontab pattern.  This may get pretty

&#x20;         advanced, like ``minute='\*/15'`` (for every quarter) or

&#x20;         ``minute='1,13,30-45,50-59/2'``.



&#x20;   .. attribute:: hour



&#x20;       - A (list of) integers from 0-23 that represent the hours of

&#x20;         a day of when execution should occur; or

&#x20;       - A string representing a Crontab pattern.  This may get pretty

&#x20;         advanced, like ``hour='\*/3'`` (for every three hours) or

&#x20;         ``hour='0,8-17/2'`` (at midnight, and every two hours during

&#x20;         office hours).



&#x20;   .. attribute:: day\_of\_week



&#x20;       - A (list of) integers from 0-6, where Sunday = 0 and Saturday =

&#x20;         6, that represent the days of a week that execution should

&#x20;         occur.

&#x20;       - A string representing a Crontab pattern.  This may get pretty

&#x20;         advanced, like ``day\_of\_week='mon-fri'`` (for weekdays only).

&#x20;         (Beware that ``day\_of\_week='\*/2'`` does not literally mean

&#x20;         'every two days', but 'every day that is divisible by two'!)



&#x20;   .. attribute:: day\_of\_month



&#x20;       - A (list of) integers from 1-31 that represents the days of the

&#x20;         month that execution should occur.

&#x20;       - A string representing a Crontab pattern.  This may get pretty

&#x20;         advanced, such as ``day\_of\_month='2-30/2'`` (for every even

&#x20;         numbered day) or ``day\_of\_month='1-7,15-21'`` (for the first and

&#x20;         third weeks of the month).



&#x20;   .. attribute:: month\_of\_year



&#x20;       - A (list of) integers from 1-12 that represents the months of

&#x20;         the year during which execution can occur.

&#x20;       - A string representing a Crontab pattern.  This may get pretty

&#x20;         advanced, such as ``month\_of\_year='\*/3'`` (for the first month

&#x20;         of every quarter) or ``month\_of\_year='2-12/2'`` (for every even

&#x20;         numbered month).



&#x20;   .. attribute:: nowfun



&#x20;       Function returning the current date and time

&#x20;       (:class:`\~datetime.datetime`).



&#x20;   .. attribute:: app



&#x20;       The Celery app instance.



&#x20;   It's important to realize that any day on which execution should

&#x20;   occur must be represented by entries in all three of the day and

&#x20;   month attributes.  For example, if ``day\_of\_week`` is 0 and

&#x20;   ``day\_of\_month`` is every seventh day, only months that begin

&#x20;   on Sunday and are also in the ``month\_of\_year`` attribute will have

&#x20;   execution events.  Or, ``day\_of\_week`` is 1 and ``day\_of\_month``

&#x20;   is '1-7,15-21' means every first and third Monday of every month

&#x20;   present in ``month\_of\_year``.

&#x20;   """



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, minute: Cronspec = '\*', hour: Cronspec = '\*', day\_of\_week: Cronspec = '\*',

&#x20;                day\_of\_month: Cronspec = '\*', month\_of\_year: Cronspec = '\*', \*\*kwargs: Any) -> None:

&#x20;       self.\_orig\_minute = cronfield(minute)

&#x20;       self.\_orig\_hour = cronfield(hour)

&#x20;       self.\_orig\_day\_of\_week = cronfield(day\_of\_week)

&#x20;       self.\_orig\_day\_of\_month = cronfield(day\_of\_month)

&#x20;       self.\_orig\_month\_of\_year = cronfield(month\_of\_year)

&#x20;       self.\_orig\_kwargs = kwargs

&#x20;       self.hour = self.\_expand\_cronspec(hour, 24)

&#x20;       self.minute = self.\_expand\_cronspec(minute, 60)

&#x20;       self.day\_of\_week = self.\_expand\_cronspec(day\_of\_week, 7)

&#x20;       self.day\_of\_month = self.\_expand\_cronspec(day\_of\_month, 31, 1)

&#x20;       self.month\_of\_year = self.\_expand\_cronspec(month\_of\_year, 12, 1)

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(\*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   @classmethod

&#x20;   def from\_string(cls, crontab: str) -> crontab:

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       Create a Crontab from a cron expression string. For example ``crontab.from\_string('\* \* \* \* \*')``.



&#x20;       .. code-block:: text



&#x20;           ┌───────────── minute (0–59)

&#x20;           │ ┌───────────── hour (0–23)

&#x20;           │ │ ┌───────────── day of the month (1–31)

&#x20;           │ │ │ ┌───────────── month (1–12)

&#x20;           │ │ │ │ ┌───────────── day of the week (0–6) (Sunday to Saturday)

&#x20;           \* \* \* \* \*

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       minute, hour, day\_of\_month, month\_of\_year, day\_of\_week = crontab.split(" ")

&#x20;       return cls(minute, hour, day\_of\_week, day\_of\_month, month\_of\_year)



&#x20;   @staticmethod

&#x20;   def \_expand\_cronspec(

&#x20;           cronspec: Cronspec,

&#x20;           max\_: int, min\_: int = 0) -> set\[Any]:

&#x20;       """Expand cron specification.



&#x20;       Takes the given cronspec argument in one of the forms:



&#x20;       .. code-block:: text



&#x20;           int         (like 7)

&#x20;           str         (like '3-5,\*/15', '\*', or 'monday')

&#x20;           set         (like {0,15,30,45}

&#x20;           list        (like \[8-17])



&#x20;       And convert it to an (expanded) set representing all time unit

&#x20;       values on which the Crontab triggers.  Only in case of the base

&#x20;       type being :class:`str`, parsing occurs.  (It's fast and

&#x20;       happens only once for each Crontab instance, so there's no

&#x20;       significant performance overhead involved.)



&#x20;       For the other base types, merely Python type conversions happen.



&#x20;       The argument ``max\_`` is needed to determine the expansion of

&#x20;       ``\*`` and ranges.  The argument ``min\_`` is needed to determine

&#x20;       the expansion of ``\*`` and ranges for 1-based cronspecs, such as

&#x20;       day of month or month of year.  The default is sufficient for minute,

&#x20;       hour, and day of week.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       if isinstance(cronspec, int):

&#x20;           result = {cronspec}

&#x20;       elif isinstance(cronspec, str):

&#x20;           result = crontab\_parser(max\_, min\_).parse(cronspec)

&#x20;       elif isinstance(cronspec, set):

&#x20;           result = cronspec

&#x20;       elif isinstance(cronspec, Iterable):

&#x20;           result = set(cronspec)  # type: ignore

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           raise TypeError(CRON\_INVALID\_TYPE.format(type=type(cronspec)))



&#x20;       # assure the result does not precede the min or exceed the max

&#x20;       for number in result:

&#x20;           if number >= max\_ + min\_ or number < min\_:

&#x20;               raise ValueError(CRON\_PATTERN\_INVALID.format(

&#x20;                   min=min\_, max=max\_ - 1 + min\_, value=number))

&#x20;       return result



&#x20;   def \_delta\_to\_next(self, last\_run\_at: datetime, next\_hour: int,

&#x20;                      next\_minute: int) -> ffwd:

&#x20;       """Find next delta.



&#x20;       Takes a :class:`\~datetime.datetime` of last run, next minute and hour,

&#x20;       and returns a :class:`\~celery.utils.time.ffwd` for the next

&#x20;       scheduled day and time.



&#x20;       Only called when ``day\_of\_month`` and/or ``month\_of\_year``

&#x20;       cronspec is specified to further limit scheduled task execution.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       datedata = AttributeDict(year=last\_run\_at.year)

&#x20;       days\_of\_month = sorted(self.day\_of\_month)

&#x20;       months\_of\_year = sorted(self.month\_of\_year)



&#x20;       def day\_out\_of\_range(year: int, month: int, day: int) -> bool:

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               datetime(year=year, month=month, day=day)

&#x20;           except ValueError:

&#x20;               return True

&#x20;           return False



&#x20;       def is\_before\_last\_run(year: int, month: int, day: int) -> bool:

&#x20;           return self.maybe\_make\_aware(

&#x20;               datetime(year, month, day, next\_hour, next\_minute),

&#x20;               naive\_as\_utc=False) < last\_run\_at



&#x20;       def roll\_over() -> None:

&#x20;           for \_ in range(2000):

&#x20;               flag = (datedata.dom == len(days\_of\_month) or

&#x20;                       day\_out\_of\_range(datedata.year,

&#x20;                                        months\_of\_year\[datedata.moy],

&#x20;                                        days\_of\_month\[datedata.dom]) or

&#x20;                       (is\_before\_last\_run(datedata.year,

&#x20;                                           months\_of\_year\[datedata.moy],

&#x20;                                           days\_of\_month\[datedata.dom])))



&#x20;               if flag:

&#x20;                   datedata.dom = 0

&#x20;                   datedata.moy += 1

&#x20;                   if datedata.moy == len(months\_of\_year):

&#x20;                       datedata.moy = 0

&#x20;                       datedata.year += 1

&#x20;               else:

&#x20;                   break

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               # Tried 2000 times, we're most likely in an infinite loop

&#x20;               raise RuntimeError('unable to rollover, '

&#x20;                                  'time specification is probably invalid')



&#x20;       if last\_run\_at.month in self.month\_of\_year:

&#x20;           datedata.dom = bisect(days\_of\_month, last\_run\_at.day)

&#x20;           datedata.moy = bisect\_left(months\_of\_year, last\_run\_at.month)

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           datedata.dom = 0

&#x20;           datedata.moy = bisect(months\_of\_year, last\_run\_at.month)

&#x20;           if datedata.moy == len(months\_of\_year):

&#x20;               datedata.moy = 0

&#x20;       roll\_over()



&#x20;       while 1:

&#x20;           th = datetime(year=datedata.year,

&#x20;                         month=months\_of\_year\[datedata.moy],

&#x20;                         day=days\_of\_month\[datedata.dom])

&#x20;           if th.isoweekday() % 7 in self.day\_of\_week:

&#x20;               break

&#x20;           datedata.dom += 1

&#x20;           roll\_over()



&#x20;       return ffwd(year=datedata.year,

&#x20;                   month=months\_of\_year\[datedata.moy],

&#x20;                   day=days\_of\_month\[datedata.dom],

&#x20;                   hour=next\_hour,

&#x20;                   minute=next\_minute,

&#x20;                   second=0,

&#x20;                   microsecond=0)



&#x20;   def \_\_repr\_\_(self) -> str:

&#x20;       return CRON\_REPR.format(self)



&#x20;   def \_\_reduce\_\_(self) -> tuple\[type, tuple\[Cronspec, Cronspec, Cronspec, Cronspec, Cronspec], Any]:

&#x20;       return (self.\_\_class\_\_, (self.\_orig\_minute,

&#x20;                                self.\_orig\_hour,

&#x20;                                self.\_orig\_day\_of\_week,

&#x20;                                self.\_orig\_day\_of\_month,

&#x20;                                self.\_orig\_month\_of\_year), self.\_orig\_kwargs)



&#x20;   def \_\_setstate\_\_(self, state: Mapping\[str, Any]) -> None:

&#x20;       # Calling super's init because the kwargs aren't necessarily passed in

&#x20;       # the same form as they are stored by the superclass

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(\*\*state)



&#x20;   def remaining\_delta(self, last\_run\_at: datetime, tz: tzinfo | None = None,

&#x20;                       ffwd: type = ffwd) -> tuple\[datetime, Any, datetime]:

&#x20;       # caching global ffwd

&#x20;       last\_run\_at = self.maybe\_make\_aware(last\_run\_at)

&#x20;       now = self.maybe\_make\_aware(self.now())

&#x20;       dow\_num = last\_run\_at.isoweekday() % 7  # Sunday is day 0, not day 7



&#x20;       execute\_this\_date = (

&#x20;           last\_run\_at.month in self.month\_of\_year and

&#x20;           last\_run\_at.day in self.day\_of\_month and

&#x20;           dow\_num in self.day\_of\_week

&#x20;       )



&#x20;       execute\_this\_hour = (

&#x20;           execute\_this\_date and

&#x20;           last\_run\_at.day == now.day and

&#x20;           last\_run\_at.month == now.month and

&#x20;           last\_run\_at.year == now.year and

&#x20;           last\_run\_at.hour in self.hour and

&#x20;           last\_run\_at.minute < max(self.minute)

&#x20;       )



&#x20;       if execute\_this\_hour:

&#x20;           next\_minute = min(minute for minute in self.minute

&#x20;                             if minute > last\_run\_at.minute)

&#x20;           delta = ffwd(minute=next\_minute, second=0, microsecond=0)

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           next\_minute = min(self.minute)

&#x20;           execute\_today = (execute\_this\_date and

&#x20;                            last\_run\_at.hour < max(self.hour))



&#x20;           if execute\_today:

&#x20;               next\_hour = min(hour for hour in self.hour

&#x20;                               if hour > last\_run\_at.hour)

&#x20;               delta = ffwd(hour=next\_hour, minute=next\_minute,

&#x20;                            second=0, microsecond=0)

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               next\_hour = min(self.hour)

&#x20;               all\_dom\_moy = (self.\_orig\_day\_of\_month == '\*' and

&#x20;                              self.\_orig\_month\_of\_year == '\*')

&#x20;               if all\_dom\_moy:

&#x20;                   next\_day = min(\[day for day in self.day\_of\_week

&#x20;                                   if day > dow\_num] or self.day\_of\_week)

&#x20;                   add\_week = next\_day == dow\_num



&#x20;                   delta = ffwd(

&#x20;                       weeks=add\_week and 1 or 0,

&#x20;                       weekday=(next\_day - 1) % 7,

&#x20;                       hour=next\_hour,

&#x20;                       minute=next\_minute,

&#x20;                       second=0,

&#x20;                       microsecond=0,

&#x20;                   )

&#x20;               else:

&#x20;                   delta = self.\_delta\_to\_next(last\_run\_at,

&#x20;                                               next\_hour, next\_minute)

&#x20;       return self.to\_local(last\_run\_at), delta, self.to\_local(now)



&#x20;   def remaining\_estimate(

&#x20;           self, last\_run\_at: datetime, ffwd: type = ffwd) -> timedelta:

&#x20;       """Estimate of next run time.



&#x20;       Returns when the periodic task should run next as a

&#x20;       :class:`\~datetime.timedelta`.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       # pylint: disable=redefined-outer-name

&#x20;       # caching global ffwd

&#x20;       return remaining(\*self.remaining\_delta(last\_run\_at, ffwd=ffwd))



&#x20;   def is\_due(self, last\_run\_at: datetime) -> tuple\[bool, datetime]:

&#x20;       """Return tuple of ``(is\_due, next\_time\_to\_run)``.



&#x20;       If :setting:`beat\_cron\_starting\_deadline`  has been specified, the

&#x20;       scheduler will make sure that the `last\_run\_at` time is within the

&#x20;       deadline. This prevents tasks that could have been run according to

&#x20;       the crontab, but didn't, from running again unexpectedly.



&#x20;       Note:

&#x20;           Next time to run is in seconds.



&#x20;       SeeAlso:

&#x20;           :meth:`celery.schedules.schedule.is\_due` for more information.

&#x20;       """



&#x20;       rem\_delta = self.remaining\_estimate(last\_run\_at)

&#x20;       rem\_secs = rem\_delta.total\_seconds()

&#x20;       rem = max(rem\_secs, 0)

&#x20;       due = rem == 0



&#x20;       deadline\_secs = self.app.conf.beat\_cron\_starting\_deadline

&#x20;       has\_passed\_deadline = False

&#x20;       if deadline\_secs is not None:

&#x20;           # Make sure we're looking at the latest possible feasible run

&#x20;           # date when checking the deadline.

&#x20;           last\_date\_checked = last\_run\_at

&#x20;           last\_feasible\_rem\_secs = rem\_secs

&#x20;           while rem\_secs < 0:

&#x20;               last\_date\_checked = last\_date\_checked + abs(rem\_delta)

&#x20;               rem\_delta = self.remaining\_estimate(last\_date\_checked)

&#x20;               rem\_secs = rem\_delta.total\_seconds()

&#x20;               if rem\_secs < 0:

&#x20;                   last\_feasible\_rem\_secs = rem\_secs



&#x20;           # if rem\_secs becomes 0 or positive, second-to-last

&#x20;           # last\_date\_checked must be the last feasible run date.

&#x20;           # Check if the last feasible date is within the deadline

&#x20;           # for running

&#x20;           has\_passed\_deadline = -last\_feasible\_rem\_secs > deadline\_secs

&#x20;           if has\_passed\_deadline:

&#x20;               # Should not be due if we've passed the deadline for looking

&#x20;               # at past runs

&#x20;               due = False



&#x20;       if due or has\_passed\_deadline:

&#x20;           rem\_delta = self.remaining\_estimate(self.now())

&#x20;           rem = max(rem\_delta.total\_seconds(), 0)

&#x20;       return schedstate(due, rem)



&#x20;   def \_\_eq\_\_(self, other: Any) -> bool:

&#x20;       if isinstance(other, crontab):

&#x20;           return (

&#x20;               other.month\_of\_year == self.month\_of\_year and

&#x20;               other.day\_of\_month == self.day\_of\_month and

&#x20;               other.day\_of\_week == self.day\_of\_week and

&#x20;               other.hour == self.hour and

&#x20;               other.minute == self.minute and

&#x20;               super().\_\_eq\_\_(other)

&#x20;           )

&#x20;       return NotImplemented





def maybe\_schedule(

&#x20;       s: int | float | timedelta | BaseSchedule, relative: bool = False,

&#x20;       app: Celery | None = None) -> float | timedelta | BaseSchedule:

&#x20;   """Return schedule from number, timedelta, or actual schedule."""

&#x20;   if s is not None:

&#x20;       if isinstance(s, (float, int)):

&#x20;           s = timedelta(seconds=s)

&#x20;       if isinstance(s, timedelta):

&#x20;           return schedule(s, relative, app=app)

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           s.app = app

&#x20;   return s





class solar(BaseSchedule):

&#x20;   """Solar event.



&#x20;   A solar event can be used as the ``run\_every`` value of a

&#x20;   periodic task entry to schedule based on certain solar events.



&#x20;   Notes:



&#x20;       Available event values are:



&#x20;           - ``dawn\_astronomical``

&#x20;           - ``dawn\_nautical``

&#x20;           - ``dawn\_civil``

&#x20;           - ``sunrise``

&#x20;           - ``solar\_noon``

&#x20;           - ``sunset``

&#x20;           - ``dusk\_civil``

&#x20;           - ``dusk\_nautical``

&#x20;           - ``dusk\_astronomical``



&#x20;   Arguments:

&#x20;       event (str): Solar event that triggers this task.

&#x20;           See note for available values.

&#x20;       lat (float): The latitude of the observer.

&#x20;       lon (float): The longitude of the observer.

&#x20;       nowfun (Callable): Function returning the current date and time

&#x20;           as a class:`\~datetime.datetime`.

&#x20;       app (Celery): Celery app instance.

&#x20;   """



&#x20;   \_all\_events = {

&#x20;       'dawn\_astronomical',

&#x20;       'dawn\_nautical',

&#x20;       'dawn\_civil',

&#x20;       'sunrise',

&#x20;       'solar\_noon',

&#x20;       'sunset',

&#x20;       'dusk\_civil',

&#x20;       'dusk\_nautical',

&#x20;       'dusk\_astronomical',

&#x20;   }

&#x20;   \_horizons = {

&#x20;       'dawn\_astronomical': '-18',

&#x20;       'dawn\_nautical': '-12',

&#x20;       'dawn\_civil': '-6',

&#x20;       'sunrise': '-0:34',

&#x20;       'solar\_noon': '0',

&#x20;       'sunset': '-0:34',

&#x20;       'dusk\_civil': '-6',

&#x20;       'dusk\_nautical': '-12',

&#x20;       'dusk\_astronomical': '18',

&#x20;   }

&#x20;   \_methods = {

&#x20;       'dawn\_astronomical': 'next\_rising',

&#x20;       'dawn\_nautical': 'next\_rising',

&#x20;       'dawn\_civil': 'next\_rising',

&#x20;       'sunrise': 'next\_rising',

&#x20;       'solar\_noon': 'next\_transit',

&#x20;       'sunset': 'next\_setting',

&#x20;       'dusk\_civil': 'next\_setting',

&#x20;       'dusk\_nautical': 'next\_setting',

&#x20;       'dusk\_astronomical': 'next\_setting',

&#x20;   }

&#x20;   \_use\_center\_l = {

&#x20;       'dawn\_astronomical': True,

&#x20;       'dawn\_nautical': True,

&#x20;       'dawn\_civil': True,

&#x20;       'sunrise': False,

&#x20;       'solar\_noon': False,

&#x20;       'sunset': False,

&#x20;       'dusk\_civil': True,

&#x20;       'dusk\_nautical': True,

&#x20;       'dusk\_astronomical': True,

&#x20;   }



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, event: str, lat: int | float, lon: int | float, \*\*

&#x20;                kwargs: Any) -> None:

&#x20;       self.ephem = \_\_import\_\_('ephem')

&#x20;       self.event = event

&#x20;       self.lat = lat

&#x20;       self.lon = lon

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(\*\*kwargs)



&#x20;       if event not in self.\_all\_events:

&#x20;           raise ValueError(SOLAR\_INVALID\_EVENT.format(

&#x20;               event=event, all\_events=', '.join(sorted(self.\_all\_events)),

&#x20;           ))

&#x20;       if lat < -90 or lat > 90:

&#x20;           raise ValueError(SOLAR\_INVALID\_LATITUDE.format(lat=lat))

&#x20;       if lon < -180 or lon > 180:

&#x20;           raise ValueError(SOLAR\_INVALID\_LONGITUDE.format(lon=lon))



&#x20;       cal = self.ephem.Observer()

&#x20;       cal.lat = str(lat)

&#x20;       cal.lon = str(lon)

&#x20;       cal.elev = 0

&#x20;       cal.horizon = self.\_horizons\[event]

&#x20;       cal.pressure = 0

&#x20;       self.cal = cal



&#x20;       self.method = self.\_methods\[event]

&#x20;       self.use\_center = self.\_use\_center\_l\[event]



&#x20;   def \_\_reduce\_\_(self) -> tuple\[type, tuple\[str, int | float, int | float]]:

&#x20;       return self.\_\_class\_\_, (self.event, self.lat, self.lon)



&#x20;   def \_\_repr\_\_(self) -> str:

&#x20;       return '<solar: {} at latitude {}, longitude: {}>'.format(

&#x20;           self.event, self.lat, self.lon,

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def remaining\_estimate(self, last\_run\_at: datetime) -> timedelta:

&#x20;       """Return estimate of next time to run.



&#x20;       Returns:

&#x20;           \~datetime.timedelta: when the periodic task should

&#x20;               run next, or if it shouldn't run today (e.g., the sun does

&#x20;               not rise today), returns the time when the next check

&#x20;               should take place.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       last\_run\_at = self.maybe\_make\_aware(last\_run\_at)

&#x20;       last\_run\_at\_utc = localize(last\_run\_at, timezone.utc)

&#x20;       self.cal.date = last\_run\_at\_utc

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           if self.use\_center:

&#x20;               next\_utc = getattr(self.cal, self.method)(

&#x20;                   self.ephem.Sun(),

&#x20;                   start=last\_run\_at\_utc, use\_center=self.use\_center

&#x20;               )

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               next\_utc = getattr(self.cal, self.method)(

&#x20;                   self.ephem.Sun(), start=last\_run\_at\_utc

&#x20;               )



&#x20;       except self.ephem.CircumpolarError:  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;           # Sun won't rise/set today.  Check again tomorrow

&#x20;           # (specifically, after the next anti-transit).

&#x20;           next\_utc = (

&#x20;               self.cal.next\_antitransit(self.ephem.Sun()) +

&#x20;               timedelta(minutes=1)

&#x20;           )

&#x20;       next = self.maybe\_make\_aware(next\_utc.datetime())

&#x20;       now = self.maybe\_make\_aware(self.now())

&#x20;       delta = next - now

&#x20;       return delta



&#x20;   def is\_due(self, last\_run\_at: datetime) -> tuple\[bool, datetime]:

&#x20;       """Return tuple of ``(is\_due, next\_time\_to\_run)``.



&#x20;       Note:

&#x20;           next time to run is in seconds.



&#x20;       See Also:

&#x20;           :meth:`celery.schedules.schedule.is\_due` for more information.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       rem\_delta = self.remaining\_estimate(last\_run\_at)

&#x20;       rem = max(rem\_delta.total\_seconds(), 0)

&#x20;       due = rem == 0

&#x20;       if due:

&#x20;           rem\_delta = self.remaining\_estimate(self.now())

&#x20;           rem = max(rem\_delta.total\_seconds(), 0)

&#x20;       return schedstate(due, rem)



&#x20;   def \_\_eq\_\_(self, other: Any) -> bool:

&#x20;       if isinstance(other, solar):

&#x20;           return (

&#x20;               other.event == self.event and

&#x20;               other.lat == self.lat and

&#x20;               other.lon == self.lon

&#x20;           )

&#x20;       return NotImplemented



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\signals.py
