```python
"""Celery Signals.



This module defines the signals (Observer pattern) sent by

both workers and clients.



Functions can be connected to these signals, and connected

functions are called whenever a signal is called.



.. seealso::



&#x20;   :ref:`signals` for more information.

"""



from .utils.dispatch import Signal



\_\_all\_\_ = (

&#x20;   'before\_task\_publish', 'after\_task\_publish', 'task\_internal\_error',

&#x20;   'task\_prerun', 'task\_postrun', 'task\_success',

&#x20;   'task\_received', 'task\_rejected', 'task\_unknown',

&#x20;   'task\_retry', 'task\_failure', 'task\_revoked', 'celeryd\_init',

&#x20;   'celeryd\_after\_setup', 'worker\_init', 'worker\_before\_create\_process',

&#x20;   'worker\_process\_init', 'worker\_process\_shutdown', 'worker\_ready',

&#x20;   'worker\_shutdown', 'worker\_shutting\_down', 'setup\_logging',

&#x20;   'after\_setup\_logger', 'after\_setup\_task\_logger', 'beat\_init',

&#x20;   'beat\_embedded\_init', 'heartbeat\_sent', 'eventlet\_pool\_started',

&#x20;   'eventlet\_pool\_preshutdown', 'eventlet\_pool\_postshutdown',

&#x20;   'eventlet\_pool\_apply',

)



\# - Task

before\_task\_publish = Signal(

&#x20;   name='before\_task\_publish',

&#x20;   providing\_args={

&#x20;       'body', 'exchange', 'routing\_key', 'headers',

&#x20;       'properties', 'declare', 'retry\_policy',

&#x20;   },

)

after\_task\_publish = Signal(

&#x20;   name='after\_task\_publish',

&#x20;   providing\_args={'body', 'exchange', 'routing\_key'},

)

task\_received = Signal(

&#x20;   name='task\_received',

&#x20;   providing\_args={'request'}

)

task\_prerun = Signal(

&#x20;   name='task\_prerun',

&#x20;   providing\_args={'task\_id', 'task', 'args', 'kwargs'},

)

task\_postrun = Signal(

&#x20;   name='task\_postrun',

&#x20;   providing\_args={'task\_id', 'task', 'args', 'kwargs', 'retval'},

)

task\_success = Signal(

&#x20;   name='task\_success',

&#x20;   providing\_args={'result'},

)

task\_retry = Signal(

&#x20;   name='task\_retry',

&#x20;   providing\_args={'request', 'reason', 'einfo'},

)

task\_failure = Signal(

&#x20;   name='task\_failure',

&#x20;   providing\_args={

&#x20;       'task\_id', 'exception', 'args', 'kwargs', 'traceback', 'einfo',

&#x20;   },

)

task\_internal\_error = Signal(

&#x20;   name='task\_internal\_error',

&#x20;   providing\_args={

&#x20;       'task\_id', 'args', 'kwargs', 'request', 'exception', 'traceback', 'einfo'

&#x20;   }

)

task\_revoked = Signal(

&#x20;   name='task\_revoked',

&#x20;   providing\_args={

&#x20;       'request', 'terminated', 'signum', 'expired',

&#x20;   },

)

task\_rejected = Signal(

&#x20;   name='task\_rejected',

&#x20;   providing\_args={'message', 'exc'},

)

task\_unknown = Signal(

&#x20;   name='task\_unknown',

&#x20;   providing\_args={'message', 'exc', 'name', 'id'},

)

\#: Deprecated, use after\_task\_publish instead.

task\_sent = Signal(

&#x20;   name='task\_sent',

&#x20;   providing\_args={

&#x20;       'task\_id', 'task', 'args', 'kwargs', 'eta', 'taskset',

&#x20;   },

)



\# - Program: `celery worker`

celeryd\_init = Signal(

&#x20;   name='celeryd\_init',

&#x20;   providing\_args={'instance', 'conf', 'options'},

)

celeryd\_after\_setup = Signal(

&#x20;   name='celeryd\_after\_setup',

&#x20;   providing\_args={'instance', 'conf'},

)



\# - Worker

import\_modules = Signal(name='import\_modules')

worker\_init = Signal(name='worker\_init')

worker\_before\_create\_process = Signal(name="worker\_before\_create\_process")

worker\_process\_init = Signal(name='worker\_process\_init')

worker\_process\_shutdown = Signal(name='worker\_process\_shutdown')

worker\_ready = Signal(name='worker\_ready')

worker\_shutdown = Signal(name='worker\_shutdown')

worker\_shutting\_down = Signal(name='worker\_shutting\_down')

heartbeat\_sent = Signal(name='heartbeat\_sent')



\# - Logging

setup\_logging = Signal(

&#x20;   name='setup\_logging',

&#x20;   providing\_args={

&#x20;       'loglevel', 'logfile', 'format', 'colorize',

&#x20;   },

)

after\_setup\_logger = Signal(

&#x20;   name='after\_setup\_logger',

&#x20;   providing\_args={

&#x20;       'logger', 'loglevel', 'logfile', 'format', 'colorize',

&#x20;   },

)

after\_setup\_task\_logger = Signal(

&#x20;   name='after\_setup\_task\_logger',

&#x20;   providing\_args={

&#x20;       'logger', 'loglevel', 'logfile', 'format', 'colorize',

&#x20;   },

)



\# - Beat

beat\_init = Signal(name='beat\_init')

beat\_embedded\_init = Signal(name='beat\_embedded\_init')



\# - Eventlet

eventlet\_pool\_started = Signal(name='eventlet\_pool\_started')

eventlet\_pool\_preshutdown = Signal(name='eventlet\_pool\_preshutdown')

eventlet\_pool\_postshutdown = Signal(name='eventlet\_pool\_postshutdown')

eventlet\_pool\_apply = Signal(

&#x20;   name='eventlet\_pool\_apply',

&#x20;   providing\_args={'target', 'args', 'kwargs'},

)



\# - Programs

user\_preload\_options = Signal(

&#x20;   name='user\_preload\_options',

&#x20;   providing\_args={'app', 'options'},

)
```

\### 代码文件: celery\\states.py

```python

"""Built-in task states.



.. \_states:



States

\------



See :ref:`task-states`.



.. \_statesets:



Sets

\----



.. state:: READY\_STATES



READY\_STATES

\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~



Set of states meaning the task result is ready (has been executed).



.. state:: UNREADY\_STATES



UNREADY\_STATES

\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~



Set of states meaning the task result is not ready (hasn't been executed).



.. state:: EXCEPTION\_STATES



EXCEPTION\_STATES

\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~



Set of states meaning the task returned an exception.



.. state:: PROPAGATE\_STATES



PROPAGATE\_STATES

\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~



Set of exception states that should propagate exceptions to the user.



.. state:: ALL\_STATES



ALL\_STATES

\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~\~



Set of all possible states.



Misc

\----



"""



\_\_all\_\_ = (

&#x20;   'PENDING', 'RECEIVED', 'STARTED', 'SUCCESS', 'FAILURE',

&#x20;   'REVOKED', 'RETRY', 'IGNORED', 'READY\_STATES', 'UNREADY\_STATES',

&#x20;   'EXCEPTION\_STATES', 'PROPAGATE\_STATES', 'precedence', 'state',

)



\#: State precedence.

\#: None represents the precedence of an unknown state.

\#: Lower index means higher precedence.

PRECEDENCE = \[

&#x20;   'SUCCESS',

&#x20;   'FAILURE',

&#x20;   None,

&#x20;   'REVOKED',

&#x20;   'STARTED',

&#x20;   'RECEIVED',

&#x20;   'REJECTED',

&#x20;   'RETRY',

&#x20;   'PENDING',

]



\#: Hash lookup of PRECEDENCE to index

PRECEDENCE\_LOOKUP = dict(zip(PRECEDENCE, range(0, len(PRECEDENCE))))

NONE\_PRECEDENCE = PRECEDENCE\_LOOKUP\[None]





def precedence(state: str) -> int:

&#x20;   """Get the precedence index for state.



&#x20;   Lower index means higher precedence.

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       return PRECEDENCE\_LOOKUP\[state]

&#x20;   except KeyError:

&#x20;       return NONE\_PRECEDENCE





class state(str):

&#x20;   """Task state.



&#x20;   State is a subclass of :class:`str`, implementing comparison

&#x20;   methods adhering to state precedence rules::



&#x20;       >>> from celery.states import state, PENDING, SUCCESS



&#x20;       >>> state(PENDING) < state(SUCCESS)

&#x20;       True



&#x20;   Any custom state is considered to be lower than :state:`FAILURE` and

&#x20;   :state:`SUCCESS`, but higher than any of the other built-in states::



&#x20;       >>> state('PROGRESS') > state(STARTED)

&#x20;       True



&#x20;       >>> state('PROGRESS') > state('SUCCESS')

&#x20;       False

&#x20;   """



&#x20;   def \_\_gt\_\_(self, other: str) -> bool:

&#x20;       return precedence(self) < precedence(other)



&#x20;   def \_\_ge\_\_(self, other: str) -> bool:

&#x20;       return precedence(self) <= precedence(other)



&#x20;   def \_\_lt\_\_(self, other: str) -> bool:

&#x20;       return precedence(self) > precedence(other)



&#x20;   def \_\_le\_\_(self, other: str) -> bool:

&#x20;       return precedence(self) >= precedence(other)





\#: Task state is unknown (assumed pending since you know the id).

PENDING = 'PENDING'

\#: Task was received by a worker (only used in events).

RECEIVED = 'RECEIVED'

\#: Task was started by a worker (:setting:`task\_track\_started`).

STARTED = 'STARTED'

\#: Task succeeded

SUCCESS = 'SUCCESS'

\#: Task failed

FAILURE = 'FAILURE'

\#: Task was revoked.

REVOKED = 'REVOKED'

\#: Task was rejected (only used in events).

REJECTED = 'REJECTED'

\#: Task is waiting for retry.

RETRY = 'RETRY'

IGNORED = 'IGNORED'



READY\_STATES = frozenset({SUCCESS, FAILURE, REVOKED})

UNREADY\_STATES = frozenset({PENDING, RECEIVED, STARTED, REJECTED, RETRY})

EXCEPTION\_STATES = frozenset({RETRY, FAILURE, REVOKED})

PROPAGATE\_STATES = frozenset({FAILURE, REVOKED})



ALL\_STATES = frozenset({

&#x20;   PENDING, RECEIVED, STARTED, SUCCESS, FAILURE, RETRY, REVOKED,

})



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\\_state.py

```python

"""Internal state.



This is an internal module containing thread state

like the ``current\_app``, and ``current\_task``.



This module shouldn't be used directly.

"""



import os

import sys

import threading

import weakref



from celery.local import Proxy

from celery.utils.threads import LocalStack



\_\_all\_\_ = (

&#x20;   'set\_default\_app', 'get\_current\_app', 'get\_current\_task',

&#x20;   'get\_current\_worker\_task', 'current\_app', 'current\_task',

&#x20;   'connect\_on\_app\_finalize',

)



\#: Global default app used when no current app.

default\_app = None



\#: Function returning the app provided or the default app if none.

\#:

\#: The environment variable :envvar:`CELERY\_TRACE\_APP` is used to

\#: trace app leaks.  When enabled an exception is raised if there

\#: is no active app.

app\_or\_default = None



\#: List of all app instances (weakrefs), mustn't be used directly.

\_apps = weakref.WeakSet()



\#: Global set of functions to call whenever a new app is finalized.

\#: Shared tasks, and built-in tasks are created by adding callbacks here.

\_on\_app\_finalizers = set()



\_task\_join\_will\_block = False





def connect\_on\_app\_finalize(callback):

&#x20;   """Connect callback to be called when any app is finalized."""

&#x20;   \_on\_app\_finalizers.add(callback)

&#x20;   return callback





def \_announce\_app\_finalized(app):

&#x20;   callbacks = set(\_on\_app\_finalizers)

&#x20;   for callback in callbacks:

&#x20;       callback(app)





def \_set\_task\_join\_will\_block(blocks):

&#x20;   global \_task\_join\_will\_block

&#x20;   \_task\_join\_will\_block = blocks





def task\_join\_will\_block():

&#x20;   return \_task\_join\_will\_block





class \_TLS(threading.local):

&#x20;   #: Apps with the :attr:`\~celery.app.base.BaseApp.set\_as\_current` attribute

&#x20;   #: sets this, so it will always contain the last instantiated app,

&#x20;   #: and is the default app returned by :func:`app\_or\_default`.

&#x20;   current\_app = None





\_tls = \_TLS()



\_task\_stack = LocalStack()





\#: Function used to push a task to the thread local stack

\#: keeping track of the currently executing task.

\#: You must remember to pop the task after.

push\_current\_task = \_task\_stack.push



\#: Function used to pop a task from the thread local stack

\#: keeping track of the currently executing task.

pop\_current\_task = \_task\_stack.pop





def set\_default\_app(app):

&#x20;   """Set default app."""

&#x20;   global default\_app

&#x20;   default\_app = app





def \_get\_current\_app():

&#x20;   if default\_app is None:

&#x20;       #: creates the global fallback app instance.

&#x20;       from celery.app.base import Celery

&#x20;       set\_default\_app(Celery(

&#x20;           'default', fixups=\[], set\_as\_current=False,

&#x20;           loader=os.environ.get('CELERY\_LOADER') or 'default',

&#x20;       ))

&#x20;   return \_tls.current\_app or default\_app





def \_set\_current\_app(app):

&#x20;   \_tls.current\_app = app





if os.environ.get('C\_STRICT\_APP'):  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;   def get\_current\_app():

&#x20;       """Return the current app."""

&#x20;       raise RuntimeError('USES CURRENT APP')

elif os.environ.get('C\_WARN\_APP'):  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;   def get\_current\_app():

&#x20;       import traceback

&#x20;       print('-- USES CURRENT\_APP', file=sys.stderr)  # +

&#x20;       traceback.print\_stack(file=sys.stderr)

&#x20;       return \_get\_current\_app()

else:

&#x20;   get\_current\_app = \_get\_current\_app





def get\_current\_task():

&#x20;   """Currently executing task."""

&#x20;   return \_task\_stack.top





def get\_current\_worker\_task():

&#x20;   """Currently executing task, that was applied by the worker.



&#x20;   This is used to differentiate between the actual task

&#x20;   executed by the worker and any task that was called within

&#x20;   a task (using ``task.\_\_call\_\_`` or ``task.apply``)

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   for task in reversed(\_task\_stack.stack):

&#x20;       if not task.request.called\_directly:

&#x20;           return task





\#: Proxy to current app.

current\_app = Proxy(get\_current\_app)



\#: Proxy to current task.

current\_task = Proxy(get\_current\_task)





def \_register\_app(app):

&#x20;   \_apps.add(app)





def \_deregister\_app(app):

&#x20;   \_apps.discard(app)





def \_get\_active\_apps():

&#x20;   return \_apps





def \_app\_or\_default(app=None):

&#x20;   if app is None:

&#x20;       return get\_current\_app()

&#x20;   return app





def \_app\_or\_default\_trace(app=None):  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;   from traceback import print\_stack

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       from billiard.process import current\_process

&#x20;   except ImportError:

&#x20;       current\_process = None

&#x20;   if app is None:

&#x20;       if getattr(\_tls, 'current\_app', None):

&#x20;           print('-- RETURNING TO CURRENT APP --')  # +

&#x20;           print\_stack()

&#x20;           return \_tls.current\_app

&#x20;       if not current\_process or current\_process().\_name == 'MainProcess':

&#x20;           raise Exception('DEFAULT APP')

&#x20;       print('-- RETURNING TO DEFAULT APP --')      # +

&#x20;       print\_stack()

&#x20;       return default\_app

&#x20;   return app





def enable\_trace():

&#x20;   """Enable tracing of app instances."""

&#x20;   global app\_or\_default

&#x20;   app\_or\_default = \_app\_or\_default\_trace





def disable\_trace():

&#x20;   """Disable tracing of app instances."""

&#x20;   global app\_or\_default

&#x20;   app\_or\_default = \_app\_or\_default





if os.environ.get('CELERY\_TRACE\_APP'):  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;   enable\_trace()

else:

&#x20;   disable\_trace()



```

```python
import os

import re

import sys

from collections import namedtuple



\# Lazy loading

from . import local



SERIES = 'immunity'



\_\_version\_\_ = '5.5.3'

\_\_author\_\_ = 'Ask Solem'

\_\_contact\_\_ = 'auvipy@gmail.com'

\_\_homepage\_\_ = 'https://docs.celeryq.dev/'

\_\_docformat\_\_ = 'restructuredtext'

\_\_keywords\_\_ = 'task job queue distributed messaging actor'



\# -eof meta-



\_\_all\_\_ = (

&#x20;   'Celery', 'bugreport', 'shared\_task', 'Task',

&#x20;   'current\_app', 'current\_task', 'maybe\_signature',

&#x20;   'chain', 'chord', 'chunks', 'group', 'signature',

&#x20;   'xmap', 'xstarmap', 'uuid',

)



VERSION\_BANNER = f'{\_\_version\_\_} ({SERIES})'



version\_info\_t = namedtuple('version\_info\_t', (

&#x20;   'major', 'minor', 'micro', 'releaselevel', 'serial',

))



\# bumpversion can only search for {current\_version}

\# so we have to parse the version here.

\_temp = re.match(

&#x20;   r'(\\d+)\\.(\\d+).(\\d+)(.+)?', \_\_version\_\_).groups()

VERSION = version\_info = version\_info\_t(

&#x20;   int(\_temp\[0]), int(\_temp\[1]), int(\_temp\[2]), \_temp\[3] or '', '')

del \_temp

del re



if os.environ.get('C\_IMPDEBUG'):  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;   import builtins



&#x20;   def debug\_import(name, locals=None, globals=None,

&#x20;                    fromlist=None, level=-1, real\_import=builtins.\_\_import\_\_):

&#x20;       glob = globals or getattr(sys, 'emarfteg\_'\[::-1])(1).f\_globals

&#x20;       importer\_name = glob and glob.get('\_\_name\_\_') or 'unknown'

&#x20;       print(f'-- {importer\_name} imports {name}')

&#x20;       return real\_import(name, locals, globals, fromlist, level)

&#x20;   builtins.\_\_import\_\_ = debug\_import



\# This is never executed, but tricks static analyzers (PyDev, PyCharm,

\# pylint, etc.) into knowing the types of these symbols, and what

\# they contain.

STATICA\_HACK = True

globals()\['kcah\_acitats'\[::-1].upper()] = False

if STATICA\_HACK:  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;   from celery.\_state import current\_app, current\_task

&#x20;   from celery.app import shared\_task

&#x20;   from celery.app.base import Celery

&#x20;   from celery.app.task import Task

&#x20;   from celery.app.utils import bugreport

&#x20;   from celery.canvas import (chain, chord, chunks, group, maybe\_signature, signature, subtask, xmap,  # noqa

&#x20;                              xstarmap)

&#x20;   from celery.utils import uuid



\# Eventlet/gevent patching must happen before importing

\# anything else, so these tools must be at top-level.





def \_find\_option\_with\_arg(argv, short\_opts=None, long\_opts=None):

&#x20;   """Search argv for options specifying short and longopt alternatives.



&#x20;   Returns:

&#x20;       str: value for option found

&#x20;   Raises:

&#x20;       KeyError: if option not found.

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   for i, arg in enumerate(argv):

&#x20;       if arg.startswith('-'):

&#x20;           if long\_opts and arg.startswith('--'):

&#x20;               name, sep, val = arg.partition('=')

&#x20;               if name in long\_opts:

&#x20;                   return val if sep else argv\[i + 1]

&#x20;           if short\_opts and arg in short\_opts:

&#x20;               return argv\[i + 1]

&#x20;   raise KeyError('|'.join(short\_opts or \[] + long\_opts or \[]))





def \_patch\_eventlet():

&#x20;   import eventlet.debug



&#x20;   eventlet.monkey\_patch()

&#x20;   blockdetect = float(os.environ.get('EVENTLET\_NOBLOCK', 0))

&#x20;   if blockdetect:

&#x20;       eventlet.debug.hub\_blocking\_detection(blockdetect, blockdetect)





def \_patch\_gevent():

&#x20;   import gevent.monkey

&#x20;   import gevent.signal



&#x20;   gevent.monkey.patch\_all()





def maybe\_patch\_concurrency(argv=None, short\_opts=None,

&#x20;                           long\_opts=None, patches=None):

&#x20;   """Apply eventlet/gevent monkeypatches.



&#x20;   With short and long opt alternatives that specify the command line

&#x20;   option to set the pool, this makes sure that anything that needs

&#x20;   to be patched is completed as early as possible.

&#x20;   (e.g., eventlet/gevent monkey patches).

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   argv = argv if argv else sys.argv

&#x20;   short\_opts = short\_opts if short\_opts else \['-P']

&#x20;   long\_opts = long\_opts if long\_opts else \['--pool']

&#x20;   patches = patches if patches else {'eventlet': \_patch\_eventlet,

&#x20;                                      'gevent': \_patch\_gevent}

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       pool = \_find\_option\_with\_arg(argv, short\_opts, long\_opts)

&#x20;   except KeyError:

&#x20;       pass

&#x20;   else:

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           patcher = patches\[pool]

&#x20;       except KeyError:

&#x20;           pass

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           patcher()



&#x20;       # set up eventlet/gevent environments ASAP

&#x20;       from celery import concurrency

&#x20;       if pool in concurrency.get\_available\_pool\_names():

&#x20;           concurrency.get\_implementation(pool)





\# this just creates a new module, that imports stuff on first attribute

\# access.  This makes the library faster to use.

old\_module, new\_module = local.recreate\_module(  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;   \_\_name\_\_,

&#x20;   by\_module={

&#x20;       'celery.app': \['Celery', 'bugreport', 'shared\_task'],

&#x20;       'celery.app.task': \['Task'],

&#x20;       'celery.\_state': \['current\_app', 'current\_task'],

&#x20;       'celery.canvas': \[

&#x20;           'Signature', 'chain', 'chord', 'chunks', 'group',

&#x20;           'signature', 'maybe\_signature', 'subtask',

&#x20;           'xmap', 'xstarmap',

&#x20;       ],

&#x20;       'celery.utils': \['uuid'],

&#x20;   },

&#x20;   \_\_package\_\_='celery', \_\_file\_\_=\_\_file\_\_,

&#x20;   \_\_path\_\_=\_\_path\_\_, \_\_doc\_\_=\_\_doc\_\_, \_\_version\_\_=\_\_version\_\_,

&#x20;   \_\_author\_\_=\_\_author\_\_, \_\_contact\_\_=\_\_contact\_\_,

&#x20;   \_\_homepage\_\_=\_\_homepage\_\_, \_\_docformat\_\_=\_\_docformat\_\_, local=local,

&#x20;   VERSION=VERSION, SERIES=SERIES, VERSION\_BANNER=VERSION\_BANNER,

&#x20;   version\_info\_t=version\_info\_t,

&#x20;   version\_info=version\_info,

&#x20;   maybe\_patch\_concurrency=maybe\_patch\_concurrency,

&#x20;   \_find\_option\_with\_arg=\_find\_option\_with\_arg,

)



```

```python
"""Entry-point for the :program:`celery` umbrella command."""



import sys



from . import maybe\_patch\_concurrency



\_\_all\_\_ = ('main',)





def main() -> None:

&#x20;   """Entrypoint to the ``celery`` umbrella command."""

&#x20;   if 'multi' not in sys.argv:

&#x20;       maybe\_patch\_concurrency()

&#x20;   from celery.bin.celery import main as \_main

&#x20;   sys.exit(\_main())





if \_\_name\_\_ == '\_\_main\_\_':  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;   main()



```

```python

"""Sending/Receiving Messages (Kombu integration)."""

import numbers

from collections import namedtuple

from collections.abc import Mapping

from datetime import timedelta

from weakref import WeakValueDictionary



from kombu import Connection, Consumer, Exchange, Producer, Queue, pools

from kombu.common import Broadcast

from kombu.utils.functional import maybe\_list

from kombu.utils.objects import cached\_property



from celery import signals

from celery.utils.nodenames import anon\_nodename

from celery.utils.saferepr import saferepr

from celery.utils.text import indent as textindent

from celery.utils.time import maybe\_make\_aware



from . import routes as \_routes



\_\_all\_\_ = ('AMQP', 'Queues', 'task\_message')



\#: earliest date supported by time.mktime.

INT\_MIN = -2147483648



\#: Human readable queue declaration.

QUEUE\_FORMAT = """

.> {0.name:<16} exchange={0.exchange.name}({0.exchange.type}) \\

key={0.routing\_key}

"""



task\_message = namedtuple('task\_message',

&#x20;                         ('headers', 'properties', 'body', 'sent\_event'))





def utf8dict(d, encoding='utf-8'):

&#x20;   return {k.decode(encoding) if isinstance(k, bytes) else k: v

&#x20;           for k, v in d.items()}





class Queues(dict):

&#x20;   """Queue name⇒ declaration mapping.



&#x20;   Arguments:

&#x20;       queues (Iterable): Initial list/tuple or dict of queues.

&#x20;       create\_missing (bool): By default any unknown queues will be

&#x20;           added automatically, but if this flag is disabled the occurrence

&#x20;           of unknown queues in `wanted` will raise :exc:`KeyError`.

&#x20;       create\_missing\_queue\_type (str): Type of queue to create for missing queues.

&#x20;           Must be either 'classic' (default) or 'quorum'. If set to 'quorum',

&#x20;           the broker will declare new queues using the quorum type.

&#x20;       create\_missing\_queue\_exchange\_type (str): Type of exchange to use

&#x20;           when creating missing queues. If not set, the default exchange type

&#x20;           will be used. If set, the exchange type will be set to this value

&#x20;           when creating missing queues.

&#x20;       max\_priority (int): Default x-max-priority for queues with none set.

&#x20;   """



&#x20;   #: If set, this is a subset of queues to consume from.

&#x20;   #: The rest of the queues are then used for routing only.

&#x20;   \_consume\_from = None



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(

&#x20;           self, queues=None, default\_exchange=None,

&#x20;           create\_missing=True, create\_missing\_queue\_type=None,

&#x20;           create\_missing\_queue\_exchange\_type=None, autoexchange=None,

&#x20;           max\_priority=None, default\_routing\_key=None,

&#x20;   ):

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_()

&#x20;       self.aliases = WeakValueDictionary()

&#x20;       self.default\_exchange = default\_exchange

&#x20;       self.default\_routing\_key = default\_routing\_key

&#x20;       self.create\_missing = create\_missing

&#x20;       self.create\_missing\_queue\_type = create\_missing\_queue\_type

&#x20;       self.create\_missing\_queue\_exchange\_type = create\_missing\_queue\_exchange\_type

&#x20;       self.autoexchange = Exchange if autoexchange is None else autoexchange

&#x20;       self.max\_priority = max\_priority

&#x20;       if queues is not None and not isinstance(queues, Mapping):

&#x20;           queues = {q.name: q for q in queues}

&#x20;       queues = queues or {}

&#x20;       for name, q in queues.items():

&#x20;           self.add(q) if isinstance(q, Queue) else self.add\_compat(name, \*\*q)



&#x20;   def \_\_getitem\_\_(self, name):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           return self.aliases\[name]

&#x20;       except KeyError:

&#x20;           return super().\_\_getitem\_\_(name)



&#x20;   def \_\_setitem\_\_(self, name, queue):

&#x20;       if self.default\_exchange and not queue.exchange:

&#x20;           queue.exchange = self.default\_exchange

&#x20;       super().\_\_setitem\_\_(name, queue)

&#x20;       if queue.alias:

&#x20;           self.aliases\[queue.alias] = queue



&#x20;   def \_\_missing\_\_(self, name):

&#x20;       if self.create\_missing:

&#x20;           return self.add(self.new\_missing(name))

&#x20;       raise KeyError(name)



&#x20;   def add(self, queue, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       """Add new queue.



&#x20;       The first argument can either be a :class:`kombu.Queue` instance,

&#x20;       or the name of a queue.  If the former the rest of the keyword

&#x20;       arguments are ignored, and options are simply taken from the queue

&#x20;       instance.



&#x20;       Arguments:

&#x20;           queue (kombu.Queue, str): Queue to add.

&#x20;           exchange (kombu.Exchange, str):

&#x20;               if queue is str, specifies exchange name.

&#x20;           routing\_key (str): if queue is str, specifies binding key.

&#x20;           exchange\_type (str): if queue is str, specifies type of exchange.

&#x20;           \*\*options (Any): Additional declaration options used when

&#x20;               queue is a str.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       if not isinstance(queue, Queue):

&#x20;           return self.add\_compat(queue, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;       return self.\_add(queue)



&#x20;   def add\_compat(self, name, \*\*options):

&#x20;       # docs used to use binding\_key as routing key

&#x20;       options.setdefault('routing\_key', options.get('binding\_key'))

&#x20;       if options\['routing\_key'] is None:

&#x20;           options\['routing\_key'] = name

&#x20;       return self.\_add(Queue.from\_dict(name, \*\*options))



&#x20;   def \_add(self, queue):

&#x20;       if queue.exchange is None or queue.exchange.name == '':

&#x20;           queue.exchange = self.default\_exchange

&#x20;       if not queue.routing\_key:

&#x20;           queue.routing\_key = self.default\_routing\_key

&#x20;       if self.max\_priority is not None:

&#x20;           if queue.queue\_arguments is None:

&#x20;               queue.queue\_arguments = {}

&#x20;           self.\_set\_max\_priority(queue.queue\_arguments)

&#x20;       self\[queue.name] = queue

&#x20;       return queue



&#x20;   def \_set\_max\_priority(self, args):

&#x20;       if 'x-max-priority' not in args and self.max\_priority is not None:

&#x20;           return args.update({'x-max-priority': self.max\_priority})



&#x20;   def format(self, indent=0, indent\_first=True):

&#x20;       """Format routing table into string for log dumps."""

&#x20;       active = self.consume\_from

&#x20;       if not active:

&#x20;           return ''

&#x20;       info = \[QUEUE\_FORMAT.strip().format(q)

&#x20;               for \_, q in sorted(active.items())]

&#x20;       if indent\_first:

&#x20;           return textindent('\\n'.join(info), indent)

&#x20;       return info\[0] + '\\n' + textindent('\\n'.join(info\[1:]), indent)



&#x20;   def select\_add(self, queue, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       """Add new task queue that'll be consumed from.



&#x20;       The queue will be active even when a subset has been selected

&#x20;       using the :option:`celery worker -Q` option.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       q = self.add(queue, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;       if self.\_consume\_from is not None:

&#x20;           self.\_consume\_from\[q.name] = q

&#x20;       return q



&#x20;   def select(self, include):

&#x20;       """Select a subset of currently defined queues to consume from.



&#x20;       Arguments:

&#x20;           include (Sequence\[str], str): Names of queues to consume from.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       if include:

&#x20;           self.\_consume\_from = {

&#x20;               name: self\[name] for name in maybe\_list(include)

&#x20;           }



&#x20;   def deselect(self, exclude):

&#x20;       """Deselect queues so that they won't be consumed from.



&#x20;       Arguments:

&#x20;           exclude (Sequence\[str], str): Names of queues to avoid

&#x20;               consuming from.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       if exclude:

&#x20;           exclude = maybe\_list(exclude)

&#x20;           if self.\_consume\_from is None:

&#x20;               # using all queues

&#x20;               return self.select(k for k in self if k not in exclude)

&#x20;           # using selection

&#x20;           for queue in exclude:

&#x20;               self.\_consume\_from.pop(queue, None)



&#x20;   def new\_missing(self, name):

&#x20;       queue\_arguments = None

&#x20;       if self.create\_missing\_queue\_type and self.create\_missing\_queue\_type != "classic":

&#x20;           if self.create\_missing\_queue\_type not in ("classic", "quorum"):

&#x20;               raise ValueError(

&#x20;                   f"Invalid queue type '{self.create\_missing\_queue\_type}'. "

&#x20;                   "Valid types are 'classic' and 'quorum'."

&#x20;               )

&#x20;           queue\_arguments = {"x-queue-type": self.create\_missing\_queue\_type}



&#x20;       if self.create\_missing\_queue\_exchange\_type:

&#x20;           exchange = Exchange(name, self.create\_missing\_queue\_exchange\_type)

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           exchange = self.autoexchange(name)



&#x20;       return Queue(name, exchange, name, queue\_arguments=queue\_arguments)



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def consume\_from(self):

&#x20;       if self.\_consume\_from is not None:

&#x20;           return self.\_consume\_from

&#x20;       return self





class AMQP:

&#x20;   """App AMQP API: app.amqp."""



&#x20;   Connection = Connection

&#x20;   Consumer = Consumer

&#x20;   Producer = Producer



&#x20;   #: compat alias to Connection

&#x20;   BrokerConnection = Connection



&#x20;   queues\_cls = Queues



&#x20;   #: Cached and prepared routing table.

&#x20;   \_rtable = None



&#x20;   #: Underlying producer pool instance automatically

&#x20;   #: set by the :attr:`producer\_pool`.

&#x20;   \_producer\_pool = None



&#x20;   # Exchange class/function used when defining automatic queues.

&#x20;   # For example, you can use ``autoexchange = lambda n: None`` to use the

&#x20;   # AMQP default exchange: a shortcut to bypass routing

&#x20;   # and instead send directly to the queue named in the routing key.

&#x20;   autoexchange = None



&#x20;   #: Max size of positional argument representation used for

&#x20;   #: logging purposes.

&#x20;   argsrepr\_maxsize = 1024



&#x20;   #: Max size of keyword argument representation used for logging purposes.

&#x20;   kwargsrepr\_maxsize = 1024



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, app):

&#x20;       self.app = app

&#x20;       self.task\_protocols = {

&#x20;           1: self.as\_task\_v1,

&#x20;           2: self.as\_task\_v2,

&#x20;       }

&#x20;       self.app.\_conf.bind\_to(self.\_handle\_conf\_update)



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def create\_task\_message(self):

&#x20;       return self.task\_protocols\[self.app.conf.task\_protocol]



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def send\_task\_message(self):

&#x20;       return self.\_create\_task\_sender()



&#x20;   def Queues(self, queues, create\_missing=None, create\_missing\_queue\_type=None,

&#x20;              create\_missing\_queue\_exchange\_type=None, autoexchange=None, max\_priority=None):

&#x20;       # Create new :class:`Queues` instance, using queue defaults

&#x20;       # from the current configuration.

&#x20;       conf = self.app.conf

&#x20;       default\_routing\_key = conf.task\_default\_routing\_key

&#x20;       if create\_missing is None:

&#x20;           create\_missing = conf.task\_create\_missing\_queues

&#x20;       if create\_missing\_queue\_type is None:

&#x20;           create\_missing\_queue\_type = conf.task\_create\_missing\_queue\_type

&#x20;       if create\_missing\_queue\_exchange\_type is None:

&#x20;           create\_missing\_queue\_exchange\_type = conf.task\_create\_missing\_queue\_exchange\_type

&#x20;       if max\_priority is None:

&#x20;           max\_priority = conf.task\_queue\_max\_priority

&#x20;       if not queues and conf.task\_default\_queue:

&#x20;           queue\_arguments = None

&#x20;           if conf.task\_default\_queue\_type == 'quorum':

&#x20;               queue\_arguments = {'x-queue-type': 'quorum'}

&#x20;           queues = (Queue(conf.task\_default\_queue,

&#x20;                           exchange=self.default\_exchange,

&#x20;                           routing\_key=default\_routing\_key,

&#x20;                           queue\_arguments=queue\_arguments),)

&#x20;       autoexchange = (self.autoexchange if autoexchange is None

&#x20;                       else autoexchange)

&#x20;       return self.queues\_cls(

&#x20;           queues,

&#x20;           default\_exchange=self.default\_exchange,

&#x20;           create\_missing=create\_missing,

&#x20;           create\_missing\_queue\_type=create\_missing\_queue\_type,

&#x20;           create\_missing\_queue\_exchange\_type=create\_missing\_queue\_exchange\_type,

&#x20;           autoexchange=autoexchange,

&#x20;           max\_priority=max\_priority,

&#x20;           default\_routing\_key=default\_routing\_key,

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def Router(self, queues=None, create\_missing=None):

&#x20;       """Return the current task router."""

&#x20;       return \_routes.Router(self.routes, queues or self.queues,

&#x20;                             self.app.either('task\_create\_missing\_queues',

&#x20;                                             create\_missing), app=self.app)



&#x20;   def flush\_routes(self):

&#x20;       self.\_rtable = \_routes.prepare(self.app.conf.task\_routes)



&#x20;   def TaskConsumer(self, channel, queues=None, accept=None, \*\*kw):

&#x20;       if accept is None:

&#x20;           accept = self.app.conf.accept\_content

&#x20;       return self.Consumer(

&#x20;           channel, accept=accept,

&#x20;           queues=queues or list(self.queues.consume\_from.values()),

&#x20;           \*\*kw

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def as\_task\_v2(self, task\_id, name, args=None, kwargs=None,

&#x20;                  countdown=None, eta=None, group\_id=None, group\_index=None,

&#x20;                  expires=None, retries=0, chord=None,

&#x20;                  callbacks=None, errbacks=None, reply\_to=None,

&#x20;                  time\_limit=None, soft\_time\_limit=None,

&#x20;                  create\_sent\_event=False, root\_id=None, parent\_id=None,

&#x20;                  shadow=None, chain=None, now=None, timezone=None,

&#x20;                  origin=None, ignore\_result=False, argsrepr=None, kwargsrepr=None, stamped\_headers=None,

&#x20;                  replaced\_task\_nesting=0, \*\*options):



&#x20;       args = args or ()

&#x20;       kwargs = kwargs or {}

&#x20;       if not isinstance(args, (list, tuple)):

&#x20;           raise TypeError('task args must be a list or tuple')

&#x20;       if not isinstance(kwargs, Mapping):

&#x20;           raise TypeError('task keyword arguments must be a mapping')

&#x20;       if countdown:  # convert countdown to ETA

&#x20;           self.\_verify\_seconds(countdown, 'countdown')

&#x20;           now = now or self.app.now()

&#x20;           timezone = timezone or self.app.timezone

&#x20;           eta = maybe\_make\_aware(

&#x20;               now + timedelta(seconds=countdown), tz=timezone,

&#x20;           )

&#x20;       if isinstance(expires, numbers.Real):

&#x20;           self.\_verify\_seconds(expires, 'expires')

&#x20;           now = now or self.app.now()

&#x20;           timezone = timezone or self.app.timezone

&#x20;           expires = maybe\_make\_aware(

&#x20;               now + timedelta(seconds=expires), tz=timezone,

&#x20;           )

&#x20;       if not isinstance(eta, str):

&#x20;           eta = eta and eta.isoformat()

&#x20;       # If we retry a task `expires` will already be ISO8601-formatted.

&#x20;       if not isinstance(expires, str):

&#x20;           expires = expires and expires.isoformat()



&#x20;       if argsrepr is None:

&#x20;           argsrepr = saferepr(args, self.argsrepr\_maxsize)

&#x20;       if kwargsrepr is None:

&#x20;           kwargsrepr = saferepr(kwargs, self.kwargsrepr\_maxsize)



&#x20;       if not root\_id:  # empty root\_id defaults to task\_id

&#x20;           root\_id = task\_id



&#x20;       stamps = {header: options\[header] for header in stamped\_headers or \[]}

&#x20;       headers = {

&#x20;           'lang': 'py',

&#x20;           'task': name,

&#x20;           'id': task\_id,

&#x20;           'shadow': shadow,

&#x20;           'eta': eta,

&#x20;           'expires': expires,

&#x20;           'group': group\_id,

&#x20;           'group\_index': group\_index,

&#x20;           'retries': retries,

&#x20;           'timelimit': \[time\_limit, soft\_time\_limit],

&#x20;           'root\_id': root\_id,

&#x20;           'parent\_id': parent\_id,

&#x20;           'argsrepr': argsrepr,

&#x20;           'kwargsrepr': kwargsrepr,

&#x20;           'origin': origin or anon\_nodename(),

&#x20;           'ignore\_result': ignore\_result,

&#x20;           'replaced\_task\_nesting': replaced\_task\_nesting,

&#x20;           'stamped\_headers': stamped\_headers,

&#x20;           'stamps': stamps,

&#x20;       }



&#x20;       return task\_message(

&#x20;           headers=headers,

&#x20;           properties={

&#x20;               'correlation\_id': task\_id,

&#x20;               'reply\_to': reply\_to or '',

&#x20;           },

&#x20;           body=(

&#x20;               args, kwargs, {

&#x20;                   'callbacks': callbacks,

&#x20;                   'errbacks': errbacks,

&#x20;                   'chain': chain,

&#x20;                   'chord': chord,

&#x20;               },

&#x20;           ),

&#x20;           sent\_event={

&#x20;               'uuid': task\_id,

&#x20;               'root\_id': root\_id,

&#x20;               'parent\_id': parent\_id,

&#x20;               'name': name,

&#x20;               'args': argsrepr,

&#x20;               'kwargs': kwargsrepr,

&#x20;               'retries': retries,

&#x20;               'eta': eta,

&#x20;               'expires': expires,

&#x20;           } if create\_sent\_event else None,

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def as\_task\_v1(self, task\_id, name, args=None, kwargs=None,

&#x20;                  countdown=None, eta=None, group\_id=None, group\_index=None,

&#x20;                  expires=None, retries=0,

&#x20;                  chord=None, callbacks=None, errbacks=None, reply\_to=None,

&#x20;                  time\_limit=None, soft\_time\_limit=None,

&#x20;                  create\_sent\_event=False, root\_id=None, parent\_id=None,

&#x20;                  shadow=None, now=None, timezone=None,

&#x20;                  \*\*compat\_kwargs):

&#x20;       args = args or ()

&#x20;       kwargs = kwargs or {}

&#x20;       utc = self.utc

&#x20;       if not isinstance(args, (list, tuple)):

&#x20;           raise TypeError('task args must be a list or tuple')

&#x20;       if not isinstance(kwargs, Mapping):

&#x20;           raise TypeError('task keyword arguments must be a mapping')

&#x20;       if countdown:  # convert countdown to ETA

&#x20;           self.\_verify\_seconds(countdown, 'countdown')

&#x20;           now = now or self.app.now()

&#x20;           eta = now + timedelta(seconds=countdown)

&#x20;       if isinstance(expires, numbers.Real):

&#x20;           self.\_verify\_seconds(expires, 'expires')

&#x20;           now = now or self.app.now()

&#x20;           expires = now + timedelta(seconds=expires)

&#x20;       eta = eta and eta.isoformat()

&#x20;       expires = expires and expires.isoformat()



&#x20;       return task\_message(

&#x20;           headers={},

&#x20;           properties={

&#x20;               'correlation\_id': task\_id,

&#x20;               'reply\_to': reply\_to or '',

&#x20;           },

&#x20;           body={

&#x20;               'task': name,

&#x20;               'id': task\_id,

&#x20;               'args': args,

&#x20;               'kwargs': kwargs,

&#x20;               'group': group\_id,

&#x20;               'group\_index': group\_index,

&#x20;               'retries': retries,

&#x20;               'eta': eta,

&#x20;               'expires': expires,

&#x20;               'utc': utc,

&#x20;               'callbacks': callbacks,

&#x20;               'errbacks': errbacks,

&#x20;               'timelimit': (time\_limit, soft\_time\_limit),

&#x20;               'taskset': group\_id,

&#x20;               'chord': chord,

&#x20;           },

&#x20;           sent\_event={

&#x20;               'uuid': task\_id,

&#x20;               'name': name,

&#x20;               'args': saferepr(args),

&#x20;               'kwargs': saferepr(kwargs),

&#x20;               'retries': retries,

&#x20;               'eta': eta,

&#x20;               'expires': expires,

&#x20;           } if create\_sent\_event else None,

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def \_verify\_seconds(self, s, what):

&#x20;       if s < INT\_MIN:

&#x20;           raise ValueError(f'{what} is out of range: {s!r}')

&#x20;       return s



&#x20;   def \_create\_task\_sender(self):

&#x20;       default\_retry = self.app.conf.task\_publish\_retry

&#x20;       default\_policy = self.app.conf.task\_publish\_retry\_policy

&#x20;       default\_delivery\_mode = self.app.conf.task\_default\_delivery\_mode

&#x20;       default\_queue = self.default\_queue

&#x20;       queues = self.queues

&#x20;       send\_before\_publish = signals.before\_task\_publish.send

&#x20;       before\_receivers = signals.before\_task\_publish.receivers

&#x20;       send\_after\_publish = signals.after\_task\_publish.send

&#x20;       after\_receivers = signals.after\_task\_publish.receivers



&#x20;       send\_task\_sent = signals.task\_sent.send   # XXX compat

&#x20;       sent\_receivers = signals.task\_sent.receivers



&#x20;       default\_evd = self.\_event\_dispatcher

&#x20;       default\_exchange = self.default\_exchange



&#x20;       default\_rkey = self.app.conf.task\_default\_routing\_key

&#x20;       default\_serializer = self.app.conf.task\_serializer

&#x20;       default\_compressor = self.app.conf.task\_compression



&#x20;       def send\_task\_message(producer, name, message,

&#x20;                             exchange=None, routing\_key=None, queue=None,

&#x20;                             event\_dispatcher=None,

&#x20;                             retry=None, retry\_policy=None,

&#x20;                             serializer=None, delivery\_mode=None,

&#x20;                             compression=None, declare=None,

&#x20;                             headers=None, exchange\_type=None,

&#x20;                             timeout=None, confirm\_timeout=None, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;           retry = default\_retry if retry is None else retry

&#x20;           headers2, properties, body, sent\_event = message

&#x20;           if headers:

&#x20;               headers2.update(headers)

&#x20;           if kwargs:

&#x20;               properties.update(kwargs)



&#x20;           qname = queue

&#x20;           if queue is None and exchange is None:

&#x20;               queue = default\_queue

&#x20;           if queue is not None:

&#x20;               if isinstance(queue, str):

&#x20;                   qname, queue = queue, queues\[queue]

&#x20;               else:

&#x20;                   qname = queue.name



&#x20;           if delivery\_mode is None:

&#x20;               try:

&#x20;                   delivery\_mode = queue.exchange.delivery\_mode

&#x20;               except AttributeError:

&#x20;                   pass

&#x20;               delivery\_mode = delivery\_mode or default\_delivery\_mode



&#x20;           if exchange\_type is None:

&#x20;               try:

&#x20;                   exchange\_type = queue.exchange.type

&#x20;               except AttributeError:

&#x20;                   exchange\_type = 'direct'



&#x20;           # convert to anon-exchange, when exchange not set and direct ex.

&#x20;           if (not exchange or not routing\_key) and exchange\_type == 'direct':

&#x20;               exchange, routing\_key = '', qname

&#x20;           elif exchange is None:

&#x20;               # not topic exchange, and exchange not undefined

&#x20;               exchange = queue.exchange.name or default\_exchange

&#x20;               routing\_key = routing\_key or queue.routing\_key or default\_rkey

&#x20;           if declare is None and queue and not isinstance(queue, Broadcast):

&#x20;               declare = \[queue]



&#x20;           # merge default and custom policy

&#x20;           retry = default\_retry if retry is None else retry

&#x20;           \_rp = (dict(default\_policy, \*\*retry\_policy) if retry\_policy

&#x20;                  else default\_policy)



&#x20;           if before\_receivers:

&#x20;               send\_before\_publish(

&#x20;                   sender=name, body=body,

&#x20;                   exchange=exchange, routing\_key=routing\_key,

&#x20;                   declare=declare, headers=headers2,

&#x20;                   properties=properties, retry\_policy=retry\_policy,

&#x20;               )

&#x20;           ret = producer.publish(

&#x20;               body,

&#x20;               exchange=exchange,

&#x20;               routing\_key=routing\_key,

&#x20;               serializer=serializer or default\_serializer,

&#x20;               compression=compression or default\_compressor,

&#x20;               retry=retry, retry\_policy=\_rp,

&#x20;               delivery\_mode=delivery\_mode, declare=declare,

&#x20;               headers=headers2,

&#x20;               timeout=timeout, confirm\_timeout=confirm\_timeout,

&#x20;               \*\*properties

&#x20;           )

&#x20;           if after\_receivers:

&#x20;               send\_after\_publish(sender=name, body=body, headers=headers2,

&#x20;                                  exchange=exchange, routing\_key=routing\_key)

&#x20;           if sent\_receivers:  # XXX deprecated

&#x20;               if isinstance(body, tuple):  # protocol version 2

&#x20;                   send\_task\_sent(

&#x20;                       sender=name, task\_id=headers2\['id'], task=name,

&#x20;                       args=body\[0], kwargs=body\[1],

&#x20;                       eta=headers2\['eta'], taskset=headers2\['group'],

&#x20;                   )

&#x20;               else:  # protocol version 1

&#x20;                   send\_task\_sent(

&#x20;                       sender=name, task\_id=body\['id'], task=name,

&#x20;                       args=body\['args'], kwargs=body\['kwargs'],

&#x20;                       eta=body\['eta'], taskset=body\['taskset'],

&#x20;                   )

&#x20;           if sent\_event:

&#x20;               evd = event\_dispatcher or default\_evd

&#x20;               exname = exchange

&#x20;               if isinstance(exname, Exchange):

&#x20;                   exname = exname.name

&#x20;               sent\_event.update({

&#x20;                   'queue': qname,

&#x20;                   'exchange': exname,

&#x20;                   'routing\_key': routing\_key,

&#x20;               })

&#x20;               evd.publish('task-sent', sent\_event,

&#x20;                           producer, retry=retry, retry\_policy=retry\_policy)

&#x20;           return ret

&#x20;       return send\_task\_message



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def default\_queue(self):

&#x20;       return self.queues\[self.app.conf.task\_default\_queue]



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def queues(self):

&#x20;       """Queue name⇒ declaration mapping."""

&#x20;       return self.Queues(self.app.conf.task\_queues)



&#x20;   @queues.setter

&#x20;   def queues(self, queues):

&#x20;       return self.Queues(queues)



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def routes(self):

&#x20;       if self.\_rtable is None:

&#x20;           self.flush\_routes()

&#x20;       return self.\_rtable



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def router(self):

&#x20;       return self.Router()



&#x20;   @router.setter

&#x20;   def router(self, value):

&#x20;       return value



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def producer\_pool(self):

&#x20;       if self.\_producer\_pool is None:

&#x20;           self.\_producer\_pool = pools.producers\[

&#x20;               self.app.connection\_for\_write()]

&#x20;           self.\_producer\_pool.limit = self.app.pool.limit

&#x20;       return self.\_producer\_pool

&#x20;   publisher\_pool = producer\_pool  # compat alias



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def default\_exchange(self):

&#x20;       return Exchange(self.app.conf.task\_default\_exchange,

&#x20;                       self.app.conf.task\_default\_exchange\_type)



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def utc(self):

&#x20;       return self.app.conf.enable\_utc



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def \_event\_dispatcher(self):

&#x20;       # We call Dispatcher.publish with a custom producer

&#x20;       # so don't need the dispatcher to be enabled.

&#x20;       return self.app.events.Dispatcher(enabled=False)



&#x20;   def \_handle\_conf\_update(self, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       if ('task\_routes' in kwargs or 'task\_routes' in args):

&#x20;           self.flush\_routes()

&#x20;           self.router = self.Router()

&#x20;       return



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\app\\annotations.py

```python

"""Task Annotations.



Annotations is a nice term for monkey-patching task classes

in the configuration.



This prepares and performs the annotations in the

:setting:`task\_annotations` setting.

"""

from celery.utils.functional import firstmethod, mlazy

from celery.utils.imports import instantiate



\_first\_match = firstmethod('annotate')

\_first\_match\_any = firstmethod('annotate\_any')



\_\_all\_\_ = ('MapAnnotation', 'prepare', 'resolve\_all')





class MapAnnotation(dict):

&#x20;   """Annotation map: task\_name => attributes."""



&#x20;   def annotate\_any(self):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           return dict(self\['\*'])

&#x20;       except KeyError:

&#x20;           pass



&#x20;   def annotate(self, task):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           return dict(self\[task.name])

&#x20;       except KeyError:

&#x20;           pass





def prepare(annotations):

&#x20;   """Expand the :setting:`task\_annotations` setting."""

&#x20;   def expand\_annotation(annotation):

&#x20;       if isinstance(annotation, dict):

&#x20;           return MapAnnotation(annotation)

&#x20;       elif isinstance(annotation, str):

&#x20;           return mlazy(instantiate, annotation)

&#x20;       return annotation



&#x20;   if annotations is None:

&#x20;       return ()

&#x20;   elif not isinstance(annotations, (list, tuple)):

&#x20;       annotations = (annotations,)

&#x20;   return \[expand\_annotation(anno) for anno in annotations]





def resolve\_all(anno, task):

&#x20;   """Resolve all pending annotations."""

&#x20;   return (x for x in (\_first\_match(anno, task), \_first\_match\_any(anno)) if x)



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\app\\autoretry.py

```python

"""Tasks auto-retry functionality."""

from vine.utils import wraps



from celery.exceptions import Ignore, Retry

from celery.utils.time import get\_exponential\_backoff\_interval





def add\_autoretry\_behaviour(task, \*\*options):

&#x20;   """Wrap task's `run` method with auto-retry functionality."""

&#x20;   autoretry\_for = tuple(

&#x20;       options.get('autoretry\_for',

&#x20;                   getattr(task, 'autoretry\_for', ()))

&#x20;   )

&#x20;   dont\_autoretry\_for = tuple(

&#x20;       options.get('dont\_autoretry\_for',

&#x20;                   getattr(task, 'dont\_autoretry\_for', ()))

&#x20;   )

&#x20;   retry\_kwargs = options.get(

&#x20;       'retry\_kwargs', getattr(task, 'retry\_kwargs', {})

&#x20;   )

&#x20;   retry\_backoff = float(

&#x20;       options.get('retry\_backoff',

&#x20;                   getattr(task, 'retry\_backoff', False))

&#x20;   )

&#x20;   retry\_backoff\_max = int(

&#x20;       options.get('retry\_backoff\_max',

&#x20;                   getattr(task, 'retry\_backoff\_max', 600))

&#x20;   )

&#x20;   retry\_jitter = options.get(

&#x20;       'retry\_jitter', getattr(task, 'retry\_jitter', True)

&#x20;   )



&#x20;   if autoretry\_for and not hasattr(task, '\_orig\_run'):



&#x20;       @wraps(task.run)

&#x20;       def run(\*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               return task.\_orig\_run(\*args, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;           except Ignore:

&#x20;               # If Ignore signal occurs task shouldn't be retried,

&#x20;               # even if it suits autoretry\_for list

&#x20;               raise

&#x20;           except Retry:

&#x20;               raise

&#x20;           except dont\_autoretry\_for:

&#x20;               raise

&#x20;           except autoretry\_for as exc:

&#x20;               if retry\_backoff:

&#x20;                   retry\_kwargs\['countdown'] = \\

&#x20;                       get\_exponential\_backoff\_interval(

&#x20;                           factor=int(max(1.0, retry\_backoff)),

&#x20;                           retries=task.request.retries,

&#x20;                           maximum=retry\_backoff\_max,

&#x20;                           full\_jitter=retry\_jitter)

&#x20;               # Override max\_retries

&#x20;               if hasattr(task, 'override\_max\_retries'):

&#x20;                   retry\_kwargs\['max\_retries'] = getattr(task,

&#x20;                                                         'override\_max\_retries',

&#x20;                                                         task.max\_retries)

&#x20;               ret = task.retry(exc=exc, \*\*retry\_kwargs)

&#x20;               # Stop propagation

&#x20;               if hasattr(task, 'override\_max\_retries'):

&#x20;                   delattr(task, 'override\_max\_retries')

&#x20;               raise ret



&#x20;       task.\_orig\_run, task.run = task.run, run



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\app\\backends.py

```python

"""Backend selection."""

import sys

import types



from celery.\_state import current\_app

from celery.exceptions import ImproperlyConfigured, reraise

from celery.utils.imports import load\_extension\_class\_names, symbol\_by\_name



\_\_all\_\_ = ('by\_name', 'by\_url')



UNKNOWN\_BACKEND = """

Unknown result backend: {0!r}.  Did you spell that correctly? ({1!r})

"""



BACKEND\_ALIASES = {

&#x20;   'rpc': 'celery.backends.rpc.RPCBackend',

&#x20;   'cache': 'celery.backends.cache:CacheBackend',

&#x20;   'redis': 'celery.backends.redis:RedisBackend',

&#x20;   'rediss': 'celery.backends.redis:RedisBackend',

&#x20;   'sentinel': 'celery.backends.redis:SentinelBackend',

&#x20;   'mongodb': 'celery.backends.mongodb:MongoBackend',

&#x20;   'db': 'celery.backends.database:DatabaseBackend',

&#x20;   'database': 'celery.backends.database:DatabaseBackend',

&#x20;   'elasticsearch': 'celery.backends.elasticsearch:ElasticsearchBackend',

&#x20;   'cassandra': 'celery.backends.cassandra:CassandraBackend',

&#x20;   'couchbase': 'celery.backends.couchbase:CouchbaseBackend',

&#x20;   'couchdb': 'celery.backends.couchdb:CouchBackend',

&#x20;   'cosmosdbsql': 'celery.backends.cosmosdbsql:CosmosDBSQLBackend',

&#x20;   'riak': 'celery.backends.riak:RiakBackend',

&#x20;   'file': 'celery.backends.filesystem:FilesystemBackend',

&#x20;   'disabled': 'celery.backends.base:DisabledBackend',

&#x20;   'consul': 'celery.backends.consul:ConsulBackend',

&#x20;   'dynamodb': 'celery.backends.dynamodb:DynamoDBBackend',

&#x20;   'azureblockblob': 'celery.backends.azureblockblob:AzureBlockBlobBackend',

&#x20;   'arangodb': 'celery.backends.arangodb:ArangoDbBackend',

&#x20;   's3': 'celery.backends.s3:S3Backend',

&#x20;   'gs': 'celery.backends.gcs:GCSBackend',

}





def by\_name(backend=None, loader=None,

&#x20;           extension\_namespace='celery.result\_backends'):

&#x20;   """Get backend class by name/alias."""

&#x20;   backend = backend or 'disabled'

&#x20;   loader = loader or current\_app.loader

&#x20;   aliases = dict(BACKEND\_ALIASES, \*\*loader.override\_backends)

&#x20;   aliases.update(load\_extension\_class\_names(extension\_namespace))

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       cls = symbol\_by\_name(backend, aliases)

&#x20;   except ValueError as exc:

&#x20;       reraise(ImproperlyConfigured, ImproperlyConfigured(

&#x20;           UNKNOWN\_BACKEND.strip().format(backend, exc)), sys.exc\_info()\[2])

&#x20;   if isinstance(cls, types.ModuleType):

&#x20;       raise ImproperlyConfigured(UNKNOWN\_BACKEND.strip().format(

&#x20;           backend, 'is a Python module, not a backend class.'))

&#x20;   return cls





def by\_url(backend=None, loader=None):

&#x20;   """Get backend class by URL."""

&#x20;   url = None

&#x20;   if backend and '://' in backend:

&#x20;       url = backend

&#x20;       scheme, \_, \_ = url.partition('://')

&#x20;       if '+' in scheme:

&#x20;           backend, url = url.split('+', 1)

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           backend = scheme

&#x20;   return by\_name(backend, loader), url



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\app\\base.py

```python

"""Actual App instance implementation."""

import functools

import importlib

import inspect

import os

import sys

import threading

import typing

import warnings

from collections import UserDict, defaultdict, deque

from datetime import datetime

from datetime import timezone as datetime\_timezone

from operator import attrgetter



from click.exceptions import Exit

&#x20;                          connect\_on\_app\_finalize, get\_current\_app, get\_current\_worker\_task, set\_default\_app)
```
