from .utils import (AppPickler, Settings, \_new\_key\_to\_old, \_old\_key\_to\_new, \_unpickle\_app, \_unpickle\_app\_v2, appstr,

&#x20;                   bugreport, detect\_settings)



if typing.TYPE\_CHECKING:  # pragma: no cover  # codecov does not capture this

&#x20;   # flake8 marks the BaseModel import as unused, because the actual typehint is quoted.

&#x20;   from pydantic import BaseModel  # noqa: F401



\_\_all\_\_ = ('Celery',)



logger = get\_logger(\_\_name\_\_)



BUILTIN\_FIXUPS = {

&#x20;   'celery.fixups.django:fixup',

}

USING\_EXECV = os.environ.get('FORKED\_BY\_MULTIPROCESSING')



ERR\_ENVVAR\_NOT\_SET = """

The environment variable {0!r} is not set,

and as such the configuration could not be loaded.



Please set this variable and make sure it points to

a valid configuration module.



Example:

&#x20;   {0}="proj.celeryconfig"

"""





def app\_has\_custom(app, attr):

&#x20;   """Return true if app has customized method `attr`.



&#x20;   Note:

&#x20;       This is used for optimizations in cases where we know

&#x20;       how the default behavior works, but need to account

&#x20;       for someone using inheritance to override a method/property.

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   return mro\_lookup(app.\_\_class\_\_, attr, stop={Celery, object},

&#x20;                     monkey\_patched=\[\_\_name\_\_])





def \_unpickle\_appattr(reverse\_name, args):

&#x20;   """Unpickle app."""

&#x20;   # Given an attribute name and a list of args, gets

&#x20;   # the attribute from the current app and calls it.

&#x20;   return get\_current\_app().\_rgetattr(reverse\_name)(\*args)





def \_after\_fork\_cleanup\_app(app):

&#x20;   # This is used with multiprocessing.register\_after\_fork,

&#x20;   # so need to be at module level.

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       app.\_after\_fork()

&#x20;   except Exception as exc:  # pylint: disable=broad-except

&#x20;       logger.info('after forker raised exception: %r', exc, exc\_info=1)





def pydantic\_wrapper(

&#x20;   app: "Celery",

&#x20;   task\_fun: typing.Callable\[..., typing.Any],

&#x20;   task\_name: str,

&#x20;   strict: bool = True,

&#x20;   context: typing.Optional\[typing.Dict\[str, typing.Any]] = None,

&#x20;   dump\_kwargs: typing.Optional\[typing.Dict\[str, typing.Any]] = None

):

&#x20;   """Wrapper to validate arguments and serialize return values using Pydantic."""

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       pydantic = importlib.import\_module('pydantic')

&#x20;   except ModuleNotFoundError as ex:

&#x20;       raise ImproperlyConfigured('You need to install pydantic to use pydantic model serialization.') from ex



&#x20;   BaseModel: typing.Type\['BaseModel'] = pydantic.BaseModel  # noqa: F811  # only defined when type checking



&#x20;   if context is None:

&#x20;       context = {}

&#x20;   if dump\_kwargs is None:

&#x20;       dump\_kwargs = {}

&#x20;   dump\_kwargs.setdefault('mode', 'json')



&#x20;   # If a file uses `from \_\_future\_\_ import annotations`, all annotations will

&#x20;   # be strings. `typing.get\_type\_hints()` can turn these back into real

&#x20;   # types, but can also sometimes fail due to circular imports. Try that

&#x20;   # first, and fall back to annotations from `inspect.signature()`.

&#x20;   task\_signature = inspect.signature(task\_fun)



&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       type\_hints = typing.get\_type\_hints(task\_fun)

&#x20;   except (NameError, AttributeError, TypeError):

&#x20;       # Fall back to raw annotations from inspect if get\_type\_hints fails

&#x20;       type\_hints = None



&#x20;   @functools.wraps(task\_fun)

&#x20;   def wrapper(\*task\_args, \*\*task\_kwargs):

&#x20;       # Validate task parameters if type hinted as BaseModel

&#x20;       bound\_args = task\_signature.bind(\*task\_args, \*\*task\_kwargs)

&#x20;       for arg\_name, arg\_value in bound\_args.arguments.items():

&#x20;           if type\_hints and arg\_name in type\_hints:

&#x20;               arg\_annotation = type\_hints\[arg\_name]

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               arg\_annotation = task\_signature.parameters\[arg\_name].annotation



&#x20;           optional\_arg = get\_optional\_arg(arg\_annotation)

&#x20;           if optional\_arg is not None and arg\_value is not None:

&#x20;               arg\_annotation = optional\_arg



&#x20;           if annotation\_issubclass(arg\_annotation, BaseModel):

&#x20;               bound\_args.arguments\[arg\_name] = arg\_annotation.model\_validate(

&#x20;                   arg\_value,

&#x20;                   strict=strict,

&#x20;                   context={\*\*context, 'celery\_app': app, 'celery\_task\_name': task\_name},

&#x20;               )



&#x20;       # Call the task with (potentially) converted arguments

&#x20;       returned\_value = task\_fun(\*bound\_args.args, \*\*bound\_args.kwargs)



&#x20;       # Dump Pydantic model if the returned value is an instance of pydantic.BaseModel \*and\* its

&#x20;       # class matches the typehint

&#x20;       if type\_hints and 'return' in type\_hints:

&#x20;           return\_annotation = type\_hints\['return']

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           return\_annotation = task\_signature.return\_annotation



&#x20;       optional\_return\_annotation = get\_optional\_arg(return\_annotation)

&#x20;       if optional\_return\_annotation is not None:

&#x20;           return\_annotation = optional\_return\_annotation



&#x20;       if (

&#x20;           annotation\_is\_class(return\_annotation)

&#x20;           and isinstance(returned\_value, BaseModel)

&#x20;           and isinstance(returned\_value, return\_annotation)

&#x20;       ):

&#x20;           return returned\_value.model\_dump(\*\*dump\_kwargs)



&#x20;       return returned\_value



&#x20;   return wrapper





class PendingConfiguration(UserDict, AttributeDictMixin):

&#x20;   # `app.conf` will be of this type before being explicitly configured,

&#x20;   # meaning the app can keep any configuration set directly

&#x20;   # on `app.conf` before the `app.config\_from\_object` call.

&#x20;   #

&#x20;   # accessing any key will finalize the configuration,

&#x20;   # replacing `app.conf` with a concrete settings object.



&#x20;   callback = None

&#x20;   \_data = None



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, conf, callback):

&#x20;       object.\_\_setattr\_\_(self, '\_data', conf)

&#x20;       object.\_\_setattr\_\_(self, 'callback', callback)



&#x20;   def \_\_setitem\_\_(self, key, value):

&#x20;       self.\_data\[key] = value



&#x20;   def clear(self):

&#x20;       self.\_data.clear()



&#x20;   def update(self, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       self.\_data.update(\*args, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def setdefault(self, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       return self.\_data.setdefault(\*args, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def \_\_contains\_\_(self, key):

&#x20;       # XXX will not show finalized configuration

&#x20;       # setdefault will cause `key in d` to happen,

&#x20;       # so for setdefault to be lazy, so does contains.

&#x20;       return key in self.\_data



&#x20;   def \_\_len\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return len(self.data)



&#x20;   def \_\_repr\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return repr(self.data)



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def data(self):

&#x20;       return self.callback()





class Celery:

&#x20;   """Celery application.



&#x20;   Arguments:

&#x20;       main (str): Name of the main module if running as `\_\_main\_\_`.

&#x20;           This is used as the prefix for auto-generated task names.



&#x20;   Keyword Arguments:

&#x20;       broker (str): URL of the default broker used.

&#x20;       backend (Union\[str, Type\[celery.backends.base.Backend]]):

&#x20;           The result store backend class, or the name of the backend

&#x20;           class to use.



&#x20;           Default is the value of the :setting:`result\_backend` setting.

&#x20;       autofinalize (bool): If set to False a :exc:`RuntimeError`

&#x20;           will be raised if the task registry or tasks are used before

&#x20;           the app is finalized.

&#x20;       set\_as\_current (bool):  Make this the global current app.

&#x20;       include (List\[str]): List of modules every worker should import.



&#x20;       amqp (Union\[str, Type\[AMQP]]): AMQP object or class name.

&#x20;       events (Union\[str, Type\[celery.app.events.Events]]): Events object or

&#x20;           class name.

&#x20;       log (Union\[str, Type\[Logging]]): Log object or class name.

&#x20;       control (Union\[str, Type\[celery.app.control.Control]]): Control object

&#x20;           or class name.

&#x20;       tasks (Union\[str, Type\[TaskRegistry]]): A task registry, or the name of

&#x20;           a registry class.

&#x20;       fixups (List\[str]): List of fix-up plug-ins (e.g., see

&#x20;           :mod:`celery.fixups.django`).

&#x20;       config\_source (Union\[str, class]): Take configuration from a class,

&#x20;           or object.  Attributes may include any settings described in

&#x20;           the documentation.

&#x20;       task\_cls (Union\[str, Type\[celery.app.task.Task]]): base task class to

&#x20;           use. See :ref:`this section <custom-task-cls-app-wide>` for usage.

&#x20;   """



&#x20;   #: This is deprecated, use :meth:`reduce\_keys` instead

&#x20;   Pickler = AppPickler



&#x20;   SYSTEM = platforms.SYSTEM

&#x20;   IS\_macOS, IS\_WINDOWS = platforms.IS\_macOS, platforms.IS\_WINDOWS



&#x20;   #: Name of the `\_\_main\_\_` module.  Required for standalone scripts.

&#x20;   #:

&#x20;   #: If set this will be used instead of `\_\_main\_\_` when automatically

&#x20;   #: generating task names.

&#x20;   main = None



&#x20;   #: Custom options for command-line programs.

&#x20;   #: See :ref:`extending-commandoptions`

&#x20;   user\_options = None



&#x20;   #: Custom bootsteps to extend and modify the worker.

&#x20;   #: See :ref:`extending-bootsteps`.

&#x20;   steps = None



&#x20;   builtin\_fixups = BUILTIN\_FIXUPS



&#x20;   amqp\_cls = 'celery.app.amqp:AMQP'

&#x20;   backend\_cls = None

&#x20;   events\_cls = 'celery.app.events:Events'

&#x20;   loader\_cls = None

&#x20;   log\_cls = 'celery.app.log:Logging'

&#x20;   control\_cls = 'celery.app.control:Control'

&#x20;   task\_cls = 'celery.app.task:Task'

&#x20;   registry\_cls = 'celery.app.registry:TaskRegistry'



&#x20;   #: Thread local storage.

&#x20;   \_local = None

&#x20;   \_fixups = None

&#x20;   \_pool = None

&#x20;   \_conf = None

&#x20;   \_after\_fork\_registered = False



&#x20;   #: Signal sent when app is loading configuration.

&#x20;   on\_configure = None



&#x20;   #: Signal sent after app has prepared the configuration.

&#x20;   on\_after\_configure = None



&#x20;   #: Signal sent after app has been finalized.

&#x20;   on\_after\_finalize = None



&#x20;   #: Signal sent by every new process after fork.

&#x20;   on\_after\_fork = None



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, main=None, loader=None, backend=None,

&#x20;                amqp=None, events=None, log=None, control=None,

&#x20;                set\_as\_current=True, tasks=None, broker=None, include=None,

&#x20;                changes=None, config\_source=None, fixups=None, task\_cls=None,

&#x20;                autofinalize=True, namespace=None, strict\_typing=True,

&#x20;                \*\*kwargs):



&#x20;       self.\_local = threading.local()

&#x20;       self.\_backend\_cache = None



&#x20;       self.clock = LamportClock()

&#x20;       self.main = main

&#x20;       self.amqp\_cls = amqp or self.amqp\_cls

&#x20;       self.events\_cls = events or self.events\_cls

&#x20;       self.loader\_cls = loader or self.\_get\_default\_loader()

&#x20;       self.log\_cls = log or self.log\_cls

&#x20;       self.control\_cls = control or self.control\_cls

&#x20;       self.\_custom\_task\_cls\_used = (

&#x20;           # Custom task class provided as argument

&#x20;           bool(task\_cls)

&#x20;           # subclass of Celery with a task\_cls attribute

&#x20;           or self.\_\_class\_\_ is not Celery and hasattr(self.\_\_class\_\_, 'task\_cls')

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       self.task\_cls = task\_cls or self.task\_cls

&#x20;       self.set\_as\_current = set\_as\_current

&#x20;       self.registry\_cls = symbol\_by\_name(self.registry\_cls)

&#x20;       self.user\_options = defaultdict(set)

&#x20;       self.steps = defaultdict(set)

&#x20;       self.autofinalize = autofinalize

&#x20;       self.namespace = namespace

&#x20;       self.strict\_typing = strict\_typing



&#x20;       self.configured = False

&#x20;       self.\_config\_source = config\_source

&#x20;       self.\_pending\_defaults = deque()

&#x20;       self.\_pending\_periodic\_tasks = deque()



&#x20;       self.finalized = False

&#x20;       self.\_finalize\_mutex = threading.RLock()

&#x20;       self.\_pending = deque()

&#x20;       self.\_tasks = tasks

&#x20;       if not isinstance(self.\_tasks, TaskRegistry):

&#x20;           self.\_tasks = self.registry\_cls(self.\_tasks or {})



&#x20;       # If the class defines a custom \_\_reduce\_args\_\_ we need to use

&#x20;       # the old way of pickling apps: pickling a list of

&#x20;       # args instead of the new way that pickles a dict of keywords.

&#x20;       self.\_using\_v1\_reduce = app\_has\_custom(self, '\_\_reduce\_args\_\_')



&#x20;       # these options are moved to the config to

&#x20;       # simplify pickling of the app object.

&#x20;       self.\_preconf = changes or {}

&#x20;       self.\_preconf\_set\_by\_auto = set()

&#x20;       self.\_\_autoset('broker\_url', broker)

&#x20;       self.\_\_autoset('result\_backend', backend)

&#x20;       self.\_\_autoset('include', include)



&#x20;       for key, value in kwargs.items():

&#x20;           self.\_\_autoset(key, value)



&#x20;       self.\_conf = Settings(

&#x20;           PendingConfiguration(

&#x20;               self.\_preconf, self.\_finalize\_pending\_conf),

&#x20;           prefix=self.namespace,

&#x20;           keys=(\_old\_key\_to\_new, \_new\_key\_to\_old),

&#x20;       )



&#x20;       # - Apply fix-ups.

&#x20;       self.fixups = set(self.builtin\_fixups) if fixups is None else fixups

&#x20;       # ...store fixup instances in \_fixups to keep weakrefs alive.

&#x20;       self.\_fixups = \[symbol\_by\_name(fixup)(self) for fixup in self.fixups]



&#x20;       if self.set\_as\_current:

&#x20;           self.set\_current()



&#x20;       # Signals

&#x20;       if self.on\_configure is None:

&#x20;           # used to be a method pre 4.0

&#x20;           self.on\_configure = Signal(name='app.on\_configure')

&#x20;       self.on\_after\_configure = Signal(

&#x20;           name='app.on\_after\_configure',

&#x20;           providing\_args={'source'},

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       self.on\_after\_finalize = Signal(name='app.on\_after\_finalize')

&#x20;       self.on\_after\_fork = Signal(name='app.on\_after\_fork')



&#x20;       # Boolean signalling, whether fast\_trace\_task are enabled.

&#x20;       # this attribute is set in celery.worker.trace and checked by celery.worker.request

&#x20;       self.use\_fast\_trace\_task = False



&#x20;       self.on\_init()

&#x20;       \_register\_app(self)



&#x20;   def \_get\_default\_loader(self):

&#x20;       # the --loader command-line argument sets the environment variable.

&#x20;       return (

&#x20;           os.environ.get('CELERY\_LOADER') or

&#x20;           self.loader\_cls or

&#x20;           'celery.loaders.app:AppLoader'

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def on\_init(self):

&#x20;       """Optional callback called at init."""



&#x20;   def \_\_autoset(self, key, value):

&#x20;       if value is not None:

&#x20;           self.\_preconf\[key] = value

&#x20;           self.\_preconf\_set\_by\_auto.add(key)



&#x20;   def set\_current(self):

&#x20;       """Make this the current app for this thread."""

&#x20;       \_set\_current\_app(self)



&#x20;   def set\_default(self):

&#x20;       """Make this the default app for all threads."""

&#x20;       set\_default\_app(self)



&#x20;   def \_ensure\_after\_fork(self):

&#x20;       if not self.\_after\_fork\_registered:

&#x20;           self.\_after\_fork\_registered = True

&#x20;           if register\_after\_fork is not None:

&#x20;               register\_after\_fork(self, \_after\_fork\_cleanup\_app)



&#x20;   def close(self):

&#x20;       """Clean up after the application.



&#x20;       Only necessary for dynamically created apps, and you should

&#x20;       probably use the :keyword:`with` statement instead.



&#x20;       Example:

&#x20;           >>> with Celery(set\_as\_current=False) as app:

&#x20;           ...     with app.connection\_for\_write() as conn:

&#x20;           ...         pass

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       self.\_pool = None

&#x20;       \_deregister\_app(self)



&#x20;   def start(self, argv=None):

&#x20;       """Run :program:`celery` using `argv`.



&#x20;       Uses :data:`sys.argv` if `argv` is not specified.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       from celery.bin.celery import celery



&#x20;       celery.params\[0].default = self



&#x20;       if argv is None:

&#x20;           argv = sys.argv



&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           celery.main(args=argv, standalone\_mode=False)

&#x20;       except Exit as e:

&#x20;           return e.exit\_code

&#x20;       finally:

&#x20;           celery.params\[0].default = None



&#x20;   def worker\_main(self, argv=None):

&#x20;       """Run :program:`celery worker` using `argv`.



&#x20;       Uses :data:`sys.argv` if `argv` is not specified.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       if argv is None:

&#x20;           argv = sys.argv



&#x20;       if 'worker' not in argv:

&#x20;           raise ValueError(

&#x20;               "The worker sub-command must be specified in argv.\\n"

&#x20;               "Use app.start() to programmatically start other commands."

&#x20;           )



&#x20;       self.start(argv=argv)



&#x20;   def task(self, \*args, \*\*opts):

&#x20;       """Decorator to create a task class out of any callable.



&#x20;       See :ref:`Task options<task-options>` for a list of the

&#x20;       arguments that can be passed to this decorator.



&#x20;       Examples:

&#x20;           .. code-block:: python



&#x20;               @app.task

&#x20;               def refresh\_feed(url):

&#x20;                   store\_feed(feedparser.parse(url))



&#x20;           with setting extra options:



&#x20;           .. code-block:: python



&#x20;               @app.task(exchange='feeds')

&#x20;               def refresh\_feed(url):

&#x20;                   return store\_feed(feedparser.parse(url))



&#x20;       Note:

&#x20;           App Binding: For custom apps the task decorator will return

&#x20;           a proxy object, so that the act of creating the task is not

&#x20;           performed until the task is used or the task registry is accessed.



&#x20;           If you're depending on binding to be deferred, then you must

&#x20;           not access any attributes on the returned object until the

&#x20;           application is fully set up (finalized).

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       if USING\_EXECV and opts.get('lazy', True):

&#x20;           # When using execv the task in the original module will point to a

&#x20;           # different app, so doing things like 'add.request' will point to

&#x20;           # a different task instance.  This makes sure it will always use

&#x20;           # the task instance from the current app.

&#x20;           # Really need a better solution for this :(

&#x20;           from . import shared\_task

&#x20;           return shared\_task(\*args, lazy=False, \*\*opts)



&#x20;       def inner\_create\_task\_cls(shared=True, filter=None, lazy=True, \*\*opts):

&#x20;           \_filt = filter



&#x20;           def \_create\_task\_cls(fun):

&#x20;               if shared:

&#x20;                   def cons(app):

&#x20;                       return app.\_task\_from\_fun(fun, \*\*opts)



&#x20;                   cons.\_\_name\_\_ = fun.\_\_name\_\_

&#x20;                   connect\_on\_app\_finalize(cons)

&#x20;               if not lazy or self.finalized:

&#x20;                   ret = self.\_task\_from\_fun(fun, \*\*opts)

&#x20;               else:

&#x20;                   # return a proxy object that evaluates on first use

&#x20;                   ret = PromiseProxy(self.\_task\_from\_fun, (fun,), opts,

&#x20;                                      \_\_doc\_\_=fun.\_\_doc\_\_)

&#x20;                   self.\_pending.append(ret)

&#x20;               if \_filt:

&#x20;                   return \_filt(ret)

&#x20;               return ret



&#x20;           return \_create\_task\_cls



&#x20;       if len(args) == 1:

&#x20;           if callable(args\[0]):

&#x20;               return inner\_create\_task\_cls(\*\*opts)(\*args)

&#x20;           raise TypeError('argument 1 to @task() must be a callable')

&#x20;       if args:

&#x20;           raise TypeError(

&#x20;               '@task() takes exactly 1 argument ({} given)'.format(

&#x20;                   sum(\[len(args), len(opts)])))

&#x20;       return inner\_create\_task\_cls(\*\*opts)



&#x20;   def type\_checker(self, fun, bound=False):

&#x20;       return staticmethod(head\_from\_fun(fun, bound=bound))



&#x20;   def \_task\_from\_fun(

&#x20;       self,

&#x20;       fun,

&#x20;       name=None,

&#x20;       base=None,

&#x20;       bind=False,

&#x20;       pydantic: bool = False,

&#x20;       pydantic\_strict: bool = False,

&#x20;       pydantic\_context: typing.Optional\[typing.Dict\[str, typing.Any]] = None,

&#x20;       pydantic\_dump\_kwargs: typing.Optional\[typing.Dict\[str, typing.Any]] = None,

&#x20;       \*\*options,

&#x20;   ):

&#x20;       if not self.finalized and not self.autofinalize:

&#x20;           raise RuntimeError('Contract breach: app not finalized')

&#x20;       name = name or self.gen\_task\_name(fun.\_\_name\_\_, fun.\_\_module\_\_)

&#x20;       base = base or self.Task



&#x20;       if name not in self.\_tasks:

&#x20;           if pydantic is True:

&#x20;               fun = pydantic\_wrapper(self, fun, name, pydantic\_strict, pydantic\_context, pydantic\_dump\_kwargs)



&#x20;           run = fun if bind else staticmethod(fun)

&#x20;           task = type(fun.\_\_name\_\_, (base,), dict({

&#x20;               'app': self,

&#x20;               'name': name,

&#x20;               'run': run,

&#x20;               '\_decorated': True,

&#x20;               '\_\_doc\_\_': fun.\_\_doc\_\_,

&#x20;               '\_\_module\_\_': fun.\_\_module\_\_,

&#x20;               '\_\_annotations\_\_': fun.\_\_annotations\_\_,

&#x20;               '\_\_header\_\_': self.type\_checker(fun, bound=bind),

&#x20;               '\_\_wrapped\_\_': run}, \*\*options))()

&#x20;           # for some reason \_\_qualname\_\_ cannot be set in type()

&#x20;           # so we have to set it here.

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               task.\_\_qualname\_\_ = fun.\_\_qualname\_\_

&#x20;           except AttributeError:

&#x20;               pass

&#x20;           self.\_tasks\[task.name] = task

&#x20;           task.bind(self)  # connects task to this app

&#x20;           add\_autoretry\_behaviour(task, \*\*options)

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           task = self.\_tasks\[name]

&#x20;       return task



&#x20;   def register\_task(self, task, \*\*options):

&#x20;       """Utility for registering a task-based class.



&#x20;       Note:

&#x20;           This is here for compatibility with old Celery 1.0

&#x20;           style task classes, you should not need to use this for

&#x20;           new projects.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       task = inspect.isclass(task) and task() or task

&#x20;       if not task.name:

&#x20;           task\_cls = type(task)

&#x20;           task.name = self.gen\_task\_name(

&#x20;               task\_cls.\_\_name\_\_, task\_cls.\_\_module\_\_)

&#x20;       add\_autoretry\_behaviour(task, \*\*options)

&#x20;       self.tasks\[task.name] = task

&#x20;       task.\_app = self

&#x20;       task.bind(self)

&#x20;       return task



&#x20;   def gen\_task\_name(self, name, module):

&#x20;       return gen\_task\_name(self, name, module)



&#x20;   def finalize(self, auto=False):

&#x20;       """Finalize the app.



&#x20;       This loads built-in tasks, evaluates pending task decorators,

&#x20;       reads configuration, etc.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       with self.\_finalize\_mutex:

&#x20;           if not self.finalized:

&#x20;               if auto and not self.autofinalize:

&#x20;                   raise RuntimeError('Contract breach: app not finalized')

&#x20;               self.finalized = True

&#x20;               \_announce\_app\_finalized(self)



&#x20;               pending = self.\_pending

&#x20;               while pending:

&#x20;                   maybe\_evaluate(pending.popleft())



&#x20;               for task in self.\_tasks.values():

&#x20;                   task.bind(self)



&#x20;               self.on\_after\_finalize.send(sender=self)



&#x20;   def add\_defaults(self, fun):

&#x20;       """Add default configuration from dict ``d``.



&#x20;       If the argument is a callable function then it will be regarded

&#x20;       as a promise, and it won't be loaded until the configuration is

&#x20;       actually needed.



&#x20;       This method can be compared to:



&#x20;       .. code-block:: pycon



&#x20;           >>> celery.conf.update(d)



&#x20;       with a difference that 1) no copy will be made and 2) the dict will

&#x20;       not be transferred when the worker spawns child processes, so

&#x20;       it's important that the same configuration happens at import time

&#x20;       when pickle restores the object on the other side.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       if not callable(fun):

&#x20;           d, fun = fun, lambda: d

&#x20;       if self.configured:

&#x20;           return self.\_conf.add\_defaults(fun())

&#x20;       self.\_pending\_defaults.append(fun)



&#x20;   def config\_from\_object(self, obj,

&#x20;                          silent=False, force=False, namespace=None):

&#x20;       """Read configuration from object.



&#x20;       Object is either an actual object or the name of a module to import.



&#x20;       Example:

&#x20;           >>> celery.config\_from\_object('myapp.celeryconfig')



&#x20;           >>> from myapp import celeryconfig

&#x20;           >>> celery.config\_from\_object(celeryconfig)



&#x20;       Arguments:

&#x20;           silent (bool): If true then import errors will be ignored.

&#x20;           force (bool): Force reading configuration immediately.

&#x20;               By default the configuration will be read only when required.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       self.\_config\_source = obj

&#x20;       self.namespace = namespace or self.namespace

&#x20;       if force or self.configured:

&#x20;           self.\_conf = None

&#x20;           if self.loader.config\_from\_object(obj, silent=silent):

&#x20;               return self.conf



&#x20;   def config\_from\_envvar(self, variable\_name, silent=False, force=False):

&#x20;       """Read configuration from environment variable.



&#x20;       The value of the environment variable must be the name

&#x20;       of a module to import.



&#x20;       Example:

&#x20;           >>> os.environ\['CELERY\_CONFIG\_MODULE'] = 'myapp.celeryconfig'

&#x20;           >>> celery.config\_from\_envvar('CELERY\_CONFIG\_MODULE')

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       module\_name = os.environ.get(variable\_name)

&#x20;       if not module\_name:

&#x20;           if silent:

&#x20;               return False

&#x20;           raise ImproperlyConfigured(

&#x20;               ERR\_ENVVAR\_NOT\_SET.strip().format(variable\_name))

&#x20;       return self.config\_from\_object(module\_name, silent=silent, force=force)



&#x20;   def config\_from\_cmdline(self, argv, namespace='celery'):

&#x20;       self.\_conf.update(

&#x20;           self.loader.cmdline\_config\_parser(argv, namespace)

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def setup\_security(self, allowed\_serializers=None, key=None, key\_password=None, cert=None,

&#x20;                      store=None, digest=DEFAULT\_SECURITY\_DIGEST,

&#x20;                      serializer='json'):

&#x20;       """Setup the message-signing serializer.



&#x20;       This will affect all application instances (a global operation).



&#x20;       Disables untrusted serializers and if configured to use the ``auth``

&#x20;       serializer will register the ``auth`` serializer with the provided

&#x20;       settings into the Kombu serializer registry.



&#x20;       Arguments:

&#x20;           allowed\_serializers (Set\[str]): List of serializer names, or

&#x20;               content\_types that should be exempt from being disabled.

&#x20;           key (str): Name of private key file to use.

&#x20;               Defaults to the :setting:`security\_key` setting.

&#x20;           key\_password (bytes): Password to decrypt the private key.

&#x20;               Defaults to the :setting:`security\_key\_password` setting.

&#x20;           cert (str): Name of certificate file to use.

&#x20;               Defaults to the :setting:`security\_certificate` setting.

&#x20;           store (str): Directory containing certificates.

&#x20;               Defaults to the :setting:`security\_cert\_store` setting.

&#x20;           digest (str): Digest algorithm used when signing messages.

&#x20;               Default is ``sha256``.

&#x20;           serializer (str): Serializer used to encode messages after

&#x20;               they've been signed.  See :setting:`task\_serializer` for

&#x20;               the serializers supported.  Default is ``json``.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       from celery.security import setup\_security

&#x20;       return setup\_security(allowed\_serializers, key, key\_password, cert,

&#x20;                             store, digest, serializer, app=self)



&#x20;   def autodiscover\_tasks(self, packages=None,

&#x20;                          related\_name='tasks', force=False):

&#x20;       """Auto-discover task modules.



&#x20;       Searches a list of packages for a "tasks.py" module (or use

&#x20;       related\_name argument).



&#x20;       If the name is empty, this will be delegated to fix-ups (e.g., Django).



&#x20;       For example if you have a directory layout like this:



&#x20;       .. code-block:: text



&#x20;           foo/\_\_init\_\_.py

&#x20;              tasks.py

&#x20;              models.py



&#x20;           bar/\_\_init\_\_.py

&#x20;               tasks.py

&#x20;               models.py



&#x20;           baz/\_\_init\_\_.py

&#x20;               models.py



&#x20;       Then calling ``app.autodiscover\_tasks(\['foo', 'bar', 'baz'])`` will

&#x20;       result in the modules ``foo.tasks`` and ``bar.tasks`` being imported.



&#x20;       Arguments:

&#x20;           packages (List\[str]): List of packages to search.

&#x20;               This argument may also be a callable, in which case the

&#x20;               value returned is used (for lazy evaluation).

&#x20;           related\_name (Optional\[str]): The name of the module to find.  Defaults

&#x20;               to "tasks": meaning "look for 'module.tasks' for every

&#x20;               module in ``packages``.".  If ``None`` will only try to import

&#x20;               the package, i.e. "look for 'module'".

&#x20;           force (bool): By default this call is lazy so that the actual

&#x20;               auto-discovery won't happen until an application imports

&#x20;               the default modules.  Forcing will cause the auto-discovery

&#x20;               to happen immediately.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       if force:

&#x20;           return self.\_autodiscover\_tasks(packages, related\_name)

&#x20;       signals.import\_modules.connect(starpromise(

&#x20;           self.\_autodiscover\_tasks, packages, related\_name,

&#x20;       ), weak=False, sender=self)



&#x20;   def \_autodiscover\_tasks(self, packages, related\_name, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       if packages:

&#x20;           return self.\_autodiscover\_tasks\_from\_names(packages, related\_name)

&#x20;       return self.\_autodiscover\_tasks\_from\_fixups(related\_name)



&#x20;   def \_autodiscover\_tasks\_from\_names(self, packages, related\_name):

&#x20;       # packages argument can be lazy

&#x20;       return self.loader.autodiscover\_tasks(

&#x20;           packages() if callable(packages) else packages, related\_name,

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def \_autodiscover\_tasks\_from\_fixups(self, related\_name):

&#x20;       return self.\_autodiscover\_tasks\_from\_names(\[

&#x20;           pkg for fixup in self.\_fixups

&#x20;           if hasattr(fixup, 'autodiscover\_tasks')

&#x20;           for pkg in fixup.autodiscover\_tasks()

&#x20;       ], related\_name=related\_name)



&#x20;   def send\_task(self, name, args=None, kwargs=None, countdown=None,

&#x20;                 eta=None, task\_id=None, producer=None, connection=None,

&#x20;                 router=None, result\_cls=None, expires=None,

&#x20;                 publisher=None, link=None, link\_error=None,

&#x20;                 add\_to\_parent=True, group\_id=None, group\_index=None,

&#x20;                 retries=0, chord=None,

&#x20;                 reply\_to=None, time\_limit=None, soft\_time\_limit=None,

&#x20;                 root\_id=None, parent\_id=None, route\_name=None,

&#x20;                 shadow=None, chain=None, task\_type=None, replaced\_task\_nesting=0, \*\*options):

&#x20;       """Send task by name.



&#x20;       Supports the same arguments as :meth:`@-Task.apply\_async`.



&#x20;       Arguments:

&#x20;           name (str): Name of task to call (e.g., `"tasks.add"`).

&#x20;           result\_cls (AsyncResult): Specify custom result class.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       parent = have\_parent = None

&#x20;       amqp = self.amqp

&#x20;       task\_id = task\_id or uuid()

&#x20;       producer = producer or publisher  # XXX compat

&#x20;       router = router or amqp.router

&#x20;       conf = self.conf

&#x20;       if conf.task\_always\_eager:  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;           warnings.warn(AlwaysEagerIgnored(

&#x20;               'task\_always\_eager has no effect on send\_task',

&#x20;           ), stacklevel=2)



&#x20;       ignore\_result = options.pop('ignore\_result', False)

&#x20;       options = router.route(

&#x20;           options, route\_name or name, args, kwargs, task\_type)



&#x20;       driver\_type = self.producer\_pool.connections.connection.transport.driver\_type



&#x20;       if (eta or countdown) and detect\_quorum\_queues(self, driver\_type)\[0]:



&#x20;           queue = options.get("queue")

&#x20;           exchange\_type = queue.exchange.type if queue else options\["exchange\_type"]

&#x20;           routing\_key = queue.routing\_key if queue else options\["routing\_key"]

&#x20;           exchange\_name = queue.exchange.name if queue else options\["exchange"]



&#x20;           if exchange\_type != 'direct':

&#x20;               if eta:

&#x20;                   if isinstance(eta, str):

&#x20;                       eta = isoparse(eta)

&#x20;                   countdown = (maybe\_make\_aware(eta) - self.now()).total\_seconds()



&#x20;               if countdown:

&#x20;                   if countdown > 0:

&#x20;                       routing\_key = calculate\_routing\_key(int(countdown), routing\_key)

&#x20;                       exchange = Exchange(

&#x20;                           'celery\_delayed\_27',

&#x20;                           type='topic',

&#x20;                       )

&#x20;                       options.pop("queue", None)

&#x20;                       options\['routing\_key'] = routing\_key

&#x20;                       options\['exchange'] = exchange



&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               logger.warning(

&#x20;                   'Direct exchanges are not supported with native delayed delivery.\\n'

&#x20;                   f'{exchange\_name} is a direct exchange but should be a topic exchange or '

&#x20;                   'a fanout exchange in order for native delayed delivery to work properly.\\n'

&#x20;                   'If quorum queues are used, this task may block the worker process until the ETA arrives.'

&#x20;               )



&#x20;       if expires is not None:

&#x20;           if isinstance(expires, datetime):

&#x20;               expires\_s = (maybe\_make\_aware(

&#x20;                   expires) - self.now()).total\_seconds()

&#x20;           elif isinstance(expires, str):

&#x20;               expires\_s = (maybe\_make\_aware(

&#x20;                   isoparse(expires)) - self.now()).total\_seconds()

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               expires\_s = expires



&#x20;           if expires\_s < 0:

&#x20;               logger.warning(

&#x20;                   f"{task\_id} has an expiration date in the past ({-expires\_s}s ago).\\n"

&#x20;                   "We assume this is intended and so we have set the "

&#x20;                   "expiration date to 0 instead.\\n"

&#x20;                   "According to RabbitMQ's documentation:\\n"

&#x20;                   "\\"Setting the TTL to 0 causes messages to be expired upon "

&#x20;                   "reaching a queue unless they can be delivered to a "

&#x20;                   "consumer immediately.\\"\\n"

&#x20;                   "If this was unintended, please check the code which "

&#x20;                   "published this task."

&#x20;               )

&#x20;               expires\_s = 0



&#x20;           options\["expiration"] = expires\_s



&#x20;       if not root\_id or not parent\_id:

&#x20;           parent = self.current\_worker\_task

&#x20;           if parent:

&#x20;               if not root\_id:

&#x20;                   root\_id = parent.request.root\_id or parent.request.id

&#x20;               if not parent\_id:

&#x20;                   parent\_id = parent.request.id



&#x20;               if conf.task\_inherit\_parent\_priority:

&#x20;                   options.setdefault('priority',

&#x20;                                      parent.request.delivery\_info.get('priority'))



&#x20;       # alias for 'task\_as\_v2'

&#x20;       message = amqp.create\_task\_message(

&#x20;           task\_id, name, args, kwargs, countdown, eta, group\_id, group\_index,

&#x20;           expires, retries, chord,

&#x20;           maybe\_list(link), maybe\_list(link\_error),

&#x20;           reply\_to or self.thread\_oid, time\_limit, soft\_time\_limit,

&#x20;           self.conf.task\_send\_sent\_event,

&#x20;           root\_id, parent\_id, shadow, chain,

&#x20;           ignore\_result=ignore\_result,

&#x20;           replaced\_task\_nesting=replaced\_task\_nesting, \*\*options

&#x20;       )



&#x20;       stamped\_headers = options.pop('stamped\_headers', \[])

&#x20;       for stamp in stamped\_headers:

&#x20;           options.pop(stamp)



&#x20;       if connection:

&#x20;           producer = amqp.Producer(connection, auto\_declare=False)



&#x20;       with self.producer\_or\_acquire(producer) as P:

&#x20;           with P.connection.\_reraise\_as\_library\_errors():

&#x20;               if not ignore\_result:

&#x20;                   self.backend.on\_task\_call(P, task\_id)

&#x20;               amqp.send\_task\_message(P, name, message, \*\*options)

&#x20;       result = (result\_cls or self.AsyncResult)(task\_id)

&#x20;       # We avoid using the constructor since a custom result class

&#x20;       # can be used, in which case the constructor may still use

&#x20;       # the old signature.

&#x20;       result.ignored = ignore\_result



&#x20;       if add\_to\_parent:

&#x20;           if not have\_parent:

&#x20;               parent, have\_parent = self.current\_worker\_task, True

&#x20;           if parent:

&#x20;               parent.add\_trail(result)

&#x20;       return result



&#x20;   def connection\_for\_read(self, url=None, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       """Establish connection used for consuming.



&#x20;       See Also:

&#x20;           :meth:`connection` for supported arguments.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.\_connection(url or self.conf.broker\_read\_url, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def connection\_for\_write(self, url=None, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       """Establish connection used for producing.



&#x20;       See Also:

&#x20;           :meth:`connection` for supported arguments.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.\_connection(url or self.conf.broker\_write\_url, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def connection(self, hostname=None, userid=None, password=None,

&#x20;                  virtual\_host=None, port=None, ssl=None,

&#x20;                  connect\_timeout=None, transport=None,

&#x20;                  transport\_options=None, heartbeat=None,

&#x20;                  login\_method=None, failover\_strategy=None, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       """Establish a connection to the message broker.



&#x20;       Please use :meth:`connection\_for\_read` and

&#x20;       :meth:`connection\_for\_write` instead, to convey the intent

&#x20;       of use for this connection.



&#x20;       Arguments:

&#x20;           url: Either the URL or the hostname of the broker to use.

&#x20;           hostname (str): URL, Hostname/IP-address of the broker.

&#x20;               If a URL is used, then the other argument below will

&#x20;               be taken from the URL instead.

&#x20;           userid (str): Username to authenticate as.

&#x20;           password (str): Password to authenticate with

&#x20;           virtual\_host (str): Virtual host to use (domain).

&#x20;           port (int): Port to connect to.

&#x20;           ssl (bool, Dict): Defaults to the :setting:`broker\_use\_ssl`

&#x20;               setting.

&#x20;           transport (str): defaults to the :setting:`broker\_transport`

&#x20;               setting.

&#x20;           transport\_options (Dict): Dictionary of transport specific options.

&#x20;           heartbeat (int): AMQP Heartbeat in seconds (``pyamqp`` only).

&#x20;           login\_method (str): Custom login method to use (AMQP only).

&#x20;           failover\_strategy (str, Callable): Custom failover strategy.

&#x20;           \*\*kwargs: Additional arguments to :class:`kombu.Connection`.



&#x20;       Returns:

&#x20;           kombu.Connection: the lazy connection instance.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.connection\_for\_write(

&#x20;           hostname or self.conf.broker\_write\_url,

&#x20;           userid=userid, password=password,

&#x20;           virtual\_host=virtual\_host, port=port, ssl=ssl,

&#x20;           connect\_timeout=connect\_timeout, transport=transport,

&#x20;           transport\_options=transport\_options, heartbeat=heartbeat,

&#x20;           login\_method=login\_method, failover\_strategy=failover\_strategy,

&#x20;           \*\*kwargs

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def \_connection(self, url, userid=None, password=None,

&#x20;                   virtual\_host=None, port=None, ssl=None,

&#x20;                   connect\_timeout=None, transport=None,

&#x20;                   transport\_options=None, heartbeat=None,

&#x20;                   login\_method=None, failover\_strategy=None, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       conf = self.conf

&#x20;       return self.amqp.Connection(

&#x20;           url,

&#x20;           userid or conf.broker\_user,

&#x20;           password or conf.broker\_password,

&#x20;           virtual\_host or conf.broker\_vhost,

&#x20;           port or conf.broker\_port,

&#x20;           transport=transport or conf.broker\_transport,

&#x20;           ssl=self.either('broker\_use\_ssl', ssl),

&#x20;           heartbeat=heartbeat,

&#x20;           login\_method=login\_method or conf.broker\_login\_method,

&#x20;           failover\_strategy=(

&#x20;               failover\_strategy or conf.broker\_failover\_strategy

&#x20;           ),

&#x20;           transport\_options=dict(

&#x20;               conf.broker\_transport\_options, \*\*transport\_options or {}

&#x20;           ),

&#x20;           connect\_timeout=self.either(

&#x20;               'broker\_connection\_timeout', connect\_timeout

&#x20;           ),

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   broker\_connection = connection



&#x20;   def \_acquire\_connection(self, pool=True):

&#x20;       """Helper for :meth:`connection\_or\_acquire`."""

&#x20;       if pool:

&#x20;           return self.pool.acquire(block=True)

&#x20;       return self.connection\_for\_write()



&#x20;   def connection\_or\_acquire(self, connection=None, pool=True, \*\_, \*\*\_\_):

&#x20;       """Context used to acquire a connection from the pool.



&#x20;       For use within a :keyword:`with` statement to get a connection

&#x20;       from the pool if one is not already provided.



&#x20;       Arguments:

&#x20;           connection (kombu.Connection): If not provided, a connection

&#x20;               will be acquired from the connection pool.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return FallbackContext(connection, self.\_acquire\_connection, pool=pool)



&#x20;   default\_connection = connection\_or\_acquire  # XXX compat



&#x20;   def producer\_or\_acquire(self, producer=None):

&#x20;       """Context used to acquire a producer from the pool.



&#x20;       For use within a :keyword:`with` statement to get a producer

&#x20;       from the pool if one is not already provided



&#x20;       Arguments:

&#x20;           producer (kombu.Producer): If not provided, a producer

&#x20;               will be acquired from the producer pool.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return FallbackContext(

&#x20;           producer, self.producer\_pool.acquire, block=True,

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   default\_producer = producer\_or\_acquire  # XXX compat



&#x20;   def prepare\_config(self, c):

&#x20;       """Prepare configuration before it is merged with the defaults."""

&#x20;       return find\_deprecated\_settings(c)



&#x20;   def now(self):

&#x20;       """Return the current time and date as a datetime."""

&#x20;       now\_in\_utc = to\_utc(datetime.now(datetime\_timezone.utc))

&#x20;       return now\_in\_utc.astimezone(self.timezone)



&#x20;   def select\_queues(self, queues=None):

&#x20;       """Select subset of queues.



&#x20;       Arguments:

&#x20;           queues (Sequence\[str]): a list of queue names to keep.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.amqp.queues.select(queues)



&#x20;   def either(self, default\_key, \*defaults):

&#x20;       """Get key from configuration or use default values.



&#x20;       Fallback to the value of a configuration key if none of the

&#x20;       `\*values` are true.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return first(None, \[

&#x20;           first(None, defaults), starpromise(self.conf.get, default\_key),

&#x20;       ])



&#x20;   def bugreport(self):

&#x20;       """Return information useful in bug reports."""

&#x20;       return bugreport(self)



&#x20;   def \_get\_backend(self):

&#x20;       backend, url = backends.by\_url(

&#x20;           self.backend\_cls or self.conf.result\_backend,

&#x20;           self.loader)

&#x20;       return backend(app=self, url=url)



&#x20;   def \_finalize\_pending\_conf(self):

&#x20;       """Get config value by key and finalize loading the configuration.



&#x20;       Note:

&#x20;           This is used by PendingConfiguration:

&#x20;               as soon as you access a key the configuration is read.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           conf = self.\_conf = self.\_load\_config()

&#x20;       except AttributeError as err:

&#x20;           # AttributeError is not propagated, it is "handled" by

&#x20;           # PendingConfiguration parent class. This causes

&#x20;           # confusing RecursionError.

&#x20;           raise ModuleNotFoundError(\*err.args) from err



&#x20;       return conf



&#x20;   def \_load\_config(self):

&#x20;       if isinstance(self.on\_configure, Signal):

&#x20;           self.on\_configure.send(sender=self)

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           # used to be a method pre 4.0

&#x20;           self.on\_configure()

&#x20;       if self.\_config\_source:

&#x20;           self.loader.config\_from\_object(self.\_config\_source)

&#x20;       self.configured = True

&#x20;       settings = detect\_settings(

&#x20;           self.prepare\_config(self.loader.conf), self.\_preconf,

&#x20;           ignore\_keys=self.\_preconf\_set\_by\_auto, prefix=self.namespace,

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       if self.\_conf is not None:

&#x20;           # replace in place, as someone may have referenced app.conf,

&#x20;           # done some changes, accessed a key, and then try to make more

&#x20;           # changes to the reference and not the finalized value.

&#x20;           self.\_conf.swap\_with(settings)

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           self.\_conf = settings



&#x20;       # load lazy config dict initializers.

&#x20;       pending\_def = self.\_pending\_defaults

&#x20;       while pending\_def:

&#x20;           self.\_conf.add\_defaults(maybe\_evaluate(pending\_def.popleft()()))



&#x20;       # load lazy periodic tasks

&#x20;       pending\_beat = self.\_pending\_periodic\_tasks

&#x20;       while pending\_beat:

&#x20;           periodic\_task\_args, periodic\_task\_kwargs = pending\_beat.popleft()

&#x20;           self.\_add\_periodic\_task(\*periodic\_task\_args, \*\*periodic\_task\_kwargs)



&#x20;       self.on\_after\_configure.send(sender=self, source=self.\_conf)

&#x20;       return self.\_conf



&#x20;   def \_after\_fork(self):

&#x20;       self.\_pool = None

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           self.\_\_dict\_\_\['amqp'].\_producer\_pool = None

&#x20;       except (AttributeError, KeyError):

&#x20;           pass

&#x20;       self.on\_after\_fork.send(sender=self)



&#x20;   def signature(self, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       """Return a new :class:`\~celery.Signature` bound to this app."""

&#x20;       kwargs\['app'] = self

&#x20;       return self.\_canvas.signature(\*args, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def add\_periodic\_task(self, schedule, sig,

&#x20;                         args=(), kwargs=(), name=None, \*\*opts):

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       Add a periodic task to beat schedule.



&#x20;       Celery beat store tasks based on `sig` or `name` if provided. Adding the

&#x20;       same signature twice make the second task override the first one. To

&#x20;       avoid the override, use distinct `name` for them.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       key, entry = self.\_sig\_to\_periodic\_task\_entry(

&#x20;           schedule, sig, args, kwargs, name, \*\*opts)

&#x20;       if self.configured:

&#x20;           self.\_add\_periodic\_task(key, entry, name=name)

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           self.\_pending\_periodic\_tasks.append(\[(key, entry), {"name": name}])

&#x20;       return key



&#x20;   def \_sig\_to\_periodic\_task\_entry(self, schedule, sig,

&#x20;                                   args=(), kwargs=None, name=None, \*\*opts):

&#x20;       kwargs = {} if not kwargs else kwargs

&#x20;       sig = (sig.clone(args, kwargs)

&#x20;              if isinstance(sig, abstract.CallableSignature)

&#x20;              else self.signature(sig.name, args, kwargs))

&#x20;       return name or repr(sig), {

&#x20;           'schedule': schedule,

&#x20;           'task': sig.name,

&#x20;           'args': sig.args,

&#x20;           'kwargs': sig.kwargs,

&#x20;           'options': dict(sig.options, \*\*opts),

&#x20;       }



&#x20;   def \_add\_periodic\_task(self, key, entry, name=None):

&#x20;       if name is None and key in self.\_conf.beat\_schedule:

&#x20;           logger.warning(

&#x20;               f"Periodic task key='{key}' shadowed a previous unnamed periodic task."

&#x20;               " Pass a name kwarg to add\_periodic\_task to silence this warning."

&#x20;           )



&#x20;       self.\_conf.beat\_schedule\[key] = entry



&#x20;   def create\_task\_cls(self):

&#x20;       """Create a base task class bound to this app."""

&#x20;       return self.subclass\_with\_self(

&#x20;           self.task\_cls, name='Task', attribute='\_app',

&#x20;           keep\_reduce=True, abstract=True,

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def subclass\_with\_self(self, Class, name=None, attribute='app',

&#x20;                          reverse=None, keep\_reduce=False, \*\*kw):

&#x20;       """Subclass an app-compatible class.



&#x20;       App-compatible means that the class has a class attribute that

&#x20;       provides the default app it should use, for example:

&#x20;       ``class Foo: app = None``.



&#x20;       Arguments:

&#x20;           Class (type): The app-compatible class to subclass.

&#x20;           name (str): Custom name for the target class.

&#x20;           attribute (str): Name of the attribute holding the app,

&#x20;               Default is 'app'.

&#x20;           reverse (str): Reverse path to this object used for pickling

&#x20;               purposes. For example, to get ``app.AsyncResult``,

&#x20;               use ``"AsyncResult"``.

&#x20;           keep\_reduce (bool): If enabled a custom ``\_\_reduce\_\_``

&#x20;               implementation won't be provided.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       Class = symbol\_by\_name(Class)

&#x20;       reverse = reverse if reverse else Class.\_\_name\_\_



&#x20;       def \_\_reduce\_\_(self):

&#x20;           return \_unpickle\_appattr, (reverse, self.\_\_reduce\_args\_\_())



&#x20;       attrs = dict(

&#x20;           {attribute: self},

&#x20;           \_\_module\_\_=Class.\_\_module\_\_,

&#x20;           \_\_doc\_\_=Class.\_\_doc\_\_,

&#x20;           \*\*kw)

&#x20;       if not keep\_reduce:

&#x20;           attrs\['\_\_reduce\_\_'] = \_\_reduce\_\_



&#x20;       return type(name or Class.\_\_name\_\_, (Class,), attrs)



&#x20;   def \_rgetattr(self, path):

&#x20;       return attrgetter(path)(self)



&#x20;   def \_\_enter\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return self



&#x20;   def \_\_exit\_\_(self, \*exc\_info):

&#x20;       self.close()



&#x20;   def \_\_repr\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return f'<{type(self).\_\_name\_\_} {appstr(self)}>'



&#x20;   def \_\_reduce\_\_(self):

&#x20;       if self.\_using\_v1\_reduce:

&#x20;           return self.\_\_reduce\_v1\_\_()

&#x20;       return (\_unpickle\_app\_v2, (self.\_\_class\_\_, self.\_\_reduce\_keys\_\_()))



&#x20;   def \_\_reduce\_v1\_\_(self):

&#x20;       # Reduce only pickles the configuration changes,

&#x20;       # so the default configuration doesn't have to be passed

&#x20;       # between processes.

&#x20;       return (

&#x20;           \_unpickle\_app,

&#x20;           (self.\_\_class\_\_, self.Pickler) + self.\_\_reduce\_args\_\_(),

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def \_\_reduce\_keys\_\_(self):

&#x20;       """Keyword arguments used to reconstruct the object when unpickling."""

&#x20;       return {

&#x20;           'main': self.main,

&#x20;           'changes':

&#x20;               self.\_conf.changes if self.configured else self.\_preconf,

&#x20;           'loader': self.loader\_cls,

&#x20;           'backend': self.backend\_cls,

&#x20;           'amqp': self.amqp\_cls,

&#x20;           'events': self.events\_cls,

&#x20;           'log': self.log\_cls,

&#x20;           'control': self.control\_cls,

&#x20;           'fixups': self.fixups,

&#x20;           'config\_source': self.\_config\_source,

&#x20;           'task\_cls': self.task\_cls,

&#x20;           'namespace': self.namespace,

&#x20;       }



&#x20;   def \_\_reduce\_args\_\_(self):

&#x20;       """Deprecated method, please use :meth:`\_\_reduce\_keys\_\_` instead."""

&#x20;       return (self.main, self.\_conf.changes if self.configured else {},

&#x20;               self.loader\_cls, self.backend\_cls, self.amqp\_cls,

&#x20;               self.events\_cls, self.log\_cls, self.control\_cls,

&#x20;               False, self.\_config\_source)



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def Worker(self):

&#x20;       See Also:

&#x20;           :class:`\~@Worker`.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.subclass\_with\_self('celery.apps.worker:Worker')

&#x20;       return self.subclass\_with\_self('celery.result:ResultSet')

&#x20;       See Also:

&#x20;           :class:`celery.result.GroupResult`.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.subclass\_with\_self('celery.result:GroupResult')



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def pool(self):

&#x20;       """Broker connection pool: :class:`\~@pool`.



&#x20;       Note:

&#x20;           This attribute is not related to the workers concurrency pool.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       if self.\_pool is None:

&#x20;           self.\_ensure\_after\_fork()

&#x20;           limit = self.conf.broker\_pool\_limit

&#x20;           pools.set\_limit(limit)

&#x20;           self.\_pool = pools.connections\[self.connection\_for\_write()]

&#x20;       return self.\_pool



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;       """Instance of task being executed, or :const:`None`."""

&#x20;       return \_task\_stack.top



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def current\_worker\_task(self):

&#x20;       Differs from :data:`current\_task` in that it's not affected

&#x20;       by tasks calling other tasks directly, or eagerly.

&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;       """Universally unique identifier for this app."""

&#x20;       # since 4.0: thread.get\_ident() is not included when

&#x20;       # generating the process id.  This is due to how the RPC

&#x20;       # backend now dedicates a single thread to receive results,

&#x20;       # which would not work if each thread has a separate id.

&#x20;       return oid\_from(self, threads=False)



&#x20;   @property
