```python

"""Built-in Tasks.



The built-in tasks are always available in all app instances.

"""

from celery.\_state import connect\_on\_app\_finalize

from celery.utils.log import get\_logger



\_\_all\_\_ = ()

logger = get\_logger(\_\_name\_\_)





@connect\_on\_app\_finalize

def add\_backend\_cleanup\_task(app):

&#x20;   """Task used to clean up expired results.



&#x20;   If the configured backend requires periodic cleanup this task is also

&#x20;   automatically configured to run every day at 4am (requires

&#x20;   :program:`celery beat` to be running).

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   @app.task(name='celery.backend\_cleanup', shared=False, lazy=False)

&#x20;   def backend\_cleanup():

&#x20;       app.backend.cleanup()

&#x20;   return backend\_cleanup





@connect\_on\_app\_finalize

def add\_accumulate\_task(app):

&#x20;   """Task used by Task.replace when replacing task with group."""

&#x20;   @app.task(bind=True, name='celery.accumulate', shared=False, lazy=False)

&#x20;   def accumulate(self, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       index = kwargs.get('index')

&#x20;       return args\[index] if index is not None else args

&#x20;   return accumulate





@connect\_on\_app\_finalize

def add\_unlock\_chord\_task(app):

&#x20;   """Task used by result backends without native chord support.



&#x20;   Will joins chord by creating a task chain polling the header

&#x20;   for completion.

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   from celery.backends.base import \_create\_chord\_error\_with\_cause

&#x20;   from celery.canvas import maybe\_signature

&#x20;   from celery.result import allow\_join\_result, result\_from\_tuple



&#x20;   @app.task(name='celery.chord\_unlock', max\_retries=None, shared=False,

&#x20;             default\_retry\_delay=app.conf.result\_chord\_retry\_interval, ignore\_result=True, lazy=False, bind=True)

&#x20;   def unlock\_chord(self, group\_id, callback, interval=None,

&#x20;                    max\_retries=None, result=None,

&#x20;                    Result=app.AsyncResult, GroupResult=app.GroupResult,

&#x20;                    result\_from\_tuple=result\_from\_tuple, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       if interval is None:

&#x20;           interval = self.default\_retry\_delay



&#x20;       # check if the task group is ready, and if so apply the callback.

&#x20;       callback = maybe\_signature(callback, app)

&#x20;       deps = GroupResult(

&#x20;           group\_id,

&#x20;           \[result\_from\_tuple(r, app=app) for r in result],

&#x20;           app=app,

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       j = deps.join\_native if deps.supports\_native\_join else deps.join



&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           ready = deps.ready()

&#x20;       except Exception as exc:

&#x20;           raise self.retry(

&#x20;               exc=exc, countdown=interval, max\_retries=max\_retries,

&#x20;           )

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           if not ready:

&#x20;               raise self.retry(countdown=interval, max\_retries=max\_retries)



&#x20;       callback = maybe\_signature(callback, app=app)

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           with allow\_join\_result():

&#x20;               ret = j(

&#x20;                   timeout=app.conf.result\_chord\_join\_timeout,

&#x20;                   propagate=True,

&#x20;               )

&#x20;       except Exception as exc:  # pylint: disable=broad-except

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               culprit = next(deps.\_failed\_join\_report())

&#x20;               reason = f'Dependency {culprit.id} raised {exc!r}'

&#x20;           except StopIteration:

&#x20;               reason = repr(exc)

&#x20;           logger.exception('Chord %r raised: %r', group\_id, exc)

&#x20;           chord\_error = \_create\_chord\_error\_with\_cause(message=reason, original\_exc=exc)

&#x20;           app.backend.chord\_error\_from\_stack(callback=callback, exc=chord\_error)

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               callback.delay(ret)

&#x20;           except Exception as exc:  # pylint: disable=broad-except

&#x20;               logger.exception('Chord %r raised: %r', group\_id, exc)

&#x20;               chord\_error = \_create\_chord\_error\_with\_cause(message=f'Callback error: {exc!r}', original\_exc=exc)

&#x20;               app.backend.chord\_error\_from\_stack(callback=callback, exc=chord\_error)

&#x20;   return unlock\_chord





@connect\_on\_app\_finalize

def add\_map\_task(app):

&#x20;   from celery.canvas import signature



&#x20;   @app.task(name='celery.map', shared=False, lazy=False)

&#x20;   def xmap(task, it):

&#x20;       task = signature(task, app=app).type

&#x20;       return \[task(item) for item in it]

&#x20;   return xmap





@connect\_on\_app\_finalize

def add\_starmap\_task(app):

&#x20;   from celery.canvas import signature



&#x20;   @app.task(name='celery.starmap', shared=False, lazy=False)

&#x20;   def xstarmap(task, it):

&#x20;       task = signature(task, app=app).type

&#x20;       return \[task(\*item) for item in it]

&#x20;   return xstarmap





@connect\_on\_app\_finalize

def add\_chunk\_task(app):

&#x20;   from celery.canvas import chunks as \_chunks



&#x20;   @app.task(name='celery.chunks', shared=False, lazy=False)

&#x20;   def chunks(task, it, n):

&#x20;       return \_chunks.apply\_chunks(task, it, n)

&#x20;   return chunks





@connect\_on\_app\_finalize

def add\_group\_task(app):

&#x20;   """No longer used, but here for backwards compatibility."""

&#x20;   from celery.canvas import maybe\_signature

&#x20;   from celery.result import result\_from\_tuple



&#x20;   @app.task(name='celery.group', bind=True, shared=False, lazy=False)

&#x20;   def group(self, tasks, result, group\_id, partial\_args, add\_to\_parent=True):

&#x20;       app = self.app

&#x20;       result = result\_from\_tuple(result, app)

&#x20;       # any partial args are added to all tasks in the group

&#x20;       taskit = (maybe\_signature(task, app=app).clone(partial\_args)

&#x20;                 for i, task in enumerate(tasks))

&#x20;       with app.producer\_or\_acquire() as producer:

&#x20;           \[stask.apply\_async(group\_id=group\_id, producer=producer,

&#x20;                              add\_to\_parent=False) for stask in taskit]

&#x20;       parent = app.current\_worker\_task

&#x20;       if add\_to\_parent and parent:

&#x20;           parent.add\_trail(result)

&#x20;       return result

&#x20;   return group





@connect\_on\_app\_finalize

def add\_chain\_task(app):

&#x20;   """No longer used, but here for backwards compatibility."""

&#x20;   @app.task(name='celery.chain', shared=False, lazy=False)

&#x20;   def chain(\*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       raise NotImplementedError('chain is not a real task')

&#x20;   return chain





@connect\_on\_app\_finalize

def add\_chord\_task(app):

&#x20;   """No longer used, but here for backwards compatibility."""

&#x20;   from celery import chord as \_chord

&#x20;   from celery import group

&#x20;   from celery.canvas import maybe\_signature



&#x20;   @app.task(name='celery.chord', bind=True, ignore\_result=False,

&#x20;             shared=False, lazy=False)

&#x20;   def chord(self, header, body, partial\_args=(), interval=None,

&#x20;             countdown=1, max\_retries=None, eager=False, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       app = self.app

&#x20;       # - convert back to group if serialized

&#x20;       tasks = header.tasks if isinstance(header, group) else header

&#x20;       header = group(\[

&#x20;           maybe\_signature(s, app=app) for s in tasks

&#x20;       ], app=self.app)

&#x20;       body = maybe\_signature(body, app=app)

&#x20;       ch = \_chord(header, body)

&#x20;       return ch.run(header, body, partial\_args, app, interval,

&#x20;                     countdown, max\_retries, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;   return chord



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\app\\control.py

```python

"""Worker Remote Control Client.



Client for worker remote control commands.

Server implementation is in :mod:`celery.worker.control`.

There are two types of remote control commands:



\* Inspect commands: Does not have side effects, will usually just return some value

&#x20; found in the worker, like the list of currently registered tasks, the list of active tasks, etc.

&#x20; Commands are accessible via :class:`Inspect` class.



\* Control commands: Performs side effects, like adding a new queue to consume from.

&#x20; Commands are accessible via :class:`Control` class.

"""

import warnings



from billiard.common import TERM\_SIGNAME

from kombu.matcher import match

from kombu.pidbox import Mailbox

from kombu.utils.compat import register\_after\_fork

from kombu.utils.functional import lazy

from kombu.utils.objects import cached\_property



from celery.exceptions import DuplicateNodenameWarning, ImproperlyConfigured

from celery.utils.log import get\_logger

from celery.utils.text import pluralize



\_\_all\_\_ = ('Inspect', 'Control', 'flatten\_reply')



logger = get\_logger(\_\_name\_\_)



W\_DUPNODE = """\\

Received multiple replies from node {0}: {1}.

Please make sure you give each node a unique nodename using

the celery worker `-n` option.\\

"""





def flatten\_reply(reply):

&#x20;   """Flatten node replies.



&#x20;   Convert from a list of replies in this format::



&#x20;       \[{'a@example.com': reply},

&#x20;        {'b@example.com': reply}]



&#x20;   into this format::



&#x20;       {'a@example.com': reply,

&#x20;        'b@example.com': reply}

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   nodes, dupes = {}, set()

&#x20;   for item in reply:

&#x20;       \[dupes.add(name) for name in item if name in nodes]

&#x20;       nodes.update(item)

&#x20;   if dupes:

&#x20;       warnings.warn(DuplicateNodenameWarning(

&#x20;           W\_DUPNODE.format(

&#x20;               pluralize(len(dupes), 'name'), ', '.join(sorted(dupes)),

&#x20;           ),

&#x20;       ))

&#x20;   return nodes





def \_after\_fork\_cleanup\_control(control):

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       control.\_after\_fork()

&#x20;   except Exception as exc:  # pylint: disable=broad-except

&#x20;       logger.info('after fork raised exception: %r', exc, exc\_info=1)





class Inspect:

&#x20;   """API for inspecting workers.



&#x20;   This class provides proxy for accessing Inspect API of workers. The API is

&#x20;   defined in :py:mod:`celery.worker.control`

&#x20;   """



&#x20;   app = None



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, destination=None, timeout=1.0, callback=None,

&#x20;                connection=None, app=None, limit=None, pattern=None,

&#x20;                matcher=None):

&#x20;       self.app = app or self.app

&#x20;       self.destination = destination

&#x20;       self.timeout = timeout

&#x20;       self.callback = callback

&#x20;       self.connection = connection

&#x20;       self.limit = limit

&#x20;       self.pattern = pattern

&#x20;       self.matcher = matcher



&#x20;   def \_prepare(self, reply):

&#x20;       if reply:

&#x20;           by\_node = flatten\_reply(reply)

&#x20;           if (self.destination and

&#x20;                   not isinstance(self.destination, (list, tuple))):

&#x20;               return by\_node.get(self.destination)

&#x20;           if self.pattern:

&#x20;               pattern = self.pattern

&#x20;               matcher = self.matcher

&#x20;               return {node: reply for node, reply in by\_node.items()

&#x20;                       if match(node, pattern, matcher)}

&#x20;           return by\_node



&#x20;   def \_request(self, command, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       return self.\_prepare(self.app.control.broadcast(

&#x20;           command,

&#x20;           arguments=kwargs,

&#x20;           destination=self.destination,

&#x20;           callback=self.callback,

&#x20;           connection=self.connection,

&#x20;           limit=self.limit,

&#x20;           timeout=self.timeout, reply=True,

&#x20;           pattern=self.pattern, matcher=self.matcher,

&#x20;       ))



&#x20;   def report(self):

&#x20;       """Return human readable report for each worker.



&#x20;       Returns:

&#x20;           Dict: Dictionary ``{HOSTNAME: {'ok': REPORT\_STRING}}``.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.\_request('report')



&#x20;   def clock(self):

&#x20;       """Get the Clock value on workers.



&#x20;       >>> app.control.inspect().clock()

&#x20;       {'celery@node1': {'clock': 12}}



&#x20;       Returns:

&#x20;           Dict: Dictionary ``{HOSTNAME: CLOCK\_VALUE}``.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.\_request('clock')



&#x20;   def active(self, safe=None):

&#x20;       """Return list of tasks currently executed by workers.



&#x20;       Arguments:

&#x20;           safe (Boolean): Set to True to disable deserialization.



&#x20;       Returns:

&#x20;           Dict: Dictionary ``{HOSTNAME: \[TASK\_INFO,...]}``.



&#x20;       See Also:

&#x20;           For ``TASK\_INFO`` details see :func:`query\_task` return value.



&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.\_request('active', safe=safe)



&#x20;   def scheduled(self, safe=None):

&#x20;       """Return list of scheduled tasks with details.



&#x20;       Returns:

&#x20;           Dict: Dictionary ``{HOSTNAME: \[TASK\_SCHEDULED\_INFO,...]}``.



&#x20;       Here is the list of ``TASK\_SCHEDULED\_INFO`` fields:



&#x20;       \* ``eta`` - scheduled time for task execution as string in ISO 8601 format

&#x20;       \* ``priority`` - priority of the task

&#x20;       \* ``request`` - field containing ``TASK\_INFO`` value.



&#x20;       See Also:

&#x20;           For more details about ``TASK\_INFO``  see :func:`query\_task` return value.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.\_request('scheduled')



&#x20;   def reserved(self, safe=None):

&#x20;       """Return list of currently reserved tasks, not including scheduled/active.



&#x20;       Returns:

&#x20;           Dict: Dictionary ``{HOSTNAME: \[TASK\_INFO,...]}``.



&#x20;       See Also:

&#x20;           For ``TASK\_INFO`` details see :func:`query\_task` return value.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.\_request('reserved')



&#x20;   def stats(self):

&#x20;       """Return statistics of worker.



&#x20;       Returns:

&#x20;           Dict: Dictionary ``{HOSTNAME: STAT\_INFO}``.



&#x20;       Here is the list of ``STAT\_INFO`` fields:



&#x20;       \* ``broker`` - Section for broker information.

&#x20;           \* ``connect\_timeout`` - Timeout in seconds (int/float) for establishing a new connection.

&#x20;           \* ``heartbeat`` - Current heartbeat value (set by client).

&#x20;           \* ``hostname`` - Node name of the remote broker.

&#x20;           \* ``insist`` - No longer used.

&#x20;           \* ``login\_method`` - Login method used to connect to the broker.

&#x20;           \* ``port`` - Port of the remote broker.

&#x20;           \* ``ssl`` - SSL enabled/disabled.

&#x20;           \* ``transport`` - Name of transport used (e.g., amqp or redis)

&#x20;           \* ``transport\_options`` - Options passed to transport.

&#x20;           \* ``uri\_prefix`` - Some transports expects the host name to be a URL.

&#x20;             E.g. ``redis+socket:///tmp/redis.sock``.

&#x20;             In this example the URI-prefix will be redis.

&#x20;           \* ``userid`` - User id used to connect to the broker with.

&#x20;           \* ``virtual\_host`` - Virtual host used.

&#x20;       \* ``clock`` - Value of the workers logical clock. This is a positive integer

&#x20;         and should be increasing every time you receive statistics.

&#x20;       \* ``uptime`` - Numbers of seconds since the worker controller was started

&#x20;       \* ``pid`` - Process id of the worker instance (Main process).

&#x20;       \* ``pool`` - Pool-specific section.

&#x20;           \* ``max-concurrency`` - Max number of processes/threads/green threads.

&#x20;           \* ``max-tasks-per-child`` - Max number of tasks a thread may execute before being recycled.

&#x20;           \* ``processes`` - List of PIDs (or thread-id’s).

&#x20;           \* ``put-guarded-by-semaphore`` - Internal

&#x20;           \* ``timeouts`` - Default values for time limits.

&#x20;           \* ``writes`` - Specific to the prefork pool, this shows the distribution

&#x20;             of writes to each process in the pool when using async I/O.

&#x20;       \* ``prefetch\_count`` - Current prefetch count value for the task consumer.

&#x20;       \* ``rusage`` - System usage statistics. The fields available may be different on your platform.

&#x20;         From :manpage:`getrusage(2)`:



&#x20;           \* ``stime`` - Time spent in operating system code on behalf of this process.

&#x20;           \* ``utime`` - Time spent executing user instructions.

&#x20;           \* ``maxrss`` - The maximum resident size used by this process (in kilobytes).

&#x20;           \* ``idrss`` - Amount of non-shared memory used for data (in kilobytes times

&#x20;             ticks of execution)

&#x20;           \* ``isrss`` - Amount of non-shared memory used for stack space

&#x20;             (in kilobytes times ticks of execution)

&#x20;           \* ``ixrss`` - Amount of memory shared with other processes

&#x20;             (in kilobytes times ticks of execution).

&#x20;           \* ``inblock`` - Number of times the file system had to read from the disk

&#x20;             on behalf of this process.

&#x20;           \* ``oublock`` - Number of times the file system has to write to disk

&#x20;             on behalf of this process.

&#x20;           \* ``majflt`` - Number of page faults that were serviced by doing I/O.

&#x20;           \* ``minflt`` - Number of page faults that were serviced without doing I/O.

&#x20;           \* ``msgrcv`` - Number of IPC messages received.

&#x20;           \* ``msgsnd`` - Number of IPC messages sent.

&#x20;           \* ``nvcsw`` - Number of times this process voluntarily invoked a context switch.

&#x20;           \* ``nivcsw`` - Number of times an involuntary context switch took place.

&#x20;           \* ``nsignals`` - Number of signals received.

&#x20;           \* ``nswap`` - The number of times this process was swapped entirely

&#x20;             out of memory.

&#x20;       \* ``total`` - Map of task names and the total number of tasks with that type

&#x20;         the worker has accepted since start-up.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.\_request('stats')



&#x20;   def revoked(self):

&#x20;       """Return list of revoked tasks.



&#x20;       >>> app.control.inspect().revoked()

&#x20;       {'celery@node1': \['16f527de-1c72-47a6-b477-c472b92fef7a']}



&#x20;       Returns:

&#x20;           Dict: Dictionary ``{HOSTNAME: \[TASK\_ID, ...]}``.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.\_request('revoked')



&#x20;   def registered(self, \*taskinfoitems):

&#x20;       """Return all registered tasks per worker.



&#x20;       >>> app.control.inspect().registered()

&#x20;       {'celery@node1': \['task1', 'task1']}

&#x20;       >>> app.control.inspect().registered('serializer', 'max\_retries')

&#x20;       {'celery@node1': \['task\_foo \[serializer=json max\_retries=3]', 'tasb\_bar \[serializer=json max\_retries=3]']}



&#x20;       Arguments:

&#x20;           taskinfoitems (Sequence\[str]): List of :class:`\~celery.app.task.Task`

&#x20;                                          attributes to include.



&#x20;       Returns:

&#x20;           Dict: Dictionary ``{HOSTNAME: \[TASK1\_INFO, ...]}``.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.\_request('registered', taskinfoitems=taskinfoitems)

&#x20;   registered\_tasks = registered



&#x20;   def ping(self, destination=None):

&#x20;       """Ping all (or specific) workers.



&#x20;       >>> app.control.inspect().ping()

&#x20;       {'celery@node1': {'ok': 'pong'}, 'celery@node2': {'ok': 'pong'}}

&#x20;       >>> app.control.inspect().ping(destination=\['celery@node1'])

&#x20;       {'celery@node1': {'ok': 'pong'}}



&#x20;       Arguments:

&#x20;           destination (List): If set, a list of the hosts to send the

&#x20;               command to, when empty broadcast to all workers.



&#x20;       Returns:

&#x20;           Dict: Dictionary ``{HOSTNAME: {'ok': 'pong'}}``.



&#x20;       See Also:

&#x20;           :meth:`broadcast` for supported keyword arguments.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       if destination:

&#x20;           self.destination = destination

&#x20;       return self.\_request('ping')



&#x20;   def active\_queues(self):

&#x20;       """Return information about queues from which worker consumes tasks.



&#x20;       Returns:

&#x20;           Dict: Dictionary ``{HOSTNAME: \[QUEUE\_INFO, QUEUE\_INFO,...]}``.



&#x20;       Here is the list of ``QUEUE\_INFO`` fields:



&#x20;       \* ``name``

&#x20;       \* ``exchange``

&#x20;           \* ``name``

&#x20;           \* ``type``

&#x20;           \* ``arguments``

&#x20;           \* ``durable``

&#x20;           \* ``passive``

&#x20;           \* ``auto\_delete``

&#x20;           \* ``delivery\_mode``

&#x20;           \* ``no\_declare``

&#x20;       \* ``routing\_key``

&#x20;       \* ``queue\_arguments``

&#x20;       \* ``binding\_arguments``

&#x20;       \* ``consumer\_arguments``

&#x20;       \* ``durable``

&#x20;       \* ``exclusive``

&#x20;       \* ``auto\_delete``

&#x20;       \* ``no\_ack``

&#x20;       \* ``alias``

&#x20;       \* ``bindings``

&#x20;       \* ``no\_declare``

&#x20;       \* ``expires``

&#x20;       \* ``message\_ttl``

&#x20;       \* ``max\_length``

&#x20;       \* ``max\_length\_bytes``

&#x20;       \* ``max\_priority``



&#x20;       See Also:

&#x20;           See the RabbitMQ/AMQP documentation for more details about

&#x20;           ``queue\_info`` fields.

&#x20;       Note:

&#x20;           The ``queue\_info`` fields are RabbitMQ/AMQP oriented.

&#x20;           Not all fields applies for other transports.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.\_request('active\_queues')



&#x20;   def query\_task(self, \*ids):

&#x20;       """Return detail of tasks currently executed by workers.



&#x20;       Arguments:

&#x20;           \*ids (str): IDs of tasks to be queried.



&#x20;       Returns:

&#x20;           Dict: Dictionary ``{HOSTNAME: {TASK\_ID: \[STATE, TASK\_INFO]}}``.



&#x20;       Here is the list of ``TASK\_INFO`` fields:

&#x20;           \* ``id`` - ID of the task

&#x20;           \* ``name`` - Name of the task

&#x20;           \* ``args`` - Positinal arguments passed to the task

&#x20;           \* ``kwargs`` - Keyword arguments passed to the task

&#x20;           \* ``type`` - Type of the task

&#x20;           \* ``hostname`` - Hostname of the worker processing the task

&#x20;           \* ``time\_start`` - Time of processing start

&#x20;           \* ``acknowledged`` - True when task was acknowledged to broker

&#x20;           \* ``delivery\_info`` - Dictionary containing delivery information

&#x20;               \* ``exchange`` - Name of exchange where task was published

&#x20;               \* ``routing\_key`` - Routing key used when task was published

&#x20;               \* ``priority`` - Priority used when task was published

&#x20;               \* ``redelivered`` - True if the task was redelivered

&#x20;           \* ``worker\_pid`` - PID of worker processing the task



&#x20;       """

&#x20;       # signature used be unary: query\_task(ids=\[id1, id2])

&#x20;       # we need this to preserve backward compatibility.

&#x20;       if len(ids) == 1 and isinstance(ids\[0], (list, tuple)):

&#x20;           ids = ids\[0]

&#x20;       return self.\_request('query\_task', ids=ids)



&#x20;   def conf(self, with\_defaults=False):

&#x20;       """Return configuration of each worker.



&#x20;       Arguments:

&#x20;           with\_defaults (bool): if set to True, method returns also

&#x20;                                  configuration options with default values.



&#x20;       Returns:

&#x20;           Dict: Dictionary ``{HOSTNAME: WORKER\_CONFIGURATION}``.



&#x20;       See Also:

&#x20;           ``WORKER\_CONFIGURATION`` is a dictionary containing current configuration options.

&#x20;           See :ref:`configuration` for possible values.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.\_request('conf', with\_defaults=with\_defaults)



&#x20;   def hello(self, from\_node, revoked=None):

&#x20;       return self.\_request('hello', from\_node=from\_node, revoked=revoked)



&#x20;   def memsample(self):

&#x20;       """Return sample current RSS memory usage.



&#x20;       Note:

&#x20;           Requires the psutils library.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.\_request('memsample')



&#x20;   def memdump(self, samples=10):

&#x20;       """Dump statistics of previous memsample requests.



&#x20;       Note:

&#x20;           Requires the psutils library.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.\_request('memdump', samples=samples)



&#x20;   def objgraph(self, type='Request', n=200, max\_depth=10):

&#x20;       """Create graph of uncollected objects (memory-leak debugging).



&#x20;       Arguments:

&#x20;           n (int): Max number of objects to graph.

&#x20;           max\_depth (int): Traverse at most n levels deep.

&#x20;           type (str): Name of object to graph.  Default is ``"Request"``.



&#x20;       Returns:

&#x20;           Dict: Dictionary ``{'filename': FILENAME}``



&#x20;       Note:

&#x20;           Requires the objgraph library.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.\_request('objgraph', num=n, max\_depth=max\_depth, type=type)





class Control:

&#x20;   """Worker remote control client."""



&#x20;   Mailbox = Mailbox



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, app=None):

&#x20;       self.app = app

&#x20;       if (app.conf.control\_queue\_durable and

&#x20;               app.conf.control\_queue\_exclusive):

&#x20;           raise ImproperlyConfigured(

&#x20;               "control\_queue\_durable and control\_queue\_exclusive cannot both be True "

&#x20;               "(exclusive queues are automatically deleted and cannot be durable).",

&#x20;           )

&#x20;       self.mailbox = self.Mailbox(

&#x20;           app.conf.control\_exchange,

&#x20;           type='fanout',

&#x20;           accept=app.conf.accept\_content,

&#x20;           serializer=app.conf.task\_serializer,

&#x20;           producer\_pool=lazy(lambda: self.app.amqp.producer\_pool),

&#x20;           queue\_ttl=app.conf.control\_queue\_ttl,

&#x20;           reply\_queue\_ttl=app.conf.control\_queue\_ttl,

&#x20;           queue\_expires=app.conf.control\_queue\_expires,

&#x20;           queue\_exclusive=app.conf.control\_queue\_exclusive,

&#x20;           queue\_durable=app.conf.control\_queue\_durable,

&#x20;           reply\_queue\_expires=app.conf.control\_queue\_expires,

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       register\_after\_fork(self, \_after\_fork\_cleanup\_control)



&#x20;   def \_after\_fork(self):

&#x20;       del self.mailbox.producer\_pool



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def inspect(self):

&#x20;       """Create new :class:`Inspect` instance."""

&#x20;       return self.app.subclass\_with\_self(Inspect, reverse='control.inspect')



&#x20;   def purge(self, connection=None):

&#x20;       """Discard all waiting tasks.



&#x20;       This will ignore all tasks waiting for execution, and they will

&#x20;       be deleted from the messaging server.



&#x20;       Arguments:

&#x20;           connection (kombu.Connection): Optional specific connection

&#x20;               instance to use.  If not provided a connection will

&#x20;               be acquired from the connection pool.



&#x20;       Returns:

&#x20;           int: the number of tasks discarded.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       with self.app.connection\_or\_acquire(connection) as conn:

&#x20;           return self.app.amqp.TaskConsumer(conn).purge()

&#x20;   discard\_all = purge



&#x20;   def election(self, id, topic, action=None, connection=None):

&#x20;       self.broadcast(

&#x20;           'election', connection=connection, destination=None,

&#x20;           arguments={

&#x20;               'id': id, 'topic': topic, 'action': action,

&#x20;           },

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def revoke(self, task\_id, destination=None, terminate=False,

&#x20;              signal=TERM\_SIGNAME, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       """Tell all (or specific) workers to revoke a task by id (or list of ids).



&#x20;       If a task is revoked, the workers will ignore the task and

&#x20;       not execute it after all.



&#x20;       Arguments:

&#x20;           task\_id (Union(str, list)): Id of the task to revoke

&#x20;               (or list of ids).

&#x20;           terminate (bool): Also terminate the process currently working

&#x20;               on the task (if any).

&#x20;           signal (str): Name of signal to send to process if terminate.

&#x20;               Default is TERM.



&#x20;       See Also:

&#x20;           :meth:`broadcast` for supported keyword arguments.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.broadcast('revoke', destination=destination, arguments={

&#x20;           'task\_id': task\_id,

&#x20;           'terminate': terminate,

&#x20;           'signal': signal,

&#x20;       }, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def revoke\_by\_stamped\_headers(self, headers, destination=None, terminate=False,

&#x20;                                 signal=TERM\_SIGNAME, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       Tell all (or specific) workers to revoke a task by headers.



&#x20;       If a task is revoked, the workers will ignore the task and

&#x20;       not execute it after all.



&#x20;       Arguments:

&#x20;           headers (dict\[str, Union(str, list)]): Headers to match when revoking tasks.

&#x20;           terminate (bool): Also terminate the process currently working

&#x20;               on the task (if any).

&#x20;           signal (str): Name of signal to send to process if terminate.

&#x20;               Default is TERM.



&#x20;       See Also:

&#x20;           :meth:`broadcast` for supported keyword arguments.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       result = self.broadcast('revoke\_by\_stamped\_headers', destination=destination, arguments={

&#x20;           'headers': headers,

&#x20;           'terminate': terminate,

&#x20;           'signal': signal,

&#x20;       }, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;       task\_ids = set()

&#x20;       if result:

&#x20;           for host in result:

&#x20;               for response in host.values():

&#x20;                   if isinstance(response\['ok'], set):

&#x20;                       task\_ids.update(response\['ok'])



&#x20;       if task\_ids:

&#x20;           return self.revoke(list(task\_ids), destination=destination, terminate=terminate, signal=signal, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           return result



&#x20;   def terminate(self, task\_id,

&#x20;                 destination=None, signal=TERM\_SIGNAME, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       """Tell all (or specific) workers to terminate a task by id (or list of ids).



&#x20;       See Also:

&#x20;           This is just a shortcut to :meth:`revoke` with the terminate

&#x20;           argument enabled.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.revoke(

&#x20;           task\_id,

&#x20;           destination=destination, terminate=True, signal=signal, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def ping(self, destination=None, timeout=1.0, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       """Ping all (or specific) workers.



&#x20;       >>> app.control.ping()

&#x20;       \[{'celery@node1': {'ok': 'pong'}}, {'celery@node2': {'ok': 'pong'}}]

&#x20;       >>> app.control.ping(destination=\['celery@node2'])

&#x20;       \[{'celery@node2': {'ok': 'pong'}}]



&#x20;       Returns:

&#x20;           List\[Dict]: List of ``{HOSTNAME: {'ok': 'pong'}}`` dictionaries.



&#x20;       See Also:

&#x20;           :meth:`broadcast` for supported keyword arguments.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.broadcast(

&#x20;           'ping', reply=True, arguments={}, destination=destination,

&#x20;           timeout=timeout, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def rate\_limit(self, task\_name, rate\_limit, destination=None, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       """Tell workers to set a new rate limit for task by type.



&#x20;       Arguments:

&#x20;           task\_name (str): Name of task to change rate limit for.

&#x20;           rate\_limit (int, str): The rate limit as tasks per second,

&#x20;               or a rate limit string (`'100/m'`, etc.

&#x20;               see :attr:`celery.app.task.Task.rate\_limit` for

&#x20;               more information).



&#x20;       See Also:

&#x20;           :meth:`broadcast` for supported keyword arguments.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.broadcast(

&#x20;           'rate\_limit',

&#x20;           destination=destination,

&#x20;           arguments={

&#x20;               'task\_name': task\_name,

&#x20;               'rate\_limit': rate\_limit,

&#x20;           },

&#x20;           \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def add\_consumer(self, queue,

&#x20;                    exchange=None, exchange\_type='direct', routing\_key=None,

&#x20;                    options=None, destination=None, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       """Tell all (or specific) workers to start consuming from a new queue.



&#x20;       Only the queue name is required as if only the queue is specified

&#x20;       then the exchange/routing key will be set to the same name (

&#x20;       like automatic queues do).



&#x20;       Note:

&#x20;           This command does not respect the default queue/exchange

&#x20;           options in the configuration.



&#x20;       Arguments:

&#x20;           queue (str): Name of queue to start consuming from.

&#x20;           exchange (str): Optional name of exchange.

&#x20;           exchange\_type (str): Type of exchange (defaults to 'direct')

&#x20;               command to, when empty broadcast to all workers.

&#x20;           routing\_key (str): Optional routing key.

&#x20;           options (Dict): Additional options as supported

&#x20;               by :meth:`kombu.entity.Queue.from\_dict`.



&#x20;       See Also:

&#x20;           :meth:`broadcast` for supported keyword arguments.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.broadcast(

&#x20;           'add\_consumer',

&#x20;           destination=destination,

&#x20;           arguments=dict({

&#x20;               'queue': queue,

&#x20;               'exchange': exchange,

&#x20;               'exchange\_type': exchange\_type,

&#x20;               'routing\_key': routing\_key,

&#x20;           }, \*\*options or {}),

&#x20;           \*\*kwargs

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def cancel\_consumer(self, queue, destination=None, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       """Tell all (or specific) workers to stop consuming from ``queue``.



&#x20;       See Also:

&#x20;           Supports the same arguments as :meth:`broadcast`.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.broadcast(

&#x20;           'cancel\_consumer', destination=destination,

&#x20;           arguments={'queue': queue}, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def time\_limit(self, task\_name, soft=None, hard=None,

&#x20;                  destination=None, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       """Tell workers to set time limits for a task by type.



&#x20;       Arguments:

&#x20;           task\_name (str): Name of task to change time limits for.

&#x20;           soft (float): New soft time limit (in seconds).

&#x20;           hard (float): New hard time limit (in seconds).

&#x20;           \*\*kwargs (Any): arguments passed on to :meth:`broadcast`.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.broadcast(

&#x20;           'time\_limit',

&#x20;           arguments={

&#x20;               'task\_name': task\_name,

&#x20;               'hard': hard,

&#x20;               'soft': soft,

&#x20;           },

&#x20;           destination=destination,

&#x20;           \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def enable\_events(self, destination=None, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       """Tell all (or specific) workers to enable events.



&#x20;       See Also:

&#x20;           Supports the same arguments as :meth:`broadcast`.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.broadcast(

&#x20;           'enable\_events', arguments={}, destination=destination, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def disable\_events(self, destination=None, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       """Tell all (or specific) workers to disable events.



&#x20;       See Also:

&#x20;           Supports the same arguments as :meth:`broadcast`.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.broadcast(

&#x20;           'disable\_events', arguments={}, destination=destination, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def pool\_grow(self, n=1, destination=None, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       """Tell all (or specific) workers to grow the pool by ``n``.



&#x20;       See Also:

&#x20;           Supports the same arguments as :meth:`broadcast`.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.broadcast(

&#x20;           'pool\_grow', arguments={'n': n}, destination=destination, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def pool\_shrink(self, n=1, destination=None, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       """Tell all (or specific) workers to shrink the pool by ``n``.



&#x20;       See Also:

&#x20;           Supports the same arguments as :meth:`broadcast`.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.broadcast(

&#x20;           'pool\_shrink', arguments={'n': n},

&#x20;           destination=destination, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def autoscale(self, max, min, destination=None, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       """Change worker(s) autoscale setting.



&#x20;       See Also:

&#x20;           Supports the same arguments as :meth:`broadcast`.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.broadcast(

&#x20;           'autoscale', arguments={'max': max, 'min': min},

&#x20;           destination=destination, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def shutdown(self, destination=None, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       """Shutdown worker(s).



&#x20;       See Also:

&#x20;           Supports the same arguments as :meth:`broadcast`

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.broadcast(

&#x20;           'shutdown', arguments={}, destination=destination, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def pool\_restart(self, modules=None, reload=False, reloader=None,

&#x20;                    destination=None, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       """Restart the execution pools of all or specific workers.



&#x20;       Keyword Arguments:

&#x20;           modules (Sequence\[str]): List of modules to reload.

&#x20;           reload (bool): Flag to enable module reloading.  Default is False.

&#x20;           reloader (Any): Function to reload a module.

&#x20;           destination (Sequence\[str]): List of worker names to send this

&#x20;               command to.



&#x20;       See Also:

&#x20;           Supports the same arguments as :meth:`broadcast`

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.broadcast(

&#x20;           'pool\_restart',

&#x20;           arguments={

&#x20;               'modules': modules,

&#x20;               'reload': reload,

&#x20;               'reloader': reloader,

&#x20;           },

&#x20;           destination=destination, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def heartbeat(self, destination=None, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       """Tell worker(s) to send a heartbeat immediately.



&#x20;       See Also:

&#x20;           Supports the same arguments as :meth:`broadcast`

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self.broadcast(

&#x20;           'heartbeat', arguments={}, destination=destination, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def broadcast(self, command, arguments=None, destination=None,

&#x20;                 connection=None, reply=False, timeout=1.0, limit=None,

&#x20;                 callback=None, channel=None, pattern=None, matcher=None,

&#x20;                 \*\*extra\_kwargs):

&#x20;       """Broadcast a control command to the celery workers.



&#x20;       Arguments:

&#x20;           command (str): Name of command to send.

&#x20;           arguments (Dict): Keyword arguments for the command.

&#x20;           destination (List): If set, a list of the hosts to send the

&#x20;               command to, when empty broadcast to all workers.

&#x20;           connection (kombu.Connection): Custom broker connection to use,

&#x20;               if not set, a connection will be acquired from the pool.

&#x20;           reply (bool): Wait for and return the reply.

&#x20;           timeout (float): Timeout in seconds to wait for the reply.

&#x20;           limit (int): Limit number of replies.

&#x20;           callback (Callable): Callback called immediately for

&#x20;               each reply received.

&#x20;           pattern (str): Custom pattern string to match

&#x20;           matcher (Callable): Custom matcher to run the pattern to match

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       with self.app.connection\_or\_acquire(connection) as conn:

&#x20;           arguments = dict(arguments or {}, \*\*extra\_kwargs)

&#x20;           if pattern and matcher:

&#x20;               # tests pass easier without requiring pattern/matcher to

&#x20;               # always be sent in

&#x20;               return self.mailbox(conn).\_broadcast(

&#x20;                   command, arguments, destination, reply, timeout,

&#x20;                   limit, callback, channel=channel,

&#x20;                   pattern=pattern, matcher=matcher,

&#x20;               )

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               return self.mailbox(conn).\_broadcast(

&#x20;                   command, arguments, destination, reply, timeout,

&#x20;                   limit, callback, channel=channel,

&#x20;               )



```

def Namespace(\_\_old\_\_=None, \*\*options):

&#x20;   if \_\_old\_\_ is not None:

&#x20;       for key, opt in options.items():

&#x20;           if not opt.old:

&#x20;               opt.old = {o.format(key) for o in \_\_old\_\_}

&#x20;   ),

)





def \_flatten\_keys(ns, key, opt):

&#x20;   return \[(ns + key, opt)]





def \_to\_compat(ns, key, opt):

&#x20;   if opt.old:

&#x20;       return \[

&#x20;           (oldkey.format(key).upper(), ns + key, opt)

&#x20;           for oldkey in opt.old

&#x20;       ]

&#x20;   return \[((ns + key).upper(), ns + key, opt)]





def flatten(d, root='', keyfilter=\_flatten\_keys):

&#x20;   """Flatten settings."""

&#x20;   stack = deque(\[(root, d)])

&#x20;   while stack:

&#x20;       ns, options = stack.popleft()

&#x20;       for key, opt in options.items():

&#x20;           if isinstance(opt, dict):

&#x20;               stack.append((ns + key + '\_', opt))

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               yield from keyfilter(ns, key, opt)





DEFAULTS = {

&#x20;   key: opt.default for key, opt in flatten(NAMESPACES)

}

\_\_compat = list(flatten(NAMESPACES, keyfilter=\_to\_compat))

\_OLD\_DEFAULTS = {old\_key: opt.default for old\_key, \_, opt in \_\_compat}

\_TO\_OLD\_KEY = {new\_key: old\_key for old\_key, new\_key, \_ in \_\_compat}

\_TO\_NEW\_KEY = {old\_key: new\_key for old\_key, new\_key, \_ in \_\_compat}

\_\_compat = None



SETTING\_KEYS = set(DEFAULTS.keys())

\_OLD\_SETTING\_KEYS = set(\_TO\_NEW\_KEY.keys())





def find\_deprecated\_settings(source):  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;   from celery.utils import deprecated

&#x20;   for name, opt in flatten(NAMESPACES):

&#x20;       if (opt.deprecate\_by or opt.remove\_by) and getattr(source, name, None):

&#x20;           deprecated.warn(description=f'The {name!r} setting',

&#x20;                           deprecation=opt.deprecate\_by,

&#x20;                           removal=opt.remove\_by,

&#x20;                           alternative=f'Use the {opt.alt} instead')

&#x20;   return source





@memoize(maxsize=None)

def find(name, namespace='celery'):

&#x20;   """Find setting by name."""

&#x20;   # - Try specified name-space first.

&#x20;   namespace = namespace.lower()

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       return searchresult(

&#x20;           namespace, name.lower(), NAMESPACES\[namespace]\[name.lower()],

&#x20;       )

&#x20;   except KeyError:

&#x20;       # - Try all the other namespaces.

&#x20;       for ns, opts in NAMESPACES.items():

&#x20;           if ns.lower() == name.lower():

&#x20;               return searchresult(None, ns, opts)

&#x20;           elif isinstance(opts, dict):

&#x20;               try:

&#x20;                   return searchresult(ns, name.lower(), opts\[name.lower()])

&#x20;               except KeyError:

&#x20;                   pass

&#x20;   # - See if name is a qualname last.

&#x20;   return searchresult(None, name.lower(), DEFAULTS\[name.lower()])



```

"""Implementation for the app.events shortcuts."""

from contextlib import contextmanager



from kombu.utils.objects import cached\_property





class Events:

&#x20;   """Implements app.events."""



&#x20;   receiver\_cls = 'celery.events.receiver:EventReceiver'

&#x20;   dispatcher\_cls = 'celery.events.dispatcher:EventDispatcher'

&#x20;   state\_cls = 'celery.events.state:State'



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, app=None):

&#x20;       self.app = app



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def Receiver(self):

&#x20;       return self.app.subclass\_with\_self(

&#x20;           self.receiver\_cls, reverse='events.Receiver')



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def Dispatcher(self):

&#x20;       return self.app.subclass\_with\_self(

&#x20;           self.dispatcher\_cls, reverse='events.Dispatcher')



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def State(self):

&#x20;       return self.app.subclass\_with\_self(

&#x20;           self.state\_cls, reverse='events.State')



&#x20;   @contextmanager

&#x20;   def default\_dispatcher(self, hostname=None, enabled=True,

&#x20;                          buffer\_while\_offline=False):

&#x20;       with self.app.amqp.producer\_pool.acquire(block=True) as prod:

&#x20;           # pylint: disable=too-many-function-args

&#x20;           # This is a property pylint...

&#x20;           with self.Dispatcher(prod.connection, hostname, enabled,

&#x20;                                prod.channel, buffer\_while\_offline) as d:

&#x20;               yield d



```
