\### 代码文件: celery\\app\\trace.py

```python

"""Trace task execution.



This module defines how the task execution is traced:

errors are recorded, handlers are applied and so on.

"""

import logging

import os

import sys

import time

from collections import namedtuple

from warnings import warn



from billiard.einfo import ExceptionInfo, ExceptionWithTraceback

from kombu.exceptions import EncodeError

from kombu.serialization import loads as loads\_message

from kombu.serialization import prepare\_accept\_content

from kombu.utils.encoding import safe\_repr, safe\_str



from celery import current\_app, group, signals, states

from celery.\_state import \_task\_stack

from celery.app.task import Context

from celery.app.task import Task as BaseTask

from celery.exceptions import BackendGetMetaError, Ignore, InvalidTaskError, Reject, Retry

from celery.result import AsyncResult

from celery.utils.log import get\_logger

from celery.utils.nodenames import gethostname

from celery.utils.objects import mro\_lookup

from celery.utils.saferepr import saferepr

from celery.utils.serialization import get\_pickleable\_etype, get\_pickleable\_exception, get\_pickled\_exception



\# ## ---

\# This is the heart of the worker, the inner loop so to speak.

\# It used to be split up into nice little classes and methods,

\# but in the end it only resulted in bad performance and horrible tracebacks,

\# so instead we now use one closure per task class.



\# pylint: disable=redefined-outer-name

\# We cache globals and attribute lookups, so disable this warning.

\# pylint: disable=broad-except

\# We know what we're doing...





\_\_all\_\_ = (

&#x20;   'TraceInfo', 'build\_tracer', 'trace\_task',

&#x20;   'setup\_worker\_optimizations', 'reset\_worker\_optimizations',

)



from celery.worker.state import successful\_requests



logger = get\_logger(\_\_name\_\_)



\#: Format string used to log task receipt.

LOG\_RECEIVED = """\\

Task %(name)s\[%(id)s] received\\

"""



\#: Format string used to log task success.

LOG\_SUCCESS = """\\

Task %(name)s\[%(id)s] succeeded in %(runtime)ss: %(return\_value)s\\

"""



\#: Format string used to log task failure.

LOG\_FAILURE = """\\

Task %(name)s\[%(id)s] %(description)s: %(exc)s\\

"""



\#: Format string used to log task internal error.

LOG\_INTERNAL\_ERROR = """\\

Task %(name)s\[%(id)s] %(description)s: %(exc)s\\

"""



\#: Format string used to log task ignored.

LOG\_IGNORED = """\\

Task %(name)s\[%(id)s] %(description)s\\

"""



\#: Format string used to log task rejected.

LOG\_REJECTED = """\\

Task %(name)s\[%(id)s] %(exc)s\\

"""



\#: Format string used to log task retry.

LOG\_RETRY = """\\

Task %(name)s\[%(id)s] retry: %(exc)s\\

"""



log\_policy\_t = namedtuple(

&#x20;   'log\_policy\_t',

&#x20;   ('format', 'description', 'severity', 'traceback', 'mail'),

)



log\_policy\_reject = log\_policy\_t(LOG\_REJECTED, 'rejected', logging.WARN, 1, 1)

log\_policy\_ignore = log\_policy\_t(LOG\_IGNORED, 'ignored', logging.INFO, 0, 0)

log\_policy\_internal = log\_policy\_t(

&#x20;   LOG\_INTERNAL\_ERROR, 'INTERNAL ERROR', logging.CRITICAL, 1, 1,

)

log\_policy\_expected = log\_policy\_t(

&#x20;   LOG\_FAILURE, 'raised expected', logging.INFO, 0, 0,

)

log\_policy\_unexpected = log\_policy\_t(

&#x20;   LOG\_FAILURE, 'raised unexpected', logging.ERROR, 1, 1,

)



send\_prerun = signals.task\_prerun.send

send\_postrun = signals.task\_postrun.send

send\_success = signals.task\_success.send

STARTED = states.STARTED

SUCCESS = states.SUCCESS

IGNORED = states.IGNORED

REJECTED = states.REJECTED

RETRY = states.RETRY

FAILURE = states.FAILURE

EXCEPTION\_STATES = states.EXCEPTION\_STATES

IGNORE\_STATES = frozenset({IGNORED, RETRY, REJECTED})



\#: set by :func:`setup\_worker\_optimizations`

\_localized = \[]

\_patched = {}



trace\_ok\_t = namedtuple('trace\_ok\_t', ('retval', 'info', 'runtime', 'retstr'))





def info(fmt, context):

&#x20;   """Log 'fmt % context' with severity 'INFO'.



&#x20;   'context' is also passed in extra with key 'data' for custom handlers.

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   logger.info(fmt, context, extra={'data': context})





def task\_has\_custom(task, attr):

&#x20;   """Return true if the task overrides ``attr``."""

&#x20;   return mro\_lookup(task.\_\_class\_\_, attr, stop={BaseTask, object},

&#x20;                     monkey\_patched=\['celery.app.task'])





def get\_log\_policy(task, einfo, exc):

&#x20;   if isinstance(exc, Reject):

&#x20;       return log\_policy\_reject

&#x20;   elif isinstance(exc, Ignore):

&#x20;       return log\_policy\_ignore

&#x20;   elif einfo.internal:

&#x20;       return log\_policy\_internal

&#x20;   else:

&#x20;       if task.throws and isinstance(exc, task.throws):

&#x20;           return log\_policy\_expected

&#x20;       return log\_policy\_unexpected





def get\_task\_name(request, default):

&#x20;   """Use 'shadow' in request for the task name if applicable."""

&#x20;   # request.shadow could be None or an empty string.

&#x20;   # If so, we should use default.

&#x20;   return getattr(request, 'shadow', None) or default





class TraceInfo:

&#x20;   """Information about task execution."""



&#x20;   \_\_slots\_\_ = ('state', 'retval')



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, state, retval=None):

&#x20;       self.state = state

&#x20;       self.retval = retval



&#x20;   def handle\_error\_state(self, task, req,

&#x20;                          eager=False, call\_errbacks=True):

&#x20;       if task.ignore\_result:

&#x20;           store\_errors = task.store\_errors\_even\_if\_ignored

&#x20;       elif eager and task.store\_eager\_result:

&#x20;           store\_errors = True

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           store\_errors = not eager



&#x20;       return {

&#x20;           RETRY: self.handle\_retry,

&#x20;           FAILURE: self.handle\_failure,

&#x20;       }\[self.state](task, req,

&#x20;                     store\_errors=store\_errors,

&#x20;                     call\_errbacks=call\_errbacks)



&#x20;   def handle\_reject(self, task, req, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       self.\_log\_error(task, req, ExceptionInfo())



&#x20;   def handle\_ignore(self, task, req, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       self.\_log\_error(task, req, ExceptionInfo())



&#x20;   def handle\_retry(self, task, req, store\_errors=True, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       """Handle retry exception."""

&#x20;       # the exception raised is the Retry semi-predicate,

&#x20;       # and it's exc' attribute is the original exception raised (if any).

&#x20;       type\_, \_, tb = sys.exc\_info()

&#x20;       einfo = None

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           reason = self.retval

&#x20;           einfo = ExceptionInfo((type\_, reason, tb))

&#x20;           if store\_errors:

&#x20;               task.backend.mark\_as\_retry(

&#x20;                   req.id, reason.exc, einfo.traceback, request=req,

&#x20;               )

&#x20;           task.on\_retry(reason.exc, req.id, req.args, req.kwargs, einfo)

&#x20;           signals.task\_retry.send(sender=task, request=req,

&#x20;                                   reason=reason, einfo=einfo)

&#x20;           info(LOG\_RETRY, {

&#x20;               'id': req.id,

&#x20;               'name': get\_task\_name(req, task.name),

&#x20;               'exc': str(reason),

&#x20;           })

&#x20;           # MEMORY LEAK FIX: Clear traceback frames to prevent memory retention (Issue #8882)

&#x20;           traceback\_clear(einfo.exception)

&#x20;           return einfo

&#x20;       finally:

&#x20;           # MEMORY LEAK FIX: Clean up direct traceback reference to prevent

&#x20;           # retention of frame objects and their local variables (Issue #8882)

&#x20;           if tb is not None:

&#x20;               del tb



&#x20;   def handle\_failure(self, task, req, store\_errors=True, call\_errbacks=True):

&#x20;       """Handle exception."""

&#x20;       orig\_exc = self.retval

&#x20;       tb\_ref = None



&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           exc = get\_pickleable\_exception(orig\_exc)

&#x20;           if exc.\_\_traceback\_\_ is None:

&#x20;               # `get\_pickleable\_exception` may have created a new exception without

&#x20;               # a traceback.

&#x20;               \_, \_, tb\_ref = sys.exc\_info()

&#x20;               exc.\_\_traceback\_\_ = tb\_ref



&#x20;           exc\_type = get\_pickleable\_etype(type(orig\_exc))



&#x20;           # make sure we only send pickleable exceptions back to parent.

&#x20;           einfo = ExceptionInfo(exc\_info=(exc\_type, exc, exc.\_\_traceback\_\_))



&#x20;           task.backend.mark\_as\_failure(

&#x20;               req.id, exc, einfo.traceback,

&#x20;               request=req, store\_result=store\_errors,

&#x20;               call\_errbacks=call\_errbacks,

&#x20;           )



&#x20;           task.on\_failure(exc, req.id, req.args, req.kwargs, einfo)

&#x20;           signals.task\_failure.send(sender=task, task\_id=req.id,

&#x20;                                     exception=exc, args=req.args,

&#x20;                                     kwargs=req.kwargs,

&#x20;                                     traceback=exc.\_\_traceback\_\_,

&#x20;                                     einfo=einfo)

&#x20;           self.\_log\_error(task, req, einfo)

&#x20;           # MEMORY LEAK FIX: Clear traceback frames to prevent memory retention (Issue #8882)

&#x20;           traceback\_clear(exc)

&#x20;           # Note: We return einfo, so we can't clean it up here

&#x20;           # The calling function is responsible for cleanup

&#x20;           return einfo

&#x20;       finally:

&#x20;           # MEMORY LEAK FIX: Clean up any direct traceback references we may have created

&#x20;           # to prevent retention of frame objects and their local variables (Issue #8882)

&#x20;           if tb\_ref is not None:

&#x20;               del tb\_ref



&#x20;   def \_log\_error(self, task, req, einfo):

&#x20;       eobj = einfo.exception = get\_pickled\_exception(einfo.exception)

&#x20;       if isinstance(eobj, ExceptionWithTraceback):

&#x20;           eobj = einfo.exception = eobj.exc

&#x20;       exception, traceback, exc\_info, sargs, skwargs = (

&#x20;           safe\_repr(eobj),

&#x20;           safe\_str(einfo.traceback),

&#x20;           einfo.exc\_info,

&#x20;           req.get('argsrepr') or safe\_repr(req.args),

&#x20;           req.get('kwargsrepr') or safe\_repr(req.kwargs),

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       policy = get\_log\_policy(task, einfo, eobj)



&#x20;       context = {

&#x20;           'hostname': req.hostname,

&#x20;           'id': req.id,

&#x20;           'name': get\_task\_name(req, task.name),

&#x20;           'exc': exception,

&#x20;           'traceback': traceback,

&#x20;           'args': sargs,

&#x20;           'kwargs': skwargs,

&#x20;           'description': policy.description,

&#x20;           'internal': einfo.internal,

&#x20;       }



&#x20;       logger.log(policy.severity, policy.format.strip(), context,

&#x20;                  exc\_info=exc\_info if policy.traceback else None,

&#x20;                  extra={'data': context})





def traceback\_clear(exc=None):

&#x20;   """Clear traceback frames to prevent memory leaks.



&#x20;   MEMORY LEAK FIX: This function helps break reference cycles between

&#x20;   traceback objects and frame objects that can prevent garbage collection.

&#x20;   Clearing frames releases local variables that may be holding large objects.

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   # Cleared Tb, but einfo still has a reference to Traceback.

&#x20;   # exc cleans up the Traceback at the last moment that can be revealed.

&#x20;   tb = None

&#x20;   if exc is not None:

&#x20;       if hasattr(exc, '\_\_traceback\_\_'):

&#x20;           tb = exc.\_\_traceback\_\_

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           \_, \_, tb = sys.exc\_info()

&#x20;   else:

&#x20;       \_, \_, tb = sys.exc\_info()



&#x20;   while tb is not None:

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           # MEMORY LEAK FIX: tb.tb\_frame.clear() clears ALL frame data including

&#x20;           # local variables, which is more efficient than accessing f\_locals separately.

&#x20;           # Removed redundant tb.tb\_frame.f\_locals access that was creating unnecessary references.

&#x20;           tb.tb\_frame.clear()

&#x20;       except RuntimeError:

&#x20;           # Ignore the exception raised if the frame is still executing.

&#x20;           pass

&#x20;       tb = tb.tb\_next





def build\_tracer(name, task, loader=None, hostname=None, store\_errors=True,

&#x20;                Info=TraceInfo, eager=False, propagate=False, app=None,

&#x20;                monotonic=time.monotonic, trace\_ok\_t=trace\_ok\_t,

&#x20;                IGNORE\_STATES=IGNORE\_STATES):

&#x20;   """Return a function that traces task execution.



&#x20;   Catches all exceptions and updates result backend with the

&#x20;   state and result.



&#x20;   If the call was successful, it saves the result to the task result

&#x20;   backend, and sets the task status to `"SUCCESS"`.



&#x20;   If the call raises :exc:`\~@Retry`, it extracts

&#x20;   the original exception, uses that as the result and sets the task state

&#x20;   to `"RETRY"`.



&#x20;   If the call results in an exception, it saves the exception as the task

&#x20;   result, and sets the task state to `"FAILURE"`.



&#x20;   Return a function that takes the following arguments:



&#x20;       :param uuid: The id of the task.

&#x20;       :param args: List of positional args to pass on to the function.

&#x20;       :param kwargs: Keyword arguments mapping to pass on to the function.

&#x20;       :keyword request: Request dict.



&#x20;   """



&#x20;   # pylint: disable=too-many-statements



&#x20;   # If the task doesn't define a custom \_\_call\_\_ method

&#x20;   # we optimize it away by simply calling the run method directly,

&#x20;   # saving the extra method call and a line less in the stack trace.

&#x20;   fun = task if task\_has\_custom(task, '\_\_call\_\_') else task.run



&#x20;   loader = loader or app.loader

&#x20;   ignore\_result = task.ignore\_result

&#x20;   track\_started = task.track\_started

&#x20;   track\_started = not eager and (task.track\_started and not ignore\_result)



&#x20;   # #6476

&#x20;   if eager and not ignore\_result and task.store\_eager\_result:

&#x20;       publish\_result = True

&#x20;   else:

&#x20;       publish\_result = not eager and not ignore\_result



&#x20;   deduplicate\_successful\_tasks = ((app.conf.task\_acks\_late or task.acks\_late)

&#x20;                                   and app.conf.worker\_deduplicate\_successful\_tasks

&#x20;                                   and app.backend.persistent)



&#x20;   hostname = hostname or gethostname()

&#x20;   inherit\_parent\_priority = app.conf.task\_inherit\_parent\_priority



&#x20;   loader\_task\_init = loader.on\_task\_init

&#x20;   loader\_cleanup = loader.on\_process\_cleanup



&#x20;   task\_before\_start = None

&#x20;   task\_on\_success = None

&#x20;   task\_after\_return = None

&#x20;   if task\_has\_custom(task, 'before\_start'):

&#x20;       task\_before\_start = task.before\_start

&#x20;   if task\_has\_custom(task, 'on\_success'):

&#x20;       task\_on\_success = task.on\_success

&#x20;   if task\_has\_custom(task, 'after\_return'):

&#x20;       task\_after\_return = task.after\_return



&#x20;   pid = os.getpid()



&#x20;   request\_stack = task.request\_stack

&#x20;   push\_request = request\_stack.push

&#x20;   pop\_request = request\_stack.pop

&#x20;   push\_task = \_task\_stack.push

&#x20;   pop\_task = \_task\_stack.pop

&#x20;   \_does\_info = logger.isEnabledFor(logging.INFO)

&#x20;   resultrepr\_maxsize = task.resultrepr\_maxsize



&#x20;   prerun\_receivers = signals.task\_prerun.receivers

&#x20;   postrun\_receivers = signals.task\_postrun.receivers

&#x20;   success\_receivers = signals.task\_success.receivers



&#x20;   from celery import canvas

&#x20;   signature = canvas.maybe\_signature  # maybe\_ does not clone if already



&#x20;   def on\_error(request, exc, state=FAILURE, call\_errbacks=True):

&#x20;       if propagate:

&#x20;           raise

&#x20;       I = Info(state, exc)

&#x20;       R = I.handle\_error\_state(

&#x20;           task, request, eager=eager, call\_errbacks=call\_errbacks,

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       return I, R, I.state, I.retval



&#x20;   def trace\_task(uuid, args, kwargs, request=None):

&#x20;       # R      - is the possibly prepared return value.

&#x20;       # I      - is the Info object.

&#x20;       # T      - runtime

&#x20;       # Rstr   - textual representation of return value

&#x20;       # retval - is the always unmodified return value.

&#x20;       # state  - is the resulting task state.



&#x20;       # This function is very long because we've unrolled all the calls

&#x20;       # for performance reasons, and because the function is so long

&#x20;       # we want the main variables (I, and R) to stand out visually from the

&#x20;       # the rest of the variables, so breaking PEP8 is worth it ;)

&#x20;       R = I = T = Rstr = retval = state = None

&#x20;       task\_request = None

&#x20;       time\_start = monotonic()

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               kwargs.items

&#x20;           except AttributeError:

&#x20;               raise InvalidTaskError(

&#x20;                   'Task keyword arguments is not a mapping')



&#x20;           task\_request = Context(request or {}, args=args,

&#x20;                                  called\_directly=False, kwargs=kwargs)



&#x20;           redelivered = (task\_request.delivery\_info

&#x20;                          and task\_request.delivery\_info.get('redelivered', False))

&#x20;           if deduplicate\_successful\_tasks and redelivered:

&#x20;               if task\_request.id in successful\_requests:

&#x20;                   return trace\_ok\_t(R, I, T, Rstr)

&#x20;               r = AsyncResult(task\_request.id, app=app)



&#x20;               try:

&#x20;                   state = r.state

&#x20;               except BackendGetMetaError:

&#x20;                   pass

&#x20;               else:

&#x20;                   if state == SUCCESS:

&#x20;                       info(LOG\_IGNORED, {

&#x20;                           'id': task\_request.id,

&#x20;                           'name': get\_task\_name(task\_request, name),

&#x20;                           'description': 'Task already completed successfully.'

&#x20;                       })

&#x20;                       return trace\_ok\_t(R, I, T, Rstr)



&#x20;           push\_task(task)

&#x20;           root\_id = task\_request.root\_id or uuid

&#x20;           task\_priority = task\_request.delivery\_info.get('priority') if \\

&#x20;               inherit\_parent\_priority else None

&#x20;           push\_request(task\_request)

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               # -\*- PRE -\*-

&#x20;               if prerun\_receivers:

&#x20;                   send\_prerun(sender=task, task\_id=uuid, task=task,

&#x20;                               args=args, kwargs=kwargs)

&#x20;               loader\_task\_init(uuid, task)

&#x20;               if track\_started:

&#x20;                   task.backend.store\_result(

&#x20;                       uuid, {'pid': pid, 'hostname': hostname}, STARTED,

&#x20;                       request=task\_request,

&#x20;                   )



&#x20;               # -\*- TRACE -\*-

&#x20;               try:

&#x20;                   if task\_before\_start:

&#x20;                       task\_before\_start(uuid, args, kwargs)



&#x20;                   R = retval = fun(\*args, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;                   state = SUCCESS

&#x20;               except Reject as exc:

&#x20;                   I, R = Info(REJECTED, exc), ExceptionInfo(internal=True)

&#x20;                   state, retval = I.state, I.retval

&#x20;                   I.handle\_reject(task, task\_request)

&#x20;                   # MEMORY LEAK FIX: Clear traceback frames to prevent memory retention (Issue #8882)

&#x20;                   traceback\_clear(exc)

&#x20;               except Ignore as exc:

&#x20;                   I, R = Info(IGNORED, exc), ExceptionInfo(internal=True)

&#x20;                   state, retval = I.state, I.retval

&#x20;                   I.handle\_ignore(task, task\_request)

&#x20;                   # MEMORY LEAK FIX: Clear traceback frames to prevent memory retention (Issue #8882)

&#x20;                   traceback\_clear(exc)

&#x20;               except Retry as exc:

&#x20;                   I, R, state, retval = on\_error(

&#x20;                       task\_request, exc, RETRY, call\_errbacks=False)

&#x20;                   # MEMORY LEAK FIX: Clear traceback frames to prevent memory retention (Issue #8882)

&#x20;                   traceback\_clear(exc)

&#x20;               except Exception as exc:

&#x20;                   I, R, state, retval = on\_error(task\_request, exc)

&#x20;                   # MEMORY LEAK FIX: Clear traceback frames to prevent memory retention (Issue #8882)

&#x20;                   traceback\_clear(exc)

&#x20;               except BaseException:

&#x20;                   raise

&#x20;               else:

&#x20;                   try:

&#x20;                       # callback tasks must be applied before the result is

&#x20;                       # stored, so that result.children is populated.



&#x20;                       # groups are called inline and will store trail

&#x20;                       # separately, so need to call them separately

&#x20;                       # so that the trail's not added multiple times :(

&#x20;                       # (Issue #1936)

&#x20;                       callbacks = task.request.callbacks

&#x20;                       if callbacks:

&#x20;                           if len(task.request.callbacks) > 1:

&#x20;                               sigs, groups = \[], \[]

&#x20;                               for sig in callbacks:

&#x20;                                   sig = signature(sig, app=app)

&#x20;                                   if isinstance(sig, group):

&#x20;                                       groups.append(sig)

&#x20;                                   else:

&#x20;                                       sigs.append(sig)

&#x20;                               for group\_ in groups:

&#x20;                                   group\_.apply\_async(

&#x20;                                       (retval,),

&#x20;                                       parent\_id=uuid, root\_id=root\_id,

&#x20;                                       priority=task\_priority

&#x20;                                   )

&#x20;                               if sigs:

&#x20;                                   group(sigs, app=app).apply\_async(

&#x20;                                       (retval,),

&#x20;                                       parent\_id=uuid, root\_id=root\_id,

&#x20;                                       priority=task\_priority

&#x20;                                   )

&#x20;                           else:

&#x20;                               signature(callbacks\[0], app=app).apply\_async(

&#x20;                                   (retval,), parent\_id=uuid, root\_id=root\_id,

&#x20;                                   priority=task\_priority

&#x20;                               )



&#x20;                       # execute first task in chain

&#x20;                       chain = task\_request.chain

&#x20;                       if chain:

&#x20;                           \_chsig = signature(chain.pop(), app=app)

&#x20;                           \_chsig.apply\_async(

&#x20;                               (retval,), chain=chain,

&#x20;                               parent\_id=uuid, root\_id=root\_id,

&#x20;                               priority=task\_priority

&#x20;                           )

&#x20;                       task.backend.mark\_as\_done(

&#x20;                           uuid, retval, task\_request, publish\_result,

&#x20;                       )

&#x20;                   except EncodeError as exc:

&#x20;                       I, R, state, retval = on\_error(task\_request, exc)

&#x20;                       # MEMORY LEAK FIX: Clear traceback frames to prevent memory retention (Issue #8882)

&#x20;                       traceback\_clear(exc)

&#x20;                   else:

&#x20;                       Rstr = saferepr(R, resultrepr\_maxsize)

&#x20;                       T = monotonic() - time\_start

&#x20;                       if task\_on\_success:

&#x20;                           task\_on\_success(retval, uuid, args, kwargs)

&#x20;                       if success\_receivers:

&#x20;                           send\_success(sender=task, result=retval)

&#x20;                       if \_does\_info:

&#x20;                           info(LOG\_SUCCESS, {

&#x20;                               'id': uuid,

&#x20;                               'name': get\_task\_name(task\_request, name),

&#x20;                               'return\_value': Rstr,

&#x20;                               'runtime': T,

&#x20;                               'args': task\_request.get('argsrepr') or safe\_repr(args),

&#x20;                               'kwargs': task\_request.get('kwargsrepr') or safe\_repr(kwargs),

&#x20;                           })



&#x20;               # -\* POST \*-

&#x20;               if state not in IGNORE\_STATES:

&#x20;                   if task\_after\_return:

&#x20;                       task\_after\_return(

&#x20;                           state, retval, uuid, args, kwargs, None,

&#x20;                       )

&#x20;           finally:

&#x20;               try:

&#x20;                   if postrun\_receivers:

&#x20;                       send\_postrun(sender=task, task\_id=uuid, task=task,

&#x20;                                    args=args, kwargs=kwargs,

&#x20;                                    retval=retval, state=state)

&#x20;               finally:

&#x20;                   pop\_task()

&#x20;                   pop\_request()

&#x20;                   if not eager:

&#x20;                       try:

&#x20;                           task.backend.process\_cleanup()

&#x20;                           loader\_cleanup()

&#x20;                       except (KeyboardInterrupt, SystemExit, MemoryError):

&#x20;                           raise

&#x20;                       except Exception as exc:

&#x20;                           logger.error('Process cleanup failed: %r', exc,

&#x20;                                        exc\_info=True)

&#x20;       except MemoryError:

&#x20;           raise

&#x20;       except Exception as exc:

&#x20;           \_signal\_internal\_error(task, uuid, args, kwargs, request, exc)

&#x20;           if eager:

&#x20;               raise

&#x20;           R = report\_internal\_error(task, exc)

&#x20;           if task\_request is not None:

&#x20;               I, \_, \_, \_ = on\_error(task\_request, exc)

&#x20;       return trace\_ok\_t(R, I, T, Rstr)



&#x20;   return trace\_task





def trace\_task(task, uuid, args, kwargs, request=None, \*\*opts):

&#x20;   """Trace task execution."""

&#x20;   request = {} if not request else request

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       if task.\_\_trace\_\_ is None:

&#x20;           task.\_\_trace\_\_ = build\_tracer(task.name, task, \*\*opts)

&#x20;       return task.\_\_trace\_\_(uuid, args, kwargs, request)

&#x20;   except Exception as exc:

&#x20;       \_signal\_internal\_error(task, uuid, args, kwargs, request, exc)

&#x20;       return trace\_ok\_t(report\_internal\_error(task, exc), TraceInfo(FAILURE, exc), 0.0, None)





def \_signal\_internal\_error(task, uuid, args, kwargs, request, exc):

&#x20;   """Send a special `internal\_error` signal to the app for outside body errors."""

&#x20;   tb = None

&#x20;   einfo = None

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       \_, \_, tb = sys.exc\_info()

&#x20;       einfo = ExceptionInfo()

&#x20;       einfo.exception = get\_pickleable\_exception(einfo.exception)

&#x20;       einfo.type = get\_pickleable\_etype(einfo.type)

&#x20;       signals.task\_internal\_error.send(

&#x20;           sender=task,

&#x20;           task\_id=uuid,

&#x20;           args=args,

&#x20;           kwargs=kwargs,

&#x20;           request=request,

&#x20;           exception=exc,

&#x20;           traceback=tb,

&#x20;           einfo=einfo,

&#x20;       )

&#x20;   finally:

&#x20;       # MEMORY LEAK FIX: Clean up local references to prevent memory leaks (Issue #8882)

&#x20;       # Both 'tb' and 'einfo' can hold references to frame objects and their local variables.

&#x20;       # Explicitly clearing these prevents reference cycles that block garbage collection.

&#x20;       if tb is not None:

&#x20;           del tb

&#x20;       if einfo is not None:

&#x20;           # Clear traceback frames to ensure consistent cleanup

&#x20;           traceback\_clear(einfo.exception)

&#x20;           # Break potential reference cycles by deleting the einfo object

&#x20;           del einfo





def trace\_task\_ret(name, uuid, request, body, content\_type,

&#x20;                  content\_encoding, loads=loads\_message, app=None,

&#x20;                  \*\*extra\_request):

&#x20;   app = app or current\_app.\_get\_current\_object()

&#x20;   embed = None

&#x20;   if content\_type:

&#x20;       accept = prepare\_accept\_content(app.conf.accept\_content)

&#x20;       args, kwargs, embed = loads(

&#x20;           body, content\_type, content\_encoding, accept=accept,

&#x20;       )

&#x20;   else:

&#x20;       args, kwargs, embed = body

&#x20;   hostname = gethostname()

&#x20;   request.update({

&#x20;       'args': args, 'kwargs': kwargs,

&#x20;       'hostname': hostname, 'is\_eager': False,

&#x20;   }, \*\*embed or {})

&#x20;   R, I, T, Rstr = trace\_task(app.tasks\[name],

&#x20;                              uuid, args, kwargs, request, app=app)

&#x20;   return (1, R, T) if I else (0, Rstr, T)





def fast\_trace\_task(task, uuid, request, body, content\_type,

&#x20;                   content\_encoding, loads=loads\_message, \_loc=None,

&#x20;                   hostname=None, \*\*\_):

&#x20;   \_loc = \_localized if not \_loc else \_loc

&#x20;   embed = None

&#x20;   tasks, accept, hostname = \_loc

&#x20;   if content\_type:

&#x20;       args, kwargs, embed = loads(

&#x20;           body, content\_type, content\_encoding, accept=accept,

&#x20;       )

&#x20;   else:

&#x20;       args, kwargs, embed = body

&#x20;   request.update({

&#x20;       'args': args, 'kwargs': kwargs,

&#x20;       'hostname': hostname, 'is\_eager': False,

&#x20;   }, \*\*embed or {})

&#x20;   R, I, T, Rstr = tasks\[task].\_\_trace\_\_(

&#x20;       uuid, args, kwargs, request,

&#x20;   )

&#x20;   return (1, R, T) if I else (0, Rstr, T)





def report\_internal\_error(task, exc):

&#x20;   \_type, \_value, \_tb = sys.exc\_info()

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       \_value = task.backend.prepare\_exception(exc, 'pickle')

&#x20;       exc\_info = ExceptionInfo((\_type, \_value, \_tb), internal=True)

&#x20;       warn(RuntimeWarning(

&#x20;           'Exception raised outside body: {!r}:\\n{}'.format(

&#x20;               exc, exc\_info.traceback)))

&#x20;       return exc\_info

&#x20;   finally:

&#x20;       del \_tb





def setup\_worker\_optimizations(app, hostname=None):

&#x20;   """Setup worker related optimizations."""

&#x20;   hostname = hostname or gethostname()



&#x20;   # make sure custom Task.\_\_call\_\_ methods that calls super

&#x20;   # won't mess up the request/task stack.

&#x20;   \_install\_stack\_protection()



&#x20;   # all new threads start without a current app, so if an app is not

&#x20;   # passed on to the thread it will fall back to the "default app",

&#x20;   # which then could be the wrong app.  So for the worker

&#x20;   # we set this to always return our app.  This is a hack,

&#x20;   # and means that only a single app can be used for workers

&#x20;   # running in the same process.

&#x20;   app.set\_current()

&#x20;   app.set\_default()



&#x20;   # evaluate all task classes by finalizing the app.

&#x20;   app.finalize()



&#x20;   # set fast shortcut to task registry

&#x20;   \_localized\[:] = \[

&#x20;       app.\_tasks,

&#x20;       prepare\_accept\_content(app.conf.accept\_content),

&#x20;       hostname,

&#x20;   ]



&#x20;   app.use\_fast\_trace\_task = True





def reset\_worker\_optimizations(app=current\_app):

&#x20;   """Reset previously configured optimizations."""

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       delattr(BaseTask, '\_stackprotected')

&#x20;   except AttributeError:

&#x20;       pass

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       BaseTask.\_\_call\_\_ = \_patched.pop('BaseTask.\_\_call\_\_')

&#x20;   except KeyError:

&#x20;       pass

&#x20;   app.use\_fast\_trace\_task = False





def \_install\_stack\_protection():

&#x20;   # Patches BaseTask.\_\_call\_\_ in the worker to handle the edge case

&#x20;   # where people override it and also call super.

&#x20;   #

&#x20;   # - The worker optimizes away BaseTask.\_\_call\_\_ and instead

&#x20;   #   calls task.run directly.

&#x20;   # - so with the addition of current\_task and the request stack

&#x20;   #   BaseTask.\_\_call\_\_ now pushes to those stacks so that

&#x20;   #   they work when tasks are called directly.

&#x20;   #

&#x20;   # The worker only optimizes away \_\_call\_\_ in the case

&#x20;   # where it hasn't been overridden, so the request/task stack

&#x20;   # will blow if a custom task class defines \_\_call\_\_ and also

&#x20;   # calls super().

&#x20;   if not getattr(BaseTask, '\_stackprotected', False):

&#x20;       \_patched\['BaseTask.\_\_call\_\_'] = orig = BaseTask.\_\_call\_\_



&#x20;       def \_\_protected\_call\_\_(self, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;           stack = self.request\_stack

&#x20;           req = stack.top

&#x20;           if req and not req.\_protected and \\

&#x20;                   len(stack) == 1 and not req.called\_directly:

&#x20;               req.\_protected = 1

&#x20;               return self.run(\*args, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;           return orig(self, \*args, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;       BaseTask.\_\_call\_\_ = \_\_protected\_call\_\_

&#x20;       BaseTask.\_stackprotected = True



```

```python
"""App utilities: Compat settings, bug-report tool, pickling apps."""

import os

import platform as \_platform

import re

from collections import namedtuple

from collections.abc import Mapping

from copy import deepcopy

from types import ModuleType



from kombu.utils.url import maybe\_sanitize\_url



from celery.exceptions import ImproperlyConfigured

from celery.platforms import pyimplementation

from celery.utils.collections import ConfigurationView

from celery.utils.imports import import\_from\_cwd, qualname, symbol\_by\_name

from celery.utils.text import pretty



from .defaults import \_OLD\_DEFAULTS, \_OLD\_SETTING\_KEYS, \_TO\_NEW\_KEY, \_TO\_OLD\_KEY, DEFAULTS, SETTING\_KEYS, find



\_\_all\_\_ = (

&#x20;   'Settings', 'appstr', 'bugreport',

&#x20;   'filter\_hidden\_settings', 'find\_app',

)



\#: Format used to generate bug-report information.

BUGREPORT\_INFO = """

software -> celery:{celery\_v} kombu:{kombu\_v} py:{py\_v}

&#x20;           billiard:{billiard\_v} {driver\_v}

platform -> system:{system} arch:{arch}

&#x20;           kernel version:{kernel\_version} imp:{py\_i}

loader   -> {loader}

settings -> transport:{transport} results:{results}



{human\_settings}

"""



HIDDEN\_SETTINGS = re.compile(

&#x20;   'API|TOKEN|KEY|SECRET|PASS|PROFANITIES\_LIST|SIGNATURE|DATABASE|BEAT\_DBURI',

&#x20;   re.IGNORECASE,

)



E\_MIX\_OLD\_INTO\_NEW = """



Cannot mix new and old setting keys, please rename the

following settings to the new format:



{renames}



"""



E\_MIX\_NEW\_INTO\_OLD = """



Cannot mix new setting names with old setting names, please

rename the following settings to use the old format:



{renames}



Or change all of the settings to use the new format :)



"""



FMT\_REPLACE\_SETTING = '{replace:<36} -> {with\_}'





def appstr(app):

&#x20;   """String used in \_\_repr\_\_ etc, to id app instances."""

&#x20;   return f'{app.main or "\_\_main\_\_"} at {id(app):#x}'





class Settings(ConfigurationView):

&#x20;   """Celery settings object.



&#x20;   .. seealso:



&#x20;       :ref:`configuration` for a full list of configuration keys.



&#x20;   """



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, \*args, deprecated\_settings=None, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(\*args, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;       self.deprecated\_settings = deprecated\_settings



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def broker\_read\_url(self):

&#x20;       return (

&#x20;           os.environ.get('CELERY\_BROKER\_READ\_URL') or

&#x20;           self.get('broker\_read\_url') or

&#x20;           self.broker\_url

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def broker\_write\_url(self):

&#x20;       return (

&#x20;           os.environ.get('CELERY\_BROKER\_WRITE\_URL') or

&#x20;           self.get('broker\_write\_url') or

&#x20;           self.broker\_url

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def broker\_url(self):

&#x20;       return (

&#x20;           os.environ.get('CELERY\_BROKER\_URL') or

&#x20;           self.first('broker\_url', 'broker\_host')

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def result\_backend(self):

&#x20;       return (

&#x20;           os.environ.get('CELERY\_RESULT\_BACKEND') or

&#x20;           self.first('result\_backend', 'CELERY\_RESULT\_BACKEND')

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def task\_default\_exchange(self):

&#x20;       return self.first(

&#x20;           'task\_default\_exchange',

&#x20;           'task\_default\_queue',

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def task\_default\_routing\_key(self):

&#x20;       return self.first(

&#x20;           'task\_default\_routing\_key',

&#x20;           'task\_default\_queue',

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def timezone(self):

&#x20;       # this way we also support django's time zone.

&#x20;       return self.first('timezone', 'TIME\_ZONE')



&#x20;   def without\_defaults(self):

&#x20;       """Return the current configuration, but without defaults."""

&#x20;       # the last stash is the default settings, so just skip that

&#x20;       return Settings({}, self.maps\[:-1])



&#x20;   def value\_set\_for(self, key):

&#x20;       return key in self.without\_defaults()



&#x20;   def find\_option(self, name, namespace=''):

&#x20;       """Search for option by name.



&#x20;       Example:

&#x20;           >>> from proj.celery import app

&#x20;           >>> app.conf.find\_option('disable\_rate\_limits')

&#x20;           ('worker', 'prefetch\_multiplier',

&#x20;            <Option: type->bool default->False>))



&#x20;       Arguments:

&#x20;           name (str): Name of option, cannot be partial.

&#x20;           namespace (str): Preferred name-space (``None`` by default).

&#x20;       Returns:

&#x20;           Tuple: of ``(namespace, key, type)``.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return find(name, namespace)



&#x20;   def find\_value\_for\_key(self, name, namespace='celery'):

&#x20;       """Shortcut to ``get\_by\_parts(\*find\_option(name)\[:-1])``."""

&#x20;       return self.get\_by\_parts(\*self.find\_option(name, namespace)\[:-1])



&#x20;   def get\_by\_parts(self, \*parts):

&#x20;       """Return the current value for setting specified as a path.



&#x20;       Example:

&#x20;           >>> from proj.celery import app

&#x20;           >>> app.conf.get\_by\_parts('worker', 'disable\_rate\_limits')

&#x20;           False

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return self\['\_'.join(part for part in parts if part)]



&#x20;   def finalize(self):

&#x20;       # See PendingConfiguration in celery/app/base.py

&#x20;       # first access will read actual configuration.

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           self\['\_\_bogus\_\_']

&#x20;       except KeyError:

&#x20;           pass

&#x20;       return self



&#x20;   def table(self, with\_defaults=False, censored=True):

&#x20;       filt = filter\_hidden\_settings if censored else lambda v: v

&#x20;       dict\_members = dir(dict)

&#x20;       self.finalize()

&#x20;       settings = self if with\_defaults else self.without\_defaults()

&#x20;       return filt({

&#x20;           k: v for k, v in settings.items()

&#x20;           if not k.startswith('\_') and k not in dict\_members

&#x20;       })



&#x20;   def humanize(self, with\_defaults=False, censored=True):

&#x20;       """Return a human readable text showing configuration changes."""

&#x20;       return '\\n'.join(

&#x20;           f'{key}: {pretty(value, width=50)}'

&#x20;           for key, value in self.table(with\_defaults, censored).items())



&#x20;   def maybe\_warn\_deprecated\_settings(self):

&#x20;       # TODO: Remove this method in Celery 6.0

&#x20;       if self.deprecated\_settings:

&#x20;           from celery.app.defaults import \_TO\_NEW\_KEY

&#x20;           from celery.utils import deprecated

&#x20;           for setting in self.deprecated\_settings:

&#x20;               deprecated.warn(description=f'The {setting!r} setting',

&#x20;                               removal='6.0.0',

&#x20;                               alternative=f'Use the {\_TO\_NEW\_KEY\[setting]} instead')



&#x20;           return True



&#x20;       return False





def \_new\_key\_to\_old(key, convert=\_TO\_OLD\_KEY.get):

&#x20;   return convert(key, key)





def \_old\_key\_to\_new(key, convert=\_TO\_NEW\_KEY.get):

&#x20;   return convert(key, key)





\_settings\_info\_t = namedtuple('settings\_info\_t', (

&#x20;   'defaults', 'convert', 'key\_t', 'mix\_error',

))



\_settings\_info = \_settings\_info\_t(

&#x20;   DEFAULTS, \_TO\_NEW\_KEY, \_old\_key\_to\_new, E\_MIX\_OLD\_INTO\_NEW,

)

\_old\_settings\_info = \_settings\_info\_t(

&#x20;   \_OLD\_DEFAULTS, \_TO\_OLD\_KEY, \_new\_key\_to\_old, E\_MIX\_NEW\_INTO\_OLD,

)





def detect\_settings(conf, preconf=None, ignore\_keys=None, prefix=None,

&#x20;                   all\_keys=None, old\_keys=None):

&#x20;   preconf = {} if not preconf else preconf

&#x20;   ignore\_keys = set() if not ignore\_keys else ignore\_keys

&#x20;   all\_keys = SETTING\_KEYS if not all\_keys else all\_keys

&#x20;   old\_keys = \_OLD\_SETTING\_KEYS if not old\_keys else old\_keys



&#x20;   source = conf

&#x20;   if conf is None:

&#x20;       source, conf = preconf, {}

&#x20;   have = set(source.keys()) - ignore\_keys

&#x20;   is\_in\_new = have.intersection(all\_keys)

&#x20;   is\_in\_old = have.intersection(old\_keys)



&#x20;   info = None

&#x20;   if is\_in\_new:

&#x20;       # have new setting names

&#x20;       info, left = \_settings\_info, is\_in\_old

&#x20;       if is\_in\_old and len(is\_in\_old) > len(is\_in\_new):

&#x20;           # Majority of the settings are old.

&#x20;           info, left = \_old\_settings\_info, is\_in\_new

&#x20;   if is\_in\_old:

&#x20;       # have old setting names, or a majority of the names are old.

&#x20;       if not info:

&#x20;           info, left = \_old\_settings\_info, is\_in\_new

&#x20;       if is\_in\_new and len(is\_in\_new) > len(is\_in\_old):

&#x20;           # Majority of the settings are new

&#x20;           info, left = \_settings\_info, is\_in\_old

&#x20;   else:

&#x20;       # no settings, just use new format.

&#x20;       info, left = \_settings\_info, is\_in\_old



&#x20;   if prefix:

&#x20;       # always use new format if prefix is used.

&#x20;       info, left = \_settings\_info, set()



&#x20;   # only raise error for keys that the user didn't provide two keys

&#x20;   # for (e.g., both ``result\_expires`` and ``CELERY\_TASK\_RESULT\_EXPIRES``).

&#x20;   really\_left = {key for key in left if info.convert\[key] not in have}

&#x20;   if really\_left:

&#x20;       # user is mixing old/new, or new/old settings, give renaming

&#x20;       # suggestions.

&#x20;       raise ImproperlyConfigured(info.mix\_error.format(renames='\\n'.join(

&#x20;           FMT\_REPLACE\_SETTING.format(replace=key, with\_=info.convert\[key])

&#x20;           for key in sorted(really\_left)

&#x20;       )))



&#x20;   preconf = {info.convert.get(k, k): v for k, v in preconf.items()}

&#x20;   defaults = dict(deepcopy(info.defaults), \*\*preconf)

&#x20;   return Settings(

&#x20;       preconf, \[conf, defaults],

&#x20;       (\_old\_key\_to\_new, \_new\_key\_to\_old),

&#x20;       deprecated\_settings=is\_in\_old,

&#x20;       prefix=prefix,

&#x20;   )





class AppPickler:

&#x20;   """Old application pickler/unpickler (< 3.1)."""



&#x20;   def \_\_call\_\_(self, cls, \*args):

&#x20;       kwargs = self.build\_kwargs(\*args)

&#x20;       app = self.construct(cls, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;       self.prepare(app, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;       return app



&#x20;   def prepare(self, app, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       app.conf.update(kwargs\['changes'])



&#x20;   def build\_kwargs(self, \*args):

&#x20;       return self.build\_standard\_kwargs(\*args)



&#x20;   def build\_standard\_kwargs(self, main, changes, loader, backend, amqp,

&#x20;                             events, log, control, accept\_magic\_kwargs,

&#x20;                             config\_source=None):

&#x20;       return {'main': main, 'loader': loader, 'backend': backend,

&#x20;               'amqp': amqp, 'changes': changes, 'events': events,

&#x20;               'log': log, 'control': control, 'set\_as\_current': False,

&#x20;               'config\_source': config\_source}



&#x20;   def construct(self, cls, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       return cls(\*\*kwargs)





def \_unpickle\_app(cls, pickler, \*args):

&#x20;   """Rebuild app for versions 2.5+."""

&#x20;   return pickler()(cls, \*args)





def \_unpickle\_app\_v2(cls, kwargs):

&#x20;   """Rebuild app for versions 3.1+."""

&#x20;   kwargs\['set\_as\_current'] = False

&#x20;   return cls(\*\*kwargs)





def filter\_hidden\_settings(conf):

&#x20;   """Filter sensitive settings."""

&#x20;   def maybe\_censor(key, value, mask='\*' \* 8):

&#x20;       if isinstance(value, Mapping):

&#x20;           return filter\_hidden\_settings(value)

&#x20;       if isinstance(key, str):

&#x20;           if HIDDEN\_SETTINGS.search(key):

&#x20;               return mask

&#x20;           elif 'broker\_url' in key.lower():

&#x20;               from kombu import Connection

&#x20;               return Connection(value).as\_uri(mask=mask)

&#x20;           elif 'backend' in key.lower():

&#x20;               return maybe\_sanitize\_url(value, mask=mask)



&#x20;       return value



&#x20;   return {k: maybe\_censor(k, v) for k, v in conf.items()}





def bugreport(app):

&#x20;   """Return a string containing information useful in bug-reports."""

&#x20;   import billiard

&#x20;   import kombu



&#x20;   import celery



&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       conn = app.connection()

&#x20;       driver\_v = '{}:{}'.format(conn.transport.driver\_name,

&#x20;                                 conn.transport.driver\_version())

&#x20;       transport = conn.transport\_cls

&#x20;   except Exception:  # pylint: disable=broad-except

&#x20;       transport = driver\_v = ''



&#x20;   return BUGREPORT\_INFO.format(

&#x20;       system=\_platform.system(),

&#x20;       arch=', '.join(x for x in \_platform.architecture() if x),

&#x20;       kernel\_version=\_platform.release(),

&#x20;       py\_i=pyimplementation(),

&#x20;       celery\_v=celery.VERSION\_BANNER,

&#x20;       kombu\_v=kombu.\_\_version\_\_,

&#x20;       billiard\_v=billiard.\_\_version\_\_,

&#x20;       py\_v=\_platform.python\_version(),

&#x20;       driver\_v=driver\_v,

&#x20;       transport=transport,

&#x20;       results=maybe\_sanitize\_url(app.conf.result\_backend or 'disabled'),

&#x20;       human\_settings=app.conf.humanize(),

&#x20;       loader=qualname(app.loader.\_\_class\_\_),

&#x20;   )





def find\_app(app, symbol\_by\_name=symbol\_by\_name, imp=import\_from\_cwd):

&#x20;   """Find app by name."""

&#x20;   from .base import Celery



&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       sym = symbol\_by\_name(app, imp=imp)

&#x20;   except AttributeError:

&#x20;       # last part was not an attribute, but a module

&#x20;       sym = imp(app)

&#x20;   if isinstance(sym, ModuleType) and ':' not in app:

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           found = sym.app

&#x20;           if isinstance(found, ModuleType):

&#x20;               raise AttributeError()

&#x20;       except AttributeError:

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               found = sym.celery

&#x20;               if isinstance(found, ModuleType):

&#x20;                   raise AttributeError(

&#x20;                       "attribute 'celery' is the celery module not the instance of celery")

&#x20;           except AttributeError:

&#x20;               if getattr(sym, '\_\_path\_\_', None):

&#x20;                   try:

&#x20;                       return find\_app(

&#x20;                           f'{app}.celery',

&#x20;                           symbol\_by\_name=symbol\_by\_name, imp=imp,

&#x20;                       )

&#x20;                   except ImportError:

&#x20;                       pass

&#x20;               for suspect in vars(sym).values():

&#x20;                   if isinstance(suspect, Celery):

&#x20;                       return suspect

&#x20;               raise

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               return found

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           return found

&#x20;   return sym



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\app\\\_\_init\_\_.py

```python

"""Celery Application."""

from celery import \_state

from celery.\_state import app\_or\_default, disable\_trace, enable\_trace, pop\_current\_task, push\_current\_task

from celery.local import Proxy



from .base import Celery

from .utils import AppPickler



\_\_all\_\_ = (

&#x20;   'Celery', 'AppPickler', 'app\_or\_default', 'default\_app',

&#x20;   'bugreport', 'enable\_trace', 'disable\_trace', 'shared\_task',

&#x20;   'push\_current\_task', 'pop\_current\_task',

)



\#: Proxy always returning the app set as default.

default\_app = Proxy(lambda: \_state.default\_app)





def bugreport(app=None):

&#x20;   """Return information useful in bug reports."""

&#x20;   return (app or \_state.get\_current\_app()).bugreport()





def shared\_task(\*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;   """Create shared task (decorator).



&#x20;   This can be used by library authors to create tasks that'll work

&#x20;   for any app environment.



&#x20;   Returns:

&#x20;       \~celery.local.Proxy: A proxy that always takes the task from the

&#x20;       current apps task registry.



&#x20;   Example:



&#x20;       >>> from celery import Celery, shared\_task

&#x20;       >>> @shared\_task

&#x20;       ... def add(x, y):

&#x20;       ...     return x + y

&#x20;       ...

&#x20;       >>> app1 = Celery(broker='amqp://')

&#x20;       >>> add.app is app1

&#x20;       True

&#x20;       >>> app2 = Celery(broker='redis://')

&#x20;       >>> add.app is app2

&#x20;       True

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   def create\_shared\_task(\*\*options):



&#x20;       def \_\_inner(fun):

&#x20;           name = options.get('name')

&#x20;           # Set as shared task so that unfinalized apps,

&#x20;           # and future apps will register a copy of this task.

&#x20;           \_state.connect\_on\_app\_finalize(

&#x20;               lambda app: app.\_task\_from\_fun(fun, \*\*options)

&#x20;           )



&#x20;           # Force all finalized apps to take this task as well.

&#x20;           for app in \_state.\_get\_active\_apps():

&#x20;               if app.finalized:

&#x20;                   with app.\_finalize\_mutex:

&#x20;                       app.\_task\_from\_fun(fun, \*\*options)



&#x20;           # Return a proxy that always gets the task from the current

&#x20;           # apps task registry.

&#x20;           def task\_by\_cons():

&#x20;               app = \_state.get\_current\_app()

&#x20;               return app.tasks\[

&#x20;                   name or app.gen\_task\_name(fun.\_\_name\_\_, fun.\_\_module\_\_)

&#x20;               ]

&#x20;           return Proxy(task\_by\_cons)

&#x20;       return \_\_inner



&#x20;   if len(args) == 1 and callable(args\[0]):

&#x20;       return create\_shared\_task(\*\*kwargs)(args\[0])

&#x20;   return create\_shared\_task(\*args, \*\*kwargs)



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\apps\\beat.py

```python

"""Beat command-line program.



This module is the 'program-version' of :mod:`celery.beat`.



It does everything necessary to run that module

as an actual application, like installing signal handlers

and so on.

"""

from \_\_future\_\_ import annotations



import numbers

import socket

import sys

from datetime import datetime

from signal import Signals

from types import FrameType

from typing import Any



from celery import VERSION\_BANNER, Celery, beat, platforms

from celery.utils.imports import qualname

from celery.utils.log import LOG\_LEVELS, get\_logger

from celery.utils.time import humanize\_seconds



\_\_all\_\_ = ('Beat',)



STARTUP\_INFO\_FMT = """

LocalTime -> {timestamp}

Configuration ->

&#x20;   . broker -> {conninfo}

&#x20;   . loader -> {loader}

&#x20;   . scheduler -> {scheduler}

{scheduler\_info}

&#x20;   . logfile -> {logfile}@%{loglevel}

&#x20;   . maxinterval -> {hmax\_interval} ({max\_interval}s)

""".strip()



logger = get\_logger('celery.beat')





class Beat:

&#x20;   """Beat as a service."""



&#x20;   Service = beat.Service

&#x20;   app: Celery = None



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, max\_interval: int | None = None, app: Celery | None = None,

&#x20;                socket\_timeout: int = 30, pidfile: str | None = None, no\_color: bool | None = None,

&#x20;                loglevel: str = 'WARN', logfile: str | None = None, schedule: str | None = None,

&#x20;                scheduler: str | None = None,

&#x20;                scheduler\_cls: str | None = None,  # XXX use scheduler

&#x20;                redirect\_stdouts: bool | None = None,

&#x20;                redirect\_stdouts\_level: str | None = None,

&#x20;                quiet: bool = False, \*\*kwargs: Any) -> None:

&#x20;       self.app = app = app or self.app

&#x20;       either = self.app.either

&#x20;       self.loglevel = loglevel

&#x20;       self.logfile = logfile

&#x20;       self.schedule = either('beat\_schedule\_filename', schedule)

&#x20;       self.scheduler\_cls = either(

&#x20;           'beat\_scheduler', scheduler, scheduler\_cls)

&#x20;       self.redirect\_stdouts = either(

&#x20;           'worker\_redirect\_stdouts', redirect\_stdouts)

&#x20;       self.redirect\_stdouts\_level = either(

&#x20;           'worker\_redirect\_stdouts\_level', redirect\_stdouts\_level)

&#x20;       self.quiet = quiet



&#x20;       self.max\_interval = max\_interval

&#x20;       self.socket\_timeout = socket\_timeout

&#x20;       self.no\_color = no\_color

&#x20;       self.colored = app.log.colored(

&#x20;           self.logfile,

&#x20;           enabled=not no\_color if no\_color is not None else no\_color,

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       self.pidfile = pidfile

&#x20;       if not isinstance(self.loglevel, numbers.Integral):

&#x20;           self.loglevel = LOG\_LEVELS\[self.loglevel.upper()]



&#x20;   def run(self) -> None:

&#x20;       if not self.quiet:

&#x20;           print(str(self.colored.cyan(

&#x20;               f'celery beat v{VERSION\_BANNER} is starting.')))

&#x20;       self.init\_loader()

&#x20;       self.set\_process\_title()

&#x20;       self.start\_scheduler()



&#x20;   def setup\_logging(self, colorize: bool | None = None) -> None:

&#x20;       if colorize is None and self.no\_color is not None:

&#x20;           colorize = not self.no\_color

&#x20;       self.app.log.setup(self.loglevel, self.logfile,

&#x20;                          self.redirect\_stdouts, self.redirect\_stdouts\_level,

&#x20;                          colorize=colorize)



&#x20;   def start\_scheduler(self) -> None:

&#x20;       if self.pidfile:

&#x20;           platforms.create\_pidlock(self.pidfile)

&#x20;       service = self.Service(

&#x20;           app=self.app,

&#x20;           max\_interval=self.max\_interval,

&#x20;           scheduler\_cls=self.scheduler\_cls,

&#x20;           schedule\_filename=self.schedule,

&#x20;       )



&#x20;       if not self.quiet:

&#x20;           print(self.banner(service))



&#x20;       self.setup\_logging()

&#x20;       if self.socket\_timeout:

&#x20;           logger.debug('Setting default socket timeout to %r',

&#x20;                        self.socket\_timeout)

&#x20;           socket.setdefaulttimeout(self.socket\_timeout)

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           self.install\_sync\_handler(service)

&#x20;           service.start()

&#x20;       except Exception as exc:

&#x20;           logger.critical('beat raised exception %s: %r',

&#x20;                           exc.\_\_class\_\_, exc,

&#x20;                           exc\_info=True)

&#x20;           raise



&#x20;   def banner(self, service: beat.Service) -> str:

&#x20;       c = self.colored

&#x20;       return str(

&#x20;           c.blue('\_\_    ', c.magenta('-'),

&#x20;                  c.blue('    ... \_\_   '), c.magenta('-'),

&#x20;                  c.blue('        \_\\n'),

&#x20;                  c.reset(self.startup\_info(service))),

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def init\_loader(self) -> None:

&#x20;       # Run the worker init handler.

&#x20;       # (Usually imports task modules and such.)

&#x20;       self.app.loader.init\_worker()

&#x20;       self.app.finalize()



&#x20;   def startup\_info(self, service: beat.Service) -> str:

&#x20;       scheduler = service.get\_scheduler(lazy=True)

&#x20;       return STARTUP\_INFO\_FMT.format(

&#x20;           conninfo=self.app.connection().as\_uri(),

&#x20;           timestamp=datetime.now().replace(microsecond=0),

&#x20;           logfile=self.logfile or '\[stderr]',

&#x20;           loglevel=LOG\_LEVELS\[self.loglevel],

&#x20;           loader=qualname(self.app.loader),

&#x20;           scheduler=qualname(scheduler),

&#x20;           scheduler\_info=scheduler.info,

&#x20;           hmax\_interval=humanize\_seconds(scheduler.max\_interval),

&#x20;           max\_interval=scheduler.max\_interval,

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def set\_process\_title(self) -> None:

&#x20;       arg\_start = 'manage' in sys.argv\[0] and 2 or 1

&#x20;       platforms.set\_process\_title(

&#x20;           'celery beat', info=' '.join(sys.argv\[arg\_start:]),

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def install\_sync\_handler(self, service: beat.Service) -> None:

&#x20;       """Install a `SIGTERM` + `SIGINT` handler saving the schedule."""

&#x20;       def \_sync(signum: Signals, frame: FrameType) -> None:

&#x20;           service.sync()

&#x20;           raise SystemExit()

&#x20;       platforms.signals.update(SIGTERM=\_sync, SIGINT=\_sync)



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\apps\\multi.py

```python

"""Start/stop/manage workers."""

import errno

import os

import shlex

import signal

import sys

from collections import OrderedDict, UserList, defaultdict

from functools import partial

from subprocess import Popen

from time import sleep



from kombu.utils.encoding import from\_utf8

from kombu.utils.objects import cached\_property



from celery.platforms import IS\_WINDOWS, Pidfile, signal\_name

from celery.utils.nodenames import gethostname, host\_format, node\_format, nodesplit

from celery.utils.saferepr import saferepr



\_\_all\_\_ = ('Cluster', 'Node')



CELERY\_EXE = 'celery'





def celery\_exe(\*args):

&#x20;   return ' '.join((CELERY\_EXE,) + args)





def build\_nodename(name, prefix, suffix):

&#x20;   hostname = suffix

&#x20;   if '@' in name:

&#x20;       nodename = host\_format(name)

&#x20;       shortname, hostname = nodesplit(nodename)

&#x20;       name = shortname

&#x20;   else:

&#x20;       shortname = f'{prefix}{name}'

&#x20;       nodename = host\_format(

&#x20;           f'{shortname}@{hostname}',

&#x20;       )

&#x20;   return name, nodename, hostname





def build\_expander(nodename, shortname, hostname):

&#x20;   return partial(

&#x20;       node\_format,

&#x20;       name=nodename,

&#x20;       N=shortname,

&#x20;       d=hostname,

&#x20;       h=nodename,

&#x20;       i='%i',

&#x20;       I='%I',

&#x20;   )





def format\_opt(opt, value):

&#x20;   if not value:

&#x20;       return opt

&#x20;   if opt.startswith('--'):

&#x20;       return f'{opt}={value}'

&#x20;   return f'{opt} {value}'





def \_kwargs\_to\_command\_line(kwargs):

&#x20;   return {

&#x20;       ('--{}'.format(k.replace('\_', '-'))

&#x20;        if len(k) > 1 else f'-{k}'): f'{v}'

&#x20;       for k, v in kwargs.items()

&#x20;   }





class NamespacedOptionParser:



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, args):

&#x20;       self.args = args

&#x20;       self.options = OrderedDict()

&#x20;       self.values = \[]

&#x20;       self.passthrough = ''

&#x20;       self.namespaces = defaultdict(lambda: OrderedDict())



&#x20;   def parse(self):

&#x20;       rargs = \[arg for arg in self.args if arg]

&#x20;       pos = 0

&#x20;       while pos < len(rargs):

&#x20;           arg = rargs\[pos]

&#x20;           if arg == '--':

&#x20;               self.passthrough = ' '.join(rargs\[pos:])

&#x20;               break

&#x20;           elif arg\[0] == '-':

&#x20;               if arg\[1] == '-':

&#x20;                   self.process\_long\_opt(arg\[2:])

&#x20;               else:

&#x20;                   value = None

&#x20;                   if len(rargs) > pos + 1 and rargs\[pos + 1]\[0] != '-':

&#x20;                       value = rargs\[pos + 1]

&#x20;                       pos += 1

&#x20;                   self.process\_short\_opt(arg\[1:], value)

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               self.values.append(arg)

&#x20;           pos += 1



&#x20;   def process\_long\_opt(self, arg, value=None):

&#x20;       if '=' in arg:

&#x20;           arg, value = arg.split('=', 1)

&#x20;       self.add\_option(arg, value, short=False)



&#x20;   def process\_short\_opt(self, arg, value=None):

&#x20;       self.add\_option(arg, value, short=True)



&#x20;   def optmerge(self, ns, defaults=None):

&#x20;       if defaults is None:

&#x20;           defaults = self.options

&#x20;       return OrderedDict(defaults, \*\*self.namespaces\[ns])



&#x20;   def add\_option(self, name, value, short=False, ns=None):

&#x20;       prefix = short and '-' or '--'

&#x20;       dest = self.options

&#x20;       if ':' in name:

&#x20;           name, ns = name.split(':')

&#x20;           dest = self.namespaces\[ns]

&#x20;       dest\[prefix + name] = value





class Node:

&#x20;   """Represents a node in a cluster."""



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, name,

&#x20;                cmd=None, append=None, options=None, extra\_args=None):

&#x20;       self.name = name

&#x20;       self.cmd = cmd or f"-m {celery\_exe('worker', '--detach')}"

&#x20;       self.append = append

&#x20;       self.extra\_args = extra\_args or ''

&#x20;       self.options = self.\_annotate\_with\_default\_opts(

&#x20;           options or OrderedDict())

&#x20;       self.expander = self.\_prepare\_expander()

&#x20;       self.argv = self.\_prepare\_argv()

&#x20;       self.\_pid = None



&#x20;   def \_annotate\_with\_default\_opts(self, options):

&#x20;       options\['-n'] = self.name

&#x20;       self.\_setdefaultopt(options, \['--pidfile', '-p'], '/var/run/celery/%n.pid')

&#x20;       self.\_setdefaultopt(options, \['--logfile', '-f'], '/var/log/celery/%n%I.log')

&#x20;       self.\_setdefaultopt(options, \['--executable'], sys.executable)

&#x20;       return options



&#x20;   def \_setdefaultopt(self, d, alt, value):

&#x20;       for opt in alt\[1:]:

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               return d\[opt]

&#x20;           except KeyError:

&#x20;               pass

&#x20;       value = d.setdefault(alt\[0], os.path.normpath(value))

&#x20;       dir\_path = os.path.dirname(value)

&#x20;       if dir\_path and not os.path.exists(dir\_path):

&#x20;           os.makedirs(dir\_path)

&#x20;       return value



&#x20;   def \_prepare\_expander(self):

&#x20;       shortname, hostname = self.name.split('@', 1)

&#x20;       return build\_expander(

&#x20;           self.name, shortname, hostname)



&#x20;   def \_prepare\_argv(self):

&#x20;       cmd = self.expander(self.cmd).split(' ')

&#x20;       i = cmd.index('celery') + 1



&#x20;       options = self.options.copy()

&#x20;       for opt, value in self.options.items():

&#x20;           if opt in (

&#x20;               '-A', '--app',

&#x20;               '-b', '--broker',

&#x20;               '--result-backend',

&#x20;               '--loader',

&#x20;               '--config',

&#x20;               '--workdir',

&#x20;               '-C', '--no-color',

&#x20;               '-q', '--quiet',

&#x20;           ):

&#x20;               cmd.insert(i, format\_opt(opt, self.expander(value)))



&#x20;               options.pop(opt)



&#x20;       cmd = \[' '.join(cmd)]

&#x20;       argv = tuple(

&#x20;           cmd +

&#x20;           \[format\_opt(opt, self.expander(value))

&#x20;            for opt, value in options.items()] +

&#x20;           \[self.extra\_args]

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       if self.append:

&#x20;           argv += (self.expander(self.append),)

&#x20;       return argv



&#x20;   def alive(self):

&#x20;       return self.send(0)



&#x20;   def send(self, sig, on\_error=None):

&#x20;       pid = self.pid

&#x20;       if pid:

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               os.kill(pid, sig)

&#x20;           except OSError as exc:

&#x20;               if exc.errno != errno.ESRCH:

&#x20;                   raise

&#x20;               maybe\_call(on\_error, self)

&#x20;               return False

&#x20;           return True

&#x20;       maybe\_call(on\_error, self)



&#x20;   def start(self, env=None, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       return self.\_waitexec(

&#x20;           self.argv, path=self.executable, env=env, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def \_waitexec(self, argv, path=sys.executable, env=None,

&#x20;                 on\_spawn=None, on\_signalled=None, on\_failure=None):

&#x20;       argstr = self.prepare\_argv(argv, path)

&#x20;       maybe\_call(on\_spawn, self, argstr=' '.join(argstr), env=env)

&#x20;       pipe = Popen(argstr, env=env)

&#x20;       return self.handle\_process\_exit(

&#x20;           pipe.wait(),

&#x20;           on\_signalled=on\_signalled,

&#x20;           on\_failure=on\_failure,

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def handle\_process\_exit(self, retcode, on\_signalled=None, on\_failure=None):

&#x20;       if retcode < 0:

&#x20;           maybe\_call(on\_signalled, self, -retcode)

&#x20;           return -retcode

&#x20;       elif retcode > 0:

&#x20;           maybe\_call(on\_failure, self, retcode)

&#x20;       return retcode



&#x20;   def prepare\_argv(self, argv, path):

&#x20;       args = ' '.join(\[path] + list(argv))

&#x20;       return shlex.split(from\_utf8(args), posix=not IS\_WINDOWS)



&#x20;   def getopt(self, \*alt):

&#x20;       for opt in alt:

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               return self.options\[opt]

&#x20;           except KeyError:

&#x20;               pass

&#x20;       raise KeyError(alt\[0])



&#x20;   def \_\_repr\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return f'<{type(self).\_\_name\_\_}: {self.name}>'



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def pidfile(self):

&#x20;       return self.expander(self.getopt('--pidfile', '-p'))



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def logfile(self):

&#x20;       return self.expander(self.getopt('--logfile', '-f'))



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def pid(self):

&#x20;       if self.\_pid is not None:

&#x20;           return self.\_pid

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           return Pidfile(self.pidfile).read\_pid()

&#x20;       except ValueError:

&#x20;           pass



&#x20;   @pid.setter

&#x20;   def pid(self, value):

&#x20;       self.\_pid = value



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def executable(self):

&#x20;       return self.options\['--executable']



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def argv\_with\_executable(self):

&#x20;       return (self.executable,) + self.argv



&#x20;   @classmethod

&#x20;   def from\_kwargs(cls, name, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       return cls(name, options=\_kwargs\_to\_command\_line(kwargs))





def maybe\_call(fun, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;   if fun is not None:

&#x20;       fun(\*args, \*\*kwargs)





class MultiParser:

&#x20;   Node = Node



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, cmd='celery worker',

&#x20;                append='', prefix='', suffix='',

&#x20;                range\_prefix='celery'):

&#x20;       self.cmd = cmd

&#x20;       self.append = append

&#x20;       self.prefix = prefix

&#x20;       self.suffix = suffix

&#x20;       self.range\_prefix = range\_prefix



&#x20;   def parse(self, p):

&#x20;       names = p.values

&#x20;       options = dict(p.options)

&#x20;       ranges = len(names) == 1

&#x20;       prefix = self.prefix

&#x20;       cmd = options.pop('--cmd', self.cmd)

&#x20;       append = options.pop('--append', self.append)

&#x20;       hostname = options.pop('--hostname', options.pop('-n', gethostname()))

&#x20;       prefix = options.pop('--prefix', prefix) or ''

&#x20;       suffix = options.pop('--suffix', self.suffix) or hostname

&#x20;       suffix = '' if suffix in ('""', "''") else suffix

&#x20;       range\_prefix = options.pop('--range-prefix', '') or self.range\_prefix

&#x20;       if ranges:

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               names, prefix = self.\_get\_ranges(names), range\_prefix

&#x20;           except ValueError:

&#x20;               pass

&#x20;       self.\_update\_ns\_opts(p, names)

&#x20;       self.\_update\_ns\_ranges(p, ranges)



&#x20;       return (

&#x20;           self.\_node\_from\_options(

&#x20;               p, name, prefix, suffix, cmd, append, options)

&#x20;           for name in names

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def \_node\_from\_options(self, p, name, prefix,

&#x20;                          suffix, cmd, append, options):

&#x20;       namespace, nodename, \_ = build\_nodename(name, prefix, suffix)

&#x20;       namespace = nodename if nodename in p.namespaces else namespace

&#x20;       return Node(nodename, cmd, append,

&#x20;                   p.optmerge(namespace, options), p.passthrough)



&#x20;   def \_get\_ranges(self, names):

&#x20;       noderange = int(names\[0])

&#x20;       return \[str(n) for n in range(1, noderange + 1)]



&#x20;   def \_update\_ns\_opts(self, p, names):

&#x20;       # Numbers in args always refers to the index in the list of names.

&#x20;       # (e.g., `start foo bar baz -c:1` where 1 is foo, 2 is bar, and so on).

&#x20;       for ns\_name, ns\_opts in list(p.namespaces.items()):

&#x20;           if ns\_name.isdigit():

&#x20;               ns\_index = int(ns\_name) - 1

&#x20;               if ns\_index < 0:

&#x20;                   raise KeyError(f'Indexes start at 1 got: {ns\_name!r}')

&#x20;               try:

&#x20;                   p.namespaces\[names\[ns\_index]].update(ns\_opts)

&#x20;               except IndexError:

&#x20;                   raise KeyError(f'No node at index {ns\_name!r}')



&#x20;   def \_update\_ns\_ranges(self, p, ranges):

&#x20;       for ns\_name, ns\_opts in list(p.namespaces.items()):

&#x20;           if ',' in ns\_name or (ranges and '-' in ns\_name):

&#x20;               for subns in self.\_parse\_ns\_range(ns\_name, ranges):

&#x20;                   p.namespaces\[subns].update(ns\_opts)

&#x20;               p.namespaces.pop(ns\_name)



&#x20;   def \_parse\_ns\_range(self, ns, ranges=False):

&#x20;       ret = \[]

&#x20;       for space in ',' in ns and ns.split(',') or \[ns]:

&#x20;           if ranges and '-' in space:

&#x20;               start, stop = space.split('-')

&#x20;               ret.extend(

&#x20;                   str(n) for n in range(int(start), int(stop) + 1)

&#x20;               )

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               ret.append(space)

&#x20;       return ret





class Cluster(UserList):

&#x20;   """Represent a cluster of workers."""



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, nodes, cmd=None, env=None,

&#x20;                on\_stopping\_preamble=None,

&#x20;                on\_send\_signal=None,

&#x20;                on\_still\_waiting\_for=None,

&#x20;                on\_still\_waiting\_progress=None,

&#x20;                on\_still\_waiting\_end=None,

&#x20;                on\_node\_start=None,

&#x20;                on\_node\_restart=None,

&#x20;                on\_node\_shutdown\_ok=None,

&#x20;                on\_node\_status=None,

&#x20;                on\_node\_signal=None,

&#x20;                on\_node\_signal\_dead=None,

&#x20;                on\_node\_down=None,

&#x20;                on\_child\_spawn=None,

&#x20;                on\_child\_signalled=None,

&#x20;                on\_child\_failure=None):

&#x20;       self.nodes = nodes

&#x20;       self.cmd = cmd or celery\_exe('worker')

&#x20;       self.env = env



&#x20;       self.on\_stopping\_preamble = on\_stopping\_preamble

&#x20;       self.on\_send\_signal = on\_send\_signal

&#x20;       self.on\_still\_waiting\_for = on\_still\_waiting\_for

&#x20;       self.on\_still\_waiting\_progress = on\_still\_waiting\_progress

&#x20;       self.on\_still\_waiting\_end = on\_still\_waiting\_end

&#x20;       self.on\_node\_start = on\_node\_start

&#x20;       self.on\_node\_restart = on\_node\_restart

&#x20;       self.on\_node\_shutdown\_ok = on\_node\_shutdown\_ok

&#x20;       self.on\_node\_status = on\_node\_status

&#x20;       self.on\_node\_signal = on\_node\_signal

&#x20;       self.on\_node\_signal\_dead = on\_node\_signal\_dead

&#x20;       self.on\_node\_down = on\_node\_down

&#x20;       self.on\_child\_spawn = on\_child\_spawn

&#x20;       self.on\_child\_signalled = on\_child\_signalled

&#x20;       self.on\_child\_failure = on\_child\_failure



&#x20;   def start(self):

&#x20;       return \[self.start\_node(node) for node in self]



&#x20;   def start\_node(self, node):

&#x20;       maybe\_call(self.on\_node\_start, node)

&#x20;       retcode = self.\_start\_node(node)

&#x20;       maybe\_call(self.on\_node\_status, node, retcode)

&#x20;       return retcode

&#x20;           self.env,

&#x20;           on\_spawn=self.on\_child\_spawn,

&#x20;           on\_signalled=self.on\_child\_signalled,

&#x20;           on\_failure=self.on\_child\_failure,

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def send\_all(self, sig):

&#x20;       for node in self.getpids(on\_down=self.on\_node\_down):

&#x20;           maybe\_call(self.on\_node\_signal, node, signal\_name(sig))

&#x20;           node.send(sig, self.on\_node\_signal\_dead)

&#x20;       return self.send\_all(signal.SIGKILL)

&#x20;       retvals = \[]



&#x20;       def restart\_on\_down(node):

&#x20;           maybe\_call(self.on\_node\_restart, node)

&#x20;           retval = self.\_start\_node(node)

&#x20;           maybe\_call(self.on\_node\_status, node, retval)

&#x20;           retvals.append(retval)



&#x20;       self.\_stop\_nodes(retry=2, on\_down=restart\_on\_down, sig=sig)

&#x20;       return retvals



&#x20;   def stop(self, retry=None, callback=None, sig=signal.SIGTERM):

&#x20;       return self.\_stop\_nodes(retry=retry, on\_down=callback, sig=sig)



&#x20;   def stopwait(self, retry=2, callback=None, sig=signal.SIGTERM):

&#x20;       return self.\_stop\_nodes(retry=retry, on\_down=callback, sig=sig)



&#x20;   def \_stop\_nodes(self, retry=None, on\_down=None, sig=signal.SIGTERM):

&#x20;       on\_down = on\_down if on\_down is not None else self.on\_node\_down

&#x20;       nodes = list(self.getpids(on\_down=on\_down))

&#x20;       if nodes:

&#x20;           for node in self.shutdown\_nodes(nodes, sig=sig, retry=retry):

&#x20;               maybe\_call(on\_down, node)



&#x20;   def shutdown\_nodes(self, nodes, sig=signal.SIGTERM, retry=None):

&#x20;       P = set(nodes)

&#x20;       maybe\_call(self.on\_stopping\_preamble, nodes)

&#x20;       to\_remove = set()

&#x20;       for node in P:

&#x20;           maybe\_call(self.on\_send\_signal, node, signal\_name(sig))

&#x20;           if not node.send(sig, self.on\_node\_signal\_dead):

&#x20;               to\_remove.add(node)

&#x20;               yield node

&#x20;       P -= to\_remove

&#x20;       if retry:

&#x20;           maybe\_call(self.on\_still\_waiting\_for, P)

&#x20;           its = 0

&#x20;           while P:

&#x20;               to\_remove = set()

&#x20;               for node in P:

&#x20;                   its += 1

&#x20;                   maybe\_call(self.on\_still\_waiting\_progress, P)

&#x20;                   if not node.alive():

&#x20;                       maybe\_call(self.on\_node\_shutdown\_ok, node)

&#x20;                       to\_remove.add(node)

&#x20;                       yield node

&#x20;                       maybe\_call(self.on\_still\_waiting\_for, P)

&#x20;                       break

&#x20;               P -= to\_remove

&#x20;               if P and not its % len(P):

&#x20;                   sleep(float(retry))

&#x20;           maybe\_call(self.on\_still\_waiting\_end)



&#x20;   def find(self, name):

&#x20;       for node in self:

&#x20;           if node.name == name:

&#x20;               return node

&#x20;       raise KeyError(name)



&#x20;   def getpids(self, on\_down=None):

&#x20;       for node in self:

&#x20;           if node.pid:

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               maybe\_call(on\_down, node)

&#x20;   def data(self):
```
