```python
"""Worker command-line program.



This module is the 'program-version' of :mod:`celery.worker`.



It does everything necessary to run that module

as an actual application, like installing signal handlers,

platform tweaks, and so on.

"""

import logging

import os

import platform as \_platform

import sys

from datetime import datetime

from functools import partial



from billiard.common import REMAP\_SIGTERM

from billiard.process import current\_process

from kombu.utils.encoding import safe\_str



from celery import VERSION\_BANNER, platforms, signals

from celery.app import trace

from celery.loaders.app import AppLoader

from celery.platforms import EX\_FAILURE, EX\_OK, check\_privileges, isatty

from celery.utils import static, term

from celery.utils.debug import cry

from celery.utils.imports import qualname

from celery.utils.log import get\_logger, in\_sighandler, set\_in\_sighandler

from celery.utils.text import pluralize

from celery.worker import WorkController



\_\_all\_\_ = ('Worker',)



logger = get\_logger(\_\_name\_\_)

is\_jython = sys.platform.startswith('java')

is\_pypy = hasattr(sys, 'pypy\_version\_info')



ARTLINES = \[

&#x20;   ' --------------',

&#x20;   '--- \*\*\*\*\* -----',

&#x20;   '-- \*\*\*\*\*\*\* ----',

&#x20;   '- \*\*\* --- \* ---',

&#x20;   '- \*\* ----------',

&#x20;   '- \*\* ----------',

&#x20;   '- \*\* ----------',

&#x20;   '- \*\* ----------',

&#x20;   '- \*\*\* --- \* ---',

&#x20;   '-- \*\*\*\*\*\*\* ----',

&#x20;   '--- \*\*\*\*\* -----',

&#x20;   ' --------------',

]



BANNER = """\\

{hostname} v{version}



{platform} {timestamp}



\[config]

.> app:         {app}

.> transport:   {conninfo}

.> results:     {results}

.> concurrency: {concurrency}

.> task events: {events}



\[queues]

{queues}

"""



EXTRA\_INFO\_FMT = """

\[tasks]

{tasks}

"""





def active\_thread\_count():

&#x20;   from threading import enumerate

&#x20;   return sum(1 for t in enumerate()

&#x20;              if not t.name.startswith('Dummy-'))





def safe\_say(msg, f=sys.\_\_stderr\_\_):

&#x20;   if hasattr(f, 'fileno') and f.fileno() is not None:

&#x20;       os.write(f.fileno(), f'\\n{msg}\\n'.encode())





class Worker(WorkController):

&#x20;   """Worker as a program."""



&#x20;   def on\_before\_init(self, quiet=False, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       self.quiet = quiet

&#x20;       trace.setup\_worker\_optimizations(self.app, self.hostname)



&#x20;       # this signal can be used to set up configuration for

&#x20;       # workers by name.

&#x20;       signals.celeryd\_init.send(

&#x20;           sender=self.hostname, instance=self,

&#x20;           conf=self.app.conf, options=kwargs,

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       check\_privileges(self.app.conf.accept\_content)



&#x20;   def on\_after\_init(self, purge=False, no\_color=None,

&#x20;                     redirect\_stdouts=None, redirect\_stdouts\_level=None,

&#x20;                     \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       self.redirect\_stdouts = self.app.either(

&#x20;           'worker\_redirect\_stdouts', redirect\_stdouts)

&#x20;       self.redirect\_stdouts\_level = self.app.either(

&#x20;           'worker\_redirect\_stdouts\_level', redirect\_stdouts\_level)

&#x20;       super().setup\_defaults(\*\*kwargs)

&#x20;       self.purge = purge

&#x20;       self.no\_color = no\_color

&#x20;       self.\_isatty = isatty(sys.stdout)

&#x20;       self.colored = self.app.log.colored(

&#x20;           self.logfile,

&#x20;           enabled=not no\_color if no\_color is not None else no\_color

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def on\_init\_blueprint(self):

&#x20;       self.\_custom\_logging = self.setup\_logging()

&#x20;       # apply task execution optimizations

&#x20;       # -- This will finalize the app!

&#x20;       trace.setup\_worker\_optimizations(self.app, self.hostname)



&#x20;   def on\_start(self):

&#x20;       app = self.app

&#x20;       super().on\_start()



&#x20;       # this signal can be used to, for example, change queues after

&#x20;       # the -Q option has been applied.

&#x20;       signals.celeryd\_after\_setup.send(

&#x20;           sender=self.hostname, instance=self, conf=app.conf,

&#x20;       )



&#x20;       if self.purge:

&#x20;           self.purge\_messages()



&#x20;       if not self.quiet:

&#x20;           self.emit\_banner()



&#x20;       self.set\_process\_status('-active-')

&#x20;       self.install\_platform\_tweaks(self)

&#x20;       if not self.\_custom\_logging and self.redirect\_stdouts:

&#x20;           app.log.redirect\_stdouts(self.redirect\_stdouts\_level)



&#x20;       # TODO: Remove the following code in Celery 6.0

&#x20;       # This qualifies as a hack for issue #6366.

&#x20;       warn\_deprecated = True

&#x20;       config\_source = app.\_config\_source

&#x20;       if isinstance(config\_source, str):

&#x20;           # Don't raise the warning when the settings originate from

&#x20;           # django.conf:settings

&#x20;           warn\_deprecated = config\_source.lower() not in \[

&#x20;               'django.conf:settings',

&#x20;           ]



&#x20;       if warn\_deprecated:

&#x20;           if app.conf.maybe\_warn\_deprecated\_settings():

&#x20;               logger.warning(

&#x20;                   "Please run `celery upgrade settings path/to/settings.py` "

&#x20;                   "to avoid these warnings and to allow a smoother upgrade "

&#x20;                   "to Celery 6.0."

&#x20;               )



&#x20;   def emit\_banner(self):

&#x20;       # Dump configuration to screen so we have some basic information

&#x20;       # for when users sends bug reports.

&#x20;       use\_image = term.supports\_images()

&#x20;       if use\_image:

&#x20;           print(term.imgcat(static.logo()))

&#x20;       print(safe\_str(''.join(\[

&#x20;           str(self.colored.cyan(

&#x20;               ' \\n', self.startup\_info(artlines=not use\_image))),

&#x20;           str(self.colored.reset(self.extra\_info() or '')),

&#x20;       ])), file=sys.\_\_stdout\_\_, flush=True)



&#x20;   def on\_consumer\_ready(self, consumer):

&#x20;       signals.worker\_ready.send(sender=consumer)

&#x20;       logger.info('%s ready.', safe\_str(self.hostname))



&#x20;   def setup\_logging(self, colorize=None):

&#x20;       if colorize is None and self.no\_color is not None:

&#x20;           colorize = not self.no\_color

&#x20;       return self.app.log.setup(

&#x20;           self.loglevel, self.logfile,

&#x20;           redirect\_stdouts=False, colorize=colorize, hostname=self.hostname,

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def purge\_messages(self):

&#x20;       with self.app.connection\_for\_write() as connection:

&#x20;           count = self.app.control.purge(connection=connection)

&#x20;           if count:  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;               print(f"purge: Erased {count} {pluralize(count, 'message')} from the queue.\\n", flush=True)



&#x20;   def tasklist(self, include\_builtins=True, sep='\\n', int\_='celery.'):

&#x20;       return sep.join(

&#x20;           f'  . {task}' for task in sorted(self.app.tasks)

&#x20;           if (not task.startswith(int\_) if not include\_builtins else task)

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def extra\_info(self):

&#x20;       if self.loglevel is None:

&#x20;           return

&#x20;       if self.loglevel <= logging.INFO:

&#x20;           include\_builtins = self.loglevel <= logging.DEBUG

&#x20;           tasklist = self.tasklist(include\_builtins=include\_builtins)

&#x20;           return EXTRA\_INFO\_FMT.format(tasks=tasklist)



&#x20;   def startup\_info(self, artlines=True):

&#x20;       app = self.app

&#x20;       concurrency = str(self.concurrency)

&#x20;       appr = '{}:{:#x}'.format(app.main or '\_\_main\_\_', id(app))

&#x20;       if not isinstance(app.loader, AppLoader):

&#x20;           loader = qualname(app.loader)

&#x20;           if loader.startswith('celery.loaders'):  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;               loader = loader\[14:]

&#x20;           appr += f' ({loader})'

&#x20;       if self.autoscale:

&#x20;           max, min = self.autoscale

&#x20;           concurrency = f'{{min={min}, max={max}}}'

&#x20;       pool = self.pool\_cls

&#x20;       if not isinstance(pool, str):

&#x20;           pool = pool.\_\_module\_\_

&#x20;       concurrency += f" ({pool.split('.')\[-1]})"

&#x20;       events = 'ON'

&#x20;       if not self.task\_events:

&#x20;           events = 'OFF (enable -E to monitor tasks in this worker)'



&#x20;       banner = BANNER.format(

&#x20;           app=appr,

&#x20;           hostname=safe\_str(self.hostname),

&#x20;           timestamp=datetime.now().replace(microsecond=0),

&#x20;           version=VERSION\_BANNER,

&#x20;           conninfo=self.app.connection().as\_uri(),

&#x20;           results=self.app.backend.as\_uri(),

&#x20;           concurrency=concurrency,

&#x20;           platform=safe\_str(\_platform.platform()),

&#x20;           events=events,

&#x20;           queues=app.amqp.queues.format(indent=0, indent\_first=False),

&#x20;       ).splitlines()



&#x20;       # integrate the ASCII art.

&#x20;       if artlines:

&#x20;           for i, \_ in enumerate(banner):

&#x20;               try:

&#x20;                   banner\[i] = ' '.join(\[ARTLINES\[i], banner\[i]])

&#x20;               except IndexError:

&#x20;                   banner\[i] = ' ' \* 16 + banner\[i]

&#x20;       return '\\n'.join(banner) + '\\n'



&#x20;   def install\_platform\_tweaks(self, worker):

&#x20;       """Install platform specific tweaks and workarounds."""

&#x20;       if self.app.IS\_macOS:

&#x20;           self.macOS\_proxy\_detection\_workaround()



&#x20;       # Install signal handler so SIGHUP restarts the worker.

&#x20;       if not self.\_isatty:

&#x20;           # only install HUP handler if detached from terminal,

&#x20;           # so closing the terminal window doesn't restart the worker

&#x20;           # into the background.

&#x20;           if self.app.IS\_macOS:

&#x20;               # macOS can't exec from a process using threads.

&#x20;               # See https://github.com/celery/celery/issues#issue/152

&#x20;               install\_HUP\_not\_supported\_handler(worker)

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               install\_worker\_restart\_handler(worker)

&#x20;       install\_worker\_term\_handler(worker)

&#x20;       install\_worker\_term\_hard\_handler(worker)

&#x20;       install\_worker\_int\_handler(worker)

&#x20;       install\_cry\_handler()

&#x20;       install\_rdb\_handler()



&#x20;   def macOS\_proxy\_detection\_workaround(self):

&#x20;       """See https://github.com/celery/celery/issues#issue/161."""

&#x20;       os.environ.setdefault('celery\_dummy\_proxy', 'set\_by\_celeryd')



&#x20;   def set\_process\_status(self, info):

&#x20;       return platforms.set\_mp\_process\_title(

&#x20;           'celeryd',

&#x20;           info=f'{info} ({platforms.strargv(sys.argv)})',

&#x20;           hostname=self.hostname,

&#x20;       )





def \_shutdown\_handler(worker: Worker, sig='SIGTERM', how='Warm', callback=None, exitcode=EX\_OK, verbose=True):

&#x20;   """Install signal handler for warm/cold shutdown.



&#x20;   The handler will run from the MainProcess.



&#x20;   Args:

&#x20;       worker (Worker): The worker that received the signal.

&#x20;       sig (str, optional): The signal that was received. Defaults to 'TERM'.

&#x20;       how (str, optional): The type of shutdown to perform. Defaults to 'Warm'.

&#x20;       callback (Callable, optional): Signal handler. Defaults to None.

&#x20;       exitcode (int, optional): The exit code to use. Defaults to EX\_OK.

&#x20;       verbose (bool, optional): Whether to print the type of shutdown. Defaults to True.

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   def \_handle\_request(\*args):

&#x20;       with in\_sighandler():

&#x20;           from celery.worker import state

&#x20;           if current\_process().\_name == 'MainProcess':

&#x20;               if callback:

&#x20;                   callback(worker)

&#x20;               if verbose:

&#x20;                   safe\_say(f'worker: {how} shutdown (MainProcess)', sys.\_\_stdout\_\_)

&#x20;               signals.worker\_shutting\_down.send(

&#x20;                   sender=worker.hostname, sig=sig, how=how,

&#x20;                   exitcode=exitcode,

&#x20;               )

&#x20;           setattr(state, {'Warm': 'should\_stop',

&#x20;                           'Cold': 'should\_terminate'}\[how], exitcode)

&#x20;   \_handle\_request.\_\_name\_\_ = str(f'worker\_{how}')

&#x20;   platforms.signals\[sig] = \_handle\_request





def on\_hard\_shutdown(worker: Worker):

&#x20;   """Signal handler for hard shutdown.



&#x20;   The handler will terminate the worker immediately by force using the exit code ``EX\_FAILURE``.



&#x20;   In practice, you should never get here, as the standard shutdown process should be enough.

&#x20;   This handler is only for the worst-case scenario, where the worker is stuck and cannot be

&#x20;   terminated gracefully (e.g., spamming the Ctrl+C in the terminal to force the worker to terminate).



&#x20;   Args:

&#x20;       worker (Worker): The worker that received the signal.



&#x20;   Raises:

&#x20;       WorkerTerminate: This exception will be raised in the MainProcess to terminate the worker immediately.

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   from celery.exceptions import WorkerTerminate

&#x20;   raise WorkerTerminate(EX\_FAILURE)





def during\_soft\_shutdown(worker: Worker):

&#x20;   """This signal handler is called when the worker is in the middle of the soft shutdown process.



&#x20;   When the worker is in the soft shutdown process, it is waiting for tasks to finish. If the worker

&#x20;   receives a SIGINT (Ctrl+C) or SIGQUIT signal (or possibly SIGTERM if REMAP\_SIGTERM is set to "SIGQUIT"),

&#x20;   the handler will cancels all unacked requests to allow the worker to terminate gracefully and replace the

&#x20;   signal handler for SIGINT and SIGQUIT with the hard shutdown handler ``on\_hard\_shutdown`` to terminate

&#x20;   the worker immediately by force next time the signal is received.



&#x20;   It will give the worker once last chance to gracefully terminate (the cold shutdown), after canceling all

&#x20;   unacked requests, before using the hard shutdown handler to terminate the worker forcefully.



&#x20;   Args:

&#x20;       worker (Worker): The worker that received the signal.

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   # Replace the signal handler for SIGINT (Ctrl+C) and SIGQUIT (and possibly SIGTERM)

&#x20;   # with the hard shutdown handler to terminate the worker immediately by force

&#x20;   install\_worker\_term\_hard\_handler(worker, sig='SIGINT', callback=on\_hard\_shutdown, verbose=False)

&#x20;   install\_worker\_term\_hard\_handler(worker, sig='SIGQUIT', callback=on\_hard\_shutdown)



&#x20;   # Cancel all unacked requests and allow the worker to terminate naturally

&#x20;   worker.consumer.cancel\_all\_unacked\_requests()



&#x20;   # We get here if the worker was in the middle of the soft (cold) shutdown process,

&#x20;   # and the matching signal was received. This can typically happen when the worker is

&#x20;   # waiting for tasks to finish, and the user decides to still cancel the running tasks.

&#x20;   # We give the worker the last chance to gracefully terminate by letting the soft shutdown

&#x20;   # waiting time to finish, which is running in the MainProcess from the previous signal handler call.

&#x20;   safe\_say('Waiting gracefully for cold shutdown to complete...', sys.\_\_stdout\_\_)





def on\_cold\_shutdown(worker: Worker):

&#x20;   """Signal handler for cold shutdown.



&#x20;   Registered for SIGQUIT and SIGINT (Ctrl+C) signals. If REMAP\_SIGTERM is set to "SIGQUIT", this handler will also

&#x20;   be registered for SIGTERM.



&#x20;   This handler will initiate the cold (and soft if enabled) shutdown procesdure for the worker.



&#x20;   Worker running with N tasks:

&#x20;       - SIGTERM:

&#x20;           -The worker will initiate the warm shutdown process until all tasks are finished. Additional.

&#x20;           SIGTERM signals will be ignored. SIGQUIT will transition to the cold shutdown process described below.

&#x20;       - SIGQUIT:

&#x20;           - The worker will initiate the cold shutdown process.

&#x20;           - If the soft shutdown is enabled, the worker will wait for the tasks to finish up to the soft

&#x20;           shutdown timeout (practically having a limited warm shutdown just before the cold shutdown).

&#x20;           - Cancel all tasks (from the MainProcess) and allow the worker to complete the cold shutdown

&#x20;           process gracefully.



&#x20;   Caveats:

&#x20;       - SIGINT (Ctrl+C) signal is defined to replace itself with the cold shutdown (SIGQUIT) after first use,

&#x20;       and to emit a message to the user to hit Ctrl+C again to initiate the cold shutdown process. But, most

&#x20;       important, it will also be caught in WorkController.start() to initiate the warm shutdown process.

&#x20;       - SIGTERM will also be handled in WorkController.start() to initiate the warm shutdown process (the same).

&#x20;       - If REMAP\_SIGTERM is set to "SIGQUIT", the SIGTERM signal will be remapped to SIGQUIT, and the cold

&#x20;       shutdown process will be initiated instead of the warm shutdown process using SIGTERM.

&#x20;       - If SIGQUIT is received (also via SIGINT) during the cold/soft shutdown process, the handler will cancel all

&#x20;       unacked requests but still wait for the soft shutdown process to finish before terminating the worker

&#x20;       gracefully. The next time the signal is received though, the worker will terminate immediately by force.



&#x20;   So, the purpose of this handler is to allow waiting for the soft shutdown timeout, then cancel all tasks from

&#x20;   the MainProcess and let the WorkController.terminate() to terminate the worker naturally. If the soft shutdown

&#x20;   is disabled, it will immediately cancel all tasks let the cold shutdown finish normally.



&#x20;   Args:

&#x20;       worker (Worker): The worker that received the signal.

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   safe\_say('worker: Hitting Ctrl+C again will terminate all running tasks!', sys.\_\_stdout\_\_)



&#x20;   # Replace the signal handler for SIGINT (Ctrl+C) and SIGQUIT (and possibly SIGTERM)

&#x20;   install\_worker\_term\_hard\_handler(worker, sig='SIGINT', callback=during\_soft\_shutdown)

&#x20;   install\_worker\_term\_hard\_handler(worker, sig='SIGQUIT', callback=during\_soft\_shutdown)

&#x20;   if REMAP\_SIGTERM == "SIGQUIT":

&#x20;       install\_worker\_term\_hard\_handler(worker, sig='SIGTERM', callback=during\_soft\_shutdown)

&#x20;   # else, SIGTERM will print the \_shutdown\_handler's message and do nothing, every time it is received..



&#x20;   # Initiate soft shutdown process (if enabled and tasks are running)

&#x20;   worker.wait\_for\_soft\_shutdown()



&#x20;   # Cancel all unacked requests and allow the worker to terminate naturally

&#x20;   worker.consumer.cancel\_all\_unacked\_requests()



&#x20;   # Stop the pool to allow successful tasks call on\_success()

&#x20;   worker.consumer.pool.stop()





\# Allow SIGTERM to be remapped to SIGQUIT to initiate cold shutdown instead of warm shutdown using SIGTERM

if REMAP\_SIGTERM == "SIGQUIT":

&#x20;   install\_worker\_term\_handler = partial(

&#x20;       \_shutdown\_handler, sig='SIGTERM', how='Cold', callback=on\_cold\_shutdown, exitcode=EX\_FAILURE,

&#x20;   )

else:

&#x20;   install\_worker\_term\_handler = partial(

&#x20;       \_shutdown\_handler, sig='SIGTERM', how='Warm',

&#x20;   )





if not is\_jython:  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;   install\_worker\_term\_hard\_handler = partial(

&#x20;       \_shutdown\_handler, sig='SIGQUIT', how='Cold', callback=on\_cold\_shutdown, exitcode=EX\_FAILURE,

&#x20;   )

else:  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;   install\_worker\_term\_handler = \\

&#x20;       install\_worker\_term\_hard\_handler = lambda \*a, \*\*kw: None





def on\_SIGINT(worker):

&#x20;   safe\_say('worker: Hitting Ctrl+C again will initiate cold shutdown, terminating all running tasks!',

&#x20;            sys.\_\_stdout\_\_)

&#x20;   install\_worker\_term\_hard\_handler(worker, sig='SIGINT', verbose=False)





if not is\_jython:  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;   install\_worker\_int\_handler = partial(

&#x20;       \_shutdown\_handler, sig='SIGINT', callback=on\_SIGINT,

&#x20;       exitcode=EX\_FAILURE,

&#x20;   )

else:  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;   def install\_worker\_int\_handler(\*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       pass





def \_reload\_current\_worker():

&#x20;   platforms.close\_open\_fds(\[

&#x20;       sys.\_\_stdin\_\_, sys.\_\_stdout\_\_, sys.\_\_stderr\_\_,

&#x20;   ])

&#x20;   os.execv(sys.executable, \[sys.executable] + sys.argv)





def install\_worker\_restart\_handler(worker, sig='SIGHUP'):



&#x20;   def restart\_worker\_sig\_handler(\*args):

&#x20;       """Signal handler restarting the current python program."""

&#x20;       set\_in\_sighandler(True)

&#x20;       safe\_say(f"Restarting celery worker ({' '.join(sys.argv)})",

&#x20;                sys.\_\_stdout\_\_)

&#x20;       import atexit

&#x20;       atexit.register(\_reload\_current\_worker)

&#x20;       from celery.worker import state

&#x20;       state.should\_stop = EX\_OK

&#x20;   platforms.signals\[sig] = restart\_worker\_sig\_handler





def install\_cry\_handler(sig='SIGUSR1'):

&#x20;   # PyPy does not have sys.\_current\_frames

&#x20;   if is\_pypy:  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;       return



&#x20;   def cry\_handler(\*args):

&#x20;       """Signal handler logging the stack-trace of all active threads."""

&#x20;       with in\_sighandler():

&#x20;           safe\_say(cry())

&#x20;   platforms.signals\[sig] = cry\_handler





def install\_rdb\_handler(envvar='CELERY\_RDBSIG',

&#x20;                       sig='SIGUSR2'):  # pragma: no cover



&#x20;   def rdb\_handler(\*args):

&#x20;       """Signal handler setting a rdb breakpoint at the current frame."""

&#x20;       with in\_sighandler():

&#x20;           from celery.contrib.rdb import \_frame, set\_trace



&#x20;           # gevent does not pass standard signal handler args

&#x20;           frame = args\[1] if args else \_frame().f\_back

&#x20;           set\_trace(frame)

&#x20;   if os.environ.get(envvar):

&#x20;       platforms.signals\[sig] = rdb\_handler





def install\_HUP\_not\_supported\_handler(worker, sig='SIGHUP'):



&#x20;   def warn\_on\_HUP\_handler(signum, frame):

&#x20;       with in\_sighandler():

&#x20;           safe\_say('{sig} not supported: Restarting with {sig} is '

&#x20;                    'unstable on this platform!'.format(sig=sig))

&#x20;   platforms.signals\[sig] = warn\_on\_HUP\_handler



```

\### 代码文件: celery\\backends\\arangodb.py

```python

"""ArangoDb result store backend."""



\# pylint: disable=W1202,W0703



from datetime import timedelta



from kombu.utils.objects import cached\_property

from kombu.utils.url import \_parse\_url



from celery.exceptions import ImproperlyConfigured



from .base import KeyValueStoreBackend



try:

&#x20;   from pyArango import connection as py\_arango\_connection

&#x20;   from pyArango.theExceptions import AQLQueryError

except ImportError:

&#x20;   py\_arango\_connection = AQLQueryError = None



\_\_all\_\_ = ('ArangoDbBackend',)





class ArangoDbBackend(KeyValueStoreBackend):

&#x20;   """ArangoDb backend.



&#x20;   Sample url

&#x20;   "arangodb://username:password@host:port/database/collection"

&#x20;   \*arangodb\_backend\_settings\* is where the settings are present

&#x20;   (in the app.conf)

&#x20;   Settings should contain the host, port, username, password, database name,

&#x20;   collection name else the default will be chosen.

&#x20;   Default database name and collection name is celery.



&#x20;   Raises

&#x20;   ------

&#x20;   celery.exceptions.ImproperlyConfigured:

&#x20;       if module :pypi:`pyArango` is not available.



&#x20;   """



&#x20;   host = '127.0.0.1'

&#x20;   port = '8529'

&#x20;   database = 'celery'

&#x20;   collection = 'celery'

&#x20;   username = None

&#x20;   password = None

&#x20;   # protocol is not supported in backend url (http is taken as default)

&#x20;   http\_protocol = 'http'

&#x20;   verify = False



&#x20;   # Use str as arangodb key not bytes

&#x20;   key\_t = str



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, url=None, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       """Parse the url or load the settings from settings object."""

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(\*args, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;       if py\_arango\_connection is None:

&#x20;           raise ImproperlyConfigured(

&#x20;               'You need to install the pyArango library to use the '

&#x20;               'ArangoDb backend.',

&#x20;           )



&#x20;       self.url = url



&#x20;       if url is None:

&#x20;           host = port = database = collection = username = password = None

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           (

&#x20;               \_schema, host, port, username, password,

&#x20;               database\_collection, \_query

&#x20;           ) = \_parse\_url(url)

&#x20;           if database\_collection is None:

&#x20;               database = collection = None

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               database, collection = database\_collection.split('/')



&#x20;       config = self.app.conf.get('arangodb\_backend\_settings', None)

&#x20;       if config is not None:

&#x20;           if not isinstance(config, dict):

&#x20;               raise ImproperlyConfigured(

&#x20;                   'ArangoDb backend settings should be grouped in a dict',

&#x20;               )

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           config = {}



&#x20;       self.host = host or config.get('host', self.host)

&#x20;       self.port = int(port or config.get('port', self.port))

&#x20;       self.http\_protocol = config.get('http\_protocol', self.http\_protocol)

&#x20;       self.verify = config.get('verify', self.verify)

&#x20;       self.database = database or config.get('database', self.database)

&#x20;       self.collection = \\

&#x20;           collection or config.get('collection', self.collection)

&#x20;       self.username = username or config.get('username', self.username)

&#x20;       self.password = password or config.get('password', self.password)

&#x20;       self.arangodb\_url = "{http\_protocol}://{host}:{port}".format(

&#x20;           http\_protocol=self.http\_protocol, host=self.host, port=self.port

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       self.\_connection = None



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def connection(self):

&#x20;       """Connect to the arangodb server."""

&#x20;       if self.\_connection is None:

&#x20;           self.\_connection = py\_arango\_connection.Connection(

&#x20;               arangoURL=self.arangodb\_url, username=self.username,

&#x20;               password=self.password, verify=self.verify

&#x20;           )

&#x20;       return self.\_connection



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def db(self):

&#x20;       """Database Object to the given database."""

&#x20;       return self.connection\[self.database]



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def expires\_delta(self):

&#x20;       return timedelta(seconds=0 if self.expires is None else self.expires)



&#x20;   def get(self, key):

&#x20;       if key is None:

&#x20;           return None

&#x20;       query = self.db.AQLQuery(

&#x20;           "RETURN DOCUMENT(@@collection, @key).task",

&#x20;           rawResults=True,

&#x20;           bindVars={

&#x20;               "@collection": self.collection,

&#x20;               "key": key,

&#x20;           },

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       return next(query) if len(query) > 0 else None



&#x20;   def set(self, key, value):

&#x20;       self.db.AQLQuery(

&#x20;           """

&#x20;           UPSERT {\_key: @key}

&#x20;           INSERT {\_key: @key, task: @value}

&#x20;           UPDATE {task: @value} IN @@collection

&#x20;           """,

&#x20;           bindVars={

&#x20;               "@collection": self.collection,

&#x20;               "key": key,

&#x20;               "value": value,

&#x20;           },

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def mget(self, keys):

&#x20;       if keys is None:

&#x20;           return

&#x20;       query = self.db.AQLQuery(

&#x20;           "FOR k IN @keys RETURN DOCUMENT(@@collection, k).task",

&#x20;           rawResults=True,

&#x20;           bindVars={

&#x20;               "@collection": self.collection,

&#x20;               "keys": keys if isinstance(keys, list) else list(keys),

&#x20;           },

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       while True:

&#x20;           yield from query

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               query.nextBatch()

&#x20;           except StopIteration:

&#x20;               break



&#x20;   def delete(self, key):

&#x20;       if key is None:

&#x20;           return

&#x20;       self.db.AQLQuery(

&#x20;           "REMOVE {\_key: @key} IN @@collection",

&#x20;           bindVars={

&#x20;               "@collection": self.collection,

&#x20;               "key": key,

&#x20;           },

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def cleanup(self):

&#x20;       if not self.expires:

&#x20;           return

&#x20;       checkpoint = (self.app.now() - self.expires\_delta).isoformat()

&#x20;       self.db.AQLQuery(

&#x20;           """

&#x20;           FOR record IN @@collection

&#x20;               FILTER record.task.date\_done < @checkpoint

&#x20;               REMOVE record IN @@collection

&#x20;           """,

&#x20;           bindVars={

&#x20;               "@collection": self.collection,

&#x20;               "checkpoint": checkpoint,

&#x20;           },

&#x20;       )



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\backends\\asynchronous.py

```python

"""Async I/O backend support utilities."""



import logging

import socket

import threading

import time

from collections import deque

from queue import Empty

from time import sleep

from weakref import WeakKeyDictionary



from kombu.utils.compat import detect\_environment



from celery import states

from celery.exceptions import TimeoutError

from celery.utils.threads import THREAD\_TIMEOUT\_MAX



E\_CELERY\_RESTART\_REQUIRED = "Celery must be restarted because a shutdown signal was detected."



\_\_all\_\_ = (

&#x20;   'AsyncBackendMixin', 'BaseResultConsumer', 'Drainer',

&#x20;   'register\_drainer',

)





class EventletAdaptedEvent:

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   An adapted eventlet event, designed to match the API of `threading.Event` and

&#x20;   `gevent.event.Event`.

&#x20;   """



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self):

&#x20;       import eventlet

&#x20;       self.evt = eventlet.Event()



&#x20;   def is\_set(self):

&#x20;       return self.evt.ready()



&#x20;   def set(self):

&#x20;       return self.evt.send()



&#x20;   def wait(self, timeout=None):

&#x20;       return self.evt.wait(timeout)





drainers = {}





def register\_drainer(name):

&#x20;   """Decorator used to register a new result drainer type."""

&#x20;   def \_inner(cls):

&#x20;       drainers\[name] = cls

&#x20;       return cls

&#x20;   return \_inner





@register\_drainer('default')

class Drainer:

&#x20;   """Result draining service."""



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, result\_consumer):

&#x20;       self.result\_consumer = result\_consumer



&#x20;   def start(self):

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   def stop(self):

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   def drain\_events\_until(self, p, timeout=None, interval=1, on\_interval=None, wait=None):

&#x20;       wait = wait or self.result\_consumer.drain\_events

&#x20;       time\_start = time.monotonic()



&#x20;       while 1:

&#x20;           # Total time spent may exceed a single call to wait()

&#x20;           if timeout and time.monotonic() - time\_start >= timeout:

&#x20;               raise socket.timeout()

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               yield self.wait\_for(p, wait, timeout=interval)

&#x20;           except socket.timeout:

&#x20;               pass

&#x20;           if on\_interval:

&#x20;               on\_interval()

&#x20;           if p.ready:  # got event on the wanted channel.

&#x20;               break



&#x20;   def wait\_for(self, p, wait, timeout=None):

&#x20;       wait(timeout=timeout)



&#x20;   def \_event(self):

&#x20;       return threading.Event()





class greenletDrainer(Drainer):

&#x20;   spawn = None

&#x20;   \_exc = None

&#x20;   \_g = None

&#x20;   \_drain\_complete\_event = None    # event, sended (and recreated) after every drain\_events iteration



&#x20;   def \_send\_drain\_complete\_event(self):

&#x20;       self.\_drain\_complete\_event.set()

&#x20;       self.\_drain\_complete\_event = self.\_event()



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(\*args, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;       self.\_started = self.\_event()

&#x20;       self.\_stopped = self.\_event()

&#x20;       self.\_shutdown = self.\_event()

&#x20;       self.\_drain\_complete\_event = self.\_event()



&#x20;   def run(self):

&#x20;       self.\_started.set()



&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           while not self.\_stopped.is\_set():

&#x20;               try:

&#x20;                   self.result\_consumer.drain\_events(timeout=1)

&#x20;                   self.\_send\_drain\_complete\_event()

&#x20;               except socket.timeout:

&#x20;                   pass

&#x20;       except Exception as e:

&#x20;           self.\_exc = e

&#x20;           raise

&#x20;       finally:

&#x20;           self.\_send\_drain\_complete\_event()

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               self.\_shutdown.set()

&#x20;           except RuntimeError as e:

&#x20;               logging.error(f"Failed to set shutdown event: {e}")



&#x20;   def start(self):

&#x20;       self.\_ensure\_not\_shut\_down()



&#x20;       if not self.\_started.is\_set():

&#x20;           self.\_g = self.spawn(self.run)

&#x20;           self.\_started.wait()



&#x20;   def stop(self):

&#x20;       self.\_stopped.set()

&#x20;       self.\_shutdown.wait(THREAD\_TIMEOUT\_MAX)



&#x20;   def wait\_for(self, p, wait, timeout=None):

&#x20;       self.start()

&#x20;       if not p.ready:

&#x20;           self.\_drain\_complete\_event.wait(timeout=timeout)



&#x20;           self.\_ensure\_not\_shut\_down()



&#x20;   def \_ensure\_not\_shut\_down(self):

&#x20;       """Currently used to ensure the drainer has not run to completion.



&#x20;       Raises if the shutdown event has been signaled (either due to an exception

&#x20;       or stop() being called).



&#x20;       The \_shutdown event acts as synchronization to ensure \_exc is properly

&#x20;       set before it is read from, avoiding need for locks.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       if self.\_shutdown.is\_set():

&#x20;           if self.\_exc is not None:

&#x20;               raise self.\_exc

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               raise Exception(E\_CELERY\_RESTART\_REQUIRED)





@register\_drainer('eventlet')

class eventletDrainer(greenletDrainer):



&#x20;   def spawn(self, func):

&#x20;       from eventlet import sleep, spawn

&#x20;       g = spawn(func)

&#x20;       sleep(0)

&#x20;       return g



&#x20;   def \_event(self):

&#x20;       return EventletAdaptedEvent()





@register\_drainer('gevent')

class geventDrainer(greenletDrainer):



&#x20;   def spawn(self, func):

&#x20;       import gevent

&#x20;       g = gevent.spawn(func)

&#x20;       gevent.sleep(0)

&#x20;       return g



&#x20;   def \_event(self):

&#x20;       from gevent.event import Event

&#x20;       return Event()





class AsyncBackendMixin:

&#x20;   """Mixin for backends that enables the async API."""



&#x20;   def \_collect\_into(self, result, bucket):

&#x20;       self.result\_consumer.buckets\[result] = bucket



&#x20;   def iter\_native(self, result, no\_ack=True, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       self.\_ensure\_not\_eager()



&#x20;       results = result.results

&#x20;       if not results:

&#x20;           raise StopIteration()



&#x20;       # we tell the result consumer to put consumed results

&#x20;       # into these buckets.

&#x20;       bucket = deque()

&#x20;       for node in results:

&#x20;           if not hasattr(node, '\_cache'):

&#x20;               bucket.append(node)

&#x20;           elif node.\_cache:

&#x20;               bucket.append(node)

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               self.\_collect\_into(node, bucket)



&#x20;       for \_ in self.\_wait\_for\_pending(result, no\_ack=no\_ack, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;           while bucket:

&#x20;               node = bucket.popleft()

&#x20;               if not hasattr(node, '\_cache'):

&#x20;                   yield node.id, node.children

&#x20;               else:

&#x20;                   yield node.id, node.\_cache

&#x20;       while bucket:

&#x20;           node = bucket.popleft()

&#x20;           yield node.id, node.\_cache



&#x20;   def add\_pending\_result(self, result, weak=False, start\_drainer=True):

&#x20;       if start\_drainer:

&#x20;           self.result\_consumer.drainer.start()

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           self.\_maybe\_resolve\_from\_buffer(result)

&#x20;       except Empty:

&#x20;           self.\_add\_pending\_result(result.id, result, weak=weak)

&#x20;       return result



&#x20;   def \_maybe\_resolve\_from\_buffer(self, result):

&#x20;       result.\_maybe\_set\_cache(self.\_pending\_messages.take(result.id))



&#x20;   def \_add\_pending\_result(self, task\_id, result, weak=False):

&#x20;       concrete, weak\_ = self.\_pending\_results

&#x20;       if task\_id not in weak\_ and result.id not in concrete:

&#x20;           (weak\_ if weak else concrete)\[task\_id] = result

&#x20;           self.result\_consumer.consume\_from(task\_id)



&#x20;   def add\_pending\_results(self, results, weak=False):

&#x20;       self.result\_consumer.drainer.start()

&#x20;       return \[self.add\_pending\_result(result, weak=weak, start\_drainer=False)

&#x20;               for result in results]



&#x20;   def remove\_pending\_result(self, result):

&#x20;       self.\_remove\_pending\_result(result.id)

&#x20;       self.on\_result\_fulfilled(result)

&#x20;       return result



&#x20;   def \_remove\_pending\_result(self, task\_id):

&#x20;       for mapping in self.\_pending\_results:

&#x20;           mapping.pop(task\_id, None)



&#x20;   def on\_result\_fulfilled(self, result):

&#x20;       self.result\_consumer.cancel\_for(result.id)



&#x20;   def wait\_for\_pending(self, result,

&#x20;                        callback=None, propagate=True, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       self.\_ensure\_not\_eager()

&#x20;       for \_ in self.\_wait\_for\_pending(result, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;           pass

&#x20;       return result.maybe\_throw(callback=callback, propagate=propagate)



&#x20;   def \_wait\_for\_pending(self, result,

&#x20;                         timeout=None, on\_interval=None, on\_message=None,

&#x20;                         \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       return self.result\_consumer.\_wait\_for\_pending(

&#x20;           result, timeout=timeout,

&#x20;           on\_interval=on\_interval, on\_message=on\_message,

&#x20;           \*\*kwargs

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def is\_async(self):

&#x20;       return True





class BaseResultConsumer:

&#x20;   """Manager responsible for consuming result messages."""



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, backend, app, accept,

&#x20;                pending\_results, pending\_messages):

&#x20;       self.backend = backend

&#x20;       self.app = app

&#x20;       self.accept = accept

&#x20;       self.\_pending\_results = pending\_results

&#x20;       self.\_pending\_messages = pending\_messages

&#x20;       self.on\_message = None

&#x20;       self.buckets = WeakKeyDictionary()

&#x20;       self.drainer = drainers\[detect\_environment()](self)



&#x20;   def start(self, initial\_task\_id, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       raise NotImplementedError()



&#x20;   def stop(self):

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   def drain\_events(self, timeout=None):

&#x20;       raise NotImplementedError()



&#x20;   def consume\_from(self, task\_id):

&#x20;       raise NotImplementedError()



&#x20;   def cancel\_for(self, task\_id):

&#x20;       raise NotImplementedError()



&#x20;   def \_after\_fork(self):

&#x20;       self.buckets.clear()

&#x20;       self.buckets = WeakKeyDictionary()

&#x20;       self.on\_message = None

&#x20;       self.on\_after\_fork()



&#x20;   def on\_after\_fork(self):

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   def drain\_events\_until(self, p, timeout=None, on\_interval=None):

&#x20;       return self.drainer.drain\_events\_until(

&#x20;           p, timeout=timeout, on\_interval=on\_interval)



&#x20;   def \_wait\_for\_pending(self, result,

&#x20;                         timeout=None, on\_interval=None, on\_message=None,

&#x20;                         \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       self.on\_wait\_for\_pending(result, timeout=timeout, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;       prev\_on\_m, self.on\_message = self.on\_message, on\_message

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           for \_ in self.drain\_events\_until(

&#x20;                   result.on\_ready, timeout=timeout,

&#x20;                   on\_interval=on\_interval):

&#x20;               yield

&#x20;               sleep(0)

&#x20;       except socket.timeout:

&#x20;           raise TimeoutError('The operation timed out.')

&#x20;       finally:

&#x20;           self.on\_message = prev\_on\_m



&#x20;   def on\_wait\_for\_pending(self, result, timeout=None, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   def on\_out\_of\_band\_result(self, message):

&#x20;       self.on\_state\_change(message.payload, message)



&#x20;   def \_get\_pending\_result(self, task\_id):

&#x20;       for mapping in self.\_pending\_results:

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               return mapping\[task\_id]

&#x20;           except KeyError:

&#x20;               pass

&#x20;       raise KeyError(task\_id)



&#x20;   def on\_state\_change(self, meta, message):

&#x20;       if self.on\_message:

&#x20;           self.on\_message(meta)

&#x20;       if meta\['status'] in states.READY\_STATES:

&#x20;           task\_id = meta\['task\_id']

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               result = self.\_get\_pending\_result(task\_id)

&#x20;           except KeyError:

&#x20;               # send to buffer in case we received this result

&#x20;               # before it was added to \_pending\_results.

&#x20;               self.\_pending\_messages.put(task\_id, meta)

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               result.\_maybe\_set\_cache(meta)

&#x20;               buckets = self.buckets

&#x20;               try:

&#x20;                   # remove bucket for this result, since it's fulfilled

&#x20;                   bucket = buckets.pop(result)

&#x20;               except KeyError:

&#x20;                   pass

&#x20;               else:

&#x20;                   # send to waiter via bucket

&#x20;                   bucket.append(result)

&#x20;       sleep(0)



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\backends\\azureblockblob.py

```python

"""The Azure Storage Block Blob backend for Celery."""

from kombu.transport.azurestoragequeues import Transport as AzureStorageQueuesTransport

from kombu.utils import cached\_property

from kombu.utils.encoding import bytes\_to\_str



from celery.exceptions import ImproperlyConfigured

from celery.utils.log import get\_logger



from .base import KeyValueStoreBackend



try:

&#x20;   import azure.storage.blob as azurestorage

&#x20;   from azure.core.exceptions import ResourceExistsError, ResourceNotFoundError

&#x20;   from azure.storage.blob import BlobServiceClient

except ImportError:

&#x20;   azurestorage = None



\_\_all\_\_ = ("AzureBlockBlobBackend",)



LOGGER = get\_logger(\_\_name\_\_)

AZURE\_BLOCK\_BLOB\_CONNECTION\_PREFIX = 'azureblockblob://'





class AzureBlockBlobBackend(KeyValueStoreBackend):

&#x20;   """Azure Storage Block Blob backend for Celery."""



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self,

&#x20;                url=None,

&#x20;                container\_name=None,

&#x20;                \*args,

&#x20;                \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       Supported URL formats:



&#x20;       azureblockblob://CONNECTION\_STRING

&#x20;       azureblockblob://DefaultAzureCredential@STORAGE\_ACCOUNT\_URL

&#x20;       azureblockblob://ManagedIdentityCredential@STORAGE\_ACCOUNT\_URL

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(\*args, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;       if azurestorage is None or azurestorage.\_\_version\_\_ < '12':

&#x20;           raise ImproperlyConfigured(

&#x20;               "You need to install the azure-storage-blob v12 library to"

&#x20;               "use the AzureBlockBlob backend")



&#x20;       conf = self.app.conf



&#x20;       self.\_connection\_string = self.\_parse\_url(url)



&#x20;       self.\_container\_name = (

&#x20;           container\_name or

&#x20;           conf\["azureblockblob\_container\_name"])



&#x20;       self.base\_path = conf.get('azureblockblob\_base\_path', '')

&#x20;       self.\_connection\_timeout = conf.get(

&#x20;           'azureblockblob\_connection\_timeout', 20

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       self.\_read\_timeout = conf.get('azureblockblob\_read\_timeout', 120)



&#x20;   @classmethod

&#x20;   def \_parse\_url(cls, url, prefix=AZURE\_BLOCK\_BLOB\_CONNECTION\_PREFIX):

&#x20;       connection\_string = url\[len(prefix):]

&#x20;       if not connection\_string:

&#x20;           raise ImproperlyConfigured("Invalid URL")



&#x20;       return connection\_string



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def \_blob\_service\_client(self):

&#x20;       """Return the Azure Storage Blob service client.



&#x20;       If this is the first call to the property, the client is created and

&#x20;       the container is created if it doesn't yet exist.



&#x20;       """

&#x20;       if (

&#x20;           "DefaultAzureCredential" in self.\_connection\_string or

&#x20;           "ManagedIdentityCredential" in self.\_connection\_string

&#x20;       ):

&#x20;           # Leveraging the work that Kombu already did for us

&#x20;           credential\_, url = AzureStorageQueuesTransport.parse\_uri(

&#x20;               self.\_connection\_string

&#x20;           )

&#x20;           client = BlobServiceClient(

&#x20;               account\_url=url,

&#x20;               credential=credential\_,

&#x20;               connection\_timeout=self.\_connection\_timeout,

&#x20;               read\_timeout=self.\_read\_timeout,

&#x20;           )

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           client = BlobServiceClient.from\_connection\_string(

&#x20;               self.\_connection\_string,

&#x20;               connection\_timeout=self.\_connection\_timeout,

&#x20;               read\_timeout=self.\_read\_timeout,

&#x20;           )



&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           client.create\_container(name=self.\_container\_name)

&#x20;           msg = f"Container created with name {self.\_container\_name}."

&#x20;       except ResourceExistsError:

&#x20;           msg = f"Container with name {self.\_container\_name} already." \\

&#x20;               "exists. This will not be created."

&#x20;       LOGGER.info(msg)



&#x20;       return client



&#x20;   def get(self, key):

&#x20;       """Read the value stored at the given key.



&#x20;       Args:

&#x20;             key: The key for which to read the value.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       key = bytes\_to\_str(key)

&#x20;       LOGGER.debug("Getting Azure Block Blob %s/%s", self.\_container\_name, key)



&#x20;       blob\_client = self.\_blob\_service\_client.get\_blob\_client(

&#x20;           container=self.\_container\_name,

&#x20;           blob=f'{self.base\_path}{key}',

&#x20;       )



&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           return blob\_client.download\_blob().readall().decode()

&#x20;       except ResourceNotFoundError:

&#x20;           return None



&#x20;   def set(self, key, value):

&#x20;       """Store a value for a given key.



&#x20;       Args:

&#x20;             key: The key at which to store the value.

&#x20;             value: The value to store.



&#x20;       """

&#x20;       key = bytes\_to\_str(key)

&#x20;       LOGGER.debug(f"Creating azure blob at {self.\_container\_name}/{key}")



&#x20;       blob\_client = self.\_blob\_service\_client.get\_blob\_client(

&#x20;           container=self.\_container\_name,

&#x20;           blob=f'{self.base\_path}{key}',

&#x20;       )



&#x20;       blob\_client.upload\_blob(value, overwrite=True)



&#x20;   def mget(self, keys):

&#x20;       """Read all the values for the provided keys.



&#x20;       Args:

&#x20;             keys: The list of keys to read.



&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return \[self.get(key) for key in keys]



&#x20;   def delete(self, key):

&#x20;       """Delete the value at a given key.



&#x20;       Args:

&#x20;             key: The key of the value to delete.



&#x20;       """

&#x20;       key = bytes\_to\_str(key)

&#x20;       LOGGER.debug(f"Deleting azure blob at {self.\_container\_name}/{key}")



&#x20;       blob\_client = self.\_blob\_service\_client.get\_blob\_client(

&#x20;           container=self.\_container\_name,

&#x20;           blob=f'{self.base\_path}{key}',

&#x20;       )



&#x20;       blob\_client.delete\_blob()



&#x20;   def as\_uri(self, include\_password=False):

&#x20;       if include\_password:

&#x20;           return (

&#x20;               f'{AZURE\_BLOCK\_BLOB\_CONNECTION\_PREFIX}'

&#x20;               f'{self.\_connection\_string}'

&#x20;           )



&#x20;       connection\_string\_parts = self.\_connection\_string.split(';')

&#x20;       account\_key\_prefix = 'AccountKey='

&#x20;       redacted\_connection\_string\_parts = \[

&#x20;           f'{account\_key\_prefix}\*\*' if part.startswith(account\_key\_prefix)

&#x20;           else part

&#x20;           for part in connection\_string\_parts

&#x20;       ]



&#x20;       return (

&#x20;           f'{AZURE\_BLOCK\_BLOB\_CONNECTION\_PREFIX}'

&#x20;           f'{";".join(redacted\_connection\_string\_parts)}'

&#x20;       )
```
