```python

"""The CosmosDB/SQL backend for Celery (experimental)."""

from kombu.utils import cached\_property

from kombu.utils.encoding import bytes\_to\_str

from kombu.utils.url import \_parse\_url



from celery.exceptions import ImproperlyConfigured

from celery.utils.log import get\_logger



from .base import KeyValueStoreBackend



try:

&#x20;   import pydocumentdb

&#x20;   from pydocumentdb.document\_client import DocumentClient

&#x20;   from pydocumentdb.documents import ConnectionPolicy, ConsistencyLevel, PartitionKind

&#x20;   from pydocumentdb.errors import HTTPFailure

&#x20;   from pydocumentdb.retry\_options import RetryOptions

except ImportError:

&#x20;   pydocumentdb = DocumentClient = ConsistencyLevel = PartitionKind = \\

&#x20;       HTTPFailure = ConnectionPolicy = RetryOptions = None



\_\_all\_\_ = ("CosmosDBSQLBackend",)





ERROR\_NOT\_FOUND = 404

ERROR\_EXISTS = 409



LOGGER = get\_logger(\_\_name\_\_)





class CosmosDBSQLBackend(KeyValueStoreBackend):

&#x20;   """CosmosDB/SQL backend for Celery."""



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self,

&#x20;                url=None,

&#x20;                database\_name=None,

&#x20;                collection\_name=None,

&#x20;                consistency\_level=None,

&#x20;                max\_retry\_attempts=None,

&#x20;                max\_retry\_wait\_time=None,

&#x20;                \*args,

&#x20;                \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(\*args, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;       if pydocumentdb is None:

&#x20;           raise ImproperlyConfigured(

&#x20;               "You need to install the pydocumentdb library to use the "

&#x20;               "CosmosDB backend.")



&#x20;       conf = self.app.conf



&#x20;       self.\_endpoint, self.\_key = self.\_parse\_url(url)



&#x20;       self.\_database\_name = (

&#x20;           database\_name or

&#x20;           conf\["cosmosdbsql\_database\_name"])



&#x20;       self.\_collection\_name = (

&#x20;           collection\_name or

&#x20;           conf\["cosmosdbsql\_collection\_name"])



&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           self.\_consistency\_level = getattr(

&#x20;               ConsistencyLevel,

&#x20;               consistency\_level or

&#x20;               conf\["cosmosdbsql\_consistency\_level"])

&#x20;       except AttributeError:

&#x20;           raise ImproperlyConfigured("Unknown CosmosDB consistency level")



&#x20;       self.\_max\_retry\_attempts = (

&#x20;           max\_retry\_attempts or

&#x20;           conf\["cosmosdbsql\_max\_retry\_attempts"])



&#x20;       self.\_max\_retry\_wait\_time = (

&#x20;           max\_retry\_wait\_time or

&#x20;           conf\["cosmosdbsql\_max\_retry\_wait\_time"])



&#x20;   @classmethod

&#x20;   def \_parse\_url(cls, url):

&#x20;       \_, host, port, \_, password, \_, \_ = \_parse\_url(url)



&#x20;       if not host or not password:

&#x20;           raise ImproperlyConfigured("Invalid URL")



&#x20;       if not port:

&#x20;           port = 443



&#x20;       scheme = "https" if port == 443 else "http"

&#x20;       endpoint = f"{scheme}://{host}:{port}"

&#x20;       return endpoint, password



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def \_client(self):

&#x20;       """Return the CosmosDB/SQL client.



&#x20;       If this is the first call to the property, the client is created and

&#x20;       the database and collection are initialized if they don't yet exist.



&#x20;       """

&#x20;       connection\_policy = ConnectionPolicy()

&#x20;       connection\_policy.RetryOptions = RetryOptions(

&#x20;           max\_retry\_attempt\_count=self.\_max\_retry\_attempts,

&#x20;           max\_wait\_time\_in\_seconds=self.\_max\_retry\_wait\_time)



&#x20;       client = DocumentClient(

&#x20;           self.\_endpoint,

&#x20;           {"masterKey": self.\_key},

&#x20;           connection\_policy=connection\_policy,

&#x20;           consistency\_level=self.\_consistency\_level)



&#x20;       self.\_create\_database\_if\_not\_exists(client)

&#x20;       self.\_create\_collection\_if\_not\_exists(client)



&#x20;       return client



&#x20;   def \_create\_database\_if\_not\_exists(self, client):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           client.CreateDatabase({"id": self.\_database\_name})

&#x20;       except HTTPFailure as ex:

&#x20;           if ex.status\_code != ERROR\_EXISTS:

&#x20;               raise

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           LOGGER.info("Created CosmosDB database %s",

&#x20;                       self.\_database\_name)



&#x20;   def \_create\_collection\_if\_not\_exists(self, client):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           client.CreateCollection(

&#x20;               self.\_database\_link,

&#x20;               {"id": self.\_collection\_name,

&#x20;                "partitionKey": {"paths": \["/id"],

&#x20;                                 "kind": PartitionKind.Hash}})

&#x20;       except HTTPFailure as ex:

&#x20;           if ex.status\_code != ERROR\_EXISTS:

&#x20;               raise

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           LOGGER.info("Created CosmosDB collection %s/%s",

&#x20;                       self.\_database\_name, self.\_collection\_name)



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def \_database\_link(self):

&#x20;       return "dbs/" + self.\_database\_name



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def \_collection\_link(self):

&#x20;       return self.\_database\_link + "/colls/" + self.\_collection\_name



&#x20;   def \_get\_document\_link(self, key):

&#x20;       return self.\_collection\_link + "/docs/" + key



&#x20;   @classmethod

&#x20;   def \_get\_partition\_key(cls, key):

&#x20;       if not key or key.isspace():

&#x20;           raise ValueError("Key cannot be none, empty or whitespace.")



&#x20;       return {"partitionKey": key}



&#x20;   def get(self, key):

&#x20;       """Read the value stored at the given key.



&#x20;       Args:

&#x20;             key: The key for which to read the value.



&#x20;       """

&#x20;       key = bytes\_to\_str(key)

&#x20;       LOGGER.debug("Getting CosmosDB document %s/%s/%s",

&#x20;                    self.\_database\_name, self.\_collection\_name, key)



&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           document = self.\_client.ReadDocument(

&#x20;               self.\_get\_document\_link(key),

&#x20;               self.\_get\_partition\_key(key))

&#x20;       except HTTPFailure as ex:

&#x20;           if ex.status\_code != ERROR\_NOT\_FOUND:

&#x20;               raise

&#x20;           return None

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           return document.get("value")



&#x20;   def set(self, key, value):

&#x20;       """Store a value for a given key.



&#x20;       Args:

&#x20;             key: The key at which to store the value.

&#x20;             value: The value to store.



&#x20;       """

&#x20;       key = bytes\_to\_str(key)

&#x20;       LOGGER.debug("Creating CosmosDB document %s/%s/%s",

&#x20;                    self.\_database\_name, self.\_collection\_name, key)



&#x20;       self.\_client.CreateDocument(

&#x20;           self.\_collection\_link,

&#x20;           {"id": key, "value": value},

&#x20;           self.\_get\_partition\_key(key))



&#x20;   def mget(self, keys):

&#x20;       """Read all the values for the provided keys.



&#x20;       Args:

&#x20;             keys: The list of keys to read.



&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return \[self.get(key) for key in keys]



&#x20;   def delete(self, key):

&#x20;       """Delete the value at a given key.



&#x20;       Args:

&#x20;             key: The key of the value to delete.



&#x20;       """

&#x20;       key = bytes\_to\_str(key)

&#x20;       LOGGER.debug("Deleting CosmosDB document %s/%s/%s",

&#x20;                    self.\_database\_name, self.\_collection\_name, key)



&#x20;       self.\_client.DeleteDocument(

&#x20;           self.\_get\_document\_link(key),

&#x20;           self.\_get\_partition\_key(key))



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\backends\\couchbase.py

```python

"""Couchbase result store backend."""



from kombu.utils.url import \_parse\_url



from celery.exceptions import ImproperlyConfigured



from .base import KeyValueStoreBackend



try:

&#x20;   from couchbase.auth import PasswordAuthenticator

&#x20;   from couchbase.cluster import Cluster

except ImportError:

&#x20;   Cluster = PasswordAuthenticator = None



try:

&#x20;   from couchbase\_core.\_libcouchbase import FMT\_AUTO

except ImportError:

&#x20;   FMT\_AUTO = None



\_\_all\_\_ = ('CouchbaseBackend',)





class CouchbaseBackend(KeyValueStoreBackend):

&#x20;   """Couchbase backend.



&#x20;   Raises:

&#x20;       celery.exceptions.ImproperlyConfigured:

&#x20;           if module :pypi:`couchbase` is not available.

&#x20;   """



&#x20;   bucket = 'default'

&#x20;   host = 'localhost'

&#x20;   port = 8091

&#x20;   username = None

&#x20;   password = None

&#x20;   quiet = False

&#x20;   supports\_autoexpire = True



&#x20;   timeout = 2.5



&#x20;   # Use str as couchbase key not bytes

&#x20;   key\_t = str



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, url=None, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       kwargs.setdefault('expires\_type', int)

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(\*args, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;       self.url = url



&#x20;       if Cluster is None:

&#x20;           raise ImproperlyConfigured(

&#x20;               'You need to install the couchbase library to use the '

&#x20;               'Couchbase backend.',

&#x20;           )



&#x20;       uhost = uport = uname = upass = ubucket = None

&#x20;       if url:

&#x20;           \_, uhost, uport, uname, upass, ubucket, \_ = \_parse\_url(url)

&#x20;           ubucket = ubucket.strip('/') if ubucket else None



&#x20;       config = self.app.conf.get('couchbase\_backend\_settings', None)

&#x20;       if config is not None:

&#x20;           if not isinstance(config, dict):

&#x20;               raise ImproperlyConfigured(

&#x20;                   'Couchbase backend settings should be grouped in a dict',

&#x20;               )

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           config = {}



&#x20;       self.host = uhost or config.get('host', self.host)

&#x20;       self.port = int(uport or config.get('port', self.port))

&#x20;       self.bucket = ubucket or config.get('bucket', self.bucket)

&#x20;       self.username = uname or config.get('username', self.username)

&#x20;       self.password = upass or config.get('password', self.password)



&#x20;       self.\_connection = None



&#x20;   def \_get\_connection(self):

&#x20;       """Connect to the Couchbase server."""

&#x20;       if self.\_connection is None:

&#x20;           if self.host and self.port:

&#x20;               uri = f"couchbase://{self.host}:{self.port}"

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               uri = f"couchbase://{self.host}"

&#x20;           if self.username and self.password:

&#x20;               opt = PasswordAuthenticator(self.username, self.password)

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               opt = None



&#x20;           cluster = Cluster(uri, opt)



&#x20;           bucket = cluster.bucket(self.bucket)



&#x20;           self.\_connection = bucket.default\_collection()

&#x20;       return self.\_connection



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def connection(self):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_connection()



&#x20;   def get(self, key):

&#x20;       return self.connection.get(key).content



&#x20;   def set(self, key, value):

&#x20;       # Since 4.0.0 value is JSONType in couchbase lib, so parameter format isn't needed

&#x20;       if FMT\_AUTO is not None:

&#x20;           self.connection.upsert(key, value, ttl=self.expires, format=FMT\_AUTO)

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           self.connection.upsert(key, value, ttl=self.expires)



&#x20;   def mget(self, keys):

&#x20;       return self.connection.get\_multi(keys)



&#x20;   def delete(self, key):

&#x20;       self.connection.remove(key)



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\backends\\couchdb.py

```python

"""CouchDB result store backend."""

from kombu.utils.encoding import bytes\_to\_str

from kombu.utils.url import \_parse\_url



from celery.exceptions import ImproperlyConfigured



from .base import KeyValueStoreBackend



try:

&#x20;   import pycouchdb

except ImportError:

&#x20;   pycouchdb = None



\_\_all\_\_ = ('CouchBackend',)



ERR\_LIB\_MISSING = """\\

You need to install the pycouchdb library to use the CouchDB result backend\\

"""





class CouchBackend(KeyValueStoreBackend):

&#x20;   """CouchDB backend.



&#x20;   Raises:

&#x20;       celery.exceptions.ImproperlyConfigured:

&#x20;           if module :pypi:`pycouchdb` is not available.

&#x20;   """



&#x20;   container = 'default'

&#x20;   scheme = 'http'

&#x20;   host = 'localhost'

&#x20;   port = 5984

&#x20;   username = None

&#x20;   password = None



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, url=None, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(\*args, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;       self.url = url



&#x20;       if pycouchdb is None:

&#x20;           raise ImproperlyConfigured(ERR\_LIB\_MISSING)



&#x20;       uscheme = uhost = uport = uname = upass = ucontainer = None

&#x20;       if url:

&#x20;           \_, uhost, uport, uname, upass, ucontainer, \_ = \_parse\_url(url)

&#x20;           ucontainer = ucontainer.strip('/') if ucontainer else None



&#x20;       self.scheme = uscheme or self.scheme

&#x20;       self.host = uhost or self.host

&#x20;       self.port = int(uport or self.port)

&#x20;       self.container = ucontainer or self.container

&#x20;       self.username = uname or self.username

&#x20;       self.password = upass or self.password



&#x20;       self.\_connection = None



&#x20;   def \_get\_connection(self):

&#x20;       """Connect to the CouchDB server."""

&#x20;       if self.username and self.password:

&#x20;           conn\_string = f'{self.scheme}://{self.username}:{self.password}@{self.host}:{self.port}'

&#x20;           server = pycouchdb.Server(conn\_string, authmethod='basic')

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           conn\_string = f'{self.scheme}://{self.host}:{self.port}'

&#x20;           server = pycouchdb.Server(conn\_string)



&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           return server.database(self.container)

&#x20;       except pycouchdb.exceptions.NotFound:

&#x20;           return server.create(self.container)



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def connection(self):

&#x20;       if self.\_connection is None:

&#x20;           self.\_connection = self.\_get\_connection()

&#x20;       return self.\_connection



&#x20;   def get(self, key):

&#x20;       key = bytes\_to\_str(key)

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           return self.connection.get(key)\['value']

&#x20;       except pycouchdb.exceptions.NotFound:

&#x20;           return None



&#x20;   def set(self, key, value):

&#x20;       key = bytes\_to\_str(key)

&#x20;       data = {'\_id': key, 'value': value}

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           self.connection.save(data)

&#x20;       except pycouchdb.exceptions.Conflict:

&#x20;           # document already exists, update it

&#x20;           data = self.connection.get(key)

&#x20;           data\['value'] = value

&#x20;           self.connection.save(data)



&#x20;   def mget(self, keys):

&#x20;       return \[self.get(key) for key in keys]



&#x20;   def delete(self, key):

&#x20;       key = bytes\_to\_str(key)

&#x20;       self.connection.delete(key)



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\backends\\dynamodb.py

```python

"""AWS DynamoDB result store backend."""

from collections import namedtuple

from ipaddress import ip\_address

from time import sleep, time

from typing import Any, Dict



from kombu.utils.url import \_parse\_url as parse\_url



from celery.exceptions import ImproperlyConfigured

from celery.utils.log import get\_logger



from .base import KeyValueStoreBackend



try:

&#x20;   import boto3

&#x20;   from botocore.exceptions import ClientError

except ImportError:

&#x20;   boto3 = ClientError = None



\_\_all\_\_ = ('DynamoDBBackend',)





\# Helper class that describes a DynamoDB attribute

DynamoDBAttribute = namedtuple('DynamoDBAttribute', ('name', 'data\_type'))



logger = get\_logger(\_\_name\_\_)





class DynamoDBBackend(KeyValueStoreBackend):

&#x20;   """AWS DynamoDB result backend.



&#x20;   Raises:

&#x20;       celery.exceptions.ImproperlyConfigured:

&#x20;           if module :pypi:`boto3` is not available.

&#x20;   """



&#x20;   #: default DynamoDB table name (`default`)

&#x20;   table\_name = 'celery'



&#x20;   #: Read Provisioned Throughput (`default`)

&#x20;   read\_capacity\_units = 1



&#x20;   #: Write Provisioned Throughput (`default`)

&#x20;   write\_capacity\_units = 1



&#x20;   #: AWS region (`default`)

&#x20;   aws\_region = None



&#x20;   #: The endpoint URL that is passed to boto3 (local DynamoDB) (`default`)

&#x20;   endpoint\_url = None



&#x20;   #: Item time-to-live in seconds (`default`)

&#x20;   time\_to\_live\_seconds = None



&#x20;   # DynamoDB supports Time to Live as an auto-expiry mechanism.

&#x20;   supports\_autoexpire = True



&#x20;   \_key\_field = DynamoDBAttribute(name='id', data\_type='S')

&#x20;   # Each record has either a value field or count field

&#x20;   \_value\_field = DynamoDBAttribute(name='result', data\_type='B')

&#x20;   \_count\_filed = DynamoDBAttribute(name="chord\_count", data\_type='N')

&#x20;   \_timestamp\_field = DynamoDBAttribute(name='timestamp', data\_type='N')

&#x20;   \_ttl\_field = DynamoDBAttribute(name='ttl', data\_type='N')

&#x20;   \_available\_fields = None



&#x20;   implements\_incr = True



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, url=None, table\_name=None, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(\*args, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;       self.url = url

&#x20;       self.table\_name = table\_name or self.table\_name



&#x20;       if not boto3:

&#x20;           raise ImproperlyConfigured(

&#x20;               'You need to install the boto3 library to use the '

&#x20;               'DynamoDB backend.')



&#x20;       aws\_credentials\_given = False

&#x20;       aws\_access\_key\_id = None

&#x20;       aws\_secret\_access\_key = None



&#x20;       if url is not None:

&#x20;           scheme, region, port, username, password, table, query = \\

&#x20;               parse\_url(url)



&#x20;           aws\_access\_key\_id = username

&#x20;           aws\_secret\_access\_key = password



&#x20;           access\_key\_given = aws\_access\_key\_id is not None

&#x20;           secret\_key\_given = aws\_secret\_access\_key is not None



&#x20;           if access\_key\_given != secret\_key\_given:

&#x20;               raise ImproperlyConfigured(

&#x20;                   'You need to specify both the Access Key ID '

&#x20;                   'and Secret.')



&#x20;           aws\_credentials\_given = access\_key\_given



&#x20;           if region == 'localhost' or DynamoDBBackend.\_is\_valid\_ip(region):

&#x20;               # We are using the downloadable, local version of DynamoDB

&#x20;               self.endpoint\_url = f'http://{region}:{port}'

&#x20;               self.aws\_region = 'us-east-1'

&#x20;               logger.warning(

&#x20;                   'Using local-only DynamoDB endpoint URL: {}'.format(

&#x20;                       self.endpoint\_url

&#x20;                   )

&#x20;               )

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               self.aws\_region = region



&#x20;           # If endpoint\_url is explicitly set use it instead

&#x20;           \_get = self.app.conf.get

&#x20;           config\_endpoint\_url = \_get('dynamodb\_endpoint\_url')

&#x20;           if config\_endpoint\_url:

&#x20;               self.endpoint\_url = config\_endpoint\_url



&#x20;           self.read\_capacity\_units = int(

&#x20;               query.get(

&#x20;                   'read',

&#x20;                   self.read\_capacity\_units

&#x20;               )

&#x20;           )

&#x20;           self.write\_capacity\_units = int(

&#x20;               query.get(

&#x20;                   'write',

&#x20;                   self.write\_capacity\_units

&#x20;               )

&#x20;           )



&#x20;           ttl = query.get('ttl\_seconds', self.time\_to\_live\_seconds)

&#x20;           if ttl:

&#x20;               try:

&#x20;                   self.time\_to\_live\_seconds = int(ttl)

&#x20;               except ValueError as e:

&#x20;                   logger.error(

&#x20;                       f'TTL must be a number; got "{ttl}"',

&#x20;                       exc\_info=e

&#x20;                   )

&#x20;                   raise e



&#x20;           self.table\_name = table or self.table\_name



&#x20;       self.\_available\_fields = (

&#x20;           self.\_key\_field,

&#x20;           self.\_value\_field,

&#x20;           self.\_timestamp\_field

&#x20;       )



&#x20;       self.\_client = None

&#x20;       if aws\_credentials\_given:

&#x20;           self.\_get\_client(

&#x20;               access\_key\_id=aws\_access\_key\_id,

&#x20;               secret\_access\_key=aws\_secret\_access\_key

&#x20;           )



&#x20;   @staticmethod

&#x20;   def \_is\_valid\_ip(ip):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           ip\_address(ip)

&#x20;           return True

&#x20;       except ValueError:

&#x20;           return False



&#x20;   def \_get\_client(self, access\_key\_id=None, secret\_access\_key=None):

&#x20;       """Get client connection."""

&#x20;       if self.\_client is None:

&#x20;           client\_parameters = {

&#x20;               'region\_name': self.aws\_region

&#x20;           }

&#x20;           if access\_key\_id is not None:

&#x20;               client\_parameters.update({

&#x20;                   'aws\_access\_key\_id': access\_key\_id,

&#x20;                   'aws\_secret\_access\_key': secret\_access\_key

&#x20;               })



&#x20;           if self.endpoint\_url is not None:

&#x20;               client\_parameters\['endpoint\_url'] = self.endpoint\_url



&#x20;           self.\_client = boto3.client(

&#x20;               'dynamodb',

&#x20;               \*\*client\_parameters

&#x20;           )

&#x20;           self.\_get\_or\_create\_table()



&#x20;           if self.\_has\_ttl() is not None:

&#x20;               self.\_validate\_ttl\_methods()

&#x20;               self.\_set\_table\_ttl()



&#x20;       return self.\_client



&#x20;   def \_get\_table\_schema(self):

&#x20;       """Get the boto3 structure describing the DynamoDB table schema."""

&#x20;       return {

&#x20;           'AttributeDefinitions': \[

&#x20;               {

&#x20;                   'AttributeName': self.\_key\_field.name,

&#x20;                   'AttributeType': self.\_key\_field.data\_type

&#x20;               }

&#x20;           ],

&#x20;           'TableName': self.table\_name,

&#x20;           'KeySchema': \[

&#x20;               {

&#x20;                   'AttributeName': self.\_key\_field.name,

&#x20;                   'KeyType': 'HASH'

&#x20;               }

&#x20;           ],

&#x20;           'ProvisionedThroughput': {

&#x20;               'ReadCapacityUnits': self.read\_capacity\_units,

&#x20;               'WriteCapacityUnits': self.write\_capacity\_units

&#x20;           }

&#x20;       }



&#x20;   def \_get\_or\_create\_table(self):

&#x20;       """Create table if not exists, otherwise return the description."""

&#x20;       table\_schema = self.\_get\_table\_schema()

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           return self.\_client.describe\_table(TableName=self.table\_name)

&#x20;       except ClientError as e:

&#x20;           error\_code = e.response\['Error'].get('Code', 'Unknown')



&#x20;           if error\_code == 'ResourceNotFoundException':

&#x20;               table\_description = self.\_client.create\_table(\*\*table\_schema)

&#x20;               logger.info(

&#x20;                   'DynamoDB Table {} did not exist, creating.'.format(

&#x20;                       self.table\_name

&#x20;                   )

&#x20;               )

&#x20;               # In case we created the table, wait until it becomes available.

&#x20;               self.\_wait\_for\_table\_status('ACTIVE')

&#x20;               logger.info(

&#x20;                   'DynamoDB Table {} is now available.'.format(

&#x20;                       self.table\_name

&#x20;                   )

&#x20;               )

&#x20;               return table\_description

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               raise e



&#x20;   def \_has\_ttl(self):

&#x20;       """Return the desired Time to Live config.



&#x20;       - True:  Enable TTL on the table; use expiry.

&#x20;       - False: Disable TTL on the table; don't use expiry.

&#x20;       - None:  Ignore TTL on the table; don't use expiry.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return None if self.time\_to\_live\_seconds is None \\

&#x20;           else self.time\_to\_live\_seconds >= 0



&#x20;   def \_validate\_ttl\_methods(self):

&#x20;       """Verify boto support for the DynamoDB Time to Live methods."""

&#x20;       # Required TTL methods.

&#x20;       required\_methods = (

&#x20;           'update\_time\_to\_live',

&#x20;           'describe\_time\_to\_live',

&#x20;       )



&#x20;       # Find missing methods.

&#x20;       missing\_methods = \[]

&#x20;       for method in list(required\_methods):

&#x20;           if not hasattr(self.\_client, method):

&#x20;               missing\_methods.append(method)



&#x20;       if missing\_methods:

&#x20;           logger.error(

&#x20;               (

&#x20;                   'boto3 method(s) {methods} not found; ensure that '

&#x20;                   'boto3>=1.9.178 and botocore>=1.12.178 are installed'

&#x20;               ).format(

&#x20;                   methods=','.join(missing\_methods)

&#x20;               )

&#x20;           )

&#x20;           raise AttributeError(

&#x20;               'boto3 method(s) {methods} not found'.format(

&#x20;                   methods=','.join(missing\_methods)

&#x20;               )

&#x20;           )



&#x20;   def \_get\_ttl\_specification(self, ttl\_attr\_name):

&#x20;       """Get the boto3 structure describing the DynamoDB TTL specification."""

&#x20;       return {

&#x20;           'TableName': self.table\_name,

&#x20;           'TimeToLiveSpecification': {

&#x20;               'Enabled': self.\_has\_ttl(),

&#x20;               'AttributeName': ttl\_attr\_name

&#x20;           }

&#x20;       }



&#x20;   def \_get\_table\_ttl\_description(self):

&#x20;       # Get the current TTL description.

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           description = self.\_client.describe\_time\_to\_live(

&#x20;               TableName=self.table\_name

&#x20;           )

&#x20;       except ClientError as e:

&#x20;           error\_code = e.response\['Error'].get('Code', 'Unknown')

&#x20;           error\_message = e.response\['Error'].get('Message', 'Unknown')

&#x20;           logger.error((

&#x20;               'Error describing Time to Live on DynamoDB table {table}: '

&#x20;               '{code}: {message}'

&#x20;           ).format(

&#x20;               table=self.table\_name,

&#x20;               code=error\_code,

&#x20;               message=error\_message,

&#x20;           ))

&#x20;           raise e



&#x20;       return description



&#x20;   def \_set\_table\_ttl(self):

&#x20;       """Enable or disable Time to Live on the table."""

&#x20;       # Get the table TTL description, and return early when possible.

&#x20;       description = self.\_get\_table\_ttl\_description()

&#x20;       status = description\['TimeToLiveDescription']\['TimeToLiveStatus']

&#x20;       if status in ('ENABLED', 'ENABLING'):

&#x20;           cur\_attr\_name = \\

&#x20;               description\['TimeToLiveDescription']\['AttributeName']

&#x20;           if self.\_has\_ttl():

&#x20;               if cur\_attr\_name == self.\_ttl\_field.name:

&#x20;                   # We want TTL enabled, and it is currently enabled or being

&#x20;                   # enabled, and on the correct attribute.

&#x20;                   logger.debug((

&#x20;                       'DynamoDB Time to Live is {situation} '

&#x20;                       'on table {table}'

&#x20;                   ).format(

&#x20;                       situation='already enabled'

&#x20;                       if status == 'ENABLED'

&#x20;                       else 'currently being enabled',

&#x20;                       table=self.table\_name

&#x20;                   ))

&#x20;                   return description



&#x20;       elif status in ('DISABLED', 'DISABLING'):

&#x20;           if not self.\_has\_ttl():

&#x20;               # We want TTL disabled, and it is currently disabled or being

&#x20;               # disabled.

&#x20;               logger.debug((

&#x20;                   'DynamoDB Time to Live is {situation} '

&#x20;                   'on table {table}'

&#x20;               ).format(

&#x20;                   situation='already disabled'

&#x20;                   if status == 'DISABLED'

&#x20;                   else 'currently being disabled',

&#x20;                   table=self.table\_name

&#x20;               ))

&#x20;               return description



&#x20;       # The state shouldn't ever have any value beyond the four handled

&#x20;       # above, but to ease troubleshooting of potential future changes, emit

&#x20;       # a log showing the unknown state.

&#x20;       else:  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;           logger.warning((

&#x20;               'Unknown DynamoDB Time to Live status {status} '

&#x20;               'on table {table}. Attempting to continue.'

&#x20;           ).format(

&#x20;               status=status,

&#x20;               table=self.table\_name

&#x20;           ))



&#x20;       # At this point, we have one of the following situations:

&#x20;       #

&#x20;       # We want TTL enabled,

&#x20;       #

&#x20;       # - and it's currently disabled: Try to enable.

&#x20;       #

&#x20;       # - and it's being disabled: Try to enable, but this is almost sure to

&#x20;       #   raise ValidationException with message:

&#x20;       #

&#x20;       #     Time to live has been modified multiple times within a fixed

&#x20;       #     interval

&#x20;       #

&#x20;       # - and it's currently enabling or being enabled, but on the wrong

&#x20;       #   attribute: Try to enable, but this will raise ValidationException

&#x20;       #   with message:

&#x20;       #

&#x20;       #     TimeToLive is active on a different AttributeName: current

&#x20;       #     AttributeName is ttlx

&#x20;       #

&#x20;       # We want TTL disabled,

&#x20;       #

&#x20;       # - and it's currently enabled: Try to disable.

&#x20;       #

&#x20;       # - and it's being enabled: Try to disable, but this is almost sure to

&#x20;       #   raise ValidationException with message:

&#x20;       #

&#x20;       #     Time to live has been modified multiple times within a fixed

&#x20;       #     interval

&#x20;       #

&#x20;       attr\_name = \\

&#x20;           cur\_attr\_name if status == 'ENABLED' else self.\_ttl\_field.name

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           specification = self.\_client.update\_time\_to\_live(

&#x20;               \*\*self.\_get\_ttl\_specification(

&#x20;                   ttl\_attr\_name=attr\_name

&#x20;               )

&#x20;           )

&#x20;           logger.info(

&#x20;               (

&#x20;                   'DynamoDB table Time to Live updated: '

&#x20;                   'table={table} enabled={enabled} attribute={attr}'

&#x20;               ).format(

&#x20;                   table=self.table\_name,

&#x20;                   enabled=self.\_has\_ttl(),

&#x20;                   attr=self.\_ttl\_field.name

&#x20;               )

&#x20;           )

&#x20;           return specification

&#x20;       except ClientError as e:

&#x20;           error\_code = e.response\['Error'].get('Code', 'Unknown')

&#x20;           error\_message = e.response\['Error'].get('Message', 'Unknown')

&#x20;           logger.error((

&#x20;               'Error {action} Time to Live on DynamoDB table {table}: '

&#x20;               '{code}: {message}'

&#x20;           ).format(

&#x20;               action='enabling' if self.\_has\_ttl() else 'disabling',

&#x20;               table=self.table\_name,

&#x20;               code=error\_code,

&#x20;               message=error\_message,

&#x20;           ))

&#x20;           raise e



&#x20;   def \_wait\_for\_table\_status(self, expected='ACTIVE'):

&#x20;       """Poll for the expected table status."""

&#x20;       achieved\_state = False

&#x20;       while not achieved\_state:

&#x20;           table\_description = self.client.describe\_table(

&#x20;               TableName=self.table\_name

&#x20;           )

&#x20;           logger.debug(

&#x20;               'Waiting for DynamoDB table {} to become {}.'.format(

&#x20;                   self.table\_name,

&#x20;                   expected

&#x20;               )

&#x20;           )

&#x20;           current\_status = table\_description\['Table']\['TableStatus']

&#x20;           achieved\_state = current\_status == expected

&#x20;           sleep(1)



&#x20;   def \_prepare\_get\_request(self, key):

&#x20;       """Construct the item retrieval request parameters."""

&#x20;       return {

&#x20;           'TableName': self.table\_name,

&#x20;           'Key': {

&#x20;               self.\_key\_field.name: {

&#x20;                   self.\_key\_field.data\_type: key

&#x20;               }

&#x20;           }

&#x20;       }



&#x20;   def \_prepare\_put\_request(self, key, value):

&#x20;       """Construct the item creation request parameters."""

&#x20;       timestamp = time()

&#x20;       put\_request = {

&#x20;           'TableName': self.table\_name,

&#x20;           'Item': {

&#x20;               self.\_key\_field.name: {

&#x20;                   self.\_key\_field.data\_type: key

&#x20;               },

&#x20;               self.\_value\_field.name: {

&#x20;                   self.\_value\_field.data\_type: value

&#x20;               },

&#x20;               self.\_timestamp\_field.name: {

&#x20;                   self.\_timestamp\_field.data\_type: str(timestamp)

&#x20;               }

&#x20;           }

&#x20;       }

&#x20;       if self.\_has\_ttl():

&#x20;           put\_request\['Item'].update({

&#x20;               self.\_ttl\_field.name: {

&#x20;                   self.\_ttl\_field.data\_type:

&#x20;                       str(int(timestamp + self.time\_to\_live\_seconds))

&#x20;               }

&#x20;           })

&#x20;       return put\_request



&#x20;   def \_prepare\_init\_count\_request(self, key: str) -> Dict\[str, Any]:

&#x20;       """Construct the counter initialization request parameters"""

&#x20;       timestamp = time()

&#x20;       return {

&#x20;           'TableName': self.table\_name,

&#x20;           'Item': {

&#x20;               self.\_key\_field.name: {

&#x20;                   self.\_key\_field.data\_type: key

&#x20;               },

&#x20;               self.\_count\_filed.name: {

&#x20;                   self.\_count\_filed.data\_type: "0"

&#x20;               },

&#x20;               self.\_timestamp\_field.name: {

&#x20;                   self.\_timestamp\_field.data\_type: str(timestamp)

&#x20;               }

&#x20;           }

&#x20;       }



&#x20;   def \_prepare\_inc\_count\_request(self, key: str) -> Dict\[str, Any]:

&#x20;       """Construct the counter increment request parameters"""

&#x20;       return {

&#x20;           'TableName': self.table\_name,

&#x20;           'Key': {

&#x20;               self.\_key\_field.name: {

&#x20;                   self.\_key\_field.data\_type: key

&#x20;               }

&#x20;           },

&#x20;           'UpdateExpression': f"set {self.\_count\_filed.name} = {self.\_count\_filed.name} + :num",

&#x20;           "ExpressionAttributeValues": {

&#x20;               ":num": {"N": "1"},

&#x20;           },

&#x20;           "ReturnValues": "UPDATED\_NEW",

&#x20;       }



&#x20;   def \_item\_to\_dict(self, raw\_response):

&#x20;       """Convert get\_item() response to field-value pairs."""

&#x20;       if 'Item' not in raw\_response:

&#x20;           return {}

&#x20;       return {

&#x20;           field.name: raw\_response\['Item']\[field.name]\[field.data\_type]

&#x20;           for field in self.\_available\_fields

&#x20;       }



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def client(self):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_client()



&#x20;   def get(self, key):

&#x20;       key = str(key)

&#x20;       request\_parameters = self.\_prepare\_get\_request(key)

&#x20;       item\_response = self.client.get\_item(\*\*request\_parameters)

&#x20;       item = self.\_item\_to\_dict(item\_response)

&#x20;       return item.get(self.\_value\_field.name)



&#x20;   def set(self, key, value):

&#x20;       key = str(key)

&#x20;       request\_parameters = self.\_prepare\_put\_request(key, value)

&#x20;       self.client.put\_item(\*\*request\_parameters)



&#x20;   def mget(self, keys):

&#x20;       return \[self.get(key) for key in keys]



&#x20;   def delete(self, key):

&#x20;       key = str(key)

&#x20;       request\_parameters = self.\_prepare\_get\_request(key)

&#x20;       self.client.delete\_item(\*\*request\_parameters)



&#x20;   def incr(self, key: bytes) -> int:

&#x20;       """Atomically increase the chord\_count and return the new count"""

&#x20;       key = str(key)

&#x20;       request\_parameters = self.\_prepare\_inc\_count\_request(key)

&#x20;       item\_response = self.client.update\_item(\*\*request\_parameters)

&#x20;       new\_count: str = item\_response\["Attributes"]\[self.\_count\_filed.name]\[self.\_count\_filed.data\_type]

&#x20;       return int(new\_count)



&#x20;   def \_apply\_chord\_incr(self, header\_result\_args, body, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       chord\_key = self.get\_key\_for\_chord(header\_result\_args\[0])

&#x20;       init\_count\_request = self.\_prepare\_init\_count\_request(str(chord\_key))

&#x20;       self.client.put\_item(\*\*init\_count\_request)

&#x20;       return super().\_apply\_chord\_incr(

&#x20;           header\_result\_args, body, \*\*kwargs)



```

```python
"""Elasticsearch result store backend."""

from datetime import datetime, timezone



from kombu.utils.encoding import bytes\_to\_str

from kombu.utils.url import \_parse\_url



from celery import states

from celery.exceptions import ImproperlyConfigured



from .base import KeyValueStoreBackend



try:

&#x20;   import elasticsearch

except ImportError:

&#x20;   elasticsearch = None



try:

&#x20;   import elastic\_transport

except ImportError:

&#x20;   elastic\_transport = None



\_\_all\_\_ = ('ElasticsearchBackend',)



E\_LIB\_MISSING = """\\

You need to install the elasticsearch library to use the Elasticsearch \\

result backend.\\

"""





class ElasticsearchBackend(KeyValueStoreBackend):

&#x20;   """Elasticsearch Backend.



&#x20;   Raises:

&#x20;       celery.exceptions.ImproperlyConfigured:

&#x20;           if module :pypi:`elasticsearch` is not available.

&#x20;   """



&#x20;   index = 'celery'

&#x20;   doc\_type = None

&#x20;   scheme = 'http'

&#x20;   host = 'localhost'

&#x20;   port = 9200

&#x20;   username = None

&#x20;   password = None

&#x20;   es\_retry\_on\_timeout = False

&#x20;   es\_timeout = 10

&#x20;   es\_max\_retries = 3



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, url=None, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(\*args, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;       self.url = url

&#x20;       \_get = self.app.conf.get



&#x20;       if elasticsearch is None:

&#x20;           raise ImproperlyConfigured(E\_LIB\_MISSING)



&#x20;       index = doc\_type = scheme = host = port = username = password = None



&#x20;       if url:

&#x20;           scheme, host, port, username, password, path, \_ = \_parse\_url(url)

&#x20;           if scheme == 'elasticsearch':

&#x20;               scheme = None

&#x20;           if path:

&#x20;               path = path.strip('/')

&#x20;               index, \_, doc\_type = path.partition('/')



&#x20;       self.index = index or self.index

&#x20;       self.doc\_type = doc\_type or self.doc\_type

&#x20;       self.scheme = scheme or self.scheme

&#x20;       self.host = host or self.host

&#x20;       self.port = port or self.port

&#x20;       self.username = username or self.username

&#x20;       self.password = password or self.password



&#x20;       self.es\_retry\_on\_timeout = (

&#x20;           \_get('elasticsearch\_retry\_on\_timeout') or self.es\_retry\_on\_timeout

&#x20;       )



&#x20;       es\_timeout = \_get('elasticsearch\_timeout')

&#x20;       if es\_timeout is not None:

&#x20;           self.es\_timeout = es\_timeout



&#x20;       es\_max\_retries = \_get('elasticsearch\_max\_retries')

&#x20;       if es\_max\_retries is not None:

&#x20;           self.es\_max\_retries = es\_max\_retries



&#x20;       self.es\_save\_meta\_as\_text = \_get('elasticsearch\_save\_meta\_as\_text', True)

&#x20;       self.\_server = None



&#x20;   def exception\_safe\_to\_retry(self, exc):

&#x20;       if isinstance(exc, elasticsearch.exceptions.ApiError):

&#x20;           # 401: Unauthorized

&#x20;           # 409: Conflict

&#x20;           # 500: Internal Server Error

&#x20;           # 502: Bad Gateway

&#x20;           # 504: Gateway Timeout

&#x20;           # N/A: Low level exception (i.e. socket exception)

&#x20;           if exc.status\_code in {401, 409, 500, 502, 504, 'N/A'}:

&#x20;               return True

&#x20;       if isinstance(exc, elasticsearch.exceptions.TransportError):

&#x20;           return True

&#x20;       return False



&#x20;   def get(self, key):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           res = self.\_get(key)

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               if res\['found']:

&#x20;                   return res\['\_source']\['result']

&#x20;           except (TypeError, KeyError):

&#x20;               pass

&#x20;       except elasticsearch.exceptions.NotFoundError:

&#x20;           pass



&#x20;   def \_get(self, key):

&#x20;       if self.doc\_type:

&#x20;           return self.server.get(

&#x20;               index=self.index,

&#x20;               id=key,

&#x20;               doc\_type=self.doc\_type,

&#x20;           )

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           return self.server.get(

&#x20;               index=self.index,

&#x20;               id=key,

&#x20;           )



&#x20;   def \_set\_with\_state(self, key, value, state):

&#x20;       body = {

&#x20;           'result': value,

&#x20;           '@timestamp': '{}Z'.format(

&#x20;               datetime.now(timezone.utc).isoformat()\[:-9]

&#x20;           ),

&#x20;       }

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           self.\_index(

&#x20;               id=key,

&#x20;               body=body,

&#x20;           )

&#x20;       except elasticsearch.exceptions.ConflictError:

&#x20;           # document already exists, update it

&#x20;           self.\_update(key, body, state)



&#x20;   def set(self, key, value):

&#x20;       return self.\_set\_with\_state(key, value, None)



&#x20;   def \_index(self, id, body, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       body = {bytes\_to\_str(k): v for k, v in body.items()}

&#x20;       if self.doc\_type:

&#x20;           return self.server.index(

&#x20;               id=bytes\_to\_str(id),

&#x20;               index=self.index,

&#x20;               doc\_type=self.doc\_type,

&#x20;               body=body,

&#x20;               params={'op\_type': 'create'},

&#x20;               \*\*kwargs

&#x20;           )

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           return self.server.index(

&#x20;               id=bytes\_to\_str(id),

&#x20;               index=self.index,

&#x20;               body=body,

&#x20;               params={'op\_type': 'create'},

&#x20;               \*\*kwargs

&#x20;           )



&#x20;   def \_update(self, id, body, state, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       """Update state in a conflict free manner.



&#x20;       If state is defined (not None), this will not update ES server if either:

&#x20;       \* existing state is success

&#x20;       \* existing state is a ready state and current state in not a ready state



&#x20;       This way, a Retry state cannot override a Success or Failure, and chord\_unlock

&#x20;       will not retry indefinitely.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       body = {bytes\_to\_str(k): v for k, v in body.items()}



&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           res\_get = self.\_get(key=id)

&#x20;           if not res\_get.get('found'):

&#x20;               return self.\_index(id, body, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;           # document disappeared between index and get calls.

&#x20;       except elasticsearch.exceptions.NotFoundError:

&#x20;           return self.\_index(id, body, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           meta\_present\_on\_backend = self.decode\_result(res\_get\['\_source']\['result'])

&#x20;       except (TypeError, KeyError):

&#x20;           pass

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           if meta\_present\_on\_backend\['status'] == states.SUCCESS:

&#x20;               # if stored state is already in success, do nothing

&#x20;               return {'result': 'noop'}

&#x20;           elif meta\_present\_on\_backend\['status'] in states.READY\_STATES and state in states.UNREADY\_STATES:

&#x20;               # if stored state is in ready state and current not, do nothing

&#x20;               return {'result': 'noop'}



&#x20;       # get current sequence number and primary term

&#x20;       # https://www.elastic.co/guide/en/elasticsearch/reference/current/optimistic-concurrency-control.html

&#x20;       seq\_no = res\_get.get('\_seq\_no', 1)

&#x20;       prim\_term = res\_get.get('\_primary\_term', 1)



&#x20;       # try to update document with current seq\_no and primary\_term

&#x20;       if self.doc\_type:

&#x20;           res = self.server.update(

&#x20;               id=bytes\_to\_str(id),

&#x20;               index=self.index,

&#x20;               doc\_type=self.doc\_type,

&#x20;               body={'doc': body},

&#x20;               params={'if\_primary\_term': prim\_term, 'if\_seq\_no': seq\_no},

&#x20;               \*\*kwargs

&#x20;           )

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           res = self.server.update(

&#x20;               id=bytes\_to\_str(id),

&#x20;               index=self.index,

&#x20;               body={'doc': body},

&#x20;               params={'if\_primary\_term': prim\_term, 'if\_seq\_no': seq\_no},

&#x20;               \*\*kwargs

&#x20;           )

&#x20;       # result is elastic search update query result

&#x20;       # noop = query did not update any document

&#x20;       # updated = at least one document got updated

&#x20;       if res\['result'] == 'noop':

&#x20;           raise elasticsearch.exceptions.ConflictError(

&#x20;               "conflicting update occurred concurrently",

&#x20;               elastic\_transport.ApiResponseMeta(409, "HTTP/1.1",

&#x20;                                                 elastic\_transport.HttpHeaders(), 0, elastic\_transport.NodeConfig(

&#x20;                                                     self.scheme, self.host, self.port)), None)

&#x20;       return res



&#x20;   def encode(self, data):

&#x20;       if self.es\_save\_meta\_as\_text:

&#x20;           return super().encode(data)

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           if not isinstance(data, dict):

&#x20;               return super().encode(data)

&#x20;           if data.get("result"):

&#x20;               data\["result"] = self.\_encode(data\["result"])\[2]

&#x20;           if data.get("traceback"):

&#x20;               data\["traceback"] = self.\_encode(data\["traceback"])\[2]

&#x20;           return data



&#x20;   def decode(self, payload):

&#x20;       if self.es\_save\_meta\_as\_text:

&#x20;           return super().decode(payload)

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           if not isinstance(payload, dict):

&#x20;               return super().decode(payload)

&#x20;           if payload.get("result"):

&#x20;               payload\["result"] = super().decode(payload\["result"])

&#x20;           if payload.get("traceback"):

&#x20;               payload\["traceback"] = super().decode(payload\["traceback"])

&#x20;           return payload



&#x20;   def mget(self, keys):

&#x20;       return \[self.get(key) for key in keys]



&#x20;   def delete(self, key):

&#x20;       if self.doc\_type:

&#x20;           self.server.delete(index=self.index, id=key, doc\_type=self.doc\_type)

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           self.server.delete(index=self.index, id=key)



&#x20;   def \_get\_server(self):

&#x20;       """Connect to the Elasticsearch server."""

&#x20;       http\_auth = None

&#x20;       if self.username and self.password:

&#x20;           http\_auth = (self.username, self.password)

&#x20;       return elasticsearch.Elasticsearch(

&#x20;           f'{self.scheme}://{self.host}:{self.port}',

&#x20;           retry\_on\_timeout=self.es\_retry\_on\_timeout,

&#x20;           max\_retries=self.es\_max\_retries,

&#x20;           timeout=self.es\_timeout,

&#x20;           http\_auth=http\_auth,

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def server(self):

&#x20;       if self.\_server is None:

&#x20;           self.\_server = self.\_get\_server()

&#x20;       return self.\_server



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\backends\\filesystem.py

```python

"""File-system result store backend."""

import locale

import os

from datetime import datetime



from kombu.utils.encoding import ensure\_bytes



from celery import uuid

from celery.backends.base import KeyValueStoreBackend

from celery.exceptions import ImproperlyConfigured



default\_encoding = locale.getpreferredencoding(False)



E\_NO\_PATH\_SET = 'You need to configure a path for the file-system backend'

E\_PATH\_NON\_CONFORMING\_SCHEME = (

&#x20;   'A path for the file-system backend should conform to the file URI scheme'

)

E\_PATH\_INVALID = """\\

The configured path for the file-system backend does not

work correctly, please make sure that it exists and has

the correct permissions.\\

"""





class FilesystemBackend(KeyValueStoreBackend):

&#x20;   """File-system result backend.



&#x20;   Arguments:

&#x20;       url (str):  URL to the directory we should use

&#x20;       open (Callable): open function to use when opening files

&#x20;       unlink (Callable): unlink function to use when deleting files

&#x20;       sep (str): directory separator (to join the directory with the key)

&#x20;       encoding (str): encoding used on the file-system

&#x20;   """



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, url=None, open=open, unlink=os.unlink, sep=os.sep,

&#x20;                encoding=default\_encoding, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(\*args, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;       self.url = url

&#x20;       path = self.\_find\_path(url)



&#x20;       # Remove forwarding "/" for Windows os

&#x20;       if os.name == "nt" and path.startswith("/"):

&#x20;           path = path\[1:]



&#x20;       # We need the path and separator as bytes objects

&#x20;       self.path = path.encode(encoding)

&#x20;       self.sep = sep.encode(encoding)



&#x20;       self.open = open

&#x20;       self.unlink = unlink



&#x20;       # Let's verify that we've everything setup right

&#x20;       self.\_do\_directory\_test(b'.fs-backend-' + uuid().encode(encoding))



&#x20;   def \_\_reduce\_\_(self, args=(), kwargs=None):

&#x20;       kwargs = {} if not kwargs else kwargs

&#x20;       return super().\_\_reduce\_\_(args, {\*\*kwargs, 'url': self.url})



&#x20;   def \_find\_path(self, url):

&#x20;       if not url:

&#x20;           raise ImproperlyConfigured(E\_NO\_PATH\_SET)

&#x20;       if url.startswith('file://localhost/'):

&#x20;           return url\[16:]

&#x20;       if url.startswith('file://'):

&#x20;           return url\[7:]

&#x20;       raise ImproperlyConfigured(E\_PATH\_NON\_CONFORMING\_SCHEME)



&#x20;   def \_do\_directory\_test(self, key):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           self.set(key, b'test value')

&#x20;           assert self.get(key) == b'test value'

&#x20;           self.delete(key)

&#x20;       except OSError:

&#x20;           raise ImproperlyConfigured(E\_PATH\_INVALID)



&#x20;   def \_filename(self, key):

&#x20;       return self.sep.join((self.path, key))



&#x20;   def get(self, key):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           with self.open(self.\_filename(key), 'rb') as infile:

&#x20;               return infile.read()

&#x20;       except FileNotFoundError:

&#x20;           pass



&#x20;   def set(self, key, value):

&#x20;       with self.open(self.\_filename(key), 'wb') as outfile:

&#x20;           outfile.write(ensure\_bytes(value))



&#x20;   def mget(self, keys):

&#x20;       for key in keys:

&#x20;           yield self.get(key)



&#x20;   def delete(self, key):

&#x20;       self.unlink(self.\_filename(key))



&#x20;   def cleanup(self):

&#x20;       """Delete expired meta-data."""

&#x20;       if not self.expires:

&#x20;           return

&#x20;       epoch = datetime(1970, 1, 1, tzinfo=self.app.timezone)

&#x20;       now\_ts = (self.app.now() - epoch).total\_seconds()

&#x20;       cutoff\_ts = now\_ts - self.expires

&#x20;       for filename in os.listdir(self.path):

&#x20;           for prefix in (self.task\_keyprefix, self.group\_keyprefix,

&#x20;                          self.chord\_keyprefix):

&#x20;               if filename.startswith(prefix):

&#x20;                   path = os.path.join(self.path, filename)

&#x20;                   if os.stat(path).st\_mtime < cutoff\_ts:

&#x20;                       self.unlink(path)

&#x20;                   break



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\backends\\gcs.py

```python

"""Google Cloud Storage result store backend for Celery."""

from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor

from datetime import datetime, timedelta

from os import getpid

from threading import RLock



from kombu.utils.encoding import bytes\_to\_str

from kombu.utils.functional import dictfilter

from kombu.utils.url import url\_to\_parts



from celery.backends.base import \_create\_chord\_error\_with\_cause

from celery.canvas import maybe\_signature

from celery.exceptions import ChordError, ImproperlyConfigured

from celery.result import GroupResult, allow\_join\_result

from celery.utils.log import get\_logger



from .base import KeyValueStoreBackend



try:

&#x20;   import requests

&#x20;   from google.api\_core import retry

&#x20;   from google.api\_core.exceptions import Conflict

&#x20;   from google.api\_core.retry import if\_exception\_type

&#x20;   from google.cloud import storage

&#x20;   from google.cloud.storage import Client

&#x20;   from google.cloud.storage.retry import DEFAULT\_RETRY

except ImportError:

&#x20;   storage = None



try:

&#x20;   from google.cloud import firestore, firestore\_admin\_v1

except ImportError:

&#x20;   firestore = None

&#x20;   firestore\_admin\_v1 = None





\_\_all\_\_ = ('GCSBackend',)





logger = get\_logger(\_\_name\_\_)





class GCSBackendBase(KeyValueStoreBackend):

&#x20;   """Google Cloud Storage task result backend."""



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       if not storage:

&#x20;           raise ImproperlyConfigured(

&#x20;               'You must install google-cloud-storage to use gcs backend'

&#x20;           )

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(\*\*kwargs)

&#x20;       self.\_client\_lock = RLock()

&#x20;       self.\_pid = getpid()

&#x20;       self.\_retry\_policy = DEFAULT\_RETRY

&#x20;       self.\_client = None



&#x20;       conf = self.app.conf

&#x20;       if self.url:

&#x20;           url\_params = self.\_params\_from\_url()

&#x20;           conf.update(\*\*dictfilter(url\_params))



&#x20;       self.bucket\_name = conf.get('gcs\_bucket')

&#x20;       if not self.bucket\_name:

&#x20;           raise ImproperlyConfigured(

&#x20;               'Missing bucket name: specify gcs\_bucket to use gcs backend'

&#x20;           )

&#x20;       self.project = conf.get('gcs\_project')

&#x20;       if not self.project:

&#x20;           raise ImproperlyConfigured(

&#x20;               'Missing project:specify gcs\_project to use gcs backend'

&#x20;           )

&#x20;       self.base\_path = conf.get('gcs\_base\_path', '').strip('/')

&#x20;       self.\_threadpool\_maxsize = int(conf.get('gcs\_threadpool\_maxsize', 10))

&#x20;       self.ttl = float(conf.get('gcs\_ttl') or 0)

&#x20;       if self.ttl < 0:

&#x20;           raise ImproperlyConfigured(

&#x20;               f'Invalid ttl: {self.ttl} must be greater than or equal to 0'

&#x20;           )

&#x20;       elif self.ttl:

&#x20;           if not self.\_is\_bucket\_lifecycle\_rule\_exists():

&#x20;               raise ImproperlyConfigured(

&#x20;                   f'Missing lifecycle rule to use gcs backend with ttl on '

&#x20;                   f'bucket: {self.bucket\_name}'

&#x20;               )



&#x20;   def get(self, key):

&#x20;       key = bytes\_to\_str(key)

&#x20;       blob = self.\_get\_blob(key)

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           return blob.download\_as\_bytes(retry=self.\_retry\_policy)

&#x20;       except storage.blob.NotFound:

&#x20;           return None



&#x20;   def set(self, key, value):

&#x20;       key = bytes\_to\_str(key)

&#x20;       blob = self.\_get\_blob(key)

&#x20;       if self.ttl:

&#x20;           blob.custom\_time = datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(seconds=self.ttl)

&#x20;       blob.upload\_from\_string(value, retry=self.\_retry\_policy)



&#x20;   def delete(self, key):

&#x20;       key = bytes\_to\_str(key)

&#x20;       blob = self.\_get\_blob(key)

&#x20;       if blob.exists():

&#x20;           blob.delete(retry=self.\_retry\_policy)



&#x20;   def mget(self, keys):

&#x20;       with ThreadPoolExecutor() as pool:

&#x20;           return list(pool.map(self.get, keys))



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def client(self):

&#x20;       """Returns a storage client."""



&#x20;       # make sure it's thread-safe, as creating a new client is expensive

&#x20;       with self.\_client\_lock:

&#x20;           if self.\_client and self.\_pid == getpid():

&#x20;               return self.\_client

&#x20;           # make sure each process gets its own connection after a fork

&#x20;           self.\_client = Client(project=self.project)

&#x20;           self.\_pid = getpid()



&#x20;           # config the number of connections to the server

&#x20;           adapter = requests.adapters.HTTPAdapter(

&#x20;               pool\_connections=self.\_threadpool\_maxsize,

&#x20;               pool\_maxsize=self.\_threadpool\_maxsize,

&#x20;               max\_retries=3,

&#x20;           )

&#x20;           client\_http = self.\_client.\_http

&#x20;           client\_http.mount("https://", adapter)

&#x20;           client\_http.\_auth\_request.session.mount("https://", adapter)



&#x20;           return self.\_client



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def bucket(self):

&#x20;       return self.client.bucket(self.bucket\_name)



&#x20;   def \_get\_blob(self, key):

&#x20;       key\_bucket\_path = f'{self.base\_path}/{key}' if self.base\_path else key

&#x20;       return self.bucket.blob(key\_bucket\_path)



&#x20;   def \_is\_bucket\_lifecycle\_rule\_exists(self):

&#x20;       bucket = self.bucket

&#x20;       bucket.reload()

&#x20;       for rule in bucket.lifecycle\_rules:

&#x20;           if rule\['action']\['type'] == 'Delete':

&#x20;               return True

&#x20;       return False



&#x20;   def \_params\_from\_url(self):

&#x20;       url\_parts = url\_to\_parts(self.url)



&#x20;       return {

&#x20;           'gcs\_bucket': url\_parts.hostname,

&#x20;           'gcs\_base\_path': url\_parts.path,

&#x20;           \*\*url\_parts.query,

&#x20;       }





class GCSBackend(GCSBackendBase):

&#x20;   """Google Cloud Storage task result backend.



&#x20;   Uses Firestore for chord ref count.

&#x20;   """



&#x20;   implements\_incr = True

&#x20;   supports\_native\_join = True



&#x20;   # Firestore parameters

&#x20;   \_collection\_name = 'celery'

&#x20;   \_field\_count = 'chord\_count'

&#x20;   \_field\_expires = 'expires\_at'



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       if not (firestore and firestore\_admin\_v1):

&#x20;           raise ImproperlyConfigured(

&#x20;               'You must install google-cloud-firestore to use gcs backend'

&#x20;           )

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(\*\*kwargs)



&#x20;       self.\_firestore\_lock = RLock()

&#x20;       self.\_firestore\_client = None



&#x20;       self.firestore\_project = self.app.conf.get(

&#x20;           'firestore\_project', self.project

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       if not self.\_is\_firestore\_ttl\_policy\_enabled():

&#x20;           raise ImproperlyConfigured(

&#x20;               f'Missing TTL policy to use gcs backend with ttl on '

&#x20;               f'Firestore collection: {self.\_collection\_name} '

&#x20;               f'project: {self.firestore\_project}'

&#x20;           )



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def firestore\_client(self):

&#x20;       """Returns a firestore client."""



&#x20;       # make sure it's thread-safe, as creating a new client is expensive

&#x20;       with self.\_firestore\_lock:

&#x20;           if self.\_firestore\_client and self.\_pid == getpid():

&#x20;               return self.\_firestore\_client

&#x20;           # make sure each process gets its own connection after a fork

&#x20;           self.\_firestore\_client = firestore.Client(

&#x20;               project=self.firestore\_project

&#x20;           )

&#x20;           self.\_pid = getpid()

&#x20;       return self.\_firestore\_client



&#x20;   def \_is\_firestore\_ttl\_policy\_enabled(self):

&#x20;       client = firestore\_admin\_v1.FirestoreAdminClient()



&#x20;       name = (

&#x20;           f"projects/{self.firestore\_project}"

&#x20;           f"/databases/(default)/collectionGroups/{self.\_collection\_name}"

&#x20;           f"/fields/{self.\_field\_expires}"

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       request = firestore\_admin\_v1.GetFieldRequest(name=name)

&#x20;       field = client.get\_field(request=request)



&#x20;       ttl\_config = field.ttl\_config

&#x20;       return ttl\_config and ttl\_config.state in {

&#x20;           firestore\_admin\_v1.Field.TtlConfig.State.ACTIVE,

&#x20;           firestore\_admin\_v1.Field.TtlConfig.State.CREATING,

&#x20;       }



&#x20;   def \_apply\_chord\_incr(self, header\_result\_args, body, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       key = self.get\_key\_for\_chord(header\_result\_args\[0]).decode()

&#x20;       self.\_expire\_chord\_key(key, 86400)

&#x20;       return super().\_apply\_chord\_incr(header\_result\_args, body, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def incr(self, key: bytes) -> int:

&#x20;       doc = self.\_firestore\_document(key)

&#x20;       resp = doc.set(

&#x20;           {self.\_field\_count: firestore.Increment(1)},

&#x20;           merge=True,

&#x20;           retry=retry.Retry(

&#x20;               predicate=if\_exception\_type(Conflict),

&#x20;               initial=1.0,

&#x20;               maximum=180.0,

&#x20;               multiplier=2.0,

&#x20;               timeout=180.0,

&#x20;           ),

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       return resp.transform\_results\[0].integer\_value



&#x20;   def on\_chord\_part\_return(self, request, state, result, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       """Chord part return callback.



&#x20;       Called for each task in the chord.

&#x20;       Increments the counter stored in Firestore.

&#x20;       If the counter reaches the number of tasks in the chord, the callback

&#x20;       is called.

&#x20;       If the callback raises an exception, the chord is marked as errored.

&#x20;       If the callback returns a value, the chord is marked as successful.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       app = self.app

&#x20;       gid = request.group

&#x20;       if not gid:

&#x20;           return

&#x20;       key = self.get\_key\_for\_chord(gid)

&#x20;       val = self.incr(key)

&#x20;       size = request.chord.get("chord\_size")

&#x20;       if size is None:

&#x20;           deps = self.\_restore\_deps(gid, request)

&#x20;           if deps is None:

&#x20;               return

&#x20;           size = len(deps)

&#x20;       if val > size:  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;           logger.warning(

&#x20;               'Chord counter incremented too many times for %r', gid

&#x20;           )

&#x20;       elif val == size:

&#x20;           # Read the deps once, to reduce the number of reads from GCS ($$)

&#x20;           deps = self.\_restore\_deps(gid, request)

&#x20;           if deps is None:

&#x20;               return

&#x20;           callback = maybe\_signature(request.chord, app=app)

&#x20;           j = deps.join\_native

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               with allow\_join\_result():

&#x20;                   ret = j(

&#x20;                       timeout=app.conf.result\_chord\_join\_timeout,

&#x20;                       propagate=True,

&#x20;                   )

&#x20;           except Exception as exc:  # pylint: disable=broad-except

&#x20;               try:

&#x20;                   culprit = next(deps.\_failed\_join\_report())

&#x20;                   reason = 'Dependency {0.id} raised {1!r}'.format(

&#x20;                       culprit,

&#x20;                       exc,

&#x20;                   )

&#x20;               except StopIteration:

&#x20;                   reason = repr(exc)



&#x20;               logger.exception('Chord %r raised: %r', gid, reason)

&#x20;               chord\_error = \_create\_chord\_error\_with\_cause(message=reason, original\_exc=exc)

&#x20;               self.chord\_error\_from\_stack(callback, chord\_error)

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               try:

&#x20;                   callback.delay(ret)

&#x20;               except Exception as exc:  # pylint: disable=broad-except

&#x20;                   logger.exception('Chord %r raised: %r', gid, exc)

&#x20;                   self.chord\_error\_from\_stack(

&#x20;                       callback,

&#x20;                       ChordError(f'Callback error: {exc!r}'),

&#x20;                   )

&#x20;           finally:

&#x20;               deps.delete()

&#x20;               # Firestore doesn't have an exact ttl policy, so delete the key.

&#x20;               self.\_delete\_chord\_key(key)



&#x20;   def \_restore\_deps(self, gid, request):

&#x20;       app = self.app

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           deps = GroupResult.restore(gid, backend=self)

&#x20;       except Exception as exc:  # pylint: disable=broad-except

&#x20;           callback = maybe\_signature(request.chord, app=app)

&#x20;           logger.exception('Chord %r raised: %r', gid, exc)

&#x20;           self.chord\_error\_from\_stack(

&#x20;               callback,

&#x20;               ChordError(f'Cannot restore group: {exc!r}'),

&#x20;           )

&#x20;           return

&#x20;       if deps is None:

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               raise ValueError(gid)

&#x20;           except ValueError as exc:

&#x20;               callback = maybe\_signature(request.chord, app=app)

&#x20;               logger.exception('Chord callback %r raised: %r', gid, exc)

&#x20;               self.chord\_error\_from\_stack(

&#x20;                   callback,

&#x20;                   ChordError(f'GroupResult {gid} no longer exists'),

&#x20;               )

&#x20;       return deps



&#x20;   def \_delete\_chord\_key(self, key):

&#x20;       doc = self.\_firestore\_document(key)

&#x20;       doc.delete()



&#x20;   def \_expire\_chord\_key(self, key, expires):

&#x20;       """Set TTL policy for a Firestore document.



&#x20;       Firestore ttl data is typically deleted within 24 hours after its

&#x20;       expiration date.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       val\_expires = datetime.utcnow() + timedelta(seconds=expires)

&#x20;       doc = self.\_firestore\_document(key)

&#x20;       doc.set({self.\_field\_expires: val\_expires}, merge=True)



&#x20;   def \_firestore\_document(self, key):

&#x20;       return self.firestore\_client.collection(

&#x20;           self.\_collection\_name

&#x20;       ).document(bytes\_to\_str(key))



```
