```python

"""AMQP 0.9.1 REPL."""



import pprint



import click

from amqp import Connection, Message

from click\_repl import register\_repl



\_\_all\_\_ = ('amqp',)



from celery.bin.base import handle\_preload\_options





def dump\_message(message):

&#x20;   if message is None:

&#x20;       return 'No messages in queue. basic.publish something.'

&#x20;   return {'body': message.body,

&#x20;           'properties': message.properties,

&#x20;           'delivery\_info': message.delivery\_info}





class AMQPContext:

&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, cli\_context):

&#x20;       self.cli\_context = cli\_context

&#x20;       self.connection = self.cli\_context.app.connection()

&#x20;       self.channel = None

&#x20;       self.reconnect()



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def app(self):

&#x20;       return self.cli\_context.app



&#x20;   def respond(self, retval):

&#x20;       if isinstance(retval, str):

&#x20;           self.cli\_context.echo(retval)

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           self.cli\_context.echo(pprint.pformat(retval))



&#x20;   def echo\_error(self, exception):

&#x20;       self.cli\_context.error(f'{self.cli\_context.ERROR}: {exception}')



&#x20;   def echo\_ok(self):

&#x20;       self.cli\_context.echo(self.cli\_context.OK)



&#x20;   def reconnect(self):

&#x20;       if self.connection:

&#x20;           self.connection.close()

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           self.connection = self.cli\_context.app.connection()



&#x20;       self.cli\_context.echo(f'-> connecting to {self.connection.as\_uri()}.')

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           self.connection.connect()

&#x20;       except (ConnectionRefusedError, ConnectionResetError) as e:

&#x20;           self.echo\_error(e)

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           self.cli\_context.secho('-> connected.', fg='green', bold=True)

&#x20;           self.channel = self.connection.default\_channel





@click.group(invoke\_without\_command=True)

@click.pass\_context

@handle\_preload\_options

def amqp(ctx):

&#x20;   """AMQP Administration Shell.



&#x20;   Also works for non-AMQP transports (but not ones that

&#x20;   store declarations in memory).

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   if not isinstance(ctx.obj, AMQPContext):

&#x20;       ctx.obj = AMQPContext(ctx.obj)





@amqp.command(name='exchange.declare')

@click.argument('exchange',

&#x20;               type=str)

@click.argument('type',

&#x20;               type=str)

@click.argument('passive',

&#x20;               type=bool,

&#x20;               default=False)

@click.argument('durable',

&#x20;               type=bool,

&#x20;               default=False)

@click.argument('auto\_delete',

&#x20;               type=bool,

&#x20;               default=False)

@click.pass\_obj

def exchange\_declare(amqp\_context, exchange, type, passive, durable,

&#x20;                    auto\_delete):

&#x20;   if amqp\_context.channel is None:

&#x20;       amqp\_context.echo\_error('Not connected to broker. Please retry...')

&#x20;       amqp\_context.reconnect()

&#x20;   else:

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           amqp\_context.channel.exchange\_declare(exchange=exchange,

&#x20;                                                 type=type,

&#x20;                                                 passive=passive,

&#x20;                                                 durable=durable,

&#x20;                                                 auto\_delete=auto\_delete)

&#x20;       except Exception as e:

&#x20;           amqp\_context.echo\_error(e)

&#x20;           amqp\_context.reconnect()

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           amqp\_context.echo\_ok()





@amqp.command(name='exchange.delete')

@click.argument('exchange',

&#x20;               type=str)

@click.argument('if\_unused',

&#x20;               type=bool)

@click.pass\_obj

def exchange\_delete(amqp\_context, exchange, if\_unused):

&#x20;   if amqp\_context.channel is None:

&#x20;       amqp\_context.echo\_error('Not connected to broker. Please retry...')

&#x20;       amqp\_context.reconnect()

&#x20;   else:

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           amqp\_context.channel.exchange\_delete(exchange=exchange,

&#x20;                                                if\_unused=if\_unused)

&#x20;       except Exception as e:

&#x20;           amqp\_context.echo\_error(e)

&#x20;           amqp\_context.reconnect()

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           amqp\_context.echo\_ok()





@amqp.command(name='queue.bind')

@click.argument('queue',

&#x20;               type=str)

@click.argument('exchange',

&#x20;               type=str)

@click.argument('routing\_key',

&#x20;               type=str)

@click.pass\_obj

def queue\_bind(amqp\_context, queue, exchange, routing\_key):

&#x20;   if amqp\_context.channel is None:

&#x20;       amqp\_context.echo\_error('Not connected to broker. Please retry...')

&#x20;       amqp\_context.reconnect()

&#x20;   else:

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           amqp\_context.channel.queue\_bind(queue=queue,

&#x20;                                           exchange=exchange,

&#x20;                                           routing\_key=routing\_key)

&#x20;       except Exception as e:

&#x20;           amqp\_context.echo\_error(e)

&#x20;           amqp\_context.reconnect()

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           amqp\_context.echo\_ok()





@amqp.command(name='queue.declare')

@click.argument('queue',

&#x20;               type=str)

@click.argument('passive',

&#x20;               type=bool,

&#x20;               default=False)

@click.argument('durable',

&#x20;               type=bool,

&#x20;               default=False)

@click.argument('auto\_delete',

&#x20;               type=bool,

&#x20;               default=False)

@click.pass\_obj

def queue\_declare(amqp\_context, queue, passive, durable, auto\_delete):

&#x20;   if amqp\_context.channel is None:

&#x20;       amqp\_context.echo\_error('Not connected to broker. Please retry...')

&#x20;       amqp\_context.reconnect()

&#x20;   else:

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           retval = amqp\_context.channel.queue\_declare(queue=queue,

&#x20;                                                       passive=passive,

&#x20;                                                       durable=durable,

&#x20;                                                       auto\_delete=auto\_delete)

&#x20;       except Exception as e:

&#x20;           amqp\_context.echo\_error(e)

&#x20;           amqp\_context.reconnect()

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           amqp\_context.cli\_context.secho(

&#x20;               'queue:{} messages:{} consumers:{}'.format(\*retval),

&#x20;               fg='cyan', bold=True)

&#x20;           amqp\_context.echo\_ok()





@amqp.command(name='queue.delete')

@click.argument('queue',

&#x20;               type=str)

@click.argument('if\_unused',

&#x20;               type=bool,

&#x20;               default=False)

@click.argument('if\_empty',

&#x20;               type=bool,

&#x20;               default=False)

@click.pass\_obj

def queue\_delete(amqp\_context, queue, if\_unused, if\_empty):

&#x20;   if amqp\_context.channel is None:

&#x20;       amqp\_context.echo\_error('Not connected to broker. Please retry...')

&#x20;       amqp\_context.reconnect()

&#x20;   else:

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           retval = amqp\_context.channel.queue\_delete(queue=queue,

&#x20;                                                      if\_unused=if\_unused,

&#x20;                                                      if\_empty=if\_empty)

&#x20;       except Exception as e:

&#x20;           amqp\_context.echo\_error(e)

&#x20;           amqp\_context.reconnect()

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           amqp\_context.cli\_context.secho(

&#x20;               f'{retval} messages deleted.',

&#x20;               fg='cyan', bold=True)

&#x20;           amqp\_context.echo\_ok()





@amqp.command(name='queue.purge')

@click.argument('queue',

&#x20;               type=str)

@click.pass\_obj

def queue\_purge(amqp\_context, queue):

&#x20;   if amqp\_context.channel is None:

&#x20;       amqp\_context.echo\_error('Not connected to broker. Please retry...')

&#x20;       amqp\_context.reconnect()

&#x20;   else:

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           retval = amqp\_context.channel.queue\_purge(queue=queue)

&#x20;       except Exception as e:

&#x20;           amqp\_context.echo\_error(e)

&#x20;           amqp\_context.reconnect()

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           amqp\_context.cli\_context.secho(

&#x20;               f'{retval} messages deleted.',

&#x20;               fg='cyan', bold=True)

&#x20;           amqp\_context.echo\_ok()





@amqp.command(name='basic.get')

@click.argument('queue',

&#x20;               type=str)

@click.argument('no\_ack',

&#x20;               type=bool,

&#x20;               default=False)

@click.pass\_obj

def basic\_get(amqp\_context, queue, no\_ack):

&#x20;   if amqp\_context.channel is None:

&#x20;       amqp\_context.echo\_error('Not connected to broker. Please retry...')

&#x20;       amqp\_context.reconnect()

&#x20;   else:

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           message = amqp\_context.channel.basic\_get(queue, no\_ack=no\_ack)

&#x20;       except Exception as e:

&#x20;           amqp\_context.echo\_error(e)

&#x20;           amqp\_context.reconnect()

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           amqp\_context.respond(dump\_message(message))

&#x20;           amqp\_context.echo\_ok()





@amqp.command(name='basic.publish')

@click.argument('msg',

&#x20;               type=str)

@click.argument('exchange',

&#x20;               type=str)

@click.argument('routing\_key',

&#x20;               type=str)

@click.argument('mandatory',

&#x20;               type=bool,

&#x20;               default=False)

@click.argument('immediate',

&#x20;               type=bool,

&#x20;               default=False)

@click.pass\_obj

def basic\_publish(amqp\_context, msg, exchange, routing\_key, mandatory,

&#x20;                 immediate):

&#x20;   if amqp\_context.channel is None:

&#x20;       amqp\_context.echo\_error('Not connected to broker. Please retry...')

&#x20;       amqp\_context.reconnect()

&#x20;   else:

&#x20;       # XXX Hack to fix Issue #2013

&#x20;       if isinstance(amqp\_context.connection.connection, Connection):

&#x20;           msg = Message(msg)

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           amqp\_context.channel.basic\_publish(msg,

&#x20;                                              exchange=exchange,

&#x20;                                              routing\_key=routing\_key,

&#x20;                                              mandatory=mandatory,

&#x20;                                              immediate=immediate)

&#x20;       except Exception as e:

&#x20;           amqp\_context.echo\_error(e)

&#x20;           amqp\_context.reconnect()

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           amqp\_context.echo\_ok()





@amqp.command(name='basic.ack')

@click.argument('delivery\_tag',

&#x20;               type=int)

@click.pass\_obj

def basic\_ack(amqp\_context, delivery\_tag):

&#x20;   if amqp\_context.channel is None:

&#x20;       amqp\_context.echo\_error('Not connected to broker. Please retry...')

&#x20;       amqp\_context.reconnect()

&#x20;   else:

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           amqp\_context.channel.basic\_ack(delivery\_tag)

&#x20;       except Exception as e:

&#x20;           amqp\_context.echo\_error(e)

&#x20;           amqp\_context.reconnect()

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           amqp\_context.echo\_ok()





register\_repl(amqp)
```

```python

"""Click customizations for Celery."""

import json

import numbers

from collections import OrderedDict

from functools import update\_wrapper

from pprint import pformat

from typing import Any



import click

from click import Context, ParamType

from kombu.utils.objects import cached\_property



from celery.\_state import get\_current\_app

from celery.signals import user\_preload\_options

from celery.utils import text

from celery.utils.log import mlevel

from celery.utils.time import maybe\_iso8601



try:

&#x20;   from pygments import highlight

&#x20;   from pygments.formatters import Terminal256Formatter

&#x20;   from pygments.lexers import PythonLexer

except ImportError:

&#x20;   def highlight(s, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       """Place holder function in case pygments is missing."""

&#x20;       return s

&#x20;   LEXER = None

&#x20;   FORMATTER = None

else:

&#x20;   LEXER = PythonLexer()

&#x20;   FORMATTER = Terminal256Formatter()





class CLIContext:

&#x20;   """Context Object for the CLI."""



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, app, no\_color, workdir, quiet=False):

&#x20;       """Initialize the CLI context."""

&#x20;       self.app = app or get\_current\_app()

&#x20;       self.no\_color = no\_color

&#x20;       self.quiet = quiet

&#x20;       self.workdir = workdir



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def OK(self):

&#x20;       return self.style("OK", fg="green", bold=True)



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def ERROR(self):

&#x20;       return self.style("ERROR", fg="red", bold=True)



&#x20;   def style(self, message=None, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       if self.no\_color:

&#x20;           return message

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           return click.style(message, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def secho(self, message=None, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       if self.no\_color:

&#x20;           kwargs\['color'] = False

&#x20;           click.echo(message, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           click.secho(message, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def echo(self, message=None, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       if self.no\_color:

&#x20;           kwargs\['color'] = False

&#x20;           click.echo(message, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           click.echo(message, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def error(self, message=None, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       kwargs\['err'] = True

&#x20;       if self.no\_color:

&#x20;           kwargs\['color'] = False

&#x20;           click.echo(message, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           click.secho(message, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def pretty(self, n):

&#x20;       if isinstance(n, list):

&#x20;           return self.OK, self.pretty\_list(n)

&#x20;       if isinstance(n, dict):

&#x20;           if 'ok' in n or 'error' in n:

&#x20;               return self.pretty\_dict\_ok\_error(n)

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               s = json.dumps(n, sort\_keys=True, indent=4)

&#x20;               if not self.no\_color:

&#x20;                   s = highlight(s, LEXER, FORMATTER)

&#x20;               return self.OK, s

&#x20;       if isinstance(n, str):

&#x20;           return self.OK, n

&#x20;       return self.OK, pformat(n)



&#x20;   def pretty\_list(self, n):

&#x20;       if not n:

&#x20;           return '- empty -'

&#x20;       return '\\n'.join(

&#x20;           f'{self.style("\*", fg="white")} {item}' for item in n

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def pretty\_dict\_ok\_error(self, n):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           return (self.OK,

&#x20;                   text.indent(self.pretty(n\['ok'])\[1], 4))

&#x20;       except KeyError:

&#x20;           pass

&#x20;       return (self.ERROR,

&#x20;               text.indent(self.pretty(n\['error'])\[1], 4))



&#x20;   def say\_chat(self, direction, title, body='', show\_body=False):

&#x20;       if direction == '<-' and self.quiet:

&#x20;           return

&#x20;       dirstr = not self.quiet and f'{self.style(direction, fg="white", bold=True)} ' or ''

&#x20;       self.echo(f'{dirstr} {title}')

&#x20;       if body and show\_body:

&#x20;           self.echo(body)





def handle\_preload\_options(f):

&#x20;   """Extract preload options and return a wrapped callable."""

&#x20;   def caller(ctx, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       app = ctx.obj.app



&#x20;       preload\_options = \[o.name for o in app.user\_options.get('preload', \[])]



&#x20;       if preload\_options:

&#x20;           user\_options = {

&#x20;               preload\_option: kwargs\[preload\_option]

&#x20;               for preload\_option in preload\_options

&#x20;           }



&#x20;           user\_preload\_options.send(sender=f, app=app, options=user\_options)



&#x20;       return f(ctx, \*args, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   return update\_wrapper(caller, f)





class CeleryOption(click.Option):

&#x20;   """Customized option for Celery."""



&#x20;   def get\_default(self, ctx, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       if self.default\_value\_from\_context:

&#x20;           self.default = ctx.obj\[self.default\_value\_from\_context]

&#x20;       return super().get\_default(ctx, \*args, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       """Initialize a Celery option."""

&#x20;       self.help\_group = kwargs.pop('help\_group', None)

&#x20;       self.default\_value\_from\_context = kwargs.pop('default\_value\_from\_context', None)

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(\*args, \*\*kwargs)





class CeleryCommand(click.Command):

&#x20;   """Customized command for Celery."""



&#x20;   def format\_options(self, ctx, formatter):

&#x20;       """Write all the options into the formatter if they exist."""

&#x20;       opts = OrderedDict()

&#x20;       for param in self.get\_params(ctx):

&#x20;           rv = param.get\_help\_record(ctx)

&#x20;           if rv is not None:

&#x20;               if hasattr(param, 'help\_group') and param.help\_group:

&#x20;                   opts.setdefault(str(param.help\_group), \[]).append(rv)

&#x20;               else:

&#x20;                   opts.setdefault('Options', \[]).append(rv)



&#x20;       for name, opts\_group in opts.items():

&#x20;           with formatter.section(name):

&#x20;               formatter.write\_dl(opts\_group)





class DaemonOption(CeleryOption):

&#x20;   """Common daemonization option"""



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(args,

&#x20;                        help\_group=kwargs.pop("help\_group", "Daemonization Options"),

&#x20;                        callback=kwargs.pop("callback", self.daemon\_setting),

&#x20;                        \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def daemon\_setting(self, ctx: Context, opt: CeleryOption, value: Any) -> Any:

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       Try to fetch daemonization option from applications settings.

&#x20;       Use the daemon command name as prefix (eg. `worker` -> `worker\_pidfile`)

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return value or getattr(ctx.obj.app.conf, f"{ctx.command.name}\_{self.name}", None)





class CeleryDaemonCommand(CeleryCommand):

&#x20;   """Daemon commands."""



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       """Initialize a Celery command with common daemon options."""

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(\*args, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;       self.params.extend((

&#x20;           DaemonOption("--logfile", "-f", help="Log destination; defaults to stderr"),

&#x20;           DaemonOption("--pidfile", help="PID file path; defaults to no PID file"),

&#x20;           DaemonOption("--uid", help="Drops privileges to this user ID"),

&#x20;           DaemonOption("--gid", help="Drops privileges to this group ID"),

&#x20;           DaemonOption("--umask", help="Create files and directories with this umask"),

&#x20;           DaemonOption("--executable", help="Override path to the Python executable"),

&#x20;       ))





class CommaSeparatedList(ParamType):

&#x20;   """Comma separated list argument."""



&#x20;   name = "comma separated list"



&#x20;   def convert(self, value, param, ctx):

&#x20;       return text.str\_to\_list(value)





class JsonArray(ParamType):

&#x20;   """JSON formatted array argument."""



&#x20;   name = "json array"



&#x20;   def convert(self, value, param, ctx):

&#x20;       if isinstance(value, list):

&#x20;           return value



&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           v = json.loads(value)

&#x20;       except ValueError as e:

&#x20;           self.fail(str(e))



&#x20;       if not isinstance(v, list):

&#x20;           self.fail(f"{value} was not an array")



&#x20;       return v





class JsonObject(ParamType):

&#x20;   """JSON formatted object argument."""



&#x20;   name = "json object"



&#x20;   def convert(self, value, param, ctx):

&#x20;       if isinstance(value, dict):

&#x20;           return value



&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           v = json.loads(value)

&#x20;       except ValueError as e:

&#x20;           self.fail(str(e))



&#x20;       if not isinstance(v, dict):

&#x20;           self.fail(f"{value} was not an object")



&#x20;       return v





class ISO8601DateTime(ParamType):

&#x20;   """ISO 8601 Date Time argument."""



&#x20;   name = "iso-86091"



&#x20;   def convert(self, value, param, ctx):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           return maybe\_iso8601(value)

&#x20;       except (TypeError, ValueError) as e:

&#x20;           self.fail(e)





class ISO8601DateTimeOrFloat(ParamType):

&#x20;   """ISO 8601 Date Time or float argument."""



&#x20;   name = "iso-86091 or float"



&#x20;   def convert(self, value, param, ctx):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           return float(value)

&#x20;       except (TypeError, ValueError):

&#x20;           pass



&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           return maybe\_iso8601(value)

&#x20;       except (TypeError, ValueError) as e:

&#x20;           self.fail(e)





class LogLevel(click.Choice):

&#x20;   """Log level option."""



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self):

&#x20;       """Initialize the log level option with the relevant choices."""

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(('DEBUG', 'INFO', 'WARNING', 'ERROR', 'CRITICAL', 'FATAL'))



&#x20;   def convert(self, value, param, ctx):

&#x20;       if isinstance(value, numbers.Integral):

&#x20;           return value



&#x20;       value = value.upper()

&#x20;       value = super().convert(value, param, ctx)

&#x20;       return mlevel(value)





JSON\_ARRAY = JsonArray()

JSON\_OBJECT = JsonObject()

ISO8601 = ISO8601DateTime()

ISO8601\_OR\_FLOAT = ISO8601DateTimeOrFloat()

LOG\_LEVEL = LogLevel()

COMMA\_SEPARATED\_LIST = CommaSeparatedList()



```

```python

"""The :program:`celery beat` command."""

from functools import partial



import click



from celery.bin.base import LOG\_LEVEL, CeleryDaemonCommand, CeleryOption, handle\_preload\_options

from celery.platforms import detached, maybe\_drop\_privileges





@click.command(cls=CeleryDaemonCommand, context\_settings={

&#x20;   'allow\_extra\_args': True

})

@click.option('--detach',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             is\_flag=True,

&#x20;             default=False,

&#x20;             help\_group="Beat Options",

&#x20;             help="Detach and run in the background as a daemon.")

@click.option('-s',

&#x20;             '--schedule',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             callback=lambda ctx, \_, value: value or ctx.obj.app.conf.beat\_schedule\_filename,

&#x20;             help\_group="Beat Options",

&#x20;             help="Path to the schedule database."

&#x20;                  "  Defaults to `celerybeat-schedule`."

&#x20;                  "The extension '.db' may be appended to the filename.")

@click.option('-S',

&#x20;             '--scheduler',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             callback=lambda ctx, \_, value: value or ctx.obj.app.conf.beat\_scheduler,

&#x20;             help\_group="Beat Options",

&#x20;             help="Scheduler class to use.")

@click.option('--max-interval',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             type=int,

&#x20;             help\_group="Beat Options",

&#x20;             help="Max seconds to sleep between schedule iterations.")

@click.option('-l',

&#x20;             '--loglevel',

&#x20;             default='WARNING',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             type=LOG\_LEVEL,

&#x20;             help\_group="Beat Options",

&#x20;             help="Logging level.")

@click.pass\_context

@handle\_preload\_options

def beat(ctx, detach=False, logfile=None, pidfile=None, uid=None,

&#x20;        gid=None, umask=None, workdir=None, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;   """Start the beat periodic task scheduler."""

&#x20;   app = ctx.obj.app



&#x20;   if ctx.args:

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           app.config\_from\_cmdline(ctx.args)

&#x20;       except (KeyError, ValueError) as e:

&#x20;           # TODO: Improve the error messages

&#x20;           raise click.UsageError("Unable to parse extra configuration"

&#x20;                                  " from command line.\\n"

&#x20;                                  f"Reason: {e}", ctx=ctx)



&#x20;   if not detach:

&#x20;       maybe\_drop\_privileges(uid=uid, gid=gid)



&#x20;   beat = partial(app.Beat,

&#x20;                  logfile=logfile, pidfile=pidfile,

&#x20;                  quiet=ctx.obj.quiet, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   if detach:

&#x20;       with detached(logfile, pidfile, uid, gid, umask, workdir):

&#x20;           return beat().run()

&#x20;   else:

&#x20;       return beat().run()



```

```python
"""The ``celery call`` program used to send tasks from the command-line."""

import click



from celery.bin.base import (ISO8601, ISO8601\_OR\_FLOAT, JSON\_ARRAY, JSON\_OBJECT, CeleryCommand, CeleryOption,

&#x20;                            handle\_preload\_options)





@click.command(cls=CeleryCommand)

@click.argument('name')

@click.option('-a',

&#x20;             '--args',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             type=JSON\_ARRAY,

&#x20;             default='\[]',

&#x20;             help\_group="Calling Options",

&#x20;             help="Positional arguments.")

@click.option('-k',

&#x20;             '--kwargs',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             type=JSON\_OBJECT,

&#x20;             default='{}',

&#x20;             help\_group="Calling Options",

&#x20;             help="Keyword arguments.")

@click.option('--eta',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             type=ISO8601,

&#x20;             help\_group="Calling Options",

&#x20;             help="scheduled time.")

@click.option('--countdown',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             type=float,

&#x20;             help\_group="Calling Options",

&#x20;             help="eta in seconds from now.")

@click.option('--expires',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             type=ISO8601\_OR\_FLOAT,

&#x20;             help\_group="Calling Options",

&#x20;             help="expiry time.")

@click.option('--serializer',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             default='json',

&#x20;             help\_group="Calling Options",

&#x20;             help="task serializer.")

@click.option('--queue',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group="Routing Options",

&#x20;             help="custom queue name.")

@click.option('--exchange',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group="Routing Options",

&#x20;             help="custom exchange name.")

@click.option('--routing-key',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group="Routing Options",

&#x20;             help="custom routing key.")

@click.pass\_context

@handle\_preload\_options

def call(ctx, name, args, kwargs, eta, countdown, expires, serializer, queue, exchange, routing\_key):

&#x20;   """Call a task by name."""

&#x20;   task\_id = ctx.obj.app.send\_task(

&#x20;       name,

&#x20;       args=args, kwargs=kwargs,

&#x20;       countdown=countdown,

&#x20;       serializer=serializer,

&#x20;       queue=queue,

&#x20;       exchange=exchange,

&#x20;       routing\_key=routing\_key,

&#x20;       eta=eta,

&#x20;       expires=expires

&#x20;   ).id

&#x20;   ctx.obj.echo(task\_id)



```

```python
"""Celery Command Line Interface."""

import os

import pathlib

import sys

import traceback

from importlib.metadata import entry\_points



import click

import click.exceptions

from click\_didyoumean import DYMGroup

from click\_plugins import with\_plugins



from celery import VERSION\_BANNER

from celery.app.utils import find\_app

from celery.bin.amqp import amqp

from celery.bin.base import CeleryCommand, CeleryOption, CLIContext

from celery.bin.beat import beat

from celery.bin.call import call

from celery.bin.control import control, inspect, status

from celery.bin.events import events

from celery.bin.graph import graph

from celery.bin.list import list\_

from celery.bin.logtool import logtool

from celery.bin.migrate import migrate

from celery.bin.multi import multi

from celery.bin.purge import purge

from celery.bin.result import result

from celery.bin.shell import shell

from celery.bin.upgrade import upgrade

from celery.bin.worker import worker



UNABLE\_TO\_LOAD\_APP\_MODULE\_NOT\_FOUND = click.style("""

Unable to load celery application.

The module {0} was not found.""", fg='red')



UNABLE\_TO\_LOAD\_APP\_ERROR\_OCCURRED = click.style("""

Unable to load celery application.

While trying to load the module {0} the following error occurred:

{1}""", fg='red')



UNABLE\_TO\_LOAD\_APP\_APP\_MISSING = click.style("""

Unable to load celery application.

{0}""")





if sys.version\_info >= (3, 10):

&#x20;   \_PLUGINS = entry\_points(group='celery.commands')

else:

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       \_PLUGINS = entry\_points().get('celery.commands', \[])

&#x20;   except AttributeError:

&#x20;       \_PLUGINS = entry\_points().select(group='celery.commands')





@with\_plugins(\_PLUGINS)

@click.group(cls=DYMGroup, invoke\_without\_command=True)

@click.option('-A',

&#x20;             '--app',

&#x20;             envvar='APP',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             # May take either: a str when invoked from command line (Click),

&#x20;             # or a Celery object when invoked from inside Celery; hence the

&#x20;             # need to prevent Click from "processing" the Celery object and

&#x20;             # converting it into its str representation.

&#x20;             type=click.UNPROCESSED,

&#x20;             help\_group="Global Options")

@click.option('-b',

&#x20;             '--broker',

&#x20;             envvar='BROKER\_URL',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group="Global Options")

@click.option('--result-backend',

&#x20;             envvar='RESULT\_BACKEND',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group="Global Options")

@click.option('--loader',

&#x20;             envvar='LOADER',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group="Global Options")

@click.option('--config',

&#x20;             envvar='CONFIG\_MODULE',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group="Global Options")

@click.option('--workdir',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             type=pathlib.Path,

&#x20;             callback=lambda \_, \_\_, wd: os.chdir(wd) if wd else None,

&#x20;             is\_eager=True,

&#x20;             help\_group="Global Options")

@click.option('-C',

&#x20;             '--no-color',

&#x20;             envvar='NO\_COLOR',

&#x20;             is\_flag=True,

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group="Global Options")

@click.option('-q',

&#x20;             '--quiet',

&#x20;             is\_flag=True,

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group="Global Options")

@click.option('--version',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             is\_flag=True,

&#x20;             help\_group="Global Options")

@click.option('--skip-checks',

&#x20;             envvar='SKIP\_CHECKS',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             is\_flag=True,

&#x20;             help\_group="Global Options",

&#x20;             help="Skip Django core checks on startup. Setting the SKIP\_CHECKS environment "

&#x20;                  "variable to any non-empty string will have the same effect.")

@click.pass\_context

def celery(ctx, app, broker, result\_backend, loader, config, workdir,

&#x20;          no\_color, quiet, version, skip\_checks):

&#x20;   """Celery command entrypoint."""

&#x20;   if version:

&#x20;       click.echo(VERSION\_BANNER)

&#x20;       ctx.exit()

&#x20;   elif ctx.invoked\_subcommand is None:

&#x20;       click.echo(ctx.get\_help())

&#x20;       ctx.exit()



&#x20;   if loader:

&#x20;       # Default app takes loader from this env (Issue #1066).

&#x20;       os.environ\['CELERY\_LOADER'] = loader

&#x20;   if broker:

&#x20;       os.environ\['CELERY\_BROKER\_URL'] = broker

&#x20;   if result\_backend:

&#x20;       os.environ\['CELERY\_RESULT\_BACKEND'] = result\_backend

&#x20;   if config:

&#x20;       os.environ\['CELERY\_CONFIG\_MODULE'] = config

&#x20;   if skip\_checks:

&#x20;       os.environ\['CELERY\_SKIP\_CHECKS'] = 'true'



&#x20;   if isinstance(app, str):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           app = find\_app(app)

&#x20;       except ModuleNotFoundError as e:

&#x20;           if e.name != app:

&#x20;               exc = traceback.format\_exc()

&#x20;               ctx.fail(

&#x20;                   UNABLE\_TO\_LOAD\_APP\_ERROR\_OCCURRED.format(app, exc)

&#x20;               )

&#x20;           ctx.fail(UNABLE\_TO\_LOAD\_APP\_MODULE\_NOT\_FOUND.format(e.name))

&#x20;       except AttributeError as e:

&#x20;           attribute\_name = e.args\[0].capitalize()

&#x20;           ctx.fail(UNABLE\_TO\_LOAD\_APP\_APP\_MISSING.format(attribute\_name))

&#x20;       except Exception:

&#x20;           exc = traceback.format\_exc()

&#x20;           ctx.fail(

&#x20;               UNABLE\_TO\_LOAD\_APP\_ERROR\_OCCURRED.format(app, exc)

&#x20;           )



&#x20;   ctx.obj = CLIContext(app=app, no\_color=no\_color, workdir=workdir,

&#x20;                        quiet=quiet)



&#x20;   # User options

&#x20;   worker.params.extend(ctx.obj.app.user\_options.get('worker', \[]))

&#x20;   beat.params.extend(ctx.obj.app.user\_options.get('beat', \[]))

&#x20;   events.params.extend(ctx.obj.app.user\_options.get('events', \[]))



&#x20;   for command in celery.commands.values():

&#x20;       command.params.extend(ctx.obj.app.user\_options.get('preload', \[]))





@celery.command(cls=CeleryCommand)

@click.pass\_context

def report(ctx, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;   """Shows information useful to include in bug-reports."""

&#x20;   app = ctx.obj.app

&#x20;   app.loader.import\_default\_modules()

&#x20;   ctx.obj.echo(app.bugreport())





celery.add\_command(purge)

celery.add\_command(call)

celery.add\_command(beat)

celery.add\_command(list\_)

celery.add\_command(result)

celery.add\_command(migrate)

celery.add\_command(status)

celery.add\_command(worker)

celery.add\_command(events)

celery.add\_command(inspect)

celery.add\_command(control)

celery.add\_command(graph)

celery.add\_command(upgrade)

celery.add\_command(logtool)

celery.add\_command(amqp)

celery.add\_command(shell)

celery.add\_command(multi)



\# Monkey-patch click to display a custom error

\# when -A or --app are used as sub-command options instead of as options

\# of the global command.



previous\_show\_implementation = click.exceptions.NoSuchOption.show



WRONG\_APP\_OPTION\_USAGE\_MESSAGE = """You are using `{option\_name}` as an option of the {info\_name} sub-command:

celery {info\_name} {option\_name} celeryapp <...>



The support for this usage was removed in Celery 5.0. Instead you should use `{option\_name}` as a global option:

celery {option\_name} celeryapp {info\_name} <...>"""





def \_show(self, file=None):

&#x20;   if self.option\_name in ('-A', '--app'):

&#x20;       self.ctx.obj.error(

&#x20;           WRONG\_APP\_OPTION\_USAGE\_MESSAGE.format(

&#x20;               option\_name=self.option\_name,

&#x20;               info\_name=self.ctx.info\_name),

&#x20;           fg='red'

&#x20;       )

&#x20;   previous\_show\_implementation(self, file=file)





click.exceptions.NoSuchOption.show = \_show





def main() -> int:

&#x20;   """Start celery umbrella command.



&#x20;   This function is the main entrypoint for the CLI.



&#x20;   :return: The exit code of the CLI.

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   return celery(auto\_envvar\_prefix="CELERY")



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\bin\\control.py

```python

"""The ``celery control``, ``. inspect`` and ``. status`` programs."""

from functools import partial

from typing import Literal



import click

from kombu.utils.json import dumps



from celery.bin.base import COMMA\_SEPARATED\_LIST, CeleryCommand, CeleryOption, handle\_preload\_options

from celery.exceptions import CeleryCommandException

from celery.platforms import EX\_UNAVAILABLE

from celery.utils import text

from celery.worker.control import Panel





def \_say\_remote\_command\_reply(ctx, replies, show\_reply=False):

&#x20;   node = next(iter(replies))  # <-- take first.

&#x20;   reply = replies\[node]

&#x20;   node = ctx.obj.style(f'{node}: ', fg='cyan', bold=True)

&#x20;   status, preply = ctx.obj.pretty(reply)

&#x20;   ctx.obj.say\_chat('->', f'{node}{status}',

&#x20;                    text.indent(preply, 4) if show\_reply else '',

&#x20;                    show\_body=show\_reply)





def \_consume\_arguments(meta, method, args):

&#x20;   i = 0

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       for i, arg in enumerate(args):

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               name, typ = meta.args\[i]

&#x20;           except IndexError:

&#x20;               if meta.variadic:

&#x20;                   break

&#x20;               raise click.UsageError(

&#x20;                   'Command {!r} takes arguments: {}'.format(

&#x20;                       method, meta.signature))

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               yield name, typ(arg) if typ is not None else arg

&#x20;   finally:

&#x20;       args\[:] = args\[i:]





def \_compile\_arguments(command, args):

&#x20;   meta = Panel.meta\[command]

&#x20;   arguments = {}

&#x20;   if meta.args:

&#x20;       arguments.update({

&#x20;           k: v for k, v in \_consume\_arguments(meta, command, args)

&#x20;       })

&#x20;   if meta.variadic:

&#x20;       arguments.update({meta.variadic: args})

&#x20;   return arguments





\_RemoteControlType = Literal\['inspect', 'control']





def \_verify\_command\_name(type\_: \_RemoteControlType, command: str) -> None:

&#x20;   choices = \_get\_commands\_of\_type(type\_)



&#x20;   if command not in choices:

&#x20;       command\_listing = ", ".join(choices)

&#x20;       raise click.UsageError(

&#x20;           message=f'Command {command} not recognized. Available {type\_} commands: {command\_listing}',

&#x20;       )





def \_list\_option(type\_: \_RemoteControlType):

&#x20;   def callback(ctx: click.Context, param, value) -> None:

&#x20;       if not value:

&#x20;           return

&#x20;       choices = \_get\_commands\_of\_type(type\_)



&#x20;       formatter = click.HelpFormatter()



&#x20;       with formatter.section(f'{type\_.capitalize()} Commands'):

&#x20;           command\_list = \[]

&#x20;           for command\_name, info in choices.items():

&#x20;               if info.signature:

&#x20;                   command\_preview = f'{command\_name} {info.signature}'

&#x20;               else:

&#x20;                   command\_preview = command\_name

&#x20;               command\_list.append((command\_preview, info.help))

&#x20;           formatter.write\_dl(command\_list)

&#x20;       ctx.obj.echo(formatter.getvalue(), nl=False)

&#x20;       ctx.exit()



&#x20;   return click.option(

&#x20;       '--list',

&#x20;       is\_flag=True,

&#x20;       help=f'List available {type\_} commands and exit.',

&#x20;       expose\_value=False,

&#x20;       is\_eager=True,

&#x20;       callback=callback,

&#x20;   )





def \_get\_commands\_of\_type(type\_: \_RemoteControlType) -> dict:

&#x20;   command\_name\_info\_pairs = \[

&#x20;       (name, info) for name, info in Panel.meta.items()

&#x20;       if info.type == type\_ and info.visible

&#x20;   ]

&#x20;   return dict(sorted(command\_name\_info\_pairs))





@click.command(cls=CeleryCommand)

@click.option('-t',

&#x20;             '--timeout',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             type=float,

&#x20;             default=1.0,

&#x20;             help\_group='Remote Control Options',

&#x20;             help='Timeout in seconds waiting for reply.')

@click.option('-d',

&#x20;             '--destination',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             type=COMMA\_SEPARATED\_LIST,

&#x20;             help\_group='Remote Control Options',

&#x20;             help='Comma separated list of destination node names.')

@click.option('-j',

&#x20;             '--json',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             is\_flag=True,

&#x20;             help\_group='Remote Control Options',

&#x20;             help='Use json as output format.')

@click.pass\_context

@handle\_preload\_options

def status(ctx, timeout, destination, json, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;   """Show list of workers that are online."""

&#x20;   callback = None if json else partial(\_say\_remote\_command\_reply, ctx)

&#x20;   replies = ctx.obj.app.control.inspect(timeout=timeout,

&#x20;                                         destination=destination,

&#x20;                                         callback=callback).ping()



&#x20;   if not replies:

&#x20;       raise CeleryCommandException(

&#x20;           message='No nodes replied within time constraint',

&#x20;           exit\_code=EX\_UNAVAILABLE

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   if json:

&#x20;       ctx.obj.echo(dumps(replies))

&#x20;   nodecount = len(replies)

&#x20;   if not kwargs.get('quiet', False):

&#x20;       ctx.obj.echo('\\n{} {} online.'.format(

&#x20;           nodecount, text.pluralize(nodecount, 'node')))





@click.command(cls=CeleryCommand,

&#x20;              context\_settings={'allow\_extra\_args': True})

@click.argument('command')

@\_list\_option('inspect')

@click.option('-t',

&#x20;             '--timeout',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             type=float,

&#x20;             default=1.0,

&#x20;             help\_group='Remote Control Options',

&#x20;             help='Timeout in seconds waiting for reply.')

@click.option('-d',

&#x20;             '--destination',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             type=COMMA\_SEPARATED\_LIST,

&#x20;             help\_group='Remote Control Options',

&#x20;             help='Comma separated list of destination node names.')

@click.option('-j',

&#x20;             '--json',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             is\_flag=True,

&#x20;             help\_group='Remote Control Options',

&#x20;             help='Use json as output format.')

@click.pass\_context

@handle\_preload\_options

def inspect(ctx, command, timeout, destination, json, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;   """Inspect the workers by sending them the COMMAND inspect command.



&#x20;   Availability: RabbitMQ (AMQP) and Redis transports.

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   \_verify\_command\_name('inspect', command)

&#x20;   callback = None if json else partial(\_say\_remote\_command\_reply, ctx,

&#x20;                                        show\_reply=True)

&#x20;   arguments = \_compile\_arguments(command, ctx.args)

&#x20;   inspect = ctx.obj.app.control.inspect(timeout=timeout,

&#x20;                                         destination=destination,

&#x20;                                         callback=callback)

&#x20;   replies = inspect.\_request(command, \*\*arguments)



&#x20;   if not replies:

&#x20;       raise CeleryCommandException(

&#x20;           message='No nodes replied within time constraint',

&#x20;           exit\_code=EX\_UNAVAILABLE

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   if json:

&#x20;       ctx.obj.echo(dumps(replies))

&#x20;       return



&#x20;   nodecount = len(replies)

&#x20;   if not ctx.obj.quiet:

&#x20;       ctx.obj.echo('\\n{} {} online.'.format(

&#x20;           nodecount, text.pluralize(nodecount, 'node')))





@click.command(cls=CeleryCommand,

&#x20;              context\_settings={'allow\_extra\_args': True})

@click.argument('command')

@\_list\_option('control')

@click.option('-t',

&#x20;             '--timeout',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             type=float,

&#x20;             default=1.0,

&#x20;             help\_group='Remote Control Options',

&#x20;             help='Timeout in seconds waiting for reply.')

@click.option('-d',

&#x20;             '--destination',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             type=COMMA\_SEPARATED\_LIST,

&#x20;             help\_group='Remote Control Options',

&#x20;             help='Comma separated list of destination node names.')

@click.option('-j',

&#x20;             '--json',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             is\_flag=True,

&#x20;             help\_group='Remote Control Options',

&#x20;             help='Use json as output format.')

@click.pass\_context

@handle\_preload\_options

def control(ctx, command, timeout, destination, json):

&#x20;   """Send the COMMAND control command to the workers.



&#x20;   Availability: RabbitMQ (AMQP), Redis, and MongoDB transports.

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   \_verify\_command\_name('control', command)

&#x20;   callback = None if json else partial(\_say\_remote\_command\_reply, ctx,

&#x20;                                        show\_reply=True)

&#x20;   args = ctx.args

&#x20;   arguments = \_compile\_arguments(command, args)

&#x20;   replies = ctx.obj.app.control.broadcast(command, timeout=timeout,

&#x20;                                           destination=destination,

&#x20;                                           callback=callback,

&#x20;                                           reply=True,

&#x20;                                           arguments=arguments)



&#x20;   if not replies:

&#x20;       raise CeleryCommandException(

&#x20;           message='No nodes replied within time constraint',

&#x20;           exit\_code=EX\_UNAVAILABLE

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   if json:

&#x20;       ctx.obj.echo(dumps(replies))



```

```python
"""The ``celery events`` program."""

import sys

from functools import partial



import click



from celery.bin.base import LOG\_LEVEL, CeleryDaemonCommand, CeleryOption, handle\_preload\_options

from celery.platforms import detached, set\_process\_title, strargv





def \_set\_process\_status(prog, info=''):

&#x20;   prog = '{}:{}'.format('celery events', prog)

&#x20;   info = f'{info} {strargv(sys.argv)}'

&#x20;   return set\_process\_title(prog, info=info)





def \_run\_evdump(app):

&#x20;   from celery.events.dumper import evdump

&#x20;   \_set\_process\_status('dump')

&#x20;   return evdump(app=app)





def \_run\_evcam(camera, app, logfile=None, pidfile=None, uid=None,

&#x20;              gid=None, umask=None, workdir=None,

&#x20;              detach=False, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;   from celery.events.snapshot import evcam

&#x20;   \_set\_process\_status('cam')

&#x20;   kwargs\['app'] = app

&#x20;   cam = partial(evcam, camera,

&#x20;                 logfile=logfile, pidfile=pidfile, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   if detach:

&#x20;       with detached(logfile, pidfile, uid, gid, umask, workdir):

&#x20;           return cam()

&#x20;   else:

&#x20;       return cam()





def \_run\_evtop(app):

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       from celery.events.cursesmon import evtop

&#x20;       \_set\_process\_status('top')

&#x20;       return evtop(app=app)

&#x20;   except ModuleNotFoundError as e:

&#x20;       if e.name == '\_curses':

&#x20;           # TODO: Improve this error message

&#x20;           raise click.UsageError("The curses module is required for this command.")





@click.command(cls=CeleryDaemonCommand)

@click.option('-d',

&#x20;             '--dump',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             is\_flag=True,

&#x20;             help\_group='Dumper')

@click.option('-c',

&#x20;             '--camera',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group='Snapshot')

@click.option('-d',

&#x20;             '--detach',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             is\_flag=True,

&#x20;             help\_group='Snapshot')

@click.option('-F', '--frequency', '--freq',

&#x20;             type=float,

&#x20;             default=1.0,

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group='Snapshot')

@click.option('-r', '--maxrate',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group='Snapshot')

@click.option('-l',

&#x20;             '--loglevel',

&#x20;             default='WARNING',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             type=LOG\_LEVEL,

&#x20;             help\_group="Snapshot",

&#x20;             help="Logging level.")

@click.pass\_context

@handle\_preload\_options

def events(ctx, dump, camera, detach, frequency, maxrate, loglevel, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;   """Event-stream utilities."""

&#x20;   app = ctx.obj.app

&#x20;   if dump:

&#x20;       return \_run\_evdump(app)



&#x20;   if camera:

&#x20;       return \_run\_evcam(camera, app=app, freq=frequency, maxrate=maxrate,

&#x20;                         loglevel=loglevel,

&#x20;                         detach=detach,

&#x20;                         \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   return \_run\_evtop(app)



```
