```python
"""The ``celery graph`` command."""

import sys

from operator import itemgetter



import click



from celery.bin.base import CeleryCommand, handle\_preload\_options

from celery.utils.graph import DependencyGraph, GraphFormatter





@click.group()

@click.pass\_context

@handle\_preload\_options

def graph(ctx):

&#x20;   """The ``celery graph`` command."""





@graph.command(cls=CeleryCommand, context\_settings={'allow\_extra\_args': True})

@click.pass\_context

def bootsteps(ctx):

&#x20;   """Display bootsteps graph."""

&#x20;   worker = ctx.obj.app.WorkController()

&#x20;   include = {arg.lower() for arg in ctx.args or \['worker', 'consumer']}

&#x20;   if 'worker' in include:

&#x20;       worker\_graph = worker.blueprint.graph

&#x20;       if 'consumer' in include:

&#x20;           worker.blueprint.connect\_with(worker.consumer.blueprint)

&#x20;   else:

&#x20;       worker\_graph = worker.consumer.blueprint.graph

&#x20;   worker\_graph.to\_dot(sys.stdout)





@graph.command(cls=CeleryCommand, context\_settings={'allow\_extra\_args': True})

@click.pass\_context

def workers(ctx):

&#x20;   """Display workers graph."""

&#x20;   def simplearg(arg):

&#x20;       return maybe\_list(itemgetter(0, 2)(arg.partition(':')))



&#x20;   def maybe\_list(l, sep=','):

&#x20;       return l\[0], l\[1].split(sep) if sep in l\[1] else l\[1]



&#x20;   args = dict(simplearg(arg) for arg in ctx.args)

&#x20;   generic = 'generic' in args



&#x20;   def generic\_label(node):

&#x20;       return '{} ({}://)'.format(type(node).\_\_name\_\_,

&#x20;                                  node.\_label.split('://')\[0])



&#x20;   class Node:

&#x20;       force\_label = None

&#x20;       scheme = {}



&#x20;       def \_\_init\_\_(self, label, pos=None):

&#x20;           self.\_label = label

&#x20;           self.pos = pos



&#x20;       def label(self):

&#x20;           return self.\_label



&#x20;       def \_\_str\_\_(self):

&#x20;           return self.label()



&#x20;   class Thread(Node):

&#x20;       scheme = {

&#x20;           'fillcolor': 'lightcyan4',

&#x20;           'fontcolor': 'yellow',

&#x20;           'shape': 'oval',

&#x20;           'fontsize': 10,

&#x20;           'width': 0.3,

&#x20;           'color': 'black',

&#x20;       }



&#x20;       def \_\_init\_\_(self, label, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;           self.real\_label = label

&#x20;           super().\_\_init\_\_(

&#x20;               label=f'thr-{next(tids)}',

&#x20;               pos=0,

&#x20;           )



&#x20;   class Formatter(GraphFormatter):



&#x20;       def label(self, obj):

&#x20;           return obj and obj.label()



&#x20;       def node(self, obj):

&#x20;           scheme = dict(obj.scheme) if obj.pos else obj.scheme

&#x20;           if isinstance(obj, Thread):

&#x20;               scheme\['label'] = obj.real\_label

&#x20;           return self.draw\_node(

&#x20;               obj, dict(self.node\_scheme, \*\*scheme),

&#x20;           )



&#x20;       def terminal\_node(self, obj):

&#x20;           return self.draw\_node(

&#x20;               obj, dict(self.term\_scheme, \*\*obj.scheme),

&#x20;           )



&#x20;       def edge(self, a, b, \*\*attrs):

&#x20;           if isinstance(a, Thread):

&#x20;               attrs.update(arrowhead='none', arrowtail='tee')

&#x20;           return self.draw\_edge(a, b, self.edge\_scheme, attrs)



&#x20;   def subscript(n):

&#x20;       S = {'0': '₀', '1': '₁', '2': '₂', '3': '₃', '4': '₄',

&#x20;            '5': '₅', '6': '₆', '7': '₇', '8': '₈', '9': '₉'}

&#x20;       return ''.join(\[S\[i] for i in str(n)])



&#x20;   class Worker(Node):

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   class Backend(Node):

&#x20;       scheme = {

&#x20;           'shape': 'folder',

&#x20;           'width': 2,

&#x20;           'height': 1,

&#x20;           'color': 'black',

&#x20;           'fillcolor': 'peachpuff3',

&#x20;       }



&#x20;       def label(self):

&#x20;           return generic\_label(self) if generic else self.\_label



&#x20;   class Broker(Node):

&#x20;       scheme = {

&#x20;           'shape': 'circle',

&#x20;           'fillcolor': 'cadetblue3',

&#x20;           'color': 'cadetblue4',

&#x20;           'height': 1,

&#x20;       }



&#x20;       def label(self):

&#x20;           return generic\_label(self) if generic else self.\_label



&#x20;   from itertools import count

&#x20;   tids = count(1)

&#x20;   Wmax = int(args.get('wmax', 4) or 0)

&#x20;   Tmax = int(args.get('tmax', 3) or 0)



&#x20;   def maybe\_abbr(l, name, max=Wmax):

&#x20;       size = len(l)

&#x20;       abbr = max and size > max

&#x20;       if 'enumerate' in args:

&#x20;           l = \[f'{name}{subscript(i + 1)}'

&#x20;                for i, obj in enumerate(l)]

&#x20;       if abbr:

&#x20;           l = l\[0:max - 1] + \[l\[size - 1]]

&#x20;           l\[max - 2] = '{}⎨…{}⎬'.format(

&#x20;               name\[0], subscript(size - (max - 1)))

&#x20;       return l



&#x20;   app = ctx.obj.app

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       workers = args\['nodes']

&#x20;       threads = args.get('threads') or \[]

&#x20;   except KeyError:

&#x20;       replies = app.control.inspect().stats() or {}

&#x20;       workers, threads = \[], \[]

&#x20;       for worker, reply in replies.items():

&#x20;           workers.append(worker)

&#x20;           threads.append(reply\['pool']\['max-concurrency'])



&#x20;   wlen = len(workers)

&#x20;   backend = args.get('backend', app.conf.result\_backend)

&#x20;   threads\_for = {}

&#x20;   workers = maybe\_abbr(workers, 'Worker')

&#x20;   if Wmax and wlen > Wmax:

&#x20;       threads = threads\[0:3] + \[threads\[-1]]

&#x20;   for i, threads in enumerate(threads):

&#x20;       threads\_for\[workers\[i]] = maybe\_abbr(

&#x20;           list(range(int(threads))), 'P', Tmax,

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   broker = Broker(args.get(

&#x20;       'broker', app.connection\_for\_read().as\_uri()))

&#x20;   backend = Backend(backend) if backend else None

&#x20;   deps = DependencyGraph(formatter=Formatter())

&#x20;   deps.add\_arc(broker)

&#x20;   if backend:

&#x20;       deps.add\_arc(backend)

&#x20;   curworker = \[0]

&#x20;   for i, worker in enumerate(workers):

&#x20;       worker = Worker(worker, pos=i)

&#x20;       deps.add\_arc(worker)

&#x20;       deps.add\_edge(worker, broker)

&#x20;       if backend:

&#x20;           deps.add\_edge(worker, backend)

&#x20;       threads = threads\_for.get(worker.\_label)

&#x20;       if threads:

&#x20;           for thread in threads:

&#x20;               thread = Thread(thread)

&#x20;               deps.add\_arc(thread)

&#x20;               deps.add\_edge(thread, worker)



&#x20;       curworker\[0] += 1



&#x20;   deps.to\_dot(sys.stdout)



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\bin\\list.py

```python

"""The ``celery list bindings`` command, used to inspect queue bindings."""

import click



from celery.bin.base import CeleryCommand, handle\_preload\_options





@click.group(name="list")

@click.pass\_context

@handle\_preload\_options

def list\_(ctx):

&#x20;   """Get info from broker.



&#x20;   Note:



&#x20;       For RabbitMQ the management plugin is required.

&#x20;   """





@list\_.command(cls=CeleryCommand)

@click.pass\_context

def bindings(ctx):

&#x20;   """Inspect queue bindings."""

&#x20;   # TODO: Consider using a table formatter for this command.

&#x20;   app = ctx.obj.app

&#x20;   with app.connection() as conn:

&#x20;       app.amqp.TaskConsumer(conn).declare()



&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           bindings = conn.manager.get\_bindings()

&#x20;       except NotImplementedError:

&#x20;           raise click.UsageError('Your transport cannot list bindings.')



&#x20;       def fmt(q, e, r):

&#x20;           ctx.obj.echo(f'{q:<28} {e:<28} {r}')

&#x20;       fmt('Queue', 'Exchange', 'Routing Key')

&#x20;       fmt('-' \* 16, '-' \* 16, '-' \* 16)

&#x20;       for b in bindings:

&#x20;           fmt(b\['destination'], b\['source'], b\['routing\_key'])



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\bin\\logtool.py

```python

"""The ``celery logtool`` command."""

import re

from collections import Counter

from fileinput import FileInput



import click



from celery.bin.base import CeleryCommand, handle\_preload\_options



\_\_all\_\_ = ('logtool',)



RE\_LOG\_START = re.compile(r'^\\\[\\d\\d\\d\\d\\-\\d\\d-\\d\\d ')

RE\_TASK\_RECEIVED = re.compile(r'.+?\\] Received')

RE\_TASK\_READY = re.compile(r'.+?\\] Task')

RE\_TASK\_INFO = re.compile(r'.+?(\[\\w\\.]+)\\\[(.+?)\\].+')

RE\_TASK\_RESULT = re.compile(r'.+?\[\\w\\.]+\\\[.+?\\] (.+)')



REPORT\_FORMAT = """

Report

======

Task total: {task\[total]}

Task errors: {task\[errors]}

Task success: {task\[succeeded]}

Task completed: {task\[completed]}

Tasks

=====

{task\[types].format}

"""





class \_task\_counts(list):



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def format(self):

&#x20;       return '\\n'.join('{}: {}'.format(\*i) for i in self)





def task\_info(line):

&#x20;   m = RE\_TASK\_INFO.match(line)

&#x20;   return m.groups()





class Audit:



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, on\_task\_error=None, on\_trace=None, on\_debug=None):

&#x20;       self.ids = set()

&#x20;       self.names = {}

&#x20;       self.results = {}

&#x20;       self.ready = set()

&#x20;       self.task\_types = Counter()

&#x20;       self.task\_errors = 0

&#x20;       self.on\_task\_error = on\_task\_error

&#x20;       self.on\_trace = on\_trace

&#x20;       self.on\_debug = on\_debug

&#x20;       self.prev\_line = None



&#x20;   def run(self, files):

&#x20;       for line in FileInput(files):

&#x20;           self.feed(line)

&#x20;       return self



&#x20;   def task\_received(self, line, task\_name, task\_id):

&#x20;       self.names\[task\_id] = task\_name

&#x20;       self.ids.add(task\_id)

&#x20;       self.task\_types\[task\_name] += 1



&#x20;   def task\_ready(self, line, task\_name, task\_id, result):

&#x20;       self.ready.add(task\_id)

&#x20;       self.results\[task\_id] = result

&#x20;       if 'succeeded' not in result:

&#x20;           self.task\_error(line, task\_name, task\_id, result)



&#x20;   def task\_error(self, line, task\_name, task\_id, result):

&#x20;       self.task\_errors += 1

&#x20;       if self.on\_task\_error:

&#x20;           self.on\_task\_error(line, task\_name, task\_id, result)



&#x20;   def feed(self, line):

&#x20;       if RE\_LOG\_START.match(line):

&#x20;           if RE\_TASK\_RECEIVED.match(line):

&#x20;               task\_name, task\_id = task\_info(line)

&#x20;               self.task\_received(line, task\_name, task\_id)

&#x20;           elif RE\_TASK\_READY.match(line):

&#x20;               task\_name, task\_id = task\_info(line)

&#x20;               result = RE\_TASK\_RESULT.match(line)

&#x20;               if result:

&#x20;                   result, = result.groups()

&#x20;               self.task\_ready(line, task\_name, task\_id, result)

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               if self.on\_debug:

&#x20;                   self.on\_debug(line)

&#x20;           self.prev\_line = line

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           if self.on\_trace:

&#x20;               self.on\_trace('\\n'.join(filter(None, \[self.prev\_line, line])))

&#x20;           self.prev\_line = None



&#x20;   def incomplete\_tasks(self):

&#x20;       return self.ids ^ self.ready



&#x20;   def report(self):

&#x20;       return {

&#x20;           'task': {

&#x20;               'types': \_task\_counts(self.task\_types.most\_common()),

&#x20;               'total': len(self.ids),

&#x20;               'errors': self.task\_errors,

&#x20;               'completed': len(self.ready),

&#x20;               'succeeded': len(self.ready) - self.task\_errors,

&#x20;           }

&#x20;       }





@click.group()

@click.pass\_context

@handle\_preload\_options

def logtool(ctx):

&#x20;   """The ``celery logtool`` command."""





@logtool.command(cls=CeleryCommand)

@click.argument('files', nargs=-1)

@click.pass\_context

def stats(ctx, files):

&#x20;   ctx.obj.echo(REPORT\_FORMAT.format(

&#x20;       \*\*Audit().run(files).report()

&#x20;   ))





@logtool.command(cls=CeleryCommand)

@click.argument('files', nargs=-1)

@click.pass\_context

def traces(ctx, files):

&#x20;   Audit(on\_trace=ctx.obj.echo).run(files)





@logtool.command(cls=CeleryCommand)

@click.argument('files', nargs=-1)

@click.pass\_context

def errors(ctx, files):

&#x20;   Audit(on\_task\_error=lambda line, \*\_: ctx.obj.echo(line)).run(files)





@logtool.command(cls=CeleryCommand)

@click.argument('files', nargs=-1)

@click.pass\_context

def incomplete(ctx, files):

&#x20;   audit = Audit()

&#x20;   audit.run(files)

&#x20;   for task\_id in audit.incomplete\_tasks():

&#x20;       ctx.obj.echo(f'Did not complete: {task\_id}')





@logtool.command(cls=CeleryCommand)

@click.argument('files', nargs=-1)

@click.pass\_context

def debug(ctx, files):

&#x20;   Audit(on\_debug=ctx.obj.echo).run(files)



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\bin\\migrate.py

```python

"""The ``celery migrate`` command, used to filter and move messages."""

import click

from kombu import Connection



from celery.bin.base import CeleryCommand, CeleryOption, handle\_preload\_options

from celery.contrib.migrate import migrate\_tasks





@click.command(cls=CeleryCommand)

@click.argument('source')

@click.argument('destination')

@click.option('-n',

&#x20;             '--limit',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             type=int,

&#x20;             help\_group='Migration Options',

&#x20;             help='Number of tasks to consume.')

@click.option('-t',

&#x20;             '--timeout',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             type=float,

&#x20;             help\_group='Migration Options',

&#x20;             help='Timeout in seconds waiting for tasks.')

@click.option('-a',

&#x20;             '--ack-messages',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             is\_flag=True,

&#x20;             help\_group='Migration Options',

&#x20;             help='Ack messages from source broker.')

@click.option('-T',

&#x20;             '--tasks',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group='Migration Options',

&#x20;             help='List of task names to filter on.')

@click.option('-Q',

&#x20;             '--queues',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group='Migration Options',

&#x20;             help='List of queues to migrate.')

@click.option('-F',

&#x20;             '--forever',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             is\_flag=True,

&#x20;             help\_group='Migration Options',

&#x20;             help='Continually migrate tasks until killed.')

@click.pass\_context

@handle\_preload\_options

def migrate(ctx, source, destination, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;   """Migrate tasks from one broker to another.



&#x20;   Warning:



&#x20;       This command is experimental, make sure you have a backup of

&#x20;       the tasks before you continue.

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   # TODO: Use a progress bar

&#x20;   def on\_migrate\_task(state, body, message):

&#x20;       ctx.obj.echo(f"Migrating task {state.count}/{state.strtotal}: {body}")



&#x20;   migrate\_tasks(Connection(source),

&#x20;                 Connection(destination),

&#x20;                 callback=on\_migrate\_task,

&#x20;                 \*\*kwargs)



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\bin\\multi.py

```python

"""Start multiple worker instances from the command-line.



.. program:: celery multi



Examples

========



.. code-block:: console



&#x20;   $ # Single worker with explicit name and events enabled.

&#x20;   $ celery multi start Leslie -E



&#x20;   $ # Pidfiles and logfiles are stored in the current directory

&#x20;   $ # by default.  Use --pidfile and --logfile argument to change

&#x20;   $ # this.  The abbreviation %n will be expanded to the current

&#x20;   $ # node name.

&#x20;   $ celery multi start Leslie -E --pidfile=/var/run/celery/%n.pid

&#x20;                                  --logfile=/var/log/celery/%n%I.log





&#x20;   $ # You need to add the same arguments when you restart,

&#x20;   $ # as these aren't persisted anywhere.

&#x20;   $ celery multi restart Leslie -E --pidfile=/var/run/celery/%n.pid

&#x20;                                    --logfile=/var/log/celery/%n%I.log



&#x20;   $ # To stop the node, you need to specify the same pidfile.

&#x20;   $ celery multi stop Leslie --pidfile=/var/run/celery/%n.pid



&#x20;   $ # 3 workers, with 3 processes each

&#x20;   $ celery multi start 3 -c 3

&#x20;   celery worker -n celery1@myhost -c 3

&#x20;   celery worker -n celery2@myhost -c 3

&#x20;   celery worker -n celery3@myhost -c 3



&#x20;   $ # override name prefix when using range

&#x20;   $ celery multi start 3 --range-prefix=worker -c 3

&#x20;   celery worker -n worker1@myhost -c 3

&#x20;   celery worker -n worker2@myhost -c 3

&#x20;   celery worker -n worker3@myhost -c 3



&#x20;   $ # start 3 named workers

&#x20;   $ celery multi start image video data -c 3

&#x20;   celery worker -n image@myhost -c 3

&#x20;   celery worker -n video@myhost -c 3

&#x20;   celery worker -n data@myhost -c 3



&#x20;   $ # specify custom hostname

&#x20;   $ celery multi start 2 --hostname=worker.example.com -c 3

&#x20;   celery worker -n celery1@worker.example.com -c 3

&#x20;   celery worker -n celery2@worker.example.com -c 3



&#x20;   $ # specify fully qualified nodenames

&#x20;   $ celery multi start foo@worker.example.com bar@worker.example.com -c 3



&#x20;   $ # fully qualified nodenames but using the current hostname

&#x20;   $ celery multi start foo@%h bar@%h



&#x20;   $ # Advanced example starting 10 workers in the background:

&#x20;   $ #   \* Three of the workers processes the images and video queue

&#x20;   $ #   \* Two of the workers processes the data queue with loglevel DEBUG

&#x20;   $ #   \* the rest processes the default' queue.

&#x20;   $ celery multi start 10 -l INFO -Q:1-3 images,video -Q:4,5 data

&#x20;       -Q default -L:4,5 DEBUG



&#x20;   $ # You can show the commands necessary to start the workers with

&#x20;   $ # the 'show' command:

&#x20;   $ celery multi show 10 -l INFO -Q:1-3 images,video -Q:4,5 data

&#x20;       -Q default -L:4,5 DEBUG



&#x20;   $ # Additional options are added to each celery worker's command,

&#x20;   $ # but you can also modify the options for ranges of, or specific workers



&#x20;   $ # 3 workers: Two with 3 processes, and one with 10 processes.

&#x20;   $ celery multi start 3 -c 3 -c:1 10

&#x20;   celery worker -n celery1@myhost -c 10

&#x20;   celery worker -n celery2@myhost -c 3

&#x20;   celery worker -n celery3@myhost -c 3



&#x20;   $ # can also specify options for named workers

&#x20;   $ celery multi start image video data -c 3 -c:image 10

&#x20;   celery worker -n image@myhost -c 10

&#x20;   celery worker -n video@myhost -c 3

&#x20;   celery worker -n data@myhost -c 3



&#x20;   $ # ranges and lists of workers in options is also allowed:

&#x20;   $ # (-c:1-3 can also be written as -c:1,2,3)

&#x20;   $ celery multi start 5 -c 3  -c:1-3 10

&#x20;   celery worker -n celery1@myhost -c 10

&#x20;   celery worker -n celery2@myhost -c 10

&#x20;   celery worker -n celery3@myhost -c 10

&#x20;   celery worker -n celery4@myhost -c 3

&#x20;   celery worker -n celery5@myhost -c 3



&#x20;   $ # lists also works with named workers

&#x20;   $ celery multi start foo bar baz xuzzy -c 3 -c:foo,bar,baz 10

&#x20;   celery worker -n foo@myhost -c 10

&#x20;   celery worker -n bar@myhost -c 10

&#x20;   celery worker -n baz@myhost -c 10

&#x20;   celery worker -n xuzzy@myhost -c 3

"""

import os

import signal

import sys

from functools import wraps



import click

from kombu.utils.objects import cached\_property



from celery import VERSION\_BANNER

from celery.apps.multi import Cluster, MultiParser, NamespacedOptionParser

from celery.bin.base import CeleryCommand, handle\_preload\_options

from celery.platforms import EX\_FAILURE, EX\_OK, signals

from celery.utils import term

from celery.utils.text import pluralize



\_\_all\_\_ = ('MultiTool',)



USAGE = """\\

usage: {prog\_name} start <node1 node2 nodeN|range> \[worker options]

&#x20;      {prog\_name} stop <n1 n2 nN|range> \[-SIG (default: -TERM)]

&#x20;      {prog\_name} restart <n1 n2 nN|range> \[-SIG] \[worker options]

&#x20;      {prog\_name} kill <n1 n2 nN|range>



&#x20;      {prog\_name} show <n1 n2 nN|range> \[worker options]

&#x20;      {prog\_name} get hostname <n1 n2 nN|range> \[-qv] \[worker options]

&#x20;      {prog\_name} names <n1 n2 nN|range>

&#x20;      {prog\_name} expand template <n1 n2 nN|range>

&#x20;      {prog\_name} help



additional options (must appear after command name):



&#x20;   \* --nosplash:   Don't display program info.

&#x20;   \* --quiet:      Don't show as much output.

&#x20;   \* --verbose:    Show more output.

&#x20;   \* --no-color:   Don't display colors.

"""





def main():

&#x20;   sys.exit(MultiTool().execute\_from\_commandline(sys.argv))





def splash(fun):



&#x20;   @wraps(fun)

&#x20;   def \_inner(self, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       self.splash()

&#x20;       return fun(self, \*args, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;   return \_inner





def using\_cluster(fun):



&#x20;   @wraps(fun)

&#x20;   def \_inner(self, \*argv, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       return fun(self, self.cluster\_from\_argv(argv), \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;   return \_inner





def using\_cluster\_and\_sig(fun):



&#x20;   @wraps(fun)

&#x20;   def \_inner(self, \*argv, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       p, cluster = self.\_cluster\_from\_argv(argv)

&#x20;       sig = self.\_find\_sig\_argument(p)

&#x20;       return fun(self, cluster, sig, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;   return \_inner





class TermLogger:



&#x20;   splash\_text = 'celery multi v{version}'

&#x20;   splash\_context = {'version': VERSION\_BANNER}



&#x20;   #: Final exit code.

&#x20;   retcode = 0



&#x20;   def setup\_terminal(self, stdout, stderr,

&#x20;                      nosplash=False, quiet=False, verbose=False,

&#x20;                      no\_color=False, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       self.stdout = stdout or sys.stdout

&#x20;       self.stderr = stderr or sys.stderr

&#x20;       self.nosplash = nosplash

&#x20;       self.quiet = quiet

&#x20;       self.verbose = verbose

&#x20;       self.no\_color = no\_color



&#x20;   def ok(self, m, newline=True, file=None):

&#x20;       self.say(m, newline=newline, file=file)

&#x20;       return EX\_OK



&#x20;   def say(self, m, newline=True, file=None):

&#x20;       print(m, file=file or self.stdout, end='\\n' if newline else '')



&#x20;   def carp(self, m, newline=True, file=None):

&#x20;       return self.say(m, newline, file or self.stderr)



&#x20;   def error(self, msg=None):

&#x20;       if msg:

&#x20;           self.carp(msg)

&#x20;       self.usage()

&#x20;       return EX\_FAILURE



&#x20;   def info(self, msg, newline=True):

&#x20;       if self.verbose:

&#x20;           self.note(msg, newline=newline)



&#x20;   def note(self, msg, newline=True):

&#x20;       if not self.quiet:

&#x20;           self.say(str(msg), newline=newline)



&#x20;   @splash

&#x20;   def usage(self):

&#x20;       self.say(USAGE.format(prog\_name=self.prog\_name))



&#x20;   def splash(self):

&#x20;       if not self.nosplash:

&#x20;           self.note(self.colored.cyan(

&#x20;               self.splash\_text.format(\*\*self.splash\_context)))



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def colored(self):

&#x20;       return term.colored(enabled=not self.no\_color)





class MultiTool(TermLogger):

&#x20;   """The ``celery multi`` program."""



&#x20;   MultiParser = MultiParser

&#x20;   OptionParser = NamespacedOptionParser



&#x20;   reserved\_options = \[

&#x20;       ('--nosplash', 'nosplash'),

&#x20;       ('--quiet', 'quiet'),

&#x20;       ('-q', 'quiet'),

&#x20;       ('--verbose', 'verbose'),

&#x20;       ('--no-color', 'no\_color'),

&#x20;   ]



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, env=None, cmd=None,

&#x20;                fh=None, stdout=None, stderr=None, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       # fh is an old alias to stdout.

&#x20;       self.env = env

&#x20;       self.cmd = cmd

&#x20;       self.setup\_terminal(stdout or fh, stderr, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;       self.fh = self.stdout

&#x20;       self.prog\_name = 'celery multi'

&#x20;       self.commands = {

&#x20;           'start': self.start,

&#x20;           'show': self.show,

&#x20;           'stop': self.stop,

&#x20;           'stopwait': self.stopwait,

&#x20;           'stop\_verify': self.stopwait,  # compat alias

&#x20;           'restart': self.restart,

&#x20;           'kill': self.kill,

&#x20;           'names': self.names,

&#x20;           'expand': self.expand,

&#x20;           'get': self.get,

&#x20;           'help': self.help,

&#x20;       }



&#x20;   def execute\_from\_commandline(self, argv, cmd=None):

&#x20;       # Reserve the --nosplash|--quiet|-q/--verbose options.

&#x20;       argv = self.\_handle\_reserved\_options(argv)

&#x20;       self.cmd = cmd if cmd is not None else self.cmd

&#x20;       self.prog\_name = os.path.basename(argv.pop(0))



&#x20;       if not self.validate\_arguments(argv):

&#x20;           return self.error()



&#x20;       return self.call\_command(argv\[0], argv\[1:])



&#x20;   def validate\_arguments(self, argv):

&#x20;       return argv and argv\[0]\[0] != '-'



&#x20;   def call\_command(self, command, argv):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           return self.commands\[command](\*argv) or EX\_OK

&#x20;       except KeyError:

&#x20;           return self.error(f'Invalid command: {command}')



&#x20;   def \_handle\_reserved\_options(self, argv):

&#x20;       argv = list(argv)  # don't modify callers argv.

&#x20;       for arg, attr in self.reserved\_options:

&#x20;           if arg in argv:

&#x20;               setattr(self, attr, bool(argv.pop(argv.index(arg))))

&#x20;       return argv



&#x20;   @splash

&#x20;   @using\_cluster

&#x20;   def start(self, cluster):

&#x20;       self.note('> Starting nodes...')

&#x20;       return int(any(cluster.start()))



&#x20;   @splash

&#x20;   @using\_cluster\_and\_sig

&#x20;   def stop(self, cluster, sig, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       return cluster.stop(sig=sig, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   @splash

&#x20;   @using\_cluster\_and\_sig

&#x20;   def stopwait(self, cluster, sig, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       return cluster.stopwait(sig=sig, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;   stop\_verify = stopwait  # compat



&#x20;   @splash

&#x20;   @using\_cluster\_and\_sig

&#x20;   def restart(self, cluster, sig, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       return int(any(cluster.restart(sig=sig, \*\*kwargs)))



&#x20;   @using\_cluster

&#x20;   def names(self, cluster):

&#x20;       self.say('\\n'.join(n.name for n in cluster))



&#x20;   def get(self, wanted, \*argv):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           node = self.cluster\_from\_argv(argv).find(wanted)

&#x20;       except KeyError:

&#x20;           return EX\_FAILURE

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           return self.ok(' '.join(node.argv))



&#x20;   @using\_cluster

&#x20;   def show(self, cluster):

&#x20;       return self.ok('\\n'.join(

&#x20;           ' '.join(node.argv\_with\_executable)

&#x20;           for node in cluster

&#x20;       ))



&#x20;   @splash

&#x20;   @using\_cluster

&#x20;   def kill(self, cluster):

&#x20;       return cluster.kill()



&#x20;   def expand(self, template, \*argv):

&#x20;       return self.ok('\\n'.join(

&#x20;           node.expander(template)

&#x20;           for node in self.cluster\_from\_argv(argv)

&#x20;       ))



&#x20;   def help(self, \*argv):

&#x20;       self.say(\_\_doc\_\_)



&#x20;   def \_find\_sig\_argument(self, p, default=signal.SIGTERM):

&#x20;       args = p.args\[len(p.values):]

&#x20;       for arg in reversed(args):

&#x20;           if len(arg) == 2 and arg\[0] == '-':

&#x20;               try:

&#x20;                   return int(arg\[1])

&#x20;               except ValueError:

&#x20;                   pass

&#x20;           if arg\[0] == '-':

&#x20;               try:

&#x20;                   return signals.signum(arg\[1:])

&#x20;               except (AttributeError, TypeError):

&#x20;                   pass

&#x20;       return default



&#x20;   def \_nodes\_from\_argv(self, argv, cmd=None):

&#x20;       cmd = cmd if cmd is not None else self.cmd

&#x20;       p = self.OptionParser(argv)

&#x20;       p.parse()

&#x20;       return p, self.MultiParser(cmd=cmd).parse(p)



&#x20;   def cluster\_from\_argv(self, argv, cmd=None):

&#x20;       \_, cluster = self.\_cluster\_from\_argv(argv, cmd=cmd)

&#x20;       return cluster



&#x20;   def \_cluster\_from\_argv(self, argv, cmd=None):

&#x20;       p, nodes = self.\_nodes\_from\_argv(argv, cmd=cmd)

&#x20;       return p, self.Cluster(list(nodes), cmd=cmd)



&#x20;   def Cluster(self, nodes, cmd=None):

&#x20;       return Cluster(

&#x20;           nodes,

&#x20;           cmd=cmd,

&#x20;           env=self.env,

&#x20;           on\_stopping\_preamble=self.on\_stopping\_preamble,

&#x20;           on\_send\_signal=self.on\_send\_signal,

&#x20;           on\_still\_waiting\_for=self.on\_still\_waiting\_for,

&#x20;           on\_still\_waiting\_progress=self.on\_still\_waiting\_progress,

&#x20;           on\_still\_waiting\_end=self.on\_still\_waiting\_end,

&#x20;           on\_node\_start=self.on\_node\_start,

&#x20;           on\_node\_restart=self.on\_node\_restart,

&#x20;           on\_node\_shutdown\_ok=self.on\_node\_shutdown\_ok,

&#x20;           on\_node\_status=self.on\_node\_status,

&#x20;           on\_node\_signal\_dead=self.on\_node\_signal\_dead,

&#x20;           on\_node\_signal=self.on\_node\_signal,

&#x20;           on\_node\_down=self.on\_node\_down,

&#x20;           on\_child\_spawn=self.on\_child\_spawn,

&#x20;           on\_child\_signalled=self.on\_child\_signalled,

&#x20;           on\_child\_failure=self.on\_child\_failure,

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def on\_stopping\_preamble(self, nodes):

&#x20;       self.note(self.colored.blue('> Stopping nodes...'))



&#x20;   def on\_send\_signal(self, node, sig):

&#x20;       self.note('\\t> {0.name}: {1} -> {0.pid}'.format(node, sig))



&#x20;   def on\_still\_waiting\_for(self, nodes):

&#x20;       num\_left = len(nodes)

&#x20;       if num\_left:

&#x20;           self.note(self.colored.blue(

&#x20;               '> Waiting for {} {} -> {}...'.format(

&#x20;                   num\_left, pluralize(num\_left, 'node'),

&#x20;                   ', '.join(str(node.pid) for node in nodes)),

&#x20;           ), newline=False)



&#x20;   def on\_still\_waiting\_progress(self, nodes):

&#x20;       self.note('.', newline=False)



&#x20;   def on\_still\_waiting\_end(self):

&#x20;       self.note('')



&#x20;   def on\_node\_signal\_dead(self, node):

&#x20;       self.note(

&#x20;           'Could not signal {0.name} ({0.pid}): No such process'.format(

&#x20;               node))



&#x20;   def on\_node\_start(self, node):

&#x20;       self.note(f'\\t> {node.name}: ', newline=False)



&#x20;   def on\_node\_restart(self, node):

&#x20;       self.note(self.colored.blue(

&#x20;           f'> Restarting node {node.name}: '), newline=False)



&#x20;   def on\_node\_down(self, node):

&#x20;       self.note(f'> {node.name}: {self.DOWN}')



&#x20;   def on\_node\_shutdown\_ok(self, node):

&#x20;       self.note(f'\\n\\t> {node.name}: {self.OK}')



&#x20;   def on\_node\_status(self, node, retval):

&#x20;       self.note(retval and self.FAILED or self.OK)



&#x20;   def on\_node\_signal(self, node, sig):

&#x20;       self.note('Sending {sig} to node {0.name} ({0.pid})'.format(

&#x20;           node, sig=sig))



&#x20;   def on\_child\_spawn(self, node, argstr, env):

&#x20;       self.info(f'  {argstr}')



&#x20;   def on\_child\_signalled(self, node, signum):

&#x20;       self.note(f'\* Child was terminated by signal {signum}')



&#x20;   def on\_child\_failure(self, node, retcode):

&#x20;       self.note(f'\* Child terminated with exit code {retcode}')



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def OK(self):

&#x20;       return str(self.colored.green('OK'))



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def FAILED(self):

&#x20;       return str(self.colored.red('FAILED'))



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def DOWN(self):

&#x20;       return str(self.colored.magenta('DOWN'))





@click.command(

&#x20;   cls=CeleryCommand,

&#x20;   context\_settings={

&#x20;       'allow\_extra\_args': True,

&#x20;       'ignore\_unknown\_options': True

&#x20;   }

)

@click.pass\_context

@handle\_preload\_options

def multi(ctx, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;   """Start multiple worker instances."""

&#x20;   cmd = MultiTool(quiet=ctx.obj.quiet, no\_color=ctx.obj.no\_color)

&#x20;   # In 4.x, celery multi ignores the global --app option.

&#x20;   # Since in 5.0 the --app option is global only we

&#x20;   # rearrange the arguments so that the MultiTool will parse them correctly.

&#x20;   args = sys.argv\[1:]

&#x20;   args = args\[args.index('multi'):] + args\[:args.index('multi')]

&#x20;   return cmd.execute\_from\_commandline(args)



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\bin\\purge.py

```python

"""The ``celery purge`` program, used to delete messages from queues."""

import click



from celery.bin.base import COMMA\_SEPARATED\_LIST, CeleryCommand, CeleryOption, handle\_preload\_options

from celery.utils import text





@click.command(cls=CeleryCommand, context\_settings={

&#x20;   'allow\_extra\_args': True

})

@click.option('-f',

&#x20;             '--force',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             is\_flag=True,

&#x20;             help\_group='Purging Options',

&#x20;             help="Don't prompt for verification.")

@click.option('-Q',

&#x20;             '--queues',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             type=COMMA\_SEPARATED\_LIST,

&#x20;             help\_group='Purging Options',

&#x20;             help="Comma separated list of queue names to purge.")

@click.option('-X',

&#x20;             '--exclude-queues',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             type=COMMA\_SEPARATED\_LIST,

&#x20;             help\_group='Purging Options',

&#x20;             help="Comma separated list of queues names not to purge.")

@click.pass\_context

@handle\_preload\_options

def purge(ctx, force, queues, exclude\_queues, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;   """Erase all messages from all known task queues.



&#x20;   Warning:



&#x20;       There's no undo operation for this command.

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   app = ctx.obj.app

&#x20;   queues = set(queues or app.amqp.queues.keys())

&#x20;   exclude\_queues = set(exclude\_queues or \[])

&#x20;   names = queues - exclude\_queues

&#x20;   qnum = len(names)



&#x20;   if names:

&#x20;       queues\_headline = text.pluralize(qnum, 'queue')

&#x20;       if not force:

&#x20;           queue\_names = ', '.join(sorted(names))

&#x20;           click.confirm(f"{ctx.obj.style('WARNING', fg='red')}:"

&#x20;                         "This will remove all tasks from "

&#x20;                         f"{queues\_headline}: {queue\_names}.\\n"

&#x20;                         "         There is no undo for this operation!\\n\\n"

&#x20;                         "(to skip this prompt use the -f option)\\n"

&#x20;                         "Are you sure you want to delete all tasks?",

&#x20;                         abort=True)



&#x20;       def \_purge(conn, queue):

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               return conn.default\_channel.queue\_purge(queue) or 0

&#x20;           except conn.channel\_errors:

&#x20;               return 0



&#x20;       with app.connection\_for\_write() as conn:

&#x20;           messages = sum(\_purge(conn, queue) for queue in names)



&#x20;       if messages:

&#x20;           messages\_headline = text.pluralize(messages, 'message')

&#x20;           ctx.obj.echo(f"Purged {messages} {messages\_headline} from "

&#x20;                        f"{qnum} known task {queues\_headline}.")

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           ctx.obj.echo(f"No messages purged from {qnum} {queues\_headline}.")



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\bin\\result.py

```python

"""The ``celery result`` program, used to inspect task results."""

import click



from celery.bin.base import CeleryCommand, CeleryOption, handle\_preload\_options





@click.command(cls=CeleryCommand)

@click.argument('task\_id')

@click.option('-t',

&#x20;             '--task',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group='Result Options',

&#x20;             help="Name of task (if custom backend).")

@click.option('--traceback',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             is\_flag=True,

&#x20;             help\_group='Result Options',

&#x20;             help="Show traceback instead.")

@click.pass\_context

@handle\_preload\_options

def result(ctx, task\_id, task, traceback):

&#x20;   """Print the return value for a given task id."""

&#x20;   app = ctx.obj.app



&#x20;   result\_cls = app.tasks\[task].AsyncResult if task else app.AsyncResult

&#x20;   task\_result = result\_cls(task\_id)

&#x20;   value = task\_result.traceback if traceback else task\_result.get()



&#x20;   # TODO: Prettify result

&#x20;   ctx.obj.echo(value)



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\bin\\shell.py

```python

"""The ``celery shell`` program, used to start a REPL."""



import os

import sys

from importlib import import\_module



import click



from celery.bin.base import CeleryCommand, CeleryOption, handle\_preload\_options





def \_invoke\_fallback\_shell(locals):

&#x20;   import code

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       import readline

&#x20;   except ImportError:

&#x20;       pass

&#x20;   else:

&#x20;       import rlcompleter

&#x20;       readline.set\_completer(

&#x20;           rlcompleter.Completer(locals).complete)

&#x20;       readline.parse\_and\_bind('tab:complete')

&#x20;   code.interact(local=locals)





def \_invoke\_bpython\_shell(locals):

&#x20;   import bpython

&#x20;   bpython.embed(locals)





def \_invoke\_ipython\_shell(locals):

&#x20;   for ip in (\_ipython, \_ipython\_pre\_10,

&#x20;              \_ipython\_terminal, \_ipython\_010,

&#x20;              \_no\_ipython):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           return ip(locals)

&#x20;       except ImportError:

&#x20;           pass





def \_ipython(locals):

&#x20;   from IPython import start\_ipython

&#x20;   start\_ipython(argv=\[], user\_ns=locals)





def \_ipython\_pre\_10(locals):  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;   from IPython.frontend.terminal.ipapp import TerminalIPythonApp

&#x20;   app = TerminalIPythonApp.instance()

&#x20;   app.initialize(argv=\[])

&#x20;   app.shell.user\_ns.update(locals)

&#x20;   app.start()





def \_ipython\_terminal(locals):  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;   from IPython.terminal import embed

&#x20;   embed.TerminalInteractiveShell(user\_ns=locals).mainloop()





def \_ipython\_010(locals):  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;   from IPython.Shell import IPShell

&#x20;   IPShell(argv=\[], user\_ns=locals).mainloop()





def \_no\_ipython(self):  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;   raise ImportError('no suitable ipython found')





def \_invoke\_default\_shell(locals):

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       import IPython  # noqa

&#x20;   except ImportError:

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           import bpython  # noqa

&#x20;       except ImportError:

&#x20;           \_invoke\_fallback\_shell(locals)

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           \_invoke\_bpython\_shell(locals)

&#x20;   else:

&#x20;       \_invoke\_ipython\_shell(locals)





@click.command(cls=CeleryCommand, context\_settings={

&#x20;   'allow\_extra\_args': True

})

@click.option('-I',

&#x20;             '--ipython',

&#x20;             is\_flag=True,

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group="Shell Options",

&#x20;             help="Force IPython.")

@click.option('-B',

&#x20;             '--bpython',

&#x20;             is\_flag=True,

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group="Shell Options",

&#x20;             help="Force bpython.")

@click.option('--python',

&#x20;             is\_flag=True,

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group="Shell Options",

&#x20;             help="Force default Python shell.")

@click.option('-T',

&#x20;             '--without-tasks',

&#x20;             is\_flag=True,

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group="Shell Options",

&#x20;             help="Don't add tasks to locals.")

@click.option('--eventlet',

&#x20;             is\_flag=True,

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group="Shell Options",

&#x20;             help="Use eventlet.")

@click.option('--gevent',

&#x20;             is\_flag=True,

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group="Shell Options",

&#x20;             help="Use gevent.")

@click.pass\_context

@handle\_preload\_options

def shell(ctx, ipython=False, bpython=False,

&#x20;         python=False, without\_tasks=False, eventlet=False,

&#x20;         gevent=False, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;   """Start shell session with convenient access to celery symbols.



&#x20;   The following symbols will be added to the main globals:

&#x20;   - ``celery``:  the current application.

&#x20;   - ``chord``, ``group``, ``chain``, ``chunks``,

&#x20;     ``xmap``, ``xstarmap`` ``subtask``, ``Task``

&#x20;   - all registered tasks.

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   sys.path.insert(0, os.getcwd())

&#x20;   if eventlet:

&#x20;       import\_module('celery.concurrency.eventlet')

&#x20;   if gevent:

&#x20;       import\_module('celery.concurrency.gevent')

&#x20;   import celery

&#x20;   app = ctx.obj.app

&#x20;   app.loader.import\_default\_modules()



&#x20;   # pylint: disable=attribute-defined-outside-init

&#x20;   locals = {

&#x20;       'app': app,

&#x20;       'celery': app,

&#x20;       'Task': celery.Task,

&#x20;       'chord': celery.chord,

&#x20;       'group': celery.group,

&#x20;       'chain': celery.chain,

&#x20;       'chunks': celery.chunks,

&#x20;       'xmap': celery.xmap,

&#x20;       'xstarmap': celery.xstarmap,

&#x20;       'subtask': celery.subtask,

&#x20;       'signature': celery.signature,

&#x20;   }



&#x20;   if not without\_tasks:

&#x20;       locals.update({

&#x20;           task.\_\_name\_\_: task for task in app.tasks.values()

&#x20;           if not task.name.startswith('celery.')

&#x20;       })



&#x20;   if python:

&#x20;       \_invoke\_fallback\_shell(locals)

&#x20;   elif bpython:

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           \_invoke\_bpython\_shell(locals)

&#x20;       except ImportError:

&#x20;           ctx.obj.echo(f'{ctx.obj.ERROR}: bpython is not installed')

&#x20;   elif ipython:

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           \_invoke\_ipython\_shell(locals)

&#x20;       except ImportError as e:

&#x20;           ctx.obj.echo(f'{ctx.obj.ERROR}: {e}')

&#x20;   \_invoke\_default\_shell(locals)



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\bin\\upgrade.py

```python

"""The ``celery upgrade`` command, used to upgrade from previous versions."""

import codecs

import sys



import click



from celery.app import defaults

from celery.bin.base import CeleryCommand, CeleryOption, handle\_preload\_options

from celery.utils.functional import pass1





@click.group()

@click.pass\_context

@handle\_preload\_options

def upgrade(ctx):

&#x20;   """Perform upgrade between versions."""





def \_slurp(filename):

&#x20;   # TODO: Handle case when file does not exist

&#x20;   with codecs.open(filename, 'r', 'utf-8') as read\_fh:

&#x20;       return \[line for line in read\_fh]





def \_compat\_key(key, namespace='CELERY'):

&#x20;   key = key.upper()

&#x20;   if not key.startswith(namespace):

&#x20;       key = '\_'.join(\[namespace, key])

&#x20;   return key





def \_backup(filename, suffix='.orig'):

&#x20;   lines = \[]

&#x20;   backup\_filename = ''.join(\[filename, suffix])

&#x20;   print(f'writing backup to {backup\_filename}...',

&#x20;         file=sys.stderr)

&#x20;   with codecs.open(filename, 'r', 'utf-8') as read\_fh:

&#x20;       with codecs.open(backup\_filename, 'w', 'utf-8') as backup\_fh:

&#x20;           for line in read\_fh:

&#x20;               backup\_fh.write(line)

&#x20;               lines.append(line)

&#x20;   return lines





def \_to\_new\_key(line, keyfilter=pass1, source=defaults.\_TO\_NEW\_KEY):

&#x20;   # sort by length to avoid, for example, broker\_transport overriding

&#x20;   # broker\_transport\_options.

&#x20;   for old\_key in reversed(sorted(source, key=lambda x: len(x))):

&#x20;       new\_line = line.replace(old\_key, keyfilter(source\[old\_key]))

&#x20;       if line != new\_line and 'CELERY\_CELERY' not in new\_line:

&#x20;           return 1, new\_line  # only one match per line.

&#x20;   return 0, line





@upgrade.command(cls=CeleryCommand)

@click.argument('filename')

@click.option('--django',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             is\_flag=True,

&#x20;             help\_group='Upgrading Options',

&#x20;             help='Upgrade Django project.')

@click.option('--compat',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             is\_flag=True,

&#x20;             help\_group='Upgrading Options',

&#x20;             help='Maintain backwards compatibility.')

@click.option('--no-backup',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             is\_flag=True,

&#x20;             help\_group='Upgrading Options',

&#x20;             help="Don't backup original files.")

def settings(filename, django, compat, no\_backup):

&#x20;   """Migrate settings from Celery 3.x to Celery 4.x."""

&#x20;   lines = \_slurp(filename)

&#x20;   keyfilter = \_compat\_key if django or compat else pass1

&#x20;   print(f'processing {filename}...', file=sys.stderr)

&#x20;   # gives list of tuples: ``(did\_change, line\_contents)``

&#x20;   new\_lines = \[

&#x20;       \_to\_new\_key(line, keyfilter) for line in lines

&#x20;   ]

&#x20;   if any(n\[0] for n in new\_lines):  # did have changes

&#x20;       if not no\_backup:

&#x20;           \_backup(filename)

&#x20;       with codecs.open(filename, 'w', 'utf-8') as write\_fh:

&#x20;           for \_, line in new\_lines:

&#x20;               write\_fh.write(line)

&#x20;       print('Changes to your setting have been made!',

&#x20;             file=sys.stdout)

&#x20;   else:

&#x20;       print('Does not seem to require any changes :-)',

&#x20;             file=sys.stdout)



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\bin\\worker.py

```python

"""Program used to start a Celery worker instance."""



import os

import sys



import click

from click import ParamType

from click.types import StringParamType



from celery import concurrency

from celery.bin.base import (COMMA\_SEPARATED\_LIST, LOG\_LEVEL, CeleryDaemonCommand, CeleryOption,

&#x20;                            handle\_preload\_options)

from celery.concurrency.base import BasePool

from celery.exceptions import SecurityError

from celery.platforms import EX\_FAILURE, EX\_OK, detached, maybe\_drop\_privileges

from celery.utils.log import get\_logger

from celery.utils.nodenames import default\_nodename, host\_format, node\_format



logger = get\_logger(\_\_name\_\_)





class CeleryBeat(ParamType):

&#x20;   """Celery Beat flag."""



&#x20;   name = "beat"



&#x20;   def convert(self, value, param, ctx):

&#x20;       if ctx.obj.app.IS\_WINDOWS and value:

&#x20;           self.fail('-B option does not work on Windows.  '

&#x20;                     'Please run celery beat as a separate service.')



&#x20;       return value





class WorkersPool(click.Choice):

&#x20;   """Workers pool option."""



&#x20;   name = "pool"



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self):

&#x20;       """Initialize the workers pool option with the relevant choices."""

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(concurrency.get\_available\_pool\_names())



&#x20;   def convert(self, value, param, ctx):

&#x20;       # Pools like eventlet/gevent needs to patch libs as early

&#x20;       # as possible.

&#x20;       if isinstance(value, type) and issubclass(value, BasePool):

&#x20;           return value



&#x20;       value = super().convert(value, param, ctx)

&#x20;       worker\_pool = ctx.obj.app.conf.worker\_pool

&#x20;       if value == 'prefork' and worker\_pool:

&#x20;           # If we got the default pool through the CLI

&#x20;           # we need to check if the worker pool was configured.

&#x20;           # If the worker pool was configured, we shouldn't use the default.

&#x20;           value = concurrency.get\_implementation(worker\_pool)

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           value = concurrency.get\_implementation(value)



&#x20;           if not value:

&#x20;               value = concurrency.get\_implementation(worker\_pool)



&#x20;       return value





class Hostname(StringParamType):

&#x20;   """Hostname option."""



&#x20;   name = "hostname"



&#x20;   def convert(self, value, param, ctx):

&#x20;       return host\_format(default\_nodename(value))





class Autoscale(ParamType):

&#x20;   """Autoscaling parameter."""



&#x20;   name = "<min workers>, <max workers>"



&#x20;   def convert(self, value, param, ctx):

&#x20;       value = value.split(',')



&#x20;       if len(value) > 2:

&#x20;           self.fail("Expected two comma separated integers or one integer."

&#x20;                     f"Got {len(value)} instead.")



&#x20;       if len(value) == 1:

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               value = (int(value\[0]), 0)

&#x20;           except ValueError:

&#x20;               self.fail(f"Expected an integer. Got {value} instead.")



&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           return tuple(reversed(sorted(map(int, value))))

&#x20;       except ValueError:

&#x20;           self.fail("Expected two comma separated integers."

&#x20;                     f"Got {value.join(',')} instead.")





CELERY\_BEAT = CeleryBeat()

WORKERS\_POOL = WorkersPool()

HOSTNAME = Hostname()

AUTOSCALE = Autoscale()



C\_FAKEFORK = os.environ.get('C\_FAKEFORK')





def detach(path, argv, logfile=None, pidfile=None, uid=None,

&#x20;          gid=None, umask=None, workdir=None, fake=False, app=None,

&#x20;          executable=None, hostname=None):

&#x20;   """Detach program by argv."""

&#x20;   fake = 1 if C\_FAKEFORK else fake

&#x20;   # `detached()` will attempt to touch the logfile to confirm that error

&#x20;   # messages won't be lost after detaching stdout/err, but this means we need

&#x20;   # to pre-format it rather than relying on `setup\_logging\_subsystem()` like

&#x20;   # we can elsewhere.

&#x20;   logfile = node\_format(logfile, hostname)

&#x20;   with detached(logfile, pidfile, uid, gid, umask, workdir, fake,

&#x20;                 after\_forkers=False):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           if executable is not None:

&#x20;               path = executable

&#x20;           os.execv(path, \[path] + argv)

&#x20;           return EX\_OK

&#x20;       except Exception:  # pylint: disable=broad-except

&#x20;           if app is None:

&#x20;               from celery import current\_app

&#x20;               app = current\_app

&#x20;           app.log.setup\_logging\_subsystem(

&#x20;               'ERROR', logfile, hostname=hostname)

&#x20;           logger.critical("Can't exec %r", ' '.join(\[path] + argv),

&#x20;                           exc\_info=True)

&#x20;           return EX\_FAILURE





@click.command(cls=CeleryDaemonCommand,

&#x20;              context\_settings={'allow\_extra\_args': True})

@click.option('-n',

&#x20;             '--hostname',

&#x20;             default=host\_format(default\_nodename(None)),

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             type=HOSTNAME,

&#x20;             help\_group="Worker Options",

&#x20;             help="Set custom hostname (e.g., 'w1@%%h').  "

&#x20;                  "Expands: %%h (hostname), %%n (name) and %%d, (domain).")

@click.option('-D',

&#x20;             '--detach',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             is\_flag=True,

&#x20;             default=False,

&#x20;             help\_group="Worker Options",

&#x20;             help="Start worker as a background process.")

@click.option('-S',

&#x20;             '--statedb',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             type=click.Path(),

&#x20;             callback=lambda ctx, \_,

&#x20;             value: value or ctx.obj.app.conf.worker\_state\_db,

&#x20;             help\_group="Worker Options",

&#x20;             help="Path to the state database. The extension '.db' may be "

&#x20;                  "appended to the filename.")

@click.option('-l',

&#x20;             '--loglevel',

&#x20;             default='WARNING',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             type=LOG\_LEVEL,

&#x20;             help\_group="Worker Options",

&#x20;             help="Logging level.")

@click.option('-O',

&#x20;             '--optimization',

&#x20;             default='default',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             type=click.Choice(('default', 'fair')),

&#x20;             help\_group="Worker Options",

&#x20;             help="Apply optimization profile.")

@click.option('--prefetch-multiplier',

&#x20;             type=int,

&#x20;             metavar="<prefetch multiplier>",

&#x20;             callback=lambda ctx, \_,

&#x20;             value: value or ctx.obj.app.conf.worker\_prefetch\_multiplier,

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group="Worker Options",

&#x20;             help="Set custom prefetch multiplier value "

&#x20;                  "for this worker instance.")

@click.option('--disable-prefetch',

&#x20;             is\_flag=True,

&#x20;             default=None,

&#x20;             callback=lambda ctx, \_,

&#x20;             value: ctx.obj.app.conf.worker\_disable\_prefetch if value is None else value,

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group="Worker Options",

&#x20;             help="Disable broker prefetching. The worker will only fetch a task when a process slot is available.")

@click.option('-c',

&#x20;             '--concurrency',

&#x20;             type=int,

&#x20;             metavar="<concurrency>",

&#x20;             callback=lambda ctx, \_,

&#x20;             value: value or ctx.obj.app.conf.worker\_concurrency,

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group="Pool Options",

&#x20;             help="Number of child processes processing the queue.  "

&#x20;                  "The default is the number of CPUs available"

&#x20;                  " on your system.")

@click.option('-P',

&#x20;             '--pool',

&#x20;             default='prefork',

&#x20;             type=WORKERS\_POOL,

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group="Pool Options",

&#x20;             help="Pool implementation.")

@click.option('-E',

&#x20;             '--task-events',

&#x20;             '--events',

&#x20;             is\_flag=True,

&#x20;             default=None,

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group="Pool Options",

&#x20;             help="Send task-related events that can be captured by monitors"

&#x20;                  " like celery events, celerymon, and others.")

@click.option('--time-limit',

&#x20;             type=float,

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group="Pool Options",

&#x20;             help="Enables a hard time limit "

&#x20;                  "(in seconds int/float) for tasks.")

@click.option('--soft-time-limit',

&#x20;             type=float,

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group="Pool Options",

&#x20;             help="Enables a soft time limit "

&#x20;                  "(in seconds int/float) for tasks.")

@click.option('--max-tasks-per-child',

&#x20;             type=int,

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group="Pool Options",

&#x20;             help="Maximum number of tasks a pool worker can execute before "

&#x20;                  "it's terminated and replaced by a new worker.")

@click.option('--max-memory-per-child',

&#x20;             type=int,

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group="Pool Options",

&#x20;             help="Maximum amount of resident memory, in KiB, that may be "

&#x20;                  "consumed by a child process before it will be replaced "

&#x20;                  "by a new one.  If a single task causes a child process "

&#x20;                  "to exceed this limit, the task will be completed and "

&#x20;                  "the child process will be replaced afterwards.\\n"

&#x20;                  "Default: no limit.")

@click.option('--purge',

&#x20;             '--discard',

&#x20;             is\_flag=True,

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group="Queue Options")

@click.option('--queues',

&#x20;             '-Q',

&#x20;             type=COMMA\_SEPARATED\_LIST,

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group="Queue Options")

@click.option('--exclude-queues',

&#x20;             '-X',

&#x20;             type=COMMA\_SEPARATED\_LIST,

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group="Queue Options")

@click.option('--include',

&#x20;             '-I',

&#x20;             type=COMMA\_SEPARATED\_LIST,

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group="Queue Options")

@click.option('--without-gossip',

&#x20;             is\_flag=True,

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group="Features")

@click.option('--without-mingle',

&#x20;             is\_flag=True,

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group="Features")

@click.option('--without-heartbeat',

&#x20;             is\_flag=True,

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group="Features", )

@click.option('--heartbeat-interval',

&#x20;             type=int,

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group="Features", )

@click.option('--autoscale',

&#x20;             type=AUTOSCALE,

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group="Features", )

@click.option('-B',

&#x20;             '--beat',

&#x20;             type=CELERY\_BEAT,

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             is\_flag=True,

&#x20;             help\_group="Embedded Beat Options")

@click.option('-s',

&#x20;             '--schedule-filename',

&#x20;             '--schedule',

&#x20;             callback=lambda ctx, \_,

&#x20;             value: value or ctx.obj.app.conf.beat\_schedule\_filename,

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group="Embedded Beat Options")

@click.option('--scheduler',

&#x20;             cls=CeleryOption,

&#x20;             help\_group="Embedded Beat Options")

@click.pass\_context

@handle\_preload\_options

def worker(ctx, hostname=None, pool\_cls=None, app=None, uid=None, gid=None,

&#x20;          loglevel=None, logfile=None, pidfile=None, statedb=None,

&#x20;          \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;   """Start worker instance.



&#x20;   \\b

&#x20;   Examples

&#x20;   --------



&#x20;   \\b

&#x20;   $ celery --app=proj worker -l INFO

&#x20;   $ celery -A proj worker -l INFO -Q hipri,lopri

&#x20;   $ celery -A proj worker --concurrency=4

&#x20;   $ celery -A proj worker --concurrency=1000 -P eventlet

&#x20;   $ celery worker --autoscale=10,0



&#x20;   """

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       app = ctx.obj.app

&#x20;       if 'disable\_prefetch' in kwargs and kwargs\['disable\_prefetch'] is not None:

&#x20;           app.conf.worker\_disable\_prefetch = kwargs.pop('disable\_prefetch')

&#x20;       if ctx.args:

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               app.config\_from\_cmdline(ctx.args, namespace='worker')

&#x20;           except (KeyError, ValueError) as e:

&#x20;               # TODO: Improve the error messages

&#x20;               raise click.UsageError(

&#x20;                   "Unable to parse extra configuration from command line.\\n"

&#x20;                   f"Reason: {e}", ctx=ctx)

&#x20;       if kwargs.get('detach', False):

&#x20;           argv = \['-m', 'celery'] + sys.argv\[1:]

&#x20;           if '--detach' in argv:

&#x20;               argv.remove('--detach')

&#x20;           if '-D' in argv:

&#x20;               argv.remove('-D')

&#x20;           if "--uid" in argv:

&#x20;               argv.remove('--uid')

&#x20;           if "--gid" in argv:

&#x20;               argv.remove('--gid')



&#x20;           return detach(sys.executable,

&#x20;                         argv,

&#x20;                         logfile=logfile,

&#x20;                         pidfile=pidfile,

&#x20;                         uid=uid, gid=gid,

&#x20;                         umask=kwargs.get('umask', None),

&#x20;                         workdir=kwargs.get('workdir', None),

&#x20;                         app=app,

&#x20;                         executable=kwargs.get('executable', None),

&#x20;                         hostname=hostname)



&#x20;       maybe\_drop\_privileges(uid=uid, gid=gid)

&#x20;       worker = app.Worker(

&#x20;           hostname=hostname, pool\_cls=pool\_cls, loglevel=loglevel,

&#x20;           logfile=logfile,  # node format handled by celery.app.log.setup

&#x20;           pidfile=node\_format(pidfile, hostname),

&#x20;           statedb=node\_format(statedb, hostname),

&#x20;           no\_color=ctx.obj.no\_color,

&#x20;           quiet=ctx.obj.quiet,

&#x20;           \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;       worker.start()

&#x20;       ctx.exit(worker.exitcode)

&#x20;   except SecurityError as e:

&#x20;       ctx.obj.error(e.args\[0])

&#x20;       ctx.exit(1)
```
