```python
"""Version of multiprocessing.Pool using Async I/O.



.. note::



&#x20;   This module will be moved soon, so don't use it directly.



This is a non-blocking version of :class:`multiprocessing.Pool`.



This code deals with three major challenges:



\#. Starting up child processes and keeping them running.

\#. Sending jobs to the processes and receiving results back.

\#. Safely shutting down this system.

"""

import errno

import gc

import inspect

import os

import select

import time

from collections import Counter, deque, namedtuple

from io import BytesIO

from numbers import Integral

from pickle import HIGHEST\_PROTOCOL

from struct import pack, unpack, unpack\_from

from time import sleep

from weakref import WeakValueDictionary, ref



from billiard import pool as \_pool

from billiard.compat import isblocking, setblocking

from billiard.pool import ACK, NACK, RUN, TERMINATE, WorkersJoined

from billiard.queues import \_SimpleQueue

from kombu.asynchronous import ERR, WRITE

from kombu.serialization import pickle as \_pickle

from kombu.utils.eventio import SELECT\_BAD\_FD

from kombu.utils.functional import fxrange

from vine import promise



from celery.signals import worker\_before\_create\_process

from celery.utils.functional import noop

from celery.utils.log import get\_logger

from celery.worker import state as worker\_state



\# pylint: disable=redefined-outer-name

\# We cache globals and attribute lookups, so disable this warning.



try:

&#x20;   from \_billiard import read as \_\_read\_\_

&#x20;   readcanbuf = True



except ImportError:



&#x20;   def \_\_read\_\_(fd, buf, size, read=os.read):

&#x20;       chunk = read(fd, size)

&#x20;       n = len(chunk)

&#x20;       if n != 0:

&#x20;           buf.write(chunk)

&#x20;       return n

&#x20;   readcanbuf = False



&#x20;   def unpack\_from(fmt, iobuf, unpack=unpack):  # noqa

&#x20;       return unpack(fmt, iobuf.getvalue())  # <-- BytesIO



\_\_all\_\_ = ('AsynPool',)



logger = get\_logger(\_\_name\_\_)

error, debug = logger.error, logger.debug



UNAVAIL = frozenset({errno.EAGAIN, errno.EINTR})



\#: Constant sent by child process when started (ready to accept work)

WORKER\_UP = 15



\#: A process must've started before this timeout (in secs.) expires.

PROC\_ALIVE\_TIMEOUT = 4.0



SCHED\_STRATEGY\_FCFS = 1

SCHED\_STRATEGY\_FAIR = 4



SCHED\_STRATEGIES = {

&#x20;   None: SCHED\_STRATEGY\_FAIR,

&#x20;   'default': SCHED\_STRATEGY\_FAIR,

&#x20;   'fast': SCHED\_STRATEGY\_FCFS,

&#x20;   'fcfs': SCHED\_STRATEGY\_FCFS,

&#x20;   'fair': SCHED\_STRATEGY\_FAIR,

}

SCHED\_STRATEGY\_TO\_NAME = {v: k for k, v in SCHED\_STRATEGIES.items()}



Ack = namedtuple('Ack', ('id', 'fd', 'payload'))





def gen\_not\_started(gen):

&#x20;   """Return true if generator is not started."""

&#x20;   return inspect.getgeneratorstate(gen) == "GEN\_CREATED"





def \_get\_job\_writer(job):

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       writer = job.\_writer

&#x20;   except AttributeError:

&#x20;       pass

&#x20;   else:

&#x20;       return writer()  # is a weakref





def \_ensure\_integral\_fd(fd):

&#x20;   return fd if isinstance(fd, Integral) else fd.fileno()





if hasattr(select, 'poll'):

&#x20;   def \_select\_imp(readers=None, writers=None, err=None, timeout=0,

&#x20;                   poll=select.poll, POLLIN=select.POLLIN,

&#x20;                   POLLOUT=select.POLLOUT, POLLERR=select.POLLERR):

&#x20;       poller = poll()

&#x20;       register = poller.register

&#x20;       fd\_to\_mask = {}



&#x20;       if readers:

&#x20;           for fd in map(\_ensure\_integral\_fd, readers):

&#x20;               fd\_to\_mask\[fd] = fd\_to\_mask.get(fd, 0) | POLLIN

&#x20;       if writers:

&#x20;           for fd in map(\_ensure\_integral\_fd, writers):

&#x20;               fd\_to\_mask\[fd] = fd\_to\_mask.get(fd, 0) | POLLOUT

&#x20;       if err:

&#x20;           for fd in map(\_ensure\_integral\_fd, err):

&#x20;               fd\_to\_mask\[fd] = fd\_to\_mask.get(fd, 0) | POLLERR



&#x20;       for fd, event\_mask in fd\_to\_mask.items():

&#x20;           register(fd, event\_mask)



&#x20;       R, W = set(), set()

&#x20;       timeout = 0 if timeout and timeout < 0 else round(timeout \* 1e3)

&#x20;       events = poller.poll(timeout)

&#x20;       for fd, event in events:

&#x20;           if event \& POLLIN:

&#x20;               R.add(fd)

&#x20;           if event \& POLLOUT:

&#x20;               W.add(fd)

&#x20;           if event \& POLLERR:

&#x20;               R.add(fd)

&#x20;       return R, W, 0

else:

&#x20;   def \_select\_imp(readers=None, writers=None, err=None, timeout=0):

&#x20;       r, w, e = select.select(readers, writers, err, timeout)

&#x20;       if e:

&#x20;           r = list(set(r) | set(e))

&#x20;       return r, w, 0





def \_select(readers=None, writers=None, err=None, timeout=0,

&#x20;           poll=\_select\_imp):

&#x20;   """Simple wrapper to :class:`\~select.select`, using :`\~select.poll`.



&#x20;   Arguments:

&#x20;       readers (Set\[Fd]): Set of reader fds to test if readable.

&#x20;       writers (Set\[Fd]): Set of writer fds to test if writable.

&#x20;       err (Set\[Fd]): Set of fds to test for error condition.



&#x20;   All fd sets passed must be mutable as this function

&#x20;   will remove non-working fds from them, this also means

&#x20;   the caller must make sure there are still fds in the sets

&#x20;   before calling us again.



&#x20;   Returns:

&#x20;       Tuple\[Set, Set, Set]: of ``(readable, writable, again)``, where

&#x20;       ``readable`` is a set of fds that have data available for read,

&#x20;       ``writable`` is a set of fds that's ready to be written to

&#x20;       and ``again`` is a flag that if set means the caller must

&#x20;       throw away the result and call us again.

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   readers = set() if readers is None else readers

&#x20;   writers = set() if writers is None else writers

&#x20;   err = set() if err is None else err

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       return poll(readers, writers, err, timeout)

&#x20;   except OSError as exc:

&#x20;       \_errno = exc.errno



&#x20;       if \_errno == errno.EINTR:

&#x20;           return set(), set(), 1

&#x20;       elif \_errno in SELECT\_BAD\_FD:

&#x20;           for fd in readers | writers | err:

&#x20;               try:

&#x20;                   select.select(\[fd], \[], \[], 0)

&#x20;               except OSError as exc:

&#x20;                   \_errno = exc.errno



&#x20;                   if \_errno not in SELECT\_BAD\_FD:

&#x20;                       raise

&#x20;                   readers.discard(fd)

&#x20;                   writers.discard(fd)

&#x20;                   err.discard(fd)

&#x20;           return set(), set(), 1

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           raise





def iterate\_file\_descriptors\_safely(fds\_iter, source\_data,

&#x20;                                   hub\_method, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;   """Apply hub method to fds in iter, remove from list if failure.



&#x20;   Some file descriptors may become stale through OS reasons

&#x20;   or possibly other reasons, so safely manage our lists of FDs.

&#x20;   :param fds\_iter: the file descriptors to iterate and apply hub\_method

&#x20;   :param source\_data: data source to remove FD if it renders OSError

&#x20;   :param hub\_method: the method to call with each fd and kwargs

&#x20;   :\*args to pass through to the hub\_method;

&#x20;   with a special syntax string '\*fd\*' represents a substitution

&#x20;   for the current fd object in the iteration (for some callers).

&#x20;   :\*\*kwargs to pass through to the hub method (no substitutions needed)

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   def \_meta\_fd\_argument\_maker():

&#x20;       # uses the current iterations value for fd

&#x20;       call\_args = args

&#x20;       if "\*fd\*" in call\_args:

&#x20;           call\_args = \[fd if arg == "\*fd\*" else arg for arg in args]

&#x20;       return call\_args

&#x20;   # Track stale FDs for cleanup possibility

&#x20;   stale\_fds = \[]

&#x20;   for fd in fds\_iter:

&#x20;       # Handle using the correct arguments to the hub method

&#x20;       hub\_args, hub\_kwargs = \_meta\_fd\_argument\_maker(), kwargs

&#x20;       try:  # Call the hub method

&#x20;           hub\_method(fd, \*hub\_args, \*\*hub\_kwargs)

&#x20;       except (OSError, FileNotFoundError):

&#x20;           logger.warning(

&#x20;               "Encountered OSError when accessing fd %s ",

&#x20;               fd, exc\_info=True)

&#x20;           stale\_fds.append(fd)  # take note of stale fd

&#x20;   # Remove now defunct fds from the managed list

&#x20;   if source\_data:

&#x20;       for fd in stale\_fds:

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               if hasattr(source\_data, 'remove'):

&#x20;                   source\_data.remove(fd)

&#x20;               else:  # then not a list/set ... try dict

&#x20;                   source\_data.pop(fd, None)

&#x20;           except ValueError:

&#x20;               logger.warning("ValueError trying to invalidate %s from %s",

&#x20;                              fd, source\_data)





class Worker(\_pool.Worker):

&#x20;   """Pool worker process."""



&#x20;   def on\_loop\_start(self, pid):

&#x20;       # our version sends a WORKER\_UP message when the process is ready

&#x20;       # to accept work, this will tell the parent that the inqueue fd

&#x20;       # is writable.

&#x20;       self.outq.put((WORKER\_UP, (pid,)))





class ResultHandler(\_pool.ResultHandler):

&#x20;   """Handles messages from the pool processes."""



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       self.fileno\_to\_outq = kwargs.pop('fileno\_to\_outq')

&#x20;       self.on\_process\_alive = kwargs.pop('on\_process\_alive')

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(\*args, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;       # add our custom message handler

&#x20;       self.state\_handlers\[WORKER\_UP] = self.on\_process\_alive



&#x20;   def \_recv\_message(self, add\_reader, fd, callback,

&#x20;                     \_\_read\_\_=\_\_read\_\_, readcanbuf=readcanbuf,

&#x20;                     BytesIO=BytesIO, unpack\_from=unpack\_from,

&#x20;                     load=\_pickle.load):

&#x20;       Hr = Br = 0

&#x20;       if readcanbuf:

&#x20;           buf = bytearray(4)

&#x20;           bufv = memoryview(buf)

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           buf = bufv = BytesIO()

&#x20;       # header



&#x20;       while Hr < 4:

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               n = \_\_read\_\_(

&#x20;                   fd, bufv\[Hr:] if readcanbuf else bufv, 4 - Hr,

&#x20;               )

&#x20;           except OSError as exc:

&#x20;               if exc.errno not in UNAVAIL:

&#x20;                   raise

&#x20;               yield

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               if n == 0:

&#x20;                   raise (OSError('End of file during message') if Hr

&#x20;                          else EOFError())

&#x20;               Hr += n



&#x20;       body\_size, = unpack\_from('>i', bufv)

&#x20;       if readcanbuf:

&#x20;           buf = bytearray(body\_size)

&#x20;           bufv = memoryview(buf)

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           buf = bufv = BytesIO()



&#x20;       while Br < body\_size:

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               n = \_\_read\_\_(

&#x20;                   fd, bufv\[Br:] if readcanbuf else bufv, body\_size - Br,

&#x20;               )

&#x20;           except OSError as exc:

&#x20;               if exc.errno not in UNAVAIL:

&#x20;                   raise

&#x20;               yield

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               if n == 0:

&#x20;                   raise (OSError('End of file during message') if Br

&#x20;                          else EOFError())

&#x20;               Br += n

&#x20;       add\_reader(fd, self.handle\_event, fd)

&#x20;       if readcanbuf:

&#x20;           message = load(BytesIO(bufv))

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           bufv.seek(0)

&#x20;           message = load(bufv)

&#x20;       if message:

&#x20;           callback(message)



&#x20;   def \_make\_process\_result(self, hub):

&#x20;       """Coroutine reading messages from the pool processes."""

&#x20;       fileno\_to\_outq = self.fileno\_to\_outq

&#x20;       on\_state\_change = self.on\_state\_change

&#x20;       add\_reader = hub.add\_reader

&#x20;       remove\_reader = hub.remove\_reader

&#x20;       recv\_message = self.\_recv\_message



&#x20;       def on\_result\_readable(fileno):

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               fileno\_to\_outq\[fileno]

&#x20;           except KeyError:  # process gone

&#x20;               return remove\_reader(fileno)

&#x20;           it = recv\_message(add\_reader, fileno, on\_state\_change)

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               next(it)

&#x20;           except StopIteration:

&#x20;               pass

&#x20;           except (OSError, EOFError):

&#x20;               remove\_reader(fileno)

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               add\_reader(fileno, it)

&#x20;       return on\_result\_readable



&#x20;   def register\_with\_event\_loop(self, hub):

&#x20;       self.handle\_event = self.\_make\_process\_result(hub)



&#x20;   def handle\_event(self, \*args):

&#x20;       # pylint: disable=method-hidden

&#x20;       #   register\_with\_event\_loop overrides this

&#x20;       raise RuntimeError('Not registered with event loop')



&#x20;   def on\_stop\_not\_started(self):

&#x20;       # This is always used, since we do not start any threads.

&#x20;       cache = self.cache

&#x20;       check\_timeouts = self.check\_timeouts

&#x20;       fileno\_to\_outq = self.fileno\_to\_outq

&#x20;       on\_state\_change = self.on\_state\_change

&#x20;       join\_exited\_workers = self.join\_exited\_workers



&#x20;       # flush the processes outqueues until they've all terminated.

&#x20;       outqueues = set(fileno\_to\_outq)

&#x20;       while cache and outqueues and self.\_state != TERMINATE:

&#x20;           if check\_timeouts is not None:

&#x20;               # make sure tasks with a time limit will time out.

&#x20;               check\_timeouts()

&#x20;           # cannot iterate and remove at the same time

&#x20;           pending\_remove\_fd = set()

&#x20;           for fd in outqueues:

&#x20;               iterate\_file\_descriptors\_safely(

&#x20;                   \[fd], self.fileno\_to\_outq, self.\_flush\_outqueue,

&#x20;                   pending\_remove\_fd.add, fileno\_to\_outq, on\_state\_change

&#x20;               )

&#x20;               try:

&#x20;                   join\_exited\_workers(shutdown=True)

&#x20;               except WorkersJoined:

&#x20;                   debug('result handler: all workers terminated')

&#x20;                   return

&#x20;           outqueues.difference\_update(pending\_remove\_fd)



&#x20;   def \_flush\_outqueue(self, fd, remove, process\_index, on\_state\_change):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           proc = process\_index\[fd]

&#x20;       except KeyError:

&#x20;           # process already found terminated

&#x20;           # this means its outqueue has already been processed

&#x20;           # by the worker lost handler.

&#x20;           return remove(fd)



&#x20;       reader = proc.outq.\_reader

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           setblocking(reader, 1)

&#x20;       except OSError:

&#x20;           return remove(fd)

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           if reader.poll(0):

&#x20;               task = reader.recv()

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               task = None

&#x20;               sleep(0.5)

&#x20;       except (OSError, EOFError):

&#x20;           return remove(fd)

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           if task:

&#x20;               on\_state\_change(task)

&#x20;       finally:

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               setblocking(reader, 0)

&#x20;           except OSError:

&#x20;               return remove(fd)





class AsynPool(\_pool.Pool):

&#x20;   """AsyncIO Pool (no threads)."""



&#x20;   ResultHandler = ResultHandler

&#x20;   Worker = Worker



&#x20;   #: Set by :meth:`register\_with\_event\_loop` after running the first time.

&#x20;   \_registered\_with\_event\_loop = False



&#x20;   def WorkerProcess(self, worker):

&#x20;       worker = super().WorkerProcess(worker)

&#x20;       worker.dead = False

&#x20;       return worker



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, processes=None, synack=False,

&#x20;                sched\_strategy=None, proc\_alive\_timeout=None,

&#x20;                \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       self.sched\_strategy = SCHED\_STRATEGIES.get(sched\_strategy,

&#x20;                                                  sched\_strategy)

&#x20;       processes = self.cpu\_count() if processes is None else processes

&#x20;       self.synack = synack

&#x20;       # create queue-pairs for all our processes in advance.

&#x20;       self.\_queues = {

&#x20;           self.create\_process\_queues(): None for \_ in range(processes)

&#x20;       }



&#x20;       # inqueue fileno -> process mapping

&#x20;       self.\_fileno\_to\_inq = {}

&#x20;       # outqueue fileno -> process mapping

&#x20;       self.\_fileno\_to\_outq = {}

&#x20;       # synqueue fileno -> process mapping

&#x20;       self.\_fileno\_to\_synq = {}



&#x20;       # We keep track of processes that haven't yet

&#x20;       # sent a WORKER\_UP message.  If a process fails to send

&#x20;       # this message within \_proc\_alive\_timeout we terminate it

&#x20;       # and hope the next process will recover.

&#x20;       self.\_proc\_alive\_timeout = (

&#x20;           PROC\_ALIVE\_TIMEOUT if proc\_alive\_timeout is None

&#x20;           else proc\_alive\_timeout

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       self.\_waiting\_to\_start = set()



&#x20;       # denormalized set of all inqueues.

&#x20;       self.\_all\_inqueues = set()



&#x20;       # Set of fds being written to (busy)

&#x20;       self.\_active\_writes = set()



&#x20;       # Set of active co-routines currently writing jobs.

&#x20;       self.\_active\_writers = set()



&#x20;       # Set of fds that are busy (executing task)

&#x20;       self.\_busy\_workers = set()

&#x20;       self.\_mark\_worker\_as\_available = self.\_busy\_workers.discard



&#x20;       # Holds jobs waiting to be written to child processes.

&#x20;       self.outbound\_buffer = deque()



&#x20;       self.write\_stats = Counter()



&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(processes, \*args, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;       for proc in self.\_pool:

&#x20;           # create initial mappings, these will be updated

&#x20;           # as processes are recycled, or found lost elsewhere.

&#x20;           self.\_fileno\_to\_outq\[proc.outqR\_fd] = proc

&#x20;           self.\_fileno\_to\_synq\[proc.synqW\_fd] = proc



&#x20;       self.on\_soft\_timeout = getattr(

&#x20;           self.\_timeout\_handler, 'on\_soft\_timeout', noop,

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       self.on\_hard\_timeout = getattr(

&#x20;           self.\_timeout\_handler, 'on\_hard\_timeout', noop,

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def \_create\_worker\_process(self, i):

&#x20;       worker\_before\_create\_process.send(sender=self)

&#x20;       gc.collect()  # Issue #2927

&#x20;       return super().\_create\_worker\_process(i)



&#x20;   def \_event\_process\_exit(self, hub, proc):

&#x20;       # This method is called whenever the process sentinel is readable.

&#x20;       self.\_untrack\_child\_process(proc, hub)

&#x20;       self.maintain\_pool()



&#x20;   def \_track\_child\_process(self, proc, hub):

&#x20;       """Helper method determines appropriate fd for process."""

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           fd = proc.\_sentinel\_poll

&#x20;       except AttributeError:

&#x20;           # we need to duplicate the fd here to carefully

&#x20;           # control when the fd is removed from the process table,

&#x20;           # as once the original fd is closed we cannot unregister

&#x20;           # the fd from epoll(7) anymore, causing a 100% CPU poll loop.

&#x20;           fd = proc.\_sentinel\_poll = os.dup(proc.\_popen.sentinel)

&#x20;       # Safely call hub.add\_reader for the determined fd

&#x20;       iterate\_file\_descriptors\_safely(

&#x20;           \[fd], None, hub.add\_reader,

&#x20;           self.\_event\_process\_exit, hub, proc)



&#x20;   def \_untrack\_child\_process(self, proc, hub):

&#x20;       if proc.\_sentinel\_poll is not None:

&#x20;           fd, proc.\_sentinel\_poll = proc.\_sentinel\_poll, None

&#x20;           hub.remove(fd)

&#x20;           os.close(fd)



&#x20;   def register\_with\_event\_loop(self, hub):

&#x20;       """Register the async pool with the current event loop."""

&#x20;       self.\_result\_handler.register\_with\_event\_loop(hub)

&#x20;       self.handle\_result\_event = self.\_result\_handler.handle\_event

&#x20;       self.\_create\_timelimit\_handlers(hub)

&#x20;       self.\_create\_process\_handlers(hub)

&#x20;       self.\_create\_write\_handlers(hub)



&#x20;       # Add handler for when a process exits (calls maintain\_pool)

&#x20;       \[self.\_track\_child\_process(w, hub) for w in self.\_pool]

&#x20;       # Handle\_result\_event is called whenever one of the

&#x20;       # result queues are readable.

&#x20;       iterate\_file\_descriptors\_safely(

&#x20;           self.\_fileno\_to\_outq, self.\_fileno\_to\_outq, hub.add\_reader,

&#x20;           self.handle\_result\_event, '\*fd\*')



&#x20;       # Timers include calling maintain\_pool at a regular interval

&#x20;       # to be certain processes are restarted.

&#x20;       for handler, interval in self.timers.items():

&#x20;           hub.call\_repeatedly(interval, handler)



&#x20;       # Add on\_poll\_start to the event loop only once to prevent duplication

&#x20;       # when the Consumer restarts due to a connection error.

&#x20;       if not self.\_registered\_with\_event\_loop:

&#x20;           hub.on\_tick.add(self.on\_poll\_start)

&#x20;           self.\_registered\_with\_event\_loop = True



&#x20;   def \_create\_timelimit\_handlers(self, hub):

&#x20;       """Create handlers used to implement time limits."""

&#x20;       call\_later = hub.call\_later

&#x20;       trefs = self.\_tref\_for\_id = WeakValueDictionary()



&#x20;       def on\_timeout\_set(R, soft, hard):

&#x20;           if soft:

&#x20;               trefs\[R.\_job] = call\_later(

&#x20;                   soft, self.\_on\_soft\_timeout, R.\_job, soft, hard, hub,

&#x20;               )

&#x20;           elif hard:

&#x20;               trefs\[R.\_job] = call\_later(

&#x20;                   hard, self.\_on\_hard\_timeout, R.\_job,

&#x20;               )

&#x20;       self.on\_timeout\_set = on\_timeout\_set



&#x20;       def \_discard\_tref(job):

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               tref = trefs.pop(job)

&#x20;               tref.cancel()

&#x20;               del tref

&#x20;           except (KeyError, AttributeError):

&#x20;               pass  # out of scope

&#x20;       self.\_discard\_tref = \_discard\_tref



&#x20;       def on\_timeout\_cancel(R):

&#x20;           \_discard\_tref(R.\_job)

&#x20;       self.on\_timeout\_cancel = on\_timeout\_cancel



&#x20;   def \_on\_soft\_timeout(self, job, soft, hard, hub):

&#x20;       # only used by async pool.

&#x20;       if hard:

&#x20;           self.\_tref\_for\_id\[job] = hub.call\_later(

&#x20;               hard - soft, self.\_on\_hard\_timeout, job,

&#x20;           )

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           result = self.\_cache\[job]

&#x20;       except KeyError:

&#x20;           pass  # job ready

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           self.on\_soft\_timeout(result)

&#x20;       finally:

&#x20;           if not hard:

&#x20;               # remove tref

&#x20;               self.\_discard\_tref(job)



&#x20;   def \_on\_hard\_timeout(self, job):

&#x20;       # only used by async pool.

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           result = self.\_cache\[job]

&#x20;       except KeyError:

&#x20;           pass  # job ready

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           self.on\_hard\_timeout(result)

&#x20;       finally:

&#x20;           # remove tref

&#x20;           self.\_discard\_tref(job)



&#x20;   def on\_job\_ready(self, job, i, obj, inqW\_fd):

&#x20;       self.\_mark\_worker\_as\_available(inqW\_fd)



&#x20;   def \_create\_process\_handlers(self, hub):

&#x20;       """Create handlers called on process up/down, etc."""

&#x20;       add\_reader, remove\_reader, remove\_writer = (

&#x20;           hub.add\_reader, hub.remove\_reader, hub.remove\_writer,

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       cache = self.\_cache

&#x20;       all\_inqueues = self.\_all\_inqueues

&#x20;       fileno\_to\_inq = self.\_fileno\_to\_inq

&#x20;       fileno\_to\_outq = self.\_fileno\_to\_outq

&#x20;       fileno\_to\_synq = self.\_fileno\_to\_synq

&#x20;       busy\_workers = self.\_busy\_workers

&#x20;       handle\_result\_event = self.handle\_result\_event

&#x20;       process\_flush\_queues = self.process\_flush\_queues

&#x20;       waiting\_to\_start = self.\_waiting\_to\_start



&#x20;       def verify\_process\_alive(proc):

&#x20;           proc = proc()  # is a weakref

&#x20;           if (proc is not None and proc.\_is\_alive() and

&#x20;                   proc in waiting\_to\_start):

&#x20;               assert proc.outqR\_fd in fileno\_to\_outq

&#x20;               assert fileno\_to\_outq\[proc.outqR\_fd] is proc

&#x20;               assert proc.outqR\_fd in hub.readers

&#x20;               error('Timed out waiting for UP message from %r', proc)

&#x20;               os.kill(proc.pid, 9)



&#x20;       def on\_process\_up(proc):

&#x20;           """Called when a process has started."""

&#x20;           # If we got the same fd as a previous process then we'll also

&#x20;           # receive jobs in the old buffer, so we need to reset the

&#x20;           # job.\_write\_to and job.\_scheduled\_for attributes used to recover

&#x20;           # message boundaries when processes exit.

&#x20;           infd = proc.inqW\_fd

&#x20;           for job in cache.values():

&#x20;               if job.\_write\_to and job.\_write\_to.inqW\_fd == infd:

&#x20;                   job.\_write\_to = proc

&#x20;               if job.\_scheduled\_for and job.\_scheduled\_for.inqW\_fd == infd:

&#x20;                   job.\_scheduled\_for = proc

&#x20;           fileno\_to\_outq\[proc.outqR\_fd] = proc



&#x20;           # maintain\_pool is called whenever a process exits.

&#x20;           self.\_track\_child\_process(proc, hub)



&#x20;           assert not isblocking(proc.outq.\_reader)



&#x20;           # handle\_result\_event is called when the processes outqueue is

&#x20;           # readable.

&#x20;           add\_reader(proc.outqR\_fd, handle\_result\_event, proc.outqR\_fd)



&#x20;           waiting\_to\_start.add(proc)

&#x20;           hub.call\_later(

&#x20;               self.\_proc\_alive\_timeout, verify\_process\_alive, ref(proc),

&#x20;           )



&#x20;       self.on\_process\_up = on\_process\_up



&#x20;       def \_remove\_from\_index(obj, proc, index, remove\_fun, callback=None):

&#x20;           # this remove the file descriptors for a process from

&#x20;           # the indices.  we have to make sure we don't overwrite

&#x20;           # another processes fds, as the fds may be reused.

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               fd = obj.fileno()

&#x20;           except OSError:

&#x20;               return



&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               if index\[fd] is proc:

&#x20;                   # fd hasn't been reused so we can remove it from index.

&#x20;                   index.pop(fd, None)

&#x20;           except KeyError:

&#x20;               pass

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               remove\_fun(fd)

&#x20;               if callback is not None:

&#x20;                   callback(fd)

&#x20;           return fd



&#x20;       def on\_process\_down(proc):

&#x20;           """Called when a worker process exits."""

&#x20;           if getattr(proc, 'dead', None):

&#x20;               return

&#x20;           process\_flush\_queues(proc)

&#x20;           \_remove\_from\_index(

&#x20;               proc.outq.\_reader, proc, fileno\_to\_outq, remove\_reader,

&#x20;           )

&#x20;           if proc.synq:

&#x20;               \_remove\_from\_index(

&#x20;                   proc.synq.\_writer, proc, fileno\_to\_synq, remove\_writer,

&#x20;               )

&#x20;           inq = \_remove\_from\_index(

&#x20;               proc.inq.\_writer, proc, fileno\_to\_inq, remove\_writer,

&#x20;               callback=all\_inqueues.discard,

&#x20;           )

&#x20;           if inq:

&#x20;               busy\_workers.discard(inq)

&#x20;           self.\_untrack\_child\_process(proc, hub)

&#x20;           waiting\_to\_start.discard(proc)

&#x20;           self.\_active\_writes.discard(proc.inqW\_fd)

&#x20;           remove\_writer(proc.inq.\_writer)

&#x20;           remove\_reader(proc.outq.\_reader)

&#x20;           if proc.synqR\_fd:

&#x20;               remove\_reader(proc.synq.\_reader)

&#x20;           if proc.synqW\_fd:

&#x20;               self.\_active\_writes.discard(proc.synqW\_fd)

&#x20;               remove\_reader(proc.synq.\_writer)

&#x20;       self.on\_process\_down = on\_process\_down



&#x20;   def \_create\_write\_handlers(self, hub,

&#x20;                              pack=pack, dumps=\_pickle.dumps,

&#x20;                              protocol=HIGHEST\_PROTOCOL):

&#x20;       """Create handlers used to write data to child processes."""

&#x20;       fileno\_to\_inq = self.\_fileno\_to\_inq

&#x20;       fileno\_to\_synq = self.\_fileno\_to\_synq

&#x20;       outbound = self.outbound\_buffer

&#x20;       pop\_message = outbound.popleft

&#x20;       put\_message = outbound.append

&#x20;       all\_inqueues = self.\_all\_inqueues

&#x20;       active\_writes = self.\_active\_writes

&#x20;       active\_writers = self.\_active\_writers

&#x20;       busy\_workers = self.\_busy\_workers

&#x20;       diff = all\_inqueues.difference

&#x20;       add\_writer = hub.add\_writer

&#x20;       hub\_add, hub\_remove = hub.add, hub.remove

&#x20;       mark\_write\_fd\_as\_active = active\_writes.add

&#x20;       mark\_write\_gen\_as\_active = active\_writers.add

&#x20;       mark\_worker\_as\_busy = busy\_workers.add

&#x20;       write\_generator\_done = active\_writers.discard

&#x20;       get\_job = self.\_cache.\_\_getitem\_\_

&#x20;       write\_stats = self.write\_stats

&#x20;       is\_fair\_strategy = self.sched\_strategy == SCHED\_STRATEGY\_FAIR

&#x20;       revoked\_tasks = worker\_state.revoked

&#x20;       getpid = os.getpid



&#x20;       precalc = {ACK: self.\_create\_payload(ACK, (0,)),

&#x20;                  NACK: self.\_create\_payload(NACK, (0,))}



&#x20;       def \_put\_back(job, \_time=time.time):

&#x20;           # puts back at the end of the queue

&#x20;           if job.\_terminated is not None or \\

&#x20;                   job.correlation\_id in revoked\_tasks:

&#x20;               if not job.\_accepted:

&#x20;                   job.\_ack(None, \_time(), getpid(), None)

&#x20;               job.\_set\_terminated(job.\_terminated)

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               # XXX linear lookup, should find a better way,

&#x20;               # but this happens rarely and is here to protect against races.

&#x20;               if job not in outbound:

&#x20;                   outbound.appendleft(job)

&#x20;       self.\_put\_back = \_put\_back



&#x20;       # called for every event loop iteration, and if there

&#x20;       # are messages pending this will schedule writing one message

&#x20;       # by registering the 'schedule\_writes' function for all currently

&#x20;       # inactive inqueues (not already being written to)



&#x20;       # consolidate means the event loop will merge them

&#x20;       # and call the callback once with the list writable fds as

&#x20;       # argument.  Using this means we minimize the risk of having

&#x20;       # the same fd receive every task if the pipe read buffer is not

&#x20;       # full.



&#x20;       def on\_poll\_start():

&#x20;           # Determine which io descriptors are not busy

&#x20;           inactive = diff(active\_writes)



&#x20;           # Determine hub\_add vs hub\_remove strategy conditional

&#x20;           if is\_fair\_strategy:

&#x20;               # outbound buffer present and idle workers exist

&#x20;               add\_cond = outbound and len(busy\_workers) < len(all\_inqueues)

&#x20;           else:  # default is add when data exists in outbound buffer

&#x20;               add\_cond = outbound



&#x20;           if add\_cond:  # calling hub\_add vs hub\_remove

&#x20;               iterate\_file\_descriptors\_safely(

&#x20;                   inactive, all\_inqueues, hub\_add,

&#x20;                   None, WRITE | ERR, consolidate=True)

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               iterate\_file\_descriptors\_safely(

&#x20;                   inactive, all\_inqueues, hub.remove\_writer)

&#x20;       self.on\_poll\_start = on\_poll\_start



&#x20;       def on\_inqueue\_close(fd, proc):

&#x20;           # Makes sure the fd is removed from tracking when

&#x20;           # the connection is closed, this is essential as fds may be reused.

&#x20;           busy\_workers.discard(fd)

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               if fileno\_to\_inq\[fd] is proc:

&#x20;                   fileno\_to\_inq.pop(fd, None)

&#x20;                   active\_writes.discard(fd)

&#x20;                   all\_inqueues.discard(fd)

&#x20;           except KeyError:

&#x20;               pass

&#x20;       self.on\_inqueue\_close = on\_inqueue\_close

&#x20;       self.hub\_remove = hub\_remove



&#x20;       def schedule\_writes(ready\_fds, total\_write\_count=None):

&#x20;           if not total\_write\_count:

&#x20;               total\_write\_count = \[0]

&#x20;           # Schedule write operation to ready file descriptor.

&#x20;           # The file descriptor is writable, but that does not

&#x20;           # mean the process is currently reading from the socket.

&#x20;           # The socket is buffered so writable simply means that

&#x20;           # the buffer can accept at least 1 byte of data.



&#x20;           # This means we have to cycle between the ready fds.

&#x20;           # the first version used shuffle, but this version

&#x20;           # using `total\_writes % ready\_fds` is about 30% faster

&#x20;           # with many processes, and also leans more towards fairness

&#x20;           # in write stats when used with many processes

&#x20;           # \[XXX On macOS, this may vary depending

&#x20;           # on event loop implementation (i.e, select/poll vs epoll), so

&#x20;           # have to test further]

&#x20;           num\_ready = len(ready\_fds)



&#x20;           for \_ in range(num\_ready):

&#x20;               ready\_fd = ready\_fds\[total\_write\_count\[0] % num\_ready]

&#x20;               total\_write\_count\[0] += 1

&#x20;               if ready\_fd in active\_writes:

&#x20;                   # already writing to this fd

&#x20;                   continue

&#x20;               if is\_fair\_strategy and ready\_fd in busy\_workers:

&#x20;                   # worker is already busy with another task

&#x20;                   continue

&#x20;               if ready\_fd not in all\_inqueues:

&#x20;                   hub.remove\_writer(ready\_fd)

&#x20;                   continue

&#x20;               try:

&#x20;                   job = pop\_message()

&#x20;               except IndexError:

&#x20;                   # no more messages, remove all inactive fds from the hub.

&#x20;                   # this is important since the fds are always writable

&#x20;                   # as long as there's 1 byte left in the buffer, and so

&#x20;                   # this may create a spinloop where the event loop

&#x20;                   # always wakes up.

&#x20;                   for inqfd in diff(active\_writes):

&#x20;                       hub.remove\_writer(inqfd)

&#x20;                   break



&#x20;               else:

&#x20;                   if not job.\_accepted:  # job not accepted by another worker

&#x20;                       try:

&#x20;                           # keep track of what process the write operation

&#x20;                           # was scheduled for.

&#x20;                           proc = job.\_scheduled\_for = fileno\_to\_inq\[ready\_fd]

&#x20;                       except KeyError:

&#x20;                           # write was scheduled for this fd but the process

&#x20;                           # has since exited and the message must be sent to

&#x20;                           # another process.

&#x20;                           put\_message(job)

&#x20;                           continue

&#x20;                       cor = \_write\_job(proc, ready\_fd, job)

&#x20;                       job.\_writer = ref(cor)

&#x20;                       mark\_write\_gen\_as\_active(cor)

&#x20;                       mark\_write\_fd\_as\_active(ready\_fd)

&#x20;                       mark\_worker\_as\_busy(ready\_fd)



&#x20;                       # Try to write immediately, in case there's an error.

&#x20;                       try:

&#x20;                           next(cor)

&#x20;                       except StopIteration:

&#x20;                           pass

&#x20;                       except OSError as exc:

&#x20;                           if exc.errno != errno.EBADF:

&#x20;                               raise

&#x20;                       else:

&#x20;                           add\_writer(ready\_fd, cor)

&#x20;       hub.consolidate\_callback = schedule\_writes



&#x20;       def send\_job(tup):

&#x20;           # Schedule writing job request for when one of the process

&#x20;           # inqueues are writable.

&#x20;           body = dumps(tup, protocol=protocol)

&#x20;           body\_size = len(body)

&#x20;           header = pack('>I', body\_size)

&#x20;           # index 1,0 is the job ID.

&#x20;           job = get\_job(tup\[1]\[0])

&#x20;           job.\_payload = memoryview(header), memoryview(body), body\_size

&#x20;           put\_message(job)

&#x20;       self.\_quick\_put = send\_job



&#x20;       def on\_not\_recovering(proc, fd, job, exc):

&#x20;           logger.exception(

&#x20;               'Process inqueue damaged: %r %r: %r', proc, proc.exitcode, exc)

&#x20;           if proc.\_is\_alive():

&#x20;               proc.terminate()

&#x20;           hub.remove(fd)

&#x20;           self.\_put\_back(job)



&#x20;       def \_write\_job(proc, fd, job):

&#x20;           # writes job to the worker process.

&#x20;           # Operation must complete if more than one byte of data

&#x20;           # was written.  If the broker connection is lost

&#x20;           # and no data was written the operation shall be canceled.

&#x20;           header, body, body\_size = job.\_payload

&#x20;           errors = 0

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               # job result keeps track of what process the job is sent to.

&#x20;               job.\_write\_to = proc

&#x20;               send = proc.send\_job\_offset



&#x20;               Hw = Bw = 0

&#x20;               # write header

&#x20;               while Hw < 4:

&#x20;                   try:

&#x20;                       Hw += send(header, Hw)

&#x20;                   except Exception as exc:  # pylint: disable=broad-except

&#x20;                       if getattr(exc, 'errno', None) not in UNAVAIL:

&#x20;                           raise

&#x20;                       # suspend until more data

&#x20;                       errors += 1

&#x20;                       if errors > 100:

&#x20;                           on\_not\_recovering(proc, fd, job, exc)

&#x20;                           raise StopIteration()

&#x20;                       yield

&#x20;                   else:

&#x20;                       errors = 0



&#x20;               # write body

&#x20;               while Bw < body\_size:

&#x20;                   try:

&#x20;                       Bw += send(body, Bw)

&#x20;                   except Exception as exc:  # pylint: disable=broad-except

&#x20;                       if getattr(exc, 'errno', None) not in UNAVAIL:

&#x20;                           raise

&#x20;                       # suspend until more data

&#x20;                       errors += 1

&#x20;                       if errors > 100:

&#x20;                           on\_not\_recovering(proc, fd, job, exc)

&#x20;                           raise StopIteration()

&#x20;                       yield

&#x20;                   else:

&#x20;                       errors = 0

&#x20;           finally:

&#x20;               hub.remove\_writer(fd)

&#x20;               write\_stats\[proc.index] += 1

&#x20;               # message written, so this fd is now available

&#x20;               active\_writes.discard(fd)

&#x20;               write\_generator\_done(job.\_writer())  # is a weakref



&#x20;       def send\_ack(response, pid, job, fd):

&#x20;           # Only used when synack is enabled.

&#x20;           # Schedule writing ack response for when the fd is writable.

&#x20;           msg = Ack(job, fd, precalc\[response])

&#x20;           callback = promise(write\_generator\_done)

&#x20;           cor = \_write\_ack(fd, msg, callback=callback)

&#x20;           mark\_write\_gen\_as\_active(cor)

&#x20;           mark\_write\_fd\_as\_active(fd)

&#x20;           callback.args = (cor,)

&#x20;           add\_writer(fd, cor)

&#x20;       self.send\_ack = send\_ack



&#x20;       def \_write\_ack(fd, ack, callback=None):

&#x20;           # writes ack back to the worker if synack enabled.

&#x20;           # this operation \*MUST\* complete, otherwise

&#x20;           # the worker process will hang waiting for the ack.

&#x20;           header, body, body\_size = ack\[2]

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               try:

&#x20;                   proc = fileno\_to\_synq\[fd]

&#x20;               except KeyError:

&#x20;                   # process died, we can safely discard the ack at this

&#x20;                   # point.

&#x20;                   raise StopIteration()

&#x20;               send = proc.send\_syn\_offset



&#x20;               Hw = Bw = 0

&#x20;               # write header

&#x20;               while Hw < 4:

&#x20;                   try:

&#x20;                       Hw += send(header, Hw)

&#x20;                   except Exception as exc:  # pylint: disable=broad-except

&#x20;                       if getattr(exc, 'errno', None) not in UNAVAIL:

&#x20;                           raise

&#x20;                       yield



&#x20;               # write body

&#x20;               while Bw < body\_size:

&#x20;                   try:

&#x20;                       Bw += send(body, Bw)

&#x20;                   except Exception as exc:  # pylint: disable=broad-except

&#x20;                       if getattr(exc, 'errno', None) not in UNAVAIL:

&#x20;                           raise

&#x20;                       # suspend until more data

&#x20;                       yield

&#x20;           finally:

&#x20;               if callback:

&#x20;                   callback()

&#x20;               # message written, so this fd is now available

&#x20;               active\_writes.discard(fd)



&#x20;   def flush(self):

&#x20;       if self.\_state == TERMINATE:

&#x20;           return

&#x20;       # cancel all tasks that haven't been accepted so that NACK is sent

&#x20;       # if synack is enabled.

&#x20;       if self.synack:

&#x20;           for job in self.\_cache.values():

&#x20;               if not job.\_accepted:

&#x20;                   job.\_cancel()



&#x20;       # clear the outgoing buffer as the tasks will be redelivered by

&#x20;       # the broker anyway.

&#x20;       if self.outbound\_buffer:

&#x20;           self.outbound\_buffer.clear()



&#x20;       self.maintain\_pool()



&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           # ...but we must continue writing the payloads we already started

&#x20;           # to keep message boundaries.

&#x20;           # The messages may be NACK'ed later if synack is enabled.

&#x20;           if self.\_state == RUN:

&#x20;               # flush outgoing buffers

&#x20;               intervals = fxrange(0.01, 0.1, 0.01, repeatlast=True)



&#x20;               # TODO: Rewrite this as a dictionary comprehension once we drop support for Python 3.7

&#x20;               #       This dict comprehension requires the walrus operator which is only available in 3.8.

&#x20;               owned\_by = {}

&#x20;               for job in self.\_cache.values():

&#x20;                   writer = \_get\_job\_writer(job)

&#x20;                   if writer is not None:

&#x20;                       owned\_by\[writer] = job



&#x20;               if not self.\_active\_writers:

&#x20;                   self.\_cache.clear()

&#x20;               else:

&#x20;                   while self.\_active\_writers:

&#x20;                       writers = list(self.\_active\_writers)

&#x20;                       for gen in writers:

&#x20;                           if (gen.\_\_name\_\_ == '\_write\_job' and

&#x20;                                   gen\_not\_started(gen)):

&#x20;                               # hasn't started writing the job so can

&#x20;                               # discard the task, but we must also remove

&#x20;                               # it from the Pool.\_cache.

&#x20;                               try:

&#x20;                                   job = owned\_by\[gen]

&#x20;                               except KeyError:

&#x20;                                   pass

&#x20;                               else:

&#x20;                                   # removes from Pool.\_cache

&#x20;                                   job.discard()

&#x20;                               self.\_active\_writers.discard(gen)

&#x20;                           else:

&#x20;                               try:

&#x20;                                   job = owned\_by\[gen]

&#x20;                               except KeyError:

&#x20;                                   pass

&#x20;                               else:

&#x20;                                   job\_proc = job.\_write\_to

&#x20;                                   if job\_proc.\_is\_alive():

&#x20;                                       self.\_flush\_writer(job\_proc, gen)



&#x20;                                   job.discard()

&#x20;                   # workers may have exited in the meantime.

&#x20;                   self.maintain\_pool()

&#x20;                   sleep(next(intervals))  # don't busyloop

&#x20;       finally:

&#x20;           self.outbound\_buffer.clear()

&#x20;           self.\_active\_writers.clear()

&#x20;           self.\_active\_writes.clear()

&#x20;           self.\_busy\_workers.clear()



&#x20;   def \_flush\_writer(self, proc, writer):

&#x20;       fds = {proc.inq.\_writer}

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           while fds:

&#x20;               if not proc.\_is\_alive():

&#x20;                   break  # process exited

&#x20;               readable, writable, again = \_select(

&#x20;                   writers=fds, err=fds, timeout=0.5,

&#x20;               )

&#x20;               if not again and (writable or readable):

&#x20;                   try:

&#x20;                       next(writer)

&#x20;                   except (StopIteration, OSError, EOFError):

&#x20;                       break

&#x20;       finally:

&#x20;           self.\_active\_writers.discard(writer)



&#x20;   def get\_process\_queues(self):

&#x20;       """Get queues for a new process.



&#x20;       Here we'll find an unused slot, as there should always

&#x20;       be one available when we start a new process.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return next(q for q, owner in self.\_queues.items()

&#x20;                   if owner is None)



&#x20;   def on\_grow(self, n):

&#x20;       """Grow the pool by ``n`` processes."""

&#x20;       diff = max(self.\_processes - len(self.\_queues), 0)

&#x20;       if diff:

&#x20;           self.\_queues.update({

&#x20;               self.create\_process\_queues(): None for \_ in range(diff)

&#x20;           })



&#x20;   def on\_shrink(self, n):

&#x20;       """Shrink the pool by ``n`` processes."""



&#x20;   def create\_process\_queues(self):

&#x20;       """Create new in, out, etc. queues, returned as a tuple."""

&#x20;       # NOTE: Pipes must be set O\_NONBLOCK at creation time (the original

&#x20;       # fd), otherwise it won't be possible to change the flags until

&#x20;       # there's an actual reader/writer on the other side.

&#x20;       inq = \_SimpleQueue(wnonblock=True)

&#x20;       outq = \_SimpleQueue(rnonblock=True)

&#x20;       synq = None

&#x20;       assert isblocking(inq.\_reader)

&#x20;       assert not isblocking(inq.\_writer)

&#x20;       assert not isblocking(outq.\_reader)

&#x20;       assert isblocking(outq.\_writer)

&#x20;       if self.synack:

&#x20;           synq = \_SimpleQueue(wnonblock=True)

&#x20;           assert isblocking(synq.\_reader)

&#x20;           assert not isblocking(synq.\_writer)

&#x20;       return inq, outq, synq



&#x20;   def on\_process\_alive(self, pid):

&#x20;       """Called when receiving the :const:`WORKER\_UP` message.



&#x20;       Marks the process as ready to receive work.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           proc = next(w for w in self.\_pool if w.pid == pid)

&#x20;       except StopIteration:

&#x20;           return logger.warning('process with pid=%s already exited', pid)

&#x20;       assert proc.inqW\_fd not in self.\_fileno\_to\_inq

&#x20;       assert proc.inqW\_fd not in self.\_all\_inqueues

&#x20;       self.\_waiting\_to\_start.discard(proc)

&#x20;       self.\_fileno\_to\_inq\[proc.inqW\_fd] = proc

&#x20;       self.\_fileno\_to\_synq\[proc.synqW\_fd] = proc

&#x20;       self.\_all\_inqueues.add(proc.inqW\_fd)



&#x20;   def on\_job\_process\_down(self, job, pid\_gone):

&#x20;       """Called for each job when the process assigned to it exits."""

&#x20;       if job.\_write\_to and not job.\_write\_to.\_is\_alive():

&#x20;           # job was partially written

&#x20;           self.on\_partial\_read(job, job.\_write\_to)

&#x20;       elif job.\_scheduled\_for and not job.\_scheduled\_for.\_is\_alive():

&#x20;           # job was only scheduled to be written to this process,

&#x20;           # but no data was sent so put it back on the outbound\_buffer.

&#x20;           self.\_put\_back(job)



&#x20;   def on\_job\_process\_lost(self, job, pid, exitcode):

&#x20;       """Called when the process executing job' exits.



&#x20;       This happens when the process job'

&#x20;       was assigned to exited by mysterious means (error exitcodes and

&#x20;       signals).

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       self.mark\_as\_worker\_lost(job, exitcode)



&#x20;   def human\_write\_stats(self):

&#x20;       if self.write\_stats is None:

&#x20;           return 'N/A'

&#x20;       vals = list(self.write\_stats.values())

&#x20;       total = sum(vals)



&#x20;       def per(v, total):

&#x20;           return f'{(float(v) / total) if v else 0:.2f}'



&#x20;       return {

&#x20;           'total': total,

&#x20;           'avg': per(total / len(self.write\_stats) if total else 0, total),

&#x20;           'all': ', '.join(per(v, total) for v in vals),

&#x20;           'raw': ', '.join(map(str, vals)),

&#x20;           'strategy': SCHED\_STRATEGY\_TO\_NAME.get(

&#x20;               self.sched\_strategy, self.sched\_strategy,

&#x20;           ),

&#x20;           'inqueues': {

&#x20;               'total': len(self.\_all\_inqueues),

&#x20;               'active': len(self.\_active\_writes),

&#x20;           }

&#x20;       }



&#x20;   def \_process\_cleanup\_queues(self, proc):

&#x20;       """Called to clean up queues after process exit."""

&#x20;       if not proc.dead:

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               self.\_queues\[self.\_find\_worker\_queues(proc)] = None

&#x20;           except (KeyError, ValueError):

&#x20;               pass



&#x20;   @staticmethod

&#x20;   def \_stop\_task\_handler(task\_handler):

&#x20;       """Called at shutdown to tell processes that we're shutting down."""

&#x20;       for proc in task\_handler.pool:

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               setblocking(proc.inq.\_writer, 1)

&#x20;           except OSError:

&#x20;               pass

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               try:

&#x20;                   proc.inq.put(None)

&#x20;               except OSError as exc:

&#x20;                   if exc.errno != errno.EBADF:

&#x20;                       raise



&#x20;   def create\_result\_handler(self):

&#x20;       return super().create\_result\_handler(

&#x20;           fileno\_to\_outq=self.\_fileno\_to\_outq,

&#x20;           on\_process\_alive=self.on\_process\_alive,

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def \_process\_register\_queues(self, proc, queues):

&#x20;       """Mark new ownership for ``queues`` to update fileno indices."""

&#x20;       assert queues in self.\_queues

&#x20;       b = len(self.\_queues)

&#x20;       self.\_queues\[queues] = proc

&#x20;       assert b == len(self.\_queues)



&#x20;   def \_find\_worker\_queues(self, proc):

&#x20;       """Find the queues owned by ``proc``."""

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           return next(q for q, owner in self.\_queues.items()

&#x20;                       if owner == proc)

&#x20;       except StopIteration:

&#x20;           raise ValueError(proc)



&#x20;   def \_setup\_queues(self):

&#x20;       # this is only used by the original pool that used a shared

&#x20;       # queue for all processes.

&#x20;       self.\_quick\_put = None



&#x20;       # these attributes are unused by this class, but we'll still

&#x20;       # have to initialize them for compatibility.

&#x20;       self.\_inqueue = self.\_outqueue = \\

&#x20;           self.\_quick\_get = self.\_poll\_result = None



&#x20;   def process\_flush\_queues(self, proc):

&#x20;       """Flush all queues.



&#x20;       Including the outbound buffer, so that

&#x20;       all tasks that haven't been started will be discarded.



&#x20;       In Celery this is called whenever the transport connection is lost

&#x20;       (consumer restart), and when a process is terminated.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       resq = proc.outq.\_reader

&#x20;       on\_state\_change = self.\_result\_handler.on\_state\_change

&#x20;       fds = {resq}

&#x20;       while fds and not resq.closed and self.\_state != TERMINATE:

&#x20;           readable, \_, \_ = \_select(fds, None, fds, timeout=0.01)

&#x20;           if readable:

&#x20;               try:

&#x20;                   task = resq.recv()

&#x20;               except (OSError, EOFError) as exc:

&#x20;                   \_errno = getattr(exc, 'errno', None)

&#x20;                   if \_errno == errno.EINTR:

&#x20;                       continue

&#x20;                   elif \_errno == errno.EAGAIN:

&#x20;                       break

&#x20;                   elif \_errno not in UNAVAIL:

&#x20;                       debug('got %r while flushing process %r',

&#x20;                             exc, proc, exc\_info=1)

&#x20;                   break

&#x20;               else:

&#x20;                   if task is None:

&#x20;                       debug('got sentinel while flushing process %r', proc)

&#x20;                       break

&#x20;                   else:

&#x20;                       on\_state\_change(task)

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               break



&#x20;   def on\_partial\_read(self, job, proc):

&#x20;       """Called when a job was partially written to exited child."""

&#x20;       # worker terminated by signal:

&#x20;       # we cannot reuse the sockets again, because we don't know if

&#x20;       # the process wrote/read anything from them, and if so we cannot

&#x20;       # restore the message boundaries.

&#x20;       if not job.\_accepted:

&#x20;           # job was not acked, so find another worker to send it to.

&#x20;           self.\_put\_back(job)

&#x20;       writer = \_get\_job\_writer(job)

&#x20;       if writer:

&#x20;           self.\_active\_writers.discard(writer)

&#x20;           del writer



&#x20;       if not proc.dead:

&#x20;           proc.dead = True

&#x20;           # Replace queues to avoid reuse

&#x20;           before = len(self.\_queues)

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               queues = self.\_find\_worker\_queues(proc)

&#x20;               if self.destroy\_queues(queues, proc):

&#x20;                   self.\_queues\[self.create\_process\_queues()] = None

&#x20;           except ValueError:

&#x20;               pass

&#x20;           assert len(self.\_queues) == before



&#x20;   def destroy\_queues(self, queues, proc):

&#x20;       """Destroy queues that can no longer be used.



&#x20;       This way they can be replaced by new usable sockets.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       assert not proc.\_is\_alive()

&#x20;       self.\_waiting\_to\_start.discard(proc)

&#x20;       removed = 1

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           self.\_queues.pop(queues)

&#x20;       except KeyError:

&#x20;           removed = 0

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           self.on\_inqueue\_close(queues\[0].\_writer.fileno(), proc)

&#x20;       except OSError:

&#x20;           pass

&#x20;       for queue in queues:

&#x20;           if queue:

&#x20;               for sock in (queue.\_reader, queue.\_writer):

&#x20;                   if not sock.closed:

&#x20;                       self.hub\_remove(sock)

&#x20;                       try:

&#x20;                           sock.close()

&#x20;                       except OSError:

&#x20;                           pass

&#x20;       return removed



&#x20;   def \_create\_payload(self, type\_, args,

&#x20;                       dumps=\_pickle.dumps, pack=pack,

&#x20;                       protocol=HIGHEST\_PROTOCOL):

&#x20;       body = dumps((type\_, args), protocol=protocol)

&#x20;       size = len(body)

&#x20;       header = pack('>I', size)

&#x20;       return header, body, size



&#x20;   @classmethod

&#x20;   def \_set\_result\_sentinel(cls, \_outqueue, \_pool):

&#x20;       # unused

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   def \_help\_stuff\_finish\_args(self):

&#x20;       # Pool.\_help\_stuff\_finished is a classmethod so we have to use this

&#x20;       # trick to modify the arguments passed to it.

&#x20;       return (self.\_pool,)



&#x20;   @classmethod

&#x20;   def \_help\_stuff\_finish(cls, pool):

&#x20;       # pylint: disable=arguments-differ

&#x20;       debug(

&#x20;           'removing tasks from inqueue until task handler finished',

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       fileno\_to\_proc = {}

&#x20;       inqR = set()

&#x20;       for w in pool:

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               fd = w.inq.\_reader.fileno()

&#x20;               inqR.add(fd)

&#x20;               fileno\_to\_proc\[fd] = w

&#x20;           except OSError:

&#x20;               pass

&#x20;       while inqR:

&#x20;           readable, \_, again = \_select(inqR, timeout=0.5)

&#x20;           if again:

&#x20;               continue

&#x20;           if not readable:

&#x20;               break

&#x20;           for fd in readable:

&#x20;               fileno\_to\_proc\[fd].inq.\_reader.recv()

&#x20;           sleep(0)



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def timers(self):

&#x20;       return {self.maintain\_pool: 5.0}



```

```python

"""Base Execution Pool."""

import logging

import os

import sys

import time

from typing import Any, Dict



from billiard.einfo import ExceptionInfo

from billiard.exceptions import WorkerLostError

from kombu.utils.encoding import safe\_repr



from celery.exceptions import WorkerShutdown, WorkerTerminate, reraise

from celery.utils import timer2

from celery.utils.log import get\_logger

from celery.utils.text import truncate



\_\_all\_\_ = ('BasePool', 'apply\_target')



logger = get\_logger('celery.pool')





def apply\_target(target, args=(), kwargs=None, callback=None,

&#x20;                accept\_callback=None, pid=None, getpid=os.getpid,

&#x20;                propagate=(), monotonic=time.monotonic, \*\*\_):

&#x20;   """Apply function within pool context."""

&#x20;   kwargs = {} if not kwargs else kwargs

&#x20;   if accept\_callback:

&#x20;       accept\_callback(pid or getpid(), monotonic())

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       ret = target(\*args, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;   except propagate:

&#x20;       raise

&#x20;   except Exception:

&#x20;       raise

&#x20;   except (WorkerShutdown, WorkerTerminate):

&#x20;       raise

&#x20;   except BaseException as exc:

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           reraise(WorkerLostError, WorkerLostError(repr(exc)),

&#x20;                   sys.exc\_info()\[2])

&#x20;       except WorkerLostError:

&#x20;           callback(ExceptionInfo())

&#x20;   else:

&#x20;       callback(ret)





class BasePool:

&#x20;   """Task pool."""



&#x20;   RUN = 0x1

&#x20;   CLOSE = 0x2

&#x20;   TERMINATE = 0x3



&#x20;   Timer = timer2.Timer



&#x20;   #: set to true if the pool can be shutdown from within

&#x20;   #: a signal handler.

&#x20;   signal\_safe = True



&#x20;   #: set to true if pool uses greenlets.

&#x20;   is\_green = False



&#x20;   \_state = None

&#x20;   \_pool = None

&#x20;   \_does\_debug = True



&#x20;   #: only used by multiprocessing pool

&#x20;   uses\_semaphore = False



&#x20;   task\_join\_will\_block = True

&#x20;   body\_can\_be\_buffer = False



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, limit=None, putlocks=True, forking\_enable=True,

&#x20;                callbacks\_propagate=(), app=None, \*\*options):

&#x20;       self.limit = limit

&#x20;       self.putlocks = putlocks

&#x20;       self.options = options

&#x20;       self.forking\_enable = forking\_enable

&#x20;       self.callbacks\_propagate = callbacks\_propagate

&#x20;       self.app = app



&#x20;   def on\_start(self):

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   def did\_start\_ok(self):

&#x20;       return True



&#x20;   def flush(self):

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   def on\_stop(self):

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   def register\_with\_event\_loop(self, loop):

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   def on\_apply(self, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   def on\_terminate(self):

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   def on\_soft\_timeout(self, job):

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   def on\_hard\_timeout(self, job):

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   def maintain\_pool(self, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   def terminate\_job(self, pid, signal=None):

&#x20;       raise NotImplementedError(

&#x20;           f'{type(self)} does not implement kill\_job')



&#x20;   def restart(self):

&#x20;       raise NotImplementedError(

&#x20;           f'{type(self)} does not implement restart')



&#x20;   def stop(self):

&#x20;       self.on\_stop()

&#x20;       self.\_state = self.TERMINATE



&#x20;   def terminate(self):

&#x20;       self.\_state = self.TERMINATE

&#x20;       self.on\_terminate()



&#x20;   def start(self):

&#x20;       self.\_does\_debug = logger.isEnabledFor(logging.DEBUG)

&#x20;       self.on\_start()

&#x20;       self.\_state = self.RUN



&#x20;   def close(self):

&#x20;       self.\_state = self.CLOSE

&#x20;       self.on\_close()



&#x20;   def on\_close(self):

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   def apply\_async(self, target, args=None, kwargs=None, \*\*options):

&#x20;       """Equivalent of the :func:`apply` built-in function.



&#x20;       Callbacks should optimally return as soon as possible since

&#x20;       otherwise the thread which handles the result will get blocked.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       kwargs = {} if not kwargs else kwargs

&#x20;       args = \[] if not args else args

&#x20;       if self.\_does\_debug:

&#x20;           logger.debug('TaskPool: Apply %s (args:%s kwargs:%s)',

&#x20;                        target, truncate(safe\_repr(args), 1024),

&#x20;                        truncate(safe\_repr(kwargs), 1024))



&#x20;       return self.on\_apply(target, args, kwargs,

&#x20;                            waitforslot=self.putlocks,

&#x20;                            callbacks\_propagate=self.callbacks\_propagate,

&#x20;                            \*\*options)



&#x20;   def \_get\_info(self) -> Dict\[str, Any]:

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       Return configuration and statistics information. Subclasses should

&#x20;       augment the data as required.



&#x20;       :return: The returned value must be JSON-friendly.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return {

&#x20;           'implementation': self.\_\_class\_\_.\_\_module\_\_ + ':' + self.\_\_class\_\_.\_\_name\_\_,

&#x20;           'max-concurrency': self.limit,

&#x20;       }



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def info(self):

&#x20;       return self.\_get\_info()



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def active(self):

&#x20;       return self.\_state == self.RUN



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def num\_processes(self):

&#x20;       return self.limit



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\concurrency\\eventlet.py

```python

"""Eventlet execution pool."""

import sys

from time import monotonic



from greenlet import GreenletExit

from kombu.asynchronous import timer as \_timer



from celery import signals



from . import base



\_\_all\_\_ = ('TaskPool',)



W\_RACE = """\\

Celery module with %s imported before eventlet patched\\

"""

RACE\_MODS = ('billiard.', 'celery.', 'kombu.')





\#: Warn if we couldn't patch early enough,

\#: and thread/socket depending celery modules have already been loaded.

for mod in (mod for mod in sys.modules if mod.startswith(RACE\_MODS)):

&#x20;   for side in ('thread', 'threading', 'socket'):  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;       if getattr(mod, side, None):

&#x20;           import warnings

&#x20;           warnings.warn(RuntimeWarning(W\_RACE % side))





def apply\_target(target, args=(), kwargs=None, callback=None,

&#x20;                accept\_callback=None, getpid=None):

&#x20;   kwargs = {} if not kwargs else kwargs

&#x20;   return base.apply\_target(target, args, kwargs, callback, accept\_callback,

&#x20;                            pid=getpid())





class Timer(\_timer.Timer):

&#x20;   """Eventlet Timer."""



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       from eventlet.greenthread import spawn\_after

&#x20;       from greenlet import GreenletExit

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(\*args, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;       self.GreenletExit = GreenletExit

&#x20;       self.\_spawn\_after = spawn\_after

&#x20;       self.\_queue = set()



&#x20;   def \_enter(self, eta, priority, entry, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       secs = max(eta - monotonic(), 0)

&#x20;       g = self.\_spawn\_after(secs, entry)

&#x20;       self.\_queue.add(g)

&#x20;       g.link(self.\_entry\_exit, entry)

&#x20;       g.entry = entry

&#x20;       g.eta = eta

&#x20;       g.priority = priority

&#x20;       g.canceled = False

&#x20;       return g



&#x20;   def \_entry\_exit(self, g, entry):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               g.wait()

&#x20;           except self.GreenletExit:

&#x20;               entry.cancel()

&#x20;               g.canceled = True

&#x20;       finally:

&#x20;           self.\_queue.discard(g)



&#x20;   def clear(self):

&#x20;       queue = self.\_queue

&#x20;       while queue:

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               queue.pop().cancel()

&#x20;           except (KeyError, self.GreenletExit):

&#x20;               pass



&#x20;   def cancel(self, tref):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           tref.cancel()

&#x20;       except self.GreenletExit:

&#x20;           pass



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def queue(self):

&#x20;       return self.\_queue





class TaskPool(base.BasePool):

&#x20;   """Eventlet Task Pool."""



&#x20;   Timer = Timer



&#x20;   signal\_safe = False

&#x20;   is\_green = True

&#x20;   task\_join\_will\_block = False

&#x20;   \_pool = None

&#x20;   \_pool\_map = None

&#x20;   \_quick\_put = None



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       from eventlet import greenthread

&#x20;       from eventlet.greenpool import GreenPool

&#x20;       self.Pool = GreenPool

&#x20;       self.getcurrent = greenthread.getcurrent

&#x20;       self.getpid = lambda: id(greenthread.getcurrent())

&#x20;       self.spawn\_n = greenthread.spawn\_n



&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(\*args, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def on\_start(self):

&#x20;       self.\_pool = self.Pool(self.limit)

&#x20;       self.\_pool\_map = {}

&#x20;       signals.eventlet\_pool\_started.send(sender=self)

&#x20;       self.\_quick\_put = self.\_pool.spawn

&#x20;       self.\_quick\_apply\_sig = signals.eventlet\_pool\_apply.send



&#x20;   def on\_stop(self):

&#x20;       signals.eventlet\_pool\_preshutdown.send(sender=self)

&#x20;       if self.\_pool is not None:

&#x20;           self.\_pool.waitall()

&#x20;       signals.eventlet\_pool\_postshutdown.send(sender=self)



&#x20;   def on\_apply(self, target, args=None, kwargs=None, callback=None,

&#x20;                accept\_callback=None, \*\*\_):

&#x20;       target = TaskPool.\_make\_killable\_target(target)

&#x20;       self.\_quick\_apply\_sig(sender=self, target=target, args=args, kwargs=kwargs,)

&#x20;       greenlet = self.\_quick\_put(

&#x20;           apply\_target,

&#x20;           target, args,

&#x20;           kwargs,

&#x20;           callback,

&#x20;           accept\_callback,

&#x20;           self.getpid

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       self.\_add\_to\_pool\_map(id(greenlet), greenlet)



&#x20;   def grow(self, n=1):

&#x20;       limit = self.limit + n

&#x20;       self.\_pool.resize(limit)

&#x20;       self.limit = limit



&#x20;   def shrink(self, n=1):

&#x20;       limit = self.limit - n

&#x20;       self.\_pool.resize(limit)

&#x20;       self.limit = limit



&#x20;   def terminate\_job(self, pid, signal=None):

&#x20;       if pid in self.\_pool\_map.keys():

&#x20;           greenlet = self.\_pool\_map\[pid]

&#x20;           greenlet.kill()

&#x20;           greenlet.wait()



&#x20;   def \_get\_info(self):

&#x20;       info = super().\_get\_info()

&#x20;       info.update({

&#x20;           'max-concurrency': self.limit,

&#x20;           'free-threads': self.\_pool.free(),

&#x20;           'running-threads': self.\_pool.running(),

&#x20;       })

&#x20;       return info



&#x20;   @staticmethod

&#x20;   def \_make\_killable\_target(target):

&#x20;       def killable\_target(\*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               return target(\*args, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;           except GreenletExit:

&#x20;               return (False, None, None)

&#x20;       return killable\_target



&#x20;   def \_add\_to\_pool\_map(self, pid, greenlet):

&#x20;       self.\_pool\_map\[pid] = greenlet

&#x20;       greenlet.link(

&#x20;           TaskPool.\_cleanup\_after\_job\_finish,

&#x20;           self.\_pool\_map,

&#x20;           pid

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   @staticmethod

&#x20;   def \_cleanup\_after\_job\_finish(greenlet, pool\_map, pid):

&#x20;       del pool\_map\[pid]



```
