```python

"""Gevent execution pool."""

import functools

import types

from time import monotonic



from kombu.asynchronous import timer as \_timer



from . import base



try:

&#x20;   from gevent import Timeout

except ImportError:

&#x20;   Timeout = None



\_\_all\_\_ = ('TaskPool',)



\# pylint: disable=redefined-outer-name

\# We cache globals and attribute lookups, so disable this warning.





def apply\_target(target, args=(), kwargs=None, callback=None,

&#x20;                accept\_callback=None, getpid=None, \*\*\_):

&#x20;   kwargs = {} if not kwargs else kwargs

&#x20;   return base.apply\_target(target, args, kwargs, callback, accept\_callback,

&#x20;                            pid=getpid(), \*\*\_)





def apply\_timeout(target, args=(), kwargs=None, callback=None,

&#x20;                 accept\_callback=None, getpid=None, timeout=None,

&#x20;                 timeout\_callback=None, Timeout=Timeout,

&#x20;                 apply\_target=base.apply\_target, \*\*rest):

&#x20;   kwargs = {} if not kwargs else kwargs

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       with Timeout(timeout):

&#x20;           return apply\_target(target, args, kwargs, callback,

&#x20;                               accept\_callback, getpid(),

&#x20;                               propagate=(Timeout,), \*\*rest)

&#x20;   except Timeout:

&#x20;       return timeout\_callback(False, timeout)





class Timer(\_timer.Timer):



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       from gevent import Greenlet, GreenletExit



&#x20;       class \_Greenlet(Greenlet):

&#x20;           cancel = Greenlet.kill



&#x20;       self.\_Greenlet = \_Greenlet

&#x20;       self.\_GreenletExit = GreenletExit

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(\*args, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;       self.\_queue = set()



&#x20;   def \_enter(self, eta, priority, entry, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       secs = max(eta - monotonic(), 0)

&#x20;       g = self.\_Greenlet.spawn\_later(secs, entry)

&#x20;       self.\_queue.add(g)

&#x20;       g.link(self.\_entry\_exit)

&#x20;       g.entry = entry

&#x20;       g.eta = eta

&#x20;       g.priority = priority

&#x20;       g.canceled = False

&#x20;       return g



&#x20;   def \_entry\_exit(self, g):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           g.kill()

&#x20;       finally:

&#x20;           self.\_queue.discard(g)



&#x20;   def clear(self):

&#x20;       queue = self.\_queue

&#x20;       while queue:

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               queue.pop().kill()

&#x20;           except KeyError:

&#x20;               pass



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def queue(self):

&#x20;       return self.\_queue





class TaskPool(base.BasePool):

&#x20;   """GEvent Pool."""



&#x20;   Timer = Timer



&#x20;   signal\_safe = False

&#x20;   is\_green = True

&#x20;   task\_join\_will\_block = False

&#x20;   \_pool = None

&#x20;   \_pool\_map = None

&#x20;   \_quick\_put = None



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       from gevent import getcurrent, spawn\_raw

&#x20;       from gevent.pool import Pool

&#x20;       self.Pool = Pool

&#x20;       self.getcurrent = getcurrent

&#x20;       self.getpid = lambda: id(getcurrent())

&#x20;       self.spawn\_n = spawn\_raw

&#x20;       self.timeout = kwargs.get('timeout')

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(\*args, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def on\_start(self):

&#x20;       self.\_pool = self.Pool(self.limit)

&#x20;       self.\_pool\_map = {}

&#x20;       self.\_quick\_put = self.\_pool.spawn



&#x20;   def on\_stop(self):

&#x20;       if self.\_pool is not None:

&#x20;           self.\_pool.join()



&#x20;   def on\_apply(self, target, args=None, kwargs=None, callback=None,

&#x20;                accept\_callback=None, timeout=None,

&#x20;                timeout\_callback=None, apply\_target=apply\_target, \*\*\_):

&#x20;       timeout = self.timeout if timeout is None else timeout

&#x20;       target = self.\_make\_killable\_target(target)

&#x20;       greenlet = self.\_quick\_put(apply\_timeout if timeout else apply\_target,

&#x20;                                  target, args, kwargs, callback, accept\_callback,

&#x20;                                  self.getpid, timeout=timeout, timeout\_callback=timeout\_callback)

&#x20;       self.\_add\_to\_pool\_map(id(greenlet), greenlet)

&#x20;       greenlet.terminate = types.MethodType(\_terminate, greenlet)

&#x20;       return greenlet



&#x20;   def grow(self, n=1):

&#x20;       self.\_pool.\_semaphore.counter += n

&#x20;       self.\_pool.size += n



&#x20;   def shrink(self, n=1):

&#x20;       self.\_pool.\_semaphore.counter -= n

&#x20;       self.\_pool.size -= n



&#x20;   def terminate\_job(self, pid, signal=None):

&#x20;       import gevent



&#x20;       if pid in self.\_pool\_map:

&#x20;           greenlet = self.\_pool\_map\[pid]

&#x20;           gevent.kill(greenlet)



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def num\_processes(self):

&#x20;       return len(self.\_pool)



&#x20;   @staticmethod

&#x20;   def \_make\_killable\_target(target):

&#x20;       def killable\_target(\*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;           from greenlet import GreenletExit

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               return target(\*args, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;           except GreenletExit:

&#x20;               return (False, None, None)



&#x20;       return killable\_target



&#x20;   def \_add\_to\_pool\_map(self, pid, greenlet):

&#x20;       self.\_pool\_map\[pid] = greenlet

&#x20;       greenlet.link(

&#x20;           functools.partial(self.\_cleanup\_after\_job\_finish, pid=pid, pool\_map=self.\_pool\_map),

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   @staticmethod

&#x20;   def \_cleanup\_after\_job\_finish(greenlet, pool\_map, pid):

&#x20;       del pool\_map\[pid]





def \_terminate(self, signal):

&#x20;   # Done in `TaskPool.terminate\_job`

&#x20;   pass



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\concurrency\\prefork.py

```python

"""Prefork execution pool.



Pool implementation using :mod:`multiprocessing`.

"""

import os



from billiard import forking\_enable

from billiard.common import REMAP\_SIGTERM, TERM\_SIGNAME

from billiard.pool import CLOSE, RUN

from billiard.pool import Pool as BlockingPool



from celery import platforms, signals

from celery.\_state import \_set\_task\_join\_will\_block, set\_default\_app

from celery.app import trace

from celery.concurrency.base import BasePool

from celery.utils.functional import noop

from celery.utils.log import get\_logger



from .asynpool import AsynPool



\_\_all\_\_ = ('TaskPool', 'process\_initializer', 'process\_destructor')



\#: List of signals to reset when a child process starts.

WORKER\_SIGRESET = {

&#x20;   'SIGTERM', 'SIGHUP', 'SIGTTIN', 'SIGTTOU', 'SIGUSR1',

}



\#: List of signals to ignore when a child process starts.

if REMAP\_SIGTERM:

&#x20;   WORKER\_SIGIGNORE = {'SIGINT', TERM\_SIGNAME}

else:

&#x20;   WORKER\_SIGIGNORE = {'SIGINT'}



logger = get\_logger(\_\_name\_\_)

warning, debug = logger.warning, logger.debug





def process\_initializer(app, hostname):

&#x20;   """Pool child process initializer.



&#x20;   Initialize the child pool process to ensure the correct

&#x20;   app instance is used and things like logging works.

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   # Each running worker gets SIGKILL by OS when main process exits.

&#x20;   platforms.set\_pdeathsig('SIGKILL')

&#x20;   \_set\_task\_join\_will\_block(True)

&#x20;   platforms.signals.reset(\*WORKER\_SIGRESET)

&#x20;   platforms.signals.ignore(\*WORKER\_SIGIGNORE)

&#x20;   platforms.set\_mp\_process\_title('celeryd', hostname=hostname)

&#x20;   # This is for Windows and other platforms not supporting

&#x20;   # fork().  Note that init\_worker makes sure it's only

&#x20;   # run once per process.

&#x20;   app.loader.init\_worker()

&#x20;   app.loader.init\_worker\_process()

&#x20;   logfile = os.environ.get('CELERY\_LOG\_FILE') or None

&#x20;   if logfile and '%i' in logfile.lower():

&#x20;       # logfile path will differ so need to set up logging again.

&#x20;       app.log.already\_setup = False

&#x20;   app.log.setup(int(os.environ.get('CELERY\_LOG\_LEVEL', 0) or 0),

&#x20;                 logfile,

&#x20;                 bool(os.environ.get('CELERY\_LOG\_REDIRECT', False)),

&#x20;                 str(os.environ.get('CELERY\_LOG\_REDIRECT\_LEVEL')),

&#x20;                 hostname=hostname)

&#x20;   if os.environ.get('FORKED\_BY\_MULTIPROCESSING'):

&#x20;       # pool did execv after fork

&#x20;       trace.setup\_worker\_optimizations(app, hostname)

&#x20;   else:

&#x20;       app.set\_current()

&#x20;       set\_default\_app(app)

&#x20;       app.finalize()

&#x20;       trace.\_tasks = app.\_tasks  # enables fast\_trace\_task optimization.

&#x20;   # rebuild execution handler for all tasks.

&#x20;   from celery.app.trace import build\_tracer

&#x20;   for name, task in app.tasks.items():

&#x20;       task.\_\_trace\_\_ = build\_tracer(name, task, app.loader, hostname,

&#x20;                                     app=app)

&#x20;   from celery.worker import state as worker\_state

&#x20;   worker\_state.reset\_state()

&#x20;   signals.worker\_process\_init.send(sender=None)





def process\_destructor(pid, exitcode):

&#x20;   """Pool child process destructor.



&#x20;   Dispatch the :signal:`worker\_process\_shutdown` signal.

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   signals.worker\_process\_shutdown.send(

&#x20;       sender=None, pid=pid, exitcode=exitcode,

&#x20;   )





class TaskPool(BasePool):

&#x20;   """Multiprocessing Pool implementation."""



&#x20;   Pool = AsynPool

&#x20;   BlockingPool = BlockingPool



&#x20;   uses\_semaphore = True

&#x20;   write\_stats = None



&#x20;   def on\_start(self):

&#x20;       forking\_enable(self.forking\_enable)

&#x20;       Pool = (self.BlockingPool if self.options.get('threads', True)

&#x20;               else self.Pool)

&#x20;       proc\_alive\_timeout = (

&#x20;           self.app.conf.worker\_proc\_alive\_timeout if self.app

&#x20;           else None

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       P = self.\_pool = Pool(processes=self.limit,

&#x20;                             initializer=process\_initializer,

&#x20;                             on\_process\_exit=process\_destructor,

&#x20;                             enable\_timeouts=True,

&#x20;                             synack=False,

&#x20;                             proc\_alive\_timeout=proc\_alive\_timeout,

&#x20;                             \*\*self.options)



&#x20;       # Create proxy methods

&#x20;       self.on\_apply = P.apply\_async

&#x20;       self.maintain\_pool = P.maintain\_pool

&#x20;       self.terminate\_job = P.terminate\_job

&#x20;       self.grow = P.grow

&#x20;       self.shrink = P.shrink

&#x20;       self.flush = getattr(P, 'flush', None)  # FIXME add to billiard



&#x20;   def restart(self):

&#x20;       self.\_pool.restart()

&#x20;       self.\_pool.apply\_async(noop)



&#x20;   def did\_start\_ok(self):

&#x20;       return self.\_pool.did\_start\_ok()



&#x20;   def register\_with\_event\_loop(self, loop):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           reg = self.\_pool.register\_with\_event\_loop

&#x20;       except AttributeError:

&#x20;           return

&#x20;       return reg(loop)



&#x20;   def on\_stop(self):

&#x20;       """Gracefully stop the pool."""

&#x20;       if self.\_pool is not None and self.\_pool.\_state in (RUN, CLOSE):

&#x20;           self.\_pool.close()

&#x20;           self.\_pool.join()

&#x20;           self.\_pool = None



&#x20;   def on\_terminate(self):

&#x20;       """Force terminate the pool."""

&#x20;       if self.\_pool is not None:

&#x20;           self.\_pool.terminate()

&#x20;           self.\_pool = None



&#x20;   def on\_close(self):

&#x20;       if self.\_pool is not None and self.\_pool.\_state == RUN:

&#x20;           self.\_pool.close()



&#x20;   def \_get\_info(self):

&#x20;       write\_stats = getattr(self.\_pool, 'human\_write\_stats', None)

&#x20;       info = super().\_get\_info()

&#x20;       info.update({

&#x20;           'max-concurrency': self.limit,

&#x20;           'processes': \[p.pid for p in self.\_pool.\_pool],

&#x20;           'max-tasks-per-child': self.\_pool.\_maxtasksperchild or 'N/A',

&#x20;           'put-guarded-by-semaphore': self.putlocks,

&#x20;           'timeouts': (self.\_pool.soft\_timeout or 0,

&#x20;                        self.\_pool.timeout or 0),

&#x20;           'writes': write\_stats() if write\_stats is not None else 'N/A',

&#x20;       })

&#x20;       return info



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def num\_processes(self):

&#x20;       return self.\_pool.\_processes



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\concurrency\\solo.py

```python

"""Single-threaded execution pool."""

import os



from celery import signals



from .base import BasePool, apply\_target



\_\_all\_\_ = ('TaskPool',)





class TaskPool(BasePool):

&#x20;   """Solo task pool (blocking, inline, fast)."""



&#x20;   body\_can\_be\_buffer = True



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(\*args, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;       self.on\_apply = apply\_target

&#x20;       self.limit = 1

&#x20;       signals.worker\_process\_init.send(sender=None)



&#x20;   def \_get\_info(self):

&#x20;       info = super().\_get\_info()

&#x20;       info.update({

&#x20;           'max-concurrency': 1,

&#x20;           'processes': \[os.getpid()],

&#x20;           'max-tasks-per-child': None,

&#x20;           'put-guarded-by-semaphore': True,

&#x20;           'timeouts': (),

&#x20;       })

&#x20;       return info



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\concurrency\\thread.py

```python

"""Thread execution pool."""

from \_\_future\_\_ import annotations



from concurrent.futures import Future, ThreadPoolExecutor, wait

from typing import TYPE\_CHECKING, Any, Callable



from .base import BasePool, apply\_target



\_\_all\_\_ = ('TaskPool',)



if TYPE\_CHECKING:

&#x20;   from typing import TypedDict



&#x20;   PoolInfo = TypedDict('PoolInfo', {'max-concurrency': int, 'threads': int})



&#x20;   # `TargetFunction` should be a Protocol that represents fast\_trace\_task and

&#x20;   # trace\_task\_ret.

&#x20;   TargetFunction = Callable\[..., Any]





class ApplyResult:

&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, future: Future) -> None:

&#x20;       self.f = future

&#x20;       self.get = self.f.result



&#x20;   def wait(self, timeout: float | None = None) -> None:

&#x20;       wait(\[self.f], timeout)





class TaskPool(BasePool):

&#x20;   """Thread Task Pool."""

&#x20;   limit: int



&#x20;   body\_can\_be\_buffer = True

&#x20;   signal\_safe = False



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, \*args: Any, \*\*kwargs: Any) -> None:

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(\*args, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;       self.executor = ThreadPoolExecutor(max\_workers=self.limit)



&#x20;   def on\_stop(self) -> None:

&#x20;       self.executor.shutdown()

&#x20;       super().on\_stop()



&#x20;   def on\_apply(

&#x20;       self,

&#x20;       target: TargetFunction,

&#x20;       args: tuple\[Any, ...] | None = None,

&#x20;       kwargs: dict\[str, Any] | None = None,

&#x20;       callback: Callable\[..., Any] | None = None,

&#x20;       accept\_callback: Callable\[..., Any] | None = None,

&#x20;       \*\*\_: Any

&#x20;   ) -> ApplyResult:

&#x20;       f = self.executor.submit(apply\_target, target, args, kwargs,

&#x20;                                callback, accept\_callback)

&#x20;       return ApplyResult(f)



&#x20;   def \_get\_info(self) -> PoolInfo:

&#x20;       info = super().\_get\_info()

&#x20;       info.update({

&#x20;           'max-concurrency': self.limit,

&#x20;           'threads': len(self.executor.\_threads)

&#x20;       })

&#x20;       return info



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\concurrency\\\_\_init\_\_.py

```python

"""Pool implementation abstract factory, and alias definitions."""

import os



\# Import from kombu directly as it's used

\# early in the import stage, where celery.utils loads

\# too much (e.g., for eventlet patching)

from kombu.utils.imports import symbol\_by\_name



\_\_all\_\_ = ('get\_implementation', 'get\_available\_pool\_names',)



ALIASES = {

&#x20;   'prefork': 'celery.concurrency.prefork:TaskPool',

&#x20;   'eventlet': 'celery.concurrency.eventlet:TaskPool',

&#x20;   'gevent': 'celery.concurrency.gevent:TaskPool',

&#x20;   'solo': 'celery.concurrency.solo:TaskPool',

&#x20;   'processes': 'celery.concurrency.prefork:TaskPool',  # XXX compat alias

}



try:

&#x20;   import concurrent.futures  # noqa

except ImportError:

&#x20;   pass

else:

&#x20;   ALIASES\['threads'] = 'celery.concurrency.thread:TaskPool'

\#

\# Allow for an out-of-tree worker pool implementation. This is used as follows:

\#

\#   - Set the environment variable CELERY\_CUSTOM\_WORKER\_POOL to the name of

\#     an implementation of :class:`celery.concurrency.base.BasePool` in the

\#     standard Celery format of "package:class".

\#   - Select this pool using '--pool custom'.

\#

try:

&#x20;   custom = os.environ.get('CELERY\_CUSTOM\_WORKER\_POOL')

except KeyError:

&#x20;   pass

else:

&#x20;   ALIASES\['custom'] = custom





def get\_implementation(cls):

&#x20;   """Return pool implementation by name."""

&#x20;   return symbol\_by\_name(cls, ALIASES)





def get\_available\_pool\_names():

&#x20;   """Return all available pool type names."""

&#x20;   return tuple(ALIASES.keys())



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\contrib\\abortable.py

```python

"""Abortable Tasks.



Abortable tasks overview

=========================



For long-running :class:`Task`'s, it can be desirable to support

aborting during execution.  Of course, these tasks should be built to

support abortion specifically.



The :class:`AbortableTask` serves as a base class for all :class:`Task`

objects that should support abortion by producers.



\* Producers may invoke the :meth:`abort` method on

&#x20; :class:`AbortableAsyncResult` instances, to request abortion.



\* Consumers (workers) should periodically check (and honor!) the

&#x20; :meth:`is\_aborted` method at controlled points in their task's

&#x20; :meth:`run` method.  The more often, the better.



The necessary intermediate communication is dealt with by the

:class:`AbortableTask` implementation.



Usage example

\-------------



In the consumer:



.. code-block:: python



&#x20;   from celery.contrib.abortable import AbortableTask

&#x20;   from celery.utils.log import get\_task\_logger



&#x20;   from proj.celery import app



&#x20;   logger = get\_logger(\_\_name\_\_)



&#x20;   @app.task(bind=True, base=AbortableTask)

&#x20;   def long\_running\_task(self):

&#x20;       results = \[]

&#x20;       for i in range(100):

&#x20;           # check after every 5 iterations...

&#x20;           # (or alternatively, check when some timer is due)

&#x20;           if not i % 5:

&#x20;               if self.is\_aborted():

&#x20;                   # respect aborted state, and terminate gracefully.

&#x20;                   logger.warning('Task aborted')

&#x20;                   return

&#x20;               value = do\_something\_expensive(i)

&#x20;               results.append(y)

&#x20;       logger.info('Task complete')

&#x20;       return results



In the producer:



.. code-block:: python



&#x20;   import time



&#x20;   from proj.tasks import MyLongRunningTask



&#x20;   def myview(request):

&#x20;       # result is of type AbortableAsyncResult

&#x20;       result = long\_running\_task.delay()



&#x20;       # abort the task after 10 seconds

&#x20;       time.sleep(10)

&#x20;       result.abort()



After the `result.abort()` call, the task execution isn't

aborted immediately.  In fact, it's not guaranteed to abort at all.

Keep checking `result.state` status, or call `result.get(timeout=)` to

have it block until the task is finished.



.. note::



&#x20;  In order to abort tasks, there needs to be communication between the

&#x20;  producer and the consumer.  This is currently implemented through the

&#x20;  database backend.  Therefore, this class will only work with the

&#x20;  database backends.

"""

from celery import Task

from celery.result import AsyncResult



\_\_all\_\_ = ('AbortableAsyncResult', 'AbortableTask')





"""

Task States

\-----------



.. state:: ABORTED



ABORTED

\~\~\~\~\~\~\~



Task is aborted (typically by the producer) and should be

aborted as soon as possible.



"""

ABORTED = 'ABORTED'





class AbortableAsyncResult(AsyncResult):

&#x20;   """Represents an abortable result.



&#x20;   Specifically, this gives the `AsyncResult` a :meth:`abort()` method,

&#x20;   that sets the state of the underlying Task to `'ABORTED'`.

&#x20;   """



&#x20;   def is\_aborted(self):

&#x20;       """Return :const:`True` if the task is (being) aborted."""

&#x20;       return self.state == ABORTED



&#x20;   def abort(self):

&#x20;       """Set the state of the task to :const:`ABORTED`.



&#x20;       Abortable tasks monitor their state at regular intervals and

&#x20;       terminate execution if so.



&#x20;       Warning:

&#x20;           Be aware that invoking this method does not guarantee when the

&#x20;           task will be aborted (or even if the task will be aborted at all).

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       # TODO: store\_result requires all four arguments to be set,

&#x20;       # but only state should be updated here

&#x20;       return self.backend.store\_result(self.id, result=None,

&#x20;                                        state=ABORTED, traceback=None)





class AbortableTask(Task):

&#x20;   """Task that can be aborted.



&#x20;   This serves as a base class for all :class:`Task`'s

&#x20;   that support aborting during execution.



&#x20;   All subclasses of :class:`AbortableTask` must call the

&#x20;   :meth:`is\_aborted` method periodically and act accordingly when

&#x20;   the call evaluates to :const:`True`.

&#x20;   """



&#x20;   abstract = True



&#x20;   def AsyncResult(self, task\_id):

&#x20;       """Return the accompanying AbortableAsyncResult instance."""

&#x20;       return AbortableAsyncResult(task\_id, backend=self.backend)



&#x20;   def is\_aborted(self, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       """Return true if task is aborted.



&#x20;       Checks against the backend whether this

&#x20;       :class:`AbortableAsyncResult` is :const:`ABORTED`.



&#x20;       Always return :const:`False` in case the `task\_id` parameter

&#x20;       refers to a regular (non-abortable) :class:`Task`.



&#x20;       Be aware that invoking this method will cause a hit in the

&#x20;       backend (for example a database query), so find a good balance

&#x20;       between calling it regularly (for responsiveness), but not too

&#x20;       often (for performance).

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       task\_id = kwargs.get('task\_id', self.request.id)

&#x20;       result = self.AsyncResult(task\_id)

&#x20;       if not isinstance(result, AbortableAsyncResult):

&#x20;           return False

&#x20;       return result.is\_aborted()



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\contrib\\migrate.py

```python

"""Message migration tools (Broker <-> Broker)."""

import socket

from functools import partial

from itertools import cycle, islice



from kombu import Queue, eventloop

from kombu.common import maybe\_declare

from kombu.utils.encoding import ensure\_bytes



from celery.app import app\_or\_default

from celery.utils.nodenames import worker\_direct

from celery.utils.text import str\_to\_list



\_\_all\_\_ = (

&#x20;   'StopFiltering', 'State', 'republish', 'migrate\_task',

&#x20;   'migrate\_tasks', 'move', 'task\_id\_eq', 'task\_id\_in',

&#x20;   'start\_filter', 'move\_task\_by\_id', 'move\_by\_idmap',

&#x20;   'move\_by\_taskmap', 'move\_direct', 'move\_direct\_by\_id',

)



MOVING\_PROGRESS\_FMT = """\\

Moving task {state.filtered}/{state.strtotal}: \\

{body\[task]}\[{body\[id]}]\\

"""





class StopFiltering(Exception):

&#x20;   """Semi-predicate used to signal filter stop."""





class State:

&#x20;   """Migration progress state."""



&#x20;   count = 0

&#x20;   filtered = 0

&#x20;   total\_apx = 0



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def strtotal(self):

&#x20;       if not self.total\_apx:

&#x20;           return '?'

&#x20;       return str(self.total\_apx)



&#x20;   def \_\_repr\_\_(self):

&#x20;       if self.filtered:

&#x20;           return f'^{self.filtered}'

&#x20;       return f'{self.count}/{self.strtotal}'





def republish(producer, message, exchange=None, routing\_key=None,

&#x20;             remove\_props=None):

&#x20;   """Republish message."""

&#x20;   if not remove\_props:

&#x20;       remove\_props = \['application\_headers', 'content\_type',

&#x20;                       'content\_encoding', 'headers']

&#x20;   body = ensure\_bytes(message.body)  # use raw message body.

&#x20;   info, headers, props = (message.delivery\_info,

&#x20;                           message.headers, message.properties)

&#x20;   exchange = info\['exchange'] if exchange is None else exchange

&#x20;   routing\_key = info\['routing\_key'] if routing\_key is None else routing\_key

&#x20;   ctype, enc = message.content\_type, message.content\_encoding

&#x20;   # remove compression header, as this will be inserted again

&#x20;   # when the message is recompressed.

&#x20;   compression = headers.pop('compression', None)



&#x20;   expiration = props.pop('expiration', None)

&#x20;   # ensure expiration is a float

&#x20;   expiration = float(expiration) if expiration is not None else None



&#x20;   for key in remove\_props:

&#x20;       props.pop(key, None)



&#x20;   producer.publish(ensure\_bytes(body), exchange=exchange,

&#x20;                    routing\_key=routing\_key, compression=compression,

&#x20;                    headers=headers, content\_type=ctype,

&#x20;                    content\_encoding=enc, expiration=expiration,

&#x20;                    \*\*props)





def migrate\_task(producer, body\_, message, queues=None):

&#x20;   """Migrate single task message."""

&#x20;   info = message.delivery\_info

&#x20;   queues = {} if queues is None else queues

&#x20;   republish(producer, message,

&#x20;             exchange=queues.get(info\['exchange']),

&#x20;             routing\_key=queues.get(info\['routing\_key']))





def filter\_callback(callback, tasks):



&#x20;   def filtered(body, message):

&#x20;       if tasks and body\['task'] not in tasks:

&#x20;           return



&#x20;       return callback(body, message)

&#x20;   return filtered





def migrate\_tasks(source, dest, migrate=migrate\_task, app=None,

&#x20;                 queues=None, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;   """Migrate tasks from one broker to another."""

&#x20;   app = app\_or\_default(app)

&#x20;   queues = prepare\_queues(queues)

&#x20;   producer = app.amqp.Producer(dest, auto\_declare=False)

&#x20;   migrate = partial(migrate, producer, queues=queues)



&#x20;   def on\_declare\_queue(queue):

&#x20;       new\_queue = queue(producer.channel)

&#x20;       new\_queue.name = queues.get(queue.name, queue.name)

&#x20;       if new\_queue.routing\_key == queue.name:

&#x20;           new\_queue.routing\_key = queues.get(queue.name,

&#x20;                                              new\_queue.routing\_key)

&#x20;       if new\_queue.exchange.name == queue.name:

&#x20;           new\_queue.exchange.name = queues.get(queue.name, queue.name)

&#x20;       new\_queue.declare()



&#x20;   return start\_filter(app, source, migrate, queues=queues,

&#x20;                       on\_declare\_queue=on\_declare\_queue, \*\*kwargs)





def \_maybe\_queue(app, q):

&#x20;   if isinstance(q, str):

&#x20;       return app.amqp.queues\[q]

&#x20;   return q





def move(predicate, connection=None, exchange=None, routing\_key=None,

&#x20;        source=None, app=None, callback=None, limit=None, transform=None,

&#x20;        \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;   """Find tasks by filtering them and move the tasks to a new queue.



&#x20;   Arguments:

&#x20;       predicate (Callable): Filter function used to decide the messages

&#x20;           to move.  Must accept the standard signature of ``(body, message)``

&#x20;           used by Kombu consumer callbacks.  If the predicate wants the

&#x20;           message to be moved it must return either:



&#x20;               1) a tuple of ``(exchange, routing\_key)``, or



&#x20;               2) a :class:`\~kombu.entity.Queue` instance, or



&#x20;               3) any other true value means the specified

&#x20;                   ``exchange`` and ``routing\_key`` arguments will be used.

&#x20;       connection (kombu.Connection): Custom connection to use.

&#x20;       source: List\[Union\[str, kombu.Queue]]: Optional list of source

&#x20;           queues to use instead of the default (queues

&#x20;           in :setting:`task\_queues`).  This list can also contain

&#x20;           :class:`\~kombu.entity.Queue` instances.

&#x20;       exchange (str, kombu.Exchange): Default destination exchange.

&#x20;       routing\_key (str): Default destination routing key.

&#x20;       limit (int): Limit number of messages to filter.

&#x20;       callback (Callable): Callback called after message moved,

&#x20;           with signature ``(state, body, message)``.

&#x20;       transform (Callable): Optional function to transform the return

&#x20;           value (destination) of the filter function.



&#x20;   Also supports the same keyword arguments as :func:`start\_filter`.



&#x20;   To demonstrate, the :func:`move\_task\_by\_id` operation can be implemented

&#x20;   like this:



&#x20;   .. code-block:: python



&#x20;       def is\_wanted\_task(body, message):

&#x20;           if body\['id'] == wanted\_id:

&#x20;               return Queue('foo', exchange=Exchange('foo'),

&#x20;                            routing\_key='foo')



&#x20;       move(is\_wanted\_task)



&#x20;   or with a transform:



&#x20;   .. code-block:: python



&#x20;       def transform(value):

&#x20;           if isinstance(value, str):

&#x20;               return Queue(value, Exchange(value), value)

&#x20;           return value



&#x20;       move(is\_wanted\_task, transform=transform)



&#x20;   Note:

&#x20;       The predicate may also return a tuple of ``(exchange, routing\_key)``

&#x20;       to specify the destination to where the task should be moved,

&#x20;       or a :class:`\~kombu.entity.Queue` instance.

&#x20;       Any other true value means that the task will be moved to the

&#x20;       default exchange/routing\_key.

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   app = app\_or\_default(app)

&#x20;   queues = \[\_maybe\_queue(app, queue) for queue in source or \[]] or None

&#x20;   with app.connection\_or\_acquire(connection, pool=False) as conn:

&#x20;       producer = app.amqp.Producer(conn)

&#x20;       state = State()



&#x20;       def on\_task(body, message):

&#x20;           ret = predicate(body, message)

&#x20;           if ret:

&#x20;               if transform:

&#x20;                   ret = transform(ret)

&#x20;               if isinstance(ret, Queue):

&#x20;                   maybe\_declare(ret, conn.default\_channel)

&#x20;                   ex, rk = ret.exchange.name, ret.routing\_key

&#x20;               else:

&#x20;                   ex, rk = expand\_dest(ret, exchange, routing\_key)

&#x20;               republish(producer, message,

&#x20;                         exchange=ex, routing\_key=rk)

&#x20;               message.ack()



&#x20;               state.filtered += 1

&#x20;               if callback:

&#x20;                   callback(state, body, message)

&#x20;               if limit and state.filtered >= limit:

&#x20;                   raise StopFiltering()



&#x20;       return start\_filter(app, conn, on\_task, consume\_from=queues, \*\*kwargs)





def expand\_dest(ret, exchange, routing\_key):

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       ex, rk = ret

&#x20;   except (TypeError, ValueError):

&#x20;       ex, rk = exchange, routing\_key

&#x20;   return ex, rk





def task\_id\_eq(task\_id, body, message):

&#x20;   """Return true if task id equals task\_id'."""

&#x20;   return body\['id'] == task\_id





def task\_id\_in(ids, body, message):

&#x20;   """Return true if task id is member of set ids'."""

&#x20;   return body\['id'] in ids





def prepare\_queues(queues):

&#x20;   if isinstance(queues, str):

&#x20;       queues = queues.split(',')

&#x20;   if isinstance(queues, list):

&#x20;       queues = dict(tuple(islice(cycle(q.split(':')), None, 2))

&#x20;                     for q in queues)

&#x20;   if queues is None:

&#x20;       queues = {}

&#x20;   return queues





class Filterer:



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, app, conn, filter,

&#x20;                limit=None, timeout=1.0,

&#x20;                ack\_messages=False, tasks=None, queues=None,

&#x20;                callback=None, forever=False, on\_declare\_queue=None,

&#x20;                consume\_from=None, state=None, accept=None, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       self.app = app

&#x20;       self.conn = conn

&#x20;       self.filter = filter

&#x20;       self.limit = limit

&#x20;       self.timeout = timeout

&#x20;       self.ack\_messages = ack\_messages

&#x20;       self.tasks = set(str\_to\_list(tasks) or \[])

&#x20;       self.queues = prepare\_queues(queues)

&#x20;       self.callback = callback

&#x20;       self.forever = forever

&#x20;       self.on\_declare\_queue = on\_declare\_queue

&#x20;       self.consume\_from = \[

&#x20;           \_maybe\_queue(self.app, q)

&#x20;           for q in consume\_from or list(self.queues)

&#x20;       ]

&#x20;       self.state = state or State()

&#x20;       self.accept = accept



&#x20;   def start(self):

&#x20;       # start migrating messages.

&#x20;       with self.prepare\_consumer(self.create\_consumer()):

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               for \_ in eventloop(self.conn,  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;                                  timeout=self.timeout,

&#x20;                                  ignore\_timeouts=self.forever):

&#x20;                   pass

&#x20;           except socket.timeout:

&#x20;               pass

&#x20;           except StopFiltering:

&#x20;               pass

&#x20;       return self.state



&#x20;   def update\_state(self, body, message):

&#x20;       self.state.count += 1

&#x20;       if self.limit and self.state.count >= self.limit:

&#x20;           raise StopFiltering()



&#x20;   def ack\_message(self, body, message):

&#x20;       message.ack()



&#x20;   def create\_consumer(self):

&#x20;       return self.app.amqp.TaskConsumer(

&#x20;           self.conn,

&#x20;           queues=self.consume\_from,

&#x20;           accept=self.accept,

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def prepare\_consumer(self, consumer):

&#x20;       filter = self.filter

&#x20;       update\_state = self.update\_state

&#x20;       ack\_message = self.ack\_message

&#x20;       if self.tasks:

&#x20;           filter = filter\_callback(filter, self.tasks)

&#x20;           update\_state = filter\_callback(update\_state, self.tasks)

&#x20;           ack\_message = filter\_callback(ack\_message, self.tasks)

&#x20;       consumer.register\_callback(filter)

&#x20;       consumer.register\_callback(update\_state)

&#x20;       if self.ack\_messages:

&#x20;           consumer.register\_callback(self.ack\_message)

&#x20;       if self.callback is not None:

&#x20;           callback = partial(self.callback, self.state)

&#x20;           if self.tasks:

&#x20;               callback = filter\_callback(callback, self.tasks)

&#x20;           consumer.register\_callback(callback)

&#x20;       self.declare\_queues(consumer)

&#x20;       return consumer



&#x20;   def declare\_queues(self, consumer):

&#x20;       # declare all queues on the new broker.

&#x20;       for queue in consumer.queues:

&#x20;           if self.queues and queue.name not in self.queues:

&#x20;               continue

&#x20;           if self.on\_declare\_queue is not None:

&#x20;               self.on\_declare\_queue(queue)

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               \_, mcount, \_ = queue(

&#x20;                   consumer.channel).queue\_declare(passive=True)

&#x20;               if mcount:

&#x20;                   self.state.total\_apx += mcount

&#x20;           except self.conn.channel\_errors:

&#x20;               pass





def start\_filter(app, conn, filter, limit=None, timeout=1.0,

&#x20;                ack\_messages=False, tasks=None, queues=None,

&#x20;                callback=None, forever=False, on\_declare\_queue=None,

&#x20;                consume\_from=None, state=None, accept=None, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;   """Filter tasks."""

&#x20;   return Filterer(

&#x20;       app, conn, filter,

&#x20;       limit=limit,

&#x20;       timeout=timeout,

&#x20;       ack\_messages=ack\_messages,

&#x20;       tasks=tasks,

&#x20;       queues=queues,

&#x20;       callback=callback,

&#x20;       forever=forever,

&#x20;       on\_declare\_queue=on\_declare\_queue,

&#x20;       consume\_from=consume\_from,

&#x20;       state=state,

&#x20;       accept=accept,

&#x20;       \*\*kwargs).start()





def move\_task\_by\_id(task\_id, dest, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;   """Find a task by id and move it to another queue.



&#x20;   Arguments:

&#x20;       task\_id (str): Id of task to find and move.

&#x20;       dest: (str, kombu.Queue): Destination queue.

&#x20;       transform (Callable): Optional function to transform the return

&#x20;           value (destination) of the filter function.

&#x20;       \*\*kwargs (Any): Also supports the same keyword

&#x20;           arguments as :func:`move`.

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   return move\_by\_idmap({task\_id: dest}, \*\*kwargs)





def move\_by\_idmap(map, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;   """Move tasks by matching from a ``task\_id: queue`` mapping.



&#x20;   Where ``queue`` is a queue to move the task to.



&#x20;   Example:

&#x20;       >>> move\_by\_idmap({

&#x20;       ...     '5bee6e82-f4ac-468e-bd3d-13e8600250bc': Queue('name'),

&#x20;       ...     'ada8652d-aef3-466b-abd2-becdaf1b82b3': Queue('name'),

&#x20;       ...     '3a2b140d-7db1-41ba-ac90-c36a0ef4ab1f': Queue('name')},

&#x20;       ...   queues=\['hipri'])

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   def task\_id\_in\_map(body, message):

&#x20;       return map.get(message.properties\['correlation\_id'])



&#x20;   # adding the limit means that we don't have to consume any more

&#x20;   # when we've found everything.

&#x20;   return move(task\_id\_in\_map, limit=len(map), \*\*kwargs)





def move\_by\_taskmap(map, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;   """Move tasks by matching from a ``task\_name: queue`` mapping.



&#x20;   ``queue`` is the queue to move the task to.



&#x20;   Example:

&#x20;       >>> move\_by\_taskmap({

&#x20;       ...     'tasks.add': Queue('name'),

&#x20;       ...     'tasks.mul': Queue('name'),

&#x20;       ... })

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   def task\_name\_in\_map(body, message):

&#x20;       return map.get(body\['task'])  # <- name of task



&#x20;   return move(task\_name\_in\_map, \*\*kwargs)





def filter\_status(state, body, message, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;   print(MOVING\_PROGRESS\_FMT.format(state=state, body=body, \*\*kwargs))





move\_direct = partial(move, transform=worker\_direct)

move\_direct\_by\_id = partial(move\_task\_by\_id, transform=worker\_direct)

move\_direct\_by\_idmap = partial(move\_by\_idmap, transform=worker\_direct)

move\_direct\_by\_taskmap = partial(move\_by\_taskmap, transform=worker\_direct)



```

"""Fixtures and testing utilities for :pypi:`pytest <pytest>`."""

&#x20;   from celery import Celery

else:

&#x20;   Celery = WorkController = object





NO\_WORKER = os.environ.get('NO\_WORKER')

def pytest\_configure(config):

&#x20;   # this prevents pytest 4.5 and newer from issuing a warning about an unknown marker

&#x20;       config=config,

&#x20;       \*\*parameters

&#x20;   )

\### 代码文件: celery\\contrib\\rdb.py

```python

"""Remote Debugger.



Introduction

============



This is a remote debugger for Celery tasks running in multiprocessing

pool workers.  Inspired by a lost post on dzone.com.



Usage

\-----



.. code-block:: python



&#x20;   from celery.contrib import rdb

&#x20;   from celery import task



&#x20;   @task()

&#x20;   def add(x, y):

&#x20;       result = x + y

&#x20;       rdb.set\_trace()

&#x20;       return result



Environment Variables

=====================



.. envvar:: CELERY\_RDB\_HOST



``CELERY\_RDB\_HOST``

\-------------------



&#x20;   Hostname to bind to.  Default is '127.0.0.1' (only accessible from

&#x20;   localhost).



.. envvar:: CELERY\_RDB\_PORT



``CELERY\_RDB\_PORT``

\-------------------



&#x20;   Base port to bind to.  Default is 6899.

&#x20;   The debugger will try to find an available port starting from the

&#x20;   base port.  The selected port will be logged by the worker.

"""

import errno

import os

import socket

import sys

from pdb import Pdb



from billiard.process import current\_process



\_\_all\_\_ = (

&#x20;   'CELERY\_RDB\_HOST', 'CELERY\_RDB\_PORT', 'DEFAULT\_PORT',

&#x20;   'Rdb', 'debugger', 'set\_trace',

)



DEFAULT\_PORT = 6899



CELERY\_RDB\_HOST = os.environ.get('CELERY\_RDB\_HOST') or '127.0.0.1'

CELERY\_RDB\_PORT = int(os.environ.get('CELERY\_RDB\_PORT') or DEFAULT\_PORT)



\#: Holds the currently active debugger.

\_current = \[None]



\_frame = getattr(sys, '\_getframe')



NO\_AVAILABLE\_PORT = """\\

{self.ident}: Couldn't find an available port.



Please specify one using the CELERY\_RDB\_PORT environment variable.

"""



BANNER = """\\

{self.ident}: Ready to connect: telnet {self.host} {self.port}



Type `exit` in session to continue.



{self.ident}: Waiting for client...

"""



SESSION\_STARTED = '{self.ident}: Now in session with {self.remote\_addr}.'

SESSION\_ENDED = '{self.ident}: Session with {self.remote\_addr} ended.'





class Rdb(Pdb):

&#x20;   """Remote debugger."""



&#x20;   me = 'Remote Debugger'

&#x20;   \_prev\_outs = None

&#x20;   \_sock = None



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, host=CELERY\_RDB\_HOST, port=CELERY\_RDB\_PORT,

&#x20;                port\_search\_limit=100, port\_skew=+0, out=sys.stdout):

&#x20;       self.active = True

&#x20;       self.out = out



&#x20;       self.\_prev\_handles = sys.stdin, sys.stdout



&#x20;       self.\_sock, this\_port = self.get\_avail\_port(

&#x20;           host, port, port\_search\_limit, port\_skew,

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       self.\_sock.setblocking(1)

&#x20;       self.\_sock.listen(1)

&#x20;       self.ident = f'{self.me}:{this\_port}'

&#x20;       self.host = host

&#x20;       self.port = this\_port

&#x20;       self.say(BANNER.format(self=self))



&#x20;       self.\_client, address = self.\_sock.accept()

&#x20;       self.\_client.setblocking(1)

&#x20;       self.remote\_addr = ':'.join(str(v) for v in address)

&#x20;       self.say(SESSION\_STARTED.format(self=self))

&#x20;       self.\_handle = sys.stdin = sys.stdout = self.\_client.makefile('rw')

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(completekey='tab',

&#x20;                        stdin=self.\_handle, stdout=self.\_handle)



&#x20;   def get\_avail\_port(self, host, port, search\_limit=100, skew=+0):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           \_, skew = current\_process().name.split('-')

&#x20;           skew = int(skew)

&#x20;       except ValueError:

&#x20;           pass

&#x20;       this\_port = None

&#x20;       for i in range(search\_limit):

&#x20;           \_sock = socket.socket(socket.AF\_INET, socket.SOCK\_STREAM)

&#x20;           \_sock.setsockopt(socket.SOL\_SOCKET, socket.SO\_REUSEADDR, 1)

&#x20;           this\_port = port + skew + i

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               \_sock.bind((host, this\_port))

&#x20;           except OSError as exc:

&#x20;               if exc.errno in \[errno.EADDRINUSE, errno.EINVAL]:

&#x20;                   continue

&#x20;               raise

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               return \_sock, this\_port

&#x20;       raise Exception(NO\_AVAILABLE\_PORT.format(self=self))



&#x20;   def say(self, m):

&#x20;       print(m, file=self.out)



&#x20;   def \_\_enter\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return self



&#x20;   def \_\_exit\_\_(self, \*exc\_info):

&#x20;       self.\_close\_session()



&#x20;   def \_close\_session(self):

&#x20;       self.stdin, self.stdout = sys.stdin, sys.stdout = self.\_prev\_handles

&#x20;       if self.active:

&#x20;           if self.\_handle is not None:

&#x20;               self.\_handle.close()

&#x20;           if self.\_client is not None:

&#x20;               self.\_client.close()

&#x20;           if self.\_sock is not None:

&#x20;               self.\_sock.close()

&#x20;           self.active = False

&#x20;           self.say(SESSION\_ENDED.format(self=self))



&#x20;   def do\_continue(self, arg):

&#x20;       self.\_close\_session()

&#x20;       self.set\_continue()

&#x20;       return 1

&#x20;   do\_c = do\_cont = do\_continue



&#x20;   def do\_quit(self, arg):

&#x20;       self.\_close\_session()

&#x20;       self.set\_quit()

&#x20;       return 1

&#x20;   do\_q = do\_exit = do\_quit



&#x20;   def set\_quit(self):

&#x20;       # this raises a BdbQuit exception that we're unable to catch.

&#x20;       sys.settrace(None)





def debugger():

&#x20;   """Return the current debugger instance, or create if none."""

&#x20;   rdb = \_current\[0]

&#x20;   if rdb is None or not rdb.active:

&#x20;       rdb = \_current\[0] = Rdb()

&#x20;   return rdb





def set\_trace(frame=None):

&#x20;   """Set break-point at current location, or a specified frame."""

&#x20;   if frame is None:

&#x20;       frame = \_frame().f\_back

&#x20;   return debugger().set\_trace(frame)



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\contrib\\sphinx.py

```python

"""Sphinx documentation plugin used to document tasks.



Introduction

============



Usage

\-----



The Celery extension for Sphinx requires Sphinx 2.0 or later.



Add the extension to your :file:`docs/conf.py` configuration module:



.. code-block:: python



&#x20;   extensions = (...,

&#x20;                 'celery.contrib.sphinx')



If you'd like to change the prefix for tasks in reference documentation

then you can change the ``celery\_task\_prefix`` configuration value:



.. code-block:: python



&#x20;   celery\_task\_prefix = '(task)'  # < default



With the extension installed `autodoc` will automatically find

task decorated objects (e.g. when using the automodule directive)

and generate the correct (as well as add a ``(task)`` prefix),

and you can also refer to the tasks using `:task:proj.tasks.add`

syntax.



Use ``.. autotask::`` to alternatively manually document a task.

"""

from inspect import signature



from docutils import nodes

from sphinx.domains.python import PyFunction

from sphinx.ext.autodoc import FunctionDocumenter



from celery.app.task import BaseTask





class TaskDocumenter(FunctionDocumenter):

&#x20;   """Document task definitions."""



&#x20;   objtype = 'task'

&#x20;   member\_order = 11



&#x20;   @classmethod

&#x20;   def can\_document\_member(cls, member, membername, isattr, parent):

&#x20;       return isinstance(member, BaseTask) and getattr(member, '\_\_wrapped\_\_')



&#x20;   def format\_args(self):

&#x20;       wrapped = getattr(self.object, '\_\_wrapped\_\_', None)

&#x20;       if wrapped is not None:

&#x20;           sig = signature(wrapped)

&#x20;           if "self" in sig.parameters or "cls" in sig.parameters:

&#x20;               sig = sig.replace(parameters=list(sig.parameters.values())\[1:])

&#x20;           return str(sig)

&#x20;       return ''



&#x20;   def document\_members(self, all\_members=False):

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   def check\_module(self):

&#x20;       # Normally checks if \*self.object\* is really defined in the module

&#x20;       # given by \*self.modname\*. But since functions decorated with the @task

&#x20;       # decorator are instances living in the celery.local, we have to check

&#x20;       # the wrapped function instead.

&#x20;       wrapped = getattr(self.object, '\_\_wrapped\_\_', None)

&#x20;       if wrapped and getattr(wrapped, '\_\_module\_\_') == self.modname:

&#x20;           return True

&#x20;       return super().check\_module()





class TaskDirective(PyFunction):

&#x20;   """Sphinx task directive."""



&#x20;   def get\_signature\_prefix(self, sig):

&#x20;       return \[nodes.Text(self.env.config.celery\_task\_prefix)]





def autodoc\_skip\_member\_handler(app, what, name, obj, skip, options):

&#x20;   """Handler for autodoc-skip-member event."""

&#x20;   # Celery tasks created with the @task decorator have the property

&#x20;   # that \*obj.\_\_doc\_\_\* and \*obj.\_\_class\_\_.\_\_doc\_\_\* are equal, which

&#x20;   # trips up the logic in sphinx.ext.autodoc that is supposed to

&#x20;   # suppress repetition of class documentation in an instance of the

&#x20;   # class. This overrides that behavior.

&#x20;   if isinstance(obj, BaseTask) and getattr(obj, '\_\_wrapped\_\_'):

&#x20;       if skip:

&#x20;           return False

&#x20;   return None





def setup(app):

&#x20;   """Setup Sphinx extension."""

&#x20;   app.setup\_extension('sphinx.ext.autodoc')

&#x20;   app.add\_autodocumenter(TaskDocumenter)

&#x20;   app.add\_directive\_to\_domain('py', 'task', TaskDirective)

&#x20;   app.add\_config\_value('celery\_task\_prefix', '(task)', True)

&#x20;   app.connect('autodoc-skip-member', autodoc\_skip\_member\_handler)



&#x20;   return {

&#x20;       'parallel\_read\_safe': True

&#x20;   }



```

\### 代码文件: celery\\contrib\\django\\task.py

```python

import functools



from django.db import transaction



from celery.app.task import Task





class DjangoTask(Task):

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   Extend the base :class:`\~celery.app.task.Task` for Django.



&#x20;   Provide a nicer API to trigger tasks at the end of the DB transaction.

&#x20;   """



&#x20;   def delay\_on\_commit(self, \*args, \*\*kwargs) -> None:

&#x20;       """Call :meth:`\~celery.app.task.Task.delay` with Django's ``on\_commit()``."""

&#x20;       transaction.on\_commit(functools.partial(self.delay, \*args, \*\*kwargs))



&#x20;   def apply\_async\_on\_commit(self, \*args, \*\*kwargs) -> None:

&#x20;       """Call :meth:`\~celery.app.task.Task.apply\_async` with Django's ``on\_commit()``."""

&#x20;       transaction.on\_commit(functools.partial(self.apply\_async, \*args, \*\*kwargs))



```

\### 代码文件: celery\\contrib\\testing\\app.py

```python

"""Create Celery app instances used for testing."""

import weakref

from contextlib import contextmanager

from copy import deepcopy



from kombu.utils.imports import symbol\_by\_name



from celery import Celery, \_state



\#: Contains the default configuration values for the test app.

DEFAULT\_TEST\_CONFIG = {

&#x20;   'worker\_hijack\_root\_logger': False,

&#x20;   'worker\_log\_color': False,

&#x20;   'accept\_content': {'json'},

&#x20;   'enable\_utc': True,

&#x20;   'timezone': 'UTC',

&#x20;   'broker\_url': 'memory://',

&#x20;   'result\_backend': 'cache+memory://',

&#x20;   'broker\_heartbeat': 0,

}





class Trap:

&#x20;   """Trap that pretends to be an app but raises an exception instead.



&#x20;   This to protect from code that does not properly pass app instances,

&#x20;   then falls back to the current\_app.

&#x20;   """



&#x20;   def \_\_getattr\_\_(self, name):

&#x20;       # Workaround to allow unittest.mock to patch this object

&#x20;       # in Python 3.8 and above.

&#x20;       if name == '\_is\_coroutine' or name == '\_\_func\_\_':

&#x20;           return None

&#x20;       print(name)

&#x20;       raise RuntimeError('Test depends on current\_app')





class UnitLogging(symbol\_by\_name(Celery.log\_cls)):

&#x20;   """Sets up logging for the test application."""



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(\*args, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;       self.already\_setup = True





def TestApp(name=None, config=None, enable\_logging=False, set\_as\_current=False,

&#x20;           log=UnitLogging, backend=None, broker=None, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;   """App used for testing."""

&#x20;   from . import tasks  # noqa

&#x20;   config = dict(deepcopy(DEFAULT\_TEST\_CONFIG), \*\*config or {})

&#x20;   if broker is not None:

&#x20;       config.pop('broker\_url', None)

&#x20;   if backend is not None:

&#x20;       config.pop('result\_backend', None)

&#x20;   log = None if enable\_logging else log

&#x20;   test\_app = Celery(

&#x20;       name or 'celery.tests',

&#x20;       set\_as\_current=set\_as\_current,

&#x20;       log=log,

&#x20;       broker=broker,

&#x20;       backend=backend,

&#x20;       \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;   test\_app.add\_defaults(config)

&#x20;   return test\_app





@contextmanager

def set\_trap(app):

&#x20;   """Contextmanager that installs the trap app.



&#x20;   The trap means that anything trying to use the current or default app

&#x20;   will raise an exception.

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   trap = Trap()

&#x20;   prev\_tls = \_state.\_tls

&#x20;   \_state.set\_default\_app(trap)



&#x20;   class NonTLS:

&#x20;       current\_app = trap

&#x20;   \_state.\_tls = NonTLS()



&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       yield

&#x20;   finally:

&#x20;       \_state.\_tls = prev\_tls





@contextmanager

def setup\_default\_app(app, use\_trap=False):

&#x20;   """Setup default app for testing.



&#x20;   Ensures state is clean after the test returns.

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   prev\_current\_app = \_state.get\_current\_app()

&#x20;   prev\_default\_app = \_state.default\_app

&#x20;   prev\_finalizers = set(\_state.\_on\_app\_finalizers)

&#x20;   prev\_apps = weakref.WeakSet(\_state.\_apps)



&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       if use\_trap:

&#x20;           with set\_trap(app):

&#x20;               yield

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           yield

&#x20;   finally:

&#x20;       \_state.set\_default\_app(prev\_default\_app)

&#x20;       \_state.\_tls.current\_app = prev\_current\_app

&#x20;       if app is not prev\_current\_app:

&#x20;           app.close()

&#x20;       \_state.\_on\_app\_finalizers = prev\_finalizers

&#x20;       \_state.\_apps = prev\_apps



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\contrib\\testing\\manager.py

```python

"""Integration testing utilities."""

import socket

import sys

from collections import defaultdict

from functools import partial

from itertools import count

from typing import Any, Callable, Dict, Sequence, TextIO, Tuple  # noqa



from kombu.exceptions import ContentDisallowed

from kombu.utils.functional import retry\_over\_time



from celery import states

from celery.exceptions import TimeoutError

from celery.result import AsyncResult, ResultSet  # noqa

from celery.utils.text import truncate

from celery.utils.time import humanize\_seconds as \_humanize\_seconds



E\_STILL\_WAITING = 'Still waiting for {0}.  Trying again {when}: {exc!r}'



humanize\_seconds = partial(\_humanize\_seconds, microseconds=True)





class Sentinel(Exception):

&#x20;   """Signifies the end of something."""





class ManagerMixin:

&#x20;   """Mixin that adds :class:`Manager` capabilities."""



&#x20;   def \_init\_manager(self,

&#x20;                     block\_timeout=30 \* 60.0, no\_join=False,

&#x20;                     stdout=None, stderr=None):

&#x20;       # type: (float, bool, TextIO, TextIO) -> None

&#x20;       self.stdout = sys.stdout if stdout is None else stdout

&#x20;       self.stderr = sys.stderr if stderr is None else stderr

&#x20;       self.connerrors = self.app.connection().recoverable\_connection\_errors

&#x20;       self.block\_timeout = block\_timeout

&#x20;       self.no\_join = no\_join



&#x20;   def remark(self, s, sep='-'):

&#x20;       # type: (str, str) -> None

&#x20;       print(f'{sep}{s}', file=self.stdout)



&#x20;   def missing\_results(self, r):

&#x20;       # type: (Sequence\[AsyncResult]) -> Sequence\[str]

&#x20;       return \[res.id for res in r if res.id not in res.backend.\_cache]



&#x20;   def wait\_for(

&#x20;       self,

&#x20;       fun,  # type: Callable

&#x20;       catch,  # type: Sequence\[Any]

&#x20;       desc="thing",  # type: str

&#x20;       args=(),  # type: Tuple

&#x20;       kwargs=None,  # type: Dict

&#x20;       errback=None,  # type: Callable

&#x20;       max\_retries=10,  # type: int

&#x20;       interval\_start=0.1,  # type: float

&#x20;       interval\_step=0.5,  # type: float

&#x20;       interval\_max=5.0,  # type: float

&#x20;       emit\_warning=False,  # type: bool

&#x20;       \*\*options  # type: Any

&#x20;   ):

&#x20;       # type: (...) -> Any

&#x20;       """Wait for event to happen.



&#x20;       The `catch` argument specifies the exception that means the event

&#x20;       has not happened yet.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       kwargs = {} if not kwargs else kwargs



&#x20;       def on\_error(exc, intervals, retries):

&#x20;           interval = next(intervals)

&#x20;           if emit\_warning:

&#x20;               self.warn(E\_STILL\_WAITING.format(

&#x20;                   desc, when=humanize\_seconds(interval, 'in', ' '), exc=exc,

&#x20;               ))

&#x20;           if errback:

&#x20;               errback(exc, interval, retries)

&#x20;           return interval



&#x20;       return self.retry\_over\_time(

&#x20;           fun, catch,

&#x20;           args=args, kwargs=kwargs,

&#x20;           errback=on\_error, max\_retries=max\_retries,

&#x20;           interval\_start=interval\_start, interval\_step=interval\_step,

&#x20;           \*\*options

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def ensure\_not\_for\_a\_while(self, fun, catch,

&#x20;                              desc='thing', max\_retries=20,

&#x20;                              interval\_start=0.1, interval\_step=0.02,

&#x20;                              interval\_max=1.0, emit\_warning=False,

&#x20;                              \*\*options):

&#x20;       """Make sure something does not happen (at least for a while)."""

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           return self.wait\_for(

&#x20;               fun, catch, desc=desc, max\_retries=max\_retries,

&#x20;               interval\_start=interval\_start, interval\_step=interval\_step,

&#x20;               interval\_max=interval\_max, emit\_warning=emit\_warning,

&#x20;           )

&#x20;       except catch:

&#x20;           pass

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           raise AssertionError(f'Should not have happened: {desc}')



&#x20;   def retry\_over\_time(self, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       return retry\_over\_time(\*args, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def join(self, r, propagate=False, max\_retries=10, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       if self.no\_join:

&#x20;           return

&#x20;       if not isinstance(r, ResultSet):

&#x20;           r = self.app.ResultSet(\[r])

&#x20;       received = \[]



&#x20;       def on\_result(task\_id, value):

&#x20;           received.append(task\_id)



&#x20;       for i in range(max\_retries) if max\_retries else count(0):

&#x20;           received\[:] = \[]

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               return r.get(callback=on\_result, propagate=propagate, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;           except (socket.timeout, TimeoutError) as exc:

&#x20;               waiting\_for = self.missing\_results(r)

&#x20;               self.remark(

&#x20;                   'Still waiting for {}/{}: \[{}]: {!r}'.format(

&#x20;                       len(r) - len(received), len(r),

&#x20;                       truncate(', '.join(waiting\_for)), exc), '!',

&#x20;               )

&#x20;           except self.connerrors as exc:

&#x20;               self.remark(f'join: connection lost: {exc!r}', '!')

&#x20;       raise AssertionError('Test failed: Missing task results')



&#x20;   def inspect(self, timeout=3.0):

&#x20;       return self.app.control.inspect(timeout=timeout)



&#x20;   def query\_tasks(self, ids, timeout=0.5):

&#x20;       tasks = self.inspect(timeout).query\_task(\*ids) or {}

&#x20;       yield from tasks.items()



&#x20;   def query\_task\_states(self, ids, timeout=0.5):

&#x20;       states = defaultdict(set)

&#x20;       for hostname, reply in self.query\_tasks(ids, timeout=timeout):

&#x20;           for task\_id, (state, \_) in reply.items():

&#x20;               states\[state].add(task\_id)

&#x20;       return states



&#x20;   def assert\_accepted(self, ids, interval=0.5,

&#x20;                       desc='waiting for tasks to be accepted', \*\*policy):

&#x20;       return self.assert\_task\_worker\_state(

&#x20;           self.is\_accepted, ids, interval=interval, desc=desc, \*\*policy

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def assert\_received(self, ids, interval=0.5,

&#x20;                       desc='waiting for tasks to be received', \*\*policy):

&#x20;       return self.assert\_task\_worker\_state(

&#x20;           self.is\_received, ids, interval=interval, desc=desc, \*\*policy

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def assert\_result\_tasks\_in\_progress\_or\_completed(

&#x20;           self,

&#x20;           async\_results,

&#x20;           interval=0.5,

&#x20;           desc='waiting for tasks to be started or completed',

&#x20;           \*\*policy

&#x20;   ):

&#x20;       return self.assert\_task\_state\_from\_result(

&#x20;           self.is\_result\_task\_in\_progress,

&#x20;           async\_results,

&#x20;           interval=interval, desc=desc, \*\*policy

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def assert\_task\_state\_from\_result(self, fun, results,

&#x20;                                     interval=0.5, \*\*policy):

&#x20;       return self.wait\_for(

&#x20;           partial(self.true\_or\_raise, fun, results, timeout=interval),

&#x20;           (Sentinel,), \*\*policy

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   @staticmethod

&#x20;   def is\_result\_task\_in\_progress(results, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       possible\_states = (states.STARTED, states.SUCCESS)

&#x20;       return all(result.state in possible\_states for result in results)



&#x20;   def assert\_task\_worker\_state(self, fun, ids, interval=0.5, \*\*policy):

&#x20;       return self.wait\_for(

&#x20;           partial(self.true\_or\_raise, fun, ids, timeout=interval),

&#x20;           (Sentinel,), \*\*policy

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def is\_received(self, ids, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       return self.\_ids\_matches\_state(

&#x20;           \['reserved', 'active', 'ready'], ids, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def is\_accepted(self, ids, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       return self.\_ids\_matches\_state(\['active', 'ready'], ids, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def \_ids\_matches\_state(self, expected\_states, ids, timeout=0.5):

&#x20;       states = self.query\_task\_states(ids, timeout=timeout)

&#x20;       return all(

&#x20;           any(t in s for s in \[states\[k] for k in expected\_states])

&#x20;           for t in ids

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def true\_or\_raise(self, fun, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       res = fun(\*args, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;       if not res:

&#x20;           raise Sentinel()

&#x20;       return res



&#x20;   def wait\_until\_idle(self):

&#x20;       control = self.app.control

&#x20;       with self.app.connection() as connection:

&#x20;           # Try to purge the queue before we start

&#x20;           # to attempt to avoid interference from other tests

&#x20;           while True:

&#x20;               count = control.purge(connection=connection)

&#x20;               if count == 0:

&#x20;                   break



&#x20;           # Wait until worker is idle

&#x20;           inspect = control.inspect()

&#x20;           inspect.connection = connection

&#x20;           while True:

&#x20;               try:

&#x20;                   count = sum(len(t) for t in inspect.active().values())

&#x20;               except ContentDisallowed:

&#x20;                   # test\_security\_task\_done may trigger this exception

&#x20;                   break

&#x20;               if count == 0:

&#x20;                   break





class Manager(ManagerMixin):

&#x20;   """Test helpers for task integration tests."""



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, app, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       self.app = app

&#x20;       self.\_init\_manager(\*\*kwargs)



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\contrib\\testing\\mocks.py

```python

"""Useful mocks for unit testing."""

import numbers

from datetime import datetime, timedelta

from typing import Any, Mapping, Sequence  # noqa

from unittest.mock import Mock



from celery import Celery  # noqa

from celery.canvas import Signature  # noqa





def TaskMessage(

&#x20;   name,  # type: str

&#x20;   id=None,  # type: str

&#x20;   args=(),  # type: Sequence

&#x20;   kwargs=None,  # type: Mapping

&#x20;   callbacks=None,  # type: Sequence\[Signature]

&#x20;   errbacks=None,  # type: Sequence\[Signature]

&#x20;   chain=None,  # type: Sequence\[Signature]

&#x20;   shadow=None,  # type: str

&#x20;   utc=None,  # type: bool

&#x20;   \*\*options  # type: Any

):

&#x20;   # type: (...) -> Any

&#x20;   """Create task message in protocol 2 format."""

&#x20;   kwargs = {} if not kwargs else kwargs

&#x20;   from kombu.serialization import dumps



&#x20;   from celery import uuid

&#x20;   id = id or uuid()

&#x20;   message = Mock(name=f'TaskMessage-{id}')

&#x20;   message.headers = {

&#x20;       'id': id,

&#x20;       'task': name,

&#x20;       'shadow': shadow,

&#x20;   }

&#x20;   embed = {'callbacks': callbacks, 'errbacks': errbacks, 'chain': chain}

&#x20;   message.headers.update(options)

&#x20;   message.content\_type, message.content\_encoding, message.body = dumps(

&#x20;       (args, kwargs, embed), serializer='json',

&#x20;   )

&#x20;   message.payload = (args, kwargs, embed)

&#x20;   return message





def TaskMessage1(

&#x20;   name,  # type: str

&#x20;   id=None,  # type: str

&#x20;   args=(),  # type: Sequence

&#x20;   kwargs=None,  # type: Mapping

&#x20;   callbacks=None,  # type: Sequence\[Signature]

&#x20;   errbacks=None,  # type: Sequence\[Signature]

&#x20;   chain=None,  # type: Sequence\[Signature]

&#x20;   \*\*options  # type: Any

):

&#x20;   # type: (...) -> Any

&#x20;   """Create task message in protocol 1 format."""

&#x20;   kwargs = {} if not kwargs else kwargs

&#x20;   from kombu.serialization import dumps



&#x20;   from celery import uuid

&#x20;   id = id or uuid()

&#x20;   message = Mock(name=f'TaskMessage-{id}')

&#x20;   message.headers = {}

&#x20;   message.payload = {

&#x20;       'task': name,

&#x20;       'id': id,

&#x20;       'args': args,

&#x20;       'kwargs': kwargs,

&#x20;       'callbacks': callbacks,

&#x20;       'errbacks': errbacks,

&#x20;   }

&#x20;   message.payload.update(options)

&#x20;   message.content\_type, message.content\_encoding, message.body = dumps(

&#x20;       message.payload,

&#x20;   )

&#x20;   return message





def task\_message\_from\_sig(app, sig, utc=True, TaskMessage=TaskMessage):

&#x20;   # type: (Celery, Signature, bool, Any) -> Any

&#x20;   """Create task message from :class:`celery.Signature`.



&#x20;   Example:

&#x20;       >>> m = task\_message\_from\_sig(app, add.s(2, 2))

&#x20;       >>> amqp\_client.basic\_publish(m, exchange='ex', routing\_key='rkey')

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   sig.freeze()

&#x20;   callbacks = sig.options.pop('link', None)

&#x20;   errbacks = sig.options.pop('link\_error', None)

&#x20;   countdown = sig.options.pop('countdown', None)

&#x20;   if countdown:

&#x20;       eta = app.now() + timedelta(seconds=countdown)

&#x20;   else:

&#x20;       eta = sig.options.pop('eta', None)

&#x20;   if eta and isinstance(eta, datetime):

&#x20;       eta = eta.isoformat()

&#x20;   expires = sig.options.pop('expires', None)

&#x20;   if expires and isinstance(expires, numbers.Real):

&#x20;       expires = app.now() + timedelta(seconds=expires)

&#x20;   if expires and isinstance(expires, datetime):

&#x20;       expires = expires.isoformat()

&#x20;   return TaskMessage(

&#x20;       sig.task, id=sig.id, args=sig.args,

&#x20;       kwargs=sig.kwargs,

&#x20;       callbacks=\[dict(s) for s in callbacks] if callbacks else None,

&#x20;       errbacks=\[dict(s) for s in errbacks] if errbacks else None,

&#x20;       eta=eta,

&#x20;       expires=expires,

&#x20;       utc=utc,

&#x20;       \*\*sig.options

&#x20;   )





class \_ContextMock(Mock):

&#x20;   """Dummy class implementing \_\_enter\_\_ and \_\_exit\_\_.



&#x20;   The :keyword:`with` statement requires these to be implemented

&#x20;   in the class, not just the instance.

&#x20;   """



&#x20;   def \_\_enter\_\_(self):

&#x20;       return self



&#x20;   def \_\_exit\_\_(self, \*exc\_info):

&#x20;       pass





def ContextMock(\*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;   """Mock that mocks :keyword:`with` statement contexts."""

&#x20;   obj = \_ContextMock(\*args, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;   obj.attach\_mock(\_ContextMock(), '\_\_enter\_\_')

&#x20;   obj.attach\_mock(\_ContextMock(), '\_\_exit\_\_')

&#x20;   obj.\_\_enter\_\_.return\_value = obj

&#x20;   # if \_\_exit\_\_ return a value the exception is ignored,

&#x20;   # so it must return None here.

&#x20;   obj.\_\_exit\_\_.return\_value = None

&#x20;   return obj



```

\### 代码文件: celery\\contrib\\testing\\worker.py

```python

"""Embedded workers for integration tests."""

import logging

import os

import threading

from contextlib import contextmanager

from typing import Any, Iterable, Optional, Union



import celery.worker.consumer  # noqa

from celery import Celery, worker

from celery.result import \_set\_task\_join\_will\_block, allow\_join\_result

from celery.utils.dispatch import Signal

from celery.utils.nodenames import anon\_nodename



WORKER\_LOGLEVEL = os.environ.get('WORKER\_LOGLEVEL', 'error')



test\_worker\_starting = Signal(

&#x20;   name='test\_worker\_starting',

&#x20;   providing\_args={},

)

test\_worker\_started = Signal(

&#x20;   name='test\_worker\_started',

&#x20;   providing\_args={'worker', 'consumer'},

)

test\_worker\_stopped = Signal(

&#x20;   name='test\_worker\_stopped',

&#x20;   providing\_args={'worker'},

)





class TestWorkController(worker.WorkController):

&#x20;   """Worker that can synchronize on being fully started."""



&#x20;   # When this class is imported in pytest files, prevent pytest from thinking

&#x20;   # this is a test class

&#x20;   \_\_test\_\_ = False



&#x20;   logger\_queue = None



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       # type: (\*Any, \*\*Any) -> None

&#x20;       self.\_on\_started = threading.Event()



&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(\*args, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;       if self.pool\_cls.\_\_module\_\_.split('.')\[-1] == 'prefork':

&#x20;           from billiard import Queue

&#x20;           self.logger\_queue = Queue()

&#x20;           self.pid = os.getpid()



&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               from tblib import pickling\_support

&#x20;               pickling\_support.install()

&#x20;           except ImportError:

&#x20;               pass



&#x20;           # collect logs from forked process.

&#x20;           # XXX: those logs will appear twice in the live log

&#x20;           self.queue\_listener = logging.handlers.QueueListener(self.logger\_queue, logging.getLogger())

&#x20;           self.queue\_listener.start()



&#x20;   class QueueHandler(logging.handlers.QueueHandler):

&#x20;       def prepare(self, record):

&#x20;           record.from\_queue = True

&#x20;           # Keep origin record.

&#x20;           return record



&#x20;       def handleError(self, record):

&#x20;           if logging.raiseExceptions:

&#x20;               raise



&#x20;   def start(self):

&#x20;       if self.logger\_queue:

&#x20;           handler = self.QueueHandler(self.logger\_queue)

&#x20;           handler.addFilter(lambda r: r.process != self.pid and not getattr(r, 'from\_queue', False))

&#x20;           logger = logging.getLogger()

&#x20;           logger.addHandler(handler)

&#x20;       return super().start()



&#x20;   def on\_consumer\_ready(self, consumer):

&#x20;       # type: (celery.worker.consumer.Consumer) -> None

&#x20;       """Callback called when the Consumer blueprint is fully started."""

&#x20;       self.\_on\_started.set()

&#x20;       test\_worker\_started.send(

&#x20;           sender=self.app, worker=self, consumer=consumer)



&#x20;   def ensure\_started(self):

&#x20;       # type: () -> None

&#x20;       """Wait for worker to be fully up and running.



&#x20;       Warning:

&#x20;           Worker must be started within a thread for this to work,

&#x20;           or it will block forever.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       self.\_on\_started.wait()





@contextmanager

def start\_worker(

&#x20;   app,  # type: Celery

&#x20;   concurrency=1,  # type: int

&#x20;   pool='solo',  # type: str

&#x20;   loglevel=WORKER\_LOGLEVEL,  # type: Union\[str, int]

&#x20;   logfile=None,  # type: str

&#x20;   perform\_ping\_check=True,  # type: bool

&#x20;   ping\_task\_timeout=10.0,  # type: float

&#x20;   shutdown\_timeout=10.0,  # type: float

&#x20;   \*\*kwargs  # type: Any

):

&#x20;   # type: (...) -> Iterable

&#x20;   """Start embedded worker.



&#x20;   Yields:

&#x20;       celery.app.worker.Worker: worker instance.

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   test\_worker\_starting.send(sender=app)



&#x20;   worker = None

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       with \_start\_worker\_thread(app,

&#x20;                                 concurrency=concurrency,

&#x20;                                 pool=pool,

&#x20;                                 loglevel=loglevel,

&#x20;                                 logfile=logfile,

&#x20;                                 perform\_ping\_check=perform\_ping\_check,

&#x20;                                 shutdown\_timeout=shutdown\_timeout,

&#x20;                                 \*\*kwargs) as worker:

&#x20;           if perform\_ping\_check:

&#x20;               from .tasks import ping

&#x20;               with allow\_join\_result():

&#x20;                   assert ping.delay().get(timeout=ping\_task\_timeout) == 'pong'



&#x20;           yield worker

&#x20;   finally:

&#x20;       test\_worker\_stopped.send(sender=app, worker=worker)





@contextmanager

def \_start\_worker\_thread(app: Celery,

&#x20;                        concurrency: int = 1,

&#x20;                        pool: str = 'solo',

&#x20;                        loglevel: Union\[str, int] = WORKER\_LOGLEVEL,

&#x20;                        logfile: Optional\[str] = None,

&#x20;                        WorkController: Any = TestWorkController,

&#x20;                        perform\_ping\_check: bool = True,

&#x20;                        shutdown\_timeout: float = 10.0,

&#x20;                        \*\*kwargs) -> Iterable\[worker.WorkController]:

&#x20;   """Start Celery worker in a thread.



&#x20;   Yields:

&#x20;       celery.worker.Worker: worker instance.

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   setup\_app\_for\_worker(app, loglevel, logfile)

&#x20;   if perform\_ping\_check:

&#x20;       assert 'celery.ping' in app.tasks

&#x20;   # Make sure we can connect to the broker

&#x20;   with app.connection(hostname=os.environ.get('TEST\_BROKER')) as conn:

&#x20;       conn.default\_channel.queue\_declare



&#x20;   worker = WorkController(

&#x20;       app=app,

&#x20;       concurrency=concurrency,

&#x20;       hostname=kwargs.pop("hostname", anon\_nodename()),

&#x20;       pool=pool,

&#x20;       loglevel=loglevel,

&#x20;       logfile=logfile,

&#x20;       # not allowed to override TestWorkController.on\_consumer\_ready

&#x20;       ready\_callback=None,

&#x20;       without\_heartbeat=kwargs.pop("without\_heartbeat", True),

&#x20;       without\_mingle=True,

&#x20;       without\_gossip=True,

&#x20;       \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   t = threading.Thread(target=worker.start, daemon=True)

&#x20;   t.start()

&#x20;   worker.ensure\_started()

&#x20;   \_set\_task\_join\_will\_block(False)



&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       yield worker

&#x20;   finally:

&#x20;       from celery.worker import state

&#x20;       state.should\_terminate = 0

&#x20;       t.join(shutdown\_timeout)

&#x20;       if t.is\_alive():

&#x20;           raise RuntimeError(

&#x20;               "Worker thread failed to exit within the allocated timeout. "

&#x20;               "Consider raising `shutdown\_timeout` if your tasks take longer "

&#x20;               "to execute."

&#x20;           )

&#x20;       state.should\_terminate = None





@contextmanager

def \_start\_worker\_process(app,

&#x20;                         concurrency=1,

&#x20;                         pool='solo',

&#x20;                         loglevel=WORKER\_LOGLEVEL,

&#x20;                         logfile=None,

&#x20;                         \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;   # type (Celery, int, str, Union\[int, str], str, \*\*Any) -> Iterable

&#x20;   """Start worker in separate process.



&#x20;   Yields:

&#x20;       celery.app.worker.Worker: worker instance.

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   from celery.apps.multi import Cluster, Node



&#x20;   app.set\_current()

&#x20;   cluster = Cluster(\[Node('testworker1@%h')])

&#x20;   cluster.start()

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       yield

&#x20;   finally:

&#x20;       cluster.stopwait()





def setup\_app\_for\_worker(app: Celery, loglevel: Union\[str, int], logfile: str) -> None:

&#x20;   """Setup the app to be used for starting an embedded worker."""

&#x20;   app.finalize()

&#x20;   app.set\_current()

&#x20;   app.set\_default()

&#x20;   type(app.log).\_setup = False

&#x20;   app.log.setup(loglevel=loglevel, logfile=logfile)



```
