```python
"""Django-specific customization."""

import os

import sys

import warnings

from datetime import datetime, timezone

from importlib import import\_module

from typing import IO, TYPE\_CHECKING, Any, List, Optional, cast



from kombu.utils.imports import symbol\_by\_name

from kombu.utils.objects import cached\_property



from celery import \_state, signals

from celery.exceptions import FixupWarning, ImproperlyConfigured



if TYPE\_CHECKING:

&#x20;   from types import ModuleType

&#x20;   from typing import Protocol



&#x20;   from django.db.backends.base.base import BaseDatabaseWrapper

&#x20;   from django.db.utils import ConnectionHandler



&#x20;   from celery.app.base import Celery

&#x20;   from celery.app.task import Task



&#x20;   class DjangoDBModule(Protocol):

&#x20;       connections: ConnectionHandler





\_\_all\_\_ = ('DjangoFixup', 'fixup')



ERR\_NOT\_INSTALLED = """\\

Environment variable DJANGO\_SETTINGS\_MODULE is defined

but Django isn't installed.  Won't apply Django fix-ups!

"""





def \_maybe\_close\_fd(fh: IO) -> None:

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       os.close(fh.fileno())

&#x20;   except (AttributeError, OSError, TypeError):

&#x20;       # TypeError added for celery#962

&#x20;       pass





def \_verify\_django\_version(django: "ModuleType") -> None:

&#x20;   if django.VERSION < (1, 11):

&#x20;       raise ImproperlyConfigured('Celery 5.x requires Django 1.11 or later.')





def fixup(app: "Celery", env: str = 'DJANGO\_SETTINGS\_MODULE') -> Optional\["DjangoFixup"]:

&#x20;   """Install Django fixup if settings module environment is set."""

&#x20;   SETTINGS\_MODULE = os.environ.get(env)

&#x20;   if SETTINGS\_MODULE and 'django' not in app.loader\_cls.lower():

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           import django

&#x20;       except ImportError:

&#x20;           warnings.warn(FixupWarning(ERR\_NOT\_INSTALLED))

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           \_verify\_django\_version(django)

&#x20;           return DjangoFixup(app).install()

&#x20;   return None





class DjangoFixup:

&#x20;   """Fixup installed when using Django."""



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, app: "Celery"):

&#x20;       self.app = app

&#x20;       if \_state.default\_app is None:

&#x20;           self.app.set\_default()

&#x20;       self.\_worker\_fixup: Optional\["DjangoWorkerFixup"] = None



&#x20;   def install(self) -> "DjangoFixup":

&#x20;       # Need to add project directory to path.

&#x20;       # The project directory has precedence over system modules,

&#x20;       # so we prepend it to the path.

&#x20;       sys.path.insert(0, os.getcwd())



&#x20;       self.\_settings = symbol\_by\_name('django.conf:settings')

&#x20;       self.app.loader.now = self.now



&#x20;       if not self.app.\_custom\_task\_cls\_used:

&#x20;           self.app.task\_cls = 'celery.contrib.django.task:DjangoTask'



&#x20;       signals.import\_modules.connect(self.on\_import\_modules)

&#x20;       signals.worker\_init.connect(self.on\_worker\_init)

&#x20;       return self



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def worker\_fixup(self) -> "DjangoWorkerFixup":

&#x20;       if self.\_worker\_fixup is None:

&#x20;           self.\_worker\_fixup = DjangoWorkerFixup(self.app)

&#x20;       return self.\_worker\_fixup



&#x20;   @worker\_fixup.setter

&#x20;   def worker\_fixup(self, value: "DjangoWorkerFixup") -> None:

&#x20;       self.\_worker\_fixup = value



&#x20;   def on\_import\_modules(self, \*\*kwargs: Any) -> None:

&#x20;       # call django.setup() before task modules are imported

&#x20;       self.worker\_fixup.validate\_models()



&#x20;   def on\_worker\_init(self, \*\*kwargs: Any) -> None:

&#x20;       self.worker\_fixup.install()



&#x20;   def now(self, utc: bool = False) -> datetime:

&#x20;       return datetime.now(timezone.utc) if utc else self.\_now()



&#x20;   def autodiscover\_tasks(self) -> List\[str]:

&#x20;       from django.apps import apps

&#x20;       return \[config.name for config in apps.get\_app\_configs()]



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def \_now(self) -> datetime:

&#x20;       return symbol\_by\_name('django.utils.timezone:now')





class DjangoWorkerFixup:

&#x20;   \_db\_recycles = 0



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, app: "Celery") -> None:

&#x20;       self.app = app

&#x20;       self.db\_reuse\_max = self.app.conf.get('CELERY\_DB\_REUSE\_MAX', None)

&#x20;       self.\_db = cast("DjangoDBModule", import\_module('django.db'))

&#x20;       self.\_cache = import\_module('django.core.cache')

&#x20;       self.\_settings = symbol\_by\_name('django.conf:settings')



&#x20;       self.interface\_errors = (

&#x20;           symbol\_by\_name('django.db.utils.InterfaceError'),

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       self.DatabaseError = symbol\_by\_name('django.db:DatabaseError')



&#x20;   def django\_setup(self) -> None:

&#x20;       import django

&#x20;       django.setup()



&#x20;   def validate\_models(self) -> None:

&#x20;       from django.core.checks import run\_checks

&#x20;       self.django\_setup()

&#x20;       if not os.environ.get('CELERY\_SKIP\_CHECKS'):

&#x20;           run\_checks()



&#x20;   def install(self) -> "DjangoWorkerFixup":

&#x20;       signals.beat\_embedded\_init.connect(self.close\_database)

&#x20;       signals.task\_prerun.connect(self.on\_task\_prerun)

&#x20;       signals.task\_postrun.connect(self.on\_task\_postrun)

&#x20;       signals.worker\_process\_init.connect(self.on\_worker\_process\_init)

&#x20;       self.close\_database()

&#x20;       self.close\_cache()

&#x20;       return self



&#x20;   def on\_worker\_process\_init(self, \*\*kwargs: Any) -> None:

&#x20;       # Child process must validate models again if on Windows,

&#x20;       # or if they were started using execv.

&#x20;       if os.environ.get('FORKED\_BY\_MULTIPROCESSING'):

&#x20;           self.validate\_models()



&#x20;       # close connections:

&#x20;       # the parent process may have established these,

&#x20;       # so need to close them.



&#x20;       # calling db.close() on some DB connections will cause

&#x20;       # the inherited DB conn to also get broken in the parent

&#x20;       # process so we need to remove it without triggering any

&#x20;       # network IO that close() might cause.

&#x20;       for c in self.\_db.connections.all():

&#x20;           if c and c.connection:

&#x20;               self.\_maybe\_close\_db\_fd(c)



&#x20;       # use the \_ version to avoid DB\_REUSE preventing the conn.close() call

&#x20;       self.\_close\_database()

&#x20;       self.close\_cache()



&#x20;   def \_maybe\_close\_db\_fd(self, c: "BaseDatabaseWrapper") -> None:

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           with c.wrap\_database\_errors:

&#x20;               \_maybe\_close\_fd(c.connection)

&#x20;       except self.interface\_errors:

&#x20;           pass



&#x20;   def on\_task\_prerun(self, sender: "Task", \*\*kwargs: Any) -> None:

&#x20;       """Called before every task."""

&#x20;       if not getattr(sender.request, 'is\_eager', False):

&#x20;           self.close\_database()



&#x20;   def on\_task\_postrun(self, sender: "Task", \*\*kwargs: Any) -> None:

&#x20;       # See https://groups.google.com/group/django-users/browse\_thread/thread/78200863d0c07c6d/

&#x20;       if not getattr(sender.request, 'is\_eager', False):

&#x20;           self.close\_database()

&#x20;           self.close\_cache()



&#x20;   def close\_database(self, \*\*kwargs: Any) -> None:

&#x20;       if not self.db\_reuse\_max:

&#x20;           return self.\_close\_database()

&#x20;       if self.\_db\_recycles >= self.db\_reuse\_max \* 2:

&#x20;           self.\_db\_recycles = 0

&#x20;           self.\_close\_database()

&#x20;       self.\_db\_recycles += 1



&#x20;   def \_close\_database(self) -> None:

&#x20;       for conn in self.\_db.connections.all():

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               conn.close()

&#x20;           except self.interface\_errors:

&#x20;               pass

&#x20;           except self.DatabaseError as exc:

&#x20;               str\_exc = str(exc)

&#x20;               if 'closed' not in str\_exc and 'not connected' not in str\_exc:

&#x20;                   raise



&#x20;   def close\_cache(self) -> None:

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           self.\_cache.close\_caches()

&#x20;       except (TypeError, AttributeError):

&#x20;           pass



```

```python

"""Loader base class."""

import importlib

import os

import re

import sys

from datetime import datetime, timezone



from kombu.utils import json

from kombu.utils.objects import cached\_property



from celery import signals

from celery.exceptions import reraise

from celery.utils.collections import DictAttribute, force\_mapping

from celery.utils.functional import maybe\_list

from celery.utils.imports import NotAPackage, find\_module, import\_from\_cwd, symbol\_by\_name



\_\_all\_\_ = ('BaseLoader',)



\_RACE\_PROTECTION = False



CONFIG\_INVALID\_NAME = """\\

Error: Module '{module}' doesn't exist, or it's not a valid \\

Python module name.

"""



CONFIG\_WITH\_SUFFIX = CONFIG\_INVALID\_NAME + """\\

Did you mean '{suggest}'?

"""



unconfigured = object()





class BaseLoader:

&#x20;   """Base class for loaders.



&#x20;   Loaders handles,



&#x20;       \* Reading celery client/worker configurations.



&#x20;       \* What happens when a task starts?

&#x20;           See :meth:`on\_task\_init`.



&#x20;       \* What happens when the worker starts?

&#x20;           See :meth:`on\_worker\_init`.



&#x20;       \* What happens when the worker shuts down?

&#x20;           See :meth:`on\_worker\_shutdown`.



&#x20;       \* What modules are imported to find tasks?

&#x20;   """



&#x20;   builtin\_modules = frozenset()

&#x20;   configured = False

&#x20;   override\_backends = {}

&#x20;   worker\_initialized = False



&#x20;   \_conf = unconfigured



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, app, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       self.app = app

&#x20;       self.task\_modules = set()



&#x20;   def now(self, utc=True):

&#x20;       if utc:

&#x20;           return datetime.now(timezone.utc)

&#x20;       return datetime.now()



&#x20;   def on\_task\_init(self, task\_id, task):

&#x20;       """Called before a task is executed."""



&#x20;   def on\_process\_cleanup(self):

&#x20;       """Called after a task is executed."""



&#x20;   def on\_worker\_init(self):

&#x20;       """Called when the worker (:program:`celery worker`) starts."""



&#x20;   def on\_worker\_shutdown(self):

&#x20;       """Called when the worker (:program:`celery worker`) shuts down."""



&#x20;   def on\_worker\_process\_init(self):

&#x20;       """Called when a child process starts."""



&#x20;   def import\_task\_module(self, module):

&#x20;       self.task\_modules.add(module)

&#x20;       return self.import\_from\_cwd(module)



&#x20;   def import\_module(self, module, package=None):

&#x20;       return importlib.import\_module(module, package=package)



&#x20;   def import\_from\_cwd(self, module, imp=None, package=None):

&#x20;       return import\_from\_cwd(

&#x20;           module,

&#x20;           self.import\_module if imp is None else imp,

&#x20;           package=package,

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def import\_default\_modules(self):

&#x20;       responses = signals.import\_modules.send(sender=self.app)

&#x20;       # Prior to this point loggers are not yet set up properly, need to

&#x20;       #   check responses manually and reraised exceptions if any, otherwise

&#x20;       #   they'll be silenced, making it incredibly difficult to debug.

&#x20;       for \_, response in responses:

&#x20;           if isinstance(response, Exception):

&#x20;               raise response

&#x20;       return \[self.import\_task\_module(m) for m in self.default\_modules]



&#x20;   def init\_worker(self):

&#x20;       if not self.worker\_initialized:

&#x20;           self.worker\_initialized = True

&#x20;           self.import\_default\_modules()

&#x20;           self.on\_worker\_init()



&#x20;   def shutdown\_worker(self):

&#x20;       self.on\_worker\_shutdown()



&#x20;   def init\_worker\_process(self):

&#x20;       self.on\_worker\_process\_init()



&#x20;   def config\_from\_object(self, obj, silent=False):

&#x20;       if isinstance(obj, str):

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               obj = self.\_smart\_import(obj, imp=self.import\_from\_cwd)

&#x20;           except (ImportError, AttributeError):

&#x20;               if silent:

&#x20;                   return False

&#x20;               raise

&#x20;       self.\_conf = force\_mapping(obj)

&#x20;       if self.\_conf.get('override\_backends') is not None:

&#x20;           self.override\_backends = self.\_conf\['override\_backends']

&#x20;       return True



&#x20;   def \_smart\_import(self, path, imp=None):

&#x20;       imp = self.import\_module if imp is None else imp

&#x20;       if ':' in path:

&#x20;           # Path includes attribute so can just jump

&#x20;           # here (e.g., ``os.path:abspath``).

&#x20;           return symbol\_by\_name(path, imp=imp)



&#x20;       # Not sure if path is just a module name or if it includes an

&#x20;       # attribute name (e.g., ``os.path``, vs, ``os.path.abspath``).

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           return imp(path)

&#x20;       except ImportError:

&#x20;           # Not a module name, so try module + attribute.

&#x20;           return symbol\_by\_name(path, imp=imp)



&#x20;   def \_import\_config\_module(self, name):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           self.find\_module(name)

&#x20;       except NotAPackage as exc:

&#x20;           if name.endswith('.py'):

&#x20;               reraise(NotAPackage, NotAPackage(CONFIG\_WITH\_SUFFIX.format(

&#x20;                       module=name, suggest=name\[:-3])), sys.exc\_info()\[2])

&#x20;           raise NotAPackage(CONFIG\_INVALID\_NAME.format(module=name)) from exc

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           return self.import\_from\_cwd(name)



&#x20;   def find\_module(self, module):

&#x20;       return find\_module(module)



&#x20;   def cmdline\_config\_parser(self, args, namespace='celery',

&#x20;                             re\_type=re.compile(r'\\((\\w+)\\)'),

&#x20;                             extra\_types=None,

&#x20;                             override\_types=None):

&#x20;       extra\_types = extra\_types if extra\_types else {'json': json.loads}

&#x20;       override\_types = override\_types if override\_types else {

&#x20;           'tuple': 'json',

&#x20;           'list': 'json',

&#x20;           'dict': 'json'

&#x20;       }

&#x20;       from celery.app.defaults import NAMESPACES, Option

&#x20;       namespace = namespace and namespace.lower()

&#x20;       typemap = dict(Option.typemap, \*\*extra\_types)



&#x20;       def getarg(arg):

&#x20;           """Parse single configuration from command-line."""

&#x20;           # ## find key/value

&#x20;           # ns.key=value|ns\_key=value (case insensitive)

&#x20;           key, value = arg.split('=', 1)

&#x20;           key = key.lower().replace('.', '\_')



&#x20;           # ## find name-space.

&#x20;           # .key=value|\_key=value expands to default name-space.

&#x20;           if key\[0] == '\_':

&#x20;               ns, key = namespace, key\[1:]

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               # find name-space part of key

&#x20;               ns, key = key.split('\_', 1)



&#x20;           ns\_key = (ns and ns + '\_' or '') + key



&#x20;           # (type)value makes cast to custom type.

&#x20;           cast = re\_type.match(value)

&#x20;           if cast:

&#x20;               type\_ = cast.groups()\[0]

&#x20;               type\_ = override\_types.get(type\_, type\_)

&#x20;               value = value\[len(cast.group()):]

&#x20;               value = typemap\[type\_](value)

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               try:

&#x20;                   value = NAMESPACES\[ns.lower()]\[key].to\_python(value)

&#x20;               except ValueError as exc:

&#x20;                   # display key name in error message.

&#x20;                   raise ValueError(f'{ns\_key!r}: {exc}')

&#x20;           return ns\_key, value

&#x20;       return dict(getarg(arg) for arg in args)



&#x20;   def read\_configuration(self, env='CELERY\_CONFIG\_MODULE'):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           custom\_config = os.environ\[env]

&#x20;       except KeyError:

&#x20;           pass

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           if custom\_config:

&#x20;               usercfg = self.\_import\_config\_module(custom\_config)

&#x20;               return DictAttribute(usercfg)



&#x20;   def autodiscover\_tasks(self, packages, related\_name='tasks'):

&#x20;       self.task\_modules.update(

&#x20;           mod.\_\_name\_\_ for mod in autodiscover\_tasks(packages or (),

&#x20;                                                      related\_name) if mod)



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def default\_modules(self):

&#x20;       return (

&#x20;           tuple(self.builtin\_modules) +

&#x20;           tuple(maybe\_list(self.app.conf.imports)) +

&#x20;           tuple(maybe\_list(self.app.conf.include))

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def conf(self):

&#x20;       """Loader configuration."""

&#x20;       if self.\_conf is unconfigured:

&#x20;           self.\_conf = self.read\_configuration()

&#x20;       return self.\_conf





def autodiscover\_tasks(packages, related\_name='tasks'):

&#x20;   global \_RACE\_PROTECTION



&#x20;   if \_RACE\_PROTECTION:

&#x20;       return ()

&#x20;   \_RACE\_PROTECTION = True

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       return \[find\_related\_module(pkg, related\_name) for pkg in packages]

&#x20;   finally:

&#x20;       \_RACE\_PROTECTION = False





def find\_related\_module(package, related\_name):

&#x20;   """Find module in package."""

&#x20;   # Django 1.7 allows for specifying a class name in INSTALLED\_APPS.

&#x20;   # (Issue #2248).

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       # Return package itself when no related\_name.

&#x20;       module = importlib.import\_module(package)

&#x20;       if not related\_name and module:

&#x20;           return module

&#x20;   except ModuleNotFoundError:

&#x20;       # On import error, try to walk package up one level.

&#x20;       package, \_, \_ = package.rpartition('.')

&#x20;       if not package:

&#x20;           raise



&#x20;   module\_name = f'{package}.{related\_name}'



&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       # Try to find related\_name under package.

&#x20;       return importlib.import\_module(module\_name)

&#x20;   except ModuleNotFoundError as e:

&#x20;       import\_exc\_name = getattr(e, 'name', None)

&#x20;       # If candidate does not exist, then return None.

&#x20;       if import\_exc\_name and module\_name == import\_exc\_name:

&#x20;           return



&#x20;       # Otherwise, raise because error probably originated from a nested import.

&#x20;       raise e



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\loaders\\default.py

```python

"""The default loader used when no custom app has been initialized."""

import os

import warnings



from celery.exceptions import NotConfigured

from celery.utils.collections import DictAttribute

from celery.utils.serialization import strtobool



from .base import BaseLoader



\_\_all\_\_ = ('Loader', 'DEFAULT\_CONFIG\_MODULE')



DEFAULT\_CONFIG\_MODULE = 'celeryconfig'



\#: Warns if configuration file is missing if :envvar:`C\_WNOCONF` is set.

C\_WNOCONF = strtobool(os.environ.get('C\_WNOCONF', False))





class Loader(BaseLoader):

&#x20;   """The loader used by the default app."""



&#x20;   def setup\_settings(self, settingsdict):

&#x20;       return DictAttribute(settingsdict)



&#x20;   def read\_configuration(self, fail\_silently=True):

&#x20;       """Read configuration from :file:`celeryconfig.py`."""

&#x20;       configname = os.environ.get('CELERY\_CONFIG\_MODULE',

&#x20;                                   DEFAULT\_CONFIG\_MODULE)

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           usercfg = self.\_import\_config\_module(configname)

&#x20;       except ImportError:

&#x20;           if not fail\_silently:

&#x20;               raise

&#x20;           # billiard sets this if forked using execv

&#x20;           if C\_WNOCONF and not os.environ.get('FORKED\_BY\_MULTIPROCESSING'):

&#x20;               warnings.warn(NotConfigured(

&#x20;                   'No {module} module found! Please make sure it exists and '

&#x20;                   'is available to Python.'.format(module=configname)))

&#x20;           return self.setup\_settings({})

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           self.configured = True

&#x20;           return self.setup\_settings(usercfg)



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\loaders\\\_\_init\_\_.py

```python

"""Get loader by name.



Loaders define how configuration is read, what happens

when workers start, when tasks are executed and so on.

"""

from celery.utils.imports import import\_from\_cwd, symbol\_by\_name



\_\_all\_\_ = ('get\_loader\_cls',)



LOADER\_ALIASES = {

&#x20;   'app': 'celery.loaders.app:AppLoader',

&#x20;   'default': 'celery.loaders.default:Loader',

}





def get\_loader\_cls(loader):

&#x20;   """Get loader class by name/alias."""

&#x20;   return symbol\_by\_name(loader, LOADER\_ALIASES, imp=import\_from\_cwd)



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\security\\certificate.py

```python

"""X.509 certificates."""

from \_\_future\_\_ import annotations



import datetime

import glob

import os

from typing import TYPE\_CHECKING, Iterator



from cryptography.hazmat.backends import default\_backend

from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric import padding, rsa

from cryptography.x509 import load\_pem\_x509\_certificate

from kombu.utils.encoding import bytes\_to\_str, ensure\_bytes



from celery.exceptions import SecurityError



from .utils import reraise\_errors



if TYPE\_CHECKING:

&#x20;   from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric.dsa import DSAPublicKey

&#x20;   from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric.ec import EllipticCurvePublicKey

&#x20;   from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric.ed448 import Ed448PublicKey

&#x20;   from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric.ed25519 import Ed25519PublicKey

&#x20;   from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric.rsa import RSAPublicKey

&#x20;   from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric.utils import Prehashed

&#x20;   from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.hashes import HashAlgorithm





\_\_all\_\_ = ('Certificate', 'CertStore', 'FSCertStore')





class Certificate:

&#x20;   """X.509 certificate."""



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, cert: str) -> None:

&#x20;       with reraise\_errors(

&#x20;           'Invalid certificate: {0!r}', errors=(ValueError,)

&#x20;       ):

&#x20;           self.\_cert = load\_pem\_x509\_certificate(

&#x20;               ensure\_bytes(cert), backend=default\_backend())



&#x20;           if not isinstance(self.\_cert.public\_key(), rsa.RSAPublicKey):

&#x20;               raise ValueError("Non-RSA certificates are not supported.")



&#x20;   def has\_expired(self) -> bool:

&#x20;       """Check if the certificate has expired."""

&#x20;       return datetime.datetime.now(datetime.timezone.utc) >= self.\_cert.not\_valid\_after\_utc



&#x20;   def get\_pubkey(self) -> (

&#x20;       DSAPublicKey | EllipticCurvePublicKey | Ed448PublicKey | Ed25519PublicKey | RSAPublicKey

&#x20;   ):

&#x20;       return self.\_cert.public\_key()



&#x20;   def get\_serial\_number(self) -> int:

&#x20;       """Return the serial number in the certificate."""

&#x20;       return self.\_cert.serial\_number



&#x20;   def get\_issuer(self) -> str:

&#x20;       """Return issuer (CA) as a string."""

&#x20;       return ' '.join(x.value for x in self.\_cert.issuer)



&#x20;   def get\_id(self) -> str:

&#x20;       """Serial number/issuer pair uniquely identifies a certificate."""

&#x20;       return f'{self.get\_issuer()} {self.get\_serial\_number()}'



&#x20;   def verify(self, data: bytes, signature: bytes, digest: HashAlgorithm | Prehashed) -> None:

&#x20;       """Verify signature for string containing data."""

&#x20;       with reraise\_errors('Bad signature: {0!r}'):



&#x20;           pad = padding.PSS(

&#x20;               mgf=padding.MGF1(digest),

&#x20;               salt\_length=padding.PSS.MAX\_LENGTH)



&#x20;           self.get\_pubkey().verify(signature, ensure\_bytes(data), pad, digest)





class CertStore:

&#x20;   """Base class for certificate stores."""



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self) -> None:

&#x20;       self.\_certs: dict\[str, Certificate] = {}



&#x20;   def itercerts(self) -> Iterator\[Certificate]:

&#x20;       """Return certificate iterator."""

&#x20;       yield from self.\_certs.values()



&#x20;   def \_\_getitem\_\_(self, id: str) -> Certificate:

&#x20;       """Get certificate by id."""

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           return self.\_certs\[bytes\_to\_str(id)]

&#x20;       except KeyError:

&#x20;           raise SecurityError(f'Unknown certificate: {id!r}')



&#x20;   def add\_cert(self, cert: Certificate) -> None:

&#x20;       cert\_id = bytes\_to\_str(cert.get\_id())

&#x20;       if cert\_id in self.\_certs:

&#x20;           raise SecurityError(f'Duplicate certificate: {id!r}')

&#x20;       self.\_certs\[cert\_id] = cert





class FSCertStore(CertStore):

&#x20;   """File system certificate store."""



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, path: str) -> None:

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_()

&#x20;       if os.path.isdir(path):

&#x20;           path = os.path.join(path, '\*')

&#x20;       for p in glob.glob(path):

&#x20;           with open(p) as f:

&#x20;               cert = Certificate(f.read())

&#x20;               if cert.has\_expired():

&#x20;                   raise SecurityError(

&#x20;                       f'Expired certificate: {cert.get\_id()!r}')

&#x20;               self.add\_cert(cert)



```

```python

"""Private keys for the security serializer."""

from cryptography.hazmat.backends import default\_backend

from cryptography.hazmat.primitives import serialization

from cryptography.hazmat.primitives.asymmetric import padding, rsa

from kombu.utils.encoding import ensure\_bytes



from .utils import reraise\_errors



\_\_all\_\_ = ('PrivateKey',)





class PrivateKey:

&#x20;   """Represents a private key."""



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, key, password=None):

&#x20;       with reraise\_errors(

&#x20;           'Invalid private key: {0!r}', errors=(ValueError,)

&#x20;       ):

&#x20;           self.\_key = serialization.load\_pem\_private\_key(

&#x20;               ensure\_bytes(key),

&#x20;               password=ensure\_bytes(password),

&#x20;               backend=default\_backend())



&#x20;           if not isinstance(self.\_key, rsa.RSAPrivateKey):

&#x20;               raise ValueError("Non-RSA keys are not supported.")



&#x20;   def sign(self, data, digest):

&#x20;       """Sign string containing data."""

&#x20;       with reraise\_errors('Unable to sign data: {0!r}'):



&#x20;           pad = padding.PSS(

&#x20;               mgf=padding.MGF1(digest),

&#x20;               salt\_length=padding.PSS.MAX\_LENGTH)



&#x20;           return self.\_key.sign(ensure\_bytes(data), pad, digest)



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\security\\serialization.py

```python

"""Secure serializer."""

from kombu.serialization import dumps, loads, registry

from kombu.utils.encoding import bytes\_to\_str, ensure\_bytes, str\_to\_bytes



from celery.app.defaults import DEFAULT\_SECURITY\_DIGEST

from celery.utils.serialization import b64decode, b64encode



from .certificate import Certificate, FSCertStore

from .key import PrivateKey

from .utils import get\_digest\_algorithm, reraise\_errors



\_\_all\_\_ = ('SecureSerializer', 'register\_auth')



\# Note: we guarantee that this value won't appear in the serialized data,

\# so we can use it as a separator.

\# If you change this value, make sure it's not present in the serialized data.

DEFAULT\_SEPARATOR = str\_to\_bytes("\\x00\\x01")





class SecureSerializer:

&#x20;   """Signed serializer."""



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, key=None, cert=None, cert\_store=None,

&#x20;                digest=DEFAULT\_SECURITY\_DIGEST, serializer='json'):

&#x20;       self.\_key = key

&#x20;       self.\_cert = cert

&#x20;       self.\_cert\_store = cert\_store

&#x20;       self.\_digest = get\_digest\_algorithm(digest)

&#x20;       self.\_serializer = serializer



&#x20;   def serialize(self, data):

&#x20;       """Serialize data structure into string."""

&#x20;       assert self.\_key is not None

&#x20;       assert self.\_cert is not None

&#x20;       with reraise\_errors('Unable to serialize: {0!r}', (Exception,)):

&#x20;           content\_type, content\_encoding, body = dumps(

&#x20;               data, serializer=self.\_serializer)



&#x20;           # What we sign is the serialized body, not the body itself.

&#x20;           # this way the receiver doesn't have to decode the contents

&#x20;           # to verify the signature (and thus avoiding potential flaws

&#x20;           # in the decoding step).

&#x20;           body = ensure\_bytes(body)

&#x20;           return self.\_pack(body, content\_type, content\_encoding,

&#x20;                             signature=self.\_key.sign(body, self.\_digest),

&#x20;                             signer=self.\_cert.get\_id())



&#x20;   def deserialize(self, data):

&#x20;       """Deserialize data structure from string."""

&#x20;       assert self.\_cert\_store is not None

&#x20;       with reraise\_errors('Unable to deserialize: {0!r}', (Exception,)):

&#x20;           payload = self.\_unpack(data)

&#x20;           signature, signer, body = (payload\['signature'],

&#x20;                                      payload\['signer'],

&#x20;                                      payload\['body'])

&#x20;           self.\_cert\_store\[signer].verify(body, signature, self.\_digest)

&#x20;       return loads(body, payload\['content\_type'],

&#x20;                    payload\['content\_encoding'], force=True)



&#x20;   def \_pack(self, body, content\_type, content\_encoding, signer, signature,

&#x20;             sep=DEFAULT\_SEPARATOR):

&#x20;       fields = sep.join(

&#x20;           ensure\_bytes(s) for s in \[b64encode(signer), b64encode(signature),

&#x20;                                     content\_type, content\_encoding, body]

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       return b64encode(fields)



&#x20;   def \_unpack(self, payload, sep=DEFAULT\_SEPARATOR):

&#x20;       raw\_payload = b64decode(ensure\_bytes(payload))

&#x20;       v = raw\_payload.split(sep, maxsplit=4)

&#x20;       return {

&#x20;           'signer': b64decode(v\[0]),

&#x20;           'signature': b64decode(v\[1]),

&#x20;           'content\_type': bytes\_to\_str(v\[2]),

&#x20;           'content\_encoding': bytes\_to\_str(v\[3]),

&#x20;           'body': v\[4],

&#x20;       }





def register\_auth(key=None, key\_password=None, cert=None, store=None,

&#x20;                 digest=DEFAULT\_SECURITY\_DIGEST,

&#x20;                 serializer='json'):

&#x20;   """Register security serializer."""

&#x20;   s = SecureSerializer(key and PrivateKey(key, password=key\_password),

&#x20;                        cert and Certificate(cert),

&#x20;                        store and FSCertStore(store),

&#x20;                        digest, serializer=serializer)

&#x20;   registry.register('auth', s.serialize, s.deserialize,

&#x20;                     content\_type='application/data',

&#x20;                     content\_encoding='utf-8')



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\security\\utils.py

```python

"""Utilities used by the message signing serializer."""

import sys

from contextlib import contextmanager



import cryptography.exceptions

from cryptography.hazmat.primitives import hashes



from celery.exceptions import SecurityError, reraise



\_\_all\_\_ = ('get\_digest\_algorithm', 'reraise\_errors',)





def get\_digest\_algorithm(digest='sha256'):

&#x20;   """Convert string to hash object of cryptography library."""

&#x20;   assert digest is not None

&#x20;   return getattr(hashes, digest.upper())()





@contextmanager

def reraise\_errors(msg='{0!r}', errors=None):

&#x20;   """Context reraising crypto errors as :exc:`SecurityError`."""

&#x20;   errors = (cryptography.exceptions,) if errors is None else errors

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       yield

&#x20;   except errors as exc:

&#x20;       reraise(SecurityError,

&#x20;               SecurityError(msg.format(exc)),

&#x20;               sys.exc\_info()\[2])



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\security\\\_\_init\_\_.py

```python

"""Message Signing Serializer."""

from kombu.serialization import disable\_insecure\_serializers as \_disable\_insecure\_serializers

from kombu.serialization import registry



from celery.exceptions import ImproperlyConfigured



from .serialization import register\_auth  # : need cryptography first



CRYPTOGRAPHY\_NOT\_INSTALLED = """\\

You need to install the cryptography library to use the auth serializer.

Please install by:



&#x20;   $ pip install cryptography

"""



SECURITY\_SETTING\_MISSING = """\\

Sorry, but you have to configure the

&#x20;   \* security\_key

&#x20;   \* security\_certificate, and the

&#x20;   \* security\_cert\_store

configuration settings to use the auth serializer.



Please see the configuration reference for more information.

"""



SETTING\_MISSING = """\\

You have to configure a special task serializer

for signing and verifying tasks:

&#x20;   \* task\_serializer = 'auth'



You have to accept only tasks which are serialized with 'auth'.

There is no point in signing messages if they are not verified.

&#x20;   \* accept\_content = \['auth']

"""



\_\_all\_\_ = ('setup\_security',)



try:

&#x20;   import cryptography  # noqa

except ImportError:

&#x20;   raise ImproperlyConfigured(CRYPTOGRAPHY\_NOT\_INSTALLED)





def setup\_security(allowed\_serializers=None, key=None, key\_password=None, cert=None, store=None,

&#x20;                  digest=None, serializer='json', app=None):

&#x20;   """See :meth:`@Celery.setup\_security`."""

&#x20;   if app is None:

&#x20;       from celery import current\_app

&#x20;       app = current\_app.\_get\_current\_object()



&#x20;   \_disable\_insecure\_serializers(allowed\_serializers)



&#x20;   # check conf for sane security settings

&#x20;   conf = app.conf

&#x20;   if conf.task\_serializer != 'auth' or conf.accept\_content != \['auth']:

&#x20;       raise ImproperlyConfigured(SETTING\_MISSING)



&#x20;   key = key or conf.security\_key

&#x20;   key\_password = key\_password or conf.security\_key\_password

&#x20;   cert = cert or conf.security\_certificate

&#x20;   store = store or conf.security\_cert\_store

&#x20;   digest = digest or conf.security\_digest



&#x20;   if not (key and cert and store):

&#x20;       raise ImproperlyConfigured(SECURITY\_SETTING\_MISSING)



&#x20;   with open(key) as kf:

&#x20;       with open(cert) as cf:

&#x20;           register\_auth(kf.read(), key\_password, cf.read(), store, digest, serializer)

&#x20;   registry.\_set\_default\_serializer('auth')





def disable\_untrusted\_serializers(whitelist=None):

&#x20;   \_disable\_insecure\_serializers(allowed=whitelist)



```

```python
"""Abstract classes."""

from abc import ABCMeta, abstractmethod

from collections.abc import Callable



\_\_all\_\_ = ('CallableTask', 'CallableSignature')





def \_hasattr(C, attr):

&#x20;   return any(attr in B.\_\_dict\_\_ for B in C.\_\_mro\_\_)





class \_AbstractClass(metaclass=ABCMeta):

&#x20;   \_\_required\_attributes\_\_ = frozenset()



&#x20;   @classmethod

&#x20;   def \_subclasshook\_using(cls, parent, C):

&#x20;       return (

&#x20;           cls is parent and

&#x20;           all(\_hasattr(C, attr) for attr in cls.\_\_required\_attributes\_\_)

&#x20;       ) or NotImplemented



&#x20;   @classmethod

&#x20;   def register(cls, other):

&#x20;       # we override `register` to return other for use as a decorator.

&#x20;       type(cls).register(cls, other)

&#x20;       return other





class CallableTask(\_AbstractClass, Callable):  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;   """Task interface."""



&#x20;   \_\_required\_attributes\_\_ = frozenset({

&#x20;       'delay', 'apply\_async', 'apply',

&#x20;   })



&#x20;   @abstractmethod

&#x20;   def delay(self, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   @abstractmethod

&#x20;   def apply\_async(self, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   @abstractmethod

&#x20;   def apply(self, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   @classmethod

&#x20;   def \_\_subclasshook\_\_(cls, C):

&#x20;       return cls.\_subclasshook\_using(CallableTask, C)





class CallableSignature(CallableTask):  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;   \_\_required\_attributes\_\_ = frozenset({

&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def name(self):

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   @abstractmethod

&#x20;   def type(self):

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   @abstractmethod

&#x20;   def app(self):

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   @abstractmethod

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   @abstractmethod

&#x20;       pass

&#x20;       pass

&#x20;       pass

&#x20;       pass

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;       pass

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   @abstractmethod

&#x20;   def clone(self, args=None, kwargs=None):

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   @abstractmethod

&#x20;   def freeze(self, id=None, group\_id=None, chord=None, root\_id=None,

&#x20;              group\_index=None):

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   @abstractmethod

&#x20;   def set(self, immutable=None, \*\*options):

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   @abstractmethod

&#x20;   def link(self, callback):

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   @abstractmethod

&#x20;   def link\_error(self, errback):

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   @abstractmethod

&#x20;   def \_\_or\_\_(self, other):

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   @abstractmethod

&#x20;   def \_\_invert\_\_(self):

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   @classmethod

&#x20;   def \_\_subclasshook\_\_(cls, C):

&#x20;       return cls.\_subclasshook\_using(CallableSignature, C)
```

\### 代码文件: celery\\utils\\annotations.py

```python

"""Code related to handling annotations."""



import sys

import types

import typing

from inspect import isclass





def is\_none\_type(value: typing.Any) -> bool:

&#x20;   """Check if the given value is a NoneType."""

&#x20;   if sys.version\_info < (3, 10):

&#x20;       # raise Exception('below 3.10', value, type(None))

&#x20;       return value is type(None)

&#x20;   return value == types.NoneType  # type: ignore\[no-any-return]





def get\_optional\_arg(annotation: typing.Any) -> typing.Any:

&#x20;   """Get the argument from an Optional\[...] annotation, or None if it is no such annotation."""

&#x20;   origin = typing.get\_origin(annotation)

&#x20;   if origin != typing.Union and (sys.version\_info >= (3, 10) and origin != types.UnionType):

&#x20;       return None



&#x20;   union\_args = typing.get\_args(annotation)

&#x20;   if len(union\_args) != 2:  # Union does \_not\_ have two members, so it's not an Optional

&#x20;       return None



&#x20;   has\_none\_arg = any(is\_none\_type(arg) for arg in union\_args)

&#x20;   # There will always be at least one type arg, as we have already established that this is a Union with exactly

&#x20;   # two members, and both cannot be None (`Union\[None, None]` does not work).

&#x20;   type\_arg = next(arg for arg in union\_args if not is\_none\_type(arg))  # pragma: no branch



&#x20;   if has\_none\_arg:

&#x20;       return type\_arg

&#x20;   return None





def annotation\_is\_class(annotation: typing.Any) -> bool:

&#x20;   """Test if a given annotation is a class that can be used in isinstance()/issubclass()."""

&#x20;   # isclass() returns True for generic type hints (e.g. `list\[str]`) until Python 3.10.

&#x20;   # NOTE: The guard for Python 3.9 is because types.GenericAlias is only added in Python 3.9. This is not a problem

&#x20;   #       as the syntax is added in the same version in the first place.

&#x20;   if (3, 9) <= sys.version\_info < (3, 11) and isinstance(annotation, types.GenericAlias):

&#x20;       return False

&#x20;   return isclass(annotation)





def annotation\_issubclass(annotation: typing.Any, cls: type) -> bool:

&#x20;   """Test if a given annotation is of the given subclass."""

&#x20;   return annotation\_is\_class(annotation) and issubclass(annotation, cls)



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\utils\\collections.py
