&#x20;       elif isinstance(exc, Ignore):

&#x20;           return self.acknowledge()

&#x20;       elif isinstance(exc, Retry):

&#x20;           return self.on\_retry(exc\_info)



&#x20;       # (acks\_late) acknowledge after result stored.

&#x20;       requeue = False

&#x20;       is\_worker\_lost = isinstance(exc, WorkerLostError)

&#x20;       if self.task.acks\_late:

&#x20;           reject = (

&#x20;               (self.task.reject\_on\_worker\_lost and is\_worker\_lost)

&#x20;               or (isinstance(exc, TimeLimitExceeded) and not self.task.acks\_on\_failure\_or\_timeout)

&#x20;           )

&#x20;           ack = self.task.acks\_on\_failure\_or\_timeout

&#x20;           if reject:

&#x20;               requeue = True

&#x20;               self.reject(requeue=requeue)

&#x20;               send\_failed\_event = False

&#x20;           elif ack:

&#x20;               self.acknowledge()

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               # supporting the behaviour where a task failed and

&#x20;               # need to be removed from prefetched local queue

&#x20;               self.reject(requeue=False)



&#x20;       # This is a special case where the process would not have had time

&#x20;       # to write the result.

&#x20;       if not requeue and (is\_worker\_lost or not return\_ok):

&#x20;           # only mark as failure if task has not been requeued

&#x20;           self.task.backend.mark\_as\_failure(

&#x20;               self.id, exc, request=self.\_context,

&#x20;               store\_result=self.store\_errors,

&#x20;           )



&#x20;           signals.task\_failure.send(sender=self.task, task\_id=self.id,

&#x20;                                     exception=exc, args=self.args,

&#x20;                                     kwargs=self.kwargs,

&#x20;                                     traceback=exc\_info.traceback,

&#x20;                                     einfo=exc\_info)



&#x20;       if send\_failed\_event:

&#x20;           self.send\_event(

&#x20;               'task-failed',

&#x20;               exception=safe\_repr(get\_pickled\_exception(exc\_info.exception)),

&#x20;               traceback=exc\_info.traceback,

&#x20;           )



&#x20;       if not return\_ok:

&#x20;           error('Task handler raised error: %r', exc,

&#x20;                 exc\_info=exc\_info.exc\_info)



&#x20;   def acknowledge(self):

&#x20;       """Acknowledge task."""

&#x20;       if not self.acknowledged:

&#x20;           self.\_on\_ack(logger, self.\_connection\_errors)

&#x20;           self.acknowledged = True



&#x20;   def reject(self, requeue=False):

&#x20;       if not self.acknowledged:

&#x20;           self.\_on\_reject(logger, self.\_connection\_errors, requeue)

&#x20;           self.acknowledged = True

&#x20;           self.send\_event('task-rejected', requeue=requeue)



&#x20;   def info(self, safe=False):

&#x20;       return {

&#x20;           'id': self.id,

&#x20;           'name': self.name,

&#x20;           'args': self.\_args if not safe else self.\_argsrepr,

&#x20;           'kwargs': self.\_kwargs if not safe else self.\_kwargsrepr,

&#x20;           'type': self.\_type,

&#x20;           'hostname': self.\_hostname,

&#x20;           'time\_start': self.time\_start,

&#x20;           'acknowledged': self.acknowledged,

&#x20;           'delivery\_info': self.delivery\_info,

&#x20;           'worker\_pid': self.worker\_pid,

&#x20;       }



&#x20;   def humaninfo(self):

&#x20;       return '{0.name}\[{0.id}]'.format(self)



&#x20;   def \_\_str\_\_(self):

&#x20;       """``str(self)``."""

&#x20;       return ' '.join(\[

&#x20;           self.humaninfo(),

&#x20;           f' ETA:\[{self.\_eta}]' if self.\_eta else '',

&#x20;           f' expires:\[{self.\_expires}]' if self.\_expires else '',

&#x20;       ]).strip()



&#x20;   def \_\_repr\_\_(self):

&#x20;       """``repr(self)``."""

&#x20;       return '<{}: {} {} {}>'.format(

&#x20;           type(self).\_\_name\_\_, self.humaninfo(),

&#x20;           self.\_argsrepr, self.\_kwargsrepr,

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def \_payload(self):

&#x20;       return self.\_\_payload



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def chord(self):

&#x20;       # used by backend.mark\_as\_failure when failure is reported

&#x20;       # by parent process

&#x20;       # pylint: disable=unpacking-non-sequence

&#x20;       #    payload is a property, so pylint doesn't think it's a tuple.

&#x20;       \_, \_, embed = self.\_payload

&#x20;       return embed.get('chord')



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def errbacks(self):

&#x20;       # used by backend.mark\_as\_failure when failure is reported

&#x20;       # by parent process

&#x20;       # pylint: disable=unpacking-non-sequence

&#x20;       #    payload is a property, so pylint doesn't think it's a tuple.

&#x20;       \_, \_, embed = self.\_payload

&#x20;       return embed.get('errbacks')



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def group(self):

&#x20;       # used by backend.on\_chord\_part\_return when failures reported

&#x20;       # by parent process

&#x20;       return self.\_request\_dict.get('group')



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def \_context(self):

&#x20;       """Context (:class:`\~celery.app.task.Context`) of this task."""

&#x20;       request = self.\_request\_dict

&#x20;       # pylint: disable=unpacking-non-sequence

&#x20;       #    payload is a property, so pylint doesn't think it's a tuple.

&#x20;       \_, \_, embed = self.\_payload

&#x20;       request.update(\*\*embed or {})

&#x20;       return Context(request)



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def group\_index(self):

&#x20;       # used by backend.on\_chord\_part\_return to order return values in group

&#x20;       return self.\_request\_dict.get('group\_index')





def create\_request\_cls(base, task, pool, hostname, eventer,

&#x20;                      ref=ref, revoked\_tasks=revoked\_tasks,

&#x20;                      task\_ready=task\_ready, trace=None, app=current\_app):

&#x20;   default\_time\_limit = task.time\_limit

&#x20;   default\_soft\_time\_limit = task.soft\_time\_limit

&#x20;   apply\_async = pool.apply\_async

&#x20;   acks\_late = task.acks\_late

&#x20;   events = eventer and eventer.enabled



&#x20;   if trace is None:

&#x20;       trace = fast\_trace\_task if app.use\_fast\_trace\_task else trace\_task\_ret



&#x20;   class Request(base):



&#x20;       def execute\_using\_pool(self, pool, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;           task\_id = self.task\_id

&#x20;           if self.revoked():

&#x20;               raise TaskRevokedError(task\_id)



&#x20;           time\_limit, soft\_time\_limit = self.time\_limits

&#x20;           result = apply\_async(

&#x20;               trace,

&#x20;               args=(self.type, task\_id, self.request\_dict, self.body,

&#x20;                     self.content\_type, self.content\_encoding),

&#x20;               accept\_callback=self.on\_accepted,

&#x20;               timeout\_callback=self.on\_timeout,

&#x20;               callback=self.on\_success,

&#x20;               error\_callback=self.on\_failure,

&#x20;               soft\_timeout=soft\_time\_limit or default\_soft\_time\_limit,

&#x20;               timeout=time\_limit or default\_time\_limit,

&#x20;               correlation\_id=task\_id,

&#x20;           )

&#x20;           # cannot create weakref to None

&#x20;           # pylint: disable=attribute-defined-outside-init

&#x20;           self.\_apply\_result = maybe(ref, result)

&#x20;           return result



&#x20;       def on\_success(self, failed\_\_retval\_\_runtime, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;           failed, retval, runtime = failed\_\_retval\_\_runtime

&#x20;           if failed:

&#x20;               exc = retval.exception

&#x20;               if isinstance(exc, ExceptionWithTraceback):

&#x20;                   exc = exc.exc

&#x20;               if isinstance(exc, (SystemExit, KeyboardInterrupt)):

&#x20;                   raise exc

&#x20;               return self.on\_failure(retval, return\_ok=True)

&#x20;           task\_ready(self, successful=True)



&#x20;           if acks\_late:

&#x20;               self.acknowledge()



&#x20;           if events:

&#x20;               self.send\_event(

&#x20;                   'task-succeeded', result=retval, runtime=runtime,

&#x20;               )



&#x20;   return Request

```python

"""Internal worker state (global).



This includes the currently active and reserved tasks,

statistics, and revoked tasks.

"""

import os

import platform

import shelve

import sys

import weakref

import zlib

from collections import Counter



from kombu.serialization import pickle, pickle\_protocol

from kombu.utils.objects import cached\_property



from celery import \_\_version\_\_

from celery.exceptions import WorkerShutdown, WorkerTerminate

from celery.utils.collections import LimitedSet



\_\_all\_\_ = (

&#x20;   'SOFTWARE\_INFO', 'reserved\_requests', 'active\_requests',

&#x20;   'total\_count', 'revoked', 'task\_reserved', 'maybe\_shutdown',

&#x20;   'task\_accepted', 'task\_ready', 'Persistent',

)



\#: Worker software/platform information.

SOFTWARE\_INFO = {

&#x20;   'sw\_ident': 'py-celery',

&#x20;   'sw\_ver': \_\_version\_\_,

&#x20;   'sw\_sys': platform.system(),

}



\#: maximum number of revokes to keep in memory.

REVOKES\_MAX = int(os.environ.get('CELERY\_WORKER\_REVOKES\_MAX', 50000))



\#: maximum number of successful tasks to keep in memory.

SUCCESSFUL\_MAX = int(os.environ.get('CELERY\_WORKER\_SUCCESSFUL\_MAX', 1000))



\#: how many seconds a revoke will be active before

\#: being expired when the max limit has been exceeded.

REVOKE\_EXPIRES = float(os.environ.get('CELERY\_WORKER\_REVOKE\_EXPIRES', 10800))



\#: how many seconds a successful task will be cached in memory

\#: before being expired when the max limit has been exceeded.

SUCCESSFUL\_EXPIRES = float(os.environ.get('CELERY\_WORKER\_SUCCESSFUL\_EXPIRES', 10800))



\#: Mapping of reserved task\_id->Request.

requests = {}



\#: set of all reserved :class:`\~celery.worker.request.Request`'s.

reserved\_requests = weakref.WeakSet()



\#: set of currently active :class:`\~celery.worker.request.Request`'s.

active\_requests = weakref.WeakSet()



\#: A limited set of successful :class:`\~celery.worker.request.Request`'s.

successful\_requests = LimitedSet(maxlen=SUCCESSFUL\_MAX,

&#x20;                                expires=SUCCESSFUL\_EXPIRES)



\#: count of tasks accepted by the worker, sorted by type.

total\_count = Counter()



\#: count of all tasks accepted by the worker

all\_total\_count = \[0]



\#: the list of currently revoked tasks.  Persistent if ``statedb`` set.

revoked = LimitedSet(maxlen=REVOKES\_MAX, expires=REVOKE\_EXPIRES)



\#: Mapping of stamped headers flagged for revoking.

revoked\_stamps = {}



should\_stop = None

should\_terminate = None





def reset\_state():

&#x20;   requests.clear()

&#x20;   reserved\_requests.clear()

&#x20;   active\_requests.clear()

&#x20;   successful\_requests.clear()

&#x20;   total\_count.clear()

&#x20;   all\_total\_count\[:] = \[0]

&#x20;   revoked.clear()

&#x20;   revoked\_stamps.clear()





def maybe\_shutdown():

&#x20;   """Shutdown if flags have been set."""

&#x20;   if should\_terminate is not None and should\_terminate is not False:

&#x20;       raise WorkerTerminate(should\_terminate)

&#x20;   elif should\_stop is not None and should\_stop is not False:

&#x20;       raise WorkerShutdown(should\_stop)





def task\_reserved(request,

&#x20;                 add\_request=requests.\_\_setitem\_\_,

&#x20;                 add\_reserved\_request=reserved\_requests.add):

&#x20;   """Update global state when a task has been reserved."""

&#x20;   add\_request(request.id, request)

&#x20;   add\_reserved\_request(request)





def task\_accepted(request,

&#x20;                 \_all\_total\_count=None,

&#x20;                 add\_request=requests.\_\_setitem\_\_,

&#x20;                 add\_active\_request=active\_requests.add,

&#x20;                 add\_to\_total\_count=total\_count.update):

&#x20;   """Update global state when a task has been accepted."""

&#x20;   if not \_all\_total\_count:

&#x20;       \_all\_total\_count = all\_total\_count

&#x20;   add\_request(request.id, request)

&#x20;   add\_active\_request(request)

&#x20;   add\_to\_total\_count({request.name: 1})

&#x20;   all\_total\_count\[0] += 1





def task\_ready(request,

&#x20;              successful=False,

&#x20;              remove\_request=requests.pop,

&#x20;              discard\_active\_request=active\_requests.discard,

&#x20;              discard\_reserved\_request=reserved\_requests.discard):

&#x20;   """Update global state when a task is ready."""

&#x20;   if successful:

&#x20;       successful\_requests.add(request.id)



&#x20;   remove\_request(request.id, None)

&#x20;   discard\_active\_request(request)

&#x20;   discard\_reserved\_request(request)





C\_BENCH = os.environ.get('C\_BENCH') or os.environ.get('CELERY\_BENCH')

C\_BENCH\_EVERY = int(os.environ.get('C\_BENCH\_EVERY') or

&#x20;                   os.environ.get('CELERY\_BENCH\_EVERY') or 1000)

if C\_BENCH:  # pragma: no cover

&#x20;   import atexit

&#x20;   from time import monotonic



&#x20;   from billiard.process import current\_process



&#x20;   from celery.utils.debug import memdump, sample\_mem



&#x20;   all\_count = 0

&#x20;   bench\_first = None

&#x20;   bench\_start = None

&#x20;   bench\_last = None

&#x20;   bench\_every = C\_BENCH\_EVERY

&#x20;   bench\_sample = \[]

&#x20;   \_\_reserved = task\_reserved

&#x20;   \_\_ready = task\_ready



&#x20;   if current\_process().\_name == 'MainProcess':

&#x20;       @atexit.register

&#x20;       def on\_shutdown():

&#x20;           if bench\_first is not None and bench\_last is not None:

&#x20;               print('- Time spent in benchmark: {!r}'.format(

&#x20;                   bench\_last - bench\_first))

&#x20;               print('- Avg: {}'.format(

&#x20;                   sum(bench\_sample) / len(bench\_sample)))

&#x20;               memdump()



&#x20;   def task\_reserved(request):

&#x20;       """Called when a task is reserved by the worker."""

&#x20;       global bench\_start

&#x20;       global bench\_first

&#x20;       now = None

&#x20;       if bench\_start is None:

&#x20;           bench\_start = now = monotonic()

&#x20;       if bench\_first is None:

&#x20;           bench\_first = now



&#x20;       return \_\_reserved(request)



&#x20;   def task\_ready(request):

&#x20;       """Called when a task is completed."""

&#x20;       global all\_count

&#x20;       global bench\_start

&#x20;       global bench\_last

&#x20;       all\_count += 1

&#x20;       if not all\_count % bench\_every:

&#x20;           now = monotonic()

&#x20;           diff = now - bench\_start

&#x20;           print('- Time spent processing {} tasks (since first '

&#x20;                 'task received): \~{:.4f}s\\n'.format(bench\_every, diff))

&#x20;           sys.stdout.flush()

&#x20;           bench\_start = bench\_last = now

&#x20;           bench\_sample.append(diff)

&#x20;           sample\_mem()

&#x20;       return \_\_ready(request)





class Persistent:

&#x20;   """Stores worker state between restarts.



&#x20;   This is the persistent data stored by the worker when

&#x20;   :option:`celery worker --statedb` is enabled.



&#x20;   Currently only stores revoked task id's.

&#x20;   """



&#x20;   storage = shelve

&#x20;   protocol = pickle\_protocol

&#x20;   compress = zlib.compress

&#x20;   decompress = zlib.decompress

&#x20;   \_is\_open = False



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, state, filename, clock=None):

&#x20;       self.state = state

&#x20;       self.filename = filename

&#x20;       self.clock = clock

&#x20;       self.merge()



&#x20;   def open(self):

&#x20;       return self.storage.open(

&#x20;           self.filename, protocol=self.protocol, writeback=True,

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def merge(self):

&#x20;       self.\_merge\_with(self.db)



&#x20;   def sync(self):

&#x20;       self.\_sync\_with(self.db)

&#x20;       self.db.sync()



&#x20;   def close(self):

&#x20;       if self.\_is\_open:

&#x20;           self.db.close()

&#x20;           self.\_is\_open = False



&#x20;   def save(self):

&#x20;       self.sync()

&#x20;       self.close()



&#x20;   def \_merge\_with(self, d):

&#x20;       self.\_merge\_revoked(d)

&#x20;       self.\_merge\_clock(d)

&#x20;       return d



&#x20;   def \_sync\_with(self, d):

&#x20;       self.\_revoked\_tasks.purge()

&#x20;       d.update({

&#x20;           '\_\_proto\_\_': 3,

&#x20;           'zrevoked': self.compress(self.\_dumps(self.\_revoked\_tasks)),

&#x20;           'clock': self.clock.forward() if self.clock else 0,

&#x20;       })

&#x20;       return d



&#x20;   def \_merge\_clock(self, d):

&#x20;       if self.clock:

&#x20;           d\['clock'] = self.clock.adjust(d.get('clock') or 0)



&#x20;   def \_merge\_revoked(self, d):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           self.\_merge\_revoked\_v3(d\['zrevoked'])

&#x20;       except KeyError:

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               self.\_merge\_revoked\_v2(d.pop('revoked'))

&#x20;           except KeyError:

&#x20;               pass

&#x20;       # purge expired items at boot

&#x20;       self.\_revoked\_tasks.purge()



&#x20;   def \_merge\_revoked\_v3(self, zrevoked):

&#x20;       if zrevoked:

&#x20;           self.\_revoked\_tasks.update(pickle.loads(self.decompress(zrevoked)))



&#x20;   def \_merge\_revoked\_v2(self, saved):

&#x20;       if not isinstance(saved, LimitedSet):

&#x20;           # (pre 3.0.18) used to be stored as a dict

&#x20;           return self.\_merge\_revoked\_v1(saved)

&#x20;       self.\_revoked\_tasks.update(saved)



&#x20;   def \_merge\_revoked\_v1(self, saved):

&#x20;       add = self.\_revoked\_tasks.add

&#x20;       for item in saved:

&#x20;           add(item)



&#x20;   def \_dumps(self, obj):

&#x20;       return pickle.dumps(obj, protocol=self.protocol)



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def \_revoked\_tasks(self):

&#x20;       return self.state.revoked



&#x20;   @cached\_property

&#x20;   def db(self):

&#x20;       self.\_is\_open = True

&#x20;       return self.open()



```

```python

"""Task execution strategy (optimization)."""

import logging



from kombu.asynchronous.timer import to\_timestamp



from celery import signals

from celery.app import trace as \_app\_trace

from celery.exceptions import InvalidTaskError

from celery.utils.imports import symbol\_by\_name

from celery.utils.log import get\_logger

from celery.utils.saferepr import saferepr

from celery.utils.time import timezone



from .request import create\_request\_cls

from .state import task\_reserved



\_\_all\_\_ = ('default',)



logger = get\_logger(\_\_name\_\_)



\# pylint: disable=redefined-outer-name

\# We cache globals and attribute lookups, so disable this warning.





def hybrid\_to\_proto2(message, body):

&#x20;   """Create a fresh protocol 2 message from a hybrid protocol 1/2 message."""

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       args, kwargs = body.get('args', ()), body.get('kwargs', {})

&#x20;       kwargs.items  # pylint: disable=pointless-statement

&#x20;   except KeyError:

&#x20;       raise InvalidTaskError('Message does not have args/kwargs')

&#x20;   except AttributeError:

&#x20;       raise InvalidTaskError(

&#x20;           'Task keyword arguments must be a mapping',

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   headers = {

&#x20;       'lang': body.get('lang'),

&#x20;       'task': body.get('task'),

&#x20;       'id': body.get('id'),

&#x20;       'root\_id': body.get('root\_id'),

&#x20;       'parent\_id': body.get('parent\_id'),

&#x20;       'group': body.get('group'),

&#x20;       'meth': body.get('meth'),

&#x20;       'shadow': body.get('shadow'),

&#x20;       'eta': body.get('eta'),

&#x20;       'expires': body.get('expires'),

&#x20;       'retries': body.get('retries', 0),

&#x20;       'timelimit': body.get('timelimit', (None, None)),

&#x20;       'argsrepr': body.get('argsrepr'),

&#x20;       'kwargsrepr': body.get('kwargsrepr'),

&#x20;       'origin': body.get('origin'),

&#x20;   }

&#x20;   headers.update(message.headers or {})



&#x20;   embed = {

&#x20;       'callbacks': body.get('callbacks'),

&#x20;       'errbacks': body.get('errbacks'),

&#x20;       'chord': body.get('chord'),

&#x20;       'chain': None,

&#x20;   }



&#x20;   return (args, kwargs, embed), headers, True, body.get('utc', True)





def proto1\_to\_proto2(message, body):

&#x20;   """Convert Task message protocol 1 arguments to protocol 2.



&#x20;   Returns:

&#x20;       Tuple: of ``(body, headers, already\_decoded\_status, utc)``

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       args, kwargs = body.get('args', ()), body.get('kwargs', {})

&#x20;       kwargs.items  # pylint: disable=pointless-statement

&#x20;   except KeyError:

&#x20;       raise InvalidTaskError('Message does not have args/kwargs')

&#x20;   except AttributeError:

&#x20;       raise InvalidTaskError(

&#x20;           'Task keyword arguments must be a mapping',

&#x20;       )

&#x20;   body.update(

&#x20;       argsrepr=saferepr(args),

&#x20;       kwargsrepr=saferepr(kwargs),

&#x20;       headers=message.headers,

&#x20;   )

&#x20;   try:

&#x20;       body\['group'] = body\['taskset']

&#x20;   except KeyError:

&#x20;       pass

&#x20;   embed = {

&#x20;       'callbacks': body.get('callbacks'),

&#x20;       'errbacks': body.get('errbacks'),

&#x20;       'chord': body.get('chord'),

&#x20;       'chain': None,

&#x20;   }

&#x20;   return (args, kwargs, embed), body, True, body.get('utc', True)





def default(task, app, consumer,

&#x20;           info=logger.info, error=logger.error, task\_reserved=task\_reserved,

&#x20;           to\_system\_tz=timezone.to\_system, bytes=bytes,

&#x20;           proto1\_to\_proto2=proto1\_to\_proto2):

&#x20;   """Default task execution strategy.



&#x20;   Note:

&#x20;       Strategies are here as an optimization, so sadly

&#x20;       it's not very easy to override.

&#x20;   """

&#x20;   hostname = consumer.hostname

&#x20;   connection\_errors = consumer.connection\_errors

&#x20;   \_does\_info = logger.isEnabledFor(logging.INFO)

&#x20;   # task event related

&#x20;   # (optimized to avoid calling request.send\_event)

&#x20;   eventer = consumer.event\_dispatcher

&#x20;   events = eventer and eventer.enabled

&#x20;   send\_event = eventer and eventer.send

&#x20;   task\_sends\_events = events and task.send\_events



&#x20;   call\_at = consumer.timer.call\_at

&#x20;   apply\_eta\_task = consumer.apply\_eta\_task

&#x20;   rate\_limits\_enabled = not consumer.disable\_rate\_limits

&#x20;   get\_bucket = consumer.task\_buckets.\_\_getitem\_\_

&#x20;   handle = consumer.on\_task\_request

&#x20;   limit\_task = consumer.\_limit\_task

&#x20;   limit\_post\_eta = consumer.\_limit\_post\_eta

&#x20;   Request = symbol\_by\_name(task.Request)

&#x20;   Req = create\_request\_cls(Request, task, consumer.pool, hostname, eventer,

&#x20;                            app=app)



&#x20;   revoked\_tasks = consumer.controller.state.revoked



&#x20;   def task\_message\_handler(message, body, ack, reject, callbacks,

&#x20;                            to\_timestamp=to\_timestamp):

&#x20;       if body is None and 'args' not in message.payload:

&#x20;           body, headers, decoded, utc = (

&#x20;               message.body, message.headers, False, app.uses\_utc\_timezone(),

&#x20;           )

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           if 'args' in message.payload:

&#x20;               body, headers, decoded, utc = hybrid\_to\_proto2(message,

&#x20;                                                              message.payload)

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               body, headers, decoded, utc = proto1\_to\_proto2(message, body)



&#x20;       req = Req(

&#x20;           message,

&#x20;           on\_ack=ack, on\_reject=reject, app=app, hostname=hostname,

&#x20;           eventer=eventer, task=task, connection\_errors=connection\_errors,

&#x20;           body=body, headers=headers, decoded=decoded, utc=utc,

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       if \_does\_info:

&#x20;           # Similar to `app.trace.info()`, we pass the formatting args as the

&#x20;           # `extra` kwarg for custom log handlers

&#x20;           context = {

&#x20;               'id': req.id,

&#x20;               'name': req.name,

&#x20;               'args': req.argsrepr,

&#x20;               'kwargs': req.kwargsrepr,

&#x20;               'eta': req.eta,

&#x20;           }

&#x20;           info(\_app\_trace.LOG\_RECEIVED, context, extra={'data': context})

&#x20;       if (req.expires or req.id in revoked\_tasks) and req.revoked():

&#x20;           return



&#x20;       signals.task\_received.send(sender=consumer, request=req)



&#x20;       if task\_sends\_events:

&#x20;           send\_event(

&#x20;               'task-received',

&#x20;               uuid=req.id, name=req.name,

&#x20;               args=req.argsrepr, kwargs=req.kwargsrepr,

&#x20;               root\_id=req.root\_id, parent\_id=req.parent\_id,

&#x20;               retries=req.request\_dict.get('retries', 0),

&#x20;               eta=req.eta and req.eta.isoformat(),

&#x20;               expires=req.expires and req.expires.isoformat(),

&#x20;           )



&#x20;       bucket = None

&#x20;       eta = None

&#x20;       if req.eta:

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               if req.utc:

&#x20;                   eta = to\_timestamp(to\_system\_tz(req.eta))

&#x20;               else:

&#x20;                   eta = to\_timestamp(req.eta, app.timezone)

&#x20;           except (OverflowError, ValueError) as exc:

&#x20;               error("Couldn't convert ETA %r to timestamp: %r. Task: %r",

&#x20;                     req.eta, exc, req.info(safe=True), exc\_info=True)

&#x20;               req.reject(requeue=False)

&#x20;       if rate\_limits\_enabled:

&#x20;           bucket = get\_bucket(task.name)



&#x20;       if eta and bucket:

&#x20;           consumer.qos.increment\_eventually()

&#x20;           return call\_at(eta, limit\_post\_eta, (req, bucket, 1),

&#x20;                          priority=6)



&#x20;       if eta:

&#x20;           consumer.qos.increment\_eventually()

&#x20;           call\_at(eta, apply\_eta\_task, (req,), priority=6)

&#x20;           return task\_message\_handler

&#x20;       if bucket:

&#x20;           return limit\_task(req, bucket, 1)



&#x20;       task\_reserved(req)

&#x20;       if callbacks:

&#x20;           \[callback(req) for callback in callbacks]

&#x20;       handle(req)

&#x20;   return task\_message\_handler



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\worker\\worker.py

```python

"""WorkController can be used to instantiate in-process workers.



The command-line interface for the worker is in :mod:`celery.bin.worker`,

while the worker program is in :mod:`celery.apps.worker`.



The worker program is responsible for adding signal handlers,

setting up logging, etc.  This is a bare-bones worker without

global side-effects (i.e., except for the global state stored in

:mod:`celery.worker.state`).



The worker consists of several components, all managed by bootsteps

(mod:`celery.bootsteps`).

"""



import os

import sys

from datetime import datetime, timezone

from time import sleep



from billiard import cpu\_count

from kombu.utils.compat import detect\_environment



from celery import bootsteps

from celery import concurrency as \_concurrency

from celery import signals

from celery.bootsteps import RUN, TERMINATE

from celery.exceptions import ImproperlyConfigured, TaskRevokedError, WorkerTerminate

from celery.platforms import EX\_FAILURE, create\_pidlock

from celery.utils.imports import reload\_from\_cwd

from celery.utils.log import mlevel

from celery.utils.log import worker\_logger as logger

from celery.utils.nodenames import default\_nodename, worker\_direct

from celery.utils.text import str\_to\_list

from celery.utils.threads import default\_socket\_timeout



from . import state



try:

&#x20;   import resource

except ImportError:

&#x20;   resource = None





\_\_all\_\_ = ('WorkController',)



\#: Default socket timeout at shutdown.

SHUTDOWN\_SOCKET\_TIMEOUT = 5.0



SELECT\_UNKNOWN\_QUEUE = """

Trying to select queue subset of {0!r}, but queue {1} isn't

defined in the `task\_queues` setting.



If you want to automatically declare unknown queues you can

enable the `task\_create\_missing\_queues` setting.

"""



DESELECT\_UNKNOWN\_QUEUE = """

Trying to deselect queue subset of {0!r}, but queue {1} isn't

defined in the `task\_queues` setting.

"""





class WorkController:

&#x20;   """Unmanaged worker instance."""



&#x20;   app = None



&#x20;   pidlock = None

&#x20;   blueprint = None

&#x20;   pool = None

&#x20;   semaphore = None



&#x20;   #: contains the exit code if a :exc:`SystemExit` event is handled.

&#x20;   exitcode = None



&#x20;   class Blueprint(bootsteps.Blueprint):

&#x20;       """Worker bootstep blueprint."""



&#x20;       name = 'Worker'

&#x20;       default\_steps = {

&#x20;           'celery.worker.components:Hub',

&#x20;           'celery.worker.components:Pool',

&#x20;           'celery.worker.components:Beat',

&#x20;           'celery.worker.components:Timer',

&#x20;           'celery.worker.components:StateDB',

&#x20;           'celery.worker.components:Consumer',

&#x20;           'celery.worker.autoscale:WorkerComponent',

&#x20;       }



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, app=None, hostname=None, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       self.app = app or self.app

&#x20;       self.hostname = default\_nodename(hostname)

&#x20;       self.startup\_time = datetime.now(timezone.utc)

&#x20;       self.app.loader.init\_worker()

&#x20;       self.on\_before\_init(\*\*kwargs)

&#x20;       self.setup\_defaults(\*\*kwargs)

&#x20;       self.on\_after\_init(\*\*kwargs)



&#x20;       self.setup\_instance(\*\*self.prepare\_args(\*\*kwargs))



&#x20;   def setup\_instance(self, queues=None, ready\_callback=None, pidfile=None,

&#x20;                      include=None, use\_eventloop=None, exclude\_queues=None,

&#x20;                      \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       self.pidfile = pidfile

&#x20;       self.setup\_queues(queues, exclude\_queues)

&#x20;       self.setup\_includes(str\_to\_list(include))



&#x20;       # Set default concurrency

&#x20;       if not self.concurrency:

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               self.concurrency = cpu\_count()

&#x20;           except NotImplementedError:

&#x20;               self.concurrency = 2



&#x20;       # Options

&#x20;       self.loglevel = mlevel(self.loglevel)

&#x20;       self.ready\_callback = ready\_callback or self.on\_consumer\_ready



&#x20;       # this connection won't establish, only used for params

&#x20;       self.\_conninfo = self.app.connection\_for\_read()

&#x20;       self.use\_eventloop = (

&#x20;           self.should\_use\_eventloop() if use\_eventloop is None

&#x20;           else use\_eventloop

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       self.options = kwargs



&#x20;       signals.worker\_init.send(sender=self)



&#x20;       # Initialize bootsteps

&#x20;       self.pool\_cls = \_concurrency.get\_implementation(self.pool\_cls)

&#x20;       self.steps = \[]

&#x20;       self.on\_init\_blueprint()

&#x20;       self.blueprint = self.Blueprint(

&#x20;           steps=self.app.steps\['worker'],

&#x20;           on\_start=self.on\_start,

&#x20;           on\_close=self.on\_close,

&#x20;           on\_stopped=self.on\_stopped,

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       self.blueprint.apply(self, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def on\_init\_blueprint(self):

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   def on\_before\_init(self, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   def on\_after\_init(self, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   def on\_start(self):

&#x20;       if self.pidfile:

&#x20;           self.pidlock = create\_pidlock(self.pidfile)



&#x20;   def on\_consumer\_ready(self, consumer):

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   def on\_close(self):

&#x20;       self.app.loader.shutdown\_worker()



&#x20;   def on\_stopped(self):

&#x20;       self.timer.stop()

&#x20;       self.consumer.shutdown()



&#x20;       if self.pidlock:

&#x20;           self.pidlock.release()



&#x20;   def setup\_queues(self, include, exclude=None):

&#x20;       include = str\_to\_list(include)

&#x20;       exclude = str\_to\_list(exclude)

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           self.app.amqp.queues.select(include)

&#x20;       except KeyError as exc:

&#x20;           raise ImproperlyConfigured(

&#x20;               SELECT\_UNKNOWN\_QUEUE.strip().format(include, exc))

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           self.app.amqp.queues.deselect(exclude)

&#x20;       except KeyError as exc:

&#x20;           raise ImproperlyConfigured(

&#x20;               DESELECT\_UNKNOWN\_QUEUE.strip().format(exclude, exc))

&#x20;       if self.app.conf.worker\_direct:

&#x20;           self.app.amqp.queues.select\_add(worker\_direct(self.hostname))



&#x20;   def setup\_includes(self, includes):

&#x20;       # Update celery\_include to have all known task modules, so that we

&#x20;       # ensure all task modules are imported in case an execv happens.

&#x20;       prev = tuple(self.app.conf.include)

&#x20;       if includes:

&#x20;           prev += tuple(includes)

&#x20;           \[self.app.loader.import\_task\_module(m) for m in includes]

&#x20;       self.include = includes

&#x20;       task\_modules = {task.\_\_class\_\_.\_\_module\_\_

&#x20;                       for task in self.app.tasks.values()}

&#x20;       self.app.conf.include = tuple(set(prev) | task\_modules)



&#x20;   def prepare\_args(self, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       return kwargs



&#x20;   def \_send\_worker\_shutdown(self):

&#x20;       signals.worker\_shutdown.send(sender=self)



&#x20;   def start(self):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           self.blueprint.start(self)

&#x20;       except WorkerTerminate:

&#x20;           self.terminate()

&#x20;       except Exception as exc:

&#x20;           logger.critical('Unrecoverable error: %r', exc, exc\_info=True)

&#x20;           self.stop(exitcode=EX\_FAILURE)

&#x20;       except SystemExit as exc:

&#x20;           self.stop(exitcode=exc.code)

&#x20;       except KeyboardInterrupt:

&#x20;           self.stop(exitcode=EX\_FAILURE)



&#x20;   def register\_with\_event\_loop(self, hub):

&#x20;       self.blueprint.send\_all(

&#x20;           self, 'register\_with\_event\_loop', args=(hub,),

&#x20;           description='hub.register',

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def \_process\_task\_sem(self, req):

&#x20;       return self.\_quick\_acquire(self.\_process\_task, req)



&#x20;   def \_process\_task(self, req):

&#x20;       """Process task by sending it to the pool of workers."""

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           req.execute\_using\_pool(self.pool)

&#x20;       except TaskRevokedError:

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               self.\_quick\_release()   # Issue 877

&#x20;           except AttributeError:

&#x20;               pass



&#x20;   def signal\_consumer\_close(self):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           self.consumer.close()

&#x20;       except AttributeError:

&#x20;           pass



&#x20;   def should\_use\_eventloop(self):

&#x20;       return (detect\_environment() == 'default' and

&#x20;               self.\_conninfo.transport.implements.asynchronous and

&#x20;               not self.app.IS\_WINDOWS)



&#x20;   def stop(self, in\_sighandler=False, exitcode=None):

&#x20;       """Graceful shutdown of the worker server (Warm shutdown)."""

&#x20;       if exitcode is not None:

&#x20;           self.exitcode = exitcode

&#x20;       if self.blueprint.state == RUN:

&#x20;           self.signal\_consumer\_close()

&#x20;           if not in\_sighandler or self.pool.signal\_safe:

&#x20;               self.\_shutdown(warm=True)

&#x20;       self.\_send\_worker\_shutdown()



&#x20;   def terminate(self, in\_sighandler=False):

&#x20;       """Not so graceful shutdown of the worker server (Cold shutdown)."""

&#x20;       if self.blueprint.state != TERMINATE:

&#x20;           self.signal\_consumer\_close()

&#x20;           if not in\_sighandler or self.pool.signal\_safe:

&#x20;               self.\_shutdown(warm=False)



&#x20;   def \_shutdown(self, warm=True):

&#x20;       # if blueprint does not exist it means that we had an

&#x20;       # error before the bootsteps could be initialized.

&#x20;       if self.blueprint is not None:

&#x20;           with default\_socket\_timeout(SHUTDOWN\_SOCKET\_TIMEOUT):  # Issue 975

&#x20;               self.blueprint.stop(self, terminate=not warm)

&#x20;               self.blueprint.join()



&#x20;   def reload(self, modules=None, reload=False, reloader=None):

&#x20;       list(self.\_reload\_modules(

&#x20;           modules, force\_reload=reload, reloader=reloader))



&#x20;       if self.consumer:

&#x20;           self.consumer.update\_strategies()

&#x20;           self.consumer.reset\_rate\_limits()

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           self.pool.restart()

&#x20;       except NotImplementedError:

&#x20;           pass



&#x20;   def \_reload\_modules(self, modules=None, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       return (

&#x20;           self.\_maybe\_reload\_module(m, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;           for m in set(self.app.loader.task\_modules

&#x20;                        if modules is None else (modules or ()))

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def \_maybe\_reload\_module(self, module, force\_reload=False, reloader=None):

&#x20;       if module not in sys.modules:

&#x20;           logger.debug('importing module %s', module)

&#x20;           return self.app.loader.import\_from\_cwd(module)

&#x20;       elif force\_reload:

&#x20;           logger.debug('reloading module %s', module)

&#x20;           return reload\_from\_cwd(sys.modules\[module], reloader)



&#x20;   def info(self):

&#x20;       uptime = datetime.now(timezone.utc) - self.startup\_time

&#x20;       return {'total': self.state.total\_count,

&#x20;               'pid': os.getpid(),

&#x20;               'clock': str(self.app.clock),

&#x20;               'uptime': round(uptime.total\_seconds())}



&#x20;   def rusage(self):

&#x20;       if resource is None:

&#x20;           raise NotImplementedError('rusage not supported by this platform')

&#x20;       s = resource.getrusage(resource.RUSAGE\_SELF)

&#x20;       return {

&#x20;           'utime': s.ru\_utime,

&#x20;           'stime': s.ru\_stime,

&#x20;           'maxrss': s.ru\_maxrss,

&#x20;           'ixrss': s.ru\_ixrss,

&#x20;           'idrss': s.ru\_idrss,

&#x20;           'isrss': s.ru\_isrss,

&#x20;           'minflt': s.ru\_minflt,

&#x20;           'majflt': s.ru\_majflt,

&#x20;           'nswap': s.ru\_nswap,

&#x20;           'inblock': s.ru\_inblock,

&#x20;           'oublock': s.ru\_oublock,

&#x20;           'msgsnd': s.ru\_msgsnd,

&#x20;           'msgrcv': s.ru\_msgrcv,

&#x20;           'nsignals': s.ru\_nsignals,

&#x20;           'nvcsw': s.ru\_nvcsw,

&#x20;           'nivcsw': s.ru\_nivcsw,

&#x20;       }



&#x20;   def stats(self):

&#x20;       info = self.info()

&#x20;       info.update(self.blueprint.info(self))

&#x20;       info.update(self.consumer.blueprint.info(self.consumer))

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           info\['rusage'] = self.rusage()

&#x20;       except NotImplementedError:

&#x20;           info\['rusage'] = 'N/A'

&#x20;       return info



&#x20;   def \_\_repr\_\_(self):

&#x20;       """``repr(worker)``."""

&#x20;       return '<Worker: {self.hostname} ({state})>'.format(

&#x20;           self=self,

&#x20;           state=self.blueprint.human\_state() if self.blueprint else 'INIT',

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def \_\_str\_\_(self):

&#x20;       """``str(worker) == worker.hostname``."""

&#x20;       return self.hostname



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def state(self):

&#x20;       return state



&#x20;   def setup\_defaults(self, concurrency=None, loglevel='WARN', logfile=None,

&#x20;                      task\_events=None, pool=None, consumer\_cls=None,

&#x20;                      timer\_cls=None, timer\_precision=None,

&#x20;                      autoscaler\_cls=None,

&#x20;                      pool\_putlocks=None,

&#x20;                      pool\_restarts=None,

&#x20;                      optimization=None, O=None,  # O maps to -O=fair

&#x20;                      statedb=None,

&#x20;                      time\_limit=None,

&#x20;                      soft\_time\_limit=None,

&#x20;                      scheduler=None,

&#x20;                      pool\_cls=None,              # XXX use pool

&#x20;                      state\_db=None,              # XXX use statedb

&#x20;                      task\_time\_limit=None,       # XXX use time\_limit

&#x20;                      task\_soft\_time\_limit=None,  # XXX use soft\_time\_limit

&#x20;                      scheduler\_cls=None,         # XXX use scheduler

&#x20;                      schedule\_filename=None,

&#x20;                      max\_tasks\_per\_child=None,

&#x20;                      prefetch\_multiplier=None, disable\_rate\_limits=None,

&#x20;                      worker\_lost\_wait=None,

&#x20;                      max\_memory\_per\_child=None, \*\*\_kw):

&#x20;       either = self.app.either

&#x20;       self.loglevel = loglevel

&#x20;       self.logfile = logfile



&#x20;       self.concurrency = either('worker\_concurrency', concurrency)

&#x20;       self.task\_events = either('worker\_send\_task\_events', task\_events)

&#x20;       self.pool\_cls = either('worker\_pool', pool, pool\_cls)

&#x20;       self.consumer\_cls = either('worker\_consumer', consumer\_cls)

&#x20;       self.timer\_cls = either('worker\_timer', timer\_cls)

&#x20;       self.timer\_precision = either(

&#x20;           'worker\_timer\_precision', timer\_precision,

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       self.optimization = optimization or O

&#x20;       self.autoscaler\_cls = either('worker\_autoscaler', autoscaler\_cls)

&#x20;       self.pool\_putlocks = either('worker\_pool\_putlocks', pool\_putlocks)

&#x20;       self.pool\_restarts = either('worker\_pool\_restarts', pool\_restarts)

&#x20;       self.statedb = either('worker\_state\_db', statedb, state\_db)

&#x20;       self.schedule\_filename = either(

&#x20;           'beat\_schedule\_filename', schedule\_filename,

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       self.scheduler = either('beat\_scheduler', scheduler, scheduler\_cls)

&#x20;       self.time\_limit = either(

&#x20;           'task\_time\_limit', time\_limit, task\_time\_limit)

&#x20;       self.soft\_time\_limit = either(

&#x20;           'task\_soft\_time\_limit', soft\_time\_limit, task\_soft\_time\_limit,

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       self.max\_tasks\_per\_child = either(

&#x20;           'worker\_max\_tasks\_per\_child', max\_tasks\_per\_child,

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       self.max\_memory\_per\_child = either(

&#x20;           'worker\_max\_memory\_per\_child', max\_memory\_per\_child,

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       self.prefetch\_multiplier = int(either(

&#x20;           'worker\_prefetch\_multiplier', prefetch\_multiplier,

&#x20;       ))

&#x20;       self.disable\_rate\_limits = either(

&#x20;           'worker\_disable\_rate\_limits', disable\_rate\_limits,

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       self.worker\_lost\_wait = either('worker\_lost\_wait', worker\_lost\_wait)



&#x20;   def wait\_for\_soft\_shutdown(self):

&#x20;       """Wait :setting:`worker\_soft\_shutdown\_timeout` if soft shutdown is enabled.



&#x20;       To enable soft shutdown, set the :setting:`worker\_soft\_shutdown\_timeout` in the

&#x20;       configuration. Soft shutdown can be used to allow the worker to finish processing

&#x20;       few more tasks before initiating a cold shutdown. This mechanism allows the worker

&#x20;       to finish short tasks that are already in progress and requeue long-running tasks

&#x20;       to be picked up by another worker.



&#x20;       .. warning::

&#x20;           If there are no tasks in the worker, the worker will not wait for the

&#x20;           soft shutdown timeout even if it is set as it makes no sense to wait for

&#x20;           the timeout when there are no tasks to process.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       app = self.app

&#x20;       requests = tuple(state.active\_requests)



&#x20;       if app.conf.worker\_enable\_soft\_shutdown\_on\_idle:

&#x20;           requests = True



&#x20;       if app.conf.worker\_soft\_shutdown\_timeout > 0 and requests:

&#x20;           log = f"Initiating Soft Shutdown, terminating in {app.conf.worker\_soft\_shutdown\_timeout} seconds"

&#x20;           logger.warning(log)

&#x20;           sleep(app.conf.worker\_soft\_shutdown\_timeout)



```

from .worker import WorkController



\_\_all\_\_ = ('WorkController',)
