\### 代码文件: celery\\worker\\consumer\\agent.py

```python

"""Celery + :pypi:`cell` integration."""

from celery import bootsteps



from .connection import Connection



\_\_all\_\_ = ('Agent',)





class Agent(bootsteps.StartStopStep):

&#x20;   """Agent starts :pypi:`cell` actors."""



&#x20;   conditional = True

&#x20;   requires = (Connection,)



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, c, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       self.agent\_cls = self.enabled = c.app.conf.worker\_agent

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(c, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def create(self, c):

&#x20;       agent = c.agent = self.instantiate(self.agent\_cls, c.connection)

&#x20;       return agent



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\worker\\consumer\\connection.py

```python

"""Consumer Broker Connection Bootstep."""

from kombu.common import ignore\_errors



from celery import bootsteps

from celery.utils.log import get\_logger



\_\_all\_\_ = ('Connection',)



logger = get\_logger(\_\_name\_\_)

info = logger.info





class Connection(bootsteps.StartStopStep):

&#x20;   """Service managing the consumer broker connection."""



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, c, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       c.connection = None

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(c, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def start(self, c):

&#x20;       c.connection = c.connect()

&#x20;       info('Connected to %s', c.connection.as\_uri())



&#x20;   def shutdown(self, c):

&#x20;       # We must set self.connection to None here, so

&#x20;       # that the green pidbox thread exits.

&#x20;       connection, c.connection = c.connection, None

&#x20;       if connection:

&#x20;           ignore\_errors(connection, connection.close)



&#x20;   def info(self, c):

&#x20;       params = 'N/A'

&#x20;       if c.connection:

&#x20;           params = c.connection.info()

&#x20;           params.pop('password', None)  # don't send password.

&#x20;       return {'broker': params}



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\worker\\consumer\\consumer.py

```python

"""Worker Consumer Blueprint.



This module contains the components responsible for consuming messages

from the broker, processing the messages and keeping the broker connections

up and running.

"""

import errno

import logging

import os

import warnings

from collections import defaultdict

from time import sleep



from billiard.common import restart\_state

from billiard.exceptions import RestartFreqExceeded

from kombu.asynchronous.semaphore import DummyLock

from kombu.exceptions import ContentDisallowed, DecodeError

from kombu.utils.compat import \_detect\_environment

from kombu.utils.encoding import safe\_repr

from kombu.utils.limits import TokenBucket

from vine import ppartial, promise



from celery import bootsteps, signals

from celery.app.trace import build\_tracer

from celery.exceptions import (CPendingDeprecationWarning, InvalidTaskError, NotRegistered, WorkerShutdown,

&#x20;                              WorkerTerminate)

from celery.utils.functional import noop

from celery.utils.log import get\_logger

from celery.utils.nodenames import gethostname

from celery.utils.objects import Bunch

from celery.utils.text import truncate

from celery.utils.time import humanize\_seconds, rate

from celery.worker import loops

from celery.worker.state import active\_requests, maybe\_shutdown, requests, reserved\_requests, task\_reserved



\_\_all\_\_ = ('Consumer', 'Evloop', 'dump\_body')



CLOSE = bootsteps.CLOSE

TERMINATE = bootsteps.TERMINATE

STOP\_CONDITIONS = {CLOSE, TERMINATE}

logger = get\_logger(\_\_name\_\_)

debug, info, warn, error, crit = (logger.debug, logger.info, logger.warning,

&#x20;                                 logger.error, logger.critical)



CONNECTION\_RETRY = """\\

consumer: Connection to broker lost. \\

Trying to re-establish the connection...\\

"""



CONNECTION\_RETRY\_STEP = """\\

Trying again {when}... ({retries}/{max\_retries})\\

"""



CONNECTION\_ERROR = """\\

consumer: Cannot connect to %s: %s.

%s

"""



CONNECTION\_FAILOVER = """\\

Will retry using next failover.\\

"""



UNKNOWN\_FORMAT = """\\

Received and deleted unknown message.  Wrong destination?!?



The full contents of the message body was: %s

"""



\#: Error message for when an unregistered task is received.

UNKNOWN\_TASK\_ERROR = """\\

Received unregistered task of type %s.

The message has been ignored and discarded.



Did you remember to import the module containing this task?

Or maybe you're using relative imports?



Please see

https://docs.celeryq.dev/en/latest/internals/protocol.html

for more information.



The full contents of the message body was:

%s



The full contents of the message headers:

%s



The delivery info for this task is:

%s

"""



\#: Error message for when an invalid task message is received.

INVALID\_TASK\_ERROR = """\\

Received invalid task message: %s

The message has been ignored and discarded.



Please ensure your message conforms to the task

message protocol as described here:

https://docs.celeryq.dev/en/latest/internals/protocol.html



The full contents of the message body was:

%s

"""



MESSAGE\_DECODE\_ERROR = """\\

Can't decode message body: %r \[type:%r encoding:%r headers:%s]



body: %s

"""



MESSAGE\_REPORT = """\\

body: {0}

{{content\_type:{1} content\_encoding:{2}

&#x20; delivery\_info:{3} headers={4}}}

"""



TERMINATING\_TASK\_ON\_RESTART\_AFTER\_A\_CONNECTION\_LOSS = """\\

Task %s cannot be acknowledged after a connection loss since late acknowledgement is enabled for it.

Terminating it instead.

"""



CANCEL\_TASKS\_BY\_DEFAULT = """

In Celery 5.1 we introduced an optional breaking change which

on connection loss cancels all currently executed tasks with late acknowledgement enabled.

These tasks cannot be acknowledged as the connection is gone, and the tasks are automatically redelivered

back to the queue. You can enable this behavior using the worker\_cancel\_long\_running\_tasks\_on\_connection\_loss

setting. In Celery 5.1 it is set to False by default. The setting will be set to True by default in Celery 6.0.

"""





def dump\_body(m, body):

&#x20;   """Format message body for debugging purposes."""

&#x20;   # v2 protocol does not deserialize body

&#x20;   body = m.body if body is None else body

&#x20;   return '{} ({}b)'.format(truncate(safe\_repr(body), 1024),

&#x20;                            len(m.body))





class Consumer:

&#x20;   """Consumer blueprint."""



&#x20;   Strategies = dict



&#x20;   #: Optional callback called the first time the worker

&#x20;   #: is ready to receive tasks.

&#x20;   init\_callback = None



&#x20;   #: The current worker pool instance.

&#x20;   pool = None



&#x20;   #: A timer used for high-priority internal tasks, such

&#x20;   #: as sending heartbeats.

&#x20;   timer = None



&#x20;   restart\_count = -1  # first start is the same as a restart



&#x20;   #: This flag will be turned off after the first failed

&#x20;   #: connection attempt.

&#x20;   first\_connection\_attempt = True



&#x20;   class Blueprint(bootsteps.Blueprint):

&#x20;       """Consumer blueprint."""



&#x20;       name = 'Consumer'

&#x20;       default\_steps = \[

&#x20;           'celery.worker.consumer.connection:Connection',

&#x20;           'celery.worker.consumer.mingle:Mingle',

&#x20;           'celery.worker.consumer.events:Events',

&#x20;           'celery.worker.consumer.gossip:Gossip',

&#x20;           'celery.worker.consumer.heart:Heart',

&#x20;           'celery.worker.consumer.control:Control',

&#x20;           'celery.worker.consumer.tasks:Tasks',

&#x20;           'celery.worker.consumer.delayed\_delivery:DelayedDelivery',

&#x20;           'celery.worker.consumer.consumer:Evloop',

&#x20;           'celery.worker.consumer.agent:Agent',

&#x20;       ]



&#x20;       def shutdown(self, parent):

&#x20;           self.send\_all(parent, 'shutdown')



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, on\_task\_request,

&#x20;                init\_callback=noop, hostname=None,

&#x20;                pool=None, app=None,

&#x20;                timer=None, controller=None, hub=None, amqheartbeat=None,

&#x20;                worker\_options=None, disable\_rate\_limits=False,

&#x20;                initial\_prefetch\_count=2, prefetch\_multiplier=1, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       self.app = app

&#x20;       self.controller = controller

&#x20;       self.init\_callback = init\_callback

&#x20;       self.hostname = hostname or gethostname()

&#x20;       self.pid = os.getpid()

&#x20;       self.pool = pool

&#x20;       self.timer = timer

&#x20;       self.strategies = self.Strategies()

&#x20;       self.conninfo = self.app.connection\_for\_read()

&#x20;       self.connection\_errors = self.conninfo.connection\_errors

&#x20;       self.channel\_errors = self.conninfo.channel\_errors

&#x20;       self.\_restart\_state = restart\_state(maxR=5, maxT=1)



&#x20;       self.\_does\_info = logger.isEnabledFor(logging.INFO)

&#x20;       self.\_limit\_order = 0

&#x20;       self.on\_task\_request = on\_task\_request

&#x20;       self.on\_task\_message = set()

&#x20;       self.amqheartbeat\_rate = self.app.conf.broker\_heartbeat\_checkrate

&#x20;       self.disable\_rate\_limits = disable\_rate\_limits

&#x20;       self.initial\_prefetch\_count = initial\_prefetch\_count

&#x20;       self.prefetch\_multiplier = prefetch\_multiplier

&#x20;       self.\_maximum\_prefetch\_restored = True



&#x20;       # this contains a tokenbucket for each task type by name, used for

&#x20;       # rate limits, or None if rate limits are disabled for that task.

&#x20;       self.task\_buckets = defaultdict(lambda: None)

&#x20;       self.reset\_rate\_limits()



&#x20;       self.hub = hub

&#x20;       if self.hub or getattr(self.pool, 'is\_green', False):

&#x20;           self.amqheartbeat = amqheartbeat

&#x20;           if self.amqheartbeat is None:

&#x20;               self.amqheartbeat = self.app.conf.broker\_heartbeat

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           self.amqheartbeat = 0



&#x20;       if not hasattr(self, 'loop'):

&#x20;           self.loop = loops.asynloop if hub else loops.synloop



&#x20;       if \_detect\_environment() == 'gevent':

&#x20;           # there's a gevent bug that causes timeouts to not be reset,

&#x20;           # so if the connection timeout is exceeded once, it can NEVER

&#x20;           # connect again.

&#x20;           self.app.conf.broker\_connection\_timeout = None



&#x20;       self.\_pending\_operations = \[]



&#x20;       self.steps = \[]

&#x20;       self.blueprint = self.Blueprint(

&#x20;           steps=self.app.steps\['consumer'],

&#x20;           on\_close=self.on\_close,

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       self.blueprint.apply(self, \*\*dict(worker\_options or {}, \*\*kwargs))



&#x20;   def call\_soon(self, p, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       p = ppartial(p, \*args, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;       if self.hub:

&#x20;           return self.hub.call\_soon(p)

&#x20;       self.\_pending\_operations.append(p)

&#x20;       return p



&#x20;   def perform\_pending\_operations(self):

&#x20;       if not self.hub:

&#x20;           while self.\_pending\_operations:

&#x20;               try:

&#x20;                   self.\_pending\_operations.pop()()

&#x20;               except Exception as exc:  # pylint: disable=broad-except

&#x20;                   logger.exception('Pending callback raised: %r', exc)



&#x20;   def bucket\_for\_task(self, type):

&#x20;       limit = rate(getattr(type, 'rate\_limit', None))

&#x20;       return TokenBucket(limit, capacity=1) if limit else None



&#x20;   def reset\_rate\_limits(self):

&#x20;       self.task\_buckets.update(

&#x20;           (n, self.bucket\_for\_task(t)) for n, t in self.app.tasks.items()

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def \_update\_prefetch\_count(self, index=0):

&#x20;       """Update prefetch count after pool/shrink grow operations.



&#x20;       Index must be the change in number of processes as a positive

&#x20;       (increasing) or negative (decreasing) number.



&#x20;       Note:

&#x20;           Currently pool grow operations will end up with an offset

&#x20;           of +1 if the initial size of the pool was 0 (e.g.

&#x20;           :option:`--autoscale=1,0 <celery worker --autoscale>`).

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       num\_processes = self.pool.num\_processes

&#x20;       if not self.initial\_prefetch\_count or not num\_processes:

&#x20;           return  # prefetch disabled

&#x20;       self.initial\_prefetch\_count = (

&#x20;           self.pool.num\_processes \* self.prefetch\_multiplier

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       return self.\_update\_qos\_eventually(index)



&#x20;   def \_update\_qos\_eventually(self, index):

&#x20;       return (self.qos.decrement\_eventually if index < 0

&#x20;               else self.qos.increment\_eventually)(

&#x20;           abs(index) \* self.prefetch\_multiplier)



&#x20;   def \_limit\_move\_to\_pool(self, request):

&#x20;       task\_reserved(request)

&#x20;       self.on\_task\_request(request)



&#x20;   def \_schedule\_bucket\_request(self, bucket):

&#x20;       while True:

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               request, tokens = bucket.pop()

&#x20;           except IndexError:

&#x20;               # no request, break

&#x20;               break



&#x20;           if bucket.can\_consume(tokens):

&#x20;               self.\_limit\_move\_to\_pool(request)

&#x20;               continue

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               # requeue to head, keep the order.

&#x20;               bucket.contents.appendleft((request, tokens))



&#x20;               pri = self.\_limit\_order = (self.\_limit\_order + 1) % 10

&#x20;               hold = bucket.expected\_time(tokens)

&#x20;               self.timer.call\_after(

&#x20;                   hold, self.\_schedule\_bucket\_request, (bucket,),

&#x20;                   priority=pri,

&#x20;               )

&#x20;               # no tokens, break

&#x20;               break



&#x20;   def \_limit\_task(self, request, bucket, tokens):

&#x20;       bucket.add((request, tokens))

&#x20;       return self.\_schedule\_bucket\_request(bucket)



&#x20;   def \_limit\_post\_eta(self, request, bucket, tokens):

&#x20;       self.qos.decrement\_eventually()

&#x20;       bucket.add((request, tokens))

&#x20;       return self.\_schedule\_bucket\_request(bucket)



&#x20;   def start(self):

&#x20;       blueprint = self.blueprint

&#x20;       while blueprint.state not in STOP\_CONDITIONS:

&#x20;           maybe\_shutdown()

&#x20;           if self.restart\_count:

&#x20;               try:

&#x20;                   self.\_restart\_state.step()

&#x20;               except RestartFreqExceeded as exc:

&#x20;                   crit('Frequent restarts detected: %r', exc, exc\_info=1)

&#x20;                   sleep(1)

&#x20;           self.restart\_count += 1

&#x20;           if self.app.conf.broker\_channel\_error\_retry:

&#x20;               recoverable\_errors = (self.connection\_errors + self.channel\_errors)

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               recoverable\_errors = self.connection\_errors

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               blueprint.start(self)

&#x20;           except recoverable\_errors as exc:

&#x20;               # If we're not retrying connections, we need to properly shutdown or terminate

&#x20;               # the Celery main process instead of abruptly aborting the process without any cleanup.

&#x20;               is\_connection\_loss\_on\_startup = self.first\_connection\_attempt

&#x20;               self.first\_connection\_attempt = False

&#x20;               connection\_retry\_type = self.\_get\_connection\_retry\_type(is\_connection\_loss\_on\_startup)

&#x20;               connection\_retry = self.app.conf\[connection\_retry\_type]

&#x20;               if not connection\_retry:

&#x20;                   crit(

&#x20;                       f"Retrying to {'establish' if is\_connection\_loss\_on\_startup else 're-establish'} "

&#x20;                       f"a connection to the message broker after a connection loss has "

&#x20;                       f"been disabled (app.conf.{connection\_retry\_type}=False). Shutting down..."

&#x20;                   )

&#x20;                   raise WorkerShutdown(1) from exc

&#x20;               if isinstance(exc, OSError) and exc.errno == errno.EMFILE:

&#x20;                   crit("Too many open files. Aborting...")

&#x20;                   raise WorkerTerminate(1) from exc

&#x20;               maybe\_shutdown()

&#x20;               if blueprint.state not in STOP\_CONDITIONS:

&#x20;                   if self.connection:

&#x20;                       self.on\_connection\_error\_after\_connected(exc)

&#x20;                   else:

&#x20;                       self.on\_connection\_error\_before\_connected(exc)

&#x20;                   self.on\_close()

&#x20;                   blueprint.restart(self)



&#x20;   def \_get\_connection\_retry\_type(self, is\_connection\_loss\_on\_startup):

&#x20;       return ('broker\_connection\_retry\_on\_startup'

&#x20;               if (is\_connection\_loss\_on\_startup

&#x20;                   and self.app.conf.broker\_connection\_retry\_on\_startup is not None)

&#x20;               else 'broker\_connection\_retry')



&#x20;   def on\_connection\_error\_before\_connected(self, exc):

&#x20;       error(CONNECTION\_ERROR, self.conninfo.as\_uri(), exc,

&#x20;             'Trying to reconnect...')



&#x20;   def on\_connection\_error\_after\_connected(self, exc):

&#x20;       warn(CONNECTION\_RETRY, exc\_info=True)

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           self.connection.collect()

&#x20;       except Exception:  # pylint: disable=broad-except

&#x20;           pass



&#x20;       if self.app.conf.worker\_cancel\_long\_running\_tasks\_on\_connection\_loss:

&#x20;           for request in tuple(active\_requests):

&#x20;               if request.task.acks\_late and not request.acknowledged:

&#x20;                   warn(TERMINATING\_TASK\_ON\_RESTART\_AFTER\_A\_CONNECTION\_LOSS,

&#x20;                        request)

&#x20;                   request.cancel(self.pool)

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           warnings.warn(CANCEL\_TASKS\_BY\_DEFAULT, CPendingDeprecationWarning)



&#x20;       if self.app.conf.worker\_enable\_prefetch\_count\_reduction:

&#x20;           self.initial\_prefetch\_count = max(

&#x20;               self.prefetch\_multiplier,

&#x20;               self.max\_prefetch\_count - len(tuple(active\_requests)) \* self.prefetch\_multiplier

&#x20;           )



&#x20;           self.\_maximum\_prefetch\_restored = self.initial\_prefetch\_count == self.max\_prefetch\_count

&#x20;           if not self.\_maximum\_prefetch\_restored:

&#x20;               logger.info(

&#x20;                   f"Temporarily reducing the prefetch count to {self.initial\_prefetch\_count} to avoid "

&#x20;                   f"over-fetching since {len(tuple(active\_requests))} tasks are currently being processed.\\n"

&#x20;                   f"The prefetch count will be gradually restored to {self.max\_prefetch\_count} as the tasks "

&#x20;                   "complete processing."

&#x20;               )



&#x20;   def register\_with\_event\_loop(self, hub):

&#x20;       self.blueprint.send\_all(

&#x20;           self, 'register\_with\_event\_loop', args=(hub,),

&#x20;           description='Hub.register',

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def shutdown(self):

&#x20;       self.perform\_pending\_operations()

&#x20;       self.blueprint.shutdown(self)



&#x20;   def stop(self):

&#x20;       self.blueprint.stop(self)



&#x20;   def on\_ready(self):

&#x20;       callback, self.init\_callback = self.init\_callback, None

&#x20;       if callback:

&#x20;           callback(self)



&#x20;   def loop\_args(self):

&#x20;       return (self, self.connection, self.task\_consumer,

&#x20;               self.blueprint, self.hub, self.qos, self.amqheartbeat,

&#x20;               self.app.clock, self.amqheartbeat\_rate)



&#x20;   def on\_decode\_error(self, message, exc):

&#x20;       """Callback called if an error occurs while decoding a message.



&#x20;       Simply logs the error and acknowledges the message so it

&#x20;       doesn't enter a loop.



&#x20;       Arguments:

&#x20;           message (kombu.Message): The message received.

&#x20;           exc (Exception): The exception being handled.

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       crit(MESSAGE\_DECODE\_ERROR,

&#x20;            exc, message.content\_type, message.content\_encoding,

&#x20;            safe\_repr(message.headers), dump\_body(message, message.body),

&#x20;            exc\_info=1)

&#x20;       message.ack()



&#x20;   def on\_close(self):

&#x20;       # Clear internal queues to get rid of old messages.

&#x20;       # They can't be acked anyway, as a delivery tag is specific

&#x20;       # to the current channel.

&#x20;       if self.controller and self.controller.semaphore:

&#x20;           self.controller.semaphore.clear()

&#x20;       if self.timer:

&#x20;           self.timer.clear()

&#x20;       for bucket in self.task\_buckets.values():

&#x20;           if bucket:

&#x20;               bucket.clear\_pending()

&#x20;       for request\_id in reserved\_requests:

&#x20;           if request\_id in requests:

&#x20;               del requests\[request\_id]

&#x20;       reserved\_requests.clear()

&#x20;       if self.pool and self.pool.flush:

&#x20;           self.pool.flush()



&#x20;   def connect(self):

&#x20;       """Establish the broker connection used for consuming tasks.



&#x20;       Retries establishing the connection if the

&#x20;       :setting:`broker\_connection\_retry` setting is enabled

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       conn = self.connection\_for\_read(heartbeat=self.amqheartbeat)

&#x20;       if self.hub:

&#x20;           conn.transport.register\_with\_event\_loop(conn.connection, self.hub)

&#x20;       return conn



&#x20;   def connection\_for\_read(self, heartbeat=None):

&#x20;       return self.ensure\_connected(

&#x20;           self.app.connection\_for\_read(heartbeat=heartbeat))



&#x20;   def connection\_for\_write(self, url=None, heartbeat=None):

&#x20;       return self.ensure\_connected(

&#x20;           self.app.connection\_for\_write(url=url, heartbeat=heartbeat))



&#x20;   def ensure\_connected(self, conn):

&#x20;       # Callback called for each retry while the connection

&#x20;       # can't be established.

&#x20;       def \_error\_handler(exc, interval, next\_step=CONNECTION\_RETRY\_STEP):

&#x20;           if getattr(conn, 'alt', None) and interval == 0:

&#x20;               next\_step = CONNECTION\_FAILOVER

&#x20;           next\_step = next\_step.format(

&#x20;               when=humanize\_seconds(interval, 'in', ' '),

&#x20;               retries=int(interval / 2),

&#x20;               max\_retries=self.app.conf.broker\_connection\_max\_retries)

&#x20;           error(CONNECTION\_ERROR, conn.as\_uri(), exc, next\_step)



&#x20;       # Remember that the connection is lazy, it won't establish

&#x20;       # until needed.



&#x20;       # TODO: Rely only on broker\_connection\_retry\_on\_startup to determine whether connection retries are disabled.

&#x20;       #       We will make the switch in Celery 6.0.



&#x20;       retry\_disabled = False



&#x20;       if self.app.conf.broker\_connection\_retry\_on\_startup is None:

&#x20;           # If broker\_connection\_retry\_on\_startup is not set, revert to broker\_connection\_retry

&#x20;           # to determine whether connection retries are disabled.

&#x20;           retry\_disabled = not self.app.conf.broker\_connection\_retry



&#x20;           if retry\_disabled:

&#x20;               warnings.warn(

&#x20;                   CPendingDeprecationWarning(

&#x20;                       "The broker\_connection\_retry configuration setting will no longer determine\\n"

&#x20;                       "whether broker connection retries are made during startup in Celery 6.0 and above.\\n"

&#x20;                       "If you wish to refrain from retrying connections on startup,\\n"

&#x20;                       "you should set broker\_connection\_retry\_on\_startup to False instead.")

&#x20;               )

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           if self.first\_connection\_attempt:

&#x20;               retry\_disabled = not self.app.conf.broker\_connection\_retry\_on\_startup

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               retry\_disabled = not self.app.conf.broker\_connection\_retry



&#x20;       if retry\_disabled:

&#x20;           # Retry disabled, just call connect directly.

&#x20;           conn.connect()

&#x20;           self.first\_connection\_attempt = False

&#x20;           return conn



&#x20;       conn = conn.ensure\_connection(

&#x20;           \_error\_handler, self.app.conf.broker\_connection\_max\_retries,

&#x20;           callback=maybe\_shutdown,

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       self.first\_connection\_attempt = False

&#x20;       return conn



&#x20;   def \_flush\_events(self):

&#x20;       if self.event\_dispatcher:

&#x20;           self.event\_dispatcher.flush()



&#x20;   def on\_send\_event\_buffered(self):

&#x20;       if self.hub:

&#x20;           self.hub.\_ready.add(self.\_flush\_events)



&#x20;   def add\_task\_queue(self, queue, exchange=None, exchange\_type=None,

&#x20;                      routing\_key=None, \*\*options):

&#x20;       cset = self.task\_consumer

&#x20;       queues = self.app.amqp.queues

&#x20;       # Must use in' here, as \_\_missing\_\_ will automatically

&#x20;       # create queues when :setting:`task\_create\_missing\_queues` is enabled.

&#x20;       # (Issue #1079)

&#x20;       if queue in queues:

&#x20;           q = queues\[queue]

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           exchange = queue if exchange is None else exchange

&#x20;           exchange\_type = ('direct' if exchange\_type is None

&#x20;                            else exchange\_type)

&#x20;           q = queues.select\_add(queue,

&#x20;                                 exchange=exchange,

&#x20;                                 exchange\_type=exchange\_type,

&#x20;                                 routing\_key=routing\_key, \*\*options)

&#x20;       if not cset.consuming\_from(queue):

&#x20;           cset.add\_queue(q)

&#x20;           cset.consume()

&#x20;           info('Started consuming from %s', queue)



&#x20;   def cancel\_task\_queue(self, queue):

&#x20;       info('Canceling queue %s', queue)

&#x20;       self.app.amqp.queues.deselect(queue)

&#x20;       self.task\_consumer.cancel\_by\_queue(queue)



&#x20;   def apply\_eta\_task(self, task):

&#x20;       """Method called by the timer to apply a task with an ETA/countdown."""

&#x20;       task\_reserved(task)

&#x20;       self.on\_task\_request(task)

&#x20;       self.qos.decrement\_eventually()



&#x20;   def \_message\_report(self, body, message):

&#x20;       return MESSAGE\_REPORT.format(dump\_body(message, body),

&#x20;                                    safe\_repr(message.content\_type),

&#x20;                                    safe\_repr(message.content\_encoding),

&#x20;                                    safe\_repr(message.delivery\_info),

&#x20;                                    safe\_repr(message.headers))



&#x20;   def on\_unknown\_message(self, body, message):

&#x20;       warn(UNKNOWN\_FORMAT, self.\_message\_report(body, message))

&#x20;       message.reject\_log\_error(logger, self.connection\_errors)

&#x20;       signals.task\_rejected.send(sender=self, message=message, exc=None)



&#x20;   def on\_unknown\_task(self, body, message, exc):

&#x20;       error(UNKNOWN\_TASK\_ERROR,

&#x20;             exc,

&#x20;             dump\_body(message, body),

&#x20;             message.headers,

&#x20;             message.delivery\_info,

&#x20;             exc\_info=True)

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           id\_, name = message.headers\['id'], message.headers\['task']

&#x20;           root\_id = message.headers.get('root\_id')

&#x20;       except KeyError:  # proto1

&#x20;           payload = message.payload

&#x20;           id\_, name = payload\['id'], payload\['task']

&#x20;           root\_id = None

&#x20;       request = Bunch(

&#x20;           name=name, chord=None, root\_id=root\_id,

&#x20;           correlation\_id=message.properties.get('correlation\_id'),

&#x20;           reply\_to=message.properties.get('reply\_to'),

&#x20;           errbacks=None,

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       message.reject\_log\_error(logger, self.connection\_errors)

&#x20;       self.app.backend.mark\_as\_failure(

&#x20;           id\_, NotRegistered(name), request=request,

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       if self.event\_dispatcher:

&#x20;           self.event\_dispatcher.send(

&#x20;               'task-failed', uuid=id\_,

&#x20;               exception=f'NotRegistered({name!r})',

&#x20;           )

&#x20;       signals.task\_unknown.send(

&#x20;           sender=self, message=message, exc=exc, name=name, id=id\_,

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def on\_invalid\_task(self, body, message, exc):

&#x20;       error(INVALID\_TASK\_ERROR, exc, dump\_body(message, body),

&#x20;             exc\_info=True)

&#x20;       message.reject\_log\_error(logger, self.connection\_errors)

&#x20;       signals.task\_rejected.send(sender=self, message=message, exc=exc)



&#x20;   def update\_strategies(self):

&#x20;       loader = self.app.loader

&#x20;       for name, task in self.app.tasks.items():

&#x20;           self.strategies\[name] = task.start\_strategy(self.app, self)

&#x20;           task.\_\_trace\_\_ = build\_tracer(name, task, loader, self.hostname,

&#x20;                                         app=self.app)



&#x20;   def create\_task\_handler(self, promise=promise):

&#x20;       strategies = self.strategies

&#x20;       on\_unknown\_message = self.on\_unknown\_message

&#x20;       on\_unknown\_task = self.on\_unknown\_task

&#x20;       on\_invalid\_task = self.on\_invalid\_task

&#x20;       callbacks = self.on\_task\_message

&#x20;       call\_soon = self.call\_soon



&#x20;       def on\_task\_received(message):

&#x20;           # payload will only be set for v1 protocol, since v2

&#x20;           # will defer deserializing the message body to the pool.

&#x20;           payload = None

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               type\_ = message.headers\['task']  # protocol v2

&#x20;           except TypeError:

&#x20;               return on\_unknown\_message(None, message)

&#x20;           except KeyError:

&#x20;               try:

&#x20;                   payload = message.decode()

&#x20;               except Exception as exc:  # pylint: disable=broad-except

&#x20;                   return self.on\_decode\_error(message, exc)

&#x20;               try:

&#x20;                   type\_, payload = payload\['task'], payload  # protocol v1

&#x20;               except (TypeError, KeyError):

&#x20;                   return on\_unknown\_message(payload, message)

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               strategy = strategies\[type\_]

&#x20;           except KeyError as exc:

&#x20;               return on\_unknown\_task(None, message, exc)

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               try:

&#x20;                   ack\_log\_error\_promise = promise(

&#x20;                       call\_soon,

&#x20;                       (message.ack\_log\_error,),

&#x20;                       on\_error=self.\_restore\_prefetch\_count\_after\_connection\_restart,

&#x20;                   )

&#x20;                   reject\_log\_error\_promise = promise(

&#x20;                       call\_soon,

&#x20;                       (message.reject\_log\_error,),

&#x20;                       on\_error=self.\_restore\_prefetch\_count\_after\_connection\_restart,

&#x20;                   )



&#x20;                   if (

&#x20;                       not self.\_maximum\_prefetch\_restored

&#x20;                       and self.restart\_count > 0

&#x20;                       and self.\_new\_prefetch\_count <= self.max\_prefetch\_count

&#x20;                   ):

&#x20;                       ack\_log\_error\_promise.then(self.\_restore\_prefetch\_count\_after\_connection\_restart,

&#x20;                                                  on\_error=self.\_restore\_prefetch\_count\_after\_connection\_restart)

&#x20;                       reject\_log\_error\_promise.then(self.\_restore\_prefetch\_count\_after\_connection\_restart,

&#x20;                                                     on\_error=self.\_restore\_prefetch\_count\_after\_connection\_restart)



&#x20;                   strategy(

&#x20;                       message, payload,

&#x20;                       ack\_log\_error\_promise,

&#x20;                       reject\_log\_error\_promise,

&#x20;                       callbacks,

&#x20;                   )

&#x20;               except (InvalidTaskError, ContentDisallowed) as exc:

&#x20;                   return on\_invalid\_task(payload, message, exc)

&#x20;               except DecodeError as exc:

&#x20;                   return self.on\_decode\_error(message, exc)



&#x20;       return on\_task\_received



&#x20;   def \_restore\_prefetch\_count\_after\_connection\_restart(self, p, \*args):

&#x20;       with self.qos.\_mutex:

&#x20;           if any((

&#x20;               not self.app.conf.worker\_enable\_prefetch\_count\_reduction,

&#x20;               self.\_maximum\_prefetch\_restored,

&#x20;           )):

&#x20;               return



&#x20;           new\_prefetch\_count = min(self.max\_prefetch\_count, self.\_new\_prefetch\_count)

&#x20;           self.qos.value = self.initial\_prefetch\_count = new\_prefetch\_count

&#x20;           self.qos.set(self.qos.value)



&#x20;           already\_restored = self.\_maximum\_prefetch\_restored

&#x20;           self.\_maximum\_prefetch\_restored = new\_prefetch\_count == self.max\_prefetch\_count



&#x20;           if already\_restored is False and self.\_maximum\_prefetch\_restored is True:

&#x20;               logger.info(

&#x20;                   "Resuming normal operations following a restart.\\n"

&#x20;                   f"Prefetch count has been restored to the maximum of {self.max\_prefetch\_count}"

&#x20;               )



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def max\_prefetch\_count(self):

&#x20;       return self.pool.num\_processes \* self.prefetch\_multiplier



&#x20;   @property

&#x20;   def \_new\_prefetch\_count(self):

&#x20;       return self.qos.value + self.prefetch\_multiplier



&#x20;   def \_\_repr\_\_(self):

&#x20;       """``repr(self)``."""

&#x20;       return '<Consumer: {self.hostname} ({state})>'.format(

&#x20;           self=self, state=self.blueprint.human\_state(),

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def cancel\_all\_unacked\_requests(self):

&#x20;       """Cancel all active requests that either do not require late acknowledgments or,

&#x20;       if they do, have not been acknowledged yet.

&#x20;       """



&#x20;       def should\_cancel(request):

&#x20;           if not request.task.acks\_late:

&#x20;               # Task does not require late acknowledgment, cancel it.

&#x20;               return True



&#x20;           if not request.acknowledged:

&#x20;               # Task is late acknowledged, but it has not been acknowledged yet, cancel it.

&#x20;               return True



&#x20;           # Task is late acknowledged, but it has already been acknowledged.

&#x20;           return False  # Do not cancel and allow it to gracefully finish as it has already been acknowledged.



&#x20;       requests\_to\_cancel = tuple(filter(should\_cancel, active\_requests))



&#x20;       if requests\_to\_cancel:

&#x20;           for request in requests\_to\_cancel:

&#x20;               request.cancel(self.pool)





class Evloop(bootsteps.StartStopStep):

&#x20;   """Event loop service.



&#x20;   Note:

&#x20;       This is always started last.

&#x20;   """



&#x20;   label = 'event loop'

&#x20;   last = True



&#x20;   def start(self, c):

&#x20;       self.patch\_all(c)

&#x20;       c.loop(\*c.loop\_args())



&#x20;   def patch\_all(self, c):

&#x20;       c.qos.\_mutex = DummyLock()



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\worker\\consumer\\control.py

```python

"""Worker Remote Control Bootstep.



``Control`` -> :mod:`celery.worker.pidbox` -> :mod:`kombu.pidbox`.



The actual commands are implemented in :mod:`celery.worker.control`.

"""

from celery import bootsteps

from celery.utils.log import get\_logger

from celery.worker import pidbox



from .tasks import Tasks



\_\_all\_\_ = ('Control',)



logger = get\_logger(\_\_name\_\_)





class Control(bootsteps.StartStopStep):

&#x20;   """Remote control command service."""



&#x20;   requires = (Tasks,)



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, c, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       self.is\_green = c.pool is not None and c.pool.is\_green

&#x20;       self.box = (pidbox.gPidbox if self.is\_green else pidbox.Pidbox)(c)

&#x20;       self.start = self.box.start

&#x20;       self.stop = self.box.stop

&#x20;       self.shutdown = self.box.shutdown

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(c, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def include\_if(self, c):

&#x20;       return (c.app.conf.worker\_enable\_remote\_control and

&#x20;               c.conninfo.supports\_exchange\_type('fanout'))



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\worker\\consumer\\delayed\_delivery.py

```python

"""Native delayed delivery functionality for Celery workers.



This module provides the DelayedDelivery bootstep which handles setup and configuration

of native delayed delivery functionality when using quorum queues.

"""

from typing import Iterator, List, Optional, Set, Union, ValuesView



from kombu import Connection, Queue

from kombu.transport.native\_delayed\_delivery import (bind\_queue\_to\_native\_delayed\_delivery\_exchange,

&#x20;                                                    declare\_native\_delayed\_delivery\_exchanges\_and\_queues)

from kombu.utils.functional import retry\_over\_time



from celery import Celery, bootsteps

from celery.utils.log import get\_logger

from celery.utils.quorum\_queues import detect\_quorum\_queues

from celery.worker.consumer import Consumer, Tasks



\_\_all\_\_ = ('DelayedDelivery',)



logger = get\_logger(\_\_name\_\_)





\# Default retry settings

RETRY\_INTERVAL = 1.0  # seconds between retries

MAX\_RETRIES = 3      # maximum number of retries





\# Valid queue types for delayed delivery

VALID\_QUEUE\_TYPES = {'classic', 'quorum'}





class DelayedDelivery(bootsteps.StartStopStep):

&#x20;   """Bootstep that sets up native delayed delivery functionality.



&#x20;   This component handles the setup and configuration of native delayed delivery

&#x20;   for Celery workers. It is automatically included when quorum queues are

&#x20;   detected in the application configuration.



&#x20;   Responsibilities:

&#x20;       - Declaring native delayed delivery exchanges and queues

&#x20;       - Binding all application queues to the delayed delivery exchanges

&#x20;       - Handling connection failures gracefully with retries

&#x20;       - Validating configuration settings

&#x20;   """



&#x20;   requires = (Tasks,)



&#x20;   def include\_if(self, c: Consumer) -> bool:

&#x20;       """Determine if this bootstep should be included.



&#x20;       Args:

&#x20;           c: The Celery consumer instance



&#x20;       Returns:

&#x20;           bool: True if quorum queues are detected, False otherwise

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       return detect\_quorum\_queues(c.app, c.app.connection\_for\_write().transport.driver\_type)\[0]



&#x20;   def start(self, c: Consumer) -> None:

&#x20;       """Initialize delayed delivery for all broker URLs.



&#x20;       Attempts to set up delayed delivery for each broker URL in the configuration.

&#x20;       Failures are logged but don't prevent attempting remaining URLs.



&#x20;       Args:

&#x20;           c: The Celery consumer instance



&#x20;       Raises:

&#x20;           ValueError: If configuration validation fails

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       app: Celery = c.app



&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           self.\_validate\_configuration(app)

&#x20;       except ValueError as e:

&#x20;           logger.critical("Configuration validation failed: %s", str(e))

&#x20;           raise



&#x20;       broker\_urls = self.\_validate\_broker\_urls(app.conf.broker\_url)

&#x20;       setup\_errors = \[]



&#x20;       for broker\_url in broker\_urls:

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               retry\_over\_time(

&#x20;                   self.\_setup\_delayed\_delivery,

&#x20;                   args=(c, broker\_url),

&#x20;                   catch=(ConnectionRefusedError, OSError),

&#x20;                   errback=self.\_on\_retry,

&#x20;                   interval\_start=RETRY\_INTERVAL,

&#x20;                   max\_retries=MAX\_RETRIES,

&#x20;               )

&#x20;           except Exception as e:

&#x20;               logger.warning(

&#x20;                   "Failed to setup delayed delivery for %r: %s",

&#x20;                   broker\_url, str(e)

&#x20;               )

&#x20;               setup\_errors.append((broker\_url, e))



&#x20;       if len(setup\_errors) == len(broker\_urls):

&#x20;           logger.critical(

&#x20;               "Failed to setup delayed delivery for all broker URLs. "

&#x20;               "Native delayed delivery will not be available."

&#x20;           )



&#x20;   def \_setup\_delayed\_delivery(self, c: Consumer, broker\_url: str) -> None:

&#x20;       """Set up delayed delivery for a specific broker URL.



&#x20;       Args:

&#x20;           c: The Celery consumer instance

&#x20;           broker\_url: The broker URL to configure



&#x20;       Raises:

&#x20;           ConnectionRefusedError: If connection to the broker fails

&#x20;           OSError: If there are network-related issues

&#x20;           Exception: For other unexpected errors during setup

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       with c.app.connection\_for\_write(url=broker\_url) as connection:

&#x20;           queue\_type = c.app.conf.broker\_native\_delayed\_delivery\_queue\_type

&#x20;           logger.debug(

&#x20;               "Setting up delayed delivery for broker %r with queue type %r",

&#x20;               broker\_url, queue\_type

&#x20;           )



&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               declare\_native\_delayed\_delivery\_exchanges\_and\_queues(

&#x20;                   connection,

&#x20;                   queue\_type

&#x20;               )

&#x20;           except Exception as e:

&#x20;               logger.warning(

&#x20;                   "Failed to declare exchanges and queues for %r: %s",

&#x20;                   broker\_url, str(e)

&#x20;               )

&#x20;               raise



&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               self.\_bind\_queues(c.app, connection)

&#x20;           except Exception as e:

&#x20;               logger.warning(

&#x20;                   "Failed to bind queues for %r: %s",

&#x20;                   broker\_url, str(e)

&#x20;               )

&#x20;               raise



&#x20;   def \_bind\_queues(self, app: Celery, connection: Connection) -> None:

&#x20;       """Bind all application queues to delayed delivery exchanges.



&#x20;       Args:

&#x20;           app: The Celery application instance

&#x20;           connection: The broker connection to use



&#x20;       Raises:

&#x20;           Exception: If queue binding fails

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       queues: ValuesView\[Queue] = app.amqp.queues.values()

&#x20;       if not queues:

&#x20;           logger.warning("No queues found to bind for delayed delivery")

&#x20;           return



&#x20;       for queue in queues:

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               logger.debug("Binding queue %r to delayed delivery exchange", queue.name)

&#x20;               bind\_queue\_to\_native\_delayed\_delivery\_exchange(connection, queue)

&#x20;           except Exception as e:

&#x20;               logger.error(

&#x20;                   "Failed to bind queue %r: %s",

&#x20;                   queue.name, str(e)

&#x20;               )

&#x20;               raise



&#x20;   def \_on\_retry(self, exc: Exception, interval\_range: Iterator\[float], intervals\_count: int) -> float:

&#x20;       """Callback for retry attempts.



&#x20;       Args:

&#x20;           exc: The exception that triggered the retry

&#x20;           interval\_range: An iterator which returns the time in seconds to sleep next

&#x20;           intervals\_count: Number of retry attempts so far

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       interval = next(interval\_range)

&#x20;       logger.warning(

&#x20;           "Retrying delayed delivery setup (attempt %d/%d) after error: %s. Sleeping %.2f seconds.",

&#x20;           intervals\_count + 1, MAX\_RETRIES, str(exc), interval

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       return interval



&#x20;   def \_validate\_configuration(self, app: Celery) -> None:

&#x20;       """Validate all required configuration settings.



&#x20;       Args:

&#x20;           app: The Celery application instance



&#x20;       Raises:

&#x20;           ValueError: If any configuration is invalid

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       # Validate broker URLs

&#x20;       self.\_validate\_broker\_urls(app.conf.broker\_url)



&#x20;       # Validate queue type

&#x20;       self.\_validate\_queue\_type(app.conf.broker\_native\_delayed\_delivery\_queue\_type)



&#x20;   def \_validate\_broker\_urls(self, broker\_urls: Union\[str, List\[str]]) -> Set\[str]:

&#x20;       """Validate and split broker URLs.



&#x20;       Args:

&#x20;           broker\_urls: Broker URLs, either as a semicolon-separated string

&#x20;                 or as a list of strings



&#x20;       Returns:

&#x20;           Set of valid broker URLs



&#x20;       Raises:

&#x20;           ValueError: If no valid broker URLs are found or if invalid URLs are provided

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       if not broker\_urls:

&#x20;           raise ValueError("broker\_url configuration is empty")



&#x20;       if isinstance(broker\_urls, str):

&#x20;           brokers = broker\_urls.split(";")

&#x20;       elif isinstance(broker\_urls, list):

&#x20;           if not all(isinstance(url, str) for url in broker\_urls):

&#x20;               raise ValueError("All broker URLs must be strings")

&#x20;           brokers = broker\_urls

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           raise ValueError(f"broker\_url must be a string or list, got {broker\_urls!r}")



&#x20;       valid\_urls = {url for url in brokers}



&#x20;       if not valid\_urls:

&#x20;           raise ValueError("No valid broker URLs found in configuration")



&#x20;       return valid\_urls



&#x20;   def \_validate\_queue\_type(self, queue\_type: Optional\[str]) -> None:

&#x20;       """Validate the queue type configuration.



&#x20;       Args:

&#x20;           queue\_type: The configured queue type



&#x20;       Raises:

&#x20;           ValueError: If queue type is invalid

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       if not queue\_type:

&#x20;           raise ValueError("broker\_native\_delayed\_delivery\_queue\_type is not configured")



&#x20;       if queue\_type not in VALID\_QUEUE\_TYPES:

&#x20;           sorted\_types = sorted(VALID\_QUEUE\_TYPES)

&#x20;           raise ValueError(

&#x20;               f"Invalid queue type {queue\_type!r}. Must be one of: {', '.join(sorted\_types)}"

&#x20;           )



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\worker\\consumer\\events.py

```python

"""Worker Event Dispatcher Bootstep.



``Events`` -> :class:`celery.events.EventDispatcher`.

"""

from kombu.common import ignore\_errors



from celery import bootsteps



from .connection import Connection



\_\_all\_\_ = ('Events',)





class Events(bootsteps.StartStopStep):

&#x20;   """Service used for sending monitoring events."""



&#x20;   requires = (Connection,)



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, c,

&#x20;                task\_events=True,

&#x20;                without\_heartbeat=False,

&#x20;                without\_gossip=False,

&#x20;                \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       self.groups = None if task\_events else \['worker']

&#x20;       self.send\_events = (

&#x20;           task\_events or

&#x20;           not without\_gossip or

&#x20;           not without\_heartbeat

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       self.enabled = self.send\_events

&#x20;       c.event\_dispatcher = None

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(c, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def start(self, c):

&#x20;       # flush events sent while connection was down.

&#x20;       prev = self.\_close(c)

&#x20;       dis = c.event\_dispatcher = c.app.events.Dispatcher(

&#x20;           c.connection\_for\_write(),

&#x20;           hostname=c.hostname,

&#x20;           enabled=self.send\_events,

&#x20;           groups=self.groups,

&#x20;           # we currently only buffer events when the event loop is enabled

&#x20;           # XXX This excludes eventlet/gevent, which should actually buffer.

&#x20;           buffer\_group=\['task'] if c.hub else None,

&#x20;           on\_send\_buffered=c.on\_send\_event\_buffered if c.hub else None,

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       if prev:

&#x20;           dis.extend\_buffer(prev)

&#x20;           dis.flush()



&#x20;   def stop(self, c):

&#x20;       pass



&#x20;   def \_close(self, c):

&#x20;       if c.event\_dispatcher:

&#x20;           dispatcher = c.event\_dispatcher

&#x20;           # remember changes from remote control commands:

&#x20;           self.groups = dispatcher.groups



&#x20;           # close custom connection

&#x20;           if dispatcher.connection:

&#x20;               ignore\_errors(c, dispatcher.connection.close)

&#x20;           ignore\_errors(c, dispatcher.close)

&#x20;           c.event\_dispatcher = None

&#x20;           return dispatcher



&#x20;   def shutdown(self, c):

&#x20;       self.\_close(c)



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\worker\\consumer\\gossip.py

```python

"""Worker <-> Worker communication Bootstep."""

from collections import defaultdict

from functools import partial

from heapq import heappush

from operator import itemgetter



from kombu import Consumer

from kombu.asynchronous.semaphore import DummyLock

from kombu.exceptions import ContentDisallowed, DecodeError



from celery import bootsteps

from celery.utils.log import get\_logger

from celery.utils.objects import Bunch



from .mingle import Mingle



\_\_all\_\_ = ('Gossip',)



logger = get\_logger(\_\_name\_\_)

debug, info = logger.debug, logger.info





class Gossip(bootsteps.ConsumerStep):

&#x20;   """Bootstep consuming events from other workers.



&#x20;   This keeps the logical clock value up to date.

&#x20;   """



&#x20;   label = 'Gossip'

&#x20;   requires = (Mingle,)

&#x20;   \_cons\_stamp\_fields = itemgetter(

&#x20;       'id', 'clock', 'hostname', 'pid', 'topic', 'action', 'cver',

&#x20;   )

&#x20;   compatible\_transports = {'amqp', 'redis'}



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, c, without\_gossip=False,

&#x20;                interval=5.0, heartbeat\_interval=2.0, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       self.enabled = not without\_gossip and self.compatible\_transport(c.app)

&#x20;       self.app = c.app

&#x20;       c.gossip = self

&#x20;       self.Receiver = c.app.events.Receiver

&#x20;       self.hostname = c.hostname

&#x20;       self.full\_hostname = '.'.join(\[self.hostname, str(c.pid)])

&#x20;       self.on = Bunch(

&#x20;           node\_join=set(),

&#x20;           node\_leave=set(),

&#x20;           node\_lost=set(),

&#x20;       )



&#x20;       self.timer = c.timer

&#x20;       if self.enabled:

&#x20;           self.state = c.app.events.State(

&#x20;               on\_node\_join=self.on\_node\_join,

&#x20;               on\_node\_leave=self.on\_node\_leave,

&#x20;               max\_tasks\_in\_memory=1,

&#x20;           )

&#x20;           if c.hub:

&#x20;               c.\_mutex = DummyLock()

&#x20;           self.update\_state = self.state.event

&#x20;       self.interval = interval

&#x20;       self.heartbeat\_interval = heartbeat\_interval

&#x20;       self.\_tref = None

&#x20;       self.consensus\_requests = defaultdict(list)

&#x20;       self.consensus\_replies = {}

&#x20;       self.event\_handlers = {

&#x20;           'worker.elect': self.on\_elect,

&#x20;           'worker.elect.ack': self.on\_elect\_ack,

&#x20;       }

&#x20;       self.clock = c.app.clock



&#x20;       self.election\_handlers = {

&#x20;           'task': self.call\_task

&#x20;       }



&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(c, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def compatible\_transport(self, app):

&#x20;       with app.connection\_for\_read() as conn:

&#x20;           return conn.transport.driver\_type in self.compatible\_transports



&#x20;   def election(self, id, topic, action=None):

&#x20;       self.consensus\_replies\[id] = \[]

&#x20;       self.dispatcher.send(

&#x20;           'worker-elect',

&#x20;           id=id, topic=topic, action=action, cver=1,

&#x20;       )



&#x20;   def call\_task(self, task):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           self.app.signature(task).apply\_async()

&#x20;       except Exception as exc:  # pylint: disable=broad-except

&#x20;           logger.exception('Could not call task: %r', exc)



&#x20;   def on\_elect(self, event):

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           (id\_, clock, hostname, pid,

&#x20;            topic, action, \_) = self.\_cons\_stamp\_fields(event)

&#x20;       except KeyError as exc:

&#x20;           return logger.exception('election request missing field %s', exc)

&#x20;       heappush(

&#x20;           self.consensus\_requests\[id\_],

&#x20;           (clock, f'{hostname}.{pid}', topic, action),

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       self.dispatcher.send('worker-elect-ack', id=id\_)



&#x20;   def start(self, c):

&#x20;       super().start(c)

&#x20;       self.dispatcher = c.event\_dispatcher



&#x20;   def on\_elect\_ack(self, event):

&#x20;       id = event\['id']

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           replies = self.consensus\_replies\[id]

&#x20;       except KeyError:

&#x20;           return  # not for us

&#x20;       alive\_workers = set(self.state.alive\_workers())

&#x20;       replies.append(event\['hostname'])



&#x20;       if len(replies) >= len(alive\_workers):

&#x20;           \_, leader, topic, action = self.clock.sort\_heap(

&#x20;               self.consensus\_requests\[id],

&#x20;           )

&#x20;           if leader == self.full\_hostname:

&#x20;               info('I won the election %r', id)

&#x20;               try:

&#x20;                   handler = self.election\_handlers\[topic]

&#x20;               except KeyError:

&#x20;                   logger.exception('Unknown election topic %r', topic)

&#x20;               else:

&#x20;                   handler(action)

&#x20;           else:

&#x20;               info('node %s elected for %r', leader, id)

&#x20;           self.consensus\_requests.pop(id, None)

&#x20;           self.consensus\_replies.pop(id, None)



&#x20;   def on\_node\_join(self, worker):

&#x20;       debug('%s joined the party', worker.hostname)

&#x20;       self.\_call\_handlers(self.on.node\_join, worker)



&#x20;   def on\_node\_leave(self, worker):

&#x20;       debug('%s left', worker.hostname)

&#x20;       self.\_call\_handlers(self.on.node\_leave, worker)



&#x20;   def on\_node\_lost(self, worker):

&#x20;       info('missed heartbeat from %s', worker.hostname)

&#x20;       self.\_call\_handlers(self.on.node\_lost, worker)



&#x20;   def \_call\_handlers(self, handlers, \*args, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       for handler in handlers:

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               handler(\*args, \*\*kwargs)

&#x20;           except Exception as exc:  # pylint: disable=broad-except

&#x20;               logger.exception(

&#x20;                   'Ignored error from handler %r: %r', handler, exc)



&#x20;   def register\_timer(self):

&#x20;       if self.\_tref is not None:

&#x20;           self.\_tref.cancel()

&#x20;       self.\_tref = self.timer.call\_repeatedly(self.interval, self.periodic)



&#x20;   def periodic(self):

&#x20;       workers = self.state.workers

&#x20;       dirty = set()

&#x20;       for worker in workers.values():

&#x20;           if not worker.alive:

&#x20;               dirty.add(worker)

&#x20;               self.on\_node\_lost(worker)

&#x20;       for worker in dirty:

&#x20;           workers.pop(worker.hostname, None)



&#x20;   def get\_consumers(self, channel):

&#x20;       self.register\_timer()

&#x20;       ev = self.Receiver(channel, routing\_key='worker.#',

&#x20;                          queue\_ttl=self.heartbeat\_interval)

&#x20;       return \[Consumer(

&#x20;           channel,

&#x20;           queues=\[ev.queue],

&#x20;           on\_message=partial(self.on\_message, ev.event\_from\_message),

&#x20;           accept=ev.accept,

&#x20;           no\_ack=True

&#x20;       )]



&#x20;   def on\_message(self, prepare, message):

&#x20;       \_type = message.delivery\_info\['routing\_key']



&#x20;       # For redis when `fanout\_patterns=False` (See Issue #1882)

&#x20;       if \_type.split('.', 1)\[0] == 'task':

&#x20;           return

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           handler = self.event\_handlers\[\_type]

&#x20;       except KeyError:

&#x20;           pass

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           return handler(message.payload)



&#x20;       # proto2: hostname in header; proto1: in body

&#x20;       hostname = (message.headers.get('hostname') or

&#x20;                   message.payload\['hostname'])

&#x20;       if hostname != self.hostname:

&#x20;           try:

&#x20;               \_, event = prepare(message.payload)

&#x20;               self.update\_state(event)

&#x20;           except (DecodeError, ContentDisallowed, TypeError) as exc:

&#x20;               logger.error(exc)

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           self.clock.forward()



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\worker\\consumer\\heart.py

```python

"""Worker Event Heartbeat Bootstep."""

from celery import bootsteps

from celery.worker import heartbeat



from .events import Events



\_\_all\_\_ = ('Heart',)





class Heart(bootsteps.StartStopStep):

&#x20;   """Bootstep sending event heartbeats.



&#x20;   This service sends a ``worker-heartbeat`` message every n seconds.



&#x20;   Note:

&#x20;       Not to be confused with AMQP protocol level heartbeats.

&#x20;   """



&#x20;   requires = (Events,)



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, c,

&#x20;                without\_heartbeat=False, heartbeat\_interval=None, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       self.enabled = not without\_heartbeat

&#x20;       self.heartbeat\_interval = heartbeat\_interval

&#x20;       c.heart = None

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(c, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def start(self, c):

&#x20;       c.heart = heartbeat.Heart(

&#x20;           c.timer, c.event\_dispatcher, self.heartbeat\_interval,

&#x20;       )

&#x20;       c.heart.start()



&#x20;   def stop(self, c):

&#x20;       c.heart = c.heart and c.heart.stop()

&#x20;   shutdown = stop



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\worker\\consumer\\mingle.py

```python

"""Worker <-> Worker Sync at startup (Bootstep)."""

from celery import bootsteps

from celery.utils.log import get\_logger



from .events import Events



\_\_all\_\_ = ('Mingle',)



logger = get\_logger(\_\_name\_\_)

debug, info, exception = logger.debug, logger.info, logger.exception





class Mingle(bootsteps.StartStopStep):

&#x20;   """Bootstep syncing state with neighbor workers.



&#x20;   At startup, or upon consumer restart, this will:



&#x20;   - Sync logical clocks.

&#x20;   - Sync revoked tasks.



&#x20;   """



&#x20;   label = 'Mingle'

&#x20;   requires = (Events,)

&#x20;   compatible\_transports = {'amqp', 'redis', 'gcpubsub'}



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, c, without\_mingle=False, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       self.enabled = not without\_mingle and self.compatible\_transport(c.app)

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(

&#x20;           c, without\_mingle=without\_mingle, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def compatible\_transport(self, app):

&#x20;       with app.connection\_for\_read() as conn:

&#x20;           return conn.transport.driver\_type in self.compatible\_transports



&#x20;   def start(self, c):

&#x20;       self.sync(c)



&#x20;   def sync(self, c):

&#x20;       info('mingle: searching for neighbors')

&#x20;       replies = self.send\_hello(c)

&#x20;       if replies:

&#x20;           info('mingle: sync with %s nodes',

&#x20;                len(\[reply for reply, value in replies.items() if value]))

&#x20;           \[self.on\_node\_reply(c, nodename, reply)

&#x20;            for nodename, reply in replies.items() if reply]

&#x20;           info('mingle: sync complete')

&#x20;       else:

&#x20;           info('mingle: all alone')



&#x20;   def send\_hello(self, c):

&#x20;       inspect = c.app.control.inspect(timeout=1.0, connection=c.connection)

&#x20;       our\_revoked = c.controller.state.revoked

&#x20;       replies = inspect.hello(c.hostname, our\_revoked.\_data) or {}

&#x20;       replies.pop(c.hostname, None)  # delete my own response

&#x20;       return replies



&#x20;   def on\_node\_reply(self, c, nodename, reply):

&#x20;       debug('mingle: processing reply from %s', nodename)

&#x20;       try:

&#x20;           self.sync\_with\_node(c, \*\*reply)

&#x20;       except MemoryError:

&#x20;           raise

&#x20;       except Exception as exc:  # pylint: disable=broad-except

&#x20;           exception('mingle: sync with %s failed: %r', nodename, exc)



&#x20;   def sync\_with\_node(self, c, clock=None, revoked=None, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       self.on\_clock\_event(c, clock)

&#x20;       self.on\_revoked\_received(c, revoked)



&#x20;   def on\_clock\_event(self, c, clock):

&#x20;       c.app.clock.adjust(clock) if clock else c.app.clock.forward()



&#x20;   def on\_revoked\_received(self, c, revoked):

&#x20;       if revoked:

&#x20;           c.controller.state.revoked.update(revoked)



```



\### 代码文件: celery\\worker\\consumer\\tasks.py

```python

"""Worker Task Consumer Bootstep."""



from \_\_future\_\_ import annotations



from kombu.common import QoS, ignore\_errors



from celery import bootsteps

from celery.utils.log import get\_logger

from celery.utils.quorum\_queues import detect\_quorum\_queues



from .mingle import Mingle



\_\_all\_\_ = ('Tasks',)





logger = get\_logger(\_\_name\_\_)

debug = logger.debug





class Tasks(bootsteps.StartStopStep):

&#x20;   """Bootstep starting the task message consumer."""



&#x20;   requires = (Mingle,)



&#x20;   def \_\_init\_\_(self, c, \*\*kwargs):

&#x20;       c.task\_consumer = c.qos = None

&#x20;       super().\_\_init\_\_(c, \*\*kwargs)



&#x20;   def start(self, c):

&#x20;       """Start task consumer."""

&#x20;       c.update\_strategies()



&#x20;       qos\_global = self.qos\_global(c)



&#x20;       # set initial prefetch count

&#x20;       c.connection.default\_channel.basic\_qos(

&#x20;           0, c.initial\_prefetch\_count, qos\_global,

&#x20;       )



&#x20;       c.task\_consumer = c.app.amqp.TaskConsumer(

&#x20;           c.connection, on\_decode\_error=c.on\_decode\_error,

&#x20;       )



&#x20;       def set\_prefetch\_count(prefetch\_count):

&#x20;           return c.task\_consumer.qos(

&#x20;               prefetch\_count=prefetch\_count,

&#x20;               apply\_global=qos\_global,

&#x20;           )

&#x20;       eta\_task\_limit = c.app.conf.worker\_eta\_task\_limit

&#x20;       c.qos = QoS(

&#x20;           set\_prefetch\_count, c.initial\_prefetch\_count, max\_prefetch=eta\_task\_limit

&#x20;       )



&#x20;       if c.app.conf.worker\_disable\_prefetch:

&#x20;           from types import MethodType



&#x20;           from celery.worker import state

&#x20;           channel\_qos = c.task\_consumer.channel.qos

&#x20;           original\_can\_consume = channel\_qos.can\_consume



&#x20;           def can\_consume(self):

&#x20;               # Prefer autoscaler's max\_concurrency if set; otherwise fall back to pool size

&#x20;               limit = getattr(c.controller, "max\_concurrency", None) or c.pool.num\_processes

&#x20;               if len(state.reserved\_requests) >= limit:

&#x20;                   return False

&#x20;               return original\_can\_consume()



&#x20;           channel\_qos.can\_consume = MethodType(can\_consume, channel\_qos)



&#x20;   def stop(self, c):

&#x20;       """Stop task consumer."""

&#x20;       if c.task\_consumer:

&#x20;           debug('Canceling task consumer...')

&#x20;           ignore\_errors(c, c.task\_consumer.cancel)



&#x20;   def shutdown(self, c):

&#x20;       """Shutdown task consumer."""

&#x20;       if c.task\_consumer:

&#x20;           self.stop(c)

&#x20;           debug('Closing consumer channel...')

&#x20;           ignore\_errors(c, c.task\_consumer.close)

&#x20;           c.task\_consumer = None



&#x20;   def info(self, c):

&#x20;       """Return task consumer info."""

&#x20;       return {'prefetch\_count': c.qos.value if c.qos else 'N/A'}



&#x20;   def qos\_global(self, c) -> bool:

&#x20;       """Determine if global QoS should be applied.



&#x20;       Additional information:

&#x20;           https://www.rabbitmq.com/docs/consumer-prefetch

&#x20;           https://www.rabbitmq.com/docs/quorum-queues#global-qos

&#x20;       """

&#x20;       # - RabbitMQ 3.3 completely redefines how basic\_qos works...

&#x20;       # This will detect if the new qos semantics is in effect,

&#x20;       # and if so make sure the 'apply\_global' flag is set on qos updates.

&#x20;       qos\_global = not c.connection.qos\_semantics\_matches\_spec



&#x20;       if c.app.conf.worker\_detect\_quorum\_queues:

&#x20;           using\_quorum\_queues, \_ = detect\_quorum\_queues(

&#x20;               c.app, c.connection.transport.driver\_type

&#x20;           )



&#x20;           if using\_quorum\_queues:

&#x20;               qos\_global = False

&#x20;               logger.info("Global QoS is disabled. Prefetch count in now static.")



&#x20;       return qos\_global



```

"""Worker consumer."""

from .agent import Agent

from .connection import Connection

from .consumer import Consumer

from .control import Control

from .events import Events

from .gossip import Gossip

from .heart import Heart

from .mingle import Mingle

from .tasks import Tasks



\_\_all\_\_ = (

&#x20;   'Consumer', 'Agent', 'Connection', 'Control',

&#x20;   'Events', 'Gossip', 'Heart', 'Mingle', 'Tasks',

)
